description:
The game is played on an 8x8 board representing a garden.

There are two players – White and Red. Each player has sixteen pieces consisting of:


    A piece of garden Décor
    Three Queens
    A dozen Roses


The garden Décor (a statue and a bench) once set does not move and cannot be captured by either side. In other words, they act as permanent Blocks.

The Queens move and capture just like Chess Queens.

The Roses, once placed, do not move.

The game is played in two phases – “Planting” and “Gathering”

Planting: The players first each set their own piece of Décor on any empty space on the board. This is then followed by their Queens. The players must place their Queens so no Queen can immediately attack an opponent’s Queen. Lastly, all the Roses are arranged around the board, in any remaining empty spaces.

Gathering: Once all the pieces are set, players then can move their Queens around the board, capturing an opponent’s pieces – both Roses and Queens.

Goal - There are two different ways to win:

    Capture all twelve of your opponent’s Roses
    Capture all three of your opponent’s Queens

Survive an undead apocalypse and deliver the cure to eliminate the pandemic. Players work together to save humanity from near certain doom.

Simulating Hordes of Zombies migrating throughout a metropolis, players move through the city attempting to combine elements of a cure to eliminate the Zombie infestation.

Each Player chooses a different Role to play for the game with a variety of different abilities and carrying capacities. With Five Roles to choose from players must develop different strategies every game in order to fight off the multitude of zombie hordes.

The five roles are; Sniper, Hunter, Medic, Mechanic and Scavenger.

Gather Weapons, Equipment, and Vehicles. 
Store Supplies: Food, Ammo, Medicine, and Fuel in Hideouts.

No Dice - No Complicated Rules - Easy and Fun to Play

ATC Rush Hour is a game in which players take role of Air Traffic Controllers on an airport. The main objective of the game is to make sure that all the planes land or take off without any problems. The game will is available to play from 4 to 6 players. Each player controls one airport with its ATC (Air Traffic Controllers) and try to take off and land as many planes as possible.

Each player receives one board at the start of the game. The board represents an airport that is controlled by the player and it is divided into five Zones and seven spaces for Action cards. The Zones are used as an areas to place ATC and Plane cards and the other spaces are destined for corresponding Action cards (Airport, Upgrade, Event and Weather).

The key element of the game is moving the Planes between the Zones and players. The Planes are moved by sending them ATC commands. To send a command to a Plane, a player must pay with ATC points, which is done by removing a number of counters from ATC cards. Moving Planes provides victory points which are needed to win the game.

What the Fact (WTF) is an outrageously fun, quick & easy (like your mom) party game. Dice make the game about the players & what you'll post on social media to win.

A player selects a black card and rolls the dice to find out which player the card is about. Each of the other players turn in a gray card that fills in the blank and completes the fact. The person giving the most outrageous card wins the round.

The dice symbol represents whichever name came up on the dice.  Where it gets even more interesting: if you roll a "WTF", the card is up for grabs! The symbol then represents YOUR OWN NAME and the first person to post the phrase online gets the point. This could lead to some interesting What The Fact moments!

The Werewolf awake at the midnight, and touches Villager's fingers. Who touched your finger? Very simple and fun party game.

This game is made as Postcard Game.

You control the destiny of five fearless warriors. You will battle your way through three types of terrain: the damp Darkwoods, the rugged mountains, and the open plains. On your quest you will encounter trolls, deadly Knights and face attacks from every side. It is your mission to advance your satyr through the regions of battle, avoiding the death traps and protecting him at all costs. Because if you're satyr dies, your chance of winning the war dies with him.

Players roll dice to move around a track, through different regions of the board, and attack the other player by again rolling dice and comparing to a combat resolution chart to modify the rolls.

Final Attack! is a real-time cooperative tabletop strategy game for 1-5 players. You will pilot the K-Machines, ancient yet technologically advanced fighting mecha that can combine to form Final Kaiser, champion of justice. You will do battle against shapeshifting mechanical monsters called Robeasts, gigantic foot soldiers of the starfaring artificial intelligence FANGER. Your only way to defeat these terrors is to use the Final Attack, an all-powerful weapon that takes the entire game to charge.

Final Attack! is a unique blend of card game, worker placement game, social game, and arcade game. You will play cards, you will move chips around the table, you will solve resource puzzles, you will yell attack names, and you will endure hardship by yourself or with whatever companions fate has provided, all in real time. The game takes between 20 and 30 minutes to play, and there is absolutely nothing else like it.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From publisher's website:

As long as there has been language, there has been storytelling. One of our oldest traditions, passed down from generation to generation for thousands of years, it's a great way to teach, to learn, or to just have fun! And it has never needed a screen or batteries.

Now it's your turn to roll your very own story with Story Time Dice! Suitable for all ages and any number of players, these seven big, chunky polyhedral dice come in a variety of fun shapes, and with a quick roll will help you spin a million grand tales. Pit 12 heroes against 10 villains in 6 settings, using 20 tools and 8 vehicles to overcome 6 obstacles and claim 6 prizes! Can you create an adventure in which a lost diver thwarts a robot in a haunted house using music and a time warp to collect a valuable treasure? It's all up to the dice, and your imagination!

This Story Time Dice set includes:
3 six-sided dice with settings, obstacles and prizes
1 eight-sided vehicles die
1 ten-sided villains die
1 twelve-sided heroes die
1 twenty-sided tools die
1 guide to every icon on the dice
4 recommended games to play: Once Upon a Time, One by One, Heroes & Villains (see below), and Rescue Operation

Once Upon a Time and One by One are different styles of storytelling.

Rescue Operation
Have each player roll the hero die and the vehicle or tool dice to determine his/her character and their abilities. When all players have finished, one player rolls the villain, obstacle and setting dice and uses them to create a sticky situation for their character. The other players then must use their characters' abilities and teamwork to create a story in which they rescue their friend. The captured character can make their job more difficult by rolling more obstacles and villains into the story!

Heroes & Villains
One player rolls the hero die to create a character, then rolls the tool die three times and the vehicle die once to create their character's arsenal. The other player does the same, using the villain dice instead of the hero. The villain begins the story by using their character and arsenal to turn the situation against the hero. The hero then continues the story by trying to beat the villain. Players continue taking turns. The game is over when one player can't image how they could possibly win!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Labyrinth: The Awakening, 2010 - ? expands on Labyrinth: The War on Terror, 2001 - ?, a 1-2 player card-driven board game simulating at the strategic level the ongoing bid by Islamic extremists to impose their brand of religious rule on the Muslim world. This expansion continues where Labyrinth left off adding new rules and cards to cover the last five years of history. Included are new mechanics to simulate the grass roots political movements of the Arab Spring and the resulting Civil Wars. Labyrinth: The Awakening provides 120 all new event cards, four new country mats, additional markers, cubes and cylinders, and 7 new scenarios, including 2 that are playable to conclusion in 7 turns or less.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: History: Conflicts against the Islamic State

description:
Hold my hand is a party card game for 4-8 players. Players are children lost in fairy forest, trying to get out without becoming fairies. Children are scared, and unaware that one of them might be a fairy.

The game is played drafting cards. In each turn players look at the cards in their hands, put one card either in front of themselves(1) or in between themselves and player next to them(2 or 3).  (look at the picture).  These cards are put face down.
Then pass rest of the cards to the left.
Then players decide which card that is in between will they look at...

You have the game and the rules in the files section, so just take a look ;)
Kickstarter-exclusive add-on pack including 3 new creatures (Orc Scout, Orc Shaman, Orc Charger), 2 new items (Rune Armor, Rune Stone), and 12 new story cards.
Kickstarter-exclusive add-on pack including 3 new creatures (Fire Wisp, Stalker, Cave Drake), 2 new items (Charred Scales, Chains of Ilthor), and 12 new story cards.
Kickstarter-exclusive add-on pack including 3 new creatures (Spore Creeper, Rock Dervish, Bore Worm), 2 new items (Repeating Crossbow, Everlight), and 12 new story cards.
Kickstarter-exclusive add-on pack including 3 new creatures (Ghoul, Wight, True Thelastian), 2 new items (Revenant Tome, Unbound Servant), and 12 new story cards.

Fallen is a card and dice game in which two players battle deep within a dungeon. One player takes on the role of the Hero, exploring the twisting halls and deadly chambers of the labyrinth. Another player takes on the role of the Dungeon Lord, summoning monstrous creatures and wielding dark magic.

The adventure is brought to life through an array of cards and dice. As players explore the dungeon, Store Cards describe the rooms and challenges the Hero will face. When a battle erupts or a trap is sprung, Challenge Dice are rolled to see who is victorious.

The adventure culminates in an epic battle between the Hero and the Dungeon Lord with only one player left standing.

The Kickstarter Edition of the game includes 50 additional story cards plus new heroes (Adren the Knight, Cael Oathbringer the Priest, and Rivern the Huntress) and new dungeon lord characters (Norathra the Forsaken, Spider Queen, and Sythean the Devourer).

Specifically this includes:


     50 Story Cards
     3 Advanced Hero Character Cards
     3 Advanced Dungeon Lord Character Cards
     23 Creature Cards
     27 Hero Skill cards
     4 Basic Equipment cards
     13 Treasure cards
     30 Power cards (Hero)
     30 Power cards (Dungeon Lord)
     8 Omen cards
     42 Final Battle cards
     3 Sythean Creature cards
     6 Sythean Critical cards
     3 Cael Rune cards
     12 Cael Rune tokens
     4 Norathra Blood Vine tokens
     1 Rivern Mark token
     1 Blood token
     2 Adren Shield tokens
     1 Spider Queen Web card
     10 Spider Queen Web tokens

Senet is an ancient Egyptian board game.

Senet and Senet-modern are almost looks same but Senet-modern rule is arranged and renewed more speedy, offensive and strategic.

Players move own 5 pieces due to 2 dice number and be up the goal side.

One difference is that players can make opponent's piece out of board and another is Senet-modern pieces are printed different number's dots 1 to 5. Number is score.
 The winner is the player who get more score when 3 pieces(both own and opponent) are upped.

Party game. In the box there is a roll of twine. This should be cut to 4 equal lengths. One end of the twine is tied to a chair leg and the other end is held by the players. So, the players at the starting line will all be holding a length of twine (about 12 feet long) and they must pull it backwards so that the twine doesn't sag in the middle and the line should be about 18 inches off the floor. Each player now has a 'Donkey' put on to their twine, as near to the players' as possible.

On the word "Off", each player has to jog their twine furiously so that their donkey heads towards their chair. The first to touch the chair leg with their donkey's nose, wins.

The game can be modified to allow young children to play.

boardgame: Malifaux: No Shelter Here – Pandora box set
description:
Let your opponent beat themselves. Literally. You'll be beating people to death with their own arms and watching them suffer the death of a thousand cuts, also self inflicted. Pandora does attract a certain kind of player...

Neverborn Box Set, containing seven 32mm 'heroic' scale miniatures made of high quality plastic, Stat Cards and six Upgrades. Will require some preparation and assembly.

This game includes both OGRE and its sequel G.E.V., tactical ground combat games set in the not-so-distant future. In 2085 A.D., armored warfare continues - faster and deadlier than ever. Hovercraft, tanks and infantry slug it out with tactical nuclear devices. But the most feared weapon of all needs no human guidance. It's a giant cybernetic tank called the OGRE.

The basic OGRE game gives one player a force of infantry and armor, and a command post he must defend. The other player has only one unit - but it's an OGRE. It's an even match. Advanced games allow solitaire or multi-player action, with OGREs on both sides.

In G.E.V., units from Ogre are carried over, with expanded rules for terrain. A larger map and more ambitious game.

User summary:
ハナノワ (Hananowa) is a game from the Japanese dojin circle Comet. Comet is a community group using board games to bring people together. This game shows their goals in action.

Hananowa is a very quick game based on communication. The players work together to create a story aiming towards the ending on the closure card. But whatever you come up with, you have to tie it to the item cards that you draw throughout the game.

Publisher summary:
このゲームはコミュニケーションを楽しむゲームです。
結末カードに書かれた結末に向かって、みんなでお話を作ります。
ただし、アイテムカードをめくってそこに書かれたものに関係する話をしなくてはなりません。

Did you know that nine U.S. Presidents didn't attend college? Or that six Presidents were born in Ohio, more than any other state? And that one President was stopped for a speeding ticket while riding a horse?

In Presidents Poker, players assemble a hand of President cards with the Presidents that have the most in common, including names, birthplace, college attended, number of children, height, and hair color. Each President card also has a fun fact about that President so that these historical figures really come to life. Chips are included for those who like to bet.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Ancient Brass Dragon Premium Figure
description:
From the publisher:

This fantastic pre-painted premium figure stands several inches tall and towers over all of the other D&D Attack Wing expansion pack miniatures. The perfect encounter for your next adventure, or the perfect ally to your force. The D&D Attack Wing Brass Dragon Premium Figure is a beautiful display piece as well!

The D&D Attack Wing version of the Brass Dragon figure also comes with additional play materials such as Cards, Tokens, Maneuver Dials, and a 6” x 6” D&D Attack Wing base.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

description: The standard board game of Monopoly but re-themed for the great city of Charlotte, NC.

Let's bring the past and the future to the present with this awesome fighting card and dice game. Come join the action packed, fast paced, imaginary galaxy described as Street Fighter meets pro wrestling! Welcome to the IFC!

This game takes elements of various games and molded its own to create what is now the IFC. It's a simple fight game that has no crazy charts, move cards or other wacky add ons...just simple cards with vibrant characters, dice and your imagination.

Set up a fight card, play the matches, create the drama...it's all about letting your imagination run wild!

Leprechaun's Revenge is an expansion for what is simply the bestest and most funnest card game in the world, Redshirts.

Redshirts is a fast paced screw-your-buddies style card game for 2 to 6 players. Technically you can play with more, if you have extra cards from expansion sets.

Redshirts is a card game set in a universe similar to, but legally distinct from, many sci-fi franchises. The goal is to be the first player to run out of crewmen because everyone knows the captains of boring voyages don't get promotions. You do this by sending them on missions they are not trained for, taking away vital equipment at the last second, and occasionally, just shooting them in the back and shoving them out an airlock.

Redshirts 2: Leprechaun's Revenge is an expansion, offering a new set of cards, featuring the evil protagonist, Leprechaun.  (Don't worry, there's lots of other stuff in there too.)

Get ready to flip out with this flip 'n slide game of tic-tac-toe! Slideways is a fast-paced puzzle game of logic and skill that raises the bar on fun as players try to outwit their opponents. Move your bar on the board or flip colors to get four in a row. Is it as easy as it seems?
Second expansion to the Redshirts base game.

Currently contains five extra special cards for the base game.

Special Cards:

Taboo - Medical
Location - Fantasy Planet
Equipment - Plot Device
Mission - Guard the Galaxy
Event - Hail Mary Play

This is a mini-expansion.

Due to differences in print sheets, we had a bunch of extra blank cards during the last print run. And so we had some more cards created, and we are offering those to you first. These will normally only be available directly from us at conventions, and there are a very limited amount. This set doesn't have a name or a theme, but we do make fun of Warhammer 40k a lot.

A game of skill and chance for two!

FROM THE GAME BOX:

OBJECT OF THE GAME: For the hunter to capture the fox.

PIECES NEEDED TO PLAY : Two different colored pegs and 1 pair of dice.  The Fox makes the first move.  Both he and the Hunter can only move along the lines.  Both Fox and Hunter alternate turns moving only one space at a time.  The Hunter tries to force the Fox into a position where the Hunter's next move would be into the same hole the Fox is in.  When the Hunter reaches this position he rolls the dice.  If he rolls an even number, he has netted the Fox.  If he rolls an odd number the Fox may take two moves and thus gets away.  The game continues until the Hunter again corners the Fox.  At this time the Hunter again rolls the dice.  If an even number is rolled the Hunter wins, but if an odd number is rolled the Fox has eluded the Hunter for the second time, and the Fox is the winner.

boardgame: Ghostbusters: The Board Game – Mass Hysteria exclusives
description:
Exclusive to Kickstarter backers of the Deluxe Edition of the base game. The backer pledge level was called 'Mass Hysteria'.

Contains:


    The Sandman ghost miniature and ghost card
    The "impossible mode" super-sized (100mm) angry Stay Puft Marshmallow Man figure and ghost card
    Four double-sided Sedgewick Hotel game board tiles
    A 12" x 12" Giclee print of the games characters and logo
    Glow in the dark versions of the 6 game dice

You and your friends are business moguls and there is a new plot of land in the city that is ripe for building!  You want to build the most floors, have the tallest sky scrapers, and screw over your opponents.  Each tunr you take your fortune into your own hands by choosing more or less risky bets on what numbers will be rolled and what resources to collect.

Corporate America was built on ruthless tactics and stock photography.  The dark humor and art reinforces this point with leadership cards that players strategically utilize to gain ground on their opponents.

My Metropolis is a 3-4 player worker game that features worker placement and resource collection.  Inspired by Settlers of Catan and Small World, My Metropolis is a fast paced and cut throat gaming experience with varied strategy and unique artwork.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Phoenix Expansion Pack
description:
The U.S.S. Phoenix is a Federation Nebula-class starship. Under the command of Captain Benjamin Maxwell, and without orders, the Phoenix destroyed a number of Cardassian ships and a Cardassian Outpost before it was intercepted by the U.S.S. Enterprise-D and ordered back to Starbase 211.
Star Trek - Attack Wing: U.S.S. Phoenix Expansion Pack is part of the Wave 18 expansion packs from WizKids Games, and this expansion comes with one new ship and a new set of cards for Star Trek: Attack Wing.

The Phoenix is a Nebula class starship.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Bioship Beta Expansion Pack
description:
Bioship Beta of Species 8472 is an organic vessel with an advanced biological technology. These ships use an electrodynamic fluid that flows through vein-like conduits as a source of energy and can travel through fluidic space.
Star Trek - Attack Wing: Bioship Beta Expansion Pack is part of the Wave 18 expansion packs from WizKids Games, and this expansion comes with one new ship and a new set of cards for Star Trek: Attack Wing.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Quark's Treasure Expansion Pack
description:
Quark’s Treasure is a Ferengi Shuttle. This vessel was given to Quark by his cousin as repayment for a debt. Quark decided to take it on a test journey and, joined by his brother, Rom, traveled to Earth to deliver his nephew, Nog, to Starfleet Academy. Of course, he also planned to make profit on the trip by smuggling a shipment of kemocite in the cargo hold.
Star Trek - Attack Wing: Quark's Treasure Expansion Pack is part of the Wave 18 expansion packs from WizKids Games, and this expansion comes with one new ship and a new set of cards for Star Trek: Attack Wing.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Thunderchild Expansion Pack
description:
The U.S.S. Thunderchild is a Federation Akira-class starship. These heavy cruisers were used throughout the Dominion War and, battled against a Borg Cube at the Battle Sector 001, alongside the U.S.S. Defiant, in a fleet under the command of Vice Admiral Hayes.
Star Trek - Attack Wing: U.S.S. Thunderchild Expansion Pack is part of the Wave 17 expansion packs from WizKids Games, and this expansion comes with one new ship and a new set of cards for Star Trek: Attack Wing.

The Thunderchild is an Akira class starship.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – I.K.S. T'Ong Expansion Pack
description:
The I.K.S. T’Ong is a Klingon K’t’inga-class battle-cruiser that was sent by the Klingon High Command on a long-range mission in the year 2290. Returning from the mission, Captain K’Temoc and the crew of the T’Ong woke from a cryogenic stasis 75 years in the future. They encountered the U.S.S. Enterprise-D and learned that the Federation-Klingon War had ended.
Star Trek - Attack Wing: I.K.S. T'Ong Expansion Pack is part of the Wave 17 expansion packs from WizKids Games, and this expansion comes with one new ship and a new set of cards for Star Trek: Attack Wing.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – I.R.W. Vrax Expansion Pack
description:
The I.R.W. Vrax is a Romulan Valdore-class starship. Along with its sister ship, the I.R.W. Valdore, the Vrax joined forces with the U.S.S. Enterprise NCC-1701-E to battle against the Reman Warbird, Scimitar, at the Battle in the Bassen Rift.
Star Trek - Attack Wing: I.R.W. Vrax Expansion Pack is part of the Wave 17 expansion packs from WizKids Games, and this expansion comes with one new ship and a new set of cards for Star Trek: Attack Wing.

Game description from the publisher:

Gomorra needs a revival! After the disastrous Election Day, Gomorra seems like a town devoid of mercy or grace, but there are some devoted to the work of the divine. Bringing blessings and simple compassion, these souls seek not only to raise up the meek, but drive out those who would destroy. But can the same be said of the new mayor?

Faith and Fear is the first Pinebox set for Doomtown: Reloaded and introduces the new Blessed dudes and their miracles — but of course other forces are always at work, and you'll find many new cards for all your favorite factions, including four entirely new outfit cards!

boardgame: Character Quest: Heroes The Card Game – Game Pack #2: Savage Beasts
description:
In CQ:Heroes, up to 4 players take turns collecting heroes and sending them out to gain victory points for your tavern.  From the mighty Paladins and Wardens of the King's Guard from Game Pack #1 to the powerful Great Wolves and Berserkers from Game Pack #2, Savage Beasts, each player will try to create a powerful combo of heroes to defeat even the toughest locations.

The heroes from Game Pack #2 - Savage Beasts feature Pet heroes that can be controlled by the Beastmaster skill.

Game Pack #2 - Savage Beasts includes the Adventure Deck: Desert Temple which features all new cards including a new card type: Raid Boss.  These encounters are much tougher than normal encounters and are placed in the location area when revealed.
The new objective in Desert Temple is to defeat 3 of these Raid Bosses to open up the Throne Room.  This triggers the final battle where all players make last party of heroes to battle each other for the room's treasures.

GAME FEATURES:

Simple and engaging: Although it's simple to learn and play, turns can become quite involved with all of the hero abilities and how you combo them during your turn.

Everyone plays to the end: No one is eliminated during the game and all players count their victory points at the end.

Quick flow of play: Because you draw up your hand at the end of your turn, you are then able to plan your next turn while others are taking theirs.

Accessible: Only 1 deck is needed for up to 4 players.  Extra decks can be used separately by each player or shuffled together for everyone to share.

Easy to collect: No mystery packs.  Each deck has all the cards you need to play.

Fast: The game sets up in less than a minute and plays in about 10-20 minutes.

HOW TO PLAY:

Starting with the first player, each player performs the following 4 actions in order:

ATTACK: Attack 1 encounter OR skip your attack to add one additional Hero to your Tavern on the next step
TAVERN: Place 1 hero in your Tavern (2 if you skipped your attack)
DRAW: Draw your hand back up to 5 cards
REFRESH: Draw and add 1 new Adventure card to the dungeon hall

boardgame: Character Quest: Heroes – Goblin Raiders Adventure
description:
Goblin Raiders Adventure intro: The nearby farms are being captured and pillaged!  Lead by a Goblin Brute that must be defeated.  Player's take turns trying to free Wheat Field locations before Goblin Torchers show up and pillage them.  Once the fields have been rescued (or burned), players will then be able to try and defeat the Goblin Brute before time runs out!

A full 50 card hero deck.  It has a few heroes from Game Pack #1, a few from Game Pack #2 and a couple heroes from a future Game Pack (sneak peak of the Storm Shaman, Half-Giant Warrior, and Templar)

The full 26 card Goblin Raiders Adventure deck is a spin off of the Goblin Keep Adventure but it also includes a few new cards made just for this demo (mostly to help with the new objective of stopping the goblins from raiding nearby farmlands!)

This introductory set does not use the final art from either Game Pack #1 or #2. The cards also reference 'Raid', this simply means a fight or battle.

A link for downloading this game can be found on the Kickstarter campaign page for Character Quest: Heroes (Tavern Edition).

King's Valley Labyrinth is a new extension game of King's Valley.

Board size is enlarged to 7x7 square grid and 4 pillar stone spaces are added.

As in the original game the simplicity of the game stems from the fact that the movement of all the pieces is the same. All pieces move horizontally or vertically or diagonally.  But when a piece is moved it must continue its movement until, and only until, it reaches a vacant square adjacent to (alongside) either the edge of the board or another piece or pillar stone.

The game is decided when one player moves the King piece to the center square--the King's Valley--thus winning the game.

Zuqi is a game of intense strategy in which players try to achieve the classic goal of getting four pieces in a row or occupy all four corners at once, but there's a twist. Each piece in your arsenal has unique movement abilities, capture abilities, and other powers at their disposal, and you may only bring 5 out of 8 of them to every game, so each game will play out very differently.

With unique rules that keep the game balanced and fair from beginning to end, every game is intense, and even if you're winning in one moment, the tide can quickly turn. Compete with your opponent for supremacy on the battlefield in a mental tug of war. You take one piece from him, he places another. He takes one from you, you place another. Eventually something has to give, and it shall. This is a game of strategy and skill and only the strong shall prevail.

Game covers series of three battles - Trzciana, Pułkowice and Straszewo fought between polish and imperial forces against army of Sweden, led by the Gustav II Adolf.

Game was published in the 39 issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.

From the publisher's website:

Create the heroes, adventures and battles of your imagination.

The Open Combat tabletop miniature wargame skirmish rules are suitable for any pre-gunpowder historical period or fantasy setting.

Use any miniatures & terrain you have
Only a small area required (24"x24")
Suitable for pre-gunpowder historical periods and fantasy settings
Customize the characteristics of your fighters, ‘stat’ them any way you like.
Fast setup and play time (30-40mins)
Open Combat puts you in command of a warband of fighters led by their charismatic (or frightening) leader as they match themselves against their foes.

Who the combatants are and what they are fighting about is down to you, your miniature collection and what you have the 'itch' to play.

The 100 page full colour hardback book includes rules for building and customising your warbands along with a range of weapons, skills & abilities (including Influence abilities where your warriors can make psychological attacks against the enemy).

You'll find rules for movement, combat, terrain and additional rules for hiding, climbing and jumping.

Scenarios include 'Open Combat' with three optional deployments including 'confrontation' where your warbands could begin the game intermingled even before the fighting starts! Additional scenarios set the warbands the task of capturing prey or discovering prizes hidden on the battlefield.

Two roster sheet approaches are included: A 'single page' for players that like all of the information in one place; A sheet of character cards for players that like to have separate information for each model.

Also included are guides to creating your first profile, terrain set-ups Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) and a Quick Reference Sheet.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
It's not easy keeping a family close, especially when everyone can be killed at any moment, and anyone could be a werewolf.

In this quirky take on the fundamentals of game theory, players need to accurately deduct who is a werewolf, and who is not. Citizens with special roles are crucial in finding out the murderous outsiders. If they cannot, they lose. If they can, they win. The twist is - can they do this quick enough before the werewolves murder them all?

Collaborate, debate, investigate... Correctly banish all the werewolves, or deceive your friends and devour them all.

The only real gestures game.

"Hints" is a game in which you can not be speak. You must make word or words combination clear to the rest of your team without speaking it. The categories 'saying', 'film', 'TV', 'songs', 'books' and 'radio' have fixed gestures. Your teammates have 60 seconds to guess what you gestures mean.

Mythic Heroes is a light strategy game, where each player controls a famed hero of Greek Mythology.  Players are vying for the title of Greatest of the ancient heroes.  Win by finding and defeating mythical creatures or by battling your fellow heroes.

Each player uses the cards in their hand to perform actions on their turn to gain an advantage or trip up the competition.  All the while, the Gods of Mt. Olympus will appear at random to mix up the game play.

Can you recite "Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star" backward? Can you name all twelve signs of the zodiac while hammered out of your mind? Can you do a curtsy while balancing a ball on your fist? Influenced by the tests issued to those who may have had tee many martoonies, Sobriety Test is a hilarious and raucous game that will have friends reeling just to prove they're totally "with it." This game will challenge memory, speech, and composure, and that's with or without drinking!

Music Rocks is a game of Music. Divide into teams or play one on one and beat each other in finding hit songs. It’s free for songbirds to sing the songs and for musicians to add optional instruments.

The symbols on the dice represent the most popular words in hit songs from 1890 to date. Roll the dice and try to think of a song lyric with the help of the symbols.
 Example: The symbol of the Sun may make you think of Soundgarden's "Black Hole Sun". Or why not "Summertime, and the living is easy"? But remember, you must always be able to explain it afterwards.

The game has one basic game play and many suggested alternative rules.

Pony Express is a game for the younger audience. Task is to deliver mail on specified places as soon as possible. Points are collected in two ways. The first way by reaching finish line (first gets 12 points, second 10, third 8 ...). Another way is to deliver as many mails. Each mail brings 1 point. The winner is the player who ends up with the most points.

Game for children aged 3-8 years, learning foreign language (English, German or Spanish).

The educational aim of the game is to develop the sense of hearing (less used at school than the sense of sight) and to memorise the correct pronunciation of the foreign vocabulary.
Square cards have got pictures on one side and QR codes at the back. You set up them like in classic memory, hiding pictures. Using a special application for mobile devices (Android or iOS), produced by publisher, you scan QR codes of two cards of your choice. You will hear two recordings. If they are the same, you’ve just found the pair. Take these cards and look at the pictures to check if you understand the meaning of the foreign words. If recordings are different, it’s not a pair so it’s a next player turn.

The game is now available only in the print-and-play version (in the teacher’s pack "Memo The Dragon", www.memothedragon.pl). Mobile apps are available for free.

Wild Cards are a collection of rules for games that utilize a deck of 52 playing cards. Each card contains the rules for a unique game made up by Nick Pace. I have played these a few times at parties and with family after being challenged to make up some good social games. Think of these as a replacement for the useless joker cards that accompany every box of playing cards.

The artwork is all done by my friend JP, which is based upon a concept sketch I give him. He does a great job with the cartoon graphics since these games are meant for people of ages 8+. Great for family outings, waits at the airport, and parties.

A shipment of rare gold bullion coins are being transported to your bank for safe overnight keeping.

As employees of the bank, you and your 'team' have special access to the outside of the vault where the coins are being stored.  Each of your team has knowledge of a small part of the combination.

But plans have changed and betrayal is imminent, your 'friends' aren't so quick to give up their information.  And an alarm has been tripped, and you all have 5 minutes to get the coins and get out...

In Bank Vault you have 5 minutes to figure out the code to the vault.  The first person to determine the full code is the winner.  The code is a series of four digits, each of a different color.

Each person at the table knows one of the numbers, and may be willing to share information via quid pro quo. Although you must realize that:


    one player must lie when asked any specific questions regarding numbers
    one player must lie when asked any specific questions regarding colors
    and everyone else is telling the truth

boardgame: King of New York: St. George Theater
description:
King of New York: St. George Theater is a promotional Discard card for King of New York. It is discounted in Staten Island, and gives +1★ and heals 1 damage.

Part of the King of Tokyo series.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
King of New York: Hibernation is a promotional Keep card for King of New York. You are forbidden from buying this card in Manhattan (too noisy!). It prevents you from taking damage, but the only rolls you can use are ♥ and ⌁ — using anything else wakes you from hibernation.

Part of the King of Tokyo series.

Doug, Doug, Goose, Caboose is a quick party game inspired by the children's playground game "Duck, Duck, Goose". The game supports 3-8 players, and plays in about 10 minutes.

The dealer shuffles the deck of cards (one per player in the game), and deals one, in turn, to each player, calling out the card each time. Once both the "Goose" and "Caboose" cards are in play, these player race to call out the other players names, going clockwise around the table. The winner scores a point. The cards are shuffled and the game continues until someone scores three points.

But wait! There's more! The "Goose" and "Caboose" players can get tripped up and have to start over if they don't say the names right, or if they don't call the other racing player "Doug". Everyone else is in charge with keeping the racing players honest.

Materials
8x8 checkerboard
64 Othello disks

Play
Board starts out empty.
One player plays black the other white.
Decide who goes first in any convenient manner, turns then alternate.

On a turn, you must,

1) Place a friendly color disk in an empty space that is not orthogonally adjacent to an opponent's color disk (diagonally adjacent is fine) and

2) Move another friendly color disk already on the board any distance in a straight line orthogonally or diagonally, without jumping over other disks and ending the move in an empty space. The last disk moved must not be orthogonally adjacent to an opponent's color disks (diagonally adjacent is fine) or

3) Pass

The game ends when players pass consecutively.

Object
The player with the largest connected group of orthogonally adjacent disks, in his color, wins.

Captain’s Quarters is a tile rummy game that replaces colored numbers with the maritime signal flag alphabet. The name Captain’s Quarters is a reference to the nautical theme but also a pun. Each of the 26 flags in the alphabet are divided into quarters creating 104 graphic tiles. Like other tile rummy games, the tiles are used to make groups that can be rearranged to create new groups; however, you will find out quickly the quartered flags add a revolutionary type of gameplay.

The goal is to be the first player to play all of his or her tiles after assembling at least two nautical signal flags correctly.

Captain’s Quarters is a fresh new addition to the world of rummy and mahjong both thematically and mechanically.

Europa is a Reversi variant.

Materials
8x8 checkerboard
64 Othello disks

Play
Board starts out empty.

One player plays black the other white.

Decide who goes first in any convenient manner, turns then alternate.

The game is played in two phases.

In the first phase on a turn, you must,

1) Place a friendly color disk in an empty space that is not orthogonally adjacent to an opponent's color disk (diagonally adjacent is fine) or

2) Pass.

The game enters the second phase after players pass consecutively.

So from now on, on a turn, either,

1) Place a disk of your color on an empty space or

2) Place a disk on an empty space so that it and another like color disk already on the board enclose a line of one or more
disks of your opponent. Then flip the disks to your color. A line of enclosed disks may run horizontally, vertically or diagonally.

The game ends when the board is full.

Object
The player with the most disks in his color on the board wins.

From the back of the box:
Children learn about the connections between objects and develop language and logic skills. This game is played with jumbo-sized, self-correcting cards. What goes with the chair? That's easy - it's the table! If you're not sure, just see if the two puzzle pieces fit together. Rules for 4 different games are included.

Quotes & Verses (Q&V)  is fun! It can liven up your connect groups, prayer meetings and even social gatherings.

Let Q&V be the instrument to deepen friendships, old and new. Share your life experiences with Q&V and be able inspire, give hope, or just simply vent your burdens.

With Q&V, you’ll be surprised at what others could say about your situation.  Everyone’s perspective counts.

Q&V is a game about life, a tool for reaching out and touching others. It plays 2-7 players and about 10 minutes per player.

There are 3 types of cards in Q&V. Ideas, Quotes and Verses. During setup each player takes 3 Quotes and 4 verses.

The leader for the round will draw an Idea card and reflect on it. The rest of the group will then take turns using their Quotes and Verses cards to make a reaction to the Leader's sharing.

The Leader gives a point to the reaction they liked the most. The player next in line to the Leader starts the next round as the new leader. Play continues until everyone has taken their turn as leader. The one who has the most points win!
A simple roll and move game. Players must race (using a die) to Home (Square 67). There are 10 squares with special instructions that must be obeyed should your car land on one. First to Home, wins.

Game covers the Battle of Dogger Bank. It was a naval battle fought in the North Sea on 24 January 1915, during the First World War, between squadrons of the British Grand Fleet and the German High Seas Fleet.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Papilio is a card game that recreates the life of butterflies in a fun and didactic way. It combines the fun of an enjoyable game with the discovery of the world of butterflies and some of the most common species in the Mediterranean area.

The aim of the game, fun apart, is to build the richest habitat, and this is achieved by gaining the maximum number of points: completing butterflies, attracting them with plants, trying to make them breed, or catching them (with final penalisation). You also have to ensure that neither predators nor changes in the weather nor human intervention have a negative influence in the strategy of each player.

The game is designed for 2-5 players of all ages, and especially from 6 years old to an adult public. We have devised three levels of difficulty so that the learning process is gradual until dominating all the aspects of the game.

The game has a total of 66 cards, and 40 of them represent butterflies, 17 are actions by the players and 9 refer to the weather. The butterflies chosen represent more or less the variety of diurnal species in the area and the majority are grouped by colours, an approximation to the different families.

Papilio is a game of simulation yet it is also competitive. This means that the dynamic of the game is agile and with continuous toing and froing, and at the end establishes a score in points that in some way is related to the concept of biodiversity and the biological cycles of these insects.

Thus there are more points for the butterflies that are free than those caught with butterfly nets. The diversity of the butterflies is also rewarded and the number of species of the same group. Finally, points are scored for the combination of plants and reproduction cards (caterpillars). Moreover, given the vulnerability of these insects, the game includes three species of protected butterflies, rare or in danger of extinction, species that in the game cannot be caught with butterfly nets.

boardgame: If I'm Going Down...: Penelope Character Card
description:
Van Ryder Games gave this very special character card for If I'm Going Down to those who backed the "Tessen" Kickstarter campaign at the $12 level or above.

Penelope:
Recently reunited with her ex-lover Agent Zero in Livingston, Penelope was forced to explain her relations with Rigo. Now that he's dead, Penelope finds herself forced to examine if the feelings she once had for Agent Zero can be reignited...
Special Ability:
When making a Fate Check, Penelope may Trash a resource from her inventory to use the Fate Icon from that card.
Special Modifiers:
All weapons +1, but only if already bitten/scratched

Zombie side of card has a 3.

Description from the publisher:

After far too long, your favorite space-faring munchkins are BACK! This expansion includes Ships your characters can use to explore the cosmos; a new Class, Ambassador, that can commandeer Ships from other players; and a new Race, Veggie, because even munchkins need fiber. Plus plenty of new monsters, Traps, and Treasures. (Yes, of course there are more Lasers. PEW PEW!) Star Munchkin 3: Diplomatic Impunity ... in space, no one can hear you squee!

Star Munchkin 3 adds 112 cards to the game, and the first printing of this expansion includes a bonus card (as noted on the cover). Said bonus card will not be included in re-printings of this expansion.

Abaku is a numeric game for 1 to 4 players, who are lovers of numbers, figures, calculations and games! Everyone who knows that one and one gives two can play it. Playing Abaku significantly improves your calculating skills, logical thinking and intuition.

The players are using playing tiles to create numeric operations on the playing board. Addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, squares and cubes of whole numbers and square and cube roots of whole numbers are allowed. The player wins over the other players – calculators thanks to the proper combination of each tile’s value and strategic use of premium squares on the playing board. The aim is to get more points than the others.
Numeric operations are created by adding (horizontally or vertically) one or more tiles to tiles already lying on the board. Newly created numeric operation must contain or adjoin one or more tiles lying on the board. Numeric operations must be legible from left to right and from top to the bottom.

Abaku is an effective supporting instrument for teaching mathematics.

boardgame: El Dorado Canyon: More Ways to Die Expansion
description: This is the second expansion to El Dorado Canyon. It includes two unique characters and 4 unique town cards to add to the game to give it more variety and replay-ability.

This is the first expansion for El Dorado Canyon which includes the starting cards needed to play with 8 players and 2 unique character cards as well. This expansion will increase the number of people who can enjoy this game at the same time and because of the simultaneous nature of the game, it doesn't slow down with more players!

In each round of 10 Down, players are dealt ten cards. On their turn, they can swap one card at a time. The object is to create a sequenced run of cards from 1 to 10; that's a "10 Down" and you earn one point token! If you can create a color set, in order, of three or more cards (e.g., 7, 8, 9 yellow), you receive bonus tokens. The first player to collect ten points wins.

You may call your "10 Down" hand as soon as you get it, but you may want to press your luck and wait until you are able to build a color set of three or more cards to earn additional tokens. Keep in mind you never know when someone else is going to call. How long can you press your luck?

One player rolls two dice and choose two cards (from the deck of nine cards). First card along with one die determines first part of the ball flight. First card is always revealed so your opponent can see it. In the first part of the ball flight, only important thing is that the ball cross over the net on the opponent's side of court. On that point your opponent can try to intercept your shot with volley, it is part of his judgement. The second card that, in combination with second die, determines second part of the shot is concealed from your opponent. That card tells which kind of shot did you choose (long shot, dropshot ....). In this part, most important thing for defending player, is to "read opponent mind" to anticipate wich shot  will he/she choose to play.

MZM – Midnight Zombie Marathon

When Crazy Earl told us that Aliens were coming, we did not believe him. That did not stop him though. He put all his supplies into the sewer to protect them from tractor beams. Little did we know, zombies would soon infest the sewers and slowly take over your town.

Run, Dive and Dash your way past Zombies in the sewers. Find the hidden treasures of gas and food and barter your way out of town. If you are lucky enough, you might even find old Earl or his crash test dummy in your fun and crazy adventures.

This 1-4 player game is rated 13+ and comes with an easy to play rule book and personal deck with unique zombies to plague your adventures.

Single Player:

Game play revolves around running through the sewers to collect enough gas and food to barter with so you can get a ride out of town. Sounds easy right? We recommend you try to be careful because there is a limited amount of cards in the deck. The zombies will pop out of you, give you hugs and then go back into the deck. This creates a fun, creative and fun horde mechanic that is both simple and exciting. Can you get all you need before you have nothing left but zombies left in your deck?

Multiplayer:

There is only one seat in Earl's truck, and with so many people buying for it, it is up to you to out collect your friends.

While this setup is designed to work much like single player mechanic wise. The cards are no longer discarded, but go back into the deck to provide nonstop fun. With only ten rounds, you need to collect all you can before your friends do. How can you do this? Simple, bet your friends during their turn to try to wrestle their cards away from them and into your personal pile. Once all the players have a single card of their own, betting opens up and allows you to bet, roll and do whatever it takes to win. Do not worry... The zombie horde is still trying to hug its way to your brains, but can you outpace them and wave goodbye to your friends?

Follow us here at:

www.ArchNemesisGames.com

Only the strongest pimp can claim the coveted Pimp Island. Pimp Island is made up of four distinct districts known as Chinatown, Trailer Park, Chalupa Village and The Ghetto. All four districts contain three territories each. These territories, in addition to the central territory and four beach territories, make up the entirety of Pimp Island. America’s Next Top Pimp is a dangerous game of manipulation. Be wary of other pimps trying to take control of the island. The power of your hoes may not suffice. You must use the Police, Cardinals, Drugs and other manipulative measures if you want a chance at victory and complete control of Pimp Island!

America's Next Top Pimp is played over a few or several pimp rounds. During each pimp round, one or more pimp fights will occur. When all but one player has played their last card, new cards will be drawn and a new pimp round will begin. At the beginning of each fight, the player with the fight marker will place it on a territory of his/her choosing. This will be the location for the next fight.

Players begin each round with 10 cards each (8 in a 2 or 3 player game.)

During a fight, players take turns placing cards in front of them, forming their pimp hand. A player’s pimp hand shows the value he/she will bring to the fight. When all players finish placing cards, the fight is over. The player with the highest total value in his/her pimp hand controls the territory. A new territory is chosen and another fight takes place.

In a game of 2 or 3 players, the first player to control four territories that all touch each other or 5 total territories wins the game. In a game of 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 players, the first player to control an entire district, four territories that touch each other or 5 total territories wins the game.

Quiz game for 3+ players.

One player is chosen as quiz master, and will hand out 3-4 cards. He then pick a player to read and answer the questions on their card; the scoring is as follows:
2 point for a correct answer
1 point for a partial correct answer
0 point for a wrong or no answer

The winner is the one with most points.

The exact date is unknown, but the first editions was with pre-1948 spelling rules.

Welcome to the wild world of demolition derby!  In the arena, nobody is safe and even taking out your opponents means you may be helping others take you out at the same time.  To survive to the end you must learn to drive the line between aggressive and defensive driving and a little luck along the way doesn’t hurt either.

A 2-4 player take that style game in which you play cards to crash, bash and crush your opponents. If you can manage to survive to the end you'll find yourself crowned as the Demolition Derby King!

Game Contains:
4 Player Health Cards
8 Hit Cards
4 Defense Cards
4 Modifier Cards
4 Special Cards

Race your team around the forests and swamps, icy tundra and lakes, caves and mountains, in a quest to collect to collect precious gold.

Fear not. If your character dies, you can resurrect her at one of the infamous Towers of Power and gain new special abilities, accumulating priceless gems and be one step closer to victory.

STYLE: Mostly Competitive
IDEAL for 3 - 5 players.

In the Tak-Tyx format, most games utilize a shared map and players track their resources and abilities using the fingers on their actual hands. Tokens are not needed and all the rules required to play are printed on the flip-side of the map.

Your party must hike through a frozen wasteland, climbing mountains and crossing dangerous frozen lakes to track down the mysterious Snow Demon.

Collect precious Ice Crystals until you have enough to form the almighty Crystal Sword, then battle the Snow Demon atop a mighty Glacier.

This is a semi-cooperative game best with 3 - 5 players.

STYLE: Semi-Cooperative
IDEAL for 3 - 5 players.

In the Tak-Tyx format, most games utilize a shared map and players track their resources and abilities using the fingers on their actual hands. Tokens are not needed and all the rules required to play are printed on the flip-side of the map.

Travel through a mad dimension of fun and fear!

Players attempt to gain as many Fun Points as possible, while chased by the Clown Patrol, trapped in Endless Mazes and the frightening Hall of Mirrors!
Can you survive long enough before becoming Absolutely Terrified?

STYLE: Competitive
IDEAL for 3 - 5 players

In the Tak-Tyx format, most games utilize a shared map and  players track their resources and abilities using the fingers on their actual hands. Tokens are not needed and all the rules required to play are printed on the flip-side of the map.

Dwarves crawl through the dark caverns to drink 2000 Year Old-Ale, Mine for Copper and discover Weapons of Extraordinary Greatness.

Descend deep into the labyrinth to find the Mighty Lantern! Can your band of greedy heroes reach Level Six?

This is a semi-cooperative game best with 3 - 5 players.

In the Tak-Tyx format, most games utilize a shared map and  players track their resources and abilities using the fingers on their actual hands. Tokens are not needed and all the rules required to play are printed on the flip-side of the map.

ISB - The Indian School of Business - is a premier B-school (top 50 global schools as per Financial Times) and Atrium was designed to celebrate ISB's 10th anniversary and make players (re)live that experience.

To graduate from ISB requires 32 academic credits. However, there is more to ISB life than academics alone. The 1 year long flagship Post Graduate Program has parties, student clubs, networking opportunities etc. In Atrium, the objective is to get to 32 points through a combination of academic courses, clubs, parties, and life@ISB.

Based on die roll, players accumulate time which they can use to collect academic, personal, or networking hours. Hours are traded for courses, clubs, parties, and life@ISB cards in a race to be the first to get to 32 points

Those promo cards are special goodies to Cannes 2015.

The "Kaboom" card can be played only when a player must play a card. He indicates a player, and the bomb explodes immediatly.

The "Jump" card can be played only when a player must play a card. He chooses his right or left neighboor, who must play instead.

From the publisher's website (Translated from Dutch):
 Play with your favorite girl trio! The K3 board game is a real hit for kids that love K3. Try to collect as many K3 trios as you can by rolling the dice. Whoever collects the most K3 trios wins the game. Suited for kids ages 4 and up.

Star trails is a simple game for younger players. While playing children can learn about the solar system. The task to orbit the solar system with space shuttle and make photos of planets and asteroids.

Points are collected in two ways. The first way by reaching finish line (first gets 12 points, second 10, third 8 ...). Another way is to collect counters. Each counter brings 1 point. The winner is the player who ends up with the most points.

What do Backgammon, Go, and Chess all have in common? They are board games that have stood the test of time. Built on simplicity but with potential for complexity these games provide a challenge for players at all levels. Tao of Peg is designed to use a combination of luck and strategy to win in a variety of ways. Tao of Peg is the perfect game for family fun or for more serious gaming nights.

The board and pegs are made of wood, and the board design is printed on a vinyl sheet and applied directly to a wooden base. The entire board is lightly coated with a protective varnish. Felt buttons are then added to the bottom corners to protect playing surfaces.

Each player is dealt four cards. A player begins by discarding a card from their hand. The player then places a game piece in any space with the corresponding color on the board. The turns ends by the player drawing a new card from the pile.

In addition to the 100 colored playing cards (25 per colored quadrant), each set also has two types of wild cards. Players may play a wild card instead of a colored card. Red wild cards allow the player to remove any game piece in play and black wild cards allow the player to place a game piece anywhere on the board.

There are four different ways to win;

    make a row of 5 pegs anywhere on the board
    make a row of 4 pegs in a single quadrant
    place a peg in each corner of a single quadrant
    place a peg in each corner of the game board

This is a game for five players, but there are six figurines. Each player controls one figurine which represents one herbivore dinosaur. They move over one great valley in search for food and water. Winner is the player whose dinosaur eats most of the food (collects most counters). But, not everything is simple as it might seems so. More important thing of number of collected counters is their diversity!
The sixth figurine is tyrannosaurus. That figurine is moved by player when he/she finish move with his/her figurine. Tyrannosaurus is carnivore dinosaur, and if he gets him one juicy herbivore dinosaur, he eats it! (a player whose dinosaur is eaten is eliminatred)...this game is neverending race for various food and water counters, and running away from tyrannosaurus.

Each player has a 1 figurine - car. While driving through the streets players have to respect all traffic rules. In each round the player must take 2 counters, one type A and one type B. Counters are deployed throughout the city. In each subsequent round of the counters are in ever more distant streets. When 2 players are playing, 6 laps are driven. When 3 players are playing, 4 laps are driven. ... When 6 players are playing, 2 laps are driven.

With this game is very easy to learn all the important traffic rules.

Formula 1 is game for younger players. Each has one figurine. 5 laps are driven. Everyone has to stop in the pit stop twice. Once for changing tires, and the second time to refill fuel. It is important to find the shortest path.

Zmajarenje (Hang gliding) is game for younger players. Each has one figurine. From the top of a hill located on a shore of a bay, kites fly over to the other side of the bay. While flying, kites should to make as many photos of birds, ships, .... In real sport kites are trying to stay as long as possible in the air. In this game the most points gets the last player who reach the finish line. Each photo (counter) also brings one point. The winner is the player who at the end of the game has the most points.

boardgame: 1860: Locomotives of the Isle of Wight
description:
A set of 48 replacement trains for 1860: Railways on the Isle of Wight, representing the vast majority of trains which actually ran on the island.

The original game's numbered trains are replaced with trains in lettered bands, and the trains in each band come in variations based on the original trains. So the eariest trains are 2+0s instead of 2+1s, but they cost less. Some are better than the norm and cost more. Some rust early, some late, and there are some slightly weird historical twists in there too, The later trains (D - H) operate in a slightly different way from the originals. The number of trains actually used in the game will broadly be the same as in the standard game, with one or two possible exceptions.

Depending on how players want to handle things, players can choose which train in the current band they buy from the bank, or the game can be played so the trains are available in a strict order, causing possible angst or joy.

User summary:
萌札 (Moefuda) is a card game from Japan. In Moefuda, the players use cards (fuda) with 'Moe' elements, such as 'Tsuinte' (bunches or pigtails), 'Tsundere' (coldness turning to love) and 'Osana-najimi' (childhood friend). Gameplay resembles Apples To Apples as, while one player acts as a judge, the other players use their cards to form combinations from their hands. The players try to make the combo with the most 'Moe' (adorable cuteness) in the view of the judge. The winning combo gets the player a chip, most chips by the end wins.

A second version of the game 萌札女学院 (Moefuda Jogakuin) has a High School Girl theme and some changes in the voting method.

Publisher summary:
「ツインテ」「ツンデレ」「幼馴染」といった萌え要素が書かれた札を使い、子に選ばれた二人が配られた手札の中でできるだけ萌える組み合わせを考えます。審判役の親に選ばれた方がチップを獲得します。ゲームを繰り返し獲得したチップが最も多いプレイヤーの勝利となります。
designers: テイダ リョウ (Ryo Teida)
publishers: 哀愁旅館きたぐに (Aisyu Ryokan Kitaguni)

description:
User summary:
ゾンビツイスト (Zombie Twist) is a quick card game from the Japanese dojin circle 哀愁旅館きたぐに (Aisyu Ryokan Kitaguni). The game is published in an English / Japanese edition.

The players take turns to play cards from their hands to a central discard pile (as in the game UNO). The cards have instructions, such as "Make circular motions with your arms"  and so on. If a player is unable to play any card, they must continue to perform the action written on the top card on the discard pile, while continuing to play the game. The first player who empties their hand is the winner, and the last player holding cards is the loser.

Publisher summary:
「手を回す」など様々な行動が書かれた手札をウノのように場に出していきます。手札を場に出せなくなったプレイヤーは場に最初に出た札に書かれた動きをとりながらゲームを続けなければなりません。手札がなくなったプレイヤーから勝者となり、最後まで場に残ったプレイヤーが敗者となります。
designers: テイダ リョウ (Ryo Teida)
publishers: 哀愁旅館きたぐに (Aisyu Ryokan Kitaguni)

description:
User summary:
マネキング (Manekingu) is a Japanese game first published in 2014 in time for the Osaka Game Market. It was quickly re-issued in time for the Tokyo Game Market in Autumn 2014, with English included on the cards and rules, and named in English as The Mimic King.

Be quick to strike a pose and shout a sentence determined by a die-roll. The slowest person is out of the game. The last survivor will be honoured as The Mimic King!

A set of word cards and pose cards are formed in a circle, with each combo numbered from 1 to 6. After the dice are rolled, players race to identify any numbers that were not rolled, and must strike the correct poses, and shout the correct phrases. The slowest player is eliminated each round, until one player remains to be crowned Mimic King.

There is also a download pack for 'Period Drama' poses.

Publisher summary:
サイコロを振って、出目によって決まる「ポーズ」と「セリフ」を、いち早く決めよう。一番遅かった人が脱落。最後の一人「マネキング」まで生き残れるか？

We’ve all been there. Embarrassing slips of the tongue, wardrobe malfunctions, secret (and not-so-secret) workplace romances, accidental emails, bathroom mishaps, copy room shenanigans. Awkward Moment at Work is the party game that helps you play out those all-too-familiar moments — and show them who’s boss!

Each turn, players try to impress “the Decider” by submitting the best reaction to the awkward moment from their hands of cards.

Awkward Moment at Work is the adult version of Tiltfactor's award-winning card game Awkward Moment. While the game’s awkward workplace situations may be unfamiliar to kids and teens, the game is family friendly (contains nothing you wouldn’t see in a PG-13 movie).

A Steampunk Card Game of Adventure Across the Multiverse.

Imagine, beyond the bounds of your world that there are in fact several worlds, several Alternate Realities. Each more fantastic than the last all given life and connected by the pure power of thought, creativity, and terror harnessed in a mystical energy source called "Aether". These Realities exist tethered together in a tumultuous ever growing and ever changing Multiverse. While most worlds are blissfully ignorant many know of it's existence and even travel between the Realities seeking knowledge, riches or power.

Twisted Skies is a Steampunk card game of Adventure and Intrigue set in the fabulous adventures of the AIRSHIP ISABELLA. Players cooperate to complete daring missions at the start of the game but will double cross and challenge each other as they near victory. Can you master the Steampunk Multiverse?

Players take control of an elite airship. By manning and equipping the ship and crew, players take turns attempting to complete mission cards in an attempt to earn enough Victory Points to win.  Other players can try to Interrupt the current player from succeeding at a mission, or can form alliances to help each other.

Twisted Skies is played in a sequence of Phases that make up each player’s turn. Players take their turns one at a time proceeding clockwise around a table. The active player taking
their turn is known as the Mission Attempter The Sequence of play for the phases of a turn are as follows:

1. Ready all Exhausted Cards
2. Draw one Manifest Card
3. First Drop. Place one Crew card into play or Equip a Tech card
4. Open Trading. Players openly trade cards with the Mission Attempter
5. Draw one Catalyst Card. If it is a Mission Card place it on the Table.
6. Mission Attempt. The Mission Attempter tries to gain points by completing Missions.
7. Pirate Trading. Players blindly trade cards from their hands with the Mission Attempter
8. Second Drop. Place one Crew card into play or Equip a Tech card

Once a player has made the Second Drop (and resolved any effect of that Drop) then their turn is over.

The vast and powerful Red Fork Empire! Elder juggernaut of the Steampunk Multiverse! This special standalone edition of Twisted Skies features the thrilling, bizarre, and adventurous citizens of the Empire as they battle their ancient enemy The Dull with the power of Creativity! This set can be played as its own special set or it can be integrated with other sets and expansions of Twisted Skies to bring the wonder of the Red Fork Empire to your games of Twisted Skies!

Players take control of an elite airship. By manning and equipping the ship and crew, players take turns attempting to complete mission cards in an attempt to earn enough Victory Points to win.  Other players can try to Interrupt the current player from succeeding at a mission, or can form alliances to help each other.

Twisted Skies is played in a sequence of Phases that make up each player’s turn. Players take their turns one at a time proceeding clockwise around a table. The active player taking
their turn is known as the Mission Attempter The Sequence of play for the phases of a turn are as follows:

1. Ready all Exhausted Cards
2. Draw one Manifest Card
3. First Drop. Place one Crew card into play or Equip a Tech card
4. Open Trading. Players openly trade cards with the Mission Attempter
5. Draw one Catalyst Card. If it is a Mission Card place it on the Table.
6. Mission Attempt. The Mission Attempter tries to gain points by completing Missions.
7. Pirate Trading. Players blindly trade cards from their hands with the Mission Attempter
8. Second Drop. Place one Crew card into play or Equip a Tech card

Once a player has made the Second Drop ( and resolved any effect of that Drop) then their turn is over.

Colmar 1945 is a wargame that simulates operations of French Army against German Colmar pocket in 1945.

Scale:

    The map covers a part from Alsace 
    One game turn for two days
    One hex. for 5 km
    Units are battalions, regiment of tactical groups (Allied Combat Command or German Kampfgruppen)


Game content:

    One 42x29,7 cm map
    108 die-cut counters
    one rules booklet (in French), English version (pdf) available on Vae Victis Web site
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Game description from the publisher:

In order to reinforce the defenses for his base, Doktor Skarov calls upon a formidable gang: The Terror Trio! Come, if you dare!

Guardians' Chronicles: The Terror Trio, an expansion for Guardians' Chronicles, introduces a band of new supervillains who will cause even more trouble for the heroes of Liberty Patrol! This expansion also contains new tiles that add tough challenges to your games and allow you to set Doktor Skarov's base in the heart of a raging volcano or in the icy winter of a polar landscape.

In a distant kingdom, the royal palace is in turmoil.  After one of the king's tasters dies of poisoning, the king no longer knows who to trust within his closest circle of confidants.

There is the Queen, who he married to secure the relationship with the neighboring kingdom, but who does not share the love he has for her.  Their daughter, the Princess, next in line to inherent the crown and friends with enemies of the king.  The Bishop, who has spoken aloud that the king should be replaced by a spiritual leader.  The Knight, just back from war with the neighboring kingdom and in a rage that the war had ended through marriage and not bloodshed.   The Rook, who has become senile in his old age and now wandering the halls of the castle aimlessly.  And the Pawn, ready to rise up in the ranks of the royalty by any means necessary.

Can you help protect the king through the seasons of time and ensure that he remains atop his throne?  Or will the tyrants prevail and take his crown from you?

En Passant (from French: in passing) is a special move in chess that allows an opponent to capture another pawn "as it passes" which was the inspiration for this game.

In this 16-card playing card game each player is dealt four cards.  The player with the King card in their hand goes first.  Starting with that player, each player plays a card and performs the action listed.  For example, one card may have you steal another players card.  Or another card may have everyone pass their hands to the player on their left.

Your goal is to keep track of the King Card as he travels from player to player.  Once someone is only left with one card the round ends and the player with the King Card gets a point.  The first player to 3 points wins the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Game description from the publisher:

Fortunately for the citizens, the Liberty Patrol is not the only group of superheroes fighting the menaces that keep us vigilant. Here comes The Night Squad!

Guardians' Chronicles: Night Squad, an expansion for Guardians' Chronicles, sets the stage for a new group of heroes to take their turn in the pursuit of Doktor Skarov. With this expansion, you can control new characters with new powers and unique combat styles!

boardgame: Guardians' Chronicles: True King of Atlantis
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Under the oceans, the revolt is growing! Aquarion, son of the deposed dictator, is determined to regain his throne! Can you prevent the rein of a dictatorship?

Guardians' Chronicles: True King of Atlantis, an expansion for Guardians' Chronicles, introduces a new threat to fight: Aquarion and his elite troops. The tiles included in this expansion allow you to ally Aquarion with Doktor Skarov, or to plunge into the depths of Atlantis.

This card game is based on the game theory of the Prisoner's Dilemma. The theory explores why two purely "rational" individuals might not cooperate, even if it is in their best interest to do so.

In the prisoner's dilemma story, two prisoners are separated and have the option to confess to the crime or deny any involvement. If both prisoners confess, they will get 2 years. If one confesses and other denies involvement, the one who confesses will get 10 years. If they both deny involvement they both get 3 years.

When the story plays out, the prisoner's choose their self interest over the risk of possibly getting less jail time if they both confess.

Our card game uses that same idea but expands on it to allow for up to 5 prisoners and includes some added incentives to keep things interesting.

PLAYING

Each player draws an Incentive Card, looks at it and places it face down in front of them. Dice are rolled and placed on the Jail Card and Reward Card. Each player selects a Color Card from their hand and plays it face down in front of them. The Witness Color Card is turned over so that everyone can see the color. All players reveal their Color Card and Incentive Card at the same time.

Starting with the player color card who matches the Witness, any player who can play a Incentive Card decides how to use it: Alibi, Bribe, Double-Cross, Bully, Outsmart, Threaten.

Colors Cards are counted up by color. The player whose color has the most cards gets negative points matching the die on the jail card. The player whose color has the least cards gets positive points matching the die on the reward card. If there is a tie, all of the tying players each receive the negative points.

Points are recorded for each player and the round is over and everything starts over again.

First player to 21 points wins.

boardgame: Memory Game at the Friendly Farm
description:
A new king of memory game, educational and more fun to play than conventional games, sure to please the youngest to the oldest players! Two ways to play for twice as much fun: combine identical pairs of picture discs or trios of discs identified with pictograms.

With this two-for-one game, children will learn about the Friendly Farmer's daily activities and develop their visual memory while establishing a foundation for reading and learning a second language.

Note: Discs illustrating activities contain no text, giving children free rein to their imagination and creativity.

From the back of the box:

Jeu de mémoire.

Un jeu de mémoire nouveau genre, plus amusant et éducatif que les jeux traditionnels, qui plaira aux tout-petits et aux plus grands! Deux façons de jouer pour deux fois plus de plaisir: en jumelant des paires de pastilles identiques ou en associant des trios identifiés par des pictogrammes.

Shiv - a game of incarceration and blood - is a 3 player micro-game where players control prison gangs vying for influence by offing other in-mates.

Each player has a unique hand of 7 gang members that they will deploy face-down to one of three locations.  Players perform actions listed on their cards as well as locations as they attempt to bluff, maneuver, and shiv the opposition.

Intrigue and drama are the focus as player's target influential members of the opposition's gang and claw for power while limiting their exposure.

Social, dynamic, and completely devoted to theme there is no other micro-game like Shiv.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Intended for families who enjoy strategy-based games, PaNiC STaTioNS is a card game suitable for 2 to 4 players age 8 to 80.

The cards are first set out in a square to form the playing area. Some of the cards are kept back to ensure that it is more difficult to predict what you will land on. The cards are initially face-down and Tokens are placed on the cards.

The aim of the game is to collect 10 Tokens and then be the player who turns the last card face-up.

Each player has two Pawns which are moved around the cards according to the role of two dice. During the first phase of the game Tokens are collected when a player lands on the cards. The card is then turned face-up and any instructions followed. For example it could be a "Booby-Trap" card which forces your Pawn out of play. In which case you need to roll a 6 to bring it back in to play.

During the second phase of the game most of the Cards will have been turned-face up and most of the Tokens collected. It now becomes more important to keep both Pawns in play to give you some flexibility in choosing which cards you land on. You and your rivals will be trying to force Pawns out of play, trying to steal Tokens, and generally attempting to turn the last card face-up. The cards have various effects which assist (or hinder) you in this. Many of the cards interact with each other. For example if a rival's Pawn is on the "Philanthropy" card (which obliges him to give away 2 Tokens every time somebody else's Pawn goes out of play) it could be a good move to land on "Maelstrom" which forces all Pawns out of play. Most of the cards have effects that can be used to advantage in this way.
Each turn you also have the option of rolling the "Panic Die" which gives you other alternatives. You may choose to do this, for example, if your initial dice roll was particularly disadvantageous of if another player is about to win.
