You do however have the chance of losing half your Tokens - but even this can be turned to your advantage if you realise how.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Choose your favorite Empire and Starship and command it in a pitched battle with enemy ships. Exciting new combat system can be learned during your first battle. Battles usually last less than an hour. The combat system can be quickly learned, but can take a lifetime to master. The game readily adapts itself to tournament and campaign play, with or without a Game Master. Starships and data also provide ready-made "allies" or "bad guys" for existing Sci-Fi and Adventure Games. Additional D6 and D10 speed play.

On Location is a 110+ pages supplement for Crooked Dice's Spy-Fi miniature game that introduces rules for playing in 6 new environments: space, desert, underwater, artic, in the dark, airborne).

In addition to these, the supplement features new gadgets, casts, episodes, etc.
family: Series: action engine (Crooked Dice Game Design Studio)

description:
Materials

25 square tiles that are dark on one side and light on the other   
4 Othello disks, two of the disks are marked on their dark and light sides with a red dot at their centers.

Setup

Arrange the 25 tiles, light side up, into a 5x5 grid. Next place the two disks with the red dots light side up on two diagonally opposed corner tiles, do the same for the remaining two disks.

Play

One player plays with the two disks that have the red dots and the other player the two without.

Decide who goes first in any convenient manner, turns then alternate.

On a turn, you must,

1) Move one of your disks one space either diagonally or orthogonally onto an empty tile and then

2) Flip an empty light sided tile that is orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to the disk last moved or remove an empty dark sided tile that is orthogonally or diagonally adjacent to the disk last moved.
 Restrictions

- A light sided disk moves on empty light sided tiles and after a move you must flip an adjacent light sided tile to its dark side.

- A light sided disk may be moved to a dark sided tile by flipping the disk to its dark side and after the move you must remove an adjacent dark sided tile from the board. From this point on the disk is a dark sided disk and cannot be flipped back.

- A dark-sided disk moves only on empty dark sided tiles and after the move you must remove an adjacent dark sided tile from the board.

Object

The player last to move wins.
Choose your horse, roll the dice. A race to the finish line.

In Fliegende Zeilen, players have to create new texts (e.g. a poem or commercial slogan), based on lines from famous poetry.

First, the type of text is picked, than the quote for inspiration is determined randomly. In a limited timeframe, players compete to produce the wittiest text, awarding points to the creations of the others. Highest total wins.

Letter Chess is a solitaire word game that combines Word Search and Chess. Players move letters like Chess pieces to string together words that connect horizontally, vertically and diagonally on a 7-by-9 grid. To score big assemble long words and make sure you have a end of game strategy to clear the board.

Setup: 48 Letter tiles are randomized on a 7-by-9 grid. At the start of the game there are 15 empty squares near the center of the board. The center horizontal row is always empty. Bordering the center row, two rows are made up of four empty squares and three letter squares (like a parapet which is the alternate name). Use any word game you may own to play the game.

Game Mechanics: The main object of the game is to assemble words on the grid. Eligible words must have letters connected on the board horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. Acceptable words must be at least three letters long. Words cannot be played more than once per game and proper nouns are not allowed. Individual letter tiles cannot be used twice in the same word (such as “o” twice to spell book or “d” to spell dad).

During a single turn, a player can move multiple letters together to construct words. All letters moved during a turn must be used to construct a word that round. Consonants can move any number of squares horizontally, vertically or diagonally (like a Queen in Chess) to an empty square. When moved, however, consonants cannot jump over other letters. Vowels can move two squares horizontally and one square vertically, or two squares vertically and one square horizontally (like a Knight in Chess) to an empty square. Unlike consonants, vowels can jump over other letters. After a word is constructed those letter tiles are removed from the board and play continues. The game ends when all letters have been used or no more plays are left. Lastly, the letter “Y” moves as a consonant even if used as a vowel.

Scoring: Letter Chess uses simple formulas to tally a score at the end of the game. So when playing keep a pencil and paper nearby. Vowels count as 2 points and Consonants count as 1 point. The letter “Y” counts as one point even if used as a vowel. A word is tallied by multiplying the letter score by the letter count. For example, “murals” has a tally of forty because there is a letter score of eight (two vowels and four consonants) and a letter count of five (8x5=40). There is a bonus score when all the letters are cleared from the board. The bonus is calculated by multiplying the number of rounds by 10 and subtracting that amount from 200 (arbitrary number). For example, a whole board was cleared in twelve rounds then 200-(12X10)= 80 points. When letters remain on the board with no more moves left those letters are tallied as a word and subtracted from the score. For example if the letters “I, X and O” remain on the board. The letter score is 5 and letter count is 3 which equals 15 minus points.

At most there are 16 rounds per game assuming all words are 3-letters long. In Letter Chess, a high score is in the 400’s and a low score is around 200.

Customer Clues is a re-theme of Clue distributed to U.S. Bank Employees as a training tool.  It plays nearly the same as Clue, but adds a deck of Mystery Product Cards.  These cards have questions about bank products, sales techniques, etc. that must be answered when players land on certain marked spaces on the board.

Like Clue, players move from room to room making guesses as to the cards in the mystery envelope, but in this rethemed version they move around a bank branch, and must deduce which product was sold to which customer in which room.  As mentioned above, as players move around the board, they may land on spaces which will force them to answer questions about bank accounts, policies and procedures.

Description from the Box:
Be the best at word building! Stack your Spelligator with as many words as you can to win! Challenge your friends to see who can build the highest stack of words!


Spelligator is a children's game designed to teach simple vocabulary, spelling and phonics.  Each player has a plastic "Spelligator" with three shaped spaces on its back.  Players then take turns turning over letter tiles, each of which can fit only in the beginning, middle or end space.  Each tile is printed with either a single letter, a blend or a digraph.  If the player can add the tile to his or her Spelligator's back to make a new word, it is added.  If not, the tile is turned back face down.  When all the tiles are used or no more words can be made, the player with the highest stack on his or her Spelligator's back wins.

Find the Rhyme is an educational matching game for young children, designed to teach the concept of rhyme.  The game consists of 20 pairs of rhyming picture cards, each of which is cut like a puzzle piece on one edge, so that each card only fits with its designated pair, and when fit together will form a rhyming phrase (e.g. "a dragon in a wagon").  Anywhere from six to twenty left-side cards are laid out in a line.  Each player takes a turn choosing a right-side card and trying to match it with one left-side card to make a rhyme.  If the match is correct, the player keeps the pair.  If not, the tile is returned.  Play continues until all pairs are matched and the player with the most pairs wins.

boardgame: Agricola: Chłopi i ich zwierzyniec – Jeszcze więcej budynków do zwierzyńca
description: A collection of two expansions for Agricola: Chłopi i ich zwierzyniec released in one box - More Buildings Big and Small and Even More Buildings Big and Small. These cannot be bought separately in a Polish translation.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Mouse-O-Mania is a roll-and-move game included as a pull-out insert in the children's book "Geronimo Stilton: A Christmas Tale."  Players must roll dice and move along a track, trying to get Geronimo Stilton to his Christmas party. Along the way, players land on spaces referring to events in the book. These spaces may move them forward or back or cause them to gain or lose turns. The first player to get Geronimo Stilton to his Christmas party by landing on the end of the track by an exact roll wins.

Build a lemonade stand and let it flourish or flounder with Lemonade Stand Board Game. 2 to 6 players can learn the basics of business by caring for their own virtual lemonade stand. Kids can purchase their own ingredients, set their own prices, and choose a location for their business. As they move across the board, they'll face challenges like weather, advertising, and customer experience. A fun game for the whole family. Includes instructions, dice, cards, and boards. Ages 7 and up.

All players begin the game by building their virtual Lemonade Stand and buying ingredients to make lemonade to sell
As players move around the board they learn about the many factors in running a business
Learning that it's all about location, advertising, weather, selling price & customer experience
Fun for kids and parents

The Kenya Safari Game is a roll-and-move game that simulates a traveler exploring the sights and tourist attractions of Kenya.  Players start from a pre-chosen base in the capital city of Nairobi.  They draw a variety of different-colored cards, each of which represents a different type of site or tourist attraction they must visit or an animal they must photograph on safari.  They then roll two dice each turn to move around the board by a roads, trails, trains and airplanes to visit the required locations.  The first player to complete all their photography assignments and see all their designated sights returns to his or her Nairobi base and is declared the winner.  Special rules allow players to take flights or extra turns when rolling doubles.

Although this is primarily a roll and move game an element of strategy is introduced by the fact that players must visit a variety of sites in a variety of areas and may travel by various routes.  Players who plan the most efficient routes from place to place may gain an advantage.
publishers: The East African Wild Life Society

description:
The goal is to be the first player to get all his ten stones to the opposite side of the board. One player moves his stones from to left to right, the other from bottom to top.

Two dice are thrown and the sum of both may be used for one dice or the separate numbers for two. When you end your move on top of your opponent's stone, that stone is moved back to its starting position.

Uprising is a game for two players that depicts, in a very abstract way, the growth and development (or containment and suffocation) of a popular movement in revolt against an oppressive government. The Rebel player, who starts the game with little apparent power but a great deal of information, is in conflict with the Government player, who has power (with his assortment of Agents) but very little information on the Rebel’s resources and capabilities.

The pieces represent small groups (Insurgents and Agents), support organizations (Sympathizers), and nonexistent things or misleading information (Dummies).

The map (a field of 91 hexes) is perhaps the most abstracted part of the design, in that it does not represent physical distance, but something more like organizational proximity. With the Rebel pieces starting on the outer perimeter, and the Agents starting in a tight group around the Nexus, it can be displayed how far the revolt must go and how much larger it must grow in order to challenge the government for ultimate power. However, being out in the margins allows time to organize and gather strength....

This game depicts, in a very abstract way, the essence of insurgent warfare. The game is for two players, the State (representing the existing government or leadership of a country or organization) and the Insurgent (representing the opposition to that government or leadership). The State has powerful forces but little information; the Insurgent has complete information but little actual power. Whichever side overcomes its disadvantage wins (the State by gaining information on the Insurgents and killing them; the Insurgents by penetrating to the centre of State power).

The Insurgent wins by capturing the Capital. This is accomplished by having, at the end of any his player-turns, Insurgent pieces occupying either four connected spaces in the Inner Circle (that is, there are at least four Insurgent-occupied spaces in the Inner Circle that also share a border with one or two other Insurgent-occupied spaces in the Inner Circle), or occupying any six of the twelve spaces of the Inner Circle.

The State player wins by killing 12 Insurgent pieces (so making an Insurgent victory impossible).

Originally designed in 2011 for an online game site, from an idea by Michael Freeman.

From the publisher:

The north wind blows in from the rugged domains north of Conan's homeland. With Asgard to the east and Vanaheim to the west, these two territories compose the frozen tundra that is Nordheim. The men of these lands are toughest of the tough, hardened by cold and battle. Blood spilling across the pristine snow as different tribes fight for supremacy.

Despite the uneasy relationship between the Cimmerians and the Aesirs, Conan earns their respect through strength of arms and embarks on more than one adventure across these glistening lands.

Join Conan and his companions as they cross swords with the Vanirs, pursue the Frost-Giant's daughter, do battle with a necromancer defiling the burial grounds of wars past and more in the Third expansion for Conan.

Featuring scenarios written by Croc, author of Claustrophobia and many roleplaying games, and his son Sippik!

This set contains:


     Niord, Aesir Hero
     5 Aesir warrior allies
     10 Vanir warrior Minions
     Atali, the daughter of Ymir, the frost-giant, Leader
     Atali's Brothers, 2 frost giant monsters
     5 Monstrous Crow minions
     1 double sided game board (Frozen battlefield and deep winter forest)
     4 scenarios written by Croc and Sippik
     Token, tiles and Character sheets.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: A variation of Fan Tan. 54 cards; 1-9 in 5 colours / 3 'joker cards' / 6 'give or take 2 cards'. On a turn, a player either plays a card or draws a card. Played cards are placed on the table next to previously placed cards. The new card has to be of an immediately higher or lower value and of the same suit, or it has to be of the same value and from another suit. Players can only play 1 card per turn. A joker card can be played at any time and at any place while a 'give or take 2 card' (used to have another player take 1 or 2 extra cards or to remove 1 or 2 cards from the table) can be played at any time except as a player's last card. When a 'give or take 2 card' is played it goes back into the stack of cards and the stack is shuffled again. The first player to have no card left wins.

Players can learn and test spatial perception. A map is seen from above. Players play simultaneously. A 'view card' is shown and all players no try to determine as quickly as possible from which area of the map this 'view card' could be seen from. The first player to grab the pawn and place it on a spot can check the back of the 'view card' to see if it was correct. If the player was right he/she can keep the 'view card'. The player with the most 'view cards' after all 48 cards have been collected wins.

From the box: Players duel to the finish while the Battle Marker determines if high or low card wins each battle. Special Action Cards add new challenges to game play so that no two games are ever the same!

TacDex: Super Mario uses gameplay similar to well-known card game War, with players revealing cards simultaneously to try to claim cards owned by the opponent, but a battle marker determines whether a high or low card wins a particular battle and players each have special action cards to add new twists to the conflict.
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
Plant. Grow. Harvest. Sell. Farmers Market is a game themed around supplying customers with what they desire at the local farmers market. During the game, players will plant corn, wheat grain, potatoes seeds, wait for them to grow so they can harvest them. Milk can be drawn from Cows, and the rare chicken can also be found annoying the players if they don't supply a customer who needs a chicken/ cow their eggs/milk. After the goods are ready, they can be supplied to the Idaho Farmers Market for consumption.

Other options in the game include advancing the technology to get a greater harvest, planting long-growing watermelons (the hit of the day).

Deck manipulation is the main mechanism in farmers market. Players each have their own deck of staple cards. Unlike a normal deck builder, during the game, a few cards will be added to their deck (plant) that will be removed next time they are drawn. Other cards may be added to your deck to help out the harvesting of crops, or change cows or chickens to milk, eggs, or meat, if the patrons request it.
On a player's turn,  they draw and play all 5 cards,  keeping one card from turn to turn if storing it. 
The game ends when all the patrons have showed up and asked for their wants.

A solo game exists that has a timer of rounds, in the way of patron cards orders that must filled before you run out of time.

The farmers market 15 pages of cards need to be printed once per player. 
The backside picture may be printed on the back of the harvest cards, not the patron cards.
A.I art from night Cafe has been used for the playtest pnp files.

boardgame: Escape: Zombie City – Expansion 1: The Survivor Chronicles
description:
Escape: Zombie City – Expansion 1 – The Survivor Chronicles is the first expansion for Escape: Zombie City, and it adds two unique modules that you can use separately or together to bring your games in entirely new directions.

The first module in this expansion adds new challenge tiles to the city. Before you can escape, you’ll need to solve whatever challenges you encounter within the city. You may need to confront a large horde of zombies, escort your elderly grandfather from the retirement home to the safety of the church, or even clear a barricade blocking your path. 
Alternatively, you may add unique characters to the game with the second module included in The Survivor Chronicles. At the beginning of the game, each player receives a character, ranging from the grandma to the policeman. Each character has two unique powers, and you choose one of these powers to use throughout the game, giving you a special edge in your fight against the undead.

Module 1: Tasks
Complete the task before escaping the city

     rock concert: defeat a zombie horde
     retirement home: escort grandpa to the church
     barricade: clear the road
     workshop: drive the van to the workshop and fix it
     the pit: consume the energy drink and the pill bottle and fix the hole in the road
     circus: all players visit the circus and draw a card


Module 2: Characters
Each of the 6 characters gives you a special ability that you can use as often as possible.

    the tough policeman
    the clever nerd
    the fearless grandma 
    the shopping queen
    the vendor
    the construction worker


The Survivor Chronicles contains:
Rules
Six challenge tiles
Seven characters with bases
Four challenge tokens
Twelve Character cards

If you are not familiar with the Bible Master card game, it is an easy to learn card game designed to challenge your knowledge of Scripture.

Players win verse cards by filling in the blanks with word cards like the ones below. Action cards keep the game challenging with wild cards, skips and more! The winner is the player with the most verse cards at the end of the game.

Our first version “Salvation” includes Bible verses focused on the Gospel. As your family plays, you are reinforcing Scripture memory in a fun yet challenging way.

There are 12 verse cards, 14 action cards and 28 word cards in the 54 card deck.

Why the Salvation theme?

1 Peter 3:15 says, "But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and be ready always to give an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the hope that is in you with meekness and fear:"

Through testing the game play I learned that there are some Christians who have been Christians for years that don't know some of the foundational  scriptures used in this game.

My goal is to help players of all ages be ready to share the hope of the Gospel by helping them memorize scriptures that will allow them to share their faith with confidence.

The initial production run will be in the King James version. Though others may be available in the future.

boardgame: The Most Princess in the World
description:
User summary:

The players are all princesses wanting to become the most princess princess in the world. They must collect accessories, dresses and education so that they will gain fame from being rescued by heroes when the demon lords attack. They also need to watch out for weight-gaining food and tea parties, and be careful not to nag too much.

The game centers around making "negative" bids for the available cards for the current round. Players bet "nagging points" to gain the card they want; however these nagging points are negative VP.

When someone gains a demon lord card, all players are captured and the game is interrupted by a hero rescue auction, in which the players bid for what hero to be rescued by. Depending on the hero and the player's stats, players will gain fame by being rescued. When three demon lords have been defeated, the game ends and the player with the most points wins.

Channeling is a game of tactical spellcasting. Each match represents a battle between channeling wizards - be it a schoolyard quarrel at the academy, the real stakes struggle of a magical war or a grand clash on the very edges of reality.

The players draw cards from a standard decks full of basic spells, but each sorcery cast and summoned magical entity becomes unique combination between its original form and the channeling abilities of the caster.

There are different modes of play - in the most basic one that's recommended for learning, each player may cast trough the four basic elemental channels (Flame, Rock, Frost and Lightning) before dabbling in asymmetrical duels where the same elements are randomly drawn and duels utilizing advanced channels and player powers.

Heroes of Normandie: Gazette #1 is the first issue of a quarterly gazette that contains new scenarios, rules, and punchboards with new units, surprises, and other items. Gazette #1 has four pages of text with special rules for mines and minesweeping items and two "Explosives" scenarios as well as one "Mines and Minesweepers" punchboard that features four cannons (1 GE, 2 US, 1 UK).

The scenarios in this Gazette require the D-Day and UK Army Box expansions.
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
From the instructions:
"Vowel Bingo is a fun-filled way to strengthen and reinforce vowel skills. Each playing card contains eight labeled illustrations of objects having long or short vowel sounds in their names. The purpose of game play is to match each object's game with its correct vowel."

Vowel Bingo is similar to standard bingo, but in order to place their markers, players must know and match short and long vowel sounds. Each player has a card with as series of pictures labeled with the corresponding words. The caller calls out a vowel sound--long a, for example--and players must search their card for a word/picture with a long a in the word. When a player has a complete row, their card is checked by the caller to make sure the vowels are identified correctly. If so, the player wins. If not, play continues until someone has a correct row.

The struggle between movie studios escalates! Lawyers poach actors, while producers offer key roles to their own relatives. This lowers overall profits, of course. All hope is for the new professionals. Gaffers and singers, choreographers and PR specialists will help you to earn the missing millions, by winning the festivals in Moscow, Cannes and Venice!

This expansion adds more cards to Hollywood. You need Hollywood to play this expansion.

BACKGROUND: The Falklands Ablaze is a solitaire semi-historical simulation of the Falklands War (May – June 1982). The Game begins shortly after the Argentinians capture the Port City of Stanley after a surprise attack. The British have mustered their Task Forces and have arrived just of the northern coast of the Falkland Islands. The British must now establish a Beach Head and advance their forces towards Port Stanley to defeat the Argentinian Forces and liberate the British Settlers.

OBJECTIVE: As the Commander of the British Naval and Land Forces, you must gather your forces and naval squadrons to capture Port Stanley, Port Stanley Airfield and 10 other Argentine controlled Settlements before the Campaign Deck is depleted.

GAME FEATURES: The Falklands Ablaze contains many colorful counters including ground forces, ships and aircraft. The Game includes rules for Ground, Sea and Air Combat.

The Falklands Ablaze can be found at the following URL:
http://www.berserkergames.net

The first pair of expansions from AoS Team 2015.

The set contains 2 new maps that takes place in Fukushima and Chernobyl.

The main twist is that hexes are worth money, but they will be destroyed in the mid game.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Human Body/Synapses
description:
The second pair of the AoS Team 2015 releases.

When the first map takes place in the human body with two cycles of O₂ and CO₂; the organs are becoming blue or red, changing the players' strategies.

The second map takes place in the brain with synapses.

ONLY for experienced players!

Wallachia 20 features two complete games covering a pair of important major battles fought during the Russo-Turkish War of 1806 – 1812 for control of the key Danube river port of Rousse in the province of Wallachia.

Rousse 20 simulates the earlier of these two engagements, fought on 22 June, 1811. In this battle Ottoman forces advanced northwards, towards the present day Bulgarian-Romanian frontier. Russian infantry and cannons were able to stall the polyglot Ottoman army, and although they managed to eke out a marginal victory their commander, General Kutuzov, chose to yield the key town of Rousse to the Ottomans, convincing them they had won a great victory. The Czar was not pleased.

Slobozia 20 focuses on the latter battle of 2 October 1811. In this engagement Kutuzov conducted a surprise march which cut off and surrounded the bulk of the Ottoman army, trapping it on the northern (Bulgarian) shore of the Danube. The result was a decisive Russian victory that permitted negotiations to end the war. This had the dual advantage of allowing Russia to transfer key reserves to oppose Napoleon’s invasion of Russia in 1812, and also prevented Austria from contributing more forces to support the French invasion.

The Gallows are ready!  Mages accused of wickedness are prepared to meet their end.  It's high noon and yet the sky darkens as if it too knows what the future brings.  Today, all of mages on the gallows will face their final fate.  One by one the wicked will be executed until only one remains...

...and you want that one remaining to be your client.  That's right you're a lawyer and the mage you are protecting is a secret.  Your job is to protect your mage by making sure all the other mages on the gallows are executed first.  Of course, the other lawyers are also secretly protecting one of the mages.

In The Gallows each player is secretly defending one of the characters in the center.  On your turn you add one vote token to any character and then move one vote token from a character to a different character.  Once a character has enough votes they are executed.  Whoever had the most votes on a character when they are executed scores the conviction worth one point.  When only one character remains in the middle, whoever was protecting them scores an acquittal worth lots of points.

Play consists of players trying to manipulate the votes so they score convictions and prevent their own defendant from getting convicted.

The boardgames sets in the Small City trilogy and expands the concepts of the game in the future.

Arcology

The 21st century…The quest for Happiness has become the ultimate holy grail for the entire population world. Thanks to your work at the town hall in the Clinic, Tramways, Card City, Town Center and Small City games, your town is growing rapidly and you are attracting new Residents almost every day. You are now in charge of a new challenge: to arrange the towers of downtown Small City so that your dear citizens are as happy as possible and get their dose of happiness too!
Succeed, in the same tower, in coexisting apartments, Commerces, and offices on several floors. Develop green spaces, relaxation and Recreation areas for the sake of harmony! But the most important thing, in such a tall tower, will remain the planning and management of the elevators so that your residents do not lose patience to get home at rush hour!

As in Yatzee you throw five dice. You can throw each die 3 times and you score by getting certain combinations.

In Star Wars Yatzy you don´t have a fixed set of combinations, but instead you draw mission cards. Each player have two mission cards and they draw a new if they have less than 2 cards. you dicard a card if you finish a mission. If you don´t use all your throws you can add one mission card.

When one player have finished 8 missions the game ends and the player with most points wins.

You and your humble lab partner are competing in the 197th Creature Faire. To win this competition, you must rob graves and hospitals for body parts and organs to make your creation. But, don’t get to greedy - collecting extra body parts and organs will make the townsfolk angrier. If they get too angry they will storm the castle. Beware of your fellow competitors also as they can steal from you and anger your mob by spreading vicious rumors.

Be the first to complete your creature and bring it to life with electricity, proclaiming… It’s Alive!

A bowling game where you spin a gyro on a sloping field against the pins

WyrmRider is a 2 player microgame in the one page "postcard" style

Setup: One player places the 3 wyrms anywhere on the 6 spaces on their side of the board. The other player places the 4 riders anywhere on the six spaces on their side. Players may orient the counters in any direction.

Play: Beginning with the wyrm player, each player takes a turn by rotating, moving and combatting.

Rotating - A player may rotate their counter 60° clockwise or counterclockwise.

Moving - The player may then move any counter 1 hex in distance in any direction, maintaining the counter’s current orientation. If the player chose not to rotate a counter they may instead move a counter 2 hexes in distance.

Combatting - After rotating, and/or moving, a player may attack one adjacent piece of the opposing player with any of their counters. A player may only attack in the directions indicated by the red arrows (arrows must point perpendicular to an adjacent hex side). Players each roll one six-sided die, the attacking player gains a +1 to their roll.

If the attacker’s roll is greater than the defender, the defending piece is removed.

If the defender has more than one counter adjacent to the attacking counter and they have lost combat, they may immediately combat once as a new attacker, before the start of their normal turn. The +1 rule applies.

Win: A player wins by moving one of their counters on to the 
opposing player’s home space.

boardgame: Star Trek: The Next Generation – The Final Frontier
description:
Star Trek The Next Generation The Final Frontier Action Game.

The Ferengi are up to their capitalist ways again. They have overrun the engineering room and are going after the energy matter/anti-matter reaction chamber. They are trying to steal the dilithium crystals to sell on the open market. These crystals are the energy system on the U.S.S. Enterprise. They would secure at least 5000 bars of gold pressed latinum for the Ferengi, and would turn the Starship Enterprise incapable of warp travel. Your mission is to defend the engineering room against the Ferengi and not let them get away with any of the crystals. Your mission is clear...Engage!

PLAY SUMMARY:

Get your scoops ready to catch the Ferengi as they leave the five Jefferies tube openings, as the phasers are pulled from the matter/anti-matter reaction chamber. Catch a Ferengi and get a piece of your Starship Enterprise. Catch the dilithium crystal by mistake and you loos a piece of your U.S.S. Enterprise. Be first to complete your scoring disk and you win.

A combination of War and Slapjack. Players place cards on the table, then duke it our War-style when a challenge card comes up, or try to slap the pile when a Smack It card comes up. Whoever has the highest card for a challenge, or is the first to slap the pile on a Smack It, gets the pile. Players who run out of cards are out of the game. As a twist, eliminated players may slap card piles to remove cards from play completely.

Description from the publisher:

Steam: Map Expansion #5 delivers a way to store your precious cargo in one place, with this box coming with two maps in its hold: India (3-5 players) and Southeastern U.S. (2-6 players). In addition, it comes packed with a variety of mini-expansions that can be played with your other Steam maps. New Action Tiles — The Surveyor, The Midnight Express, The Local Payoff, and Corporate Charity — can be added to your games to change the dynamic of previous maps, while the Gray Market Goods expansion adds a level of mystery to your goods supply and creates dual goods.

Steam: Map Expansion #5 includes the previous mini-expansions that were found only as promos: Demanding Suppliers, City Growth Special Contracts, and the Five Way Town as well as more track tiles, new complex hard curve tracks, and a Universal Display board to be placed beside your expansion maps rather than having to play your expansion on top of the original board.

Finally, in addition to a box that's designed to hold all other Steam expansions, Steam: Map Expansion #5 includes an updated omnibus of all the previous expansions' rules with more in-depth examples.

Settle the wilds of Georgia in our latest Catan Geographies expansion: Catan Geographies: Georgia! These alternate maps provide alternate rules and serve to spice up your Catan adventures!

Base set contains 54 unique Cards, Token Punchboard, the game's Board and Rulebook. The Extended version contains 84 unique cards, as opposed to 54 cards in the regular set.

Gomora : Downtown is a Neo-noir card game for 2-4 players. It can be played 1vs1, 2vs2 (co-op mode) or Free for all. The game is for ages 15+ and it lasts 15-45 minutes.

You are a detective in the city of Gomora. You will take a path between good and evil in a corrupt city full of eccentric and controversial characters. Nothing can be taken for granted. Your goal is to try and solve perplexing crimes that implicate multiple powerful interests. Gomora's criminal underbelly is after you....so hurry up detective.

This game consists of 84 cards each with a unique photo-realistic artwork. There are 4 tokens representing the 8 Detectives and a game board with 4 locations to move around in order to find clues and complete your case.

When your turn starts you draw a card or you store cards under your case. Then you have to move to a different location and play it's ability if you wish to. The third step allows you to play your cards. Alternatively you can rest during the third step so that you recover one life point. After you are done playing cards, make sure to keep only 4 cards in your hand and you turn is over.

description:
Two fleets clash in a fast paced and exciting game of naval and air combat for two players - or up to four players if two sets of the game are used, Each player tries to find his opponent's ships, hidden within sea cards - and sink them before his opponent do the same.

The game is played with two sets of 'sea' cards arrayed in 5 stacks of 7 cards each - a total of 35 sea cards for each player/fleet, in those cards the players must hide 6 ships: Battleship, Cruiser, Aircraft carrier, two destroyers, submarine and two Mines cards, the ships and mines can be placed in any order and possition within those 5 stacks.

During the game the players use a selection of three types of action cards, attack cards (red) Recon cards (blue) and Fleet cards (green), at each round players start with six cards on their hand, their composition (how many cards from each type they will have) is given by the 'formation' their fleet is following, for example: the recon formation will give more recon cards to a player than attack and fleet cards while a more aggresive formation (Full attack) will give to the player more attack cards than recon and fleet.

While the players only have six action cards on their hands at the start of each round discarding any that they use during the round and drawing new ones to replenish their hand (according to the formation they are in) they also have the 'Hangar deck' space, where they may land aircraft cards (some cards within the action decks are 'Aircraft cards') the hangar deck can have up to 6 aircraft cards and those cards can be used to attack and or to defend the fleet from attacking aircraft, a land/resupply system of not-ready / ready aircraft in the game simulates the whole process of landing aircraft supplying them and taking them off to accomplish missions.
   
Game Contents:
70 sea cards.
63 attack cards.
63 recon cards.
63 fleet cards.
lots of damage counters.
2 formation dials
2 formation pointers
2 hangar space decks.
Rulebook
 game was ranked 6th in the Epitrapezio contest (Greece 2015)

Sub Runners is a historical-fiction Pacific submarine adventure.

STORY

In 1942, As VJ day looms near, Maximillion Erhardt directs a secret submarine full of Chinese gold to secret locations to secure retirement gold for Nazi SS. Gary Shaft, a captain in the American Navy, is on the hunt for the submarine and, most importantly, the gold. Meanwhile, Hajime Yamamoto stockpiles ammunitions and weapons to mine the incoming American Fleet.

A generation later...

America Shaft looks on as her son Kirk dives deeper than he has ever swam before checking on a local tip about the Nazi Gold. The modern spy and government infiltrator, Olli Valentiner, manipulates the local governments, listing the Shafts as enemies of state. In other news, a string of murders has occurred across Asia as the twins, Hero and Kira Yamamoto leave their mark on the traitors who lost their family’s wealth.
 GOALS

Sub Runners is a card-driven deduction game for three players. Players take on the roles of three families: The Erdharts, The Shafts, or The Yamamotos. Players gather resources across the world trying to complete their initial goals. The Erhardts: to make three gold deposits, The Yamamotos: to sink the search Corvette, and The Shafts: to sink the submarine. Once the submarine has been mined, Heinrich dies, or the submarine completes three drops, it crashes somewhere in the vast ocean.

The final goals now come to bear. The Shafts must find the crash in the ocean and then recover the gold. The Yamamotos may recover the gold or kill all the other players. The Erhardts may kill the other players or defend the crash site until one of the play decks has been exhausted to win.

Black Hand is a bluffing and deduction microgame for 3 to 6 players. In it, players take on the role of members of the Black Hand, the secret society responsible for the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand and the beginning of World War I. The  aim  is  to  correctly  guess  the  correct combination of assassination weapons (pistols, knives, bombs, and poison) required to kill the Archduke. There’s a hitch in the plans, however—there are traitors hidden within the Black Hand, who work for the Crown and are looking to uncover the assassination plot! Players are secretly divided into  those  loyal  to  the  cause  and traitors who seek to thwart the plot to assassinate the Archduke. Players have to determine who they can trust and who is hiding an ulterior motive!

This game was designed to be entered into the 2015 BGG & Geekway to the West Microgame Competition. It is played with 46 mini-cards (each mini-card is half the size of a poker-size playing card).

Pitch'n Goal is a flicking sports simulation along the lines of Pitch'n Dunk, Pitch Bowl, or Caveman Curling. Just as those games simulate their respective sports, this one simulates soccer (known as football everywhere but the USA). Discs represent the players and the ball. The game sports variants for 3–4 participants and star players with special abilities. The first team to score 2 goals wins the game.

The board for The Siege  features a 3D castle. The castle is easy to assemble and fits snugly back into the box without the need to dismantle it. The game is asymmetric – one player (or a team of up to three) attacks the castle, and the other player (or team) is defending it. Knight models come in six colours. All knights are armed with either swords or maces. The blue ones always attack the castle, and are the only team to have two catapults. The red ones always defend. Four of the red knights have bows and arrows instead of melee weapons. The remaining four teams are black, orange, yellow and green. The number of knights in different teams is not always the same. There are always more defenders than attackers. Combat is conducted in duels. Mace wielding combatants have the upper hand, but the outcome is decided by rolling the dice. Each player is trying to overpower their opponents by outnumbering them in isolated combats. Apart from the knights, the box also includes a king, a queen, a princess, a wizard, a dragon and a squire. These models are used in special scenarios.

boardgame: Anything To Declare?: The International Game of Smuggling and Detection
description:
You are the owner of a shipping line required to unload containers in a port, and move around the board collecting cargoes for delivery to a warehouse.  As well as this, you must control movements of your lorry and also use your own customs security piece to follow and challenge containers carried by other players.

The bluff element in the game centres around decisions on whether to pay premiums to insure cargoes moving through the docks, and once at the lorry park you must decide whether to declare goods for payment or not.

Strategy elements include decisions on which piece you will move on each turn, or whether you use you throw to hire a forklift or crane.  The detection element involves guessing whether all your opponents are as innocent as they pretend or if they are not declaring their full cargo.

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Viking Conquerors: An Epic Game of Viking Adventure
description:
BACKGROUND: Viking Conquerors is an adventure game for 1-4 Players, where players assume the roles of young Viking Huskarls (minor Chieftains) during in the Middle Ages. Each Player creates a Viking Character in search of Wealth, Adventure and Renown. To survive and thrive in the harsh and unforgiving world of Medieval Europe, the Player Vikings must take part in Raids, Blood-Feuds, Exploration, and Conquest. As your Viking gains experience and renown they can improve their attributes and Social and Military Status to eventually become Jarls (Earls) or even a King!

GAME OBJECTIVE: The Objective of each Player Viking is to Gain the most Territories and Renown Points in order to increase your Social and Military Standing. Player Vikings start out as Huskarls, a minor Chieftain with right to own a Ship and in control of one Territory.

GAME FEATURES: Viking Conquerors features many, many colorful game counters, rules for brutal man-to-man combat resolved in a fast and fun manner, and loads of Viking lore.

Smoking Gun is the winner of the 2015 BGG Microgame Design Contest!

Smoking Gun, a noir tale of crimes, secrets, and investigations. Players take on the role of detectives, piecing together a central case in which their reputations (and even their lives) are at stake. In the end, only one detective will crack the case and become the hero... but at what cost?

This microgame consists of only 18 cards (plus player references), 12 of which are used in a given game. Players can enjoy one game, lasting 5-10 minutes, or a series of games, using the included "The Long Night" mode.

A complete Print & Play file (3 pages of cards, black and white, double-sided), including rules, is available.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Chimera Expansion Pack
description: The Chimera Torquo is the competitive prize for Month 5 of the Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, Tyranny of Dragons. The prize includes one pre-painted miniature and numerous upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Pass the Blame: The Party Game For Irresponsible Adults
description:
This is a game of concocting the best excuse to get out of unfortunate situations.  Players are presented with a Situation card, for example:

    There’s a giant slice missing from the birthday cake, and the party hasn’t even started.
    There are muddy footprints all over the new white carpet.
    Your friend’s collection of vintage action figures was mistakenly donated to the holiday toy drive.


The player must then write down who is to blame and why.  Then the answers are scored based on best and worst excuse.

Chaos Tactics is a dexterity game where skill and strategy are needed to defeat your opponent by using your army of eight units. Players battle over a series of rounds by flicking their units until one player claims the entire board. Each army contains seven different unit types with one type, the soldier, having two units per side. The six non soldier units have special abilities that can be used throughout the game. The constant action will create chaotic battles making each round feel like an entirely new game.
 The player using the blue pieces starts the game by choosing one of their pieces and flicking it. Once the piece is flicked, resolve any special abilities if applicable. The red player then takes their first turn by doing the same. Play alternates like this until the round is over; with the goal being to knock all of the opponent’s pieces off the game board entirely. If any part of a piece is still on the game board, it is still in play.

A round is won when only one color remains on the board. With each round won, the winner claims a new row allowing them to place a piece or pieces in that row in the next round. For the following rounds, players may place their pieces in any of the spaces of the rows they own, with at least one piece in the row closest to their opponent.

The game is won when one color has claimed all eight rows.

Inspired by Chapayev

Round We Go is a strategic family board game.

The goal is to race to the center of the board. First person to get both of their player pieces to the center wins the game.

The trick is that pieces are unable to turn on their own, which can cause you to go round and round on the circular board, unless you plan accordingly.

Players must strategically place arrow pieces on the board to either help them advance or force opponents to backtrack. However, it is important to remember, in a game of circles, sometimes what goes around comes around.

Sikarra, a volcano island rich in a rare resource that could launch your corporation years ahead in their research…or one of the multiple other companies that are racing to find it before you. Extracting the resources from Sikarra will not be an easy job; it is rife with dangers; cannibals, pterodactyls, and the volatile volcano itself. The slopes of the volcano are covered in a dense jungle that is very difficult for modern machinery to penetrate. The only people able to move through this difficult terrain easily are the local tribe…rumor has it they may even be able to travel on the backs of the great winged pterodactyls that circle the volcano. Befriending the tribe may be the only way for your corporation to be the first to reach the rare resources and come out on top.

“Jungle power rules Sikarra. In the jungle the tribe is king…”

The game is playable by 2-4 players.

Players vie against one another for control of the 6 territories of Sikarra attempting to harvest its rare resources while making use of favors traded by other players for much needed equipment. Players will need to stay a step ahead of the deadly volcano, cannibals and circling pterodactyls all the while trying to befriend the ruling tribe through completion of tasks in the tribes name if they want to succeed let alone stay alive.

Winning Conditions: Players must gain support of the tribe (10 support) or have enough forces to overpower the tribe (10 pieces of equipment). The way players gain the support of the tribe is by completing tasks that support the tribe from the tasks list.

Task List:


     Build a tribute to the tribe on a territory you control. Cost: 2 Resources & 1 Equipment
     Give back to nature. Select one free area and keep it clear of harvesting for one round.
     Slash and Burn. Destroy a section of jungle in a territory you control. Gain 3 Resources. Move down one spot on tribe support track.
     Bribe local officials. Move once for free at any time this round. Cost: 2 Resources. Move down one spot on tribe support track.


Players move through the following phases each round while trying to show the tribe they are most worthy of their support to control each resource rich territory:

1. Purchase/Place Equipment
2. Cannibals Attack.
3. Politics/Tasks.
4. Lava/Ash Track.
5. Harvest.
6. Intrigue.
7. Move.

Can you lead your corporation to success? Can you harvest the power of the jungle and rule Sikarra: Volcano Island?

Microgame submission for 2015 Geekway to the West Design Competition. The game is a two player card game that takes about 30 minutes to play. It utilizes a deck of 18 cards.

When the mishpuchah get together, it should be a time of nachas, but there are always some tsouris.  Such mishegoss!  (Translation for the Yiddush impaired:  When the relatives get together, it should be a time of joy, but there are always woes.  Such craziness!)

Deal out the guests for the family get-together, then seat them one at a time.  Gain points by making your guests happy.  Some members are particular about whom they sit with, while others have family members whom they wouldn't be caught dead next to.  For most rounds, there will be one branch of the family who is offended and won't score any points at all.  The game is over when everyone has been offended at least once.

Set up: Place this location off to the side of the play area, apart from the regular locations.

Special Location Ability: Discard a card here to steal a card from any player with more than 2 cards. Do not draw at the end of the turn. Cards played here are not added to the discard pile before reshuffling the Virtus deck.

A strategic cycling parlor game for 2-4 players, 8 years and above

Will your team win the tour?
Going for the yellow or do you choose for the team? Climbing the hills or pushing it on a flat stage?
Keep in mind you’re not the only one cycling the road… A small steering error, misjudgment or a ‘cycling strategy’ can cause a sensational collision.
Let your cycling team ride as fast as possible and win the bike race!

Introduction
In this parlor game you play within the stages as a cycling team. A team consists of three riders. The basic game can be played with 2 to 4 players. The duration of a one stage game with 4 players is 1 to 1.5 hours.
. The results of your team and players standings are recorded. A Tour consists of several stages, with the fastest rider over several stages wearing the yellow jersey. You decide for how long and which stages you want to play.
The game is played with cards. By placing a card on the table you decide how fast you are moving your riders on the trail. You try taking tactical moves and benefit from wind advantage by letting your opponent do the hard work. Cast or save your good cards to démarrage when you feel that your opponent cannot. In short: “Linkeballen”.

Note:
If you have the additions as well, you can play up to 24 players.

This is a card deck & game system with multiple suits, inspired by little ponies, and designed with young players in mind.

Unlike a standard deck of cards, this deck features six "mane" suits: Generosity, Honesty, Kindness, Laughter, Loyalty, and Magic.

The deck also features three other suits: Earth Pony, Pegasus, and Unicorn. The mane suits of Honesty and Laughter are adorned with the Earth Pony suit. Kindness and Loyalty are adorned with the Pegasus suit. Generosity and Magic feature the Unicorn suit.

Furthermore, another pair of suits split the deck in half: the Moon and Sun. Honesty, Laughter, and Loyalty lie under the Moon. Generosity, Kindness, and Magic under the Sun.

The deck is available as a print-and-play game.

Crack the Code with three levels of challenge is a brain testing game of logic and mental concentration. The object of the game is to crack the secret code of your opponent by means of simple logic and deduction. Players challenge each other in turn by setting out a row of three, four or five coloured code counters according to the level of skill and difficulty to be played. The game can be made even tougher by allowing the code maker to use any number of the same coloured counters, and or leave spaces, to make up the code.

The code maker sets up the sliding screen so that it is placed directly in front of them just allowing space to place and hide their secret code in the spaces provided. The code breaker must try to duplicate the exact colours and positions of the secret code. Each attempt is marked with a tick for an exact colour and position match or a cross for the correct colour but incorrect position.

Players swap roles and the winner is the one who breaks the code in the least number of attempts.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Wrath of the Righteous – "Ekkie" Promo Character Card Set
description:
This is Paizo's Free RPG Day 2015 promotional character card set for the Pathfinder Adventure Card game.

Ekkie, like her compatriot Ranzak, likes to eat, stab, and steal, not necessarily in that order. She comes with a character card, a token card, and a role card, and she can be played with any Adventure Card Game Base Set.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

boardgame: The Arab-Israeli Wars: Tank Battles in the Mideast – Lebanon: New Scenario for Arab Israeli Wars
description: Published in The General 21/6, add new counters ( MBT Merkava, T-72, Gazelle helicopters, and MRL artillery) and 5 new scenarios for arab Israeli Wars.

When gods fight, the blood of simpletons flows freely. Teomachia lets you become one of the ancient gods and spend one hour playing the lord of a group of devoted followers. You have one goal and one goal only: Annihilate your foe's believers and thereby move yourself one step closer to absolute power.

Drafting prayer cards and power cards allows you to slowly build your might. Using those cards, you send your followers into battles, build temples, and inflict terrible curses on the unfaithful. You face a tough decision in every single hand: Should you put your own power on the line to help your believers win their battles, or should you turn your back on them, sending them to a certain death? If no god yields, hurricanes will face off with deathly plagues, tempests will extinguish walls of fire, and the ranks of the faithful will grow smaller every second. Once you get rid of your opponent's last prophet, it's time to rest and listen to the cheerful song of your people. You have won the clash of gods.

Teomachia: Mitologia słowiańska (Theomachy: Slavonic Mythology) is the first expansion. It introduces the Slavonic pantheon to the game previously consisting of four mythologies: Celtic, Greek, Norse and Sumerian. The expansion provides three new gods of Slavonic pantheon (Mokosh, Perun and Veles), as well as four additional gods to previously existing pantheons (one to each). Also in package are new Slavonic myth cards, myth cards from basic edition reprinted using improved graphics, a player aid card and a small expansion Priests, consisting of ten wooden discs representing priests with special abilities (for all 5 pantheons).
