description: Card game answering trivia about America.

Twirk is a game of spelling quickly for 2-7 players who race to make five letter words from dice and cards.

Each round, players race to make words using cards that have a single letter and a two-letter pair on them, and dice that have vowels and wilds. Each card can be used as either its single letter or pair of letters. Each player has two cards in hand to use, as well as three community cards in the middle of the table.

The first to find a five-letter word wins the round, and the first to win five rounds wins the game!

Dhex (from Dual-Hex) is a Hex derivative that is played with the same components.

Players in turn place 2 adjacent discs on the board, one of each colour.

Whenever a 1-cell hole is created, the player that created it places a disc of her colour on it. Notice that several holes can be created at once, but none as a result of filling.

First to connect his sides wins.

The games are shorter than in Hex, so large boards are recommended (14x14).

In Patron’s Protégé, you are a lord or lady in the Middle Ages and the time has come for your wards to receive higher education.  You will attempt to give them the best education possible by sending them to classes where they will eventually gain proficiency and use their knowledge to teach their own classes.

Patron's Protégé is a two to four player worker placement micro-game.  The game consists of 12 cards, which are: 7 class cards, 4 player credentials cards, and one Core classes card.  Additionally, there are three punch cards that contain various markers for tracking resources and player pieces.

Ayoayo is a Nigerian mancala game, which is played by the Yoruba. It should not be confused with Oware, which is also sometimes called "Ayoayo" when played in Nigeria. Related games are Andada in Eritrea and Endodoi in Tanzania.

Ayoayo is played on a board of two rows, each consisting of six round pits that have a large store at either end. Each pit contains four seeds at the start.

At his turn a player takes the contents of one of his smaller holes and distributes it, one by one, counterclockwise into the following holes, but not in the stores. If the last seed falls into a non-empty hole, its contents including the last distributed seed is continued to be distributed.

The move ends when the last seed falls in an empty hole.

If the last seed falls in an empty hole on the player's own side, the seeds in the opponent's hole directly across and the seed effecting the capture are captured and placed into the store. If the emptied hole contains twelve or more seeds, it is skipped.

When the opponent has no seeds in his holes, he must get some seeds in the next move, if possible. Otherwise the game is over and the remaining seeds are captured by the player who moved last.

The player who captured most counters wins the game.

Description taken from Mancala World.

Rather clever party game.

The box contains 18 pictures and 12 answer cards.

Pablo, always wearing his sombrero, (represented by thick black rings) is a man of many parts. Our artist has drawn his impressions of some of Pablo's activities as seen through a bird's eye from above.

Distribute the pictures about the room and give each player an answer card. They should then be allowed abouf 20 minutes in which to write down their guesses as to what Pablo is supposed to be doing in each picture. The most correct answers, wins.

A word game for children.

In his turn, a player draws a card which will have a letter on it. He can now move one of his pawns on to a coloured space if he can think of a word that begins with the letter on his card.
On a purple space he must name an object.
On a red space he must name an animal.
On a blue space he must name a fruit or plant.
On a white space he must name a boy or girl's name.
If a correct answer is said, the player keeps the card.
Eventually, both of a players' pawns will reach the middle of the board. Everybody now tries to make the longest word they can, using the letter cards they have collected during the game. The player with the longest word, wins.

Party game. The box contains 18 Picture Cards and 12 Answer Cards.
Each picture has some noticeabie feature and the words expressing it generally convey, in everyday usage, quite a different or wider meaning.
Example — 'TO SIT ON THE FENCE'
The usual Interpretation of this phrase is ' to be neutral' or ' not to take sides.' In the picture the expression is conveyed by showing hens (and boy) sitting on a fence.
Distribute the pictures about the area of play and give each player an answer card. A time limit should be fixed and the player who writes down most correct expressions is the winner.
Answer cards should be interchanged for checking.

Party game with delightful cards.

The illustrated cards are placed around the room where they can easily be seen. Give each player an answer card. Set a time limit. The player who has the most correct answers when 'Time' is called, wins.

The cards feature a number of different illustrations and they are numbered. Players have to look at these images in number order and extract a number of letters from each answer to form the name of an English town or city. They give an example card. There are 5 pictures on that card.
The 1st is a Seal - use the ES
The 2nd is a Step - use the ST
The 3rd is a Bottle - use the BO
The 4th is a Nurse - use the UR
the 5th is a Net - use the NE
Thus, the answer is EA-ST-BO-UR-NE, which is a town on the southern coast of England.

Mob Tower Promo Pack was a Kickstarter cross promotion between Tower and Mob Town. The 10-card promo pack has some Tower-inspired cards in Mob Town, and some Mob Town-inspired cards in Tower.

Specifically, for Tower, two black market cards representing the law, where you can team up with undercover cops in the black market to momentarily have a conscience and thwart crooks!

And then, in Mob Town, an alternate set of cash cards, a camel law card, and a destination card featuring the wonderfully obscure city that Tower takes place in!

An English party game, loosely based on a long forgotten television show.

12 cards are provided, each of which contains a different personal “ story,” all of them famous in the sense that the Daily Press has, or would have, given them headline publicity. They are modern, historical, legendary, or fiction.

One player to be Chairman. He takes all the cards; no one eise must see them yet. He selects one card and chooses a player (the Challenger) who will answer questions as though he were the person about whom that story was made. The Challenger and the Chairman sit together facing the other players, who become the “ panel.” The Chairman announces to the panel the free information, as printed at the top of the card. This is the only “ free ” information given. The panel have to discover what the Challenger’s story is by questioning him (or her), and they ask any questions they wish, one at a time and in turn. The Challenger gives the answers, Consulting the information provided on the card about “ his own ” story. The Chairman may help and advise the Challenger in his answers. In answering questions, the Challenger should use the first person “ I.”

It is the story which has to be discovered, not necessarily the name of the person concerned, though it is usually simpler for the panel to find the name first, which will lead them to the story quickly.

The Chairman may announce a time limit, say 5 minutes, after which the panel if still unsuccessful have been defeated, and the Challenger announces his story. A new Challenger is selected for each story.

English party game.

A quiz for your party guests. Up to 12 may play. The host or hostess will read out the questions and the players write down their answers on their answer card.

As the title suggests, the questions will fall into 5 different categories;
Proverbial Last Words: Players must name the proverb just by being told only the last word.
Car Cavalcade: Players must name the manufacturer of the listed car models.
Sur Me: Players are given eight Christian names and eight surnames. Match them correctly to make a list of eight writers.
Triangulation: Players are given a pyramid shape triangle with lines criss-crossing and must write down the correct number of triangles in the image.
Chop and Change: Players are given a statement and the answer must be two words that are anagrams of each other.

The guest with the most correct answers, wins and, hopefully, gets a small gift from the hosts.

Melee in the Mines is a print-and-play microgame that pits two players against one another. The dwarves’ silver mine has cut into the tunnels of a monster lair, and now a band of adventurers is fighting for their lives in the narrow confines of the mine against a band of monsters. Each player starts with 7 of 18 characters (18 Monsters or 18 Adventurers). The winner is the party that drives the other from the mine!

Only the first character in each party can use hand-to-hand combat. Other party members can use ranged weapons or special attacks -- provided they have a clear line-of-sight over their companions’ heads. In these tight quarters, jostling for position is important. It’s great to have your troll at the head of your party, given his strength, but then no one behind him can see past him to use a ranged attack. If your halfling is at the front, everyone taller can fire over him, but he himself won’t be doing much damage.

Each turn you can either battle (roll 2 six-sided dice) or take an action (see below) and attack with 1 die. Each die roll is assigned to a character whose abilities match that roll – each card has die faces on it, with corresponding attacks: 1 = Rush, 2 = Strike, 3 = Panic, 4 = Fell, 5 = Do, 6 = Kill.

You can download the 4-page PDF from the Files section. You will need two 6-sided dice.

This game was created for the 2015 BGG Microgame Prototype Design Competition.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Dora the Explorer Let's Go! Adventure Game Book
description:
Five basic Dora the Explorer Games, to teach color and number recognition, sequencing, patterning and game play skills.

Included games are "The Race to Abuela's House", "Vines & Logs","Catch the Stars!", "The Nutty Forest Maze" and "Beach day!"

Short description per game:

The Race to Abuela's House: players spin the spinner to race to Abuela's house, but if they land on an obstacle, they need the spinner to land on the corresponding tool before moving on.

Vines & Logs: shortened version of chutes (logs) and ladders (vines).

Catch the Stars!: the game board shows a pattern with six different colours. Players spin the spinner (that shows colours now) to spin the required colours for their next step, in a race to the other side of the board, where te stars await.

The Nutty Forest Maze: players spin the spinner to race to Tico's treehouse. They have to choose the right way through the maxe, and can loose turns (when encountering Swiper the fox) or getting extra moves (when encountering stars).

Beach Day!: players spin the spinner and move to the first next square showing the same picture as the picture shown by the spinner to race to the beach (candy land variation).

boardgame: ATS Core Module: Clash Along The Psel I
description:
Clash Along the Psel I: A brand new take on the Battle of Kursk, with Tigers, a contested river crossing ... and Russian counter-attacks. A titanic struggle, recreated in detail down to the last tank ... squad ... and forward observer!

Welcome to the ATS CORE MODULES, all new for 2014. New map, larger, on cardstock, folding with no trimming needed -- did we mention new terrain art throughout AND larger hexes? Perfect for those classic -- YET ALL NEW ATS COUNTERS -- the you'll have on the map deciding history ... all ... over ... again! This module is for owners of the ATS 2014 Color Rules plus ATS Standard Small Markers + Large Markers but may also be used easily by owners of any past edition of the ATS rules. All Special Rules and AFV Cards needed for play are included, along with ALL NEW FOLIO STYLE packaging. 
IMPORTANT NOTE: This product contains ALL the combat counters you need to play -- IT IS NOT A COMPLETE GAME. You need small and large marker counter sheets and the ATS Rules Binder/Play Aid set to use this product.

Det er skægt at tælle med Hr. Skæg. 
I dette spil for førskolebørn lærer de små børn at genkende tallene fra 1 til 10, at tælle fra 1 til 10 og at øve sig på at skrive tallene. 
Drej pilen, flyt brikken på spillepladen og udfør opgaven på kortet. Børnene skal tælle ting, skrive tal, hente ting og pege på ting med forkerte farver. Desuden skal de hoppe forskellige slags hop. For hver opgave barnet klarer vinder det en Skægbrik til sin samleplade.

IN ENGLISH:


It is fun to count with Hr. Skæg.
In this game pre-school children learn to recognize, write and count the numbers from 1 to 10. 
Turn the arrow, move the gamepiece on the board and do the task on the card. The children must count, write numbers, find things og point at objects with wrong colors. And they get to jump different kinds of jumps. For each task the child does, it wins a Skæg-token for the pointboard.

boardgame: ATS Core Module: Clash Along The Psel II
description:
Clash Along the Psel II: A never before presented battlefield -- the push north out of the Psel River crossing .. and the Russian response? Also includes 2 BRAND NEW never before published maps, Central Stalingrad I and Tin Pan Alley, Sevastopol -- with and exciting and detailed scenario to play (and replay) on each!

Welcome to the ATS CORE MODULES, all new for 2014. New map, larger, on cardstock, folding with no trimming needed -- did we mention new terrain art throughout AND larger hexes? Perfect for those classic -- YET ALL NEW ATS COUNTERS -- the you'll have on the map deciding history ... all ... over ... again! This module is for owners of the ATS 2014 Color Rules plus ATS Standard Small Markers + Large Markers but may also be used easily by owners of any past edition of the ATS rules. All Special Rules and AFV Cards needed for play are included,

boardgame: Undead States of America: The Zombie War Card Game
description:
You aren't trying to escape a neighborhood filled with walking dead - that was weeks ago.  This isn't about survival any more.

This is war.

The object of Undead States of America is to gather survivors and hold off the undead masses.  It is up to you to rally refugees, build safe zones and lead your rag-tag forces through a psychological nightmare.  In the end, you will find your army of men, women & children surrounded.  Only then will you learn if you've prepared well enough to secure a future for the human race...  (from the back of the box)

Players attempt to build a cadre of fellow survivors, giving them a place to stay and supplies.  There are cards for survivors, supplies, military action (even a nuke!), and even for antagonists (used to fight against you).  The winner is who has scored the best after surviving 4 years.

The game represent race of sailboats. They start from the shore. There are different paths between many islands. Players roll dice and move figurines from field to field in direction of paths.

The first who reach finish gets the most points, but points are also received by collecting counters along the path, by standing on marked field next to counter. The winner is the player who ends up with the most points.

Knight race. The game presents racing of knights. There are different paths. Players roll dice and move figurines in direction of paths.

First who reach finish gets the most points, but points are also received by collecting counters along the path, by standing on marked field next to counter. The winner is the player who ends up with the most points.

Šmizling is a game for girls. On the board is presented a shopping center. In stores can be purchased (collected) different clothing items (counters), by standing on marked field next to counter.

The task in not to collect as many counters, but to make certain outfits. The first player who succeed in that is the winner.

Tiger in hunt is a simple game for children. Each player has one figurine representing a tiger. On the board are counters where are pictures of various animals that are food for tigers.

Players collect counters by standing on marked field next to counter. Players need to collect as many counters as possbile.

World of Silence is an educational game. Playing the game children learn about animals that live in the sea. Animals are divided by groups: fishes, mammals, reptiles, birds, soft shells, crabs ...

Each player has one figurine, which represents a diver. Divers swim and take photos of a variety of animals, by standing on field with counter. Each photograph (counter) brings one point. It is important to collect as many counter, but it is more important that the counters are in different colors. Each color represents one group of animals.

Photo Safari is a simple game for younger players. Each player has one figurine. The figurine represents off road vehicle in which are visitors of safari park. There are different paths.

On the board are counters with pictures of different animals. Players collect counters (photos) by standing on marked filed next to counter. During the game, players need to collect as many counters.

From back of the box:

Object of the game:
Ninja warriors attack the Fortress. The defenders inside the Fortress walls must prevent them from stealing the treasure.

Ninja is a simple game of movement and dice combat. One player is the attacker while the other player is the defender. Each player's ninja pieces show its movement, range, and attack properties. When two pieces battle, each player adds the attack number of their battling piece to the roll of two dice, with the highest total winning the battle. Eventually, the defender will either have prevented the attacker from stealing the treasure, or the attacker will make off with the treasure, winning the game.  It is suggested that two rounds are played, with players switching roles and comparing the points that they gained when they played the attacker in order to determine a winner.

Arrezzo is played on 8x8 board with a green and a red set of 16 playing pieces with symbols on top. Each set has 2 monks (a cross ), 2 ladies (a circle), 2 lords (a star ), 6 farmers (a square) and 4 knights (a triangle).

These symbols hint on the direction of the slides and jumps every piece is able to make.
Slides are towards a free square in any ortagonal or diagonal direction.
Jumps are combinations ortagonal or diagonal jumps across your own or opponents pieces, depending on the type of piece. Multiple jumps are possible.

Starting from your own two base lines object of the game is to move all your pieces off the board on the opposite side and prevent the opponent from doing so.

Sheeplike is a Theocratic Card Game for family worship based upon going out in the ministry and finding Sheeplike ones and starting bible studies while avoiding Do Not Calls. 2-4 players ages 9+ can play in 30-60 min using tools like the bible, pioneers, Jesus and even Jehovah to win the householders hearts over.  The goal is the first player to 4 bible studies. You gain a bible study by finding the Sheeplike one out in the territory or be the last player still in the round.

Setup:  The territory consists of 9 cards (houses) grouped in 3 piles hiding where the Sheeplike and Do Not Call cards are.   Shuffle the Sheeplike, Do Not Call and 1 Not Interested cards and create 3 piles.  Then deal 2 more territory cards on top of the 3 piles.  Players are each dealt 1 card each from the publisher cards.

The game consists of 2 stages:

Door To Door: Players draw and play cards searching through the territory stacks for the Sheeplike one. There are 17 publisher cards that allow players to flip the cards in the territory and move cards around within the stacks. This allows for players to hide traps and uncover the Sheeplike card. If a Do Not Call is flipped without the protection of the Bible card, that player has to sit out of the current round. If the Sheeplike one is not found by the time that there is no more publisher cards to draw from then the next stage starts.

Return Visit: Once all cards have been drawn from the publisher draw pile. All players turns over their one card in their hand.  Whoever has the highest value card, they get the first pick of where they think the Sheeplike card is at. Remember the players have had a few turns to deduce the most likely location of the Sheeplike one. So when the publisher pile is getting low, it is good to hold onto higher value cards.

This is a 100 card version of the viral card game superfight, designed by Skybound for sale on Loot Crate . These cards are only available in this exclusive deck. Create hilarious fighters with these cards, then argue over who would win in a fight between them.
An expansion for Loonacy containing five new cards featuring Timmy the Monkey, ThinkGeek's mascot.  This expansion was included for free in many packages sent to ThinkGeek customers.

It is a glorious, shining moment for the National Space Program: launch day is finally here. The crew is suited up and looking dashing, journalists are lined up for miles, and the sky is clear and inviting. There's just one problem: due to a bureaucratic mishap, the rockets aren't scheduled to be assembled until next Tuesday.

You and the other players are high-ranking scientists at the National Space Program, competing to put together the best rocket you possibly can in the final ten minutes before launch. Whoever builds the rocket that gets closest to space, without exploding or suffering a similar fatal catastrophe, wins the game and keeps their job. If every rocket is destroyed, the winner is whoever got the closest.

Don't Explode takes place over two phases, Construction and Launch. During Construction, players will draft cards from the construction deck and add components to their control module, in order to build the best rocket they possibly can in the limited time available. During Launch, players will see how well they did, by using the components which make up their rocket to advance their pawn from the Earth to space (or as close to space as they can get).

boardgame: A Step to Victory: The Bougainville Campaign, 1943-45
description:
A Step to Victory is a small Operational level board-wargame of the Bougainville campaign, included in "This Sim-Game is Awesome! 2013", an annual zine of wargame review.

This is a 2 pages leaflet game inside the book titled above.

This game covers the Bougainville campaign from American landing on Torokina to the end of war.

The rule is printed in first page with units, and the map is printed in second page. So, you must first make the units.

The game uses very basic land operation sequences, move and combat. This game uses differential CRT instead of usual combat ratio.

The game ends in 10 turns.  And victory is decided by the number of locations the American side has captured.

AVAILABLE FREE NOW.

boardgame: Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game – The Thousand Young
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Call of Cthulhu – The Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Fotbollsspelet (“The Soccer Game”) is a two player game where your soccer knowledge decides the outcome. The rulebook also provides rules for playing the game with more players, in teams, making it a cooperative game.

You play as in real soccer, with passes, clinches, shots, penalties, and offside.  Eventually you will discover how important cooperation between the single players are for teamwork. Dice rolling and event cards will bring in randomness, just like in a real game of soccer.

Situation cards with questions about soccer rules are an important part of the game. If you know the rules, it will give you advantages in the game. If you don’t, you will learn the rules playing Fotbollsspelet.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: A game set in the Wild West at the time of the gold miners. In the first part of the game players turn to the cities to equip, after facing the journey to the mountain to climb and reach the large deposit of gold. During the journey will find gold and dangers such as Indians and bandits, who can overcome only with equipment cards collected before. Upon reaching the summit of the mountain by a player what he found most gold is the winner.

Parasites have invaded a zoo in a small town. They are moving from host to host trying to exploit all the different animals. Players will take on the role of parasites and their respective hosts in an attempt to gain or steal the most points by the end of the game.

The game is played with a deck of cards containing a set of colored host cards for each player numbered 2,3,4 and 5. The deck also contains special action cards, one for each player.

Players will also receive four parasite tokens of their selected color valued 1,2,3 and 4. The players colored host cards and parasite tokens represent potential points.

The game starts with one card turned face up next to the draw pile. Drawn cards will be placed next to this card. A new row will start after five cards have been drawn.

On your turn players can (1) place a parasite on a host card, (2) move a parasite to an adjacent host or action card, (3) draw a card to place in play or (4) pass.

Drawing a card will activate a scoring phase. All previous cards are then evaluated for scoring condition. Cards of your color without any parasite tokens score the full value for the player. Cards with parasites where the value of the parasites are of less than value that the host card it is on will score points for the owner of the parasite and card owner. EXAMPLE: A three green parasite on a five blue host card will score three points for green and two points for blue.

Cards where the total of the parasites is equal to or more than the host card total, will NOT score for either player. Action cards allow players to perform a special action like move other players tokens or look through the deck.

The game is over when all the cards have been drawn and placed into the play area. The player with the highest point after all the cards have been drawn is the winner.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Castle Ravenloft Board Game – Bonus Adventures
description:
Two additional adventures for use in Castle Ravenloft, including one concerning the Sunsword—which may well prove instrumental in the hunt for the nefarious count Strahd.

Zombie Guards of Castle Ravenloft

The Heroes are trapped in the crypts of Castle Ravenloft with a horde of zombies. They must find the exit and escape with their lives! 
Goal: Find the Secret Stairway and escape the crypts. 
Number of Heroes: 2 to 5 Heroes (group adventure).

Search for the Sunsword

The Heroes seek a magic sword's missing piece. Once assembled, the legendary Sunsword becomes a powerful weapon against the evil threatening Barovia. 
Goal: You must recover the last piece of the Sunsword from the crypts below Castle Ravenloft. 
Number of Heroes: 2 to 5 Heroes (group adventure).

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Castle Ravenloft Board Game – Contest Adventures
description:
The winning fan submissions from the Castle Ravenloft Board Game contest. Scenarios designed by Dwayne S., Kevin G., and Greg F. and published by Wizards of the Coast.

Curses!

The Heroes, with Thorgrim at their lead, head back into Castle Ravenloft for revenge. They must defeat the werewolf that killed Thorgrim’s brother.

Goal: Defeat the werewolf.

It has been days since you were last in these crypts with your brother Hargrinn. You and Hargrinn had set out to stop a werewolf that had been terrorizing the locals. Hargrinn was armed with a silver blade to kill it, but he underestimated the werewolf's strength and died at the jaws of the werewolf. After seeing that, you retreated. Now you have come back with a band of adventurers to kill the werewolf and avenge your brother.

A Hero Lost

The Heroes must find the infected Hero in the lower levels of the dungeon and locate the dark fountain. Combine its water with the vial of holy water to cure the infected Hero.

Goal: Cure the infected Hero.

One of your party has been captured and infected by Strahd’s minions. The infected hero is now stranded deep in the crypt. An elixir made by combining holy water with the water from the dark fountain can cure the hero of the disease. Can you make it before the infection overwhelms the lost hero?

The Skull of Necromancy

The Heroes must enter the dungeons of Castle Ravenloft to obtain a powerful artifact and escape with it so that it can be destroyed.

Goal: Recover the Skull of Necromancy and escape the dungeon.

Using divination, you have learned that the Skull of Necromancy, an evil artifact of terrible power, is in Strahd’s dungeon. You have volunteered to explore the dungeon to find the Skull and bring it out to be destroyed.

description: Wipeout is played on a vertical plastic tower with rows and columns of slots into which plastic surfers can be inserted. Each player starts with one surfer at the top edge of the tower. A "wave" made of green slime is loaded into a "regulator" at the top of the tower, which dispenses it to pour down the tower at a preselected speed. Players then race their surfers down the tower by rolling a die which controls movement. They try to reach the bottom of the tower first while avoiding being engulfed by the slime and "wiping out".

Craok! Croak!
Colorful parrots are waiting to fill their plumage with chips of the correct color which can be found in the eggs. 
The player who completes his parrot with all the color chips first, has won the game. 
Have fun with this thrilling seeking/searching game!

How to play:
At your turn you take an egg and open it. 
Hopefully you will find a chip that isn't already on your board. 
If this is the case, you can add it to the board. 
If not, you put the chip back into the egg and withdraw it with the other eggs. Then it's the next players turn. 
The first player who has filled his board with all colored chips has won the game.

Description from the publisher:

"The Underway is a funky, chaotic, and eclectic mix of architecture and ethnicities. What unifies it is a general counterculture vibe – the sense that they are pioneers and iconoclasts. But this independent spirit paved the way for the rise of Los Muertos."
–Exposé on The Underway: Part IV – Days of the Muertos

The Underway, the fourth Data Pack in the SanSan Cycle for Android: Netrunner, explores the meat space, culture, and residents of the megacity SanSan. The cradle of high-end tech, home to beautiful beaches, and the host of the World Expo, SanSan is also still recovering from "The Big One", the cataclysmic earthquake that opened up the San Andreas fault and redefined America's western coast. However, this event that was disastrous for many was an opportunity for others, some of whom quickly moved their homes and businesses into the new space that was created.

Accordingly, the sixty new cards in The Underway (three copies each of twenty different cards) explore this new space and its motley assembly of different architectural styles, ethnicities, and cultural influences. They also explore the pervasive influence of Los Muertos, a street gang whose members are identified by their bioluminescent tattoos and who deal in illegal tech as much as they deal in flesh and drugs. Through the introduction of new resources, illicit hardware, and clever programs, the gang’s criminal influence pervades every aspect of this Data Pack, starting with a new Criminal Runner identity, then reaching outward as other Runner factions are supplied with the sort of tech you don't normally find in America, and finally rippling into every aspect of every card as Corps are forced to deal with the gang’s members in one way or another.

description:
Game is about learning how to safety use computer and handle your digital data and personal information. 
Players spent "time" to buy available cards that can be used to handle specific bad events.
Example of cards that can be bought:
"Secure Connection"
"Encrypted Emails"
"Privacy settings"
Example of bad events: 
"Data leak"
"Stalker"

Good cards help prepare for bad events that will damage players. Player who received the least damage wins the game.

Beginning Sounds 3-in-a-Row Game is a bingo-type game designed to teach the recognition of beginning letter sounds. Players have 3-by-3 cards marked with pictures. Like Bingo, in order to win, a player must complete a row of three across, down or diagonally.  Unlike Bingo, each player takes turns spinning a spinner marked with five letters. They must then find a picture that starts with the letter spun. Only the player spinning may mark his or her card.  The game comes with four separate sets of cards to cover the entire alphabet.

boardgame: Innløsning: A Rune Casting Game of Redemption
description:
Rise again, noble Völva! You are being given a second chance for Glory!

As you walk the grey and barren lands of Helheim, songs of Valhalla fill your heart and empower your very soul.  However, you are Völva, a seer or ‘wand carrier’.  Unlike warriors that got to fight and die in battle to be carried off to Valhalla, you were only an adviser and tactician who assisted by drawing upon the power of the Runes to help your fellow warriors in their battles.

However, Freya smiles upon you and offers redemption to the Völva who can prove their abilities.  By using the runes to create gateways, you must work your way up from Helheim to Asgard.  By being victorious in challenges and tests of strength, you gain valour and come one step closing to being accepted in Valhalla!

OVERVIEW
Innløsning is an abstract 2 player rune casting game where players are fallen Völva souls in Helheim looking for redemption so that they may enter Valhalla, the hall of the Chosen.  Players must create gateways, starting in Helheim, and sequentially working their way up to Asgard.  By casting the runes, they will generate power to create those gateways and activate them.  However, this is not enough to gain entrance to Valhalla.  You must also gain enough valour by using the runes to overcome challenges, or to defeat your rival, by drawing upon the strength provided by the gateways.  When you have earned enough valour and opened all the gateways, only then will you be allowed entrance to Valhalla!

GOAL
The first player to have gateways in each of the 9 realms, open all their gateways and have 11 valour wins.

Box O'Cats challenges 3 to 5 players to fire up their imaginations solving bizarre dilemmas. Each round, players creatively convince the others that their unusual items best solve the presently perplexing predicament.

Giant robots are attacking. Do you whip out the dynamite or the magic spells?

Or maybe you're pulling the ultimate prank. Will you choose the rubber chicken or the warped time machine? Or maybe... the Box O'Cats?

boardgame: Huzzah! 2: Dark Woods, Dreadful Fields – the Wilderness Campaign
description:
The second volume in the Huzzah series concentrates entirely upon the Wilderness Campaign. It will consist of 8 scenarios.

May 5th Scenarios
1 Opening Guns North. Warren's Fifth Corps temporarily punctures Ewell's center, only to be hurled back by a vicious Confederate counter-attack led by the inestimable John Gordon.
2 Sixth Corps Joins the Fray. An extension of scenario 1, as reinforcements from Sedgewick's Corps descends upon Ewell's left flank. Hay's Louisiana Tigers help stem the blue flood.
3 Hill to the Attack! Two divisions of A.P. Hill's 3rd Corps rattles up the Orange Plank Road and attempts to cut Grant in half by capturing the Brock Road intersection. A single division, commanded by VIth Corps George Getty, stands in his way. Hancock's II corps arrives just in time to save the day. Or will he?

May 6th Scenarios
1 Lee to the Rear! Early in the morning of may 6th Hancock strikes Heth's and Wilcox's divisions of Hill's 3rd Corps. Both divisions have been strung out and exhausted from the previous days fighting. They expected Longstreet's early arrival, and so neglect to fortify their line. Hancock initially drives them almost handsomely, and nearly captures Lee's HQ at the Widow Tapp farm. The engagement witnesses one of the most dramatic moments of the war, when the Marble Man himself proposes to lead the Texas Brigade of Longstreet corps in a counter-charge.
2 Like a Wet Blanket. Now fully arrived, Longstreet strikes the over-extended flank of Hancock's corps. In a swift and dramatic reversal it's now the Yankee's turn to run. Will Longstreet capture the ever important Brock Road intersection?
3 Gordon's Assault. John B. Gordon, ever the thorn in Grant's side, jumps the VI corps and nearly shatters the Yankee's right. Only the coming of darkness brings his assault to an end.

The Campaign Games
These games implement Custom command rules simulating the problems of controlling armies over vast broken terrain. Lee struggles to coordinate his widely separated wings, while Grant whittles away the days, goading reluctant subordinates to action.
1 The May 5th Mini-Campaign game. Combines scenarios 1, 2, and 3, and provides additional units and player options.
2 The full Campaign Game. Play the entire 2 day battle. The ever-frustrating Burnside provisions both the Yankee crux and the Rebel hope. Will you dare achieve better than the historical stalemate?

Projected Contents:

    8 11"x17" maps that mate together to form various portions of the battlefield, or all together for the entire campaign.
    560 half inch die-cut full-color counters
    1 Rules booklet
    1 Scenarios booklet
    2 Sheets of charts and tables
    2 Ten-sided dice
    1 Game box


(source: One Small Step website))

description:
From the rules:
"Holidays in Malaysia is a fun and enjoyable game that showcases the beauty of Malaysia.  Plan your perfect vacation to Malaysia; you can experience the unique multicultural heritage, sumptuous cuisine, eclectic arts, and architecture of Malaysia.  Discover its natural wonders; from some of the most beautiful islands and beaches in the world, to lost tropical jungles that have been wildlife for millions of years.  With a little luck and clever planning, you might just outwit your fellow travelers.  The first traveler to visit 6 landmarks, collect at least 2 souvenirs, taste 2 cuisines and arrive at the final square wins the game."

Holidays in Malaysia is a roll-and-move game where players move along a track, accumulating vacation experiences in Malaysia.  Players roll a die to move along a multi-path track.  Spaces may gain them vacation experiences or souvenirs, move them to other places on the board or cause them to gain or lose turns.  After a player gains all the necessary experiences, they proceed to the finish to win the game.

In Virtual Empire, players try to create a business empire and amass a fortune by moving along a track, collecting various cards that represent ideas, assets and business developments, collecting income along the way. Spaces or cards may send you to court or help or hinder the development of your business. In order to finish the game, players must collect one each of nine different types of cards and land on the Empire Money Chest at the end of the track. Players get bonus money for finishing before other players.  At the end of the game, the player with the most money wins.

Every year, the Royal Society for Folklore Studies, holds a meeting where its members can impress their fellows by showing the result of their efforts. To do so you will try to document the existence of fairies and leprechauns, supplying proof to support your claims. But be careful, for the rest of ladies and gentleman will do whatever it’s on their hands to debunk you and harm your reputation at the club while increasing their own.!

The basic mechanic consist in assembling sets of “proof cards” of the same type in order to get “recognition points” at the end of the game. Players can also try to debunk other player’s sets of cards.

This game entered the 24 hour contest - February 2015

Microgame for 3 - 5 players lasting 20 - 30 minutes.  Game components are deck of 24 cards, although sheet of paper and writing implement are needed for scoring.

You and your opponents are city managers trying to improve the quality of your municipality in terms of Arts, Environment and Industry.  During each of the four rounds you draft three cards to improve one of these qualities with one round devoted to improving an omnibus combined score.  Player with the highest score at the end of the fourth round wins.

ifWHATthen? is the non-stop fun, action-packed party game for friends and family, old and young alike.

ifWHATthen? is the sentence creation game that starts with an "if..." statement and ends when you choose the funniest "...then" statement.

If I wonder which pants to wear...
...then I have a party for one.
...then we decide to call the band 'Mighty Hat'.
...then you oversleep and miss your first day of BEING AWESOME.

If your card is chosen as the funniest...
...then you win that round!

ifWHATthen? invites everyone at the table to use their imagination to find the more than 50,000 card combinations right out of the box.

IF… you are ready for fun, 
THEN... this is the game for you!

The Barnyard Legions March to War
From the frigid north to the arid Sahara, the Barnyard Legions march forth to claim their place in history. In Barnyard Legions you, the general, have been given a mission to keep the other Legions from ever completing their goal of lining up a proper battle line or two. Will you command the Sheep Legionnaires under the rule of Julius Sheepsar? Or will the hoof of Auroch the Red Boviking crush the Celtucky Chickens?

How do you play? 
Each player takes their choice of Barnyard Legion and mixes some of their cards into a common deck. The rest of their cards are kept as their own deck. Each turn, players take 2-3 actions, attempting to complete a battle line of five cards from their Legion. To keep the game moving, every player draws a card from their deck at the start of each round, hoping to not run out of cards, causing elimination, before the battle has been won.

What is unique about it?
The game has built in subtleties that will appeal to gamers, while the unique decks and humorous tongue-in-cheek art make Barnyard Legions a great gateway game for players who are not familiar with more complicated card games. Families will be able to sit down and follow the very simple rules and start playing immediately.

Publisher's description:
Who will be the first predator to reach the top of the food chain?  It could be you. . . if you can answer questions about life science correctly!

King of the Jungle is an educational game designed to test and improve students' knowledge of life science. Players move pawns along a track. In order to move, a player must draw a card and answer the question on the card correctly. He or she may then roll the die and move along the track. Along the track, spaces may take away or grant extra turns, send the player back or forward along the track or allow the player to trade places with another. If a player lands on another player's space, the first player there must move back along the track to the closest space marked with an animals.  The first player to reach the end of the track wins.

Mix & Match ABC is a self-correcting matching game designed to teach the alphabet and reading.  It consists of a series of matching cards, one side with a letter and word printed on it, and the other with a corresponding picture.  The cards have a puzzle piece edge, and each card only fits its pair.

There are two possible ways to play.  One is called Match Out.  In this game, cards are placed face-down and each player draws an equal number.  The first player places the top tile on their stack face-up.  Each player in turn then turns up his or her top tile.  If it matches any of the turned-up tiles, the player matches them, puts them aside, and turns up the next tile in his or her stack.  This continues until no matches are made, and play passes to the next player.  The first player to eliminate his or her stack wins.

In ABC Rummy, letter and picture tiles are places in separate piles.  Players take turns drawing from either stack until they all have four tiles in their hand.  If any players have matches, they may lay them down immediately.  Each player then draws a tile from either pile and tries to match it to tiles in their hand.  If there is a match, they are put aside and the player may draw again.  If not, the player may keep up to four tiles and must discard any others face-up in the middle of the table.  Further players may draw from either the piles or the face-up tiles, making as many matches as possible.  Play continues until one player has five pairs.

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: German Fusilier MG42 Team expansion
description: The German Fusilier MG42 Team offers devastating sustained automatic firepower, capable of keeping the enemy pinned down. Featuring a MG42 and Ammo bearer, this team is a welcome addition to any assault or defense. These soldiers come with their basic playing cards but also 8 new Specialist cards that may include Story, Command, React and all new Equipment cards.

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: German Fusilier STG44 Leader expansion
description: Each German Fusilier STG44 Specialist Leader comes as a private soldier, junior NCO and senior sergeant. Also included are an assortment of Specialist cards that may include Story, Command, React and all new Equipment cards.

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: German Fusilier Assault Team expansion
description:
The German Fusilier Assault Team brings to bear awesome automatic firepower for assaults on the enemy. Featuring a STG44 and MP40 armed soldiers, this team is a welcome addition to any assault. These soldiers come with their basic playing cards but also 8 new Specialist cards that may include Story, Command, React and all new Equipment cards.

CONTENTS:
1 - German STG44 armed Standee with base and markers
1 - German MP40 armed Standee with base and markers
2 - sets of Soldier, Core, Rank and Specialist Cards (16 cards)

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: US Paratrooper Glider Rifle Grenadier Section expansion
description: The US Paratrooper Glider Rifle Grenadier Section has a great mix of long range capabilities as well as being heavily stocked with grenades and ammunition. These soldiers come with their basic playing cards but also 8 new Specialist cards that may include Story, Command, React and all new Equipment cards. These soldiers definitely pack a punch!

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: US Paratrooper Glider Assault Section expansion
description: The US Glider Assault Section brings to bear tremendous automatic firepower, capable of keeping the enemy pinned down. Featuring a BAR and Thompson armed soldiers, this team is a welcome addition to any assault. These soldiers come with their basic playing cards but also 8 new Specialist cards that may include Story, Command, React and all new Equipment cards.

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: US Paratrooper Glider Rifle Leader expansion
description: Each US Paratrooper Glider Rifle Specialist Leader comes as a private soldier, junior NCO and senior sergeant.

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: US Paratrooper Glider Thompson Leader expansion
description: Each US Paratrooper Glider Thompson Specialist Leader comes as a private soldier, junior NCO and senior sergeant.

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: US Paratrooper Assault Team expansion
description: The US Paratrooper Assault Team brings all the tools necessary to get in close to assault an enemy position. The main assault force is one soldier armed with a M1A1 Thompson sub-machine gun and one carrying a M1 Garand rifle with grenade attachment for explosive firepower at short range. Along with the long range fire support from two soldiers carrying M1 Garand rifles, this team is dynamic and effective.

boardgame: Sergeants D-Day: US Paratrooper .30 Cal Team expansion
description: The US Paratrooper .30 Cal Team deploys the Browning M1919A4 Light Machine Gun. Capable of sustained long range fire, this powerful weapon is an asset anywhere on the battlefield. Along with a machine gun assistant, carrying ammo and a M2 Carbine, and two M1 Garand riflemen, this team excels at digging in to defend a position, or breaking down an enemy advance.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Zariah the Summoner
description:
Zariah is a special kind of sommoner mage – one who befriends and encourages her summons rather than bending them to her will. Three of them, Scrappy the Lion, Bandit the Ferret and Amber the Alemental, are her hardest-working companions. So, of course, after the adventure she brings them along to the tavern for some well-deserved rest and relaxation.


    The Good: Those summons are soooo cuuute!!!



    The Bad: When they combine to form the fearsome Chimera, you’ll be looking for the nearest exit!


The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Zariah the Summoner expands the party at the Red Dragon Inn with a powerful summoner. While her friends are generally tame and benign partygoers watch out when all three of them come together. When Scrappy, Bandit and Amber form the mighty Chimera, expect to see some serious trouble!

The Red Dragon Inn: Allies is a series of expansions for any standalone The Red Dragon Inn game. Each Allies set includes a single Character Deck, plus components to add a brand new character to your game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A tile based tactical skirmish game based on the Realms of Norse Mythology. 
Each realm is comprised of unique Norse characters and their wondrous abilities. The game allows you to pit these against each other in a bid to claim total victory. The victors will reshape the World in their own form.

Each Realm has a choice of Heroic or Epic play styles that focus on either raw power or tactical advantage. Heroic has simple abilities but is harder hitting. Epic adds advanced abilities and lore based content. Overall the game is balanced to mix Heroic and Epic styles together. Experienced players can involve themselves in the valorous abilities and tactics of Epic and still have a balanced fight against new players learning Heroic.

Each realm has nine individual card stock figures mounted on a base. Movement and combat in the varied terrain hexes is fuelled by an Action Pool. Combat uses-twelve sided dice that can be modified with Fate Cards. The winner is either the Realm with the last ruler standing or who has filled up their Blood Cauldron up with the sanguine fluid of their enemies!

The War of the Nine Realms is a tactical skirmish game for 2-4 players, aged 10 or above. Game time is 60 minutes and the complexity is 7 ( on a scale of 1-10). Varied board layouts, Heroic and Epic play styles, alternate victory goals, combine to produce a game playable time after time.

description:
Purgatory is a place souls go after physical death before Ascending to Heaven or Descending to Hell. In this Game, Angels and/or Demons are assigned the task to oversee the Souls and assist them on a final journey one way or the other. In some cases, multiple Angels or Demons are assigned. In these cases, they compete to see who can influence the souls better and win the favor of their father.

Angels Ascend Souls to Heaven. Demons Descend Souls to Hell. Also, Souls may be influenced to move toward the light or away from the light. Some actions require or gain grace or cruelty. After each round, Pure Souls move toward the light and Corrupted Souls move away from the light.

Souls vary in value depending on the Soul and if the player is an Angel or Demon. A player wins by gaining the most points after one of several Game Ending conditions are met. It is possible all players lose if Souls are not managed properly.

A free fantasy wargame released by designer Alan Emrich for use in his Game Design course. The full game download is available on his website.

From the rulebook:

The Scourging of the North was a campaign of retaliation by the Alliance of western Men and Elves against the Goblins from the Northern Wastes. This was a war intended to break the Goblin army and scatter it forever.

Aimed at the great Goblin citadel of Angmar, with the northern snows fast approaching, whoever controlled that foul fortress would be in a strong position to survive the winter intact and finish the campaign of annihilation in the spring. The Alliance came very close to seizing Angmar, but a rallying of Goblins from across the Northern Waste to its defenses overcame the forces of the West.

Battle for Angmar is played on a map of the desolate Goblin lands where the 3-day battle was fought. It uses playing pieces that represent the military units that partici-pated in this campaign. Each player is a leader commanding an army of troops. The individual Alliance ground units represent approximately 250 Men or Elves and what was left of their equipment after the long march across the southern regions of Middle Earth. The Goblin units represent approximately 400 Goblin warriors each.

General course of play:

Players alternate turns moving units and attacking enemy units in order to achieve the victory condition of controlling the city of Angmar at the end of turn 7.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Bronze Dragon Expansion Pack
description: The Adult Bronze Dragon Quelindritar is the competitive prize for Month 3 of the Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, Tyranny of Dragons. The prize includes one pre-painted miniature and numerous upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Solar Angel Expansion Pack
description: The Solar Angel Auroniel is the competitive prize for Month 6 of the Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing Organized Play Tournament, Tyranny of Dragons. The prize includes one pre-painted miniature and numerous upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

description: Santa Cruz is a simulation Wargame of WWII Carrier Combat, focusing on Japanese and United States forces in late October 1942. The game has both basic and advanced rules.

The Cat Box is both a card expansion for Box O'Cats or game of its own. The Cat Box game play is cat situations and cat-related items taken to the extreme. Can you placate your new catlike alien masters? Can you get a cat out of a tree using some cattails or a catapult? What if WE were shrunk to the size of cats? What if dogs and cats started a war? Can you solve these dilemmas with the items on the cards you hold in your hands?

Investing is designed to teach potential investors where is the best place to keep one's money under various economic conditions.

The game's main equipment is a standard fifty-two card deck. Each rank has been assigned a different type of investment. For example: aces represent retail stocks and tens represent silver. Each player gets five Business Trend Cards which let the players know each investment's value during the five possible economic conditions: recession, stability, depression, stagflation, and inflation.

To play the game, poker chips are distributed among the players equally. A die is rolled to determine the economic condition for this hand. Players proceed to play Draw Poker using the appropriate Business Trend Card to determine the value of one's own hand.

The instruction booklet gives the rules for four other variants of poker but all other poker variants could be played using this deck.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Zoo Webs is a card game built around the rich world of wild animals that offers a variety of ways to play. It develops problem solving skills in a way that is accessible to preschoolers, enjoyable to kids, but is also challenging to parents.

On each card is an animal species and on each side of the square are that animal's: Classification, Diet, Conservation Status (from the IUCN) and World Region. These will be used to match cards that are played in the game. Depending on the game style chosen, a player's cards may be face-down or held. Depending on the number of cards in the final version, it could range from at most 5 to 8 players.

The challenge of each game style is to make connections. Only cards that have a link (a correct match) can be played off of another. When the game area begins to get larger it, playing cards may require more than one side to match. Every side of the card must match. In some games there are rewards for playing cards with 2, 3, or 4 connections (a double, triple or quadruple link).

The goal of each game style is to play all of your cards first.

description:
A print and play variant of the classic game Pit! Designed to be used for 4 or 5 small groups as a warm up activity for an event promoting fair trade.

The game is played in four or five small teams of 3-5 members. 
Jigsaw-style pieces of bananas with pictures of banana producers around the world plus letters spelling out the word FairTrade are shuffled and distributed evenly. Teams then have to swap pieces with other teams (always one for one, two for two etc) to collect all the pieces with the same picture. However, trading is done by shouting the number of pieces you want to swap. You only know how many pieces you are getting, not what picture is on them. Teams then have to assemble the pieces into a complete banana. Whichever team does this first wins.

Pets are awesome companions that join you on your quest for Pixel Glory! Pets stay by your side every turn to help you slay monsters in the dungeon. You acquire a pet in the town/drafting phase by choosing to draft one instead of a spell. Before the town/drafting phase, one pet for each player is shuffled into the spell deck - players may only use one pet.

This expansion is exclusive to Kickstarter and conventions.

Base-game reminder:
Pixel Glory is a fast-paced dungeon crawling card game for two to four players that plays in 20 minutes and takes five minutes to learn. Team up with fellow wizards to vanquish the sinister monsters that lurk in the dungeon, and save the nearby village. This is a noble and honourable quest, and... oh, who are we kidding? In reality, you're a greedy wizard who is all about fame and personal gain, and your fellow wizards are cut from the same cloth! Prevail over your opponents by learning the best spells and using that knowledge to out-manoeuvre and kill-steal in the dungeon, so you can gain admiration of the townsfolk, fame across the realm, and achieve Pixel Glory.

description:
Warband provides all the rules you need to bring great battles between fantasy armies to your tabletop.

Using miniature figures and terrain, you can fight battles from the glorious sword and sorcery epics you've read about.  
Miniatures are based on 10x5cm bases, allowing you to create miniature vignettes or dioramas for each unit.

Warband is designed to be simple to learn and difficult to master, and so it uses a single consistent task resolution method throughout the rules.  Whether you’re trying to get a unit of warriors to move, seeing how effective it’s shooting or fighting is, or how well it has defended itself, you’ll be using the same dice pool mechanism.  You determine the number of dice to roll and each result of 4+ is a success.  The more successes you roll, the better your units will do, and the more likely you are to win.  Simple!

Of course fighting a good tactical battle, getting your units into advantageous situations, and making effective command decisions will mean you’ll be far more likely to win.  Coming up with effective tactics for your chosen army to defeat your opponents is what will take you time to master.

So with Warband’s intuitive rules you’ll be fighting the battle not the rulebook, and hopefully having a lot of fun into the bargain!

You'll be able to decide which of the fantastic races you'd like to command and then construct your force from the lists provided.  
Warband includes complete army lists for twelve armies, with abilities, options, spell lists, and descriptions including; 
Barbarian men, Dark elves, Dragon men, Eldritch vampires, Goblins, High elves, Hill dwarves, Lizardmen, Samurai apes, Taurians, Undead, and Wood elves.  Each army is balanced by being constructed from a fixed 500 point cost.  Powerful units cost more so are rarer.  A typical warband will have 10-12 units, representing an army of maybe 3000 warriors.

Army composition and optional upgrades are highly flexible, allowing you to build the force that best fits your style of play.  If you'd like to command a horde of bison riding barbarians, rampaging minotaurs, skirmishing ghouls, or serried ranks of deadly elven archers then you'll find something to suit you in the Warband rules.  This flexibility means that you should be able to play with your favourite fantasy race in a way that suits your personal style and preferences, while your force still retains a unique and interesting character.

Elemental dice is inspired by the classical elements of Earth,Water,Fire and Air. This five dice game comes with rules for 2 different games; Elemental Battle (played with 2 dice) and Elemental Bind*.
---
Elemental Battle: Each player Taket 2 dice. One spare dice is left aside. Each player throws one dice in turn. the strongest element thrown wins and the winning player retains both dice.
Players continue to throw until one player is out of dice and must pick up the spare. The winner is the player who takes all of the dice.If at any stage players get the same result the dice must be rolled again.
---
Elemental Bind*:
The first player throws all the dice, trying to get the best result according to the chart or they may pick only 2 or 3 dice and throw the again to increase the score. Extra scoring rules are adding different ways to gain points giving to Elemental Dice a touch of strategy.
Both games are using the Elements value chart provided: 1)Creation(strongest) - 2)Earth - 3)Water - 4)Fire - 5)Air - 6)Time(weakest)and rolling scoring chart: 5 x creation - 5 x Air - 5 x Water etc.
If there are only two players, the winner is the best out of three rounds.

User summary:
Soft Fixers is a deck- building game that includes an identity-hiding mechanism. The game is quick and plays in around seven minutes.

Each player shall play one of the 6 Fixers, who dream of world domination. Players buy cards from the fixed supply of 6 cards, and try to either fulfill the victory condition or eject all the other players from the game. If your identity is revealed by an Attack, you are out of the game.

Publisher's summary:
正体隠匿システムを複合させた7分で完了するデッキ構築ゲーム。
プレイヤーは世界を自分の望むようにしたい6人のフィクサーの1人となり
6種のカードによる固定サプライを購入しつつ
自分の勝利条件を満たすか、他人を全員脱落させることを目指す。
ATTACKにより自分の正体が暴かれるとゲームから脱落させられてしまう。

Backing Zong Shi will allow you to acquire a number of exclusive game components that will only be available for this Kickstarter project. The first exclusive component is a representation of this project within the game, so you can help Zong Shi succeed both in real life and in the game!  In addition to the project card you can also receive two additional Scrolls of Fortune not found in the game.

Both the project card and the Scroll of Fortune cards are exclusive to this Kickstarter Project and will never be reprinted again for any purpose.

Welcome to the RoboDrome. Test your skills as you control your robot in the premier robot battle arena. Make use of all your tools and wits to out think and out maneuver your opponent. Two bots enter, one bot leaves.

RoboDrome is a microgame for two players that uses elements of programming, hand management, and strategic movement to maneuver around the board and damage their opponent with a variety of special weapons.

boardgame: Party Time: The Game That's as Fun as You Are
description:
Description of Party Time:

Party Time is the perfect party game - test your friends with wacky prompts and wild challenges in this new twist on truth or dare. Party Time is easy to play, interactive, works well for all group sizes and is never the same game twice!

Some examples of challenges are:


    Truth or Dare
    Kiss the person across from you
    Take a mystery shot
    Keep in physical contact with the person on your left for the rest of the game
    Shotgun a beer with a friend
    Exchange an article of clothing with the person on your right
    Share your last five Google searches
    Give out superlatives to all players

boardgame: Schlock Mercenary: Kickstarter promotional items pack
description:
Includes:


    Tagon Dice: 5 custom dice that includes Tagon's logo in place of the 6.
    Extra Character Sheet + Token: Andy, our multi-limbed friend.  He has a lot of actions.  This is an advanced copy of the character that will be included in a future release, but you get to play with him early.

    Dice Cup + Punchboard
    Wooden discs for the characters in addition to the normal chipboard tokens
    Additional chipboard tokens featuring a number of characters with their heads in the Nanny Jars. Sure to be a hit when push comes to shove comes to knife comes to pistol comes to plasma cannon comes to crippling injury with horrific pain.

Κατάκτηση (Conquest) is a wargame very much alike to Stratego.

The gameboard is a 3D battlefield. You have an army of men at your disposal and your mission is to win your opponent's army.

boardgame: Firestorm Planetfall: Battle for Proteus Prime
description: Battle for Proteus Prime is an action-packed two player game set in the exciting Firestorm Planetfall Galaxy. Discovery of a hidden technology has turned the surface of the long-abandoned world of Proteus Prime into a deadly warzone as the Aquan Prime and Directorate battle for victory.

boardgame: Firestorm Armada: Stormzone – Return of the Overseers
description:
This exhilarating Firestorm Armada Battle Box brings two stunningly detailed fleets to the gaming table: the fast and nimble Aquan Prime and the stealthy mega-corporation that is the Directorate.

Locked in a deadly conflict above the planet of Proteus Prime, the Commanders of these great fleets must not only do battle with each other, but face the unknown mystery of the giant Dimensional Gate in orbit above the planet and the strange probe ship that has emerged from it. An epic battle is taking place above the planet of Proteus Prime. So gather your forces, Commanders... it is time for war!

Firestorm Armada is a fast paced, exciting tabletop game of spaceship combat set in a deadly, distant future.

description:
Κόντρα στο χρόνο (Against Time) is a party game that requires a touch of knowledge but mainly quick thinking!

Every player in his turn rolls a dice and depending on where his pawn goes, he picks a card. For example he picks a card that requires him to note as many phytophagous animals starting from different letters as he can in 30 seconds. The next player has 60 seconds to write phytophagous animals, starting from different letters to the ones used by the first player.

Players get points going around the board and after a defined time the game is over and the player with the most points wins.

Codename Iron Scorpion is designed to be a self-contained game that packs a box full of fabulous models and tons of excitement. It is a perfect spring board for players wishing to immerse themselves in the Dystopian Legions Universe.

This boxed set is crammed with all you need to get to grips with the new Dystopian Legions 2.0 rules engine.

Animalia Fighting PCG is a free print&play card game for 2 players.

In the game,you take the role of a leader of an animal society. You have to lead your animals to beat your opponent.

Try to let the opponent's foods more than six to win the game. But be careful! Food is resource. The more food the more powerful. Your opponent can use more food to call his animal fight back.

A series composed of seven packs.
The first pack in the series is basic Pack.The Basic pack includes 31 kinds of animals.Another six packs includes 14 kinds of animals.

Now,game has published the first series.

Σχέδιο & Παντομίμα (Design & Pantomime) is a party game that combines pictionary and pantomime!

Players are divided in two or more teams of at least two players each. Teams are tested in two tasks with time limits:
For the first one, a player is drawing in order to make his teammates find what he is drawing. In the same manner for the second one a player is acting without making any sounds, so that his teammates find the right answer!

A lot of fun and competitiveness comes through playing.

Set in the waters around South America, Operation Shadow Hunter pits two bitter enemies against each other: the Empire of the Blazing Sun and the Federated States of America.

In territory that once belonged to the Republic of Argentina, the forces of the Empire of the Blazing Sun are once again on the move. Their plan is complex, their overall objective still a secret, but the dice have already been rolled and the battle lines drawn. The eastern forces have a new and deadly ally. The infamous Wani have returned from exile, contributing never-before-seen technology to seal their new pact of service with the Empire.

To the north, the Federated States of America has responded with speed to the loss of its own vessels, both military and civilian. In a bid to counter the mysterious 'Ghost Ship' the chief scientists of this great nation have accelerated development of their own secret weapon - the Boston Class Submersible - and the American Admirals are eager to prove it in battle. Designed to engage and destroy even the most formidable enemy ships, the Boston Class comes armed with the latest advanced weaponry and a complement of Assault Subs.

The scene is set for lethal conflict as two of the deadliest naval forces on the planet clash in the once calm waters off the coast of Brazil.

Dare you take command in Operation Shadow Hunter?

The game contains 300 cards - crosswords. It' s something between battleship and crossword puzzle, where you guess letters and words of your opponent to solve the crossword.
Highly competitive game which combines knowledge with brightness!

In a fantasy world ravaged by warfare, warring factions clash in a primal struggle for domination. Huge warbeasts stride into battle, tearing enemies apart with tooth and claw, while epic warlocks smite their enemies with ancient spells - in this bitter age of fighting, who will emerge victorious? 
 The Legion of Everblight inhabit the harsh frozen northern wastes, and have descended upon Immoren - tainting the lands with corruption. The Druids of the Circle Orboros clash with the forces of Everblight in an attempt to halt their spread. 
 The seemingly unstoppable Kaya the Wildborne leads the Orboros attack, accompanied by her loyal pack of warbeasts - while Lylyth, the Herald of Everblight has called upon her savage Dragonspawn to help her repel this dark foe.

Famous Missions is a party game where players put together their best team of famous (and sometimes infamous) personalities to handle the mission at hand.

You need to dismantle a bomb, QUICKLY! You look around and see George Lucas, Donald Trump, Oprah, Gene Simmons, Danny DeVito, Joan of Arc and Shaquille O'Neal. Who will you choose?

Choose 3 and defend your choice. You'll need to argue for them later.

Like its predecessor Last Mouse Lost, the playing area of Go Bong! consists of a rubber grid with raised "bumps" that can be pushed down with your fingers. Once all of the bumps have been pressed, you can flip the board over and play again on the other side.

To play, two players alternate turns pressing down one or more bumps in the grid, with these bumps needing to be in the same horizontal, vertical or diagonal row. Whoever pushes down the final bump loses.

Go Bong! includes solitaire challenges.

You are a dwarf, and tasked with building track for a mine cart that has already left its starting point and is rushing fast to its destination.

Your team of fellow dwarves will attempt to assist you in this endeavor, however the task is not proving as easy as you'd hoped...

In Mine Cart, players will cooperatively lay down cards as track for a runaway mine cart.  Cards can path off in any direction, limited only by your table space, but must match connecting symbols.  These symbols also grant one time special actions, some good, some bad.  No matter what one action you choose on your turn (draw, place, take track from back on line) the mine cart always moves one space further.  The color of the space it lands on also causes 'something' to happen, so careful as you lay your track, giving lots of choices...

Players cooperatively lay track until the last track (last card on bottom of deck) is placed, however the dwarf with the most vp left in his hand of track is declared the master track builder and receives the promotion from his boss.

This game is an entry for the geekway to the west design contest.  And while it won't win, i'm just blessed to be in a cool contest with such an amazing and awesome community that supports each other and is growing every day.  Saint louis!!!

FlipFight is a series of strategic manual dexterity micro-games. Battle of Gettysburg is the first game in the FlipFight series. The game pits Union and Confederate forces against each other in the pivotal Battle of Gettysburg during the American Civil War.

The players assume the roles of field commanders during the battle with access to different cavalry and infantry units. At the beginning of each game the battlefield is dynamically generated from a deck of key strategic locations -- from Round Top and the Devil's Den to Cemetery Hill and Seminary Ridge -- ensuring no two games will be the same.

On their turns, players select a combat unit from their reserves and flip it onto the battlefield in an attempt to seize control of a location or capture enemy forces to gain victory points. Infantry and cavalry units are not only different shapes, each has distinct strengths -- capturing a location featuring High Ground with infantry allows the player to perform an artillery strike, while cavalry can capture enemy infantry forces and remain in field.

Each strategic location has different values representing how difficult they were to control (units needed), and how valuable they were to each side's efforts (victory points). Combat units have varying levels of effectiveness, representing the makeup of their forces and the quality of their leadership.

The first player to earn 10 Victory Points through control of key locations or captured enemy forces wins the game!

description:
The cards in Smiley Dice Game show 1-4 smileys in five different colors, while the five dice show six different smileys (with the red smiley not being on any card).

During your turn, first secure any cards in front of you that weren't stolen, then roll the dice and place at least one die on an open card, either one in front of an opponent or one from the cards in the general play area. (Fifteen of the thirty cards are used in a single game.) Then either take those cards and place them in front of you — if enough corresponding dice are on the card — and end your turn, or roll again; if you cannot place at least one die on your turn, don't take any cards and give the dice to your left neighbor.

Once all the cards have been claimed, whoever has the most smileys wins.

Each turn in Smiley Action Game, someone turns over one of the 52 cards, then everyone checks which green and red smileys are visible. Grab the cards where these smileys are, but do not take the card if smileys from the red side are on them.

The player with most cards in the end wins.

boardgame: Hats On: The Jet Set Name Game
description:
Geographical guessing game.
Take turns to pull ticket from the hat and guess correctly who, what or where in the world you are to get the ticket as a point. 
Collect the most tickets to win

The set contains 8 card hats and 120 tickets.

A presidential election game that tracks elections from 1960 to 2012.  Republicans fight Democrats for control of the White House and advancing their agendas.

Players run the gauntlet of primaries using chits and dice to resolve debates, scandals and all the pitfalls that come in American politics to determine who will represent their party in a national election.  Your goal is to somehow get the best nominee through the process and win the general election.

The dying King is a shrewd man with more gold than compassion. Unable to choose between his two offspring whom to name as his successor, the King devised a challenge, bequeathing to them small Kingdoms in the far corners of his vast territory.

Who will earn the right to the throne? Only the most clever will succeed either by building an impenetrable fortress or eliminating the competition.

Objective:  The winner is the first to either have a knight outside the forest that cannot be captured or be the only player with a knight on the board outside the forest.

Play: On their turn, each player will place, move, or flip pieces on the game board.  Player options come from the pieces not-currently-on-the-board, meaning greater options are available to those who have fewer pieces in play.

boardgame: How to Train Your Dragon 2: Sheep Launcher Game
description: How To Train Your Dragon 2: Sheep Launcher Game is a game for 2-4 players. Players place plastic, disc-shaped sheep tokens onto little plastic catapults, which they use to launch the tokens into nets located on a stand erected on the center of the board. Each player moves their dragon marker forward by the number of points scored each shooting round. Double points are scored with the black sheep token, or by shooting a token into your own net (matched to your catapult color). The first person to get their dragon marker around the board first wins the game.
