Game description from the publisher:
10 new Robot cards for the Free Agent Deck introducing two new Immediate Actions: Cloning and Gambler.

Card numbers RH-161 to RH-170

Game description from the publisher:
10 new Cyborg cards for the Free Agent Deck introducing 1 new pitch and new versions of some of the pitches in the base set.

Cards CP-171 to CP-180

boardgame: Baseball Highlights: 2045 – Naturals & Magna Glove Expansion
description:
Game description from the publisher:
10 new Natural cards for the Free Agent Deck in Baseball Highlights 2045, featuring the Magna Glove and Teamwork Actions.

Cards NM-151 to NM-160

Šumska slagalica (Forest puzzle) is game for younger players. On the board is presented forest with many paths. Along the paths are parts of 5 different mini puzzles. Players roll the dice and move figurines along the paths. When figurine comes to marked field that is next to piece of puzzle - player takes the part. The player needs to bring figurine to the place on board where puzzle is assembled. After that, the player moves the figurine to another part of puzzle ... The winner is the player who first successfully compile puzzle.

The game can be played on way that is not competitive. Here all the players help each other in assembling the puzzle.

Swing State DICE  is a free, downloadable 2-player game that simulates a US presidential election. One player is the Republican candidate, the other is the Democrat, and they manage resources (votes and funds) to compete for the 12 swing states.

Each round, a player will roll the dice, which will gain him votes and funding. The votes can be invested in more staff (dice) in future rounds, or can be used to win the elections in the states. Funding is needed to roll the dice, but can also be used to gain various upgrades and one-time rewards.

Download the 2-page PDF for free here
https://kanegames.wordpress.com/

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – HMS Orpheus 1780 / HMS Amphion 1780
description:
Publisher Blurb:

HMS Orpheus was a 32-gun fifth rate frigate of the Royal Navy. She was launched in 1780, and served for more than a quarter of a century, participating in numerous actions, mostly in the East Indies against French and Dutch ships, and later in the North Sea and Jamaica. She was wrecked in 1807 sailing toward Port Royal. This ship pack can also be used to represent the British Amazon frigate "HMS Amphion 1780". Each Sails of Glory Ship Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other Sails of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the ship: a special base with gaming stats, a ship log and a specific deck of maneuver cards. The ship base, ship card and ship log are printed on two sides - The game data of a second ship is printed on the back. This is not a complete game. You must own a Sails of Glory Starter Set to play.

boardgame: Conflict of Heroes: Awakening the Bear – Firefight Generator
description:
Conflict of Heroes: Awakening the Bear – Firefight Generator builds on our award-winning Conflict of Heroes series for a whole new experience. Players use strategy and cunning to manipulate the battlefield and available forces to create an engagement set to their advantage. This expansion for Awakening the Bear 2nd Edition includes everything you need to create countless Firefights, from solo Firefights that make use of Awakening the Bear - Solo Expansion to giant, multi-map, four-player battles.

To create a unique new Firefight, players alternate playing Generator Cards to choose their units, Victory Point conditions, weapons, weather conditions, and more. Players can bluff, outmaneuver, and thwart their opponents... all before the Firefight even begins! Successful commanders will make smart and strategic choices by allocating their limited resources to set themselves up for victory. This is a groundbreaking new system that is challenging and fun to play! Create a 1 - 4 player Firefight in under 10 minutes!

Includes: Soviet and German Generator Card Decks,Option Record Boards, and Rule Book with Mission Guidelines. 
Special Addition: Solo Mission Generation System, including  the Solo Mission Sheet and Artificial Intelligence Tiles!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The All-Seeing Eye is a speed-based card game featuring simultaneous play and symbol-recognition for two to four players.

Players are trying to make points by slapping matches as fast as possible. Players can only hold one card in each hand and play to three piles in the center of the table. They may discard cards from either hand to their discard pile. If Players do not have cards in their hand, they may slap the center three piles if they see a match. A match may be made by slapping three of the following combinations: three of the same color of top symbol, any three same parts of a symbol regardless of color, and the whole symbol matching regardless of color. Slapped matches or missed matches are removed from the board quickly and play is continued. Color matches score one point, part symbol matches score two points, whole symbol matches score five, and missed matches are scored minus two.

Tower Babies is a 30-minute hex tower defense board game for 2 players.

STORY

The monster baby parents have left for the weekend and the babies need food and hugs. Smelling porridge and comfy beds, they head to the village. 
In the village, the grown ups won’t wake up! The babies wake each other up and grab their parents armor and head to the wall.

ACCESSIBILITY

Tower Babies is a fantasy themed board game for ages 8 and up.

GOALS

Tower player must defeat all monsters. Monster player wins if one monster reaches the end.

SETUP

Tower player begins by placing the ₩ wall pattern piece. Place the walls so that they do not prevent a path from the starting to ending spot.
After Tower player has placed a wall, Monster player rolls a d4 and places that many paths. Walls may not be placed on paths.
Tower player places a wall from wall pattern ₩ to pattern ¥ to pattern £ and back to pattern ₩. Monster player rolls a d4 and places paths until the path reaches the ending spot.

PLAY

After the path has reached the end, Tower player places a tower on a wall that is not within the starting zone.
Monster player spawns a monster.
Towers may fire or reload. Then, place a tower until a maximum of five towers is reached.
Tower player may move towers that can move.
Monster player moves monsters up to their full movement as long as they do not block the starting spot. 
Monster player spawns a monster.
Repeat these steps until the sixth monster is to be placed. Monster player may now play a monster and the boss monster. No more monsters are spawned. 
Play continues until all monsters are killed or one monster reaches the end of the path.

boardgame: Wild West Exodus: Unfinished Business – Confederate Rebellion Faction Bundle
description:
Wild West Exodus is a table top miniatures game set in a sci-fi filled 1800's America. Technology and greed rule the old west. Wild West Exodus: Unfinished Business introduces 4 new factions to the game; The Confederate Rebellion, Dark Nation, Golden Army, and The Watchers.

About The Confederate Rebellion:
The Confederate Rebellion army has seen death and destruction ravage their states.  Beaten by the Union during the Civil War, the leaders of the Confederate States of America have abolished slavery and joined forces with all men and women of the South.  Their fight against a stronger and more advanced enemy has ignited a strong love of state and government.  The CSA generals fight side by side with their men and want nothing but the complete destruction of the Union army.  They use surprise assaults and gorilla warfare to even the odds against much more powerful foes.  Their men and women are supplied by Dr. Carpathian Industries allowing access to deadly and corrupt weapons.  Rumors of Voodoo magic and the undead fighting alongside Rebel troops have been whispered about for years.
Wild West Exodus is a Sci-Fi Wild West Miniatures Game set in an alternate history wild west. Strange technologies corrupt the land and death lurks at every corner. RJ-1027, the infamous power source created by Dr. Burson Carpathian is the catalyst behind the constantly escalating arms race between the factions that make up Wild West Exodus.

About Wild West Exodus:
Wild West Exodus is a miniatures game played by 2 to 4 players in control of the faction of their choice.  In the world of WWX, every model has the potential to be a hero or a villain.  The game mechanics and rule set feature exciting game play from the first dice roll.  Each player will be allowed to field a posse of 6 to 60 models or more.  The WWX rules have been designed to allow for scaling of the game to high or low model counts without causing rules issues.

One of the most exciting parts of the Wild West Exodus game mechanic is the reusable influence pool.  The influence pool will give players the option to gamble away a set amount of tokens in return for a reroll of the dice.  Be careful what you gamble on because once the tokens are committed they are lost for that turn.  The D10 system gives the game life and options that round out easily and allow for quick decision making.

The Halo that each model possesses creates an area of assault around the miniature allowing for fast and deadly close combat.  This helps move the game along quickly and gives each fight a more cinematic feel.

Wild West Exodus also benefits from an alternating 1-3 model activation system.  This system lets both players choose the most strategic way of advancing their characters across the battle field.  This means that you never have to wait more than a minute or two before taking control of your own posse for your next activation.

Each posse consists of a structured army list.  All factions will have 1 Boss model, 0 to 4 Underboss models, 0 to 8 Sidekick models, and an endless amount of Hired Hands.  Heavy and Light Support vehicles also make up an army list.

This really gives the gamer the option to field a force led by one mighty general commanding a horde of troops or the complete opposite.

Some gamers may choose to load up on skilled Sidekicks and Underbosses or go heavy on Hired Hands for a true horde army.

No matter what option you take, the world of Wild West Exodus will engulf you in fun and excitement from your very first game.

Naruto Sugoroku is a roll-and-move game that was included as a tear-out insert in the Shonen Jump Naruto Collector magazine for Winter 2007/2008.  This game is loosely based on the traditional Chinese/Japanese game Sugoroku.

In this game, players choose a character from the Naruto manga and then roll a die to move their characters along a track.  Some spaces have instructions that only affect one character--other characters may ignore the instructions.  If two players land on the same space, they play rock-paper-scissors, with the loser moving back one space.  Various "stage" spaces along the way allow a player to roll a die and move all other players back that number of spaces or to jump ahead is a one a rolled twice consecutively.  Certain events can put a player into a "passive state."  While in a passive state, players are protected from most negative effects.  The last part of the path splits so each character has their own individual track.  Players must roll the exact number to land on the ending space, and must move back any excess spaces.  First player to arrive there wins.

boardgame: Wild West Exodus: Female Dark Council miniature
description: This figure is a Kickstarter exclusive miniature. It was included at most of the pledge levels during the 2015 Kickstarter campaign for Wild West Exodus: Unfinished Business - Faction Bundles.

In Make It! Animal Babies, players compete to assemble a three-dimensional cardboard animal.  Each player chooses an animal and takes the five pieces (head, body, forelegs, hind legs and tail).  The body parts are depicted tiles, which are laid out face-down.  Players take turns choosing a tile.  If it depicts a part of their animal, they take that piece, add it to their animal and discard the tile.  If it is not a part of their animal, they return it to the same spot face-down.  The first player to completely assemble their animal wins.

From the box:
"In Suitcase Scramble, Paddington must go through the Brown's house to fill his suitcase so he can accompany Mr. Gruber on an exciting trip to France!  You must make sure he packs all of his useful items. . .  Paddington has to be quick because Mr. Gruber is in a big hurry.  Be careful that he doesn't lose any of his items while he packs.  The first player to fill Paddington's suitcase with all 10 items wins the game."

Each player has flat cardboard suitcase marked with ten items Paddington must pack in his suitcase. On their turn, they spin a spinner and move along a track. Each space is marked one of Paddington's ten items. If they need it, they may take a marker with the that item and cover its space on their suitcase. Some spaces allow the player to take a marker of their choice. Players circle the track until they have collected all their items. The first player to fill their suitcase and reach the "bon voyage" space wins.

The Ring of the Nibelung is a micro-game (18 cards) for 2-4 players. Each player starts with 4 cards that will be used to try to find and steal the cursed ring. That is unless you already have the ring - which then means you will have to work to try to play the ring as soon as you can. The player who successfully plays The Ring Card wins the game.

High Frontier Lite is a streamlined version of the game High Frontier. The actions and movement in the game are simplified, but the game also uses new mechanics, simulating the planets orbits. The aim of the game is to take control of different planets in the solar system.

boardgame: Metropolis Rescue Mission: The Chase Game
description:
From the box:
"Superman has a new mission of stopping the falling meteorite from striking the city of Metropolis.  Superman gets information that the falling meteorite is heading towards Metropolis and is targeting the Daily Planet office (where he works as a reporter.)  On his way to destroy the meteorite, Superman encounters three deadly villains and has to knock them down and collect the tokens to destroy the meteorite with all his new super powers."

Metropolis Rescue Mission is a roll-and-move game where players move around a track, trying to reach the end and destroy the meteorite.  Along the way, they may land on spaces that force them to defeat villains as well as other spaces that will grant them extra powers. "Diversion" tracks and transportation spaces allow players to shorten the track and avoid some villains.  The first player to the finish wins.

Division War is a version of the classic card game War with a twist, designed to teach division facts.  Players are dealt an equal number of the 86 cards showing division problems.  Like in war, each player then turns over a card.  They must then solve the division problem displayed on the card.  The player with the highest answer wins and takes all the cards.  If a player's answer is wrong, he or she gives her card to the player to the right.  A wild card automatically gains all the cards in that round for the player who plays it.  Like in wars, equal answers result in a "war" where players play two face-down cards and the an face-up card, and the winner wins all the cards.  Players play a predetermined number of times through the deck or until one player has all the cards.

A solitary game about commanding a US Army field hospital during the Vietnam War. The game features a resources management aspect in the building of the base and its staff as an effective task force, plus a detailed handling of casualties according to their medial triage level, having to be treated in different medical modules.

The game prototype is currently playable as print-and-play or using a Vassal Game Engine Module (see Files section). The Vassal Module supports an Observer player for a tutorial session or online shared play.

During the game, the player will recruit new personnel for the base, also improving its installations. The game engine will challenge the player with events and the need to fulfill missions to score points. The scarcity of resources will test your judgement as the base commander, having to prioritize, push-your-luck and even to make hard sacrifices.

From the rulebook:

Nha Trang, Vietnam. November 1967. Orders come from Long Binh HQ.
You are assigned to 498th Medical Company, 55th Medical Group, 44th Medical Brigade, as Battalion Surgeon with the rank of Captain (O-3). Your duty is to set up and manage a Mobile Army Surgical Hospital (MASH), to be deployed as part of the Counteroffensive Phase III Campaign.
In order to do so, you will be recruiting Personnel (specialist operatives and general personnel), and commissioning new buildings and vehicles (installations). With those assets, you will face threats to your facilities (events) and be called upon to participate in military operations (missions).
Sometimes you will be ordered to take wounded people into treatment in your Base. Wounded people will also flow your way as a result of enemy opposition during missions, and sometimes in the case of security, health or supplies threats to your Base. Having a poorly managed Base, forfeiting missions, and suffering casualties will reduce your promotion merits. Stay sharp, or your men will suffer and you’ll never make it to Major (O-4).
Taking part on the campaign operations (your combat readiness), along with an overall good security, healthiness and supplies situation in your Base will grant you promotion merits (victory points).
Godspeed, Captain!

boardgame: Wild West Exodus: Unfinished Business – The Watchers Faction Bundle
description:
Wild West Exodus is a table top miniatures game set in a sci-fi filled 1800's America. Technology and greed rule the old west. Wild West Exodus: Unfinished Business introduces 4 new factions to the game; The Confederate Rebellion, Dark Nation, Golden Army, and The Watchers.

About The Watchers:
As the portals once again open on earth, mankind has no idea what is coming.  The destructive power of the Watchers will completely strip the land of all living things.  The Watchers use their Alpha leaders to guide and advance their many species of aliens.  Each and every member of the Watchers knows his role in their society and follow orders without question.  As their enemies attempt to stop the Watchers, the Betas drain the life energy from their bodies and transport it to their Alpha leader.  This transforms the Alpha during the course of any battle into an enemy that knows every move you will make before you even make it.  With inhuman weapons and a thirst for human blood, they plan to wipe mankind off the planet once and for all.

boardgame: Wild West Exodus: Unfinished Business – Golden Army Faction Bundle
description:
Wild West Exodus is a table top miniatures game set in a sci-fi filled 1800's America. Technology and greed rule the old west. 
Wild West Exodus: Unfinished Business introduces 4 new factions to the game; The Confederate Rebellion, Dark Nation, Golden Army, and The Watchers.

About The Golden Army:
The once famous explorer Ponce de Leon and his conquistadors were subjected to a mix of Watcher blood and tropical jungle water.  What they call the fountain of youth has now kept them alive for hundreds of years endlessly searching for the source.  Their influence over the Mexican people and robust army has pushed them to the door step of the country’s boarder.  The men of the Golden Army know of only one thing.  Fight until dead or until the enemy is destroyed.  This horde faction sends endless supply of men to battle.  They are led and protected by the destructive weapons of the conquistadors directing  them from the rear.  They will soon push across the border ready to destroy any army that stands in their way.

About The Dark Nation:
Once a proud and spiritual people, the Warrior Nation has fought long and hard to keep technology out of their lands.  However, the pull on its people has consumed many of their warriors, and the Dark Council has influenced even the strongest of their leaders.  With the corruption spreading like a virus, the shape shifting Warrior Nation men and women become trapped in a half animal half human form left to walk the land as deformed creatures with no home.  Once these proud warriors leave the guiding light of the great spirit, they are doomed to a blood thirsty life of war.  Their beast and human form aide them in their battles against anyone who would dare oppose them.  With weapons and technology at their side, these beasts may never be stopped.

boardgame: Wild West Exodus: H-BOT 44 Mercenary miniature
description: This miniature was included at most of the pledge levels during the 2015 Kickstarter campaign for Wild West Exodus: Unfinished Business - Faction Bundles.

The goal of the game? 
Don't die.

In this fast-paced card game of death, destiny, and deception, players compete for the kingdom in a classic battle of wits. Careful, though! Drink Wine to expand your lead, drink Water to keep your cards, but drink Poison and you'll lose half your hand! Who will live long enough to be crowned the king?

Players play cards and complete the instructions, hoping to avoid drinking a poisoned chalice.  The last person alive wins.

Based on the Hit AMC Television Show The Walking Dead. Over 400 questions will challenge even the most savvy survivors. Players start as humans, but a few wrong answers will get you turned into a Walker!

A splendid action game for young people.

Fourteen perforated sheets are provided and on each are 6 pairs of words which can be separated by tearing along the Perforation which runs down the middle. Each pair consists of related words, e.g., BEE—H1VE, BIRD—NEST, etc.
INSTRUCTIONS TO M.C.
PREPARATION FOR PLAY
Tear each sheet along the perforations so that there are 168 slips in all. The first halves are distinguished from the second halves by the spot in the top right córner of the former.
Distribute the second halves about the house or hall with the printed faces upwards and retain the first halves.
List the players by name or number on a score sheet.
Give each player a “first half” slip.
HOW PLAY PROCEEDS
At the word " GO ” the players set off to find the counterpart of the slips they hold. A player does not know what the word is, but on seeing the words on the “second half” slips he will recognise the one he is looking for by its close connection with the word on the slip he is carrying.
When a player beiieves he has found the correct counterpart he presents the two slips to the M.C.; if correct the M.C. records one point for that player and gives him another slip.
If during the game a player presents an incorrect pair of slips to the M.C. he must resume looking for the correct counterpart and is not given another “first half” slip until he finds it.
OR
If he cannot lócate the counterpart and would prefer to have another “first half” slip, he is entitled to do so but one point is deducted from his score.
THE WINNER is the player who scores the hlghest number of points in an agreed time or when the whole of the slips have been used.
The material in each packet provides for about eight young people. If more are taking part extra perforated sheets should be obtained.
The game can be varied by distributing the first halves and giving the players the second halves.
SOLUTION SHEET
On this are listed the correctly paired words.
N.B.—The pairs of words have been so chosen that there should be little doubt in a player’s mind as to whether he has found the correct counter¬part.

"A clever and interesting game for afternoon and evening parties."

Each guest is given a card and a time limit to resolve the clues. On each card are 32 rhyming couplets. The first line of the rhyme describes one word and the second line describes another word for which the correct answer is found by taking off the first letter of the first word. An example:
It's bright and sparkling, seen when dark.
Daub well your boat ere you embark.
First line = STAR. Second line = TAR

When "Time2 is called, the winner is the guest with the most correct answers.

boardgame: Malifaux: The Swamp Hag – Zoraida Box Set
description: Who do Voodoo? You do Voodoo. Puppet your models or enemy ones, stab your voodoo doll with detrimental conditions to give them to your opponent or play a trickier game and make a doll of your own models to fling long range buffs at them... Zoraida also plays the card cycling game and is a Master Controller. Zoraida is Dual Faction with the Neverborn.
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

boardgame: Malifaux: Hired Swords – Viktoria Box Set
description: Why have one Master when you could have two? Viktoria comes along with her sister Viktoria, one's good at swords and guns, one's good at just swords. Viktorias buff other "sisters", which is normally each other but there are a couple models like Ronin that have the characteristic as well. Picture some twins leaping across a crowded bar, jumping from target to target. There. That's Viks.

boardgame: Malifaux: Hired Guns – Von Schill Box Set
description: For he is the mustache. Von Schill leads his mercenary group; the Freikorps. Their main strength as a whole is they really don't have a weakness. There's no hard counter to Freikorps, they are good at melee, good at shooting, decent Df, good Wp, hardy amount of wounds. And then you have VS tossing out a buff here and there, or just straight up murdering some tough targets and you have a truly fearsome crew. Don't underestimate the power of your opponent not having an easy way to deal with you.

boardgame: Malifaux: The Bayou Boss – Som'er Box Set
description: Somer bring the boys to the yard. Except the yard is a swamp. And the boys are rowdy Gremlins. He walks loudly and still carries a big (boom)stick. He's a bit of everything, he can summon, he can shoot and he can buff. His ability to out activate his opponents with hordes of pigs and expendable gremlins is legendary.

Great men and women are rarely created overnight. They are crafted through years of work and behind each student lies an exceptional teacher. Outwardly, Yamaziko appears to be little more than an old woman, one who might need some help getting across that busy Malifaux street. In truth, she is the master who trained Misaki, gifted daughter of the Ten Thunders' Oyabun, both in fighting and in honor.

Ancient warriors who live by a code of honour, trained masters in the art of death and massive gun toting Samurai.

These warriors belong to the crime family the Ten Thunders, who are a mystery to the residents of Malifaux. Their origins date back to the Three Kingdoms back on Earthside and are shrouded in secrecy and legend, though completely twisted and tainted most of their history is lies. Membership is exclusive to only those of the Three Kingdoms, sacrifice is demanded over capture then to reveal its secrets. Even the Ten Thunders' locally hired thugs do not know how their employers have been able to reach across the Breach.

Party game, "A jolly game for any party".

There are 9 Names' List Cards provided. Place these at suitable distances apart about the room (attach to walls, !f convenient).
Look at the Key Sheet and you will note that a girl's Name heads each list. Now the idea of the game is that the girl is friendly in turn with each of the boys whose names follow and finally marries the young man whose full name is made up of the first letters of the Christian names reading from top to bottom of the list and the wedding date is that at the foot of the list.

By filling in his recording card as instructed below a player is able to make a list exactly the same as that on the key sheet corresponding to a particular girl's name.

Suppose we take the name Barbara. Write Barbara in the first space of a recording card; then look for Barbara in the left columns of the Names Lists and on finding it write in the second space the boy's name opposite in the right column, i.e. Roland. Next find Roland in the left column of a Names List and write in the third space the name opposite, For example - lan. Continue in this way until the date June I2th is found opposite Stuart and entered in the last space alongside the word WHEN.
Now enter the first letters of each name recorded in the column headed "FIRST LETTER" and it should then be fairly easy to divide them into two groups giving a Christian name and surname. Write them in the space alongside the word WHO.

Give each player a recording card and see that he has a pencil.
To begin the game call out one of the girls' names on the Key Sheet and the player who is first to present a correctly completed recording card is the winner.

An alternative way of playing is to write in the first space of each recording card to be used, before the game starts, the name of a girl and at the word " GO " each player completes his card in respect of the girl whose name appears on it.

boardgame: OpenSpace: the game of corporate pawns
description:
OpenSpace: the game of corporate pawns is a board game where players take on a role of corporate employees racing for a promotion and struggling with burnout syndrome during a fast paced gameplay using pawns, cards and a board.

During the gameplay, the actions you take will affect your career and stress levels. You win the game when you reach maximum of your career level before other players do. However, reaching the maximum of stress level, makes you lose immediately.

During each round of the game, you secretly decide whether you will work normally, work hard or relax and have a break. Working hard will get you closer to promotion, but even more close to burnout - that is why sometimes you need to relax during working hours. When relaxing, you are at risk of being denounced to superiors by your fellow players. What is more – each time a denouncing person proves right, his or her career thrives. Still, you can gain an upper hand by using event cards such as air-conditioning breakdown, deadline or annual report.

There are three elements to magic: fire, water, and wood. Each has its weakness. Fire burns Wood, Wood absorbs Water, and Water extinguishes Fire.

Your goal is to become the most powerful wizard by gathering as many Magic Stones of your element as you can, while avoiding the Magic Stones that your element is weak against.

But do not let other wizards know your element, or they will use their magic to thwart you!

"What's your Magic? forces you to conceal your affinity, while trying to deduce what affinity your opponents are.

The wizard with the most points at the end of the game, calculated by [stones in your affinity - stones your affinity is weak against], is the winner.

On your turn you may cast a spell using the stones in your tableau and then you must use a card in your hand to move the fairy that many times around the central decks.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – HMS Zealous 1785 / HMS Superb 1760
description:
From the publisher:

HMS Zealous was an Edgar class ship of the line. Similarly to the Bellona class, the Edgar class was one of the designs by Thomas Slade, using a slightly modified version of the original Arrogant design. Launched on June, 25th 1785, HMS Zealous served in a number of battles of the French Revolutionary Wars and the Napoleonic Wars - notably the Battle of the Nile, where she engaged the French ship Guerriere, helping to force her surrender. This ship pack can also be used to represent the British Bellona Class ship-of-the-line "HMS Superb 1760". Each Sails of Glory Ship Pack is a ready-to-play model, painted and assembled, 100% compatible with any other Sails of Glory game product. In each pack you will find all you need to play with the ship: a special base with gaming stats, a ship log and a specific deck of maneuver cards. The ship base, ship card and ship log are printed on two sides - The game data of a second ship is printed on the back.

A word game, in Catalan, with just 13 letters dice (in different colours, which implies different values). A player chooses between 4 and 8 dice and rolls them (at least two different dice colours must be present). With the result she must find the highest valued word (letters in white dice = 1 point; letters in green dice = 2 points; letters in yellow dice = 3 points; letters in red dice = 4 points). Any dice not used for her word are lost for the next round. The other players also look for the best word.

Once the rolling player has finished with her word, she receives points, while the other players receive points if they have found a higher-valued word. The rolling player keeps rolling (each round with the same dice except for those not used in the previous word) until she has less than four dice. Then is the next player's turn.

Word game, in Catalan, that freely reimplements the hangman game. Each player begins with a certain amount of points and one of the players thinks a word, placing on the table as many "?" counters as letters in the word. Then players take turns to guess a letter in the word; if the guess is incorrect, the player must pay one point. At any time, a player can guess the hidden word: if correct she wins as many points as "?" counters still on the table plus 2; if not, she must pay two points.

This is a cardgame where the cards have a drawing on one side, that depicts a Dutch proverb. On the other side the proverb is listed, along with an explanation, example phrases, and a point value. Each turn a player takes one card (with picture visible) and tries to guess the proverb. If possible the explanation can be given. If a player is right, they get the card/points. Whoever has the most points wins.

In 2014 a second part appeared.
Special Monopoly edition for Peru.

From Ares Magazine issue 2: 
Invasive Species is a game about a crisis aboard the CFS Quicksilver, a Terran Commonwealth Corvette on an exploration mission. On a routine close survey of an unexplored garden planet, the crew comes under Attack by ferocious local fauna. Some of the crew is killed before the crew makes it back to the ship and the ship boosts into orbit.

Invasive Species is a two-player game. One player controls the Human crew of the Quicksilver and the other player controls an alien predator loose aboard the ship. The game is played in a series of turns, each composed of activity taken by the Human crewmembers and the Alien.

This is a set of items for Blood Rage that were only available during the Kickstarter campaign.

Among the exclusives there were four new monsters:

     Wolfman: This monster comes with its figure and a card to add him to your Age 2 deck.
     Mystic Troll: This monster will come with his miniature and a card to add him to your Age 3 deck.
     Mountain Giant: This fearsome monster comes with its figure and card to add to your Age 1 deck. The Mountain Giant is a true behemoth, but since it counts as a Leader, it can Invade for free, and even benefit from Leader Upgrades you add to your clan!
     Fenrir: the giant wolf, son of Loki, comes with its figure and card to add to your Age 1 deck. Whatever province Fenrir is in during the Ragnarök phase is the one that is actually destroyed this Age! (unless he's in Yggdrasil, in which case his ability doesn't take effect)


In addition to the monsters, there were several upgraded components:

     Sculpted Clan Tokens: For each clan, a unique sculpt representing its clan animal. Four tokens are used by each clan to keep track of their current Rage, and their three stats: Rage, Axes, and Horns.
     Sculpted Saga Token: To help keep track of your game's progress through the last Ages of the world. This monolith engraved with Viking runes was designed by Adrian Smith to represent the telling of your Viking saga!
     Sculpted Doom Token: This is used to mark the province that will be destroyed next by Ragnarök, something that bloodthirsty Vikings can forget in the heat of battle. Designed by Adrian Smith, this token depicts the Triple Horn of Odin.
     Sculpted First Player Token: With this token, you'll never forget who's supposed to start the round. It is a hand holding an axe up high.
     Further, an extra variant set of miniatures, the Wild Boar Clan, was included, allowing an expanded choice of clans. This was not, however, a fifth player expansion, as it lacked the needed cards. 


Those who also purchased 5th Player Expansion during the campaign additionally received extra exclusives.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Set of seven promotional cards offered to people who pre-ordered the Cursed Sands and  Depths of the Glarr expansions.

From the publisher's webpage:

Beyond the Namoren swamp, an old dungeon lies hidden beneath the waters. Between crumbling statues of dragons and forgotten heroes, water cascades down the ancient hallways of the labyrinth.
 However this dungeon is not forgotten, nor is it still. Fierce reptilian creatures now call this dark place their home. They prey on merchant barges that pass through their waters. They attack any traveler foolish enough to enter their swamp. They show they mercy. They are...the glarr.
 What riches do they have scurried away to the bottom of their lair? What dark power now drives them? Can you brave the Depths of the Glarr?

Long ago the kingdoms of men rose up against the Dungeon Lord and banished the dark power far across the distant sands. Centuries passed, and the horrors of that time have become stories all too easily forgotten.
 But the darkness of old has returned once again. Undead terrors and titans of sand guard an ancient pyramid that no one dares to enter.
 Something has stirred. Something is coming. What lies beneath the Cursed Sands?

This 6-card set adds new & interesting wrinkles to your Student Bodies game. Included are: 1 rules card, 1 Hallway set-up card, 1 Lab set-up card, 2 Exit set-up cards and an Item card. The new rules and set-up cards provide additional terrain conditions such as: Blood Slick Hazards, Impassable Areas and Fortifications! While the "Tool Belt" Item card allows you to equip an extra weapon.

String Thing is a pen-and-paper dexterity game. Players draw maps with a start point and an end point, and goals and obstacles in between. Then they take a long string with a bead tied to one end, drop the bead at the start point of a map, and slowly lower the string, attempting to draw a line with it from start to end while connecting the goals and avoiding the obstacles. Crossing (or capturing) a goal gives points, while touching an obstacle subtracts points. If a player runs out of string before they reach the end point, they score zero for that map. After playing through several maps, the player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Villainous Vikings (Second Edition): Ernie the Geek Promo
description:
"The bow of his Longship splits the water like the skulls of his countless victims. His reputation precedes him, causing panicked villagers to abandon the town to escape his wrath. From Greenland to Constantinople he is known as the scourge of mankind. Every corner of the known world fears the sight of his vengeful prow. Stepping onto shore, the ground trembles at the might of... Ernie the Geek?"

Expand your crew in this BoardGameGeek exclusive promo pack for Villainous Vikings 2nd Edition. You can now give four different Ernie captains control of your Longship in your continuing quest for gold, glory, and Valhalla. Each of the new Ernie captains gives a unique bonus that makes for a wild, unpredictable game.

Comes with four unique captains based on BGG's beloved mascot, Ernie the Geek.

The Space Base Race is set in the somewhat distant future where asteroid mining is a huge industry. The mega companies involved in the process are fiercely competitive. When a new mining location is discovered, each company sends out a fully robotic mining base. Before mining begins, the companies attack each others bases. When only one base is left standing, the victor gets exclusive rights to reap the benefits.

Players play as one of the companies and try to build their own base while eliminating the competition. The last player with a base still standing wins. The base game mechanism involves laying cards in a grid to form a "base". Each card or "Module" acts differently. The five base cards are the solar panel, the turret, the repair bot, the ray shield and the processor.

The DOME is the game for 2~4 players. With two players, they fight to drop down the opponent's HP to 0 or make opponent's deck out; with 3~4 players, one will be the overlord and sabotage other players.

Each player carries his own deck regarding his choice of character. There are four characters -'Lia Backtrack', 'Jerry Netbus', 'Odic Sue' and 'Yellow Dragon'. Each character's deck is optimized to use their own skills. In his turn, player can choose four actions with 3 AP.

1) Download : Draw up to 2 cards.
2) Resource Distribution : Place up to 2 cards on resource pool.
3) Installation : Play a card from hand after paying its cost. 
4) Cracking : Crack the central server with your own resource.

When player do an action, it can cause the opponent's HP down or force opponent to reduce his own deck. Also some cards already installed can prevent the player from being damaged.

Cracking the central server will give the great benefits to who breaks the security track. So if you want to get that benefits, you must be the first one to break the security than your opponent.

With 3 or 4 players, players will play with 'Mastermind Rule'. One player will be the Mastermind who will use 8 Mastermind cards. These powerful cards allow Mastermind player to make other players in trouble. So they must to cooperate to down the Mastermind. However, Mastermind is also not easy for he has to accumulate enough energies to use mastermind cards.

A fast and fun game of deception and Espionage.
You take on the roles of spies in this simple card game. You are doing all that you can to find out the roles of your opponents and force them out of the game.

You and your friends (or perhaps enemies...) will each receive a single card. On your turn, draw a single card and then you are faced with a simple decision, Keep the old card or keep the new card. The catch is, whatever card you decide to discard will have an effect on the other players and maybe even on yourself. So choose wisely who you trust to discard and even more carefully who you decide to keep around.

In each box are fifteen perforated sheets which provide 150 slips. A letter is printed on each and in all there are 10 of each vowel and 5 of each consonant except Z which is entirely omitted. There are also 3 dice.

INSTRUCTIONS TO THE M.C.
Suppose there are 12 players. Divide them into 4 teams of 3 players and seat them at tables or suitable furniture in groups of four so that at each fable there is a member of each team. A dice is allotted to each table.
Put the vowels (well mixed) in one box and the consonants in another; then place the boxes in a convenient position.
At a starting signal one player at each table throws the dice. If he throws a 6 he gets a vowel, 1 blind from the box as quickly as possible. If he throws 1 or 5 he collects a consonant. Figures 2, 3, 4 do not count. If he throws a useless figure he passes the shaker to the player on his left who shakes and plays accordingly, but if he has thrown a useful number then after collecting a letter he throws again until his is unsuccessful, when he passes the dice on. (This prevents crowding round the letter boxes).

When all the letters are used up, or at the end of an agreed time the 3 members of each team get together and make up as many words as possible (plural nouns not counting) from their letters, bearing in mind the following method of scoring.
A word of 6 letters or more ...    Scores 20 points
A word of 5 letters scores 15 points
A word of 4 letters scores 10 points
A word of 3 letters scores 5 points

A time limit of 5 minutes or so should be fixed for composing. The team scoring the highest number of points is the winner.

boardgame: The Front Nine: AUGUSTUS, The Emerald Blazer Expansion
description:
This year the Royal Golf Association have decided to set some new standards for their annual course design competition. This year their aim is to provide courses with exceptional natural beauty, with character, individuality and personality. Trees, lots of them, some sand, a little water. The land required for such holes will be more expensive but a compliant design will score more highly with the judges.

Eighteen new course cards to be spliced into the original deck. Six new special cards with an emphasis on earning the capital necessary to create such a prestigious course. This first expansion for The Front Nine offers a different and more challenging set of demands for the prospective golf course designer.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Lost Bible is a take on the game Lost Legacy with the story and characters from the game Bible Hunter. It contains two sets of 16 cards: The Dead Seas Scrolls and The Last Supper. It also contains a promo card for the latter, Crucified Christ.

It plays the same way as the previous games in the Lost Legacy family. However, unlike the other sets, the cards in this game may not be mixed and matched with any of the previous sets as they have a different card back bearing the Lost Bible title.

Rules Summary

Lost Bible is a game of risk, deduction, and luck for 2–4 players. You start the game with one card in hand from a deck of sixteen cards. On a turn, you do the following:


     Draw: Draw the top card from the deck and add it to your hand.
     Play: Choose one of the two cards in hand to play and place it face up in front of you.
     Effect: Carry out the played card's effect, after which the card is considered as discarded.
     End: If there is at least one card left in the deck, the turn goes to the next player; if not, the investigation phase starts. Using the investigation speed indicated on the card each player has in hand, players take turns guessing which player (whether yourself or someone else) holds the "Lost Bible" card; this card might also be in the "Ruins", a location that holds one card at the start of the game and possibly acquires more cards during play. The player who guesses correctly wins; if no one finds the "Lost Bible" card, then everybody loses.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Pandánte is a gambling game played by the Pandas of the Pandalands. In this game, lying is encouraged (humans use the softer term “bluffing”). You’ll often have to claim you have a hand that you don’t really have. Be careful though, because other players can call you out for fun and profit.

Pandánte: Light & Dark expansion adds different Light and Dark Panda abilities to the base game for players who want fun and joy or misery and woe! The expansion also adds several extras that can be mixed and matched, including:

Casino Cards:
Each gambit, your group visits a new casino in the Pandalands. Each casino plays the game a little bit differently!

Panda Champions:
Each player takes on the role of a famous Panda. Choose Pandas however you want before the game starts and stick with your Panda throughout the game. Each Panda gives a different special power.

Diamond Dreams:
The blue diamond chips are power ups in this mode. Spend them wisely to get big boosts when you need it.

The Light & Dark abilities, Casinos, Panda Champions, and Diamond Dreams mode options give you 16 possible combinations of game modes. In addition to that, the 17th mode is a seriousface gambling mode. Are you Panda enough to risk real money?

In Zombie Mutation, each player will take the role of one of the six heroes, all fighting to escape the growing Zombie hordes. During the game, players will fight their way through a multi-level 3D game board searching for items that can help them survive, rescuing other survivors and of course killing zombies.

Description from the publisher:

Build the Arena of your dreams and battle against enemies for control of the V'Tar orbs with an all new rule set for the award-winning Mage Wars Arena! Battlegrounds Domination is a new set of expansions for Mage Wars Arena that introduce new rules to play by and incorporate the use of custom puzzle-cut tiles for players to build their own Arena boards to play on! And yes, as you can see, there are images on the tiles that are accompanied by optional rules that introduce terrain effects as well!

The first Battlegrounds expansion, Domination, introduces Domination Mode, where players will vie for control of powerful V’Tar orbs that grant new powers and offer a new option to win, by gaining points through maintaining control of these powerful devices. Additionally, there are 20 customized tiles for players to use in creating their own, or suggested, Arenas to play in. Finally, there are also 116 cards including new spells for use in traditional Mage Wars Arena play and the new Domination mode, introduced in this expansions’ rules. As an added bonus, Battlegrounds Domination introduces two new promo cards, available only in the first printing of the set: The Ankh of Asyra and Blur!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: BattleLore: Second Edition – Razorwings Reinforcement Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Hunt your opponents from above with the Razorwings Reinforcement Pack for BattleLore (Second Edition). These bloodthirsty predators swoop down from their cavernous lair, soar over the battlefield in search of prey, and descend swiftly to brutally slash and tear their victims apart.

This Reinforcement Pack contains three sculpted plastic Razorwing figures,  the cards necessary to muster your Razorwings unit, three new lore cards, and a unique cavern terrain tile. It also features a pre-built scenario, A Shot in the Dark, in which the Daqan forces must traverse hostile territory, fighting against both the Uthuk Y’llan and the ravenous Razorwings in the black of night. Whether you use the Razorwings in this scenario or harness them in other battles, their savage and sudden assaults bring new tactical possibilities and fresh terrors to your games of BattleLore.

Game description from the publisher:

Baseball Highlights: 2045 is like watching TV highlights of early 21st-century baseball games, with the gameplay being full of theme with no outs or innings and without bogging down in a play-by-play baseball simulation. In this quick and interactive game, two players build their teams as they play, combining both strategy (building your team) and tactics (playing the game) without any of the downtime. During each "mini-game", each player alternates playing six cards to simulate a full game's highlights. The mini-game includes defensive and offensive actions, and your single card play may include elements of defensive and/or offensive plays. Do you try to thwart your opponent's pending hits, put up strong offensive action of your own, or use your better players to do both? Players buy new free agents after each mini-game to improve their roster, and the team who wins the most mini-games in the series is the champ!

The deluxe edition of Baseball Highlights: 2045 comes with all five initial expansions (Coaches, Rally Cap, Hitters, Pitchers, Magna Glove) already included.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Wren's Nest is a Colors & Counting micro-game (18 cards) for 2-4 players. Each Nest Card contains 4 distinct colors (Red, Yellow, Green, and Blue) with a varying number of points assigned to each color. Players "build" a 4-card nest using one side of each card. The player with the highest combined total of all 4 cards wins the round and attracts the wren (bird). But be careful! Players can only use each color to score once AND Wild Cards are only useful if you have an odd number.

Blanchette the cow begins the game disassembled. Each round players roll dice and in order to get certain combinations. The winner of each round gets to add a piece to Blanchette. When Blanchette is fully assembled the game ends and the player with the most points gets to make a fart noise using the included "pâte pèteuse".

From our earliest years onward, fairytales have an enormous impact on our lives. They entertain us with the daring deeds of colorful heroes and villains. They inspire us to go on adventures of our own and see the world in whole new ways. And they remind us that no matter how dark life gets, there’s always hope, always a reason to fight for our dreams. Inspired by the fairytale lore we’ve come to love. 12 Realms is a constantly expanding game that offers players the chance to act as fairytale heroes—everyone from Sinbad to King Arthur to Red Riding Hood, Nutcracker, Snow White, Hansel & Gretel, Pinocchio, Robin Hood and beyond—facing down villains and interacting with other lively characters and companions. There the players are tasked with leading their beloved heroes through a magical journey full of excitement and danger to save the Realms from the Dark Lords’ overwhelming hordes. Every hero is represented by a lovingly crafted miniature, already included in the box.
 Bedtime Story continues to build on that legacy and brings four new realms: Tang Dynasty, Horrorville, Clockwork Shire and Atlantis. This time Heroes must destroy once and for all the Dark Forces! In Tang Dynasty a god has been born with unlimited power. Player begin their journey from the same realm and they must travel from one land to another but to do that they must destroy the Walls which separate each land. Each Wall has three parts and they can be destroyed by accomplishing different tasks: Find all Artifacts, destroy a Dark Lord or destroy the Black Fortress. When a part of  the Wall is removed, the player who did wins some extra and new Talents: the Green ones. These Talents determine the vulnerability of the player at the end of the game. In each round archers on top of the wall attack and all players lose talents, making the game progressively more difficult! Things will become more challenging... As long as player try to destroy the Wall in a Realm, Invaders take over the rest Active Realms! When you get at the Palace, you must all face the god who born there and then one of you can take that power!

On the other other side of the fairy world, Atlantis is sinking and the flood affects all the rest active Realms. Each time you destroy Invaders the water level rise in more than one Realms and you need more Talents to travel there as for the Invaders, they become more powerful. Save as many people, sacrifice your character and buy some time trying to find the solution and save yourselves. The time is running but in Clockwork Shire there is no time. Pinocchio and Geppetto must work to find all the missing gear wheel and re-activate all the Areas and only then you can attack the Invaders who raid the Realm. Use speed, travel among all the realms and find the correct gear wheel but be careful! Steam Baron will be there and you must face his mechanical skills! Regroup your team, this war is not over yet. A madman is running loose in Horrorville, a doctor who transforms people into beasts. If he transforms them all, you lose, so you must work together and find the cure and then you must find this madman and cure him but he is way to strong!
Σκραμπλοκρεμάλα is a boardgame combination of two word games (scrabble and 'hangman game') creating  a completely new and fun game!

DuploHex is a connection game, related to Hex, that includes disc and rings. In order to play DuploHex you need a 7×7 Hex board, 24 black and 24 white rings, and 24 black and 24 white discs.

White starts by placing one disc or ring on any cell of the board. Then, each player in turn must perform two actions: 1. add one of her discs to an empty cell or move one of her discs on the board into any ring located in a neighbor cell, and 2. add one of her rings to an empty cell or move one of her rings on the board to a neighbor cell occupied by a disc. The disc-ring pair (a disc inside a ring) cannot be modified for the rest of the game.

The game ends the moment that a player establishes a chain of her discs or rings connecting the two opposing sides of the board marked by her color.

boardgame: Κυνήγι ερωτήσεων: για παιδιά (Quiz Hunt: For Children)
description:
A competitive game with 600 questions about Greece and the whole world especially for children.

The game play is simple: Each player rolls the dice and according to the outcome of the dice he is being asked the corresponding question. If he answers right, he collects the card.

The player or the team with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

It extends a series of trivia games series: Κυνήγι Ερωτήσεων
1.History
2.Animals - Plants
3.Science
4.Culture
5.Sports
6.Geography
7.20th century
8.Greece

Robot Empire is a strategy and battle card game set in a large space age universe where Robot armies battle it out for control of the galaxy. Use Super Weapons, Human Agents, Upgrade Components, Base Equipment, and Monster Mercenaries to wage your war. Power Management is crucial in the game as you will need to mine for Power Coins to obtain enough energy to run your empire.

The cards have beautiful and colorful imagery right out of 1950s science fiction. Players will easily get emerged into the genre of the game.

Game Play:
Each turn, players spend their Power Coins (earned by minting or mining or landing on Mining Moon cards)to bring out fighters like Robots or Monster Mercenaries that can attack opponent's. Building a strong Base (Base cards form the back row on tabletop) allows players to shoot Long Range Weapons, Use Force Fields for extra defense, and create Power Coins. Players can customize their draw decks to follow different strategies and playing styles. A player looses Life Points when attacking fighters are not blocked. When a player reaches zero Life Points, they are out of the game.

You are an Egyptian military foreman leading your workers to carry large boulders to the great pyramid Giza, one of the seven great wonders of the world.

Players cooperatively pull a giant boulder first to the pyramid, then up the pyramid to be set in place.

On your turn, you may choose two actions:


    play a track card from your hand
    give a clue to the other players
    draw a card (usable only once per turn)


No matter the actions, the boulder moves one space further at the end of each player's turn.

All players win if they successfully pull the boulder and put it in place on the pyramid, players lose if the boulder ever falls off the track.

At game end, IF all players make it to the end, the player with the most cards in hand wins.

A microgame for the BGG / Geekway to the West design contest.

Abstract strategy in ancient Babylon.

Choose your path wisely!
Each path will bring you different results and will condition your rivals' decisions.
As you leave a trail of water, every plant that you fully irrigate will award you precious nectar.
The nectar is the key to winning the favor of the goddess Ishtar and winning the game!

The rules of the game are really simple, in your turn:
1- Choose a path from the central well without using the same tile twice
2- Put a water gem in each tile in the path
3- Collect a nectar wink for each tile filled with as many water gems as flowers
4- Put that nectar wink in your scoreboard
5- Repeat the steps for each player until someone completes the scoreboard

Despite the simplicity, strategy can be quite deep, you'll have to consider:

    Which tiles will give the next player an advantage if you add a water gem?
    Which tiles are worth completing to mess with your rivals' strategy?
    Which tiles are worth completing based in your position in the scoreboard?
    Speed vs precision. Is it better to finish first or to get more points per wink?

Puppy Phonics is an educational game about consonant blends and short vowel sounds. It is designed to be enjoyed by 2, 3, or 4 players age four and older. Each player twirls a spinner and names the sound indicated... for example, A E I O U. The player then advances his or her puppy token to the next space on the illustrated pathway which features an image matching that sound. The first player to reach the finish line is declared the winner.

Two children have lost control of their pet Monsters. Help them collect these Monsters-on-the-loose. Find My Monsters is 2 fun and fast card games in 1.

In Game 1, the deck is shuffled, then spread out face down. Players decide who goes first. They each then take turns turning over two cards in order to match the Alfi card (Green Background) with one of his pet Monsters (Green Background), or the Penny card (Orange Background) with one of her pet Monsters (Orange Background). (Or vice versa – matching Monster to matching Alfi/Penny) The cards that match are then placed in front of you to form your Point pile. At the end of the game, players will add up the total Monsters in their Point pile, and the player with the most Monsters wins!

In Game 2, the deck is shuffled and placed between both players. Each player draws four cards, and then one person takes the top four cards of the deck and places them face up in a row next to it. Decide who goes first. During each of their turns, players will look at the cards in their hands and the cards face up next to the deck in order to pair up two cards, Penny to one of her Monsters or Alfi to one of his Monsters(or vice versa). Your goal is to put together the cards that add up to the highest value (using the numbers on the bottom right of each card). These cards are then placed in front of you to form your Point pile. At the end of the game, each player adds up all the numbers on all of the cards in their respective Point piles, and the player with the higher total wins!
Trains: Map Pack 2 introduces an exciting, new, double-sided game board featuring a four-player map of Europe on one side and a pair of two-player boards on the other, including Italy and California.

description:
Party game for observant guests.

Each player is given a card with a vast amount of scribble on it. At least, it looks like scribble but hidden within the mess are 36 objects. The aim is to be the first to identify the objects.

The game is rather like the more famous Word Search but with images rather than letters.

The Blood Rage: 5th Player Expansion introduces the mighty Ram clan into Blood Rage! This clan comes complete with 8 Warrior figures (in two different sculpts), 1 Leader Figure, 1 Ship figure, 1 Clan Sheet, 1 Plastic Glory Marker, 4 cardboard Clan tokens, 11 small colored bases, and 2 large colored bases.

This expansion introduces the option of playing Blood Rage with 5 players. The game board supports this by simply not having any province destroyed by Ragnarök before the beginning of the game. However, more players need more cards, so this expansion box comes with 24 "Gods' Gifts" cards (8 for each Age deck). All of these cards have the "5+" players pre-requisite listed on them. While most of them are extra copies of cards from the base game, there are a few new ones, like the "Widespread!" Quest, where you need to dominate any 2 provinces.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The metropolis from Light Rail is growing fast and the citizens need express lines to keep up with demand! Downtown is an expansion for Light Rail.

Downtown adds new "express line" cards which help you gain majority on your valuable routes!

Downtown also adds cards with three different buildings, making them prime targets for scoring.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description: The Elf Hero Set consists of a set of 5 heroes: 1x rare Elf Duelist, 1x rare Earth Mage, and 3x Elf Archer.

boardgame: Character Quest: Desert Warrior's Hero Set
description: The Desert Warrior's Hero Set consists of of 5 heroes: 1x rare Guardian Templar, 1x rare Giant Scorpion, and 3x Javelinier heroes.

"This is another of UPL's fascinating 'action' games. Ideal for players of all ages" - so says the cover.

Hundreds of slips are provided, each one being a match for another one. Each guest will receive a slip from the host and told if it is from a famous couplet, proverb, song or hymn. The other slips are piled on a suitable table. On the word "Go", the guests (all at the same time) must sort through the table slips until they find the match. If correct, they are given another slip and must go back to find that slip's match. The guest who pairs up the most slips in a given time will win.

Example matches:
Rank and File
Two plain and one purl
Love me, Love and my Dog

This game is a kind of treasure hunt played sitting down instead of moving about the house.

A box contains 12 similar Picture Cards, 12 Clue & Answer Cards together with the Instruction and Solutions sheets.

The clues in some cases are somewhat cryptic and use is made of a play on words.

Each player is given a Picture Card and a Clue & Answer Card. The answer to each clue is an object in the picture; its name should be written in the 2nd column and the first letter of the name written in the end column. All these letters when correctly arranged give the answer to 'Find the....'. The blank space at the bottom is usable for this purpose, the dashes indicating letters of one or more words.

A SECOND GAME
The pictures can be used for a Memory Game, preferably before the hunt. Give each player a Picture Card and allow them to look at it for say a minute. Then collect the pictures; give each player a sheet of paper and the one who writes down most objects from memory is the winner.

This is a promo card / special card "Herausgeber" ("Publisher" in English) for Alchemists from issue #01/2015 of the German Spielbox magazine, released at the beginning of March 2015. An English language version is announced to be published around the middle of March 2015 with the English Spielbox version.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A challenging adventure awaits!

CardPG takes the best elements of such games as Munchkin, Talisman, and Dungeons & Dragons and polishes their best features, resulting in a game that's both easy to learn and fun to play.

Choose from one of six adventuring classes and battle a wide array of monsters, from the lowly Dire Rat up to a fearsome dragon! Each class has unique strengths and weaknesses, so you can always find a character that matches your play style.

Team up with other players to take on challenging foes, or try to go it alone and reap all the rewards for yourself.

Game play is structured so that everyone gets to do something meaningful on every turn. There's even a handy "Rules Card" that both keeps track of whose turn it is AND serves as a reminder of all the turn phases, turning a potentially complex turn into something that anyone can understand!

The aim of the game is to level up your character to level 5 or collect 30 gold. You level up by gaining experience from defeating monsters. In each turn players can commit their characters to fight one of the enemies, buy/sell equipment, spawn new monsters or heal themselves. Players can join forces to attack enemies together and share the prize, but only if the first attacker agrees, so there's an element of negotiation involved. Additionally players have equipment and action cards at their disposal which allow them to boost their stats and perform additional actions. Monsters are defeated by comparing your characters and the enemies attack/defence stats modified by the result of a dice roll.

It's time to challenge your fellow employees on a climb up the career ladder, taking revenge on them whenever possible when your expectations don't come to fruition in reality.

In Офис!, however, the battle for position is played out through a card battle, and playing the largest number isn't a guarantee of success. Instead the second best card is what boosts people up the corporate ladder...

User summary:
Pirates Code is a game of deduction and bluffing. There are 14 cards, each numbered 1 through 14, and 12 of them are dealt to players evenly. The remaining 2 cards compose a cipher of the treasure cache. A player wins the game by specifying the correct numbers of these 2 cards.

Each card has its own ability to help gain some clues about the treasure cache. But using the ability comes with a risk of revealing clues to other players. Players have to weigh the risk and the benefit when playing a card.

When a player is sure they have enough clues, it's time for them to declare the codes. The player wins if they are correct. If another player says they have one of the declared numbers, the declaration fails. The declared player misses one turn, but they don't get eliminated, and at least they gain a clue that another player has one. Therefore players may declare codes without enough clues, and experienced players will find out other benefits from early declaration.

「パイレーツコード」は推理とブラフのゲームです。
1～14の番号が書かれた14枚のカードのうち、12枚をプレイヤーに均等に配ります。残った2枚は財宝の隠し場所を示す暗号となります。この2枚の番号を正しく言い当てたプレイヤーが勝者です。
カードにはそれぞれ独自の効果があり、財宝の隠し場所に関する手がかりが得られます。しかし、それには相手に手がかりを与えるリスクも付きまといます。カードを使用する際は、このリスクとリターンを測る必要があります。
十分な手がかりを得られたと確信したら、暗号を宣言します。その暗号が正しければ勝利します。正しくなくても1ターンを費やすだけで、脱落はしません。そのため、実は十分な手がかりがなくても宣言することができますし、熟練者はこの宣言を他の目的で使うことを思いつくでしょう。

Publisher summary:
Pirates Code is a deduction card game without player elimination, where you can hide a trap inside your deduction (the literal translation of 推理の影に罠を仕込む is "to set a trap in the shadow of your deduction"). You lead your pirates and try to decipher the codes for the treasure cache. Your goal is to be the first player who identifies and declares the 2 correct numbers out of 14 candidates. You have more than one chance to declare, so you don't always have to declare the direct answer. Hide your true intention when you declare.

「推理の影に罠を仕込む」ことができる脱落のない推理カードゲームです。あなたは海賊たちを従え、14の数字から財宝の隠し場所を示した2つの数字を、誰よりも早く解き明かすことを目指します。

Advent Saga is a story-driven physical and digital Tactical Card Game that combines the excitement and traditions of popular card games, with the strategic elements of Tactical RPG's. The game takes place in an original universe where a devastating war is waged upon 3 different galactic factions. Players will choose from hundreds of cards to battle with, while discovering endless possibilities in combat strategy.

Advent Saga is a highly customizable game, in that each of the 5 resources have their own strengths and weaknesses, while each of the 10 advents have unique abilities that set them apart from each other. This allows you to build a deck to cater to your own style of play! The object of the game is to reduce the opposing Advent’s morale to zero. This can be achieved by strategically using the cards in your hand to deploy, attack and dominate your opponent.

One goal with Advent Saga was to add elements that rewarded meaningful choices over just drawing powerful cards. With the creation of our 4x4 battleground, players would not only need to rely on strength and power, but would also need to discover and develop new strategies involving Battleground position, Range, areas of effect, and much more.
Be the first to spot characters in a crowd.  With punched out people in a bowl, you spin the wheel and find that person before the timer runs out.

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 3 – Legion of Super-Heroes
description:
Game description from the publisher:

A legion of young guns from the DC Universe are ready for their time in the spotlight! In DC Comics Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 3 – Legion of Super-Heroes, play as a founding member of the Legion of Super-Heroes: Lightining Lad, Saturn Girl, Cosmic Boy, and more! Take on Super-Villains like Time Trapper, Emerald Empress, Persuader, and many others. Find interesting new Heroes, Villains, Equipment, Super Powers, and Locations in this set as well.

This set features an exciting new keyword called "Time Travel", and all but one card in this set has it. A Time Travel card can be played while in the Line-Up or on the Super-Villain stack at the cost of discarding a card from hand...but then you can't buy or gain that card that turn! Time Travel creates a lot of new and interesting decisions for players to make each turn.

DC Comics Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 3 – Legion of Super-Heroes can be used with any standalone DC Comics Deck-Building Game, replacing the Super-Heroes and Super-Villains from that set with the ones in this crossover pack.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Each player commands a lord or lady from an established noble house who has traveled to the king's annual tournament to expand their influence with the crown and to earn favors and patrons for the coming year.  You must balance your efforts between sponsoring knights (who may yield you honor and glory) and managing the subtle manipulations of the king’s court after each day’s battles.

Lavish the powerful with gifts, ply the gullible with a tapestry of empty promises, publicly humiliate your rivals, or back the people’s champion in the arena -- nothing matters except royal favor, and if your enemies are dragged down in the process, well… they knew the risks.

King's Tournament is a card-driven game that sets a random assortment of unique knights onto a play mat styled after a single-elimination tournament. Gameplay centers around the ebb and flow of two recurring phases: the action- and auction-based Festival Round and the card dueling-oriented Tournament Round.

Festival Rounds are play-or-pass affairs wherein players use actions, cards, tokens, and written abilities to sponsor knights, sabotage their foes, or benefit from the tournament in more subtle ways. The Tournament Round follows, during which the knights battle one another using their printed attributes and special maneuver cards played by their patrons. Victorious knights advance further down the bracket, and a new Festival Round begins afterward.

Play continues until a single knight has been crowned the tournament's champion, at which point players compare the favor tokens earned over the course of the game. Bonuses are doled out for reaching certain conditions, and the player with the most favor tokens is then declared the winner.

boardgame: Vault Wars: Mirrored Chest Promo Card
description:
Promo card that was first available during the Kickstarter campaign for Vault Wars.

Card effect:  
AUCTION MASTER
Draw Items face-down. You may peek at 2 Items. The opening bid is 1 Gold, you may continue to bid.

WINNER
Split all Items into two piles, revel 1 Item from each pile. Second place player selects 1 pile to keep, the Winner keeps the other. Both players must pay their bids as normal

boardgame: Vault Wars: Brass Lamp Promo Card
description:
Promo card that was first available during the Kickstarter campaign for Vault Wars.

The card says:
Auction Master
After revealing Items, select 3 players. Starting clockwise from the Auction Master, each selected player Peeks at all remaining items, places one face-down on the Item Pile, then passes the rest to the next selected player. After the third item is placed, shuffle the Item Pile.

From the box:

"Knuckle Down the Nile with your marbles.

A classic game of marble madness for ages 5 to adult.

Using skill and strategy, outmaneuver your opponent. Enter the sacred kingdom of the Nile. But watchout!! You may lose your marbles...and the game!!

Don't let the legions of dinosaurs barricade your path. Capture them to score and become pharaoh of the Nile."

From the rule book:

"Object of the game: To score the most points by shooting marbles to knock down barriers, hit your opponents' marbles and to storm through the entrances to the pyramids, the sphinx and into a dinosaurs mouth."

The Great Devourer is the first deluxe expansion for Warhammer 40,000: Conquest, and it introduces a brand-new faction to the game: the Tyranids! Two Tyranids warlords lead these horrific creatures into battle, each offering a very different play style. In addition to these warlords and their signature squads, you’ll find a host of new units, supports, events, and attachments to support the Tyranids, including an entirely new type of unit, the synapse unit. The Tyranids aren’t the only ones to benefit from this expansion, however. Every other faction must make its stand against the Great Devourer in the Traxis sector, and brand-new unique characters help them to battle the Tyranid threat.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
24 cards with a clue to a correct name are placed around the room. Players are given a card to record their answers and a time limit. When 'Time' is called, the player who returns to the party host with the most correct answers, wins.

It is undated but must have first appeared in the 1930's. The answer to the 2 example cards in the images are Norma Shearer and Bessie Love, both stars then but almost forgotten after WWII.

boardgame: Name the Lady for the Dressmaker
description:
Party game that comes in two parts.

First is a simple word game. Each player gets a card headed with What is the Female Name for each of the following words.
First to get all correct, wins.

Second game is another word game. This time the host puts around the room cards that ale all identical (thus to cut down over-crowding around just one card). On each card there is a little story and hidden within the story are items associated with a dressmaker. This is a considerably harder game than the first one. The first player to return with the most correct answers, wins.

User summary:
Postman Race is a race by postmen across the continent. By delivering letters before anyone else, you aim to become the world's best postman.

Publisher summary:
大陸を横断する郵便配達員のレースです。誰よりも早く手紙を届けて、世界一のポストマンになることを目指します。

From the rule book (used with permission):
Postman Race is a demanding and pop race between postmen to deliver a letter across the continent to a castle before anyone else. To cross various landscapes, you need to drive various vehicles. The players are free to choose the vehicle to own, the vehicle to drive, and the route to take. Once in a while, the players' courage is tested to take a reckless chance.

ポストマンレースは、1通の手紙を誰よりも早くお城に届ける、
過酷でポップな大陸横断レースです。様々な地形を乗り越えるために、
多種多様な乗り物をうまく乗り継がなければなりません。
どの乗り物を所有し、どの乗り物に乗り、どのルートを選択するかは
プレイヤーの自由です。ときには無謀な賭けにチャレンジする勇気が試されます。
