publishers: イマジンゲームズ (imagine GAMES)

description: Puzzle-themed game where the players switch, destroy or modify the gem cards in the gamezone in order to fulfill their objectives and score the most points.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Escape from Mount Gram
description:
From the publisher's website:

"It was deep, deep, dark, such as only goblins that have taken to living in the heart of the mountains can see through. The passages there were crossed and tangled in all directions, but the goblins knew their way."
     –J.R.R. Tolkien, The Hobbit

Escape from Mount Gram is the second Adventure Pack in the Angmar Awakened cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game.

For centuries, the Goblins of Mount Gram have remained hidden within their dark stronghold. Their past attempts at invading Eriador are but distant memories. Still, they live in lands tainted by ancient evils, and evil lives within their hearts. Now, in Escape from Mount Gram, the Goblins of Mount Gram are once again up to trouble, and when they capture a host of Rangers and heroes, it's up to you and your heroes to lead a daring and dramatic prison break!

In addition to its wild, new scenario, Escape from Mount Gram introduces a new hero for the Lore sphere and three copies each of nine different player cards. These cards explore a variety of different themes, divided among the different spheres of influence. While Leadership players benefit from a pair of cards that encourage direct action against the Enemy, Tactics players gain a pair of Ent-focused cards, Spirit players gain a new side quest, and Lore players gain two cards that support a new mechanic based around manipulating the encounter deck and the victory display.

description:
This game is a simulation of an exercise held at a military training ground involving tanks and helicopters. There are three levels. In the first level only tanks participate. In the second level are added combat helicopters. In the third level participate transport helicopters and Surface to Surface Missiles.

The main characteristic is that the game board is covered with a net of equilateral triangles. Vertices of these triangles are points at which the players hold position and move figurines.

Players have a task to destroy enemy headquarters or to use transport helicopters which carry commandos who can capture enemy headquarters and in that way make a quick victory. Other way of determining the winner is by counting points. Every hit, destroyed tank and shot down helicopter counts as one point.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
This is a game similar to Skat but with an Rhenish touch and some special rules that differ from the original Skat.
The gameplay is quite simple: each player plays a card and the player who played the highest card wins all played cards. The player who has collected the most tricks when all cards are played is the winner of the game.

Text on the box:

Das Rheinische Doppelklammerskatspiel

"Klammeraap" ist das ultimativ spaßige rheinische Familienkartenspiel mit Elementen aus Herzblättchen, Doppelkopf und Ramsch. Leicht verständlich, unterhaltsam und überraschend.
Die Schachtel enthält 45 Spielkarten und eine ausführliche Spielanleitung mit vielen witzigen Sonderregeln, die für Spaß und Abwechslung sorgen. Wem die kölnisch-ripuarische Mundart noch fremd ist, mit der die Karten bezeichnet sind, der kann sich trotzdem entspannt zurück lehnen. Ein Wörterverzeichnis liegt bei.

The game represent a simulation of aircraft maneuvers, which are held in Western Europe every year. All squadrons participating in maneuvers have image of a tiger in their emblem. The first such exercise was held back in 1949.

This is a game for 2 or 4 players. Each player has one base with airport. In the base are anti-aircraft guns, ammunition depot, radar, ... The game has 3 levels. In the first level are light attack aircrafts and fighter-bombers. In the second level supersonic attacker aircrafts are added, and in the third level interceptors are added. The planes are armed with guns, bombs, Air to Ground missiles and Air to Air Missiles.

The main characteristic is that the game board is covered with a net of equilateral triangles. Vertices of these triangles are points at which the players hold position and move figurines.

Each player has a task to achieve as many hits as possible in the enemy bases. Every hit and shot down aircraft counts as one point. The game lasts as long as players have planes and bombs, missiles... The winner is the player who has the best ratio of given and received hits.
family: Series: Triangle System (Predrag Lazovic / Dragan Lazovic)

description:
The game represent a realistic simulation of water polo. The game lasts 4 x 10 minutes, but it can also be played for a certain number of goals.

In each turn the player who attacks rolls three dice and moves three pieces and the ball. The player who is defending rolls two dice. The attack is limited to five turns.

Content:
Box 24 x 24 x 4 cm (9,5 x 9,5 x 1,5 in); Board 47 x 47 cm (18,5 x 18,5 in); 2 cardboard goals; 15 pieces; 2 markers, 5 dice; score counter and rules.

Alien Frontiers Big Box includes:

    Alien Frontiers
    Alien Frontiers: Factions
    Alien Frontiers: Faction Pack #1, #2, #3 and #4
    Alien Frontiers: Expansion Pack #1, #2, #3, #4, #5, #6 and #7
    Alien Frontiers: Outer Belt

description:
WW2: Lost Chronicles is a PNP wargame for 1-2 players. The game is a fan made project for fun & sharing.

It is a tactical wargame based on historical battles & fictional what if battles. Game scale is 200 meters per hex & roughly 10-15 minutes per turn. Game unit is platoon size(company for USSR). A game will last 6 - 12 turns or with a variable turn system.

Each player is a regimental commander who controls 4 - 9 companies. All companies are color coded & under command of a battalion HQ. A HQ can issue orders to three companies (three colors). Platoons of same color are from same company which should share same morale & training. Morale of a company will vary from scenario to scenario.

Air-support, OB artillery, reinforcement, mines, snipers, hidden units, weather change & other unexpected battlefield events will be handled by cards. Leadership will be implemented by command points & cards.

There is also a solitaire mode in which a player will play as a fictional regimental commander who will do daily routines like refit, replace units or send units for patrol & recon when there is no action. Enemy force is unknown until first contact. Your regiment will gain more & better resources if you proof yourself in battle. If you see too many casualties, you may request to retreat from battlefield & end the battle of the day but withdrawal request is not always permitted depending on the overall battle situation.

There is a battle-fatigue system. As a regiment commander, you choose which of your one/two battalions or attached companies will be upfront for attack or defense mission. The company will get one fatigue point for mission without contact and two fatigue points for contact with enemy force after each mission. A company's morale will drop by one if fatigue point is 2 or more. So you need to decide whether to send more troops upfront or call troops back for refit to remove fatigue points.

The players start on space 1, have to visit all numbered spaces in the right order and return to space 1. While doing this, they have to mind the traffic signs. Movement is determined by a die. The first player to return to space 1 wins the game.

This twisted tea time party game was inspired by Lewis Carol's "Alice's Adventure in Wonderland."  Who will out wit whom in this zany tea party?  This mixed up game has two to five players trading seats and switching cards in an attempt to be the player who can score the most points in a round before one player completes a full tea setting.

A strategic kids game with the goal to get the treasure at the bottom of the sea.

The game is set up by setting three boards on top of each other and each players sets his/her pawns in position. By strategically decide which die (as these have different numbers) to roll the player will try to move into the holes in the current layer. When all holes are filled, the current layer is removed along with all pawns that are not in a hole. The last layer contains only one hole, which leads to the treasure.

Cards: The Attackening! is a limited collectible card game (LCCG) for 2 - 5 players. Games take anywhere from 15 - 45 minutes, depending on how many people are playing. The game works on a system of sharing decks. I.e., instead of everyone bringing separate decks, there are three decks in each copy of the game from which every player draws. Specifically, Cards: The Attackening! consists of an Attackener deck (characters); Arsenal deck (Pets, Weapons, Armor, Evasions, and miscellaneous Items); and Twists of Fate (scenarios that affect gameplay - e.g., SciFi Attackeners gain a certain advantage while attacking.).

The game is played by every player drawing three Attackeners, three Arsenal cards, playing their first Attackener and then rolling to see who draws and plays the first Twist of Fate. The player with the highest roll chooses three Twists of Fate, and choosing the one that benefits them the most. That player then draws one Arsenal card, plays one, and attacks an opponent by rolling a die. Damage is determined by the amount rolled (1 is a miss, 2 is half damage, 3-5 is full, and 6 is double). If the opponent does not evade the attack, they take the damage and the move is passed to the next player, who follows the above steps to complete their turn.

When an Attackener's health is depleted, the person who beat that Attackener collects the card as a trophy. The person who was beaten chooses the next Twist of Fate, but does not play their next Attackener until it is their turn to play. This gives them a few rounds of immunity while play continues. The game is over when one player has collected three trophies from any player in the game.

24th July 1993, La Ciudad, Republic of Lanolinas.

People are celebrating, flocks of sheeps coming form the Meseta are invading the streets, the Plaza Merinos, hearth of the Nation, where the Parliament overlooks the Presidential Palace, is overcrowded... the dictatorship has finished!

The President Candidates of the two main Parties, National and Popolar, are speaking to the crowd... the elections are set in the next month!

Will you manage to win the Elections, gain the majority at the Parliament and elect the President?

Welcome to Eleciones en Lanolinas!

A 18 card game fast and interesting!


Here some hints on Lanolinas:

Capital City: La Ciudad

Goverment: Presidential Republic

History: Found by Colombo in 1495, Lanolinas gets its name from the flocks imported from Spain since 1545. La Ciudad is founded in 1553 as commercial distribuition of the wool here produced. Since 1648 Lanolinas is a Spanish Royal Colony, till the indipendence, declared on 19th March 1891.

As independent republic, Lanolinas is influenced by the US and knows a period of dictatorship, ended in 1969 by the Marxist Revolution of Ignacio Las Casas. At its death, in 1980, Guillermo Rosas restores the Presidentail Republic using terror and corruption, till its recent resignements.

Economy: Sheep Wool, treated into the Woolen Mill, the main local industry.

Politics:there are two main parties, National and Popolar. The country is divided into three Constituencies, each electing one Candidate to the Parliament:

First: La Ciudad, urban, with officies, University and Army Headquarter;

Second: El Centro, rural, with medium villages with shops and the Woolen Mill.

Third: La Meseta, rural, with small villages, farms, sheep breeding, hills.
The game is for two players,each represents one of the political parties of the Republic of Lanolinas; one is the National Party (its badge is a blue N), the other is the Popolar Party (its badge is a red P).

Each Party shuffles its Candidates cards (with its party badge), draws all four of them, turns them on the opposite face and gets the Candidate President and a Candidate for each Constituency.

Each Party has to nominate the Candidate President and to assign one Candidate for each Constituency, showing them on the table in echelon.

The country is divided into three Constituencies, each electing one Candidate to the Parliament:

First: La Ciudad, urban, with officies, University and Army Headquarter;
Second: El Centro, rural, with medium villages with shops and the Woolen Mill.
Third: La Meseta, rural, with small villages, farms, sheep breeding, hills.

Game Rules (available on cards as well)
Each Candidate has a Vote Value valid in all or some Consituencies only, a Note with a special empowerement of a specific Electoral Card and might have a Candidate President Special Ability too, to be used if designed as Candidate President.

Then after having shuffled the Electoral Cards, each Party draws 4 cards each; these are the cards to be assigned to the Constituencies in order to get the most Votes and therefore manage to elect its Candidate in the Constituency.

The Ecards are valid in specific Constituencies only, it is possible that a Party doesn't have any Ecard in a Constituency.

Each Party assigns the ECards for each Constituency face down; when all the cards have been assigned, the Polls start and the ECards are revealed, starting from the First Constituency.

If any Candidate President Special Ability is to be solved, solve it when necessary; the exchange of one Ecard is with an adverse revealed one at Candidate choose!

In each Constituency each Party calculates how many votes got (including the Candidate President Vote Value where is valid and all the appliable Notes of the Candidates), the most elect its Candidate at the Parliament; in case of draw, the seat remains unassigned!

The party with the most elected Candidates elects its Candidate President as President of the Presidential Republic of Lanolinas.
In case of draw, the Elections are annulled and the game restarts.

The President’s Party can retain one card for the new Eleciones; one Candidate (kept to be used as Candidate President or Constituency Candidate) or one ECard, which will be not reshuffled but kept apart.

Then restart the game for a new Electoral Campaign!

A Party wins if gets the President elected three times on five Elections.

Will YOU manage to make your Candidate President?

Solar System Spin is a spin-and-move game where players move along a track from Pluto to the sun, trying to collect planet cards with facts about the planets. Along the way, the spinner may send players to the black hole, supernova or worm hole, causing them to move backward, lose turns or lose cards.

The first player to reach the sun with at least three planet cards wins.

Scar plots to get rid of Simba by telling him about a dangerous elephant graveyard.  Just as he hopes, Simba and Nala head for this forbidding place, where a pack of hungry hyenas await them.  Can you help King Mufasa rescue the cubs?

Published in Disney's Giant Board Game Book, The Lion King is a roll-and-move game where players must move two markers representing Simba and Nala simultaneously along a track, trying to reach the end and be rescued by Mufasa.  Along the way, they may land on spaces that move them forward or backward or cause loss or gain of turns.  If a player lands on another marker, they move back six spaces, and in order to start moving a marker at the beginning of the game, they must roll at least a three.  The book comes with an "electronic" die that generates random number from 1 to 6. A small element of strategy is added by the ability to move either marker, offering the choice to move the marker that will land on an advantageous space or avoid a negative space.

A mob of angry villagers, led by Gaston, has decided to invade the forbidding castle in order to kill the Beast.  Belle saddles Phillipe and sets out to save the Beast.  However, you must help her to get to him before the last petal of the Enchanted Rose falls, or the Beast will never become a prince again.

Published as part of Disney's Giant Board Game Book, Beauty and the Beast is a roll-and-move game where players take the part of Belle, rushing to save the Beast.  Each player in turn presses the "electronic die" and moves the indicated number of spaces along the track.  Each player also has a marker on the rose board, indicating petals falling from the enchanted rose.  As they move along the track, they will land on spaces that may move them forward or backward or cause loss or gain of turns.  Landing on another player's marker moves a player back 2 spaces.  Players landing on spaces marked with a rose must move their marker one space forward on the rose board.  The first player to reach space 42 by exact roll without their rose board marker reaching the end of the rose board rescues the beast and wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Beauty and the Beast

description:
Prepare yourself for the bizarre as you venture deep into a world of radioactive insanity!

Wasteman: A game of desperate conflicts in a desolate future! A frantic game, set amongst the ruins of a neglected and scorched earth. Engage in desperate battles between post-humans, hideous monstrosities and killer robots over the dregs of an age of gluttony. Take control of you own syndicate and test your metal against the weird and terrifying denizens of the wastes!

Wasteman is a fun, easy to learn, and refreshingly crazy game! Players or 'comrades' control syndicates, typically comprised of between 5 and 20 ruthless denizens of the wastes, in an attempt to vanquish their opponent, survive bizarre encounters and/or capture much needed supplies.

During a game of Wasteman, comrades take it turn to activate individual figures to perform several actions; such as attacking, moving, hiding or even bringing people back to life with the power of their mind in some cases! Figures can normally only be activated once per turn so you have real scope for strategy – goad your opponent into activating the wrong figure at the wrong time and they could be left open to all sorts of cunning shenanigans!

Once a syndicate drops below 5 figures they become desperate and the restriction for activating a figure only once in a turn is lifted. This leads to some really cinematic moments, where often lone survivors hopelessly attempt to fend off hordes of enemies and occasionally succeed! This really keeps the game fun right until the last man.

Figures take different kinds of damage – they can be spooked, crippled, seriously injured, or killed outright. To make it easier to remember what’s happened, each figure comes with a base equipped with slots and removable tokens. These are cast in high quality resin and are included with all but the largest figures. Each figure will receive 1 base out of 10 designs selected at random.

M.A.D cards really bring the crazy to the game! Each comrade receives a hand of 5 M.A.D cards that can be played at any time (for the most part) to either help yourself or hinder your opponent. There’s nothing quite like having the enemy boss abducted by aliens just before riding a conveniently timed tornado to the objective on the last turn! Though normally limited to 5 cards, you can seek the aid of traders or vending machines to top up your hand during a game leading to some very tense moment as your prize fighter sits in the middle of the battlefield kicking a coffee machine.

The cards themselves are lavishly illustrated by yours truly, and beautifully printed on quality playing card stock.

As well as a myriad of rules for hazards, mods to alter the game and a stockpile of scenarios, Wasteman has an in depth syndicate creation system, with loads of equipment, skills and Boss Rosters to choose from. Although each figure comes with a stat card, you are more than welcome to create your own and/or combine figures from different posse packs – the choices are vast!

Wasteman miniatures are sculpted by hand to approximately 35mm scale and will be cast in metal, unless specified to the contrary. All figures will come with stat cards and token slot bases. They will be supplied unpainted and, in some cases, unassembled.

boardgame: Mexico Ablaze: A Solitaire Game of the Mexican-American War (1846-1848).
description:
BACKGROUND: Mexico Ablaze is semi-historical simulation of the Mexican-American War (1846-1848). The Game begins shortly after the United States of America (USA) and Mexico have begun hostilities near the border of the Rio Grande. The Mexican Army is now building up their forces to kick the United States out of Mexico. The US must capture enough Mexican controlled Settlements before the Mexican Army is able to effectively mobilize an overwhelming force.

OBJECTIVE: Player takes command of the United States Forces in Mexico. The Player’s Objective is to attempt to capture three or more of the five Key Mexican Controlled Settlements of Monterrey, Buena Vista, Vera Cruz, Tampico and Mexico City before the “Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo” Campaign Card is Drawn. If the Mexicans capture 3 of the four US Settlements of Fort Texas, Laredo, Corpus Christi or San Patricio, they win. Also, if the US has not captured three of the four Key Mexican Settlements and the “Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo” Campaign Card was played, the Mexicans Win.

GAME FEATURES: Mexico Ablaze features many colorful game counters of the various Unit types that fought in the Mexican War, including individual Heroic Officers that later served in the Civil War. Battles can be fought on a large Battle Mat and includes assaults on fortified settlements.

MEXICO ABLAZE can be found at http://www.berserkergames.net

Sylvion: Below Ground is a 16-card expansion that adds an additional challenge to Sylvion.

Shuffle the cards, then place them face down in the 4x4 battlefield grid. During the game, when you want to place a tree or a fountain on a terrain card on the battlefield, you must first pay the cost, then reveal the terrain card. Then:


    If the terrain card is unoccupied, remove it from play and place the tree or fountain in this space.
    If the terrain card shows an owl, remove it from play, place the tree or fountain in this space, then draw two cards from the defender deck.
    If the terrain card shows a swallower, remove it from play along with the tree or fountain that you were going to place; you receive no compensation. (If the removed card was a fountain, draw one card from the defender deck as if the fountain were defeated in battle.)

Robot for the world invasion was out of control during the experiment! In order to activate the emergency stop program, it's insert a stop key to all the robot quickly!

In order to play this game the player have to provide 5 D6 that are not included in the game.

The goal of the game is to be able to put out all the dice on the slots on the game board in order 1 to 5.

You put out all the dice in order 1 to 5 on the different slots on the board, the problem is that each slot have an effect on the dice that have not yet been placed. The slots can make you either turn up or down the results of one other die, you could re-roll 1 or all dice and one slot will let you turn over one die to the other side (For an example 1 to a 6 or 5 to a 2).

This is a postcard sized solo game that was given away free at Essen 2014
This expansion includes 4 additional Vortex tiles. They have similar function as the teleport, although their range of effect is greater: 12 surrounding tiles instead of 3.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Earth Cult Warrior Expansion Pack
description: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Earth Cult Warrior Expansion Pack includes one pre-painted miniature, upgrade cards and tokens.

description: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Air Cult Warrior Expansion Pack includes one pre-painted miniature, upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Human Barbarian Fire Cult Expansion Pack
description: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Fire Cult Warrior Expansion Pack includes one pre-painted miniature, upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

description:
Cruella hired Horace and Jasper to kidnap lots of Dalmatian puppies.  She wants to make a fur coat out of them.  Can you help Perdita and Pongo find all of them and bring them back home?

Published as part of Disney's Giant Board Game Book, 101 Dalmatians is a roll-and-move game where players take the part of the Dalmatian parents Pongo and Perdita, trying to save their puppies from the evil Cruella de Vil.  Each player in turn presses the "electronic die" which generates a random number from 1 to 6 to move two markers representing the dogs simultaneously along a track, trying to reach the end and be save their offspring.  Along the way, they may land on black paw prints or white paw prints, which move them back or forward six spaces respectively.  Other spaces may also move them forward or backward or cause loss or gain of turns.  If a player lands on another marker, they move back five spaces.  A small element of strategy is added by the ability to move either marker, offering the choice to move the marker that will land on an advantageous space or avoid a negative space.

In the future, sooner than you’d bet on…

As the world progressed towards the future, the wealthy elite one-percenters found themselves losing their passions.  Golf’s formerly exclusive clubs were opened to the masses, horse racing was banned as cruel, and auto racing was found to be not "carbon credit cost effective".  The elite needed a new pastime.

Fortunately for them, a new class of mass-spectacle gladiators was found. Questionable lab projects involving human-cloning, attempting to solve the world’s worst diseases, created a global zombie pandemic.  Luckily, the crisis was put down quickly – but the lab-created undead still existed.

Someone with a bit of redneck, and way too much time, picked up a few up zombie “assets” at a company bankruptcy liquidation. After a little tinkering, he managed to build a control collar contraption. When a zombie was outfitted with a collar, a skillful operator could, sort-of, get the zombies to walk in a straight line.  Conveniently, the undead had no legal rights, which made them the perfect attraction.

A viral video and a crowd-sourced funding campaign later, these not-so-intelligent shamblers were being billed as the latest thing in racing spectacles.  Wealthy backers quickly formed teams, a league and championship cup was created, and the wealthy few who could afford the labs, zombie jockeys, and all that related to it now had a new prestigious exclusive club.

As with all good clubs, betting became part of the social fun.  As one could never really count on one’s zombie to come through, betting for, or even against one’s own entry into the race was not only allowed, it was encouraged!

While these new zombie drivers competed against each other for the series championship, the owners had their own prestige cup based on bets, side bets, crowd ratings and worse.

Bet on your friends zombie driver to make the podium … SURE!   Bet on your zombie to place third … WHY NOT? WIN when your zombie shambles over the line backwards … CHECK!

Welcome to the wacky, zany, risky but always crazy world of high stakes championship zombie racing!

In Twenty Yard Shamble, players are owners of racing zombies. They pit their zombie against the other zombies, in a series of 3 races, out of an arena where armed Hunters and helpless Victims prowl. Zombies move in a semi-controlled fashion based on dice rolls. The arena has 6 finish lines spaced equally around the outside edge. The first zombie to cross a finish line is first, and earns points for it's owner AND anyone who bet on it. The second zombie to cross gets second, and the third zombie gets third. After the third zombie crossed a line, the crowd gets bored, and all remaining zombies are shot and killed.

During a race, players place bets on any of the racing zombies, including their own. If a players zombie is not performing well, that player can place a bet on another player's zombie who is performing better, and may be able to help that zombie finish better in the race.

At the end of 3 races, the player with the most points wins.

The year is 596 A.D. and the Gregorian Mission has decreed a conversion of all pagans to Christianity. Under attack, the Druids - the prophets and scholars of ancient Paganism - must try and prevent the Christians from spreading their new religion, but it won’t be easy. As the church gathers more converts, they grow stronger with each passing day...

Druids is a press-your-luck style dice game with a twist where, with the help of the gods, you play as a priest of the ancient Celts, desperately trying to stop the Christians from converting your believers. The twist is that the other players are rolling against you! If they can convert your believers, the victory will be theirs instead. Use the special abilities of the gods to keep the missionaries at bay and keep your faithful strong.

King Triton has lost his magic trident.  He sets out to search for it with Sebastian, Ariel and Flounder.  They find a sunken ship at the bottom of the sea, and divide into two teams.  Which team will find the trident and end up with the most objects?

Published in Disney's Giant Board Game Book, The Little Mermaid is a roll-and-move game where players must move along a track, collecting objects along the way as they try to reach the end of the track and find King Triton's trident.  Players choose to play as either Ariel and Flounder or Triton and Sebastian, and press the "electronic die" to move along separate tracks which merge at the end.  As they progress, they may land on spaces that move them forward or backward or cause loss or gain of turns.  Some spaces are also marked with objects to collect (designated by taking a marker).  The player who finishes with the most objects collected wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Little Mermaid

description: Tryptic is a tile placement game involving bluffing.  Players try to create multiple copies of their own (secret) pattern, without anyone else noticing, and try to determine which patterns other players are creating.

Aladdin has lost the magic lamp!  You must help him and Princess Jasmine find it before Jafar does.

Published in Disney's Giant Board Game Book, Aladdin is a roll-and-move game where players must press the "electronic die" to move two markers representing Aladdin and Jasmine simultaneously along a track, trying to reach the end and recover the magic lamp.  Along the way, they may land on spaces that move them forward or backward or cause loss or gain of turns.  A small element of strategy is added by the ability to move either marker, offering the choice to move the marker that will land on an advantageous space or avoid a negative space.  The first player to reach the end of the track with both markers wins, and the other players must "grant three wishes" to the player by roaring like a genie, making a face like Abu and flying around the room like a magic carpet.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Class Deck – Paladin
description:
Faith and the Sword! Paladins serve as a shining beacon of purity and hope in a world beset by darkness. The Paladin Class Deck, a portable play accessory for the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game, allows players to bring these guardians of duty and honor to any Base Set, including the demon-filled Wrath of the Righteous campaign.

The 109-card Paladin Class Deck contains 3 new characters, including the iconic holy warrior Seelah, and enough new and familiar weapons, spells, mounts, and other boons to bring your paladin through an entire Adventure Path. The deck is self-contained, so you can play your character at any table, and is also all you need to dive into the exciting Pathfinder Society Adventure Card Guild organized play campaign.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

boardgame: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
description:
Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs are out in the woods picking berries.  The forest animals warn them that the wicked Queen, disguised as an ugly old hag, is after them.  In order to deceive the wicked Queen, Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs separate into four teams and set out, each in a different direction, for their little cottage.

Published in Disney's Giant Board Game Book, Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs is a roll-and-move game where players simultaneously move four markers on a grid, trying to get all four safely to their designated cottage.  Each player presses the "electronic die" and then chooses one of four markers to move from its starting point onto the grid.  Markers must make their entire move either vertically or horizontally, not both.  Many grid squares are marked with various character, events or objects from the movie.  Landing on these may move the player's marker or cause loss or gain of a turn.  An element of strategy is added by the ability to move any marker, offering the choice to move the marker that will land on an advantageous space or avoid a negative space.  The first player to move all four markers from their starting spaces to the safety of the cottage wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

boardgame: Operation East Front: A Solitaire Game of Tactical Combat on the Russian Front
description:
Operation East Front is a solitaire game of Combat on the Russian Front during World War II. The player assumes the role of a German commander of an Infantry Company during a German Offensive Operation to advance on a Key Soviet City. As the Company Commander, you are responsible for securing your Sector of Advance by successfully taking and holding key objectives with the least amount of casualties. Although there are many support units available to you, they may often times be limited to you by political, logistical or geographical obstacles.

GAME OBJECTIVE 
The objective of Operation East Front is to successfully take and secure an number of key objectives and destroy enemy forces in order to gain Victory Points (VP) for the German Forces. Once all Objectives have been secured or the Campaign Deck is exhausted, the Campaign is considered over. The Player must then total his VP and compare this with the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success.

You can find Opeation East Front at http://www.berserkergames.net

Wizard's Mansion is a game of search of objects, missions and deduction. It is played on a mobile board of 36 tiles that moves and turn. The tiles are drawn in his two faces. Every face represents a floor of the Mansion.
The players will have to enter the Mansion to look for magic objects and to go out with then for one of four doors. But in the Mansion it has soldiers that will prevent your mission and the Wizard who will be able to turn you into a ghost.

Descripción en Español:
Wizard's Mansion es un juego de búsqueda de objetos, misiones y deducción. Se juega sobre un tablero móvil de 36 baldosas que se mueve y giran. Las baldosas están dibujadas en sus dos caras. Cada cara representa un piso de la mansión.
Los jugadores deberán entrar en la mansión para buscar objetos mágicos y salir con ellos por una de las cuatro puertas. Pero en la mansión hay soldados que impedirán tu misión y el Hechicero que te podrá convertir en un espectro.

Make the most valuable games collection! Use the special powers of three characters - Pena (rolling two equal numbers), Poaiola (three) and Sergião (a sequence) - to earn games from stores or another players. If you have a complete game collection of a publisher, scores five points. If you have at least one game of each publisher, more five points. Extra copies loses 3 points.
This game was a "Jogando Offline - 2nd season" crownfounding's stretch goal.

In Hold Your Breath!, the game is played in two rounds. In the first round each player may dive deeper, providing they have the "Swim" and "Air" cards to do so. Players press their luck each turn since it is the pirate who dives the deepest in round one AND resurfaces in round two who is the winner. Players can play "Panic", "Shark" and other nasty cards on the other players, slowing their progress. In the second round each player struggles to reach the surface before they run out of air and therefore decide to take a rest (aka LOSE). Most of the pirates may not make it back to the surface, but the one who makes it back alive AND dove the deepest is the winner.

This expansion allows for up to two additional players to take a dive and press their luck.

Elliot Moose Counting Game is a game designed to teach numbers and test memory for young children. It can be played three different ways. The main game is called "Elliot Moose Counting Board Game." In this game, players roll a die to move their pieces around a track around the outside edge of the board. When they land on a space, they take the tile depicting a number or objects. They must then choose a face-down tile from the middle with a number printed on it. If the number matches, they keep the pair. If it does not match, both tiles are returned to their places and the next player takes a turn. When all pairs are claimed, the game ends and the player with the most pairs wins. Players can also choose to play "Counting Memory," which plays similarly but with the die and track removed. Players simply choose a tile from a stack and try to pick the face-down number tile that matches the number of items depicted on the card. Another option is "Guess Which Number!" where one number tile is hidden, the other are placed face-down and players take turns guessing which number is missing.

Math Bingo is a twist on traditional Bingo designed to reinforce basic arithmetic skills. In this game, each player has a 4x4 card with a series of numbers. A caller reads a series of math problems (either addition and subtraction for younger children or multiplication and division for older). Players must solve the math problem and mark a space on their card corresponding to the answer. Once a player completes a row or column and claims bingo, the caller checks the math to make sure their answers are correct. If so, they are declared the winner. If not, incorrect answers are removed from the card and play continues until a player completes a row or column of correct answers.

The Great Dreidel Race is a roll-and-move game contained in The Great Big Box of Hanukkah Fun. Players use dreidels and playing pieces, rolling a die to move them along a track.  Along the way, they may encounter a variety of spaces that will help or hinder them.  Some spaces move the player forward or back, others may cause gain or loss of a turn, some move other players' dreidels, some offer shortcuts.  When landing on a "spin" space, a player must spin his or her dreidel and move to the space corresponding to the Hebrew letter spun.  If a player lands on another player's dreidel, the other player is sent back to the nearest space marked with a candle.  The first player to reach the finish space wins.

Shootin' Blanks is an easy to learn (less than 5 minutes) 2-8 player elimination card game that fits right in your pocket. Compete for fun, drinks, money or anything you want!

Each player begins the game with three lives. Use poker chips, pennies, dollars, pieces of paper, beers, etc. Be creative.

Deal three cards to each player (one at a time). Place the rest of the deck in the middle of the table to draw from throughout the game. Turn the top card of the draw pile over to begin the discard pile.

The player to the left of the dealer can draw the top card from the draw pile or the discard pile and then must discard a card on top of the discard pile. Continue clockwise around the table.

Players try to collect the highest possible hand value in the same color OR in the same number.

Play continues clockwise until a player bangs or the draw pile is exhausted. Players cannot bang on their first turn.

When a player believes their hand is better than at least one other players hand, they bang on the table (or say bang) during their turn instead of drawing and discarding.

All other players get to draw and discard one more time.

At the end of the round, players show and announce their hand value (only adding cards of the same color OR the same number, not both).
 The player(s) with the worst hand lose one life. 
When a player loses all three lives, they are eliminated from the game. The last person with a life wins.

If a player at any time during the game has three blank cards in their hand, they throw down their cards and yell "I'm Shootin’ Blanks!" The round ends and all other players lose one life. Blanks are worth 0.

If a High/Low card is ever face up in the discard pile prior to a player banging, the scoring for the round changes from high to low. You still calculate your high hand, but instead the player with the highest hand value loses a life (ties work the same way for low hand rounds). It is possible to switch from High to Low back to High back to Low in a single round. High/Low cards are worth 0 and can never be drawn from the discard pile.

Wild cards can be any color and any number 1 through 8. They cannot be Blanks or High/Low cards.

Hurry Home is a game designed to teach letter sounds to young children. It was published as part of a Scholastic Phonics Reading Program boxed set.

In Hurry Home, players must get Sound Hound home to his doghouse.  Each player chooses a path, made up of a number of spaces marked with pictures, leading to the doghouse. Each player takes turns spinning a spinner marked with 0 to 3. The player then draws that number of cards from a stack of cards marked with words or pictures. The opposite side of the card shows the first letter of the picture or words. The player must then say the name of the pictured item or read the words (an adult facilitator may help with reading) and the say the letter. The player then looks at his or her path and places a marker on any picture that starts with that letter. If no matches can be found, the card is placed at the bottom of the pile. The first player to place marker on every space on his or her path brings Sound Hound home and wins the game.

From the rules:
 The object of Fishing Camp - To be the first person to travel from the docks, catch a fish or multiple fish, and get back to the docks with their catch.

Fishing Camp is a roll-and-move fishing trivia game in which player move along a track trying to catch a fish before reaching the home space.  All player start out at the dock with a fishing boat playing piece.  They choose a level of difficulty from 1 to 4 for their questions.  This is a way to handicap the game.  Older or more experienced fishermen should choose a higher number.

Each player in turn rolls the die and moves along a track marked with lily pads.  Green lily pads are safe spots.  On a yellow (or orange) lily pad, the player must choose a question card and answer a fishing trivia question at their level.  A decoder (transparent red plastic) is used to reveal the answer.  If the answer is correct, the player rolls and moves again.  If the card depicts a fish, the player keeps it and has now made a catch.  If the card says so, the player may take the GPS card (of which there is only one), which may allow him or her to take a shortcut along the path.  Purple cards may move the player to "Shore Lunch Island" or other locations, possibly delaying him or her.  The first player to move completely around the track and reach a "Home" space with at least one caught fish wins the game.  However, if a player finished without a fish, he or she may move immediately to "Last Chance Launch" and move toward the home space from there, attempting to catch a fish along the way.

Strange Conversations is a humorous social game for 3 to 7 players that tests players' abilities to convey hints about their secret identities to one another throughout the course of a simple role-played sit-down conversation.

In each game players find themselves in an interesting hypothetical setting and scenario having a conversation with a group of people with strange and often funny personality quirks.

At the start of each round, each player is assigned a secret JOB and a secret character QUIRK.   For example, a player may be an olympic athlete (job) whose hands keep getting stuck to the table (quirk).   Or, a doctor (job) who offers other players back-handed compliments (quirk).  Or, a dancing instructor (job) who is hitting on other players (quirk).

With inspiration from a random scenario, players role-play a discussion and provide clues to each other about what their two qualities (job/quirk) are.

There are no "turns" in this game.  Conversations are free-flowing discussions where players may ask and answer questions whenever appropriate.  Players drop creative hints about their identities through the use of verbal hints, including puns and keywords related to their jobs, hints to aspects of their jobs, and cultural references.  Players may also use physical gestures to help convey their identities.

Players earn the most points if they can signal what their jobs and quirks are to some -- but not all -- other players, and also earn points for guessing who other players are.

Noteworthy is a card game that teaches "ear training" by prompting players to race to arrange cards with musical notes on them while listening to a melody played by the Noteworthy Game app.

The fastest players to arrange their scores get bonuses before scoring points based on the accuracy of the notes and timing of the melody. The player with the highest score wins!

Each card has a single note (A-G) that can be modified in length based on how cards overlap each other (including cards that mark rests in the music). The arrangement of these cards represents the melody that players are trying to perfectly match.

The best mix of Trivia with multiple choise answers, mimic, sounds, challenges and more fun stuff about Ecuador with unique handcrafted tokens made with tagua "vegetal ivory".

The goal is to become the Embassador of Ecuador by being the first to seal the passport.

It´s a unique way to gather again with your family and friends while you travel around your country.

There are 36 cards in Ujub või Upub kaardimäng (Floats or sinks cardgame).
Some of the items on the cards float, some sink if on water.
The cards are turned around one by one and player who first shouts correctly floats of sinks wins the card as a point.
If the deck is exhausted the player with the most cards is the winner.

Cargotrain Duel is a promotional board game which serves to promote the full game Cargotrain. The game is just a double sided sheet of paper with instructions on one side and the board on the other.

Each player manages a fleet of trains. The goal of the game is to gather most points. On a player's turn she rolls five dice. Then the players takes alternatively one dice each other, starting with the active player untill both take two. The last 5th die is a support die.

The active player moves first. She chooses wich die will be the  resource die and which will be the route die. Then she takes an amount of resources equal to the sum of resource and support dice. Now she can move a train on route which corresponds to route die. In order to do that she must spend her resources in amount indicated on route fields. If a player reaches or passes through a field with factory, then

When both players have taken their turns, there is a scoring round. Each player scores the points indicated on the route fields which their trains are on.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Young Copper Dragon Expansion Pack
description: Dungeons & Dragons: Attack Wing – Copper Dragon Expansion Pack includes one pre-painted miniature, upgrade cards and tokens.
family: Game: Dungeons & Dragons – Attack Wing Miniatures Game

description: In Hidden Treasures you have a set of legendary treasures you're seeking to find. Every player places one of these treasures face down on the table and all players begin to play Expedition cards in order to claim the treasures for their own. You know what you're after, but what are the other players after?? and can you beat them to it? Through clever card play and careful observation of other player's expeditions, maybe you can swoop in and get what they're after before they do!

A precursor of Pergame, Singidunum has been conceived as a game to bridge the gap between traditional wargames and abstract games. It mainly relies on the use of only four types of units and simple movement and attack rules. The main principle is that a ready unit can make only one move or/and attack during a turn of the game. This will allow and even entice players to cleverly coordinate their moves and anticipate their opponent’s actions. 
The game still retains some of the wargame appeal by using dice to simulate fortunes of war and giving the players a chance to immerse themselves into the brutal fighting between Huns and Romans on the Balkan Peninsula.

The game is designed to appeal to wargamers looking for a fast game as well as abstract gamers. It is also quite welcoming to the younger players (and their parents) as it offers a fun and easy gateway to wargaming.
The small box dimensions (171x151x36mm) make it perfect for gaming on the go.
Under the sprawling ruins of the once great trading city of Blackwall lies the Knot. The maze of tunnels house uncounted evils. In the deep dark of the warrens courage is tested and fear takes hold.
The kingdom of the Okian lies at the heart of sun-scorched Kanis. It is a sand-blasted land where massive stone monuments rise to honor the dark master Anunkara, Keeper of the Dead.

Mediterranean Sea, Summer 1941

Your SM79 bomber is looking for a target...

The engines work well and suddedly here is an enemy ship!

The attack has started... will you manage to torpedo the ship?

It is a solitaire fast pace 18 cards game where YOU will be the pilot of an Italian SM79 torpedobomber which is preparing to lauch its Whitehead torpedo to sink her!

Will you survive the AA or the escort Fighter?

No dice, just choose your card on the Resolution Draw and see if you get one Hit or if you Miss, or check if AA hits you!

Italian description:

Mare Mediterraneo Estate 1941.

Il tuo SM79 è in azione alla ricerca di un bersaglio...

I motori ronzano, ad un certo punto ecco il segnale... nave nemica!

Inizia la manovra di attacco... riuscirai ad affondare l'obiettivo con il tuo siluro Whitehead?
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Imbroglio is a fun, fast-paced tile laying game where the players are racing to escape an ever changing maze full of mythological beasts, natural disasters, and a handful of colorful characters who are also trying to be the first to escape. Each time you play, the game is quite different with custom characters, a lot of strategy, and a dash of luck.
 Imbroglio is a game for 2-7 players and has multiple styles of play, from player vs player to players vs board.  An average game can be played in 45-60 minutes, but with possible expansions in the future, games could be expanded to almost any length.  The basic mechanics of the game are a mix of tile laying, resource management, and dice rolling.  
 Our original thought would be that the game would be targeted toward families or gamers who like mythology, strategy, and 'sticking it' to other players.  That being said, the game is quite modular and could accommodate many different game genres!

Plunder Straits is a pirate-themed strategy board game where players sail around a map, entering harbors and trading cargo for gold. Each turn, players draw Plunder cards which can benefit them by giving them extra turns or more gold or by increasing their ability to defend, attack, or move. Players can also attack other players and take their cargo, gold, or abilities for themselves. The first player to bury 50 gold and keep their Jolly Roger flying win the game.

The war continues and spreads further and further. New factions have arrived from the north igniting more epic battles than before. Heroes rise over the fallen bodies of their enemies. Kingdoms spread across the frozen wastelands in search of silver. War Towers grow taller than the trees. Who will be victorious in the end?

Tiny Epic Kingdoms: Heroes' Call introduces heroes to the war. Heroes will bring to play a variety of benefits unique to their class. Players will use a larger meeple to represent the hero of their faction.

Additionally new mechanisms are introduced to the building action of the game. This will have players tracking their tower progress by placing tower cubes onto regions they possess. Players are encouraged to keep these tower cubes safe because losing them in war results in a loss of progress on the tower card.

Furthermore Tiny Epic Kingdoms: Heroes' Call introduces a slew of new factions, new regions with new mechanisms and a new resource type, and even brings solo play to Tiny Epic Kingdoms!

The lore of Tiny Epic Kingdoms: Heroes' Call occurs after Tiny Epic Kingdoms and before Tiny Epic Defenders.

description:
Boarding! is team microgame where players have to board the enemy ship, and defend their own.

Each player have 4 different cards/actions to choose from each round, they can choose to Fire!, Charge!, Defend! or just hide behind a Barrel, the game has a mechanism like Rock Paper Scissors, but all the cards have a different reward/risk relation, is safer to hide behind a barrel, but if the enemy charge, you are gonna walk the plank for sure!

This game is family oriented, or for a small party of 4, 6 even 8 players on each team

boardgame: The Rise and Fall of Galactic Empires
description:
The Rise and Fall of Galactic Empires is a game where the players garden the civilizations through the phases of birth, rise, glory, fall, and death.  It's played in turns, and each turn you do one of the following:


     Explore: push the boundaries of the galaxy and find seeds of civilizations.  Mechanically, draw and place a few hexagonal sector tiles and then deal and draft three event cards per player.



     Conquer: plant a civilization in a sector, in control over anyone who's already there.  Mechanically, play a card and place a matching disk on a tile.  Cards have special powers that trigger (most often) during this action.



     Reap: harvest the power of the civilizations you've cultivated, then decide whether they live or die.  Mechanically, simultaneously sell a card with the other players.  Its value is based on the value of sectors the matching civilization controls.  Then do a simultaneous bid to determine how many disks survive the reaping.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Pocket Sports Gridiron is a fast dice rolling game that see two teams attempting to drive to the end zone when they have possession of the football.

The objective is to complete a pass or run into the end zone for a touchdown, without your opponent’s defense preventing the play. As the game only uses dice, there is no need to measure yards or mark a line of scrimmage. Think of the game as a highlights package of your team on the offense drive. They either score or your opponent takes over on offense.

The Quarterback die will be the central die. Rolling this will choose the play 'called' by the QB. The subsequent die rolled next will be one of the other passing/running positions.

The WR , TE , & RB all have different plays on each die face with an 'intensity' value that the DEFENSE must match to prevent the play being successful. There are Field Goal options, Flags for penalties, Sacks, Intercepts, Fumbles and an Audible Play option (for PRO MODE)

The game includes 5 team cards to be used for an optional PRO MODE.

Light, compact and portable.

In Rocketships to Eos, players race to be the first to reach Planet Eos. Players will travel through 10 locations in the solar system, facing seen and unforeseen challenges that could jeopardize not only chance to be first to the planet - but their chances of making it in one piece!

Gameplay Summary:
Rocketships TE uses a combination of cards, dice, and stat tracking. Players choose between 1 of 3 actions each turn: move, repair/rest, and sabotage.  For each action, the d6 is rolled to determine the condition for performing that action - sometimes preventing the action from happening, or at least making it very costly. Along the journey, Players encounter malfunctions, asteroid fields, and supply stations in the form of Event Cards.

The era of this world is coming to a close. Before the planet collapses, we need you to extract the essence of the Five Elements so we can rebuild.

The game revolves around the idea of the Elemental Cycle: when elemental energies are used, rather than being depleted, they just change state.
Fire burns things, leaving only Earth.
Earth pours out springs of Water.
Water evaporates and becomes Wind.
Wind blows in storms with Lightning.
Lightning strikes and creates Fire.

This game's defining feature is the use of the "dice progression" mechanic. Each die has the five elements on a different face, with an arrow pointing to the next element in the cycle. During the game, the dice will be rotated, following the arrows, through the cycle of elements.

Use your dice to obtain cards, which in turn help you build clever combos to manipulate your dice even more. Align your dice and cards to get essence tokens. Whoever can collect essence of all Five Elements wins.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

boardgame: Battle of the Electric Vikings: None More Black
description: In the new expansion you will get 11 new cards based on the two Spinal Tap albums, "This is Spinal Tap" and "Break like the Wind" to play along with the original Battle of the Electric Vikings.

boardgame: Battle of the Electric Vikings: Thrall – Vikingsweatlive
description: 10 new cards based on one of the designer's favourite metal bands, Danzig. With the new words you'll be making awesome Danzig songs like: Mother, Left Hand Black, and It's Coming Down.

boardgame: Battle of the Electric Vikings: Can I Play With Vikings?
description: 10 new cards based on the ever popular Iron Maiden! With  the new words you'll be making great Maiden songs like: Run to the Hills, Aces High and Number of the Beast.

You and your fellow gang members have robbed a bank together, but while trying to divvy up the spoils, everyone's getting greedy and wanting more! With their loyalties now divided between the old boss and new, members are fighting one another over money — and naturally you'll try to take this opportunity to make a few suspicious deals of your own to take a share for yourself!

In Big Side, the game lasts several rounds, and in each phase each player takes one character card secretly, then after negotiation with the old boss decided which team to join. After everyone has divided into two groups, each player reveals their character and uses its ability. All players then simultaneously decide whether to join the battle or not, with the old boss and new having a battle with their followers to determine how to split the money.

After the third round, the player with the most money wins!

In The Writer: Lovecraft Playing Cards, only one player will prove worthy of the title "The Writer" by publishing the Cthulhu Mythos books using cards.

Each game card represents an idea for a story: a creature, a character, a place, an event... The game has eight unique stories, and you must use the card abilities to "write" these accounts to show that you are the authentic Lovecraft. The first player who completes the eight stories on their board is declared the true Lovecraft and wins!

boardgame: Lagoon: Land of Druids – Forest Sentinel / Ancient Sentinel
description:
This is the third promo tile for Lagoon: Land of Druids, introducing a new site type (Totem) with special rules given below.

Forest Sentinel (Side Numbered 3A)
Site type - Totem
Energy - Elemeen/Vowelon/Deonin*
Text - When you unravel any non-totem site, if any of your druids are not on Forest Sentinel, move 1 of them to Forest Sentinel.

Ancient Sentinel (Side Numbered 3B)
Site type - Totem
Energy - Elemeen/Vowelon/Deonin*
Text - When you unravel any non-totem site, gain 1 seed of that site's energy and move 1 of your druids on Ancient Sentinel to any haven.


     - A site with all 3 energies provides 1 energy of your choice for unraveling. A site with all 3 energies can be unraveled using 3 of any other single energy — either 3 yellow, 3 red, or 3 blue.


Totem Site Type Rules

When a totem site is unraveled, its tile is not removed from play like all other sites. Instead, when a totem site is unraveled, flip that site tile over to the site on the other side. The new site occupies the same position on the board as the original site. Additionally, all druids on the original site, including the unraveling druid, are transferred to the new site exhausted. The unraveling druid is not returned to its owner’s supply.

Note that, like any locked site, a locked totem site cannot be unraveled.

Promo Tile Setup Rules

To use the promo tile in a game of Lagoon, setup the game in the normal way. Then add the promo tile to the starting tile setup, adjacent to the two non-haven sites. Either side of the promo tile can begin face up.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Wrath of the Righteous Adventure Deck 4 – The Midnight Isles
description: The Wrath of the Righteous campaign continues with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: The Midnight Isles Adventure Deck. Venture into the abyssal realm of the Midnight Isles, lair of the succubus queen Nocticula and the source of foul infusions that threaten to give her demonic legions blasphemous new powers. This 110-card expansion adds new locations, monsters, villains, and more to the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game; it also includes rare loot cards and 5 new scenarios that make up the complete "The Midnight Isles" adventure.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
Battle of the Electric Vikings gives you full reign to make the most epic metal song titles and lyrics. Be your own Lemmy or Rob Halford or Bruce Dickinson. With a 100 card deck the possibilities are seemingly endless. No board to hold you back from making the most brutally epic metal title known to man! The only limit is yourself.

The rules and game play are simple:

2-6 player get 6 cards each to start and you create your own song titles or lyrics by placing cards horizontal, vertical and on top of other cards. 
Score the points that are on the cards. Next player builds off of what's out on the table. (Scrabble style)

Included in the pack is 100 cards with 88 different words and rule card all in Black Slither's signature packaging, big enough to hold expansion packs.

The Gods of Ásgard expansion:

Only two gods will be part of each game session. Randomly draw two god cards and place them next to the board so all players can see their powers. At the end of the initial setup, shuffle the Ragnarök tokens that are not on the board or on the Age Track and draw two of them. Look at the names of the two provinces listed on the tokens and place one god figure in each of them (these Ragnarök tokens are then put aside).

Each god's ability is always in effect, changing the rules of the province they're in. The rest of the provinces are not affected. After a player attempts to pillage a province containing a god figure, whether he succeeds in the pillaging or not, he must then move the god to a different unpillaged province that does not contain a god figure. So the two gods can never occupy the same province, and they never go to a pillaged or destroyed province.

At the beginning of the 2nd and 3rd Ages, the two gods are again placed in random provinces, determined by drawing 2 unused Ragnarök tokens.

The combination of the two gods used in each game creates a unique texture to that session. They affect draft choices and influence the flow of the battles. Will you try to benefit from a god's power again and again by moving him where you want and always pillaging his province? Or will you do your best to neutralize him by pillaging around his province? Perhaps you'll want to attempt a hopeless pillage on his province just to move him onto another province where he will hinder another player's plans. They add a whole new layer to the game, without adding hardly any complexity to the rules.

The miniatures each come with a tarot-sized God card.

description:
Red Baron is a casino game where up to six players square off against the dealer in a format very similar to Blackjack. The goal is to "shoot down" the dealer by finishing with a point total that is equal to, or not more than three points less than, the dealer's.

The deck contains 70 cards: ten each of the numbers 1 through 6, and ten Red Baron cards, which are worth 0 points.

During the American Revolution, no officer on the rebel side was a better field commander than Major General Benedict Arnold. This has been overlooked for well over two centuries; nothing is taught about Arnold except his treason. The fact is: if not for him, the rebellion would probably have failed and its principal players hanged. If he had died at Saratoga instead of being seriously wounded, his legacy would have been nothing short of legendary. Cities, counties and perhaps even a state would be named after him. Statues of Arnold would be standing in parks nationwide and his monument would be visited by throngs of people in the District of Columbia every day.

Unfortunately, Arnold had enemies as well as supporters, and those enemies contributed significantly to his conclusion that the colonies were not yet ready for independence. In Benedict Arnold Poker, a breezy combination of poker and rummy, you will meet some of the men in each group, and Arnold himself.

[Note: this game is an unfinished, although playable prototype. - ASF]

Starting the Game: Players choose a playing piece and place it on Start Space. Roll the die to choose player rank, if a tie youngest player goes first. Each player starts the game with 10 Coins. Place Bowser Cards and Event Cards in marked areas on the game board. Place the Coins within reach of all players. Place one Star in pawn holder and follow diagram below under Buying a Star for placement. Place all remaining unused stars off to the side of the board until needed.

Objective: Be the player with the most collected Stars at the end of the game to be the winner of the Mushroom Kingdom!

Winner: Player with the most collected Stars after 20 Rounds. After each player has taken a turn a round has finished. Keep track of which round you are on by moving the round counter after each round finishes.

family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
※The first player will be the one who has the most recently eaten a hamburger.
1. The first player reveals the ingredient cards from the card deck, placing on the game table. 2-player: 4 cards, 3-player: 5 cards, 4-player: 7 cards, 5-player: 8 cards.
places it on the second deck. (But the rat card is not an ingredient.) She reveals the fourth mushroom card and places it on the second deck. She reveals the fifth cheese card, places it on the first deck, and stops revealing the cards.
3. If all decks are made, each player chooses one of the tools cards (tongs card or teas card) and places it face down in front of him/her. If there is only one tools card left for each player, take all the used cards to their hands.
4. Reveal the tools cards at once. The player who played the teas card cannot take any ingredient deck. For all players who played the tongs card, take one of the ingredient decks in clockwise order starting from the next player of the first player, till there is no ingredient deck left.
If all players played the teas card, all ingredient decks are placed on the first player’s wastebasket.
Place the used tools cards into a deck, keeping them secret to other players.
2. The first player arranges the cards on the table one by one, according to the number of players. Then, place the    game rest of the cards on those to make new decks. The rat card is not considered as ingredient, but should be placed on one of the new decks.
Example for 2-player game
Alice reveals the first lettuce card and places it on the first new deck. She reveals the
5. For players who took the ingredient deck, stack the ingredient cards to make a hamburger.
5-1. If a rat card is included in the ingredient deck, take the rat tokens as the number of rat cards, and place the
second onion card and places it on the second new deck. She reveals the third rat card and
After placing the cards, Alice plays a tongs card. Other two players play a teas card. Alice
takes all the ingredient decks on the table.
Example for 3-player
rat cards under the wastebasket card. The rat tokens should not be revealed.
5-2. To stack the ingredients, the bottom bun should be placed first. If there is no bottom bun card, flip one of the ingredient cards to use. (Since the rat card is discarded, it cannot be flipped into the bottom bun.)
5-3. To make the complete hamburger, it should contain both bottom and top bun, with ingredients according to the recipe. The top bun must be placed after all other ingredients are placed.
5-4. Hamburgers can only be made according to the recipe. The ingredients may be stacked regardless of the order.
5-5. Place the unused ingredient cards under the wastebasket card and keep them hidden.
6. The game ends if there are 8 or more complete hamburgers in 2- or 3-player game, 9 hamburgers in 4-player
game, and 10 hamburgers in 5-player game. Also, the game ends if there are no cards enough left in the draw deck to make new ingredient decks. If any of the conditions are not satisfied, the player who takes the last ingredient deck becomes the first player. Go back to No.1 and continue the game.
7. After the game ends, you may flip the wastebasket card to use as the last top bun.

boardgame: Lagoon: Land of Druids – Mytreia's Glory / Cosmic Vault
description:
This is the second promo tile for Lagoon: Land of Druids, introducing a new site type (Totem) with special rules given below.

Mytreia's Glory (Side Numbered 2A)
Site type - Totem
Energy - Elemeen/Vowelon/Deonin*
If Mytreia's Glory is in play at the end of the game, seeds of the dominant energy are worth 2 Destiny Points instead of 1.
- The infinity action type is new. It mean that the tile has end of game effects.

Cosmic Vault (Side Numbered 2B)
Site type - Totem
Energy - Astral*
If the invoking druid's site is not a haven, gain 1 seed of that site's energy and move the invoking druid to any haven.

Triple Energy Rules

A site with all 3 energies provides 1 energy of your choice for unraveling. A site with all 3 energies can be unraveled using 3 of any other single energy — either 3 yellow, 3 red, or 3 blue.

Astral Energy Rules

Cosmic Vault is a site of the astral energy, identified by purple seed icons having a crystalline shape as well as a purple tile border. The astral energy is cosmic in nature — powerful, mysterious, and beyond human understanding.

The energy provided by occupying an astral site cannot be used to unravel any other site. However, an astral site can be unraveled. The required energy to unravel an astral site is three of any other single energy — either three yellow, three red, or three blue.

Astral sites also have the Astral keyword appearing in italics at the bottom of their text boxes. Whenever a player summons a druid, if any sites with the Astral keyword are on the board, they may choose for the summoned druid to enter play on a site with the Astral keyword instead of on a haven site. Sites with the Astral keyword do not count as havens in any way, however.

Totem Site Type Rules

When a totem site is unraveled, its tile is not removed from play like all other sites. Instead, when a totem site is unraveled, flip that site tile over to the site on the other side. The new site occupies the same position on the board as the original site. Additionally, all druids on the original site, including the unraveling druid, are transferred to the new site exhausted. The unraveling druid is not returned to its owner’s supply.

Note that, like any locked site, a locked totem site cannot be unraveled.

Promo Tile Setup Rules

To use the promo tile in a game of Lagoon, setup the game in the normal way. Then add the promo tile to the starting tile setup, adjacent to the two non-haven sites. Either side of the promo tile can begin face up.

boardgame: Neuroshima Tactics: The Outpost – Assault Troopers II
description:
30 years ago...civilization as we know it was blasted to smithereens. Later we learned what happened - that it was the rebellion of machines, but back then it was pure chaos. Bombs vanquished cities and factories, architecture and culture, science and social etiquette. In the radioactive pyres, raging pandemics and chemical assaults an immense number of good people lost their lives. Today...the nuclear holocaust marks an end to taxes, school exams and insurance brokers, so there were some perks to all this mess.

Neuroshima Tactics is a dynamic skirmish wargame with immense tactical possibilities. It's a stealth shooter, with a twisted Mad Max vibe. Sneaking. Shooting. Neuroshima. Are you in? This is a 28mm tabletop based game. The English rulebook is available for free download online. "Tough guys in full battle gear: heavy guns, Kevlar armour and unquenchable thirst for battle. There's no room for wusses among their ranks - they are the strongest regular Squads of the Outpost, a true elite. They carry out the riskiest missions, defend the most important strategical spots, on front-end posts, where the enemy is superior in numbers. Hardened in the heat of battle and very experienced, they are key Outpost unit. They can break the enemy resistance in a flash, and hold the seized zones for indefinite amounts of time. They're great in defense - perfect in attack. They're awesome shooters, and even if they get hit, their sturdy armour and great Constitution allows them to push through more, than anyone else could take."

Assault Troopers II, The Outpost. Blister includes: 1 Assault Trooper 3 figure, 1 Assault Trooper 4 figure, 2 bases, 1 dual language location card & 5 counters.

Six Gods have forged a world together from their elemental powers, and now vie to control them. Realms is a bidding and bluffing game for 2-6 players.

Each round, players bid for control of "Realms," cards that are selected for auction by the active player. The only currency players have is the cards in their hand (the same cards that represent the Realms for auction), but each of those cards also contains a scoring condition at the end of the game.

How will you use your cards? Will you bid them often and try to control as many realms as possible? Or will you pick your spots and focus on scoring in specific ways?

Players are pressured keep cards in hard for scoring and later bids, but they have to bid successfully to claim the Realms needed to make the scoring cards as effective as possible - all while setting traps and avoiding the traps of the other Gods.

Realms plays in about 10 minutes per player, and will be available in print-and-play soon. For questions, please contact the designer, JR Honeycutt.

From the rules:
A new construction zone has been designated. The architects want to develop it and are planning new houses and parks on the building area.

Your task in Plan B is to be the first to build three houses of the same colour next to each other on the building area. Only horizontal and vertical combinations are allowed.

Choose your Character...Ready...FIGHT!

Button Bashers Turbo is a 2-6 player, fast-paced, tabletop dueling card game modeled after popular arcade style fighting games.

Players choose from 2 of 12 possible characters, each with a personalized list of attacks to help you pummel your opponent into the ground! Be the first to reduce your opponents life-points from 20 to 0 to win the game!

Button Bashers Turbo features multiple modes of play, including Versus Mode (1v1),Tag Team Mode (2v2, 3v3), FFA (Free for All), and Possibly Boss Mode (all versus one).

With easy to learn combos, stylized strategic gameplay, and a little luck of the cards, you will send your friends running to the change machine.
