The first to gain 42 followers is the winner! Gain Followers by sending Disciples on Mission Trips. Have a Preach-Off to take out an enemy. Defend against enemy Disciples with Agnostics. Beef up, slow down, or mess with the guys in play using Blessings, Curses, and Miracles. Baby Jesus, the scorekeeper, also has super powers.

How to Play:

All cards that you play or actions that you take happen on your own turn, in any order that you like.

Communion Wafers are the currency for this game. The number on the top left of a card is how many wafers it costs to play. On the first turn you can only spend one wafer. The second turn you have two to spend. By the time the third turn comes around… You guessed it! 3 wafers! And so on. Spend them wisely and you will dominate. As the game goes on, things get very exciting because you are able to play more powerful cards. If you lose track of how many wafers you have available: Smoke less weed or keep track with dice. Your choice but the author prefers the latter.

Each player starts with their Baby Jesus to the right of their deck, to help keep score. Slide a paper clip around Baby Jesus and when you get to 42 Followers, you become the winner, but more importantly - they become the loser. Baby Jesus' Super Powers help keep the game moving and are great for using up excess wafers. Only one Relic may be played on Baby Jesus at a time and may be used to damage an enemy once per turn. When it has been exhausted, it is sent to hell, a warm cozy place to the left of your deck.

Besides Baby Jesus and his Relics, there are two main types of cards:

1) Character cards

Characters have Attack and Defense. This is how they compete with each other. If their Defense reaches zero, they are sent straight to hell. During a Mission Trip, Followers are gained equal to the Disciple’s Attack number.

Agnostics - Defenders who keep your opponent from gathering Followers. If your enemy has an Agnostic in play, you must send it to hell before your Disciples can go on a Mission Trip or have a Preach-Off against a non-Agnostic.
Disciples - Offenders who may either be sent on a Mission Trip or have a Preach-Off once per turn. Without gaining Followers, you cannot win so it usually makes more sense to go on a Mission Trip. If you do elect to have a Preach-Off this is how it works: You choose the character you would like to Preach off against (remember, Agnostics must be targeted first). Defense points are deducted from both characters equal to the attack points of the opposing character. This happens at the same time. So, if a 1/5 (1 Attack, 5 Defense) dude attacks a 2/1 enemy, the enemy will perish and your Disciple is now a 1/3 [mark damage taken with pennies]. Moreover, it might be a smart move to sacrifice a 1/1 against an 8/1 to take him out of commision.

2) Manipulation cards

Unless otherwise stated, Blessings and Curses affect only one character. These cards may stay in play to help keep track of who they have an effect on. Whenever they become irrelevant, feel free to toss them into the fiery pit.

Blessings - Turn your dudes into badasses.  
Curses - Mess with your enemies.  
Miracles - Save these for when you could really use one.

Now let's begin:

The game starts with each player shuffling their own deck and drawing 4 cards. Good cards to start with cost 1 or 2 wafers; you will be able to use them sooner. If you aren't happy with your hand, you may shuffle the cards you don't like back into the deck and replace them. You may do this twice. If you still don't like your hand, too bad. You'll draw a new card every turn so don't make a big fuss about it. Now flip a coin. The winner chooses to either go first (starting with just the 4 cards they have) or draw first.

Alternative 4 player version (2-Headed-Jesus): Each team helps one Baby Jesus attract 84 followers. Each player has their own deck. Make your moves at the same time as your teammate. Help each other out. You may look at each other’s hands to help strategize.

Remember to have fun. Because if you're not having fun... What's the point?

If you're into card games, ultimate, or yeti attacks this is the game for you. It's a perfect party game, 2 decks allow for 16 players.

Deal 5 cards to each player.
Flip over a goal card from the goal deck.
Players can choose from one of three card types:
Offence (O), Defence (D), and Game Changer (GC).
Having the highest O-Card on the board at the end of the round is the primary way to collect Goal cards.
D-cards allow players to steal O-cards of equal or lesser value.
GC-cards add some mayham to the round and include powerful effects.
The first player to reach 7 goals wins.
Once obtained, Goal cards are hidden in your hand so that opponents cannot easily sum up your total - trickery, deception, and subterfuge are encouraged.

Offense Cards : Dump, Scoober, Hammer Time, Pinpoint Huck, Jimmy Mickle, Brodie Smith

Defense Cards:  Tall Grass, Tree-Fence, Hand-Block, Gale Force Winds, Jonathan Helton, Mark Lloyd

Game Changers : Wholesale, Bird Block, Mixed, Give and Go-al, Poach, Integrity Rule, Good Spirit, Empire Builder, Yeti Attack, Beau Kittredge (Dual Threat), Injury Sub, Timeout

Booster Cards: Buzzer Beater, Wiley Vet, Lockdown Defender, Callahan, Hospital Pass, Radicals Zone, Royal Crowd Surge, Double Team, Flow

boardgame: Help Star Swirl Find the Valentine's Day Picnic!
description:
Help Star Swirl Find the Valentine's Day Picnic! is a simple My Little Pony-themed flip (a coin, that is) and move game published on the back cover of the children's picture book "Valentine's Day, Up...Up...and Away!"

In this game, players hope to be the first to move the length of the track and reach the Valentine's Day picnic at the end. Each player flips a coin and moves one space for heads and two spaces for tails.  They may land on spaces that move them forward or back, cause them to lose a turn or return them to the start.  The first player to reach the finish wins.

Big Bucks is one of the games published in Math on a Roll, a book of number games using polyhedral dice. It is designed to help teach adding decimals to children. It is played with a 20-sided and a 12-sided die. In turn, players roll the two dice. The smaller number represents the dollars they gain, and the larger number the cents.  Players take turns, adding up their totals and they go. After ten turns, the player with the highest total wins.

With ten sculpted plastic figures, twelve double-sided map tiles, and over sixty new cards, Twin Shadows brings new levels of danger and skulduggery to every Imperial Assault game.

description: Intercardinal is an asymmetric N-in-a-row game.  Players take turns placing pieces on a 12x8 board.  The first to make a row of 4 contiguous pieces of their colour in a North/South direction (the short side) or 5 in an East/West direction (the long side) wins.  Diagonal lines do not count.
Intercardinal X is an asymmetric N-in-a-row game. Players take turns placing pieces on a 15x15 board. The first to make a row of 6 contiguous pieces of their colour orthogonally or 5 diagonally wins.

Intercardinal Yavalath is an asymmetric N-in-a-row game.  Players take turns placing pieces on an 8x8 board.  The first to make a row of 4 contiguous pieces of their colour orthogonally wins, but a line of 3 pieces diagonally loses.

boardgame: The Godfather: An Offer You Can't Refuse
description:
Description from the publisher:

Hunt for those who would take sides against the family. The Godfather: An Offer You Can't Refuse is a mafia-style, deductive party game based on the iconic film trilogy by Francis Ford Coppola that starred Marlin Brando as Don Corleone.

In this card game, players play as either members of the Corleone crime family or undercover cops trying to end the reign of the Corleones. Played out over several rounds, the tension of the game comes in trying to figure out who's on which side, and never knowing when you might receive an offer you can't refuse!

Unfair is an asymmetric N-in-a-row game.  Players take turns placing pieces on an 11x11 board.

White plays first, and places two pieces per turn.
Black plays second, and places four pieces per turn.

White wins by making an orthogonal contiguous line of 4 pieces.
Black wins by making an orthogonal contiguous line of 7 pieces.

Diagonal lines do not count.

VikinGalaxy is a cooperative game for 2-4 players with an average playing time of 45 minutes. Players take the role of the crew of a ship of Viking exploration mission to find new worlds to conquer, but one of these is attacked by a dark force, and the goal is to survive to give notice to the fleet.

The game consists of 6 phases of play, each with a number of attacks, which are added to increase the number of phases, begins with 2 attacks in the first phase and ends with 7 in the sixth. Attacks can be overcome according to the reaction card out, and you can meet with your runic letters and your runic dice roll.

Now the turn of mechanical reaction all the cards are removed and replaced with white sheets explosion. and the crew began their shift repairing these tabs with the combination you have in your letter. if the end of the shift from mechanical two chips of white explosion in a sector, these are converted into a red tab explosion.

The board consists of 7 sections of which 6 are marked with numbers, which is not the core, these sectors are destroyed if they have 2 records red explosion and core 3.

The game ends or exceeding the 6 phases of play or the destruction of the nucleus.


VikinGalaxy es un juego cooperativo de 2 a 4 jugadores con un tiempo de juego promedio de 45 minutos. Los jugadores toman el papel de los tripulantes de una nave de exploración vikinga con la misión de encontrar nuevos mundos para conquistarlos, pero uno de estos es atacado por una fuerza oscura, y el objetivo es sobrevivir para dar aviso a la flota.

El juego consta de 6 fases de juego y cada uno con cierta cantidad de ataques, de los cuales van sumando al aumentar el numero de fases, se inicia con 2 ataques en la primera fase y termina con 7 en la sexta. 
Cada jugador al iniciar la primera fase toma 5 cartas rúnicas e inicia el Navegante, seguido del artillero, ingeniero y por ultimo la mecánica.

Los ataques pueden ser superados según la carta de reacción salga, y la puedes igualar con tus cartas rúnicas y tu tirada de dados rúnicos.

Llegado el turno de la mecánica se retiran todas las cartas de reacción y se reemplazan por las fichas de explosión blanca. y este tripulante comenzara su turno reparando estas fichas con la combinación que tenga en su carta. si al finalizar el turno de la mecánica hay dos fichas de explosión blancas en un sector, estas se convierten en una ficha de explosión roja.

El tablero consta de 7 sectores de los cuales 6 están marcados con números, el que no es el núcleo, estas sectores se destruyen si llegan a haber 2 fichas de explosión roja y el núcleo con 3.

El juego termina o superando las 6 fases de juego o con la destrucción del núcleo.

boardgame: Duel in the North: The Leningrad Campaign, Jun-Sep 1941
description:
Duel in the North: The Leningrad Campaign, Jun-Sep 1941  simulates fighting between Soviet and German forces in the Leningrad region from 22 June to 27 Sep, 1941.

This design covers the campaign of Germany's Army Group North, from the border to Leningrad, during the first year of the war in the east. This game uses the same rules system as Sedan: The Decisive Battle for France, May 1940 (W24), Tobruk: Operation Crusader, Nov-Dec, 1941 (ST278) and Duel on the Steppe (ST285), albeit with some occasional modifications appropriate to the scale and theater portrayed.

The map area portrays the Leningrad region of the Soviet Union, encompassing about 600 miles from east to west; each hexagon is about 12.5 miles. The various game pieces represent the historic combat units, as well as all necessary chits to facilitate game play. Soviet combat units are primarily divisions, the German units are divisions and Kampfgruppen and are color-coded by nationality and command affiliation. Combat units may represent from 2,000 to 14,000 men, and 50-400 armored vehicles.

World at War #48 April-May 2016

Game Scale:
Game Turn: 6 days
Hex: 12.5 miles / 20 kilometers
Units: Kampgruppe to Army

Game Inventory:
One 22 x 34" full color mapsheet
One dual-side printed countersheet (280 1/2" counters)
One 16-page rulebook

Solitaire Playability: Medium
Complexity Level: Medium
Players: 2 or more
Playing Time: 4-15

This counter set includes one errata counter (the blue 4-6-6 mechanized division) for the game Red Tide West (Modern War #15).

boardgame: New World Order Battles: Kiev & Ulan Bator
description:
New World Order Battles is a grand tactical wargame of battles that were, or could possibly be fought, in the era since the fall of the Berlin Wall, and even into the near future. Players control actual and hypothetical combat units using a wide range of technologies and forces to gain objectives. Two scenarios are included: Kiev, covering a hypothetical Russian clash with NATO forces at the Ukrainian capital, and Ulaan Baatar, a hypothetical clash between Russian and Chinese forces at the Mongolian capital.

There are two players in each game, commanding opposing forces. Both games use a system of shared rules, and each also has its own scenario special instructions. The game pieces represent military units, supporting firepower, and Netwar operations.

Modern War #22, Mar-Apr 2016

boardgame: Thomas & Friends Tipsy Topsy Turvy Game
description:
This is a dexterity game based on the classic Thomas Tank Engine cartoon. The board is a 3-D railroad track display with a Thomas engine and a freight car. Players take turns drawing freight tiles. Each child must place the matching item (barrel, logs, etc.) or place an item of the matching color. The stack grows more unstable as the game continues. Other cards require a child to rotate the track, and this can cause the pile to tumble.

Items that fall off of the pile are returned to play. When the last tile in the deck has been flipped over the game ends. The player who successfully placed the most items is declared the winner.
family: TV Shows: Thomas the Tank Engine

description:
English description:

A Colmeia - Micro-game (The Beehive - Micro-game) is a game reimplementation of A Colmeia.

In this game, players will have to leave with his bee his hive, and take 3 nectar the game board, and return to their hive. The first player to do this will be the winner.

To move your bee, use tiles of arrows that will make your bee walk through the board. There is also, at the end of your turn, choose 1 tile (Green, Yellow or Red) and put these Tiles in Tray. These Tiles, change the tempo of the game, adding new barriers and also giving some advantages to bees. You can also earn Nectar + 1, more trees or problems with pesticides.

Portuguese description:

A Colmeia - Micro-jogo é uma reimplementação do jogo A Colmeia.
Neste jogo, os jogadores terão que sair com sua abelha de sua colmeia, e pegar 3 néctares pelo tabuleiro do jogo, e voltar para sua colmeia. O primeiro jogador que fizer isso, será o vencedor.
Para mover sua abelha, utilizará tiles de setas que fará com que sua abelha ande pelo tabuleiro. Terá também, no final de seu turno, que escolher 1 tile (Verde, Amarelo ou Vermelho) e colocar estes Tiles no Tabuleiro. Estes Tiles, alteram o andamento do jogo, acrescentando novas barreiras e também dando algumas vantagens para as abelhas. Poderá também ganhar + 1 Nectar, mais árvores ou surgirem problemas com agrotóxicos.

Machi Koro: Deluxe Edition is a game for 2-5 players in which each player develop the city on their own in order to complete all of the landmarks under construction faster than their rivals. On their turn, each player rolls one or two dice. If the sum of the dice rolled matches the number of a building that a player owns, they get the effect of that building. The first player to construct all of their landmarks wins!

Machi Koro: Deluxe Edition features the base game, Harbor expansion, Millionaire's Row expansion, upgraded punchboard coins, two sculpted dice, an exclusive drawstring coin bag, six copies of "Convention Center" card, and six copies of the exclusive "Diamine" card! Only available in this printing, the Diamine features a special appearance by the diamond hungry Diamonsters!

description:
Little Football Game (LFG) is a fast tactical card game in which you control your own football team. Every team has it's own strengths and weaknesses. So you must choose your tactics wisely.

A game is played by 2 players and will last only 20 minutes. You can play the game in tournament mode and play several matches, so more players can join in.

There are 3 types of players: Defenders, midfielders and attackers. You can only play them when the ball marker is in the corresponding playing area on the pitch card. Every player has 14 player cards. You can choose which cards to play first, but keep in mind that you can play each card only once.

The game has a balanced system between several player qualities. Some teams are stronger, but more likely to make fouls. Some teams are weaker but are very experienced or specialized at set pieces. etc.
 Every box contains 3 different teams. There are already 6 different teams made for the game. When you buy a game, you decide which 3 out of 6 teams you get in your box. As an expansion, you can get the other teams later on if you like.

After the auctions phase, the game takes place in different hands. Each player must reconstruct the perforated cardboard on your TV the same path of red and green stamps and which are reproduced on the diagram of the presenter. During each hand is also held a treasure hunt to find the green peg. At this point you will face the test of the quiz that will to win more points.

boardgame: Least I Could Do: The Card Game – Ladies Night
description:
Play as some of your favourite ladies from the series (and Gayne!) as you do your best to pick up as many fellas as you can.

Use Action Cards to boost your stats or cripple your friends and, whatever you do, watch out for Creepsters!

Stand-alone game OR add it to the original!

Your lips are moving while you're reading. That's just weird.

THE KINGDOMS OF HUMANITY STAND AS ONE.

The rising threat of the Cryxian armies has become too great. Death permeates the war-torn region, and Empress Ayn and King Leto themselves chance incredible danger to meet and coordinate their stand. The Protectorate’s Northern Crusade joins Khador and Cygnar in a final alliance of men against the unending armies of the Dragonfather. As these armies combine to expel the deathless forces of Cryx from the mainland, in tunnels below the battleground a lone Khadoran soldier fights to save one of the Motherland’s greatest legends.

WarMachine: Reckoning brings you the next thrilling chapter of the WarMachine saga. Fight for survival with:


    New warcasters, including new heroes as well as epic incarnations of some of WarMachine’s most feared and revered legends.
    A new generation of colossals to level the opposition.
    An array of new warjacks, including the personal warjacks of several powerful warcasters.
    New solos to bring a greater range of utility to the battlefield.
    New narrative fiction continuing the action of WarMachine: Vengeance.
    A painting and modeling guide to help you prepare your forces for battle.
    Theme Force lists for each new warcaster, which allow you to create armies based on specialized forces found in the WARMACHINE world.

Coin Horror is an abstract microgame for 2 players set in Myths of Cthulhu. Players must solve a case in the town of Arkham, and prevent at all costs the plans of the evil cultists.

To play you only need one board -from size of a coasters-, and a handful of coins.

In turn, players place coins on the board to score better than your opponent, while trying not to lose all the Stamina and Sanity. The game ends if any of these cases occurs:


    The fourth coin of Doom is placed.
    The two players can not put more coins or decide not to.
    Both players are eliminated.


The player with more Clue (Victory Points) wins the game.
A card game of rummy in which players form correct verb/adverb combinations of two or more cards. Reinforce language arts skills with the 60 cards in the deck.
A card game of rummy in which players collect two or more cards showing synonyms. Reinforce language arts skills with the 60 cards in this deck.
A card game of rummy in which players collect two or more cards showing the three parts of a contraction (I, will, I'll). Reinforce language arts with the 60 cards in this deck.

boardgame: La traversée de la baie du Mont Saint-Michel à D
description:
The players are moving their pilgrims from one side of the bay to the other side. They use the three white dices to move to adjacent spaces (each space has a number form 1 to 6) or to jump over other pilgrims as a group effect. A gray dice cause good (collecting an archangel cube) and bad (quicksands) events to occur. Each turn the tide moves forward and any pilgrim reached by the tide drown.

From the publisher:

It cannot be stressed too often, you shouldn't attempt to cross the Mont Saint-Michel bay without an experienced guide.
Be warned, if you attempt this on your own, you do so entirely at your own risk. Crossing the bay is certainly no picnic!
You'll need to keep clear of the quicksands, take a biscuit break at the right moment and, above all, keep a careful watch
on the rising tide in order to reach your goal. Luckily for you, archangel Saint-Michel is there to help...

Capitalise on the best combinations of the dice, making good use of the biscuit pieces and archangel cubes to move forward as quickly as
possible. The winner is the first player to reach the Mont Saint-Michel with their two pilgrims.
If everyone is washed away by the tide, then no one wins, unless someone has taken refuge at Tombelaine.

Beer Party is a fast game card, in which players try to serve and drink more beer than the opponent.
The more you drink the closer you are to victory ,but the harder it will achieve it !

Players will play face down cards in columns that form the "jars" (setting the correct cards). You may consult cards and swap cards every turn, until someone decides to "drink" one. If you can drink 3 beers will have finished the game! Time to count points.


Beer Party es un juego de cartas rápido y de reglas simples, en el que los jugadores tratarán de servir y beber más cervezas que el rival. 
Cuantas más bebas más cerca estarás de la victoria, ¡pero más difícil será alcanzarla!

Los jugadores irán jugando cartas ocultas en columnas que formarán las "jarras". Estás cartas podrán consultarse y moverse cada turno, hasta que alguien decida "beber" una de ellas. Si consigues beber 3 cervezas ¡habrá terminado la partida! Hora de contar puntos.

User summary:
Mayor is a co-operative board game themed on the election of a local mayor. The game works as a simulation of the process in Japan, with players acting the roles in the election headquarters. They decide the different campaign strategies needed in each district of the city, with the aim of their candidate winning the election.

The game is published in Japanese only, but English rules are available for download.

Publisher summary:
地方市長選挙がテーマの全員協力型シミュレーションゲーム。
プレイヤーは選挙対策本部の役員となり、市内の各地区へどのような選挙活動を行うか戦略を練り、市長候補者の当選を目指します。

Kids dice game. Roll two dice per turn, and pick up either one or two bones, based on the dice combination, or claim an empty bowl. The player with the most bones (or puppies in empty bowls) at the end of the game wins.
The box states a starting age of 7, but kids age 5+ should have no problem playing this.

From the box: Rolly, the tiny and ever-hungry Dalmatian puppy, is not the only one intent on hoarding as many bones as possible for himself. All his cute little friends are just as eager and make a dash, barking loudly, to empty the doggy bowls. The one who retrieves the greatest number of bones is the winner.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Imperial Class Star Destroyer Expansion Pack
description:
At the height of the Galactic Civil War, the Imperial-class Star Destroyer was the commanding cornerstone of the Imperial Navy. Measuring 1,600 meters in length, a single Imperial-class Star Destroyer would boast a crew of more than 37,000 and carried a standard complement of four TIE fighter squadrons, one TIE interceptor squadron, and one squadron of TIE bombers. It could also carry an entire legion of stormtroopers, along with AT-ATs, AT-STs, and other landing craft. Most importantly, each Imperial-class Star Destroyer sported armament powerful enough to bring wayward systems to heel under Imperial control.

The Imperial-class Star Destroyer Expansion Pack brings this behemoth battleship to your games of Armada with one pre-painted miniature, two ship cards, three command dials, eighteen upgrades, and all requisite tokens.

Moreover, even as it affords you the opportunities to snare your foes with a tractor beam and fly your fleet to battle under the command of Darth Vader, the Imperial-class Star Destroyer Expansion Pack introduces a new type of defense token to your Imperial fleet: the Contain defense token. Featured on both the Imperial I-class and Imperial II-class Star Destroyers, the Contain token can cancel the standard critical effect of a shot that passes through your shields to your hull. Given the devastating nature of many of the critical effects in the Armada damage deck, the Contain token is a valuable tool that you'll want to spend wisely as you use your Star Destroyers to spread fear throughout the galaxy.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Home One Expansion Pack
description:
Admiral Ackbar and his flagship star cruiser, Home One, arrive to Armada in the Home One Expansion Pack. Featuring one large-size, pre-painted miniature MC80 star cruiser, two ship cards, three command dials, and fourteen upgrades, the Home One Expansion Pack gives the Rebel Alliance a ship that can trade blow for blow with nearly any Imperial vessel.

Boasting battery armaments of six dice from both its left and right hull zones, the MC80 star cruiser is as hard-hitting as a Victory-class Star Destroyer and the Rebellion's best answer to the Imperial-class Star Destroyer. Better yet, this Mon Calamari starship is even more powerful when flown under the command of Admiral Ackbar, who can add two red attack dice to any attack made by a starship that fires only from its left and right hull zones during its activation.

Will you race your star cruiser boldly down the middle of the Imperial fleet, firing at enemy ships on both sides? Will you dare your opponent to allow you the use of the Advanced Gunnery objective? Or will you circle the Imperial fleet at a distance, taking shots from a battery armament enhanced by Admiral Ackbar, Slaved Turrets, and the Defiance title? No matter how you bring it to battle, the MC80 star cruiser gives you the muscle to employ a whole new set of tactics in your battles for galactic freedom.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Rogues and Villains Expansion Pack
description:
Han Solo, Boba Fett, and a handful of the galaxy's most notorious rogues and villains race to the battlefield in the Rogues and Villains Expansion Pack for Armada!

Featuring some of the Star Wars galaxy's best known freighters, patrol craft, and bombers, the Rogues and Villains Expansion Pack introduces eight new irregular squadrons to Armada, each of which can be represented by either a non-unique squadron card or flown by an ace. Like the squadrons from the Core Set and the first wave of expansions, these come unpainted, but are presented in colors that complement their fleets.

Meanwhile, since these squadrons include the signature starships of some of the galaxy's most individualistic pilots, each is represented by a single miniature, and several of them allow you to take advantage of the new Rogue keyword, which grants a squadron the ability to both move and attack when it's activated during the squadron phase. Still, that's not the only new keyword introduced in this collection; both the Grit and Intel keywords appear on a number of its squadrons and introduce new tactical abilities that can help you steer the course of your Armada conflicts.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Imperial Raider Expansion Pack
description:
First introduced to the world as a huge starship designed for Epic games of X-Wing™, the Raider-class corvette is the Empire's response to the need for a designated anti-fighter vessel. Its six dual heavy laser cannons are more accurate against snubfighters than the turbolaser batteries of its Star Destroyer cousins, and the disruptive effects of its ion cannon emplacements, along with the efficiency of its localized command, make it a powerful addition to small strike forces.

This potent anti-fighter starship enters Armada in the Imperial Raider Expansion Pack. The first small ship for the Imperial Navy, the Raider is a quick and nimble ship that excels at dealing with pesky starfighter squadrons, but it can just as easily dart across the battlefield to pour concentrated fire into the broadside of a Rebel cruiser. Whether you field your pre-painted miniature as a Raider I-class Corvette or Raider II-class Corvette, the Imperial Raider Expansion Pack offers you plenty of ways to customize it for battle with either squadrons or capital ships. Among its eight upgrade cards, you'll find Ordnance Experts, a new commander, Quad Laser Turrets, two unique titles, and more.

Of course, as always, the Imperial Raider Expansion Pack comes with a command dial, speed dial, and all the tokens you need to enter the fray.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – MC30c Frigate Expansion Pack
description:
One of the most powerful mid-level ships in the Galactic Civil War, the MC30c frigate was of Mon Calamari design and presented a lethal combination of speed, high-powered armament, and advanced deflector shields. The trade-off, however, was that the ship was relatively light on hull plating. This trade-off becomes evident, as well, in the MC30c Frigate Expansion Pack, which offers this ship to the Rebel Alliance as a fast, nimble, small-size vessel that can fire as many as five attack dice from its left and right hull zones. However, to do so, it must get within close range of enemy ships where its shields won't long protect it from taking damage against its four hull points.

In addition to its pre-painted MC30c frigate miniature, this expansion pack contains two command dials and ten upgrade cards, along with all the tokens you need to bring this Rebel ship to battle. Of course, as with other Armada expansion packs, the MC30c Frigate Expansion Pack also comes with two ship cards that let you field your MC30c as either the MC30c Scout Frigate or MC30c Torpedo Frigate. Though they share many of the same design elements, the different costs and battery armaments of these two different configurations ensure a wide range of tactical flexibility for your MC30c.

boardgame: Ghostbusters: The Board Game – Kickstarter Exclusives
description:
A number of bonus characters, bosses, ghosts, tiles, and other upgrades. All unlocked as stretchgoals, and offered only to Kickstarter backers. Comprised of:

5 x characters (Janine Melnitz, Kylie Griffin, Ron Alexander, Melanie Ortiz, and the Rookie)
2 x Tilesets (Library/Bookstacks and Shandor Building rooftop/interior)
2 x Giant Bosses (Cathulhu [75mm] and Boogaloo [100mm])
2 x Minion sets (Spawn of Cathulhu and Fearsome Flushes)
7 x individual entities (Zuul, Vinz Clortho, Gozer, Grey Lady, Boogieman, Samhain, and Ivo Shandor)
1 x Walter Peck
1 x Tobin's Spirit Guide tuckbox

There are 9 red and 9 blue meeples in 3 different sizes. Each player has 3 meeples of the same color and size. The red meeples represent native American warriors of 3 tribes and the blue ones white settlers, cowboys and trappers. The players start at space 1 and try to reach space 70. Red and Blue travel in opposite directions on the blue or red path. On several spaces, there are special rules to observe, which are described in the game rules. The first player to get all 3 meeples to space 70 wins the game.

Deadzone is the science fiction miniatures game where two Strike Teams do battle in a war-torn urban battlezone.

In Deadzone, you will build your force from deadly soldiers and powerful heroes. Across one-off games or a linked campaign, your squad will grow in number and experience, earning special abilities and new equipment.

Deadzone is played on an interactive 3D gaming board built using the innovative hard plastic Battlezones Terrain Tiles. These tiles are fully modular, simply use the connectors and click the pieces together. No glue required.


New Faction: Veer-myn
Veer-myn are a new faction in Deadzone, and this is the first time we are creating miniatures for them in hard plastic.

An insidious disease ridden race, the Veer-Myn fester in the darkest corners of man’s world. Their broods writhe and chatter in a rodent like manner, equally matched by their physical appearance. The uncertainty of whether the Veer-Myn act with a motive, or just the need to infest and expand, only adds to the nightmare created from what lurks within the shadows.


New Miniatures: Enforcer Pathfinders
New in hard plastic, the Enforcer range gets a boost with these deadly scouts.

Even amongst the ranks of the Enforcers, the Pathfinders are an elite and highly specialised group. Their role demands that they avoid direct contact with the enemy wherever possible and their standard equipment and weaponry reflect this. Deployed ahead of the main force, the Pathfinders are tasked with gathering all the necessary Intel required to support a successful campaign, while eliminating any obstacles that may hinder the deployment of the main Enforcer vanguard.


New Scenery: Industrial Scenery

An innovative range of modular Battlezones terrain tiles. The tiles clip together using special connectors. These tiles expand the range with new Industrial scenery, giving you a new style of tiles to play with



    Getting Started: we want gamers everywhere to be playing Deadzone. The new Rulebook will be streamlined and reordered to make getting started simple, whilst incorporating the abilities and skills from the original expansion for easy reference.



    New Force Organisation: We are implementing a new Force Organisation Chart to make building your strike team easy, and helping make your next purchase that little bit simpler. More information on this during the campaign!



    Deep Campaign Setting: one of the original's shortcomings was the campaign section, and we'll tackling this with a new exciting system in the updated book after incorporating feedback from playtest groups and the community at large.


All existing miniatures are fully compatible with this new rulebook.

boardgame: Bitten: An Erotic Game for Couples
description:
Do you love sexy vampires? Then this board game is for you! The board game is designed to be easily printed on 8.5”x11” paper or cardstock (or A4, for those not in the USA). You will need two regular decks of cards and dice (which you can find at almost any dollar store) for a night of sexy vampire fun with your partner. Fangs optional.

There are two major aspects to gameplay. The first aspect to the gameplay are the individual rooms (setup something like Clue). There are six rooms, each with a different theme. Within the rooms, players perform sexual or erotic tasks in order to earn poker cards. At the end of the game, the player with the best poker hand wins (although, if played correctly, both players really win ;) )

The second aspect of gameplay is the movement from one room to another. Players don't just move, they must perform various foreplay acts in order to successfully move their game piece.

A simple kids' CCG based off War. Starter sets are expandable with booster packs with over 200+ cards in the set.

Each player draws 3 cards and each turn plays cards simultaneously. Powers are resolved and each player rolls a die to add to the cards power. Highest number wins. Players place their winning cards into the Winner pile, and the defeated cards in their dungeon. First player to get all the other player's cards in the dungeon wins.

User summary:
Known as バトルク！(Batoruku!) in Japan, Batorque! is a 2 player robot fighting game. The robots' actions are determined from a combination of cards and dice. By combining the dice rolls in different ways, players can use the card actions with various combos. And players can multiply or reserve effects with duplicate rolls or sequences.

Publisher's summary:
ダイスの出目を組み合わせて戦うロボット対戦ゲームです。
複数の出目を任意に組み合わせることで、様々な行動が可能となります。

Puppy Dogs From Space is a fast playing card game for 3-6 players where each player tries to assemble their team before the other puppy dog factions can. Players must balance getting their puppies assembled while also preventing opponents from doing the same with cards like ” flea infestation”, “the cone of shame”, or “the dinosaur bone”. Each puppy also has their own special power that can be used to help their faction accomplish it's mission.

Each player has the same 9 puppies and powers.  Players take their 9 puppies and shuffle 20 random cards from the 120 core cards to create their own draw deck.  This will randomize the cards from the core deck and the order their 9 puppies come out in play.  No two games are the same.

64 cubes with different adjectives on all sides and 13 family-friendly and gamer-friendly games to play with them.

ADJItation is fun, funny - and subtly educational. Shake the cubes in the ADJitator(box) and you are ready to play.

Some of the games that challenge your mental ADJility are:

Spin Cycle- keep spinning cubes until you hit a stumper.
Dirty Laundry - find out what your friends really think.
Air Dry - an air-writing amusement.
Lonely Launderomat - create and repeat your own urban legend.
Clothesline - name the next alphabetical noun in line.

On its retail site, Repos Production now offers published versions of two Ghost Stories: White Moon scenarios — Green Roots and The Cursed Children — that were available previously only as downloads.

Rules for both scenarios are available via download on Repos' website.

This is a double sided board with a scenario on each side.

Green Roots scenario - green ghosts and Wu-Feng are not affected by artifacts

Cursed Children scenario - Child villagers can not be exorcised or moved by the Pavilion of the Winds. Instead the cemetery tile can be used to remove one cursed child
A local "monopoly game", about an area in Malmö, Sweden called "Möllan".

The game is to create a panorama of a visit to the zoo. All 30 cards that make up this scene are numbered. Each player gets 5 cards and the top card on the draw pile is revealed. This is the start card.

In her turn, a player must play a card (if she has one that fits) one before or one after the card on the table. Thus the panorama is created. At the end of her turn, whether she played a valid card or not, she draws a new card from the draw-pile. If there are no cards in the draw-pile, she ends her turn by drawing a card from the hand of the player on her right.

The Zookeeper is a Joker. Once played, if a player has the correct card which the Keeper has replaced, that player may play that card and take the Joker for herself. The Snake card acts like an Old Maid card in that whoever has it when the panorama is complete, loses.

Valskyrr expands Mistfall with a campaign system in different ways. Some of the features can also be used in the classic game variant.

    Legacy Feats: A new deck of special Feats that can be purchased by any Hero. Use these abilities to sharpen your keen edge or supply your Hero with a little bit of what is not usually their forte. 
    Setting Cards: As your Hero Develops, so do the Mists. The expansion pack comes with a small deck of cards that will randomize ways in which each subsequent Quest will become harder, as the relentless Mists, aware of your presence, will push back, trying to stop your advance. 
    Elite Enemies: The Valskyrr will also come with an alternative version of one type of Enemy from each of the three groups (Wildlanders, Borderlanders and Undead). When introduced by a Setting, these will succeed the Regular Enemies in their respective decks, making the most basic footman of your Enemy a greater challenge to face.
    Epic Villains: Just like the Regular Enemies, some of the biggest and baddest evildoers will receive an upgrade. The expansion will provide you with a final battle that will truly feel like a worthy conclusion to an epic tale.

boardgame: The Ancient World: The Sacred Cave
description:
A downloadable mini-expansion created for Kulkmanns' G@mebox SPIEL14-reports.

Rules: When a player sends a citizen here, he may pay 1 coin to temporarily gain 1 sword. He may then immediately draw the top 3 Titans and attack one of them. This temporary sword can count for additional Titan attacks in the round.

Although technically compatible with The Ancient World (Second Edition), this tile and two others like it are not included in the latter edition because other new elements in the game cover the same ground in better ways.

family: Game: The Ancient World (Red Raven Games)

description:
The object is to collect the most possible of a complete “set” of cards in sequence before the Inspector turns up. A valid set of cards is either a;
Male Nudist + Shirt + Trousers + Tie + Shoes (M) + Hat (M) + Pipe
or
Female Nudist + Undies + Jumper + Skirt + Shoes (F) Hat (F) + Gloves.

METHOD OF PLAY
The dealer gives one unexposed card to each player, places the rest of the pack face down on the table and turns up the top card which is put face up near the pack.
In the event of the Inspector being dealt or turned up in this round (the first of any hand), the cards are reshuffled and the next player on left of dealer deals.
The player on the left of dealer must now choose two cards, the exposed one and one from the pack or two from the pack.
He then discards one, placing it face up on the exposed heap.
The next player does the same, until the Inspector is brought into play. On turning up the Inspector, the player must call “Inspector” at which all hands are exposed on the table.

SCORING
Player turning up “Inspector” counts “ 0 ” for hand.
The numbers on the cards headed by a nudist and in sequence, of the same set, are not counted.
But all cards not of the same set, and not in sequence, are added and scored against player.
If a player has two or more Nudists, 12 points is counted for each one after the first.

REMNANT CARD
Player holding “Remnant Card”, may call it any card except a Nudist, but if it is found in a hand, when inspected, not holding a Nudist, it counts 15 points against the player.

THE WINNER
The player with the least number of points after playing as many deals as there are players wins

DESCRIPTION:
Your family is away on vacation and having a blast.  Unfortunately, your big sister was not able to come along.  Since you're at the arcade for the afternoon, you've decided to get her the best prize you can in the redemption center to bring home.  Your family thinks that is a great idea, and wants to do the same.

Since your family is a little competitive, everyone wants to get the most tickets for the best prize.  It's game on!

GAME OBJECTIVE:
Gain as many redemption tickets as possible by playing games with your limited coins

GAMEPLAY:
Players play games of various difficulty by matching squares based upon color, shape, and numbers.  Half of the players will be active each turn.  Each turn is timed, so players have a limited time to complete matches.  The more matches made, the higher the prize.

GAME ELEMENTS:

    Timed card matching
    Variable game difficulty, with higher risk/rewards
    Each player is active (at least) every other turn

User summary:
６つ子７組 (Mutugo Nanakumi) (literally "Seven Sextuplets") is a set-collection card game. There are 42 cards with 7 ranks and 6 suits. You make collections of 3 or more cards of the same suit or rank and display them in front of you. A collection usually comes from your hand, but you can use one card from the table, including a card from a collection an opponent displayed. You may even disband a collection you previously displayed and make multiple collections or one bigger collection.

Publisher's summary:
"カードを取ったり捨てたりしながら、手持ちのカードを整えます。
既に取ったカード、場の捨て札、手札を組み合わせることで、カードを連続で取得することができます。"

Cast & Capture is a unique and innovative take on classic fishing card games (ex. Casino).

In Cast & Capture, you are a mage specializing in the collection of wondrous creatures for the Arcane Academy.  You must use your knowledge of the elements and arcane powers to capture a variety of mystical and magical creatures before your fellow mages.  It is only by using your wits to blend the right spells and group the right creatures that you can earn the title of Master Capturer.

There are 2 decks in the game: a Creature deck and a Spell Deck. Each creature and Spell have a Power and Element tied to them. To begin, mages are given 4 Spells at random from the Spell deck and 4 creatures are placed on the field.  Each turn a new creature will come onto the field and the mage whose turn it is will use their Spells to attempt to capture any number of creatures on the field that match the Power and Element of the Spell they cast.  If they cannot capture a creature, then their spell is added to the Spell Pool to be used by any mage. Mages refresh their hand of Spells when they run out.

Using the Spell Blending and Creature Grouping mechanics, mages will use light strategy and symbol recognition to blend spells to create new ones and even group creatures together to make bizarre and whimsical combinations.  By mastering Blending/Grouping, it helps the mage to ensure that the right spell is always at hand and maximum number of creatures are captured per spell.

With beautiful, hand-drawn illustrations and a unique split-card, dual-deck design; Cast and Capture updates an old gaming favorite for a new generation of players both young and old.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: CARRIER STRIKE FORCE: A Solitaire Game of Carrier Operations in the Pacific (1942-1945)
description:
INTRODUCTION
CARRIER STRIKE FORCE is a Game where you are a Commander of a Carrier Air Group (CAG) in the Pacific during World War II (1942-1945). Your Goal is to manage your aircraft to successfully search for and destroy Japanese Naval, Air and Ground Forces in order to bring victory to the allied forces.

OBJECTIVE
The Objective of CARRIER STRIKE FORCE is to score a pre-determined number of Victory Points (VP) by destroying Japanese Naval, Air and Ground Units. The US has up to 24 Turns to Control at least 150 VP worth of Bases OR have destroyed at least 60 VP worth of Japanese Ships and Aircraft. If the Player does not accomplish this, the Player loses and Victory is uncertain for the Allies.

GAME FEATURES
CARRIER STRIKE FORCE is a BIG Game with many counters and a 6-Sheet Map with large Hexes. Aircraft include F4F Wildcats, F6F Hellcats, F4U Corsairs, SBD Dauntless Dive Bombers, TBF Avenger Torpedo Bombers, A6M Zero Fighters, D3A Val Dive Bombers, B5N Kate Torpedo Bombers and G4M Betty Medium Bombers. Ships include Destroyers, Light and Heavy Cruisers, Battle Ships and Carriers. Ground units include Artillery batteries, Anti-Aircraft Batteries, and Convoys.

CARRIER STRIKE FORCE can be found at the following URL: 
http://www.berserkergames.net/

Smorgasboard is a board game for foodies, friends and family alike. Players take on the guide of aspiring chefs as they work their way around the board in search of gastronomic success.

The object of the game is to be the first team of chefs to graduate from Rick's Culinary Academy by answering questions from the following categories:

4BIDDEN-FRUIT
KITCHEN-KAOS
NUUDLE-DOODLE
TASTO-QUEST

Basically a food and drink based variant of Trivial Pursuit for 2-4 players/teams.

Sherlock Holmes is a variant on the famous whodunnit detective game (called Cluedo in the UK, Clue in the US) especially for younger children to play.

In this variant players move between 9 different places. One item has been stolen from mr. Newlyrich's summer cottage. The goal is to find out the stolen item, the thief and the place where the item is hidden.

This game also exist in a Swedish edition, made by Alga.
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
The Baroness DeMalus has hired two of the barony’s most skilled gardeners to beautify her walking grounds. Unfortunately, and as the baroness well knows, these two gardeners are vicious enemies and will do anything to ruin the displays of the other. But there is beauty to be found in their creations, if only they can line them up properly before the other can stop it from happening!

Seeds is a print-and-play microgame of competitive gardening and flower arranging for two players that uses only sixteen cards. It takes about ten minutes to play a game and is provided for free.

Players attempt to plant three of their own plants in a row, but are restricted on where they can plant by the plant that their opponent has played.

boardgame: Film Festival in a Box: The Interactive Movie Game
description:
Film Festival in a Box is the new interactive movie game that brings award-winning independent films into your living room! The game is easy to play, an intriguing conversation starter, and a fun way to entertain family and friends.

Players watch DVDs of indie movies and then vote on the best pictures or rank the films. Winner is the one with most individual  matches to overall group votes.
A kickstarter exklusive golden base robot. Was sent too all backers months before the game was published.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Modern Africa: Rules for modern skirmish battles on the dark continent
description:
Modern Africa is a set of rules to fight/refight battles on the dark continent in the 1960´s through the 1990´s. The majority of these battles were smaller scale fought between warlords or other dictators. The arms were either leftovers from an earlier era, state-sponsor (US or USSR) supplied, or black market purchased. These are not the latest, greatest weaponry and they may not even be welded by trained soldiers, but the goal was always freedom.
The game is played with 15mm miniatures.

In the two-player game Glaisher, players separate and move their stacks in order to connect the opposite sides of the board. The mechanisms for separation and movement of stacks are dictated by integer partitions with distinct parts, i.e., splitting numbers into sets of different smaller numbers. Players might recognize this mechanism from Kakuro puzzles. The game is therefore named after mathematician James Glaisher, who devised a theorem about integer partitions.

boardgame: Ghostbusters: The Board Game – Zombie Taxi Drivers
description: A Kickstarter exclusive add-on reward, of five alternative Class I ghosts.

Every player has to get as many of their markers to the opposite edge, preferably in the right order, thus achieving the highest points. They must also avoid losing their markers, if one of your markers goes off the sidelines on your left or right, or if your market is "eaten" by other player.

boardgame: Berlin '45: The Nightmare Ends – German Heavies
description:
This variant adds 6 new units to the German OB (heavy Assault Guns and Self Propelled Heavy antitank Guns, for the 3 scenarios: Historical, "Maximum" Historical and Operation "Eclipse".
Published in Command magazine #19.

description: The players begin at the parking lot near space 1. Movement is determined by a die. The first player to reach space 48 at the Salamander shoe store wins the game. At severaL spaces special rules are printed on the game board.

boardgame: Soviet Maximum Effort and the Estonians
description:
This variant adds ten Soviet and one Estonian counters for the Non Historical Scenarios.
Also contains errata for the Historical Scenario German reinforcements.

A farm isn't a farm without it's animals. Throw the dice, move your pawn and purchase the animals and machines by paying the requested amount. Primary goal of this game is to purchase the animals related to your farm. First one to succeed is the winner of the game.

An educative game of trade and luck

Four games to learn reading.

Game 1 (alphabet game): Match upper and lower case letters.

Game 2 (caterpillar game): Find the matching words for given starting letters.

Game 3 (pictures and words): Find the matching words for given pictures.

Game 4 (pictures and letters): Create the words for given pictures.
Memory-type game with lots of characters from Sesame Street to learn matching colors and shapes.

A wild-west adventure with Benjamin Blümchen and his friends, Otto, Chief Black Feather and his daughter, Red Feather.

Twelve Cattle were stolen and must now be returned home. But the way home is hard to find: you turn right, then left and sometimes there is no way! You have to look closely and build the way anew, relocating the game boards if nescessary.

To play, the 16 large game boards are laid out in a 4x4 grid on the floor.

Each cattle is assembled by putting the die-cut cattle in a colored standee. Then, the cattle are randomly distributed between the players. All players start at the same spot on the grid, Mike Donalds Ranch.

On your turn, a player throws the die and moves his pawn along the way, one field for each pip. The goal is to reach the color-coded pasture, matching the color of one of his cattle. To do this, it is allowed to change the grid of game boards to open up new paths and maybe hinder the other players on their plans. The first player to place all his cattle in the right pastures wins the game!

Learn first language ability with this game!

The game is played in four rounds. In the first round, you only use the cards 1 to 10 and the blue cardboard counters. Shuffle the cardboard counters face down. Give each player a card with a short phrase in french on it, the lowest number starts then. Pick up a cardboard counter and read the word aloud (it is written both in latin letters and in phonetics). Each player listens carefully, and says "yes" if he thinks this word belongs to his card. He then put the counter on his tray. If the sentence from his card is now complete, he reads it to the other players and gives a translation. The translation can be checked with the translation wheel. If it is correct, he keeps the card, otherwise the first player giving a correct translation gets the card. A round is played until all cardboard counters are gone. Then you start another round with new cards and new counters. Afer four rounds, the player with the most cards is the winner.

The game plays in the palace of a maharajah. It's a mixture of board game and role play.

A crime has happened: a smaragd was stolen. The players try to find out, what happened and which Person(s) have committed the crime.

Each player gets a character (Maharajah, Maharani, Guru, ...), and instructions on how to play their character.

There are 2 parts:

     Moving a pawn around the plan of the palace by rolling a die to find objects.
     Interrogate the other players to get more information.


Both should lead the players as team or against each other to solve the crime.

boardgame: The Ancient World: Citadel of Ivories Promo
description:
Promo tile for The Ancient World, made available through the Dice Tower 2015 Season 11 Kickstarter.

Rules: When you send a citizen here, roll a die. If you roll a Titan, you get 1 knowledge. If you roll a coin, you get 1 coin. If you roll nothing, you get nothing.

Although technically compatible with The Ancient World (Second Edition), this tile and two others like it are not included in the latter edition because other new elements in the game cover the same ground in better ways.
family: Game: The Ancient World (Red Raven Games)

description:
Be the first to lay all your stair parts on the gameboard to win! A simple color and recognition game for younger kids.

Each player gets stair parts in different lenghts (10 with 2 players, 7 with 3 and 6 with 4). Spinn the color wheel and put one of your stair parts on the matching stair on the game board with exactly two persons step on it. It is possible that you can't place a any of your stair parts (no matching size, not able to match exactly two persons, not enough free space on the chosen stair). If so, you must collect all stair parts from the chosen stair. The player who has placed sucessfully all of his stair parts is the winner!

Charlie and Lola are always in the middle of the action! Lola likes to play with her friend Lotta, drinks strawberry milk or goes on a walk with Sizzles. Charlie likes to play soccer with his friend Marv or looks after his little sister. But what exactly is happening? Two spinners show which tile the players must find, as fast as possible! Whoever got six tiles, wins!

Teenage Mutant Hero Turtles need their pizzas badly!

Shuffle the 16 pizza pieces facedown and lay them in a 4x4 pattern facedown on the table.

Each player gets a Turtle board with its 9 matching counters. On his turn, a player turns pizza pieces face up as long as they all are matching. He stops after the first different piece and turns all pieces facedown. Then it's the next players turn. If a player happens to find all four pieces, he fills his Turtle board with four of his nine counters. The second completed pizza only nets three counters, the third two counters and the fourth and last pizza only one counter. When all four pizzas are found, the pieces are shuffled and placed anew. The first player to place all his nine counters on his turtle board wins!
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
Dora Tip & Flip a educative Memory game with a special flip mechanic based on the series character Dora.

There are some variants in playing:

    Normal searching of pairs
    Searching a Special pair given by a Card
    Searching a Special pair given by the English Name on the Card.

A traffic game with dice and event cards.

Each player rolls the six-sided die and moves his pawn around the game board. At a roll of "1" you draw an event card and follow the instructions. At a roll of "6" you may roll and move again. The goal is to collect a picture card in each shop visited. Be the first to have 6 cards and win the game.

Four different game variants to learn the alphabet with Benjamin Blümchen!

The games included are:

Color-Dominoes, where player have to match color-coded buffers of the waggons. If done right, the ape is in the first, the bear in the second, the chameleon in the third waggon and so on until the zebra in the last waggon. The player to match all of his waggon tiles first, wins.

Color-Memo, where the train build in the first game is used again. Here, the waggons are turned on the other sid, where only a color and a uppercase letter is visible. All players use now the pawn showing Benjamin Blümchen and a six-sided die to walk along the train. Where Benjamin stopps, the player must name the animal in the waggon. If correct, the player gets the matching card. When all cards are found, the player with the most cards wins.

A B C game. The waggons must be added in the correct sequence, the color-coded buffers are used for self-control. Can be played by one player or cooperatively.

Where is my waggon? Each animal wants to find his own waggon. All the waggons are shuffled and laid out in the middle of the table, showing only the backside. The cards are also used again here. A card is drawn and the animal on it announced. The player to the right of the card drawer is the first to try to find the matching waggon. If he is correct, he gets the card, otherwise the next player tries it. When all cards are found, the player with the most cards wins.

boardgame: Chef, ärgere dich nicht!: Das Spiel um Wissen und Karriere
description:
This game is a party game. The story behind it is making career in your company. The career level is shown by the position of your figure in the company building.

You go your way up by rolling the dice and fulfilling your tasks. These tasks can be knowledge questions (getting harder the higher you come on your career) and actions. Both tasks are given by cards to be taken on action fields.

Actions could be e.g. making gymnastic lessons or kiss the other players.

boardgame: 101 Dalmatians: The game based on the film
description:
Cruella, the cruel fashion designer holds many little Dalamatian puppies hostage in Hell Hall to make their pretty coats into elegants furs. She did not, however, reckon with Pongo, Perdy, Fogey and Sergeant Tips. These four are on their way to release the puppies. Teh player who can release the most puppies from Hell Hall and bring them home has won the game.

Three routes are going to Hell Hall, where you can find the puppies. Choose the short one, and test your luck. Cruella may hide here often and takes the puppies away. On the long one, your odds are better and you may find a soot chip to mask your puppies. The last route goes over the river and you face the chance to hit the water, losing your puppies to Cruella again!

A memory game with a twist.

Shuffle the 40 tiles and place them facedown on the table. Get each player a board. On your turn, turn two tiles face up. If you find a pair of an animal or Mama Noah and Papa Noah, place them on your board. Else, turn the tiles face down and pass the turn. The first player to completely fill his board with Mama and Papa Noah and four pairs of animals wins the game.

Learn the colors and match them with pictures. Three learning games in one box.

Variant 1:
Three cards make a set. These sets are marked with a little animal (a bug). If you place cards of one set side by side, the bugs match each other, when placed correct. 
Shuffle all 36 cards and place them faceup on the table. On your turn, throw the die. On 2, 4 or 6 take any one card from the table and place it front of yourself. On 1, 3 or 5 pass the turn to the next player. Try to collect all three cards of a set. No one may take a card belonging to a set started by another player. You may start a new set only after you finished a set. The game ends when all cards are collected. The player with the most cards in front of him wins.

Variant 2:
Memory - play with the 24 picture cards or with all 36 cards. If played with 36 cards, always turn up three cards and try to find the matching triplets.

Variant 3:
I spy with my litte eye something ... Use the color cards to determine the color to spy and use the chips as victory points.

Short print run dexterity and dice game from Waddingtons. Players pull back and release Barney the dog to propel him around a track looking to uncover a number of bones buried at points along the track by the players as determined by a die roll.

Play continues until all 40 bones have been uncovered, the player with the most wins.

boardgame: Gunship Platoon: A Solitaire Game of Helicopter Gunship Combat in Vietnam (1965-1973).
description:
GUNSHIP PLATOON is a Game where you are a Commander of Platoon of Helicopter Gunships (the Gun Platoon) in a Helicopter Assault Company in Vietnam. Your Goal is to manage your aircraft and Complete a Campaign consisting of a series of Missions and score as many Victory Points (VP) as possible to assist the war efforts. Campaigns can be Short (8 Missions), Moderate (12 Missions), or Long (16 Missions). Combat is based on an innovated Initiative system that allows a single player to manage many units and gives the feeling of lining up a strafing run.

GAME FEATURES: GUNSHIP PLATOON features many colorful game counters of a large variety of Units including UH-1D and UH-B Hueys, OH-6 Cayuse and AH-1 Cobras, US Aero-Rifle Squads, VC and NVA infantry, M-113s, M-48s, T-54s, PT-76s and others.

GUNSHIP PLATOON can be found at the following URL: 
http://www.berserkergames.net/
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Each player has 4 cowboys in 4 colors (each player the same).
A player start with 1 field diagonal movement each turn.
After the player has reached special marked fields with all 4 cowboys, he now moves like the chess horse trying to get on the field were the wild horses are placed at the begin of the game.
Reaching one of them he brings it with the same kind of moves back to his ranch.

There is one White Mustang on the field this can be caught by one Player in the same way after bringing 3 wild horses back to the ranch.

Game ends after a preset number of rounds.

Wild horses in the ranch 10 Points, on the way to the ranch 5 Points, White Mustang in the ranch 20 Points.
Special rule: The Player has to choose a different Color as the openent had chosen on his last turn.

Based on the high-flying German TV franchise "Die Sendung mit der Maus", Das Quiz-Spiel mit der Maus is a family game that requires you to puzzle, guess, combine, and evaluate.

The game board shows many different tasks to solve, placed in a circular course. Your goal is to race once around the course and be the first to reach your starting point again. Each field gives you another task to solve. It is always you against all others. If you succeed, you step one field forward, otherwise all other players step one field forward. The tasks test your knowledge, your evaluation skills, your memory, and your dice-throwing skills.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Players move around the board and must answer trivia questions from one of four color categories matching the space they are on, for the level (1 thru 5) they are currently on in order to continue their turn. If space is indicated as a bridge, advance to the next level. Correctly answer a Level 5 question and win the game.

Spaßvogel Spiel is  a role-and-move game.
The track has 3 parts.

Each Player has to collect a full set of city Cards to get in the next part of the track (1st and 2nd part).
There are fields with the city names. Each time a player hits one of these fields he gets one of the cards of this city. 
Due to the fact that the number of cards of 1 City is limited the player gets the card from another player if he knows who has this card.

The third part is only a short part to come to the finish field. 
There are some special event fields (fixed events and card driven). Cards are for example: Joker city-Card, Exchange city-Cards, has to skip 1 or 2 turns.

A exciting encircle game for 2 players played in a 4x4 grid with a few special spaces.

The aim of the game is to encircle the opponent in 21 turns so that he is unable to move.

Setup:
The five blue pawns are set up in the 4x4 grid in the spaces with a single circle at one corner of the board. The green pawn is started in the opposite corner in a space with a double circle. This circular field markings apply only to the starting positions.

Movement:
The player  with the green pawn starts and counts his turns. He may move orthogonality (forward, backward, up or down) and diagonally. The blue pawn may only move move orthogonality. Turns are take alternately, moving always only one pawn. Skipping over another pawn is not allowed.

Object:
The player with the blue pawns must now try to encircle his opponent so that he can not move anymore. If he can do that in at last 21 turns, he's the winner. Otherwise, the other player has won.

Trivia:
"Zingeln" is a non-existent short form of "umzingeln", meaning encircling in german.

From the intro:

"The Poleaxed wargames system is designed to represent, as far as possible, the type of warfare that was waged in Western Europe during the 15th century.

The prime aim of the system has always been to wargame large battles, to get a clear cut result (pun intended) and to bring out the flavor of the period.  To do this, the rule mechanisms have been made as simple as possible.  Morale checks and book-keeping are kept to a minimum.  The need to remove figures has been eliminated and all movement can be carried out by contingents on movement trays."
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Learing by playing, that's fun!

variant 1,2: Memory. Match the number card with the picture card showing excatly this number of items. In the second variant, use the plus card also.

variant 3: Place the 12 number cards faceup on the table. Shuffle plus and minus cards into one stack. On your turn, draw a card from the stack and read one of the tasks on it to the next player, clockwise. If he can solve the task by pointing to the correct number card on the table, he gets the task card, otherwise the next player has a chance to solve it. Whoever gets to solve six tasks first is the winner!

This game is based on a German TV game show.

The game is played in 2 teams.
On teams turn one member is chosen.

He looks on a card what he has to draw. His team members try to name the Thing as fast as possible. If this is right the drawer takes the next card and starts drawing this.
This continues till the time has gone (hourglass finished).
Each card gives 1 Point and the other team, makes the same with new cards.

The game ends after each Player was the drawer.

boardgame: Ruck und Sack: ein Aufstieg mit Haken
description:
In this game the players climb a mountain.
The mountain is a 3D pyramid, build out of 4 triangles.
This mountain has many holes, which are connected to a net of paths. Each hole is marked with a colored ring around.
On players turn he rolls a dice, and decides which path he wants to go with exact this number of holes.
If there is already a hook placed, he can hang his backpack (marks the players positon) on this.
Is there is no hook he has to make an action or answer a question (dependent on the hole color). If he was successful he can place a new hook and place his backpack on it.
For some cards and wrong answered questions the player gets garbage.
To win the game, the player must had climbing on all four sides of the mountain (called 4 seasons of the year) and reach the Peak cross first. This is only allowed if the player has no garbage. The garbage can be reduced with some of the actions done or some Kind of questions answered. Sometimes just with getting the right card on the action Points you reduce garbage.

For more than 4 players there is a team rule included.

boardgame: Modern Africa: Scenario Book 1 – Somalia Intervention
description:
Modern Africa is not a great period for any of the so-called superpowers. No one has been able to pacify or even influence for any length of time events in Africa. As stated in the rules, Modern Africa, generic is the key with a tribal mindset against the big green (or red) machine. When time has passed, only the Africans are left in Africa. So, with this in mind, let´s move on this set of seven scenarios based on the book "Blackhawk down".

And good luck on those morale rolls!

Scenarios include:
Raid, The Gauntlet, Cornered, Defense of Crash Site Super 6, Blackhawk Down Campaign Game, Embassy Evacuation, and Overland Embassy Relief.

Special Rules cover:
Body Armor, Night Vision, Off-Board Artillery, Airstrikes, Hostages, and Helicopters.

boardgame: Modern Africa: Scenario Book 2 – Marine! Intervention Operations in the Third World
description:
Modern Africa continues to be the world´s weak sister. Unable to govern themselves and ungovernable from without, Africa stands as an enigma. The scenario book that James Meldrum has put together is a superb collection of "what if", thought provoking gaming possibilities.

And good luck on those morale rolls!

Scenarios include: 
Beach Head Assault 1 and 2, Embassy Raid, Ship Rescue, Recovery of a Warship, POW Rescue, Installation Raid 1 and 2, Urban Hostage Rescue, Embassy Relief and Arctic Confrontation.

Special Rules cover:
Elite Troops, Body Armor, Night Vision, Off-Board Artillery, Airstrikes, Hostages, Helicopters, Hostile Casualties, Terrain, Snipers and Amphibious Landings.

boardgame: Modern Africa: Scenario Book 3 – Tears: SEAL Operations in Nigeria
description:
Modern Africa continues to be the world´s weak sister. Unable to govern themselves and ungovernable from without, Africa stands as an enigma. The scenario book that James Meldrum has put together is yet another superb collection of thought provoking gaming possibilities.

And good luck on those morale rolls!

Scenarios include: 
Doctor Evacuation, Encounter with Militia, Ethnic Cleansing, Initial Skirmish, Main Battle, Retreat to the Border, Battle Campaign, and Campaign.

Special Rules cover:
SEAL Team Detachment, Body Armor, Night Vision, Off-Board Artillery, US Navy Airstrikes, Hostages, Nigerian Army Casualties, Hidden Movement, and Silenced Weapons.

Publisher's summary:
陰陽賽　－おんみょうさい－ (Yin-yang dice) is published in Japanese and English and combines dice play and strategy. Players roll or place dice to score more points than the others. Players can get more points with "Holy beast" coloured dice, and they can hide their colour to score even higher. Although this is a dice game, and players need a little luck, there is a lot of strategy involved.

Publisher's summary:
プレイヤーは陰陽師となり、ダイスを配置してその点数を競います。
ダイスを配置する・転がすのに加え、一度だけ使用できる「霊獣」でコンボを組めばより大きな点数が狙えます。
ダイスを使っても運の要素の少ない、心理と戦略のゲームです。

Publisher's summary:
コロポックル　見～つけた! (7 little korpokkurs) is a children's game published in Japanese and English.

A Korpokkur is a small fairy in the Hokkaido district in Japan. In this game, you play hide and seek with Korpokkurs. You trade chips and gather not leaves but Korpokkurs. Simple rules and easy to play with small children, but adults can play it as a harder mind game.

Publisher's summary:
アイヌのコロポックルたちが隠れんぼしています。
プレイヤーはフキの葉に隠れるコロポックル達を捕まえましょう。
ルールは簡単。でも心理戦の熱い、チップ交換ゲームです。

Solve the math problems and collect the treasure chests on this mysterious island with TIPTOI interactive learningsystem.

The treasure chests on this island are guarded by a parrot, a mermaid, a dolphin and a pirate. They teach simple math problems to the players during their turn. If the player solves the problem he can keep the treasure chest. The player who collects the most treasures wins the game.

The game contains four difficulty levels.
