The Board: Fantasy skirmish wargame.
Chapter One: The Frondglaucous Conflict.

In a world of God and mortals, several races fighting for control of The Triple Gift. Make a band of humans, dwarves, ugris, samaris, egir, sorgoins (snake-humans), alares, mercenaries...

In this chaptes you can use both dwarves and ugris.

Bible Hunter is a card game with themes from the Old and New testament, with artwork in manga style. The game has three types of cards, characters (red cards), that can be persons like the patriarchs or prophets, Jesus or the apostles, and items, like swords or money (green), and mission cards (blue).

Players starts with four cards, two characters and two missions. The players take turns, selects one of the three cards on the board, and then tries to collect this by playing one character card. The strongest character gets the card. It is good to have strong characters, but some of the gives minus points if in hand then a round is completed, eg. Satan (-20 victory points)

The cards collected can be put together to complete a story. The round ends then a player can complete a whole biblical story, and then all players count there victory points. The game is played in three rounds, and the player with most victory points after the third round is the winner.

From the rules:
"Capital Edition is designed to help you enjoy Washington, D.C. before and after you visit."

Capital Edition is a roll-and-move trivia game designed to teach players about Washington, D.C.  Players start at the beginning of a track printed around the outer edge of the board.  On each turn, they roll the die and move along the track, landing on one of many types of spaces.  On a monument space, the player gains the trophy card for that monument.  A Metro space allows a player to move to any other Metro space.  A Tourmobile space allows movement to any monument, but the player must answer a question from a Challenge card to win a trophy card.  On a challenge space, the player draws a challenge card and asks another player the question.  On an incorrect answer, that player must return a trophy card to the board.  On a take a card space, the player must choose a card and answer the question to continue.  There are also roll again and lose a turn spaces.

Players' turns end in one of four ways--answering a question incorrectly, answering three consecutive questions correctly, winning a trophy card or landing on a "lose a turn" square.

Players continue to circle the board until one player collects trophy cards for all four monuments, winning the game.

Developed by University of Southern California Alumni, Trojan Challenge is a Monopoly-type game where players move around a track, landing on activity spaces which represents conferences, exhibits, performances, athletic contests and other events at the university.  When a player lands on an activity space, he may purchase that activity for the designated cost.  He may then purchase up to two upgrades (which are called "reserved tables," "group reservations," etc.) either immediately or on a later turn.  When other players land on these spaces, they must pay the owner the amount designated on the card.

A few extra wrinkles are added.  Detour spaces place a player onto an alternate track when they are landed on.  "Go Trojans Go!" and "We Are SC" cards can give players extra money, take away money or offer other benefits or detriments.  Parking Lot spaces cause loss of a turn, and various other spaces may increase or decrease the player's money total.  Passing the start space, unlike in Monopoly, costs the player $600.

Players start with $6000 each (tracked on paper--no paper money is included) and try to gain funds as they play.  Landing on other players' spaces forces a player to pay them a designated amount.  If a player cannot pay, he or she may sell any assets owned to other players or give them back to the bank for half value.  If still unable to pay in full, the player is out of the game.  The game can be played to one of three pre-agreed ending conditions--a time limit, until one or more player are eliminated or until only player is left.  The player with the most money wins.

Optional rules allow a player to move another player back to the starting space by landing on their pawn and allow for discounted purchase of upgrades on spaces if matching sets are collected.

A metagame rule governs the use of a "Trojan Gold" space.  If a player lands on this space and agrees to donate a pre-determined amount of real (not in-game) money to the real University of Southern California, he or she gains an upgrade to place on any owned property.

The great armies of the Elemental Kings march from the furthest corners of the known world and beyond.

In this expansion, each player uses a TKG ARENA Standard Deck and shares 11 action tiles to explore, move, build, harvest, raid, trade, deconstruct, purge, consolidate, seal and emulate their enemies across 48 different world tiles as the TKG ARENA universe unfolds ...

This game requires the following components from TKG ARENA: The Elemental Kings & TKG ARENA: Rise of the Magi:

LIFE:    9 - 18 for each player (Variable)

New components for this expansion.

STARTING PLAYER TOKEN: 1
PLAYER TOKEN:    1 for each player
ACTION TILES:    11
WORLD TILES    48

To win the game, players accrue victory points pertaining to the amount of Elemental Oinks they harvest from the worlds and the presence (in terms of Life tokens) established in each of these worlds. As their trusted generals, the Elemental Kings expect players to conquer these spaces, reap their resources towards their own campaigns and bring those who tries to stop them to their knees.

The Arena has just got bigger ...

エトランゼ (étranger) is a mystery game from Spieldisorder. Players are freelance writers, chasing around the world to uncover the genius "Etranger". Special reports are left in certain places, and players must find and announce them to the public to gain prestige. The game has absolutely no element of luck.

This was the first game produced by Spieldisorder and is designed to be packaged in a DVD case. The components were ordered from Germany.

description:
User summary:
ディッシズ (dishes) is a postcard game from Spieldisorder. Alex the cat is working hard today as a waiter at the restaurant Uoza-tei. Suddenly, the waiters collide, and all the dishes go flying up in the air! Alex races to catch them on his tray before they hit the floor. Can he save the best dishes?

This is a postcard game, and can be played by one person or others working together (typically it's for two players). You hold the postcard like a tray with one hand, and flip chips in the air to try and catch them on the tray. The tray has scoring positions for 5, 3 and 1 points. Can you get the perfect score of 18 points? The game is printed as a Japanese postcard, a new style of game introduced at the Tokyo Game Market in Autumn 2014.

Description from the publisher:

From the vaults of Haarpsichord Studios come sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) full of glitter and glitz. As the SanSan Cycle continues its tour along the western coastline, Old Hollywood brings us whole servers worth of romantic comedies, action flicks, red carpet events, and fan sites. Everyone wants a taste of fame. Everyone’s jostling to get a cut of the market.

Visual entertainment is still big business in Old Hollywood, and fame is still solid currency. As the game’s megacorps pour speculative funds into summer blockbusters and celebrity-driven vanity projects, you’ll find some sensies aimed toward the institution’s highest awards and others aimed toward the lowest common denominator. You’ll find secrets, gossip, and security precautions.

Who is Miranda Rhapsody dating now? Why did Ronny Five fail to show for his appearance at the Bangkok Mall? Which surprising celebrities are hooked on stims? In Old Hollywood, everything’s a story, and the best stories are worth big money.

Long way home is a game for 3 to 6 players about a drunk guy trying to get home. But, due to his drunkenness, he gets a lot of ideas. Some of them are good, some of them not so much. But all of them sound fun. At least to him.

In a game players are playing as ideas in a drunk guys head. They are controlling him, and his actions, trying to achieve secret objectives. There are two types of objectives, big ones, that happen at the end of the game, and small ones that are scored when done. Big objectives are connected to finishing the game - Get home in time, Give wife flowers, sober up, or lose consciousness in bar, while small objectives are different, some logical (eat a hamburger, drink coffee), some irrational (punch person in bar), and some just plain crazy (eat a flower, punch a wall).

The game is print and play, and files and rules can be found in files section.

boardgame: Benedict Arnold and the Northern Theater
description:
An insightful strategist and leader of men, Benedict Arnold was the true hero of the Battles of Saratoga in 1777, which resulted in a formal alliance with France and the turning point of the American Revolution.

Operating in the Northern Department (the northern theater of the war), Arnold worked tirelessly in the cause of liberty for years. If he had died at Saratoga instead of being seriously wounded, his legacy would have been nothing short of legendary.

Benedict Arnold and the Northern Theater is designed to provide players with a neglected yet important slice of history -- and, using the Do or Die Roll(TM) game system -- an easy-playing, exciting gaming experience for both experienced wargamers and newbies alike.

Now in development with a well-known, respected publisher!


Update 1/9/18: unfortunately, for various reasons the production of this game has been canceled. There may be some interest from another publisher, so cross your fingers. Meanwhile, I've uploaded all the files to the game as print-and-play.

A branch from the Tree of Life has the power to stop plague and heal illness. Now, you must find this lost artifact, for the good of all Terrinoth in the Bonds of the Wild Hero and Monster Collection! In this Hero and Monster Collection, you’ll find twenty-two plastic figures, previously only available in the first edition of Descent: Journeys in the Dark, now reimagined with new figure sculpts and artwork. In addition to these detailed figures, you’ll find two brand-new quests chronicling your quest for the healing power of the Tree of Life.

Four heroes gather together and venture into the wild in this expansion. You can work magic alongside Lyssa or Challara, or rely on the aid of your familiars with Vyrah the Falconer or Ronan of the Wild. The overlord’s monsters stand to oppose these noble heroes, however. Twelve kobolds, four hellhounds, and two deep elves oppose the heroes' quest and seek the branch for their own nefarious ends.

In Cardville players take on the roll of mayor of a developing town, growing it by adding more districts, developing existing ones, and adding landmarks.

On their turn a player rolls 3 dice and allocates the results to increasing their cash reserves and gathering resources, but they must choose carefully because the result of the remaining dice is given to their opponent and used by them to gather resources.

They must also beware of rolling triples, as the result is never good - alien abductions, meteor strikes or riots are just some of the calamities triggered by the wrong roll!

The game uses a deck of cards to represent districts and landmarks that players may purchase and add to their growing town.  Coloured tokens are used to record resources and cash, and also to represent areas of the town that have been developed.

Undeveloped plots can generate resources, but developed ones generate victory points at the end of the game.

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Crossover Pack 4 – Watchmen
description:
1986’s Watchmen is one of the most celebrated stories in the history of comics. And now that world is coming to you in a new Crossover Pack for the DC Deck-Building Game. This expanded Crossover Pack includes Hidden Roles, Villainous Machinations, Challenge Cards, Mastermind Plots, plus new main deck cards and new oversized Super Hero cards.

This Watchmen set adds a new element to deck-building games: Hidden Roles! As in the original story, one of the members of the team has their own ideas on the best way to solve the world’s problems. The Villain must work towards his or her secret plot while not making it too obvious. “Loyal” players will strive to overcome various challenges, though the “Villain” of the story can disguise his or her intentions by helping with these challenges as well. Should the Mastermind Plot become hatched, the Loyal players will certainly have their work cut out for them…

It all adds up to a deck-building experience like nothing you’ve experienced before, yet played out using the best-selling Cerberus Deck-building Game engine!

Tri-trix Legend is a Korean board game which draws inspiration from the Legend of Zelda series.

It is designed in a trigonometral manner and each tri-Gon represents a primitive tribe in the continent which tries to expand in a world which is overshadowed by a comet collisions in the background.
The different shape of the map, forming a modular board to start the game and is randomly generated, fundamental progress after each roll of the dice to get the resources infrastructure construction (stone) on board with this, similar to the Settlers of Catan here, is done by constructing a purpose Temple (loop) of the game. The player who build the specified number of temples first wins the game.

A Gentleman's Game Of Cannibalism

Players take on the role of wealthy victorian era socialites and compete through braggadocious storytelling to determine who is the most interesting person. The only problem is that when money is no object what could you possibly wager with? Well winning a hand in Samuel Sandwich may be more literal than you might think.

Samuel Sandwich is designed for 2 to 4 players
The game consists of cards, playmats, counters and coins.

Each player is dealt a hand of “doings & deeds” cards each of theses cards has a selection of color coded words which are used to construct a story. These cards also contain a custom Samuel Sandwich suit and a point value so that at the end of each round your point total can be determined.

At the beginning of the round a “consorts & cohorts” card is flipped to reveal a famous historical figure. This person will be the subject of everyone’s  story as they try to outplay each other. There are 2 wagering rounds one when the “consorts & cohorts” card is revealed and then again when players select and place the cards they will be using face down on the table.
after wagering is resolved players take turns revealing and telling their story to the group. After everyone has told their tall tale the points are added up and the winner takes the money bet during this round as well as all cards played during the round (minus their own).

Feeling hungry?

Winning A Hand
After winning a round a player may then select one part of another player to eat. The correct counter is removed from that persons player mat and is placed in the stomach of the round winner.

Trading In
Players may also trade in 10 doings and deeds cards they have won from other players to select an additional part to eat.

Selling Off
If a player finds themselves low on funds at any time in the game they may sell off a part to the highest bidder in order to replenish their financial stockpile to continue wagering.

At the end of the game the player who has eaten the most is declared the winner. 
The key to success is not what you eat, it’s who you eat and how much!

In 2055, with the land having been developed and polluted rapidly due to the explosive growth of population, all eyes are turning to the ocean as our source for survival — but roughly one thousand species go extinct each day from among the six million forms of marine life, the destruction taking place faster than the revitalization that must happen.

Now the Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission (IOC) within the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) has been given a huge budget from the world powers to invest in marine life and acquire the resources needed to survive, to create an "Aqua Park" akin to Noah's Ark from legends that will protect marine life and allow for research into the nature of the ocean and coastal areas.

In Aqua Park, players each endeavor to create their own aqua park based on the four mission cards that they hold in hand and the $10 million in their starting pool. Each player uses one of four dice (4-sided, 6-sided, 8-sided, 12-sided) during the game to try to acquire marine life cards from the five ocean zones.

On a turn, a player rolls their die, pays to reroll (if desired), asks for die help from others (paying if their dice are used), then collects all the fish in one ocean zone that correspond to the die total. If the player collects marine life needed to complete a mission card, the player can discard one life card to score those points. They can buy new mission cards for $3, with a maximum of four cards in hand. (If a player acquires marine life they don't want, they can sell it for $3 million; penguins bring in $5 million.)

Every $5 million in hand is worth 1 point, and players race to collect 15 points first. Whoever does so wins!

Hero Detected! is a board game for 2 to 4 players who are enjoying dungeon building.

The player is the boss of the dungeon fantasy world, slaying, decorating your own dungeon. Curious adventurers and righteous warrior must prove their skills in the Underworld.

The idea comes from ​​the PC game Dragon Nest building from Japan. The concept art was advocating the classic RPG games, we have tried to configure the traditional fantasy world with interesting cards.

In the game you try to build the biggest dungeon to lure the most heroes in.

The players will play cards to build the dungeon and can play special action cards to mess with the other players.

Basic Overview

1. Plunder 
Select a Village Card and place it face down. 
If you loot the cards the card information stays private. If the card placed in the dungeon entrance it comes to a battle of the player.

Purchase 
Place a Monster over the loot card or magic card to buy this card.

Building 
Acquired by purchase monster card facilities to be placed one card per turn, and in disposing the dungeon, and be able to place all three cards, disposed on existing cards.

Battle 
Occurs if you have played a card at the entrance of the dungeon. By default, the proceeds from the fight card was placed on the left, and if this level is higher mowing the mighty warrior of the level as the level of the card goes to the next one. The opposite can be sent to prison veterans card. If you mighty men broke through in all the fields of the dungeon to the damage the card. Three damage card is the defeat of one player.

Roll the dice! 
Without looking at the bag in hand, touch only 5 runes! 
Take out quickly the runes! 
Throw in the center of the box furnace first! 
The board game Touchstone uses touch and agility!

The one who is the first to throw the rune into the hole in the middle is the winner of the round and scores.

Each player is one of the warriors to win this competition.
In order to achieve a wish or a warrior lays out to prove their strength.
Players use the cards that make up your own warrior and harmony to create the ultimate warrior hero to subdue the other people.

Defeat the monsters to get the treasure, and you can buy the equipment in a store for the treasures you collected.

The equipment will be the key for a player to success. 
Treasure card consists of mounting the card and passive cards, trap cards, counter cards. 
By combining these cards you can create your own unique tactics.

A mysterious dungeon emerged from the middle of the continent with great power to summon creatures and control them. All nearby kingdoms have sent heroes. Some are here to destroy the dungeon, some to take that great power. You are one of these heroes. By your king's order, you must defeat all other heroes and monopolize this dungeon.

The dungeon will help you summon powerful creatures, but you need an altar to do this — and if you lose your altars, you can hardly survive this battle. By the same token, you can dominate this dungeon by conquering your enemies' altars. You can also collect victory points by destroying the enemies themselves. Making the most powerful army is one way to win. Now it's time to begin the battle!

GSBM (Great Strategy of Battle Monsters) is a fantasy-based strategy game that gives you extreme freedom to maximize your strategic choices and control the game. Each player forms an army of monsters to compete with other players in phases such as Produce, Summon, Move, Battle, and Conquer; each monster has its own unique skill, allowing you to devise clever strategies by combining it with other skills or tactic cards. Players must manage their resources, action points and five mana elements: water, fire, air, earth, and ether. Using these resources effectively is the key to victory. Players must also use field effects, elemental superiority, unit type differences (infantry/archer/cavalry/flyer), joint attacks, and joint defense to their advantage.

The game offers several victory conditions, so each player can take strategic approaches to different victories based on how the game plays out. The game field is randomized for each new game, altering territorial dynamics between players and providing near-infinite start settings. As players become more skilled with the game, their tactics and strategy develop, allowing them to play against others with more unique and original play styles.

•••

GSBM은 판타지 세계관을 배경으로 한 2~8인용 전략 게임으로서 최대한의 자유도 및 무한한 확장성을 바탕으로, 게이머의 게임 지배력과 전략적 선택가능성을 극대화한 작품입니다. 핸드와 필드 관리, 마나 관리, 승점 관리, 소환 거점 관리 등의 여러 요소가 게임에 녹아 있습니다. 이들을 균형있게 유지해야만 마법진에서  승리의 영광을 눌리 수 있을 것입니다.

In this exciting, fast paced card game, players are competing against each other to acquire as many cards from their opponents as possible! Some cards are worth more than others though, so be strategic about which cards you decide to “Swipe” from your foes!

The game is played in rounds, and in one single round only four "Master Points" are available for players to win, either splitting the points between the players, or one player winning them all. Rounds continue until a single player reaches five Master Points, in which they win the game. The four Master Points in any given round consist of the following: 1) Master Number Point 2) Master Color Point 3) Master Shape Point and 4) Master Total Point.

The game starts out with the dealer revealing the "Master Card" for that round (which is a Power Card that gets placed in the center of the playing field). The Master Card will have a number (1-10), a color (red,blue,purple,green), and a shape (X,O). Whatever the elements might be are what dictate the four ways to earn the "Master Points" in that round. For example: If the dealer reveals the Master Card that has the number 7, the color red, and the shape X, then players must steal cards from each other that have any of those elements (7s, reds, X's) and always high numbered cards. At the end of a round, players will reveal to each other all of the cards they have acquired throughout the round. In this example, the player who acquired the most cards with the number 7 wins the Master Number Point, the player who acquired the most cards with the color red wins the Master Color Point, the player who acquired the most cards with the shape X wins the Master Shape Point, and the player who acquired the highest sum after adding up the numbers on all acquired cards wins the Master Total Point.

There are only two main types of cards in Swipe it!™ There are the Power Cards (cards with a number, a color, and a shape; one of these cards gets promoted to master card at the beginning of the round) and then there are the Swipe Cards which have an "S" and a swipe arrow . Players need to combine one of each type of cards in order to initiate an attack against another player. Players can only acquire Power Cards from each other in order to gain points, where Swipe Cards simply allow the player's Power Cards to make an attack. In Swipe it!™ a combined pair (a Power Card combined with a Swipe Card) can always freely attack a lower numbered Power Card or enemy pair. Example: a 10 on a Black Swipe Card can freely attack an opponent's 9 on a Black Swipe Card). Now the intensity rises when you are using a combined pair to attack a higher numbered Power Card or enemy pair. This is where the dice and rock-paper-scissors comes into effect, where essentially the attacker is wagering their lower numbered Power Card in a series of battles against their opponent's higher numbered Power Card. If the attacker loses, their opponent acquires the attacker's Power Card in play. This is especially huge when the attacker is risking their Power Card that happens to match the "Master Number", "Master Color", "Master Shape", or is a high numbered card like a 9 or 10. On the other hand, if the attacker is wagering a Power Card that is low like the number 1 and does not match any of the Master Point categories, then who cares if they lose to their enemy right? The attacker may as well take the risk with that type of card in order try and win a great one.

There are also three different types of Swipe Cards (Black, Silver, and Gold) each one offering unique abilities; such as the Gold Swipe Card raising your Power card by +2 when it is attacking and by +1 when on defense, or the Silver Swipe Card granting the ability to "Punish" another player's Swipe Card, or being able to "Reassign" a combined pair with a different Power card (if you had a 1 combined with Silver Swipe Card, you can switch it to lets say a 10 on the Silver Swipe Card allowing you to attack higher numbered enemy cards for free). There are also some special rules too, such as "merge", "master merge", and "cash-in" which can help players out of some sticky situations. A "merge" allows you to combine two Power Cards that share the same number (such as combining a 9 with another 9), forcing the combined cards to act as if it were a Black Swipe card, giving you the ability to combine a third Power Card to it to make an attack. "Master merge" is the same as a "merge" with the exception that the combined cards match the "Master Number" in play, forcing them to act as if it were a Gold Swipe Card. Lastly, the "cash-in" action allows a player to discard two alike Swipe Cards (such as discarding two Black Swipe Cards) to receive one new card from the dealer. This action can only be done once per turn, so if you were hoping for a particular card, you will not be able to cash in another pair of Swipe Cards until the dealer makes its way around to you again.

The round ends once the dealer has dealt the last card, and the player dealt the last card takes their final turn. All players discard any remaining face up cards. They then flip over all acquired face down cards and determine the winners of the 4 Master Points for the round in this order; Master Number, Master Color, Master Shape, Master Total.

Earth Encounters is a board game fueled by the concept that conversations among friends ignite big ideas that not only impact our world, but make it a better place as well. Earth Encounters brings the Einstein in all of us to life. It’s not only fun to play, but it sparks dialog among friends, brings value to our community, and perhaps can even change our world for the better! Earth Encounters is anti-trivia.

The game starts with the Timeship Encounter. An alien creature escaped the timeship and is hiding somewhere in the Eastern Time Zone. Encounter launches an elite team to recapture the alien creature. Players travel across the U.S. and the seven continents, competing to be the first to locate and capture the powerful yet invisible alien before it awakens and consumes all the knowledge possessed by Earth’s inhabitants. Throughout this adventure, each player learns about the states and continents as they collect as many states and continent deeds as possible – they’ll be needed when you come face-to-face with the alien creature.

Each player can only advance by correctly answering a question from the Q&A Deck or successfully completing a thought experiment from the Challenge Deck.   In doing so, if they are the first to land on that particular state or continent, they are awarded a State Deed of Trust.  That means the state or continent is now under the players protection from the alien. The player who protects the state where the alien is hiding is considered the winner!

boardgame: Wings of Glory: WW1 Giants of the Sky – British Handley Page O/400
description:
The year is 1918. All across Europe, men face a last-ditch battle in the mud of the trenches, and for the first time in history, a new battlefield is open: the sky. While colourful biplanes gallantly duel each other as true "knights of the air," colossal “giants of the sky” also take flight, bringing destruction to distant cities and over the battlefield.

Wings of Glory is probably the most popular World War One aerial combat tabletop game in the world, with a great collection of pre-painted, ready-to-play model airplanes. Thanks to its elegant and innovative game system, it is possible to start playing minutes after opening the rules. Each airplane has a unique, historically accurate, maneuver deck, yet it is easy to play.

The British Handley Page O/400 was a colossal Entente bomber used to support ground troops on the Western Front and fly bombing raids deep into the enemy territory.

This leviathan of the air was used in the last months of the war in pivotal historical events, such as the strategic bombing of industrial complexes in Germany.

The wingspan of the O/400 scale model is 21 cm (more than 8"). The model will be a complete, ready to play miniature in 1/144 scale, painted and assembled.

The model also includes everything you need to include it in your Wings of Glory aerial battles - maneuver deck, airplane card, crew management card and damage counters, new rules, and the special gaming base.

boardgame: Wings of Glory: WW1 Giants of the Sky – Zeppelin Staaken R.VI
description:
The year is 1918. All across Europe, men face a last-ditch battle in the mud of the trenches, and for the first time in history, a new battlefield is open: the sky. While colourful biplanes gallantly duel each other as true "knights of the air," colossal “giants of the sky” also take flight, bringing destruction to distant cities and over the battlefield.

Wings of Glory is probably the most popular World War One aerial combat tabletop game in the world, with a great collection of pre-painted, ready-to-play model airplanes. Thanks to its elegant and innovative game system, it is possible to start playing minutes after opening the rules. Each airplane has a unique, historically accurate, maneuver deck, yet it is easy to play.

The German Zeppelin Staaken R.VI was the largest airplane ever to fight during World War 1, with a wingspan more than 30 feet larger than a WW2 B-17 Flying Fortress.

This leviathan of the air was used in the last months of the war in pivotal historical events, such as the raids over London in 1918.

The wingspan of the R.VI model is about 30 cm (1 foot!). The model will be a complete, ready to play miniature in 1/144 scale, painted and assembled.

The model also includes everything you need to include it in your Wings of Glory aerial battles - maneuver deck, airplane card, crew management card and damage counters, new rules, and the special gaming base.

boardgame: Old Hickory: The Battle for L'Abbaye Blanche – A Pint Sized Campaign for Chain of Command
description:
Old Hickory is the third Pint-Sized campaigns for Chain of Command, designed to be played using the campaign handbook At the Sharp End.

At thirty pages long, Old Hickory contains an overview of US operations in Normandy and beyond from Operation Cobra to the German counter-attack at Mortain, Operation Luttich. It goes on to present a mini-campaign covering the defence of L'Abbaye Blanche, a key position to the North of Mortain where the US 30th Division stood firm against 2nd SS Das Reich, and Der Fuhrer regiment in particular.

The campaign is a total of six game tables with the duration running between three and eight games. Briefings are provided for both sides, along with measurable objectives, period maps, force and support option listings.

Like all Pint-Sized campaigns, it is available for the price of a pint in the publisher’s local pub.
family: Series: Pint Sized Campaigns for Chain of Command

description:
The object of This or That? is to discover fun and interesting things about the other players. Each token in the game bag has two questions – one on each side, and each question has two options – This or That. Most of the questions give you a choice between things you might like, such as “Pumpkin Pie or S’Mores?”. Other questions might give you a choice between things you might not enjoy, such as “Smelly or Slimy?”. In either case, you might really like both of the answers, or neither of them. That’s the catch – you HAVE to pick one answer!
Players take turns drawing tokens from the bag. Silently read your token and decide which side you want to answer. Read that question out loud. Decide what your answer is but don’t say it out loud! Each players guesses what they think your answer is. After all the players have guessed, reveal your answer and be honest! If you draw a black All Play token, read the question out loud, and everyone takes turns sharing their answer to the question.

Keep the token you drew and pass the bag along to the next player. The game ends when everyone has five This or That? tokens in front of them.

- En kappsegling runt Gotland (A race around Gotland).

The players competing in a race around the Island Gotland in the Baltic sea. The boats are moving by picking up the right picture from facedown tiles that correspond to a field in front of the playing piece. If a wrong tile are picked, then the boat have to move back. The tiles are put back in the same place and after a while you have to trust your memory to pick the right tile.

Open Field Battle is a simple wargame portraying generic 17th-century combat; no specific battle is represented.

Each player has a force of pikemen, cavalry, musketeers, and cannons. Each turn a player gets a random number of command points to spend on musketeers and cannons firing and on pikemen and cavalry moving and assaulting.

Combat is deterministic; the result depends on the formation the defender is in and the status of the defender and any neighboring units. E.g., a lone defender or one at the end of a line is disrupted (or eliminated if already disrupted), but one with two undisrupted neighbors merely retreats. The neighboring units may suffer effects as well.

Optional rules allow for different ways of using the command points and the possibility of saving them from turn to turn.

boardgame: Credo!: The Game of the Early Church
description:
As in the original Credo, in Credo!: The Game of the Early Church players compete to get their versions of the articles of the Nicene Creed voted in. There are numerous event cards which affect the proceedings.

This edition makes some significant changes, among which are:

     The game can be played with up to eight players, although play mats for just six are provided.
     The alternate winning conditions (11 million flock or 117 council votes) have been eliminated.
     The game ends when the creed is complete or when the event deck is exhausted.
     The creed is divided into 13 articles instead of 10, and there are furthermore additional optional articles (designated X, Y, and Z) which the Emperor can choose to vote on at any time. (Consequently, players can have up to 12 articles on their mats instead of 9.)
     All flock cards have the same value.

Tactical GameX is a new interpretation of the classic Avalon Hill games PanzerBlitz and Panzer Leader. An effort has been taken to bring the play of these games into the 21st Century. Rather than moving all units at a time, players swap impulses, moving an unknown number of units at a time. There are also options to turn the game into a card-driven system complete with random events.

My inspiration for creating this new rules set was the vast (and I do mean vast!) number of supplemental units and maps created by the folks at the Imaginative Strategist. The goal was to create a system that was fully backward compatible with the classic games, but provide a more interactive and suspenseful experience with the option to play with armies from all the nations fielding substantial armies during World War 2.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Rasslin' Strategy is a 1990 pro wrestling card game by Mike Besky that normally requires (if not using an alternate rule) that the game be played WHILE wrestling is on, specifically wrestling that neither player has seen yet (original airdates, PPVs, etc).

The cards, which are moves like Choke, Bodyslam and Dropkick, are realistically and competently illustrated black and white images with a few rules and values that are used to resolve success and score points. Gameplay involves playing cards from your hand that match those being performed in the currently playing matches on your television (if the match you're watching has Shawn Michaels using a dropkick, you can play Dropkick).

The goal is to pin your opponent of course, by playing the correct cards/sequences to end the match.

Cards Against Humanity: Retail Pack is a 5-card expansion for Cards Against Humanity. It is included with a purchase of the base game from a physical store that was accepted into Cards Against Humanity's retailer program.

"Reading is fun" is the game for 1st and 2nd graders.

There are 81 picture cards, 34 word tiles and 140 letters.

One of the variants:
Players must take the word tiles and find the letters from the table needed for those tiles. If all letters are found, the picture card must be find. Who is the first to find certain number of picture cards is the winner.

"Computing is fun" is the game for 1st and 2nd graders.

There are 48 puzzle cards: 24 with numbers and 24 with images.

In his turn player takes any number tile and must find the image tile with same number of elements. If correct take the tile as a point, if incorrect put both tiles back. If all tiles are taken who has the most tiles is the winner.

May be played also as a Memory.

A necromantic ritual in a chicken farm marks the beginning of a horror night full of dreary cackles. In ONE-LEGGED ZOMBIE CHICKEN, players try to use the resources of a trapped population to escape from hungry zombie chicken. Use the characters' powers, get rid of the chicken cards and be the survivor of this gallinaceous apocalypse.

In each turn, a player must make three actions in sequence: 1) replace their hand until acquiring 6 cards; 2) throw out a card on the discard pile; 3) play a character card and activate its power. It's important to highlight that chicken cards can NEVER be discarded. Each character activates a special power setting the gaming dynamics.

Nominated for UK Game Expo 2015 Strategy Card or Dice Game of the Year.
Bid. Build. Battle.
Orc Warlords buying Orc Slaves at Orction to then fight to the death in Gladiatorial Combat.
Uses a variety of classic auction techniques and a blend of worker placement strategy to build a gladiator school of Orcs.
Culminates in combat via a dice system of d4 and d6 based on Orc and Gladiator equipment gained.
Players bid on Orc Lots from a deck of 84 individual Orcs in 7 categories to build sets. Orctions occur when a player decides to visit the city of Ve'Tath during the worker placement rounds. Orctions are in 6 different styles. Once one player has the designated number of sets they win stage one of the game and then decide which pairs of other players must fight in the dice combat game in the arena until there is only one Orc left alive. The player who has this Orc is the winner of the entire game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: This mini - even micro - game has just the 6 German armies/panzergruppen and 9 Russian army and front units, plus 2 air units for each side. 3 hexes from Smolensk to Moscow. Uses Dark Crusade's, Tiger Attack's, and Darkest Crusade's simple firing point/hit system.  20-day turns as in Moscow Defended! Blitzkrieg. Siberians. A half-hour-lunch game, FREE to print off and play from CoatneyHistory (where the rules are).
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Strat is an easy game to learn. It is not a game of chance.

Each game is a unique adventure itself. Your only ally is your strategy.

Strat can be played with two or four players. The goal is to earn as many points as possible stealing your opponents’ pieces and placing them in safety on the points squares. Following the rules for moving pieces, players strive to capture their opponents’ pieces and stack them in towers. But watch out. If you don’t have the best strategy, your adversary will be the one collecting the points!

If you like games where winning is all up to you, Strat will offer you a world of strategic possibilities.

As easy to learn as checkers but as strategic to play as chess!
Beware: the simple rules are not what they seem!

Turds is a Fun, Strategic, Humoristic and Nasty card game.

Turds is so nasty!! For the first time you really have "Sh#tty Cards" and "This is some bullsh#t!" moments. Be ready for everything, Turds are coming from everywhere!!

How it's played;
Every player has 4 Turds in front and want to get rid of them as quickly as they can.

You can do two things in a turn;
Play and Draw or Discard and Draw from your hand. When you play a card, you fulfill the written action. On the cards you find actions that can destroy, capture, teleport, transfer, disable and more.
The first player to lose his or her turds wins the game!!

There are alot of different cards in one deck (total of 86 cards) and for that reason there are plenty strategic and dirty ways to win the game.

Are you ready for the most nasty and explosive fight of the world!?

The Nobles is a fast-paced medieval fighting game where you take turns hacking and slashing at each other to see who can strike the killing blow first.  Every single strike there's a chance to lose your head, adding a suspenseful life-threatening urgency to every move!

The game is simple.  Like in the classic black knight fight scene from Monty Python and the Holy Grail, each player fights until they lose all their limbs, or lose their head.  Play cards to fight and confuse your opponent.  Roll the dice to charge and retreat.  And draw from the bag o' death to see what body part gets cut off.

The Nobles can be played with two players in a one-on-one duel, or with three to four players in a free-for-all death match, or played with teams (2-on-2 or even 3-on-1).

With a mix of strategy and luck, games can average around 20-30 minutes, or can be over in as little as 60 seconds.  We've played a lot of games and only once witnessed a killing blow in the first turn, but boy was it satisfying.  Boom... head shot!

boardgame: The Nobles: Kickstarter Exclusive – The Ranger
description:
The Ranger is a new character the designer decided to add as a bonus for all those people helping to get The Nobles funded on Kickstarter.

She comes with two new playing cards, a new player mat, and of course her game piece. For those who backed at The Lord level, she will come as an upgraded miniature.

From the publisher:

Things are getting out of control around here. Those wandering clones are still doing their thang, aimlessly meandering about, waiting for you to round them up so you won't get fired or fed to anything. The thing is, now they've entered the burg (some of them, anyway) and they're populating the digs and whatnot. This BRILLIANT expansion to the Dementalism game introduces brand new DIGS cards. These guys alter the very fabric of reality, influencing the way the game is played for everyone. Do you have the NOGGIN to match wits with Oith's greatest dementalists, or should I call your mommy so she can come kiss your boo boos? That's what I thought...

From the publisher:

In Hold Your Breath! each player assumes the role of a pirate treading water in the ocean. These brilliant tacticians have decided to play a game of "Hold Your Breath" to see who is the toughest pirate because, you know, what else are you gooing do in the middle of the ocean facing certain death? The game is played in two rounds. In the first round, each player may dive deeper, providing they have the "Swim" and "Air" cards to do so. Players press their luck each turn since it is the pirate who dives the deepest in round one and resurfaces in round two who is the winner. What about the losers? Who cares, they are losers. But if you really want to know, well, they dove down and ran out of oxygen before they could resurface, so now they are just "resting" underwater until the next game, okay? But we digress... Players can play "Panic", "Shark", and other nasty cards on the other players, slowing their progress. In the second round each player struggles to reach the surface before he runs out of air and therefore decides to take a rest. Most of the pirates may not make it back to the surface, but the one who makes it back alive and dove the deepest is the winner. Be careful, though, because if you dove the deepest in the first round, you will surely have the toughest time getting back to the surface since A) you swam the deepest and B) no one else wants to see you make it back alive. This is a 4 pack bonus card set with manual.

From the publisher:

Zap, Zoom, Boom, Bam, Wham... Yowza! You Gotta Say It to Play It in this quick discarding game. Chant along, and if the card you discard matches the word you just chanted, then you get stuck taking the discard pile! If you discard a "wild" card, you get to keep going... but at your own risk! Just be sure you're the first to get rid of all your cards so you can be the winner... Yowza!

This is an expansion for Hot Tin Roof. 4 Turf tiles are included that allow the player to claim a patio as their home turf, and whenever another player places a cat on the board from a Home tile on home turf, they must pay that player 1 can.

This expansion contains both the Turf Tiles and cans required for the expansion.

Mud Boggin' is a mini-expansion for Road Rally USA that allows players to place Mud Tiles on the board at start up.

During the game, when a player starts their movement in a space containing a mud tile, the first card they play will give a movement of 1.

Put the ham, cheese and tomato between two slices of bread and the toast will be yours!

Your goal is to have created more toasts than your opponents when the game ends.Using wisely the ingredients you are given, you can delay the other players' toasts and be declared the winner!

The uncompleted toasts are face up in the middle of the table.When it is your turn to play you simply draw the top card of the face down ingredient deck.Then you have to choose in which uncompleted toast the ingredient will go.Every toast needs to consist of two slices of bread, one slice of cheese, ham and tomato. Cheese, ham and tomato have to be placed on top of the first slice of bread.In order to complete the toast you need to place the second slice of bread on top of all these ingredients and you simply take the toast in front of you as a reward!

A word game in which players compete to earn the most picture cards. Players earn points for taking word tiles and grammatically using them correctly in a sentence or phrase.  The player earning the most points in a round gets a chance to answer a trivia question on the back of a picture card won.  They may tell a joke in the process.  At the end of game play, the player with the most picture cards wins.
Roll the die, get through the obstacles and be the player whose goose passes first the finish line!
Your opponent will ask you questions about a specific issue.You have to answer correctly as many as possible to win and learn!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Triba Nation is a turn based card game where each player is required to construct a total of five buildings, with the palace as the final building. The first player who builds the palace is recognised as the king and wins the game.

Players start the game with 3 Tribesmen cards. A shared Building Option line of 4 Building cards and a Palace card is setup in the middle of the play area. 
Each round then consists 4 phases:

    Phase 1 (Draw) - The player draws 2 Tribesmen cards.
    Phase 2 (Tribesmen) - The player gets to play Tribesmen cards. A max of 5 Tribesmen can be in the player's area. Players can also swap out Tribesmen during this phase (discarding the one they replaced). Tribesmen are required to build buildings and also activate Attack/Magic cards.
    Phase 3 (Construction) - Players can build one Building from the Building Option line if their Tribesmen colors match the Building's requirements. Building special abilities also take place during this phase.
    Phase 4 (Action) - Players can play any Attack or Magic cards in hand if they have more Warrior(Attack) or Shaman(Magic) cards than their target. These allow the player to destroy, steal from  and generally muck with the targeted opponent.


At the end of the 4th phase, the player trims his hand down to 5 cards and play continues with the next player. The players try to build 4 building and 1 palace (which must be built last). The first player to build the palace wins.

Variant 1:
Everybody takes the cubes in his color.
Make one central pile, everybody puts one cube on the pile. When it falls, you take your cube back. Other cubes stay in the middle. 
Then start a new pile.
The first player that has no cubes left this round and gains one point.

Variant 2:
The start player builds a construction with his cubes. 
Then the other players try to rebuild this one. The first player to achieve this receives one point.

The game challenges you to invent new variants whith these cubes.

Some important high-security documents are lost from the Prime Minister's Office. Prime Minister calls hurriedly the Secret Service and asks them to rush and catch the spy the fastest. The spy succeeds to erase his tracks.

Will the Secret Agents be able to surround and catch the spy?A hunt from town to town and from country to country, which takes the breath away from all those involved!

Do you want to be a Spy or a Secret Agent?Choose sides and start the hunting all over Europe until you complete your mission!

In Secret Service, one of the players takes on the role of the Spy. His job is to move from point to point around the map of Europe taking cars, trains, boats or airplanes. The secret agents – that is, the remaining players acting in concert – move around similarly in an effort to move into the same space as the Spy. But while the criminal's mode of transportation is nearly always known, his exact location is only known intermittently throughout the game.

This is the first game in the Magical Legend series of games. The game is set in a mythical Chinese world like that of the Chinese classic "Investiture of the Gods". The game world is further fleshed out in other Magical Legend comics and novels.

There are 2 game modes.

Game Mode 1 (Deities vs Demons): 
Gameplay-wise this is similar to Bang. There are 3 roles (Deity, Demons, Spirits). The Deity wins if he kills all Demons and non-subjugated Spirits. The Demons win if they kill the Deity. The Spirits win if they kill the Deity and 1 Demon or 1 Spirit who has killed a Demon. The Deity has 3 power up cards which he can offer as a reward to subjugate(ie. ally) a Spirit. Spirits can renege on the alliance/subjugation but will suffer penalties for doing so. The game replaces the suits (Heart, Spades, etc) of Bang with a 5-element system (Metal, Wood, Water, Fire, Earth) and characters, abilities and card actions vary depending on the elements involved. There is no concept of Distance and no Missed cards. Attacks succeed or are countered based on the elements. Card actions often require Magic points to power them. Otherwise the gameplay is quite similar.

Game Mode 2 (Faction Battles):
This mode can only be played with an even number of players (4-10). Players split into 2 camps and a representative from each camp draw 1 of 5 faction cards to see what faction they are. Factions have specific powers which can be triggered to allow cooperation between members of the faction. There are no hidden roles in this mode and all players of a faction win if the other faction is eliminated. Other aspects of gameplay (ie. drawing, attacking, etc) are similar to game mode 1.

Players in this game compete to be the first to collect the 6 cards depicting the 6 pieces of the Armor of God. The first person to collect all 6 cards and discard all other cards from his hand wins.

The players start with 3 cards each. On their turn, they draw a card from the draw pile and decide to either keep the card or play it. The rules do not mention a hand limit but logically a hand limit of 6 should be applied. Otherwise there is no way to win the game.

Cards can be pieces of the Armor of God, or action cards. Action cards include :

     Drop 1 and Reverse - the targeted player needs to discard 1 piece of the Armor of God failing which they will miss a turn. Then the direction of play is reversed.
     Drop 2 and Reverse - Similar to Drop 1 and Reverse except 2 cards need to be discarded.
     Jumper - This card can be played at any time and takes precedence over all other cards. Play jumps back to the player who played the card.
     Skip - This skips the next player's turn.
     Little Angel - This counters the Drop 1 and 2 cards.

Description from the publisher:

Just when you had that bear attack under control, things got all Trail Mix'd! See if you can adapt as the game changes with each roll of the die!

Before each round of Bears!, the expansion die is rolled and a new rule is in effect. One round you're scoring double for Runners, the next your rerolls are limited — or maybe you're trying for bonus points by shooting the most bears.

Think fast and roll even faster in this ever-changing expansion to Bears!
Glide along the trail rolling dice, completing odd & even cards & gathering Whirly tokens. The first snail to join Whirly in the center of the garden wins the game!

A Christmas-themed racing game for children.

The four (yes, four!) wooden Magi are placed on a table or on the floor, about a meter from the pieces representing Mary, Joseph and baby Jesus. At the start of the game, each player is secretly assigned a color, and to win he/she has to be the first to move the wise man of the corresponding colour to the Holy Family. Movement is decided by rolling a die and moving a king the length of a wooden camel piece. Players can move their own wise man as well as those of other players. Give your colour away too soon and you risk that the other players move you back!

A murder mystery dinner-style party game in which each player is assigned a role and players try to find out who the murderer is. Each player has his/her own information booklet, and five game rounds are spent discussing clues and inconsistencies until players vote on who to accuse of the murder.

The setting for this case is downtown Manhattan, 1981. The members of the Assassino clan are having a meeting at Ristorante Assassino when Vico, son and right hand of Don Assassino is found murdered at the lavatory.

A murder mystery dinner-style party game in which each player is assigned a role and players try to find out who the murderer is. Each player has his/her own information booklet, and five game rounds are spent discussing clues and inconsistencies until players vote on who to accuse of the murder.

This case takes place at an almost deserted college campus during the Christmas holidays. All remaining inhabitants of the campus have been invited to a Christmas dinner, but the last guest arrives with horrible news: Stewart White, janitor, has been found murdered in his room, and the killer is among the guests.
Roles include the campus cop, a football player, a cheerleader and an exchange student.

A murder mystery dinner-style party game in which each player is assigned a role and players try to find out who the murderer is. Each player has his/her own information booklet, and five game rounds are spent discussing clues and inconsistencies until players vote on who to accuse of the murder.

This case is set in the Berlin of the golden 20s. The producer of a silent movie is hosting a press dinner behind the scenes to present the newest star actress, Marlies Reinhold, to the public. Celebrities and press representatives are waiting for Marlies' entrance when the host appears alone, reporting he has just found Marlies murdered. Since the set had been closed to anyone but the people present at the dinner all day long, one thing is certain: The killer is among those present.

A murder mystery dinner-style party game in which each player is assigned a role and players try to find out who the murderer is. Each player has his/her own information booklet, and five game rounds are spent discussing clues and inconsistencies until players vote on who to accuse of the murder.

Cognac, France, in 1938: The ladies and gentlemen have left the property for the weekend. The remaining employees are jumping at the chance to have a pompous dinner. Butler, cook, assistant cook, gardener, chambermaid and nanny - everyone is preparing for the occasion when a murder is discovered and the killer has to be found.

A murder mystery dinner-style party game in which each player is assigned a role and players try to find out who the murderer is. Each player has his/her own information booklet, and five game rounds are spent discussing clues and inconsistencies until players vote on who to accuse of the murder.

This case offers a cast of 8 particularly bizarre characters, among them a power-hungry conductor, an extreme eco-terrorist, a genius inventor, an old lady suffering from actinic dermatitis and a man in a kangaroo costume. The 8 craziest gangsters of the world have met to capture their common arch-enemy Major Sunshine, whose secrets would grant them world dominance. But then a bomb goes off in the cell in which the Major is kept, and now they have to find out who betrayed their alliance and killed the Major.

From the pulbisher:

If you like Speedy Recall you'll LOVE Speedy Recall Double-Take! Take the extra challenge to give your funny bone a boost! Flip over a category card and a letter but wait... DOUBLE TAKE! Your answer has to start with "G" and it must also consider the Double-Take card! Easy? Maybe if everyone else wasn't also trying to come up with an answer at the same time. The giggles quickly turn to hoots and hollers when Speedy Recall-Double Take is on the table!

Each player forms 10 little marbles from one of the 3 containers of play-doh that come with the game. Then Kalle, a can that opens and closes its lid and moves its arms while rotating at the center of three catapults, is activated. The players try to shoot their marbles into the can with the catapults. The first player to get in three marbles wins the game.

(The bases of the catapult also contain some molds for sculpting play-doh.)

From the publisher:

From the cold region of the Northern Mountains, comes a man with tremendous strength and fortitude. He has heard the call for adventure, and answered it, with attitude! The Barbarian is a tough Hero that adds new strategy and options to the game. This Expansion adds an additional Character to the game and can be played with 5 players.

"Prussia 20" features two complete wargames covering key battles fought in Central Europe during the 1806-1807 War of the Fourth Coalition: Jena 20 2nd Edition, and Eylau 20!

Jena 20 2nd Edition (October, 1806) highlights the stunning French victory which shattered the vaunted Prussian army at the twin battles of Jena and Auerstadt. With the Prussians realizing they have already been out maneuvered, their army is desperately trying to re-position itself while the French attempt to encircle and destroy them. Can the Prussian rear guard under Hohenlohe hold off Napoleon's Grand Armée long enough at Jean to allow Brunswick's main body to crush Davout's lone French corps under which blocks their path to safety, or will the "Iron Marshal's" veteran 3rd Corps stand firm against three times their numbers and seal the Prussian's doom? The fate of two great empires is yours to decide.

Fought in some of the most severe weather conditions imaginable, the confused and indecisive battle of Eylau 20! (February, 1807) stands as a testament to the misery and horror of warfare. In the dead of winter, Russian General Levin August von Bennigsen halted his exhausted army at a small deserted crossroads village and prepared to give battle. For two days, in sub-zero temperatures with heavy snow falling almost continuously, Napoleon's troops battered themselves against the unyielding Russian lines to little avail. Is the historical bloody and inconclusive draw the most likely outcome, or can you do better than the actual commanders and manage to eke out a victory in the terrible "Battle in a Blizzard?"

boardgame: Mensch freue Dich!: Das Quadriga Rennspiel
description:
Each player starts with four horses. The first player to get all his horses behind the finish line wins the race. If a horse gets on a space already occupied by the horse of another player, it sends this horse back to the starting line. Horses on a space of their own color are safe. Several horses of the same color may occupy the same space. If a player rolls a 1 he may roll again.

If the players chose to bet on the winner of the race, the winner of the game is the player who won the bet. Since the bets are revealed only after the end of the game, the players can hinder or favor their favorite to influence the outcome of the race.

Each player receives the same number of meeples (4, 5 or more). At the beginning of the game, a pawn is placed on each of the spaces 1-5. If a player rolls the number of an occupied space, he must take away the pawn. If the space is free, he may put a meeple there and rolls again. If he rolls the devil's head, he may put a pawn on the inner space. No pawns is taken away from this space. The first player who loses all his pawns, wins the game.

At the beginning of the game, four token are placed on every colored body-circle of the eight figures on the game board. The players start from the circle of their color in one of the four corners of the board. If they get on a colored body-circle, they get to stack one of the token on their playing piece. On a circle of his own color, the player takes all token on the circle. If a player gets on a space that is occupied by another player, he gets all the token from his playing piece, which is then returned to it's starting circle. The captured token can be brought back to the safety of ones starting circle. The game ends when all body-circles are empty and all playing pieces have returned to their starting circles. The player, who catures the most token wins the game.

The night of the summer solstice, all the goblins are on the moor to designate their king. Enter the round and build the most beautiful dolmen to win the title.

Dolmen is a beautiful wooden play on the Celtic theme published in limited series. You play the Goblins, these small Breton goblins, who will build their dolmen picking up the slabs arranged in a circle around the stone table.

GOAL OF THE GAME

The goal is to have the most points in its own construction at the end of the game. It stops when a player has 7 tiles in its dolmen.

In a turn, players will either move their korrigan AND watch a slab OR try to add a tile to their dolmen AND watch a slab

A korrigan moves from 0 to 3 (without rolling the dice) on the light points around the circle in any direction.

Each tile has a colored light on two of its sides. Arranged on the circle early in the game, the face of each slab has points (2 to 6). These will serve as the final countdown to determine the winner.

To add a stone to the dolmen, the korrigan must be in front of it and one of the colored sides of the stone must be the same color as the ends of the dolmen.

The player rolls the dice and if the result is more than the score of the slab, it can be placed by matching lights. 
If the dice score is not enough, you need to consume power points symbolized by colored gems and give the difference on the stone table of the fairy.

To recover its power points, the player must go see the fairy. This will also take the opportunity to replace the stones around the circle.

As soon as a dolmen is finished, each player counts his points (even those with incomplete dolmen) and adds the unused points of powers. The winner is the one with the highest score.

Warning, this is not necessarily the fastest who is the winner ...

A cooperative children's game in which distinguishing the weight of objects is a central element.

Aim of the game: 6 wooden hippos (two light ones, two medium-weight ones, two heavy ones) have to be moved to the finishing spaces that correspond to their weights. If the players are successful, they all win together.

How it is played: Players take turn rolling the die and completing the corresponding actions (moving a hippo 1, 2 or 3 spaces on the board, switching two hippos, moving 2 hippos one space each). During each turn, they lift the hippo and comment on whether they think it is light, medium or heavy.
(The weight category is specified on the underside of the hippos' feet, but no peeking is allowed during the game.)

Posthuman pits you as one of the last human survivors in a world where nature is reclaiming the planet and our evolved offspring are working to eradicate us.

In the game, players are humans on a solitary journey to a promised safe house. This journey takes them through ten zones, with the zones being tile-based and randomly generated. As they travel, they encounter each other, other humans, and posthuman mutants. Encounters with mutants may inflict scars, and the more scars players have, the less human they are and the more posthuman they become.

Defiant Expansion introduces the option of playing Posthuman with 5-6 players and includes new types of armour and home-made weapons.

description:
Carcassonne: Burgen in Deutschland is a mini-expansion for Carcassonne that consists of six double-sized tiles depicting German castles.

At the start of the game, each player receives one (with 4-6 players) or two (with 2-3 players) castle tiles. During the game, a player can choose to place one of their castle tiles instead of the regular tile in hand, with tile parts needing to match as usual; when the player places it, they can place a follower somewhere on the tile. If the follower is in the main castle, the tile scores like a cloister and is worth 12 points when completely surrounded. If a city or road is completed during the game and part of that structure is on a castle tile, then the city or road is worth 3 additional points, no matter who placed the tile.

Part of the Carcassonne series.

Long before famous Irishman, Bram Stoker, penned his famous novel, the vampire stalked the night in the Emerald Isle. The Dearg Due is Ireland’s original vampire. A woman of stunning beauty, the Dearg Due was forced into a marriage to a man she didn’t love by her controlling father. Distraught about never being able to be with the man she truly loved, she killed herself, but death could not hold her in the grave. She rose up and exacted revenge on her father and husband, and drained their bodies of blood. Now, she rises from her grave in County Waterford to spread her curse upon the land.

Her vampire minions must drink in the counties of Ireland, vying to become the top vampire of all the island. Or, choose to be a werewolf and try to thwart the vampires.

Gampeplay
The gameplay is designed so that the game is interesting and fun, even for those not drinking, but simple enough that people who are drinking can play the game. The game will still be quite enjoyable if the drinking aspect is omitted entirely.

Goal
The first player to reach 25 victims, and make it safely back to the Waterford Cemetery, wins the game.

Players travel from adjacent county to county (or by train, if they wish to skip to a different part of the island). They then roll the dice to determine how many victims they will get that night (if any). Drinks are determined by the number of victims. The players may also attack each other.

If the players wish, one player may take on the role of the werewolf, whose mission is to kill all of the vampires.
A card game of rummy in which players form correct subject/verb agreement combinations of two or more cards. Reinforce language arts skills with this 60 card deck.

Silvanus is a co-operative 2 player card game in which players are spiders with human like battle tactics. The goal is to survive to game end, possibly carrying remaining health to a more challenging scenario. Threats deal a sequence of colored card icons which must be matched in a given time for success. This is accomplished with small icon decks called "Halots".

Action action decks contain either enemies or Items that can be gathered to help in encounters. At certain points of the game, "Happenstance" cards will sometimes hinder players actions whereas Character cards present helpful traits.

Silvanus is a versatile game, many of the components can be added or adjusted to fit a desired difficulty for players. There are optional modes of play such as allotted moves instead of real time play, and more. Short story snippets are included in the rules to give players a glimpse of Silvanus lore.

boardgame: Aqua Brunch: The Sea Dragon Promo Card
description:
The Sea Dragon is a promo card for Aqua Brunch.

Shuffle the Sea Dragon in the predators deck. The Sea Dragon is a predator with Strenght 10.

It can be kept in hand or passed like any other predator. At the end of the round, if the player who has the Dragon scores the highest sum, he/she will choose the food first. Otherwise he/she will be the last to choose, regardless of his/her outcome.

From the back of the box:

"The goal of Siege! is to collect unit cards or take them from other players, until you have 3 full sets!

"Use Order cards to exchange a unit card with another player, receive the winnings from a jousting tournament, pay money to the treasury, draw more cards, retreat, capture the treasury, and more fun actions, to help you build your war chest and be the first to have 3 full sets!"

Brainy Bear Learning Game is an educational game for young children. It is designed for 2, 3, 4, or 5 children of kindergarten age. This version is designed to enhance verbal and reading skills. Players advance along a pathway by responding correctly to flash card cues. The board has a travel theme with automobile graphics. The first child to reach the finish line (a garage at home) is declared the winner.

Players roll dice and move their wooden boats toward the finish line. There are 24 squares to move along. The first player to reach the finish line is the winner.
Complete game comes with 6 wooden boats (3 in blue and 3 in yellow colors), dice, some coins (made of paper) and a plate for components.

Feirmeoir is a simulation of the Irish Potato famine in game form. The object of the game is to build the greatest family by accumulating the most points by the end of the game (or beating 
your previous single player high score).  The year is 1844 and you are a young Irish couple. You have just been given your first plot of land as a wedding gift from your parents. There are many 
plots for sale, so be sure to pick the best spot! Your parents and neighbors can help you out by building your first two room Peat House, but from then on you are on your own. The nearby 
village will provide you with all you need to start your own farm, and hopefully a family.  Make sure you prepare well, there is no telling what hardships await you!

boardgame: Heavy Gear Blitz!: War for Terra Nova
description:
Heavy Gear is a fast paced tabletop miniatures mecha game and this edition continues that focus. Heavy Gear is about deploying your army of Gears, Tanks, Infantry, and Striders, and using that army to defeat your friends in a game of skill and tactics. Your tabletop becomes the battlefield. You see and feel the ebb and flow of the game in a visceral way as your decisions, and the dice, control the outcome. Your choices and how you move your forces will determine your fate and reputation as a winning commander.

This version of Heavy Gear Blitz is often referred to as the 'Living Rulebook' edition

description: The round trip begins and ends in Wernigerode. The winner of the game is not the player who ist the first to return, but the one who vists the most cities and landmarks. This is achieved by collecting points at the spaces marked with the number of points awarded. If a player gets on a big red space, he has to follow the blue path, till it leads back to the main red path.

From the introduction:

These Ancient Wargaming rules are a set of tactical simulation rules for miniatures, which allow players to recreate and fight, historic or hypothetical battles which occurred from the rise of organised warfare to the beginning of the age of gunpowder.

The rules are set out in detailed format, divided into logical chapters, and sub-chapters. In the rear are a set of charts and tables most frequently used, thus saving time and trouble when playing.

boardgame: Apex Theropod Deck-Building Game: Exotic Predators Limited Edition
description:
Limited Edition is the initial Kickstarter print run of Apex Theropod Deck-Building Game (delivered 2015)

Apex is a deck-building game, played solo or with up to 7 friends. You play as a prehistoric predator competing for territory and resources against other predators. Each playable species has a unique deck to master. Each deck has different strengths, weaknesses, and strategies— creating a varied and constantly evolving experience.

Your species must overcome a very brutal environment including harsh climate changes, disease, attacks from predators, grievous wounds, infections, and deadly prey. The game incorporates many dinosaurs that behave in their own distinct way. The goal of the game is to endure the environment, build up the population and evolve your species, and become the apex predator.

Exotic Predators LE Includes:

• 600 cards + 15 Card Dividers

• 32pg Rulebook

• Supports up to 8 players


Includes 9 Playable Apex Decks:
