description:
Alfons Åberg is the main character in a series of very popular children’s books from Sweden.

It´s a game in two steps. First it is a memory and roadbuilding-game, there the players pick-up puzzle-shaped tiles and try to finish the path to the treehouse. When the last piece is placed and the path is finished, that player can then start to move their pawns. First player that reach the treehouse with his/hers pawns wins.

English description:
Each player, at their turn, has either to answer a question about French language (orthography, litterature...), or mime or draw a French expression, depending on the space their pawn is located on the board. If they answer correctly, they roll the dice and advance the corresponding number on the board.
The first player reaching the final space and answering correctly the final question wins the game.

French description:
Chaque joueur, à son tour, doit soit répondre à une question sur la langue française (orthographe, littérature...), ou mimer ou dessiner une expression française, suivant la case où leur pion est situé sur le plateau. S'il répond correctement, il lance le dé et avance du nombre de cases correspondantes sur le plateau. Le premier joueur à atteindre la case finale et à répondre correctement à la dernière question remporte la partie.

Have you ever built your dream sandwich? Imagine doing that and having a great card game too! Introducing Turkey My Mayonnaise, the hottest new card game on the market!

The object is simple. Be the first to complete sandwich recipes... any way you can. See a piece of cheese on someone's sandwich you like? Take it with a "Steal" card. Pepperocinis in the discard pile? Time for a dumpster dive! Sandwich combinations are virtually endless so the game is unique every time!

A set collection and building game.

Zeppelin Attack!: Robotic Promo Packet contains five "promo" cards for your Zeppelin Attack! game.

- Edison's Atomic Robot (Attack)
- Tesla's Atomic Robot (Defense)
- Man with a Plan (Operative)
- No Rest For The Wicked (Fate)
- The Invizeppelin (Zeppelin)

Power Legion is a fast-playing tabletop wargame ruleset. Each player controls a team of heroes or villains, each with their own attributes, traits, and tricks.
 Create any hero from comics, animation or film with a flexible point-based system. Use 3 to 5 figures per side or play larger games. Playable on a 3'x3' table using any figures you already own.
family: Series: Song of Blades and Heroes

description:
Aurelian is a tabletop game about the Crisis of the Third Century, the period during which the Roman empire nearly disintegrated in constant civil war and foreign invasions.

Players take one of four roles: a Roman, Germanic, Sarmatian, or Persian commander. Any number of players can conduct a campaign, in which each type of army has a different set of victory conditions. While holding off Rome's many enemies, the Roman players are also trying to defeat each other and ultimately become (and remain!) emperor.

The Persians are trying to carve away provinces in the east and add them to the Sassanian monarchy. The barbarians are trying to raid and carry away as much loot as possible while amassing glory from victorious battles over any sort of enemy, even one's own tribe. Every battle is therefore a balancing act, in which the military and political context is constantly shifting.
 Aurelian can be played with miniatures, or with "unit tiles" on any flat surface, much in the same manner as Blücher's unit cards. The centerpiece of the game is the campaign, in which each player manages the fortunes of his character and faction, much in the manner of the campaigns in Maurice and Longstreet. 
 Unlike most Ancients games, which attempt to span the millennia from Moses to Columbus, Aurelian concentrates on a specific period of roughly 70 years. Players fight a series of nine battles against a variety of enemies, often from one's own side. The composition of their armies changes. Veteran Roman legionaries become incresingly scarce and irreplaceable as the Roman players cobble together vexillations and recruit barbarian foedorati and tributarii. Will the Persians squander their expensive elite cataphracts and elephant units in early battles, or hold them in reserve? Will a barbarian warlord play it safe and not raid as deeply into the empire, at the risk of not bringing back enough treasure and glory to maintain the loyalty of his optimates?

description:
When life gives you Lemons... make lemonade!

Lemons is a card game where each Lemon card is played in front of a player and it counts as a minus point, but if a player gathers 3 matching Lemon cards he makes Lemonade and the 3 negative points become 1 positive point. So keep an eye on his Lemon stacks and make sure you don't play a 3rd card of the same type.

Each type of Lemon card in front of a player allows that player to play cards on his/her turn in a particular way. Which can be to his left or right, straight across or on his own stacks. On a player's turn he/she must choose one of the Lemon card stacks in front of him/herself and play cards as allowed by that card.

To add a bit of suspense, when a player legally chooses to play a card in front of themself, it may be placed face down in their secret stash. When the game ends, these cards are added to the face up stacks, so you might not know how many pitchers of Lemonade have been made by a player until it's time to...  drink up.

Vuelo 747 ("Flight 747") is a game about traveling the world, Flight aboard the worldwide famous Jumbo 747 Airplaine;, each player begins with an amount of money and takes its turn by spining the airplaine, placing the flag and taking the country card landed if its the first arrived or paying to the country card location owner the amount of the fee.

Like a fast monopoly version where you win by having more money at the end, or by eliminating other players before the last country is taken.

Good way for teaching kids (and late kids) about countries flags, currency and several 70s facts.

This expansion adds 14 new factions and 7 home worlds, 5 unique game tiles, 2 new types of dice, the Leader and Entrepreneur; plus optional objectives.

One of each player’s initial Home dice in the cup is replaced with a Leader die, accelerating the start of the game. Leader dice, unlike other types of dice, cannot be acquired during game play.

Some Leader and Entrepreneur die faces show two phase symbols. With these faces, if their assigned phase doesn't occur but its other phase does, then the die automatically shifts to the other phase. Other Leader and Entrepreneur die faces show a dollar sign in addition to a phase symbol. With these faces, if the die is used for that phase (instead of being reassigned), then it returns to the cup (not the Citizenry) after being used. When a Leader die is used to Consume, it always matches the world’s color (Entrepreneur dice never match any world).

20 objective tiles are supplied, with 6 randomly chosen each game. When an objective is satisfied, the player(s) who met it that round receive its listed number of Talent counters (from 2-5) and then that objective is no longer available. A player may assign Talent counters as "one-shot" wild workers (who, if used for Consume, always match their world). If their assigned phase doesn't occur, those Talent counters aren't used up, allowing for conditional assignments. At game end, any unused Talent counters are each worth a victory point.

description:
Wood Wars 3D is a highly accessible table top strategy game for all developed by Joe Larson. In Wood Wars 3D, players build armies by taking race heads and putting them on class bodies to mix-and-match pawns. Then players take turns moving herds of pawns using measured distances, and attacking each other, simulating artillery attacks with marble rolls. It extends the rules of the original Wood Wars with detailed 3D printed modular minis.

Wood Wars 3D is an open sourced, light, strategy game, rooted in classic strategy game and manufacture by the future with 3D printing.

At the dawn of time in an enchanted woods where no man lives there was a spring of sweet water. The animals that drank from that spring found themselves possessed of intelligence. But an evil saw this and tainted the water so that the creatures would also gain pride, greed, and form similar to man. And by so doing, soon the woods knew war.

9292 is a card game for 2 to 4 players. You are are a novice cook, who finished the race that more food. Each player uses the dice and cook cards, then select the "make dishes card" to embellish one by one, alternating clockwise roll the dice.

The eyes of the dice representing the fire temperature, because each player turns plus the value rolled the dice continue, as the game progresses the fire temperature is getting higher.

Each card has its cooking dishes needed for completion [temperature] is expressed as a number, (the center of the card number), and the sum of the roll out [Fire Temperature dice, if the number of matching food card, completed its cuisine will be the.

The player rolls the dice so snugly to the number listed on the card can be obtained as a score card that dish, but if the sum of the dice eyes of fire temperatures exceeding written in cooking card, the food is discarded, and the other negative will have to take a penalty.

he game basically ends in the fifth round. Playtime is around about 30 minutes to about 20 minutes. After five rounds of fine cooking all the points in the lower-right corner of each card are counted. Charred food (upside down when imported shown) by calculating the minus 1 point per sheet of subtract points from the finished dish. The biggest sum determines the winner of the game.

Welcome Archeologists! This tomb has been recently uncovered by a freak sand storm. It’s up to you and your rival archeologists to traverse the long forgotten tunnels, defeat monsters, and search for the vast treasures beyond your wildest dreams Before the tomb is once again lost to the sands of time.

On each turn, a player rolls 1d6 to determine their movement to explore the tomb. The tomb itself is broken down into 3x3 square tiles that you draw randomly and add to the map as you go. Sometimes you might find a monster Hiding in the halls. These monsters are hoarding the treasures for themselves and it's up to you to defeat them by rolling a higher dice roll than their power level. Once the monster is defeated you get the treasure for yourself. Some of this treasure may help you in your adventure, or be a curse that hinders your progression.

Be careful though. The tomb is only open for a limited time and anyone can trigger the collapse. Once the tomb collapses you have a limited amount of time to get back to the entrance. If you make it out then congratulations, count up your treasure. The archeologist with the most treasure wins! If you don't make it out, sorry about your luck. Maybe someday the tomb will be uncovered again and a future archeologist will find your corpse among a pile of valuable treasures.

User summary:
こねくとこねくと (Connect Connect) is a game based on the lottery mechanic called Ghost Leg ('Amidakuji' in Japan). In this game, you use strings to make connections between face-down cards, trying to reach a high score. The true paths are hidden but you get to see two cards. Can you make the right connections?

Designer's summary:
[あみだくじ]と言うくじ引きのルールを取り入れたゲームです。実際の紐を使って得点を競い合います。

Postcard mini-expansion (available for download from the designer's website in German or English)

Free Choice is a new merchandise card

The owner of the card can freely choose when stocking up or when placing a Merchandise token in a town with two trading houses one which trading house there he places a token.

But he may still place a merchandise token only in a city where he already has merchandise token(s)

Postcard mini-expansion (available for download from the designer's website in German or English)

Christmas Sale is a new merchandise card.

The owner can sell once in the game more than one Merchandise token in one town on the trading card.

If used carriages have to be removed don't do it before the complete sale.

A bonus for the last trading card in the game will only be counted once for that sale

You and your fellow players are mushroom hunters. You're attempting to go out onto the mushroom mountain and bring back the most mushrooms, while also selling them for the best price at market. Mushroom Hunters is played using colored meeples, curved mushroom cards, and flat price cards. Who will be the best mushroom hunter?!

Initial setup of the game requires mixing up all of the curved mushroom cards and kind of shoving them into a big pile (aka mushroom mountain). Then shuffle all of the market / price cards and deal the top five face up and place the rest in a deck face down (which I messed up for this picture, oops!). Each player gets all 5 of their specified meeple color.

On each player's turn, they can take one of the following three actions:

1) Place one of your meeples on any ONE mushroom card on mushroom mountain. The meeple must have both feet touching the card and must be STANDING in order to count. If it falls, I think you must remove it from mushroom mountain and your turn ends with no properly placed meeple mushroom hunters.

2) Return any ONE of your meeples, along with the card it is standing on, to your play area. The number of mushrooms on the card is the number that you have collected.

3) Return ALL of your meeples, along with the cards that they were standing on, to your play area. The number of mushrooms on ALL of these cards is the number that you have collected.

At any time during your turn, you can cash in any amount of mushrooms you have collected during the current or previous turns for a market / price card.

The game continues until either all of the market / sell cards have been taken by players, or all of the cards from mushroom mountain have been taken. Once the end is triggered, players count up their money and the player who has earned the most from their mushroom hunting adventure is declared the winner!

boardgame: Drizzit: il gioco di carte – Draghi, spose e coccole estreme
description:
First espansion for Drizzit: the card game.
It includes:
3 new Adventure sets, 
2 new Characters, 
14 new Powers or Equipment cards, 
10 new Adventure cards 
4 Crystal cards that introduce a new rule in the game

Der Grüffelo: Das große Schattenspiel is a cooperative game for 2-4 players, ages 5 and up. The game box is turned into a small shadow theater, where silhouettes are shown in the theater before each turn. Each player is going to be the "Shadow Master", who mustn't take part in the guessing then, however. The shadows have to be discovered (colored and lovingly illustrated) as quickly as possible on the board. Who discovered the correct one of the 48 different shades scenes will be rewarded with a nut and may continue to move the team to to the next space on the forest path field. If the guess was incorrect, it costs a nut-chip and the team stone also is not being moved.

You can only win together when the team reaches the finish. In addition, the player with the most nut chips is then also the team winner. The team can just as well lose together, because we know that the Gruffalo is sometimes a pretty bad guy, which is not to be trusted so completely. Therefore, there are also shadows in the game for which there is - how mean! - no matching scene on the board. As it is, be extra careful then and be not deceived by the Gruffalo. If the Gruffalo card was four times in the game, unfortunately, the whole team has lost.

Snowtide Event Cards is a mini-expansion for Oddball Aeronauts and/or for Oddball Aeronauts 2, which contains 5 cards. It was added as a funding reward to all backers of the Oddball Aeronauts 2: Double the Trouble Kickstarter campaign in 2014. The 5 cards are: Mermaids, Gift Ficklers, Icemordbergs, the Snowfather, and the Snow Maker.

From the Kickstarter page:
"Swaying and spinning their way down to this Kickstarter through the myriad flakes of snow, the Gift Ficklers have brought 5 Snowtide Event Cards (one including the Snowfather himself!)."
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Snowtide Mercenary Cards is a mini-expansion for Oddball Aeronauts and/or for Oddball Aeronauts 2, which contains 5 cards. It was a paid add-on during the Oddball Aeronauts 2: Double the Trouble Kickstarter campaign in 2014. The 5 cards are: Fir Fae, Flurriers, Snow Heralds, the Dragoneer, and the Drift Patrol.

From the Kickstarter page:
"But, not to be outdone, the Fir Fae witches have been weaving their frosty spells and have conjured from the icicles on the Snowmaker’s runners a Snowtide add-on (...) you can get your grubby paws on 5 Snowtide Mercenaries, including a new Dragoneer!."
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
User summary:
コクハク (Kokuhaku) means 'Confession'. The players have all recently died and are taken down to Hell. Before you reach Hell, you have a chance to ascend to Heaven by confessing to your crimes and sins. Each player has seven face-down cards in front of them, each of which represents a crime or a sin they had committed during their lifetime. You must get rid of your cards before anyone else does. Only one player can take the ticket to Heaven!

Publisher's summary:
プレイヤーは全員、地獄に案内された人達・・・
天国に行ける一席を巡って生前犯した罪を「コクハク」する時間がやってきた。各プレイヤーの前に並べた7枚裏向きのカード。このカードを1番最初に無くした人の勝ち

Play Bowling with Cards

There are 156 cards in the deck, consisting of: 
Number cards (1 to 9)
Strike/Spare cards
Gutterball cards
Hole cards (that's an 8 )
Joker cards

There are different ways to play the game.
version 1:
Every player gets 21 cards and has to combine them as 
good as possible in one serie of Bowling

version 2:
Player starts with 5 cards and then plays 1 of them and discard one card. 
Before next turn player draws 2 cards and one of the cards can be the top card from discard pile,
it's not allowed to discard holecards or gutterball cards

User summary:
Princess Escort 魔法と機械 (Princess Escort - Magic & Machine) is an expansion to Princess Escort. It adds new professions; the witch and machine soldiers. Six new cards are added to the base game, making conversation and voting more dramatic. There are also new matching tokens and a manual to explain the new powers.

To play Princess Escort - Magic & Machine, you require a copy of Princess Escort. Princess Escort - Magic & Machine cannot be played on its own.

Publisher summary:
PRINCESS ESCORTの拡張となります。新たな職業[魔法使い]と[機械兵]６種が加わり、より会話と投票が劇的になります。

From the rulebook:

You are a Star Mogul and the owner of a newly formed Salvage Crew. The outer rim is a treasure trove of lost
or abandoned property. Your mission is to stake your claim by force if necessary to these forgotten riches.

Salvage Crew: Star Mogul is a set of rules for use with the 25mm and 15mm scale, table-top sci-fi war games miniatures manufactured by Mega Miniatures.

Battle Merchants: New Kingdoms, an expansion for Battle Merchants, features an alternative Kingdom Card deck that replaces the game's existing Kingdom Cards. A few cards remain the same as originally published, but the deck includes many new cards and a few updates to existing cards.

This expansion also features a couple of new rules and Craft Tracker cards that allow all players to easily show the Craft levels of all of their weapons.

Unofficial fan-made board game based on the Castlevania series, previously available to download as a free print and play until Konami demanded in November 2018 that it be taken down.

The game is mostly co-op, and loosely based on Castlevania 2: Simon's Quest. 2-6 players work together to fight their way through the castle while a curse is slowly killing all of them. The goal is to move through the castle, fighting monsters and overcoming various traps and other events, to gather 5 parts of Dracula's body. Bring them to the tower to resurrect Dracula and break the curse before time runs out, and then, hopefully, kill him again to win the game.

13 playable characters to choose from, each with their own deck of unique weapons and skills which are gradually unlocked throughout the game, and plenty of monsters, events, and items, so that no two games are exactly the same.

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: Game Instructor / Rodney Smith
description:
Pixel Tactics promo card from Watch It Played! Season 4 Indiegogo campaign

Card text: You can choose to take up to 2 extra actions each turn. Each time you take an extra action, your opponent takes an extra action o their next turn as well.

Eccentric Exhibits is a 2-player, 20-card trick-taking game. In this game, players are rival museum curators trying to arrange the most prestigious exhibitions of bizarre and unusual artifacts - unicorn hooves, kraken tentacles, books of pure evil, and alien skulls, to name a few.

Over four rounds, two players will play a series of tricks, trying to win artifacts for their museums and score prestige. The twist is that the highest scoring cards are not always the cards most likely to win a trick. So huge bonus points are available if you can deduce what cards your opponent is able to play and which tricks they are able to win.

With only 20 cards to keep track of, skills in deduction and risk management are paramount. Eccentric Exhibits is a tense, high-skill game for two players.

A sandy game for 2 to 4 players aged 4 years and up. Children love sand and enjoy playing with it. They fill their sand moulds and build the loveliest sand figures. So, why wait ages for the next holiday or moan about the rain? After all, there's this super sand game for at home! In Pass auf, kleine Krabbe you have to fill the sand moulds with sand and cleverly place the sand figures onto the game board. However, you have to think carefully about where you build: because the waves wash everything away that is too close to the water! The game ends as soon as all spaces have been covered by sand figures or waves. Now you have to count up, who has the most sand figures on the game board. The player with the most is the winner.

Buongiorno, Signorina is an Italian style reaction game for 2 to 6 players aged 8 years and up. Shuffle the cards and deal them face down to each player, so that each player has approximately the same number of cards. Don’t look at your cards, but place them face down in a deck in front of you. Then you continue in a clockwise direction. When it’s your turn, draw the top card from your deck. Reveal it by turning it away from you, so that you are not the first one to see it. That way, no one can accuse you of cheating! Place it in the middle of the table. Then each take it in turns to place your card on top of the card previously laid. Continue doing this until a card demands an action. Tip: the faster you turn over your cards, one after the other, the more fun it becomes.

Which cards demand which actions?

Pasta: 
As long as pasta appears, nothing happens. The sort makes no difference whatsoever. The neighbour on your left plays the next card.

Re: As soon as one of the four Kings turns up, salute him very quickly. Move your flat hand to your forehead and touch it with your fingertips. It makes no difference whether you use your right or left hand.

Signorina:
As soon as one of the four Ladies appears, quickly call out “Buongiorno, Signorina” (pronounced Buonchorno Sinnjorina).

Mandriano:
As soon as one of the four Cowherds appears, quickly blow a kiss. To do this, simulate a kiss on your fingertips and symbolically blow it away.

Cavallo:
As soon as one of the four Horses turns up, like lightning, bang your flat hand down on the card(s) in the middle of the table.

This is how the game ends:
The game ends as soon as one of you has no more cards left. Have you laid your last card in the middle of the table? Then you’ve almost won. But Attenzione: if instead of showing pasta, it demands an action, then you must carry this out quickly and correctly. Therefore, make an effort so that you don’t have to take all the cards from the middle of the table. Did everything go well? Complimenti – you have won!

Bibelbingo & Bibelmemory has two games in the box. Bibelbingo is a variation of bingo, but with bible characters, where every player gets one of the 25 bingo cards. Every card having 16 characters from the bible on them. One person draws one of the 36 characters that are in the game, explain who that is, and then all players puts a marker on that character if they have in on their own bingo card. The goal is to get a line of markers on the card. The second game in the box, is memory (Concentration), with a bible theme.

boardgame: One Shot Away: Operation Lam Son 719
description:
Operation Lam Son 719 was the first major offensive waged by the Army of the Republic of South Vietnam (ARVN) outside of its borders. Supported by US airpower, the offensive sought to destroy the Communist supply dumps along the Ho Chi Minh trail in Cambodia and Laos, as well as disrupt any offensive by the forces of the North Vietnamese Army (NVA) and its People’s Army of Vietnam (PAVN), better known in the USA as the Vietcong. The offensive was also the first large scale test of President Nixon’s policy of “Vietnamization” in which the bulk of the war effort was to be borne by the ARVN as the US forces were withdrawn home.

The Communist high command was well aware an offensive was in the works, and planned a counter-offensive. What ensued was one of the largest battles of the war that resulted in a very costly and controversial outcome. Both sides claimed victory but in the end the overall course of the war was little changed and America’s resolve to exit the conflict only deepened.

Operation Lam Son is published by High Flying Dice Games. It is designed by Paul Rohrbaugh, with graphics by Bruce Yearian. Players use a standard deck of playing cards to activate units and resolve most combats.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Standard Deck Activation System (High Flying Dice Games)

description:
From the box:
"The Pound Puppies are on the loose trying to avoid the dogcatcher and a stretch in the city pound.  As Pound Puppies are collected the cards are inserted into holders which are specially designed for small hands to grasp.  The player who is the first to collect all the puppies pictured on his/her card holder will be a doggone hero and winner of the game."

Players start with 4 dog cards in their holder. The remaining cards are shuffled and placed in the center of the table.  One card is flipped over next to the deck to start the discard pile.  Players can choose a card from the discard pile or the deck to add to their holder. They must exchange this new card with one in their holder. If the dogcatcher card is drawn the player must discard all of their cards and draw new ones. The first player to match all the dogs on their holder with drawn cards wins.

Promo item from FairPlay No. 111: 
Whenever you build a trading station in a town situated on a river, you may buy 1 wine for 1 coin.

Later included in the Orléans: Invasion expansion.

boardgame: Oh Snap! A 'Blank Cards With Text On Them' Game of Insults
description: Oh Snap! is a game for three or more players in which the players have a hand of cards. The goal is to create the best insults and retorts that include the phrases on the cards in your hand. Two of these players put down the cards they want, and the rest of the players vote on which one they like the best. The first player to reach an arbitrary number of points is the winner.

The origins of Skystones go way back to the days of the Benevolent Ancients. While experimenting with early Portal technology and magic, they discovered a form of enchanted stone that would allow anything painted on it to become real. The Ancients worried that this power might fall into the wrong hands so they turned it into a strategy game purely for fun and leisure.

Collect all 4 SKYSTONE sets. 40 cards in all!

Four different skystone packs could be found inside marked boxes of General Mills cereal during the month of March 2015, and are used to play Skystones using the game board found on the back of the box.

GAMEPLAY:

Gameplay is identical to the mini-game within the Skylanders series of video games.

The game is played on a 3x3 grid with skystone that are red on one side and blue on the other. The object is to have more of your color skystones on the grid than your opponent.

Each player starts with 5 skystone cards dealt from a shuffled deck, and players take turns placing one of their cards with their color face up into any open space on the board. When a player places their skystone next to one or more of an opponent's skystones a battle takes place. If the attacking player wins, the defending player must flip their skystone so that it now shows the attacking player's color. Play continues until all 9 spaces are filled. The player with the most skystones of their color is the victor.

Barricado is an abstract strategy game of tile placement and area control. Each turn, players get to choose between controlling the layout of the board (by placing a tile) or claiming territory (by placing lines of tokens). The player's goal is to connect dots of their own color with a line of tokens to block other players from doing the same and to end up with the most pieces on the board at the end of the game.

The layout of the board and the barricades built upon it is always different on each play-through, as each player's strategy (and the number of players) affects the others. Optional rules can adjust play time for different audiences, as well as provide for expanded strategy for advanced players.

A grid of tiles are placed face-down with four stations in the corners.  Players in turn take a face-down tile into their hand and choose to play it or a reserve track tile face-up in the position just vacated.  The tile can be rotated in any direction they choose.  Each player holds a secret hand of routes from station to station.  When their routes have been completed, they are the winner.

A light tile placement game for fans of Ticket to Ride and Tsuro. Simple enough for children 5 and up.

Similar in mechanics to Indigo.

Gather some beer, snacks, and your closest friends for Dinner Murder Party, a remix on Werewolf-Mafia like party games.

You play as one of a group of celebrity Dinner Guests gathered at an old mansion located in the Hollywood Hills or as a Murderer hidden in their midst.

As a Dinner Guest, you will be tasked with the singular purpose of bringing about the end of the world through evil, Illuminati-like, sacrificial rituals. That is unless the heroes, err the Murderers, kill all the celebrities at the party. But be careful if you’re a murderer because the celebrities will be more than happy to feast on you as the main course.

This game is great for seven or more players. An odd number of players is best, although not mandatory. Use the handy table below to figure out which roles you should be using based on the number of players you are playing the game with.

All the paranoid 60s spy fun of [redacted], now in the open air, and for up to 7 players!

In [redacted]: Garden Party, the action moves outside. The added board allows for more space, enough for a 7th player to enter the embassy. The added locations also allow for even more cool actions and feints to be done, in addition to more ways to reach critical parts of the building.

The added space to maneuver, additional missions, and new ways to escape with the intel actually make the game even faster by using the expansion. For example, using the Garbage Drop allows for an agent to look at discarded items and grab the gear necessary to escape a sticky situation.

50 States and the District of Columbia make up our Electoral College, the goal of the game is to win states and get the most votes. Each player will be dealt a total of three candidate cards--these will include 24 of our nations most influential Presidents throughout history. From those cards you will choose one candidate and that will be yours for the remainder of the game. Not all candidates will be able to gain states as easily as others, the 'Political Cause' cards can help get a candidate Electoral Votes that they normally could not get just on merit alone.
Throughout your turns, you will gain Political Allies and use their Historical Influence to gain States (each worth different Electoral Votes) and to assist in becoming the next President. You will have to come up with the best strategy to ensure you get the most votes to beat your opponents.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A BOARD GAME CREATED FOR SOCCER AND GAME FANS ALIKE!

Mini Copa is a soccer board game where you can play with the athletes behaviour and skills in a realistic and simple way, Each new play benefits from your good technical eye and an athletes’ sense of opportunity.

The mechanics of the game is broad enough to represent anything that can happen in a live soccer match. Each player, in alternating rounds, can use different actions with his team in an agile manner, while deciding to focus on the technical, physical or psychological aspects of the sport. These choices allow the development of teams with very varied personalities.

The board game will begin in a more basic form, with four national teams and rules to play endlessly unique matches. But, with the help of additional goals, we can unlock and release the game’s full fledged potential by means of the campaign mode, in which the rules for managing the entire technical and administrative team, including trainings, casting athletes, managing the teams resources, fully customizable teams and even stadiums construction will be out allowing a hugely expanded strategical and tactical options in the best and definitive soccer game ever made!

NOTE: WE ARE CURRENTLY UNDER A CROWDFUNDING CAMPAIGN ON: https://www.sibite.com.br/ViewCampaign.php?cid=63

Check out the project in english and english subtitles as well! Don't forget to invest and share!

On The Run is a strategic turn-based card game about bank robberies, tampering with evidence, and backstabbing your friends.

You and your crew have robbed a bank and have split the money evenly. Unfortunately, for you and your thieving friends, you've left behind evidence at the scene of the crime.

Each player will take turns trying to remove the evidence, place additional evidence to link other players to the crime, and attempt to steal more money.  Win the game by collecting 12 coins and starting and ending your turn out of jail.

In Dirty Detectives, everyone has been assigned to solve the same murder, but solving murders is hard work, so instead each detective is going to try to frame someone for murder. Each time a player touches a piece of evidence, they learn what it is, but unfortunately now that evidence points to their involvement in the crime!

The games uses 17 cards, 16 of which are laid out in a 4x4 grid with the 17th card in the active player's hand. Each card has an arrow on the back of it, and during play these arrows point at the players.

On a turn, you have the one extra card in hand and pick up any card from the board. You choose one of these cards and place it face-down in the empty space with the arrow pointing to you; to end your turn, pass the card still in hand to your left-hand opponent, who now takes their turn.

On your turn, you can also make an accusation. To do this, name a weapon and a motive, then pick up two cards pointing to the same opponent and look at them. If these cards match what you said, reveal these cards to everyone because you've just won the game; if you're wrong, however, place these cards facedown once again, but now pointing at yourself.

Each card also has a special reveal benefit. When you place a card facedown on the board pointing at yourself, normally only you know what that card is; if you reveal the card, everyone now knows what it is, but you get to do the reveal benefit on that card, e.g., looking at cards without pointing them at yourself or rotating cards to point at opponents.

Treasure Map uses 16 unique double-sided cards.  One side of the cards is used to layout the island.  There is treasure hidden somewhere on this island. Then players use the other side (the map-side) of the card to play one of the several different ways to play.

Game Mode: Treasure Island
One every space of the island without an icon, place a coin.  The each player chooses a space with an icon and places their pawn on it.

Then players take turns playing cards, and choosing on of the available actions from the card.

    Move to the icon - move your pawn to the icon on the card, if this cause your pawn to move in a straight line then pick up any coins passed along the way
    Move steps - move your pawn one space for each set of footprints on the card, picking up any coins passed along the way
    Pick up via strokes - for each stroke on the card, pick up one coin from an adjacent space


First one to 12 coins wins.

Game Mode: Gentlemen Pirates
Each player takes one card and looks at the icon on the map-side of the card.  This is where they treasure is hidden.

Players then asking yes or no questions to try to locate each others' hidden treasure.

Example questions:
Is you treasure hidden next to a red icon?
Is your treasure hidden on the coast of the island?
Is you treasure hidden by a palm tree or a lighthouse?

Players take turns asking a question, the first one to figure out where their opponent's treasure is hidden wins!

Unstable Isotope is a micro party game. Each player gets a subset of the cards that represent an unstable isotope that is about to become stable by kicking something out of it.

If you are going to get ejected out of the isotope, you can save yourself by correcting finding a different subatomic particle to eject in someone else's hand.

If you get ejected, you are out until the next round.

Knocking someone out scores you subatomic particles. The first one to collect a whole set wins!

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Hood Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for Month 1 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, the Q Continuum.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – I.K.S. Korinar Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for Month 2 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, the Q Continuum.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – I.R.W. Terix Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for Month 3 of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Organized Play tournament, the Q Continuum.

Bedroom Battle is a game for couples where you play against each other to win rewards in the form of sexy favors.

The concept is very simple. Each game round you use battle cards to compete for a reward card containing three different sexy challenges. The winner of the round then gets to select one of the sexy challenges for you both to act out. Once you have completed the challenge you move on to the next round and start playing for a new reward card. To make things interesting the rewards will become more and more naughty until the game ends with the final reward - which of course is the naughtiest of them all.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Evasive Action Resource
description:
The Evasive Maneuver Action Template is a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 1, "Encounter at Farpoint," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, Q Continuum. It is considered a Resource, and costs 5 Squadron Points.

Each round, one of your ships may use the Evasive Action Maneuver instead of a normal maneuver by selecting a red or come about maneuver on its dial.  You perform the maneuver using the special template.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Damage Control Teams Resource
description: Damage Control Teams are a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 2, "Deja Q," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, Q Continuum. It is considered a Resource.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Ready Room Resource
description:
Ready Room Cards are a participation prize given to players who participated in Month 3, "All Good Things," of the Star Trek: Attack Wing Storyline Organized Play Event, Q Continuum. It is considered a Resource and costs 5 squadron points.

When using the resource, you begin the game with 4 Mission Tokens on your Ready Room Card.

Once per round, one of your ships may spend 1 of these Mission Tokens (i.e. remove it from the game) to achieve the following effects:

1) During the Activation Phase, when you reveal your maneuver, before you move, you may add up to +2 to your speed.

2) At the start of the Combat Phase, increase your Captain Skill Number by +2 until the End Phase.

3) At any time you may replace 1 [EVADE], [SCAN], or [BATTLE STATIONS] Token that is beside your ship with 1 [EVADE], [SCAN], or [BATTLE STATIONS] Token.

OR

4) Prevent your Captain from being disabled or discarded by an enemy ship's Captain or Upgrade Card.

A player cannot use these abilities for a ship that has no Captain Card or whose Captain Card is disabled.

From Varese to Tre Ponti is an expansion to San Fermo with two more battles of the same 1859 Garibaldi campaign in the Italian Second Independence War. It contains two battles, each with their own small map, rules to play the three events as a campaign, and just a few information counters usable for both games. With a VP-based bidding, players can change almost all of the battle conditions, making each game different from the ones before.

A curiosity: The counter sheet includes several nice and colorful counters for a future game (by the same team) dedicated to WW2 tactical combat.

boardgame: Castle Panic: The Dark Titan – Level 6 Agranok Promo
description: A special promotional card that lets you face off against a level 6 version of the Dark Titan Agranok when using The Dark Titan expansion. This version of Agranok has a 50% chance to make you banish a card, and a 50% chance to move two additional spaces.

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Die Jagd
description:
Die Jagd ("The Hunt") is a freely-downloadable solitaire legend released as chapter 3 of the "Adventure Andor" community forum game. This legend tells of the events back in Andor while most heroes began the journey to the north. It is designed specifically for the (also freely-downloadable) bonus hero Orfen or Marfa, the Wolf Warrior.

German description
“Die Jagd” ist eine neue Bonus-Legende für einen Spieler.
Diese Legende erzählt die Geschehnisse im Lande Andor, während die meisten Helden die Reise in den Norden antraten und ist speziell für den Bonus-Helden Orfen, der Wolfskrieger gestaltet.

Fluxx: International TableTop Day Expansion has ten cards for use with Fluxx: five new Keepers (Felicia Day, Wil Wheaton, Anne Wheaton, the Loser's Couch, and the Trophy of Awesomeness), four Goals that use these Keepers, and one action card called "Pound on the Table".

boardgame: A Game for Good Christians: Why We Can't Have Nice Things
description:
The Why We Can’t Have Nice Things Expansion Deck includes such biblical gems as:

Your father being your grandfather and uncle (Genesis 19:30-38)
Being liable for morons falling off your roof (Deuteronomy 22:8)
Loving God while not being a dick to everyone else (The whole sum of the Law, the Prophets, and the Gospel)

boardgame: A Game for Good Christians: Wisdom Decks
description:
The Wisdom Books Expansion Deck (with content taken from the books of Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and Songs of Solomon) with sage words like:
You: an arrow from your father’s quiver  (Psalm 127:4-5)
A virtuous woman as defined by old Jewish men (Proverbs 31)
Going down to the nut orchard (Song of Solomon 6:11)

A children's game in which the first player to collect all 7 bugs on his/her player board wins.

Game play:
The bug chits (ants, spiders, butterflies, bees, caterpillars, ladybugs) are put on six cardboard leaves in different shapes. Players take turns rolling the two dice. One die shows the leaf shapes, the other the bugs. The combination shows which bug the active player may take and put on his/her board. The empty spot is then filled with a new bug. If the combination from the roll doesn't exist, the player has to hope for better luck next round.

Monstrocards is a party card game in which players quickly create their own cards and pit them against other players’ cards in a series of duels. Because you don’t have much time to create your deck, what comes out is often weird, sometimes creepy, and usually hilarious. When two players pit their cards against each other, the players who aren’t dueling vote on which card wins.

Arcasam: Beat The Dragon is a strategy role-playing game for 2 - 5 players. In this game, where you can fight monsters as well as your opponents, and get a reward if you win the fight. At the end, when you are strong and equipped enough, you can dare to fight and beat the evil Dragon - which is menacing the world of Arcasam - before your opponents do. You will get better, stronger and more powerful each round, however your victory depends on the strategy you choose.

It's up to your heroes to cleanse Minas Tirith of this evil, but The Steward's Fear Nightmare Deck ensures that the conspiracies run deeper than ever, your foes are more cunning and deadly, and the fate of Gondor rests upon a razor's edge!

description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Bottrop, a city in Germany.

Welcome to Rogue Planet! A derelict world where you can experience true sci-fantasy gaming that allows you to fight skirmishes that focus on the cinematics and action of the genre without skimping on the tactics. It features:


    ROGUE Die Mechanic
    PAWN System
    FX Phyisics Engine
    Skill Based Combat
    ENERGY Expenditure System
    No labor intensive bookkeeping!
    No measuring!
    Custom point based force creation
    Equipment and Upgrades
    Sorcery and Psionics
    Multiple game levels
    Scenarios


-Play Aids

INNOVATIVE SYSTEMS
ROGUE DIE™ | The ROGUE DIE mechanic allows you to counteract your enemies’ actions during their turns and fuels your powered weapon, powered armor, and psionic force weapon abilities and effects. Counter-actions include things like: dodging, returning ranged fire, counter casting, and intercept maneuvers.

PAWN SYSTEM™ | The innovative PAWN system provides a unique mechanism for representing your leader’s retinue and followers and makes for dynamically changing battles as your hero loses abilities, power, and support during a game. PAWNS are also a prime feature of the campaign system and allow a leader to gain new abilities, change strategies, and evolve as a adventure progresses.

Pawns include: ammo grunts, bots, expendables, familiars, intimidators, and brutes [and many more!]. Who will follow you into battle?

FX | The FX Physics Engine™ powers the epic throws, collisions, and staggering blows on ROGUE PLANET. It also gives players the option to make the battlefield more interactive with the implementation of destructible, throwable, and treacherous terrain types. Want to throw an orc in powered armor halfway across the battlefield into a group of goblins and see what survives? FX makes it happen!

ENERGY | The ENERGY expenditure system allows you to simulate the strength of your force, powers the various armor ratings of your troops, and gives you the option to keep your units in play when you want or need to- but there’s always a cost!

STREAMLINED GAMEPLAY
GOT SKILLS? | Actions on ROGUE PLANET are a measure of skill. You’ll find no ‘buckets-of-dice’ or straight-line probabilities here. The interactive skill check system means that all players are constantly involved in the game, and you’ll need to work for the best possible tactical advantage or risk having your opponent counter you and take advantage of your failures.

NO BOOKKEEPING! | There are no hit point boxes, extensive tables, or detailed stat tracking on ROGUE PLANET™. Not only that, but there are no chits, tokens, spell, psionic, or effect markers littering the table either. The focus is always on the miniatures, terrain, and action on the table.

MEASUREMENT? | There are no measuring sticks, grids, rulers, centimeters, or inches used for the game. Measurements are based on the relative position of units, line-of-sight [LOS], terrain, and the in-game FX Physics Engine™.

CREATE WHAT YOU WANT
CUSTOM FORCE CREATION | Point based unit creation system gives you total control to bring your force to the tabletop in the way you envision it.

EQUIPMENT AND UPGRADES | There are over 30 different types of base weapons, armor ratings, and enhancements in the game that be used by themselves to equip your troops or combined with one another to create almost limitless supply of custom combi-weapons. These upgrades include: axes, blades, lances, blasters, heavy launchers, powered armor, powered fists, gravity hammers, chained blades, psionic force weapons, and grenades.

SORCERERS AND PSYCHERS | What sci-fantasy world would be complete without the inclusion of sorcerers and psychers. Heroes on ROGUE PLANET can be either [or both!] and wield powers like: psionic throw, anchor, time stop, magic missile, blink, deflection, and more. May the psionic force be with you space wizard- always.

PLAY HOW YOU WANT
GAME TYPES | There are multiple levels of play that allow players with various collection sizes and gameplay preferences to enjoy the game and have it grow with them. Patrols, Strikes, Missions, and Extended Ops ensure that there’s always a new way to experience adventures and combat on this derelict world. For example, during Extended Ops, your heroes may not only gain new followers but just might gain [or become] a nemesis.

SCENARIOS | The included scenarios range from simple battles to ambushes and there are also options for unique modes-of-play featuring ROGUE elements.

It's the Maine event! The Pedal Beach bicycling competition is here with trophies provided by Allspeed cycling and snow.

In this two player bicycle race, player "pedal" their bike by alternating between right and left hands in attempt to match goal cards in the center of the table. Try to acquire the most goal cards to earn a trophy at round end.

Players may choose alternate play methods such as taking turns in play,all out race with no turns, or even play in teams with one player per pedal.

Swerve to avoid bears and moose and be sure to celebrate a victory with lobstah! Have fun with this easy to learn card filler, where memory and speed will lead to victory.

boardgame: Zombie Army: The bloody card game
description:
The goal as the name suggests is to form the largest army of zombies as possible, attacking your enemies and "zombifying" them.

First, each player must pick a card in the package identified as HUMAN, this is an open card depicting their character to start the game.

Then, players are distributed a package of 7 ACTION cards each. These are the cards that will enable them to attack or defend themselves against their opponents.

In Zombie Army, the player that is attacked by a zombie is considered out after three strikes, same as Baseball.
So in order to make a human become a zombie: 
Zombie attack: Strike one
Neck, arm or leg bite: Strike two
Human infected: Strike tree, you're dead
 In turn, clockwise, players must attack their opponents using strategy and luck in order to "zombify" the characters of their opponents and take possession of them.

The winner is the player who has the most HUMAN cards in their army.

It is extremely simple to play based on the principle of action reaction, it is "addictive". It combines strategy, luck, scheming and friendship and it is easy to carry and play everywhere. No board game, no dice, no pieces, no calculation or complicated numbers, only cards.

Contains:

    Die California Extrakarte
    California: Bonuskarten Katalog Edition 7


6 cards on cardstock with rules

VERBA is the new language learning card game developed by The Pericles Group. The concept is incredibly simple -- one player (acting as a judge) draws a random card containing a fill in the blank sentence in the target language and the other players play a card containing a noun from their hand. The player who has played the sentence card then chooses the best card played and that player scores a point. Play continues with that player as the new judge.

All VERBA players benefit from seeing high frequency words, with many repetitions, in a context that fosters authentic learning and comprehensible input. As with all of The Pericles Group's educational games, the play objectives and the learning objectives are one in the same. Players win by making comprehensible sentences, scaffolded by the cards, the images, and the helpful hints included on the sentence cards. We always want to ensure that the learners are seeing correct forms and those forms are continually reinforced by the mechanics.

boardgame: The Chronicle of Onin: The Great Onin-Bunmei War
description:
Issue game of Wargame Japanese History #23.

Covering the most important civil war in Japan from 1467 to 1473.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ōnin_War
This war was the start of long and chaotic era, Sengoku jidai (戦国時代).

The game focuses the battle in Kyoto area. But, also covers the country areas.
The game uses the abstract country area battles and detailed "I go, you go" system Kyoto battle.

The game is mainly decided by the control of most important palace areas in Kyoto. But, determined by accumulated victory points at the end, if no side can achieve the sudden death victory.

Players try to get rid of their cards first by responding to hilarious subjects. Play a card, slap the timer, and pass to the next player...But be careful, a Switcheroo can happen at any time-changing up the subject-just when you had a great response in mind!

Cosplay Grab lets 2-4 players build the best costume wardrobe in five different genres of costume (fantasy, sci-fi-, steampunk, anime, and superhero). Build the best selection, collect the most in each genre, and stick your friends with some #epicfail pieces that look like they bought them on clearance at a drug store!

On a player's turn, they draft an item from the racks (card stacks) and based on what they select, another player gets a costume item, too. The trick is to select items that will get you a better take and help you build the best costumes. Players also score for having the most costume items in each genre.

Cosplay Grab is a quick-playing game with fun elements but a eurogame soul, borrowing the central mechanism from Zomboozle, another game from 3SidedCard.

boardgame: Setting Sun, Rising Sun: The Russo-Japanese War 1904-1905
description:
From the publisher's website:

"Setting Sun, Rising Sun: The Russo-Japanese War" is an uncomplicated wargame centered on the naval aspects of the Russo-Japanese War of 1904-1905. The game depicts this conflict at a strategic level, with most operational and tactical details represented by fast and easy-to-play systems, rather than intricate mechanics. The intent of the game is to provide a broad overview of the historical events while being fun to play. The object of the game for the Japanese player is to control the sea areas around Japan to prevent Russian combatants from bombarding and cutting off supplies to the island nation, and to destroy the naval forces of Russia. The object of the game for the Russian player is to disrupt Japanese sea control, retain sea control around the Russian and Manchurian coastline for as long as possible, and destroy Japanese naval forces. Both players must obtain their objectives by deploying their naval resources into the sea areas on the map, earning victory points at the end of each of six turns for areas under their control, and for opposing units destroyed.

Setting Sun, Rising Sun owes its original inspiration to War at Sea, designed by Jon Edwards and Don Greenwood and published by The Avalon Hill Game Company, a game which covered the war in the Atlantic in World War Two. Setting Sun, Rising Sun borrows a number of concepts from this earlier game as a starting point, such as area movement, individual capital ships, battle line resolutions, and rolling sixes to hit, and then diverges significantly to reflect the Russo-Japanese War situation. The game includes about 280 counters, a 22"x28" map sheet of the combat areas including charts and tables, and a set of rules to play the game.

description:
An expansion for Arcade by Néstor Romeral Andrés.

Situation: Tanks are powerful and rugged, but slow, and they must manoeuvre around obstacles.

Solution: Deploy the interceptors.

Interceptors are a new type of unit that can hover over tanks; they’re faster but have less endurance and firepower.

Sidewinder Checkers is a Checkers variant for play by two to four players. The game board is a 14x14 square checkerboard with the four corners of nine squares replaced with artwork. Each player has twelve checkers of a different color. Rules of movement are similar to other Checkers variants; the differences in Sidewinder Checkers are mainly to do with its emphasis on partnership play and different variations called "Strike," "Ruler of the Pit," and "Criss Cross," which have to do with where players' pieces may be promoted, and in winning conditions.

A devastating earthquake ravaged the island of The Raya Republic. The initial devastation crippled major cities and damaged most of the island's main infrastructure. The tsunami that followed caused thousands to lose their lives, including the government and most of the military. In the aftermath of the earthquake, the island is in a major state of emergency with no one to lead the people. The world is looking for someone to whom they can to send desperately needed resources. With such an opportunity, many have risen up to claim power. Will you rise to the challenge?

Aftermath: The Beginning is a game about gaining political influence by providing desperate provinces the resources they need. Players use their influence to acquire resources, use publicity to bolster their campaign or hinder their opponents, and make deals with each other to reach the final goal of becoming the next leader of the country.

Players customize the island at the beginning of each game, giving each game variance and loads of replay value. The objective of the game is to acquire the most population control through your influence. Every turn, an aid package - full of valuable resources - is sent to the island. Players bid for resources and spend them to gain influence in the different provinces. Each turn, each province has different resource needs. Each province has a population value. As players gain influence, they gain control of those provinces. The player at the end of the game with the most population control wins.

A card game very much like Foolish Questions, but with some differences:

The game contains one deck of cards with questions (Green) and one deck of answers (Blue). The Answer cards are dealt to all players equally and the questions are spread out on the table.

One player begins by selecting another player and draws a Question card from the pile and ask the question. The player selected then takes the top most answer card and read this.
Now (s)he pics a player, draws a card and reads the question

The game continues until there are no more Questions and Answer cards.
The one who first get rid of Answer Cards is the winner; if the game ends because the Question cards run out, the one with least Answer cards is the winner.

Possibly illustrated by Hans Quist (1922-1983).

The roundtrip starts and ends in East-Berlin. Movement ist determined by a die. If a player gets on a blue space, he has to use the detour. On a red space (city) the player misses a turn. The last player to arrive in East-Berlin, wins the game.

boardgame: Lords of War: Elves versus Lizardmen 2 – The Magic and Monsters Expansion
description:
The second in a series of sequel decks to the original, UK Games Expo Award Winning Lords of War games, this expansion adds a huge number of new Unit Types, new Ranks and new rules to the base game.

Alongside adding in new gameplay functions and mechanics, this expansion also introduces Moving Units to the Elves and Lizardmen Factions.

The first of the major new Unit Types included in this expansion are Magic Units, capable of buffing allies and damaging foes using Elemental Attacks and Spell Tokens.

The game introduces Monstrous Units - extremely tough cards with 'Monstrous Defence' which allows them to ignore low damage attacks.

Finally, the Magic and Monsters Expansion also introduce Flying Units which are only vulnerable to Ranged Units and other Flying Units.

While primarily designed as an addition to Lords of War: Elves versus Lizardmen, this expansion is also compatible with all other Lords of War Factions and adds additional Command Cards, Special Units and Elite Units to the base game.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Across the Ettenmoors
description:
From the publisher's website:

"This new country seemed threatening and unfriendly. As they went forward the hills about them steadily rose. Here and there upon heights and ridges they caught glimpses of ancient walls of stone, and the ruins of towers: they had an ominious look."
     –J.R.R. Tolkien, The Fellowship of the Ring

The scenario introduced in Across the Ettenmoors thrusts you squarely into the heart of the troll-fells, a stretch of land rumored to be riddled with Trolls. As your heroes travel deeper into this dangerous territory with the hordes of Mount Gram at their back, they know there's no path for them but forward. The hills rise, the rocky ground lies quiet under dark clouds and flashes of lightning, and your heroes press on. In this scenario, the oppressive silence is broken only intermittently by the heavy footfalls of towering, lumbering Trolls. To complete your journey through the Ettenmoors, you don't need to slay some fell rider or uncover the Dark Lord's hidden servants; you need only survive…

Fortunately, the sixty new cards in Across the Ettenmoors include more than its scenario; they also introduce a new Tactics hero, who lends versatility to your fellowship's defensive efforts, as well as three copies each of nine different player cards. These touch on several different themes simultaneously, and you'll find new Signals, Mounts, and Ents taking their place alongside new Valour effects and a Tactics side quest.

description:
Nocturn is a game that combines two genres — strategy and horror. It takes place in the underworld where players take control over one of the nations struggling to survive among the dead realm. Players establish Houses and grant them lands, obtaining more Lords for their wars and intrigues. Through these Houses they also call armies or build mighty cities and strongholds for their nations. Their goals are simple: expanding borders and finally subjugating each other. During the gameplay the fifth player will lead diffrent force. Death, the original sovereign of the realm. In an attempt to recapture his lands, now lost to the living, he will pose a major threat to all the other players.

Easy to play/Difficult to master

The game can be played using Short Campaign rules (for 3 players, game time ~150 min) or Long Campaign rules (for 4-5 players, game time ~180-240 min). The basics of Nocturn are simple to learn and a game can start even with only one person fmailiar with rulebook and other players having only general idea about it. Gameplay favors not the players who blindly memorize all the rules but rather those who can scheme and think outside the box.

The possibilities on how to play Nocturn are only up to player imagination. 
As a player you can shut your nation inside a massive well defended valley surrounded by the mountains, waiting for an outside world to degrade into chaos. You can play as an agressor, focusing on conquering other players cities and strongholds while building few yourself. Or if you want to scheme in darkness, submit early in the game to the neigbour and deceive stronger players while engaging in secret actions destabilizing the world everywhere but in your lands. Still, you have to remember the World is constantly evolving around you with each passing turn. It may happen that your well devised plan will crumble - your Court taken by other stronger player, forcing you to wonder around with few loyal troops left, selling your services to whoever would give you a glimpse of hope.

Nocturn is played for 10 turns. Each consists of three Phases:

1) Darkness Phase         
During this phase Rulers ressurect their dead Lords.
– Death plays Darkness Cards and Omen Cards (Long Campaign).

2) Action Phase     
During this phase players, one after another, execute actions using their Action Tokens.
Available actions for them are: 
-Moving Lords of the chosen House, 
-Building up to 2 new Fiefdoms, 
-Developing existining Fiefdom, 
-Collecting Tributes form Houses, 
-Calling Lords to arms, 
-Buying new Technologies.

3) Siege Phase
During this phase, players may assault besieged enemy strongholds or relieve allied ones.

boardgame: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic – Curse of the Werewolf Game Supplement
description:
Dark Gothic: Curse of the Werewolf Game Supplement is a 15-card expansion for the Dark Gothic deck-building game that introduces a new "Werewolf's Curse" Secret card, which is similar to the Dark Secret cards from the main game but acquired in new ways. You can contract the new curse from a totem or a deadly face-off with a lycanthrope.

This expansion consists of seven cards to shuffle into the main deck and eight werewolf's curse secrets.
family: Game: A Touch of Evil: Dark Gothic

description:
Designer Uwe Rosenberg has a talent for dreaming up new and inventive card games, and Mamma Mia! is a perfect example of this talent, with players placing pizza ingredients into a combined pool, then trying to time their pizza baking in order to make use of what's available in the kitchen.

Each player starts the game with eight different pizza orders to fill, with one of those order cards in hand; each player also starts with a hand of ingredient cards (showing pepperoni, mushroom, pineapple, green pepper or olive). On a turn, a player lays down one or more ingredient cards of a single type into a common stack, then optionally adds a pizza order card to that stack. He then refills his hand by drawing as many cards as he played from either the shared ingredient deck or his own order deck.

After the draw pile is exhausted, the shared pile of cards is flipped over and sorted by ingredients until an order comes up. If enough ingredient cards are available to fill that order, the owner scores it; if not, the player can make up the difference by playing cards from his hand (and thereby scoring the order) or else return the card to the bottom of his order deck.

Once all the orders are scored or returned, shuffle the ingredients used in pizzas and start another round of play. After three complete rounds, the player who's filled the most orders wins!

It includes a promotional expansion consisting of 10 double ingredient cards, two in each of the five ingredients.

During game set-up, the double ingredient cards are substituted into the deck in place of two ingredients of each type. For game play, a double ingredient is handled exactly the same as the single ingredients, but of course count as two ingredients of that kind.

If an ingredient is needed to complete an order, double ingredients on display must be used first. If necessary, a player must overpay, which also applies to double ingredients played from the hand to complete an order.

This expansion introduces the 'Collection' variant and includes 12 new cards and artwork.

COLLECTION VARIANT:

Each Collections set is in pairs.  At the beginning of a game, you shuffle the Collections deck and then deal out all of the cards evenly to each player.  You keep your collection secret from all other players.  At the end of a turn that you did NOT add cards to your Hoard you may 'Trade' with another player like so:

- Choose one of your own collection cards and set it aside.
- Choose a player and draw one of their collection cards at random, adding it to your collection.
- Give that player the collection card you set aside.

When The Crown is drawn to end the game everyone turns over all of their collection cards. For each pair that a player has they are awarded 3 additional points.

Expansion to Aftermath: The Beginning

Includes:

    16 new Publicity cards
    16 new Objective cards
    4 new Character cards
    80 Influence tokens (20 for each new character)

boardgame: Aftermath: The Beginning – Kickstarter Exclusive Characters
description: The Gambler and The Sailor character cards are exclusive for backers of the base game on Kickstarter. Each character comes with its own set of 20 influence tokens.

Michigan Rummy game, looks similar to Tripoley, but instead of 10-J-Q-K-A of Hearts (5 spots), 8-9-10 all one suit (one spot), Q-K of Hearts (one spot), and the Kitty, the spots are


    Jack of Clubs
    Queen of Diamonds
    King of Hearts
    Ace of Spades
    Joker
    Queen of Spades/Jack of Diamonds
    Sequence
    Ten of Diamonds/Two of Spades
    Poker



Game play:

Give each player a quantity of chips. Each player must then place one chip of the same denomination on each of the ten spaces on the Bonanza wheel. Deal the cards one at a time beginning at the dealer's left and dealing one extra hand as a "widow." Continue dealing until the pack is exhausted, regardless of the fact that some of the players will receive an extra card.

The players examine their cards. The dealer, without looking at the "widow," chooses either to discard his hand in exchange for it or to keep his hand and sell the "widow" to the highest bidder. If he announces that the "widow" is for sale, the other players, without looking at it, may bid for it if they choose, the bidding starting at the dealer's left and continuing around the table as many times as necessary to determine the highest bidder. The successful bidder gives the dealer the number of chips bid, discards his original hand, and picks up the "widow." If the dealer offers the "widow" for sale and no one bids, it is discarded.

The play of the hand falls into two parts, the first on the upper half of the wheei (Joker, Queen-Jack, Sequence, Ten-Two, and Poker) and the second on the lower half (Ace of Spades, King of Hearts, Queen of Diamonds, Jack of Clubs, and Center Section).

VALUE OF POKER HANDS

Poker hands are ranked as such:

1. Royal Straight Flush - Ace, King, Queen, Jack, Ten. All one suit.
2. Straight Flush - 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 (or any other sequence except Royal Flush) all one suit.
3. Four of a Kind.
4. Full House - Three of a kind and one pair.
5. Flush - All one suit, but not necessarily in any sequence.
6. STRAIGHT - 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 (or any other sequence) and cards of two or more different suits.
7. Three of a Kind.
8. Two Pairs.
9. One Pair.

A simple colour matching game with spinner-based play by Orchard Toys.

Each player takes a field board with six coloured spots on it.  Players then take it in turns to spin.  If a colour matching a spot on their board comes up, they take a sheep stand-up wearing that colour and place it on the appropriate spot on their board.

The player who fills their board with six sheep first is the winner.

There is a wildcard spot on the spinner, allowing players to choose the colour themselves.

From the back of the box:
"Can you be the first to collect all of your sheep?
Gather your flock in this fun colour matching game for young players!"

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from the Cantal region, in France.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Swamps of Jargono Artifacts #1 Game Supplement
description:
In the hot and humid swamps of Jargono, primitive bands of humans and savage Serpentmen build their civilizations on the ancient ruins of those who came before. Filled with ancient ceremonial relics and brutal Dark Stone-edged weapons, the jungle is home to many artifacts worth uncovering. This 15-card supplement adds a collection of all new Swamps of Jargono Artifacts for Shadows of Brimstone, themed around the primitive but brutal Dark Stone weapons used by the tribal humans and Serpentmen of Jargono as well as lost relics and medicines native to the murky swamps.

A collection of 15 Artifact cards for use with the Shadows of Brimstone: Swamps of Death core set. Roughly doubles the number of artifacts available for the Jargono Otherworld.


     Card #41 Bogbat Ichor ×2
     Card #42 Terri-Terri Leaf ×2
     Card #43 Dark Stone Club ×2
     Card #44 Sacrificial Dagger
     Card #45 Totem of De'nar
     Card #46 Hunting Net
     Card #47 Statue of a Serpent God
     Card #48 Stone Tablet
     Card #49 Swamp Parasite
     Card #50 Se'Parri Flute
     Card #51 Tribal Armor
     Card #52 Trophy Shield

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Targa Plateau Artifacts #1 Game Supplement
description:
Description from the publisher:

In the frozen over Targa Plateau, many relics of the past are buried in the ice… left dormant by the alien inhabitants of this once great city. These artifacts and technological wonders have been lost for eons, waiting to be discovered, and reactivated.

This 15-card supplement adds a collection of all new Targa Plateau Artifacts for Shadows of Brimstone, themed around ancient alien technology and the lost terrors preserved in the ice for millennia.

A collection of 15 Artifact cards for use with the Shadows of Brimstone: City of the Ancients core set. Roughly doubles the number of artifacts available for the Targa Plateau Otherworld.

     Card #35 Plasma Sword

     Card #40 Dark Orb

When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.
The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Groningen, in the Netherlands.
note:
big part of the profit was donated to charity foundation Beatrix children's hospital.

Get out Alive is a card game where you find yourself in an abandoned building with instructions saying only the first person to escape will live. Not remembering why or how you got there, you plan your escape.

When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Dead Man's Bounty Game Supplement
description:
Description from the publisher:

The deserts and mines near the ruins of Brimstone are harsh and unforgiving, made only more so by the cutthroat and backstabbing nature of the outlaws and gangs that reside there. Often times, one man’s treasure is another man’s dead body. This 15-card supplement adds a collection of all new Gear for Shadows of Brimstone, themed around items found on the bodies of outlaws and prospectors betrayed by their companions and left for dead.

15 new Gear cards for use with Shadows of Brimstone. Themes include the Dead Man's equipment, items useful for fighting undead, and new outlaw-specific gear. There's also a new mining lantern that lets you spend grit to reroll the "hold back the darkness" roll.

CONTENTS

     Card #5 Dead Man’s Boots ×2
     Card #6 Canteen ×2
     Card #7 Sawed-Off Shotgun ×2
     Card #8 Dead Man’s Vest

     Card #12 Gold Teeth

     Card #14 Outlaw’s Gun Belt
     Card #15 Jack of Hearts
     Card #16 Bandana


(Unlike all the other game supplements, this one's cover "card" does not list the contents on its back)

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Treasures of the Void Game Supplement
description:
Description from the publisher:

The vastness of the cosmos and the Void between space and time are home to treasures unimaginable to the Human mind.  Sometimes these magical forces are bound within amulets and rings by unknown intelligence with a mastery of sorcery too dark and terrible to contemplate, deposited into the real world to corrupt and ensnare lesser beings who would dare to use them.   This 15 card supplement adds a collection of all new Mine Artifacts for Shadows of Brimstone, themed around lost treasures and magical relics from the Void and other worlds beyond.

15 new Artifacts for use with Shadows of Brimstone. Includes a variety of enchanted jewelry, several items that do Corruption Hits to you in trade for some other benefit, a new Lantern that uses Peril Dice to Hold Back The Darkness, and two copies of an item that can heal Mutations or remove Parasites.

     Card #28 Tome of Ro’kal
