description:
The Field Generator is a single tile supplement for Neuroshima Hex! and it is one of the prizes which can be acquired only by participating in Polish Championships in Neuroshima Hex 3.0 ongoing in 2015.

Controllable Token - Until under control of another token, it doesn't belong to any faction and is treated by every player as enemy's token: it blocks line of fire, cannot be moved by an own Move tile, own Modules cannot be connected to it, wounds  every target within its range and vice versa, it can be pushed away, netted, taken over etc. If controllable token is connected to any unit of any faction (also HQ or Module), it immediately becomes a unit of this faction and is treated by it as its own. Controllable token is always connected with adjecent token, indicated by connection icon (identically as in the case of modules).

Enters play at the cost of 1 token from the draw - At the beginning of the game the token lies by the board and waits to be entered to the game by any player. To do it, player must additionaly discard one more token from his 3-token draw. Player then puts this token on any empty field on the board. After elimination of this token, it is again put next to the board and any player can again put it into play at the cost of 1 token from the draw.

Description of Field Generator
A Field Generator is a unit that operates continuously, irrespective of the initiative phase (similar to Nets).

Field - Field Generator affects every token within its line of fire (similar to Gauss Cannon). The effect of the Field is only Temporary Paralysis, working on a target as long as it is within Field Generator's line of fire. Temporary Paralysis deprives the target of possibility of any attack. The rest of the abilities of paralysed unit remains unchanged (Modules, Nets, Toughness, Armor etc. works normally). If the Field Generator is nobody's, then it affect all units of all players. If the Field Generator is under control of one player, then it only affects opponents units.

Rotation - When controlled by a player (see Controllable Token above), Filed Generator can be rotated in any direction by the controlling player once per his/her turn, including the turn when a controlling unit is placed adjacent to Filed Generator's connection icon.

Mini-expansion designed by Michał Oracz! Set of 6 cards - three for each player, compatible with both 2.5 and 3.0 editions.

Cards allow to change the situation on the board at critical moment, save the key unit and even keep the token for later! Huge opportunities, but remember - you can use each card only once, so you really need to think if now is the right moment...

BASE
On your turn, during drawing new tiles, you can put one of the drawn tiles on the Base card instead of mandatory discarding it. There can be only one tile lying on the Base card. During any of your following draws, you can draw this tile instead of one of the randomly drawn tiles, then you must remove the Base card from game.

MEDIC
At any time during the game, when any of your units receive wounds, you can use the Medic card. This unit is treated at this moment as if the Medic module was connected to it. After using, Medic card is removed from the game.

TACTIC
At any time during your turn, you can use Tactic card to choose one of the three following effects: Move, Battle, Push back. This card replaces corresponding instant tile. After using, Tactic card is removed from the game.

boardgame: Demonic Toys vs. Blood Dolls Game:  Battle of the Tiny Terrors
description:
Demonic Toys vs. Blood Dolls: Battle of the Tiny Terrors Game!

Play as your favorite Tiny Terror from the popular film franchise in this classic style board game of dice rolling melee battles!

Roll the die and move your character around the game board fighting the other player's Tiny Terror’s. Use special abilities on your character’s game card to perform range or melee attacks when in battles offensively or defensively attacks against them.

How the game works: Defeat the other player's Tiny Terror’s to win. Each player (in a standard game; two players) picks 4 characters to play as. In a 3+ player game, players only choose 3 characters. Players can also choose to do a Team Battle game; two vs. two. Players work as a team controlling 2 Tiny Terrors to defeat their opponent.

Game Objective: Players choose their Tiny Terrors and battle each other till one player has defeated all of other the other player’s characters!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Players build a four-column tower from blocks chosen based on rolling two dice.  They score points after each round depending on how many columns, two to four, in the tower have equal heights.  Scores are kept over multiple rounds, with the highest score, after a number of predetermined rounds, winning.

The BIA is a game of two teams. One team are the loyal agents with the yellow agent tokens and the other team is the double agent with the blue agent chip. Players are not allowed to ever reveal their agent chips to the other players unless they are killed.

During a player's turn, the player can choose one of the available action cards to perform. Once all action cards have been used they are flipped back over and can be used again.

Loyal agents are trying to defuse danger zones while double agents want to make them happen more.

The game ends when one of the following conditions are met:
1.) Three disasters have taken place.
2.) All danger zones are gone from the board.
3.) If an agent has been killed and there are no more agent chips left.

boardgame: ASL Comp: Afrikakorps – El Guettar Stakes
description: The twelfth and final portion of the North Africa collection is ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS: El Guettar Stakes and it brings those pretty green American bonus counters into play with actions that all take place in Tunisia. Almost all of the scenarios depict American troops in action (a couple depict British units vs. Germans) and once again the overlay collection is used to create one interesting battlefield after another in ways that just cannot be done with geomrphic boards. This set of 8 scenarios includes the namesake Battle of El Guettar, as well as action alongthe Gafsa-Gabes road and one of the final encounters of the War in North Africa, outside the gates of Tunis.

Garam Masala is a card game, in which every player try to enhance his farming and commerce systems in order to gain money and prestige. Buying new fields and hiring new workers, players have the opportunity to yield spices and sell them at the Indian people obtaining prestige points and the chance to recover the used Action cards. The Merchant can reclaim used action cards and he can also control the goods at opponents disposal, but in the meantime the opponents will try to steal the title of merchant.

boardgame: Lap Dance Kickstarter Stretch Rewards and 5th Player Expansion
description: During the Kickstarter several new Dancers and Staff members were unlocked. These were not included in the first print run and could be pledged for in the Kickstarter alone.

Welcome to the swashbuckling Caribbean, young buccaneer! It is a world of savage danger and merciless pirates. Take command of your own Flagship, arm it to the teeth with powerful cannons, and pile as many gold doubloons into your cargo hold as you can! The New world is yours for the taking, but beware there are other ambitious adventurers who would dare try and take what is rightfully yours. Forge your own destiny and make history on The Spanish Main!

In Cutthroats, you and up to five other players vie for control of key towns in the Spanish Main. Players utilize one of seven skills to attack each other's ships and towns.

Ships move by use of a 12 sided die. But players can only move in certain directions on the board, which can be changed after reaching a port. Players that land on another player's port must pay port fees to that player or try to conquer the town.

Combat takes place when a player passes another on the high seas and decides to attack. Each player rolls a 10 sided die and adds to that number, the number of cannon upgrades they have. Best two out of three wins the fight. Losers are forced to lose an upgrade, and pay a ransom to the victorious player as well as being sent to the nearest town.

Players are eliminated if they owe more doubloons than they have in their hold. Last player with money wins. A secondary way of winning is to conquer all five towns and hold them for a full round of play.

In this fast paced family card game, you must get your rats from the dirty bilges of the lower decks to the life rafts on the upper levels of the ship! Race the rising flood waters and other rats to the top. But beware! Your opponents will try anything to slow your progress and beat you out!

Rats! is all about friendly competitive play. You and your family will have hours of fun leading your rats to safety. Use well placed cheese and holes to stop and slow your opponents down before they do the same to you!

In Rats! players have three moves to move their rats from room to room. Cards players gain can increase the number of moves they have and also can be played on other players to slow them down. Rats can only go up a level when they find a ladder after exploring the doomed ship.

After each round of play, each player rolls a die to determine if they can flood the ship. Each flooded space is removed from play, along with any rats trapped inside.

To score points, players must escape the five levels of the ship to safety. The player with the highest score wins!

Little boy, Smolicek, is lost in magical forest.

Player rolls a die, lifts plastic cup of his/her choice from the gaming board and thereby uncovers hidden item. If uncovered item matches picture on dice, player keeps the item and discards the covering cup. If boy is found, player can keep the lost boy regardless of dice-rolled symbol. If item doesn't match the rolled picture, player discards item into box and keeps the covering cup.
When all cups are uncovered, players sum points scored for their items. Some items, such as poisonous mushrooms make you lose points rather than gain. Kept cups also count as negative points.
Player with most points wins the game.

boardgame: Commands & Colors: Napoleonics Expansion #5 – Generals, Marshals & Tacticians
description:
Generals, Marshals, Tacticians is the fifth expansion for GMT’s Commands & Colors: Napoleonics game system.

The Commands & Colors Napoleonics game system allows players to effectively portray epic Napoleonic battles, as well
as smaller historical actions. The scale of the game is flexible and varies from battle to battle. In some scenarios, an Infantry unit may represent an entire division, while in others a unit may represent a single regiment or battalion. During play, the Command cards drive movement, while creating a “fog of war”, and the battle dice resolve combat quickly and efficiently. 
The stylized battlefield maps emphasize the important terrain features and highlight the historical deployment of forces in scale with the game system. Finally, the Napoleonic tactics you will need to execute to gain victory conform remarkably well to the advantages and limitations inherent to the various Napoleonic National Armies of the day and the battlefield terrain features on which they fought.
Generals, Marshals & Tacticians is the fifth expansion for GMT’s Commands & Colors Napoleonics game system. There are 18
historical scenarios in this expansion. A number of the scenarios focus on the engagements of the French army of 1813 against the Russian, Austrian and Prussian armies around Leipzig.

The expansion also introduces a brand new deck of Tactician cards. The Tactician cards, along with the updated deck of Napoleonic Command cards, are designed to spice up each and every player’s Napoleonic experience by enhancing the role
of Leaders on the battlefield, without adding additional complexity or pages of rules. Overall, the Tactician cards add some exciting new command possibilities to the scenarios included in this expansion. Also included is a comprehensive listing of Commander tactical ratings for all Commands & Colors Napoleonics scenarios released to date. This listing will allow players to refight any previously released Napoleonic scenario using the
new Tactician card deck, the updated set of Command cards and these Generals, Marshals & Tacticians rules.

Welcome to some enhanced fun!
Richard Borg

Scenarios
Sahagun (cavalry action) - 21 December 1808 (British vs French)
Sorauren (French right early afternoon) - 28 July 1813 (British/Portuguese vs French)
Sorauren (French left) - 28 July 1813 (British/Portuguese vs French)
Göhrde - 18 September 1813 (Coalition Allies vs French)
Wartenburg - 3 October 1813 (Prussian vs French)
Dohna - 9 October 1813 (Russian vs French)
Leipzig - Liebertwolkwitz (morning) - 14 October 1813 (Russian/Prussian vs French)
Leipzig - Liebertwolkwitz (afternoon) - 14 October 1813 (Russian/Prussian/Austrian vs French)
Leipzig - Lindenau - 16 October 1813 (Austrian/Russian vs French)
Leipzig - Möckern (French right) - 16 October 1813 (Russian/Prussian vs French)
Leipzig - Möckern (French left) - 16 October 1813 (Prussian vs French)
Leipzig - Gohlis - 17 October 1813 (Prussian vs French & Allies)
Brienne - 29 January 1814 (Russian vs French)
Chateau-Thierry - 12 February 1814 (Prussian vs French)
Vauchamps - 14 February 1814 (Prussian/Russian vs French)
Orthez (French Right) - 27 February 1814 (British/Portuguese vs French)
Orthez (French Left) - 27 February 1814 (British/Portuguese vs French)
Hougoumont - 18 June 1815 (British/Allies vs French)
 (In order to play many of the scenarios in this expansion, the basic game or earlier expansions, you will need a copy of the Commands & Colors Napoleonic game and the Spanish, Russian, Austrian and Prussian expansions.)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
At the mouth of the river of the dead is the mighty city of Asphodel. It is known by many other names, some call it Purgatory some Stygia. Many lives that were cut short by violence or tragedy resume here fighting each other in a nightly battle to gain the strength to move on while fending off the attacks of rival factions. Asphodel is a strategic skirmish game set in the afterlands.

Factions of ghosts fight over locations to gain enough resolve to move on. These locations exist as shadows of places in the world of the living. In the afterlands locations may be adjacent to other places that are actually many miles apart in the living world. Set up -----Each player begins by either choosing or randomly drawing one of the available factions:

The Drowned, whose lives were cut short by tragedy at sea, or perhaps at the bottom of a well.

The Burned, victims of fires, radiation, and explosions. Their connection to the fire has lasted into the next world.

The Gremlins, tinkering souls who lost their lives to the machines they still love.

The Mystics whose pursuit of knowledge and power has become even greater on the shadowy side of the shroud.

And the Poltergeists whose violent brutal ends leave them raging, and lashing out across the asphodel meadows and possibly beyond.

Players take turns moving their Ghosts, who are represented by dice. Ghosts will be banished, and summoned back to Asphodel. Ghosts may find the resolve to transcend this purgatory, scoring points for the player or they may find themselves stuck in Asphodel, wandering the twilight city forever.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Collect as many "Golden Beans" and other material possessions as you can before the final auction ends.

Great fun for the whole family as you wheel and deal yourself into huge mansions, exotic cars, and other rarities that can only be achieved if you're "Not Your Average Joe"

boardgame: Roll for It! Deluxe Edition: Owlbear
description:
Roll for It! Deluxe Edition: Owlbear is a Promotional card produced and distributed during International Tabletop Day 2015

The Owlbear is a new target card that can be integrated in with the Roll for It! Deluxe Edition set.

boardgame: Android: Netrunner – The Universe of Tomorrow
description:
From the publisher's website:

Who's developing tomorrow's hottest technologies? What did you never know you needed, but will soon find utterly indispensible? At the Universe of Tomorrow expo, you'll find tens of thousands of brands on display, featuring everything from the trendiest new sportswear to the latest in cybernetics. Join us today for a peek at tomorrow. The future is now!

What do the world's largest and wealthiest megacorps have cooking in their R&D departments? You don't need to run to find out. All their latest projects are on display at The Universe of Tomorrow, the sixth and final Data Pack in the SanSan Cycle for Android: Netrunner. Its sixty cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) transport you to the expo's plazas and pavilions, where all the major players are eager to unveil their latest innovations. While Haas-Bioroid shows off its newest cybernetics at the Cybernetics Court and NBN pitches new products in Franchise City, you'll find Jinteki showing off its work at the Genetics Pavilion and the Weyland Consortium garnering public support for its newest initiatives at the Worlds Plaza.

Still, you'll find others who aren't swayed by the expo's optimism and shiny veneer. These include the game's Runners, who approach the expo and its technologies in their own fashions. In The Universe of Tomorrow, the game's Anarchs push to wrest control of the expo away from those who created it while Shapers view it as a place of wonder and an opportunity to learn more about themselves. Meanwhile, the game's Criminals see the expo as yet another opportunity to turn a profit.

family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
"Zombies are reflections of our fears and failures. Tame your inner zombie and you’ll achieve half the victory. The other half lies in rapid-fire weapons."

A man of action, Gabriel grew from a rank-and-file policeman to a respected anti-drug cop. Fighting zombies well before the real zombies came, he designed a very efficient intervention method to deal with drug-laced thugs in close-quarters. A zombie tide destroyed the society he was fighting for and soon made him a legend among survivors: a wise gunslinger willing to help anyone meeting him with a good cause to fight for.

This promotional Survivor is available as a prize for the map/scenario design contest sponsored by Guillotine Games, which can be found on their website. A zombie version (Zombivor) is also available.

boardgame: Cthulhu Madness: Midnight in the Graveyard
description:
From the publisher's website:

It's 1923 and you are in a graveyard. Bad things are about to happen. Real bad things... Hope you survive!

This is an intermediate level Engle Matrix game. This now comes as a story telling game. Players take turns moving character tokens on a map to set up scenes and create sections of the narrative. Each addition to the story is evaluated in terms of how likely it is to happen and how well it fits with the overall story. This evaluation determines the number that needs to be rolled for the new segment of the story "to happen" and to be added to the main narrative.
The player who contributed the most significant elements of the story is the winner of the game.

From the publisher's website:

This is the world's only American Civil War espionage game. It allows the players to spy for the south and see if they can influence the war. In the first scenario they try to steal the plans of the Monitor. In the next scenario it is 1863 and they get to try and influence the outcome of the Battle of Gettysburg. The last scenario is in 1865 and involves a fellow named John Wilkes Booth.

This is an Engle Matrix game of moderate complexity. Players take turns moving character tokens on a map to set up scenes and create sections of the narrative. Each addition to the story is evaluated in terms of how likely it is to happen and how well it fits with the overall story. This evaluation determines the number that needs to be rolled for the new segment of the story "to happen" and to be added to the main narrative.

The player who contributed the most significant elements of the story is the winner of the game.

Vegas: A Night on the Strip

This mini expansion was a GAMA Trade Show retailer exclusive. It comes with new missions and 2 copies each of 5 stickers that add an ability to a Runner card only during Vegas missions. It also adds 8 new cards to the game. Gambling and tourism are the major thematic elements of the game.

From publisher's website:

This contents five rubber Munchkin zipper pulls in a small plastic bag.

Official Rule: You may reroll a die once per game per zipper pull attached to a bag or item of clothing you are wearing. You can only reroll each roll once. You must actually zip (or unzip) that zipper to reroll the die, so please consider your zipper pull placement carefully!

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Dark Elements
description:
From the publisher's website:

Experience the battles and quests of Descent: Journeys in the Dark Second Edition in a new way with the Dark Elements expansion! This expansion, available via FFG’s in-house manufacturing, offers an entirely cooperative variant to Descent without replacing the main game. Up to four players take the roles of heroes, working to escape from a nightmarish dream world. A series of Exploration cards dictate which rooms and areas the heroes encounter, ensuring that you’ll never play the same game twice.

In the Dark Elements expansion, the overlord is replaced by an overlord track and a deck of monster activation cards, which control the monsters you encounter. Your heroes must battle through waves of monsters to escape your fate, but you still have a chance to gain experience and grab items along the way. Dark Elements simulates a mini campaign in one night, offering all the excitement and adventure of Descent in a fully cooperative environment.

Important: The Lair of the Wyrm expansion is required to play the Dark Elements cooperative adventure.
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

description:
It is a millisecond second after the Big Bang. Now is your chance to guide the creation of particles, fundamental forces, and the physical laws of the rapidly expanding universe and use them to your advantage. Through judicious planning and aggressive maneuvering, you will be able to guide the creation of stars, solar systems, and even galaxies.

God Plays Dice is a micro-style game of managing and manipulating probabilities. By collecting and rolling dice you can gain control of cards that have special powers that allow you to better collect and roll dice, and to score victory points. Sometimes you will be at the mercy of the dice, but sometimes the other players will be at the mercy of you.

A free, downloadable, print-and-play, mini-expansion for Eight-Minute Empire. It adds Mountain tokens and the Settlers and Builders cards.

Mountain tokens may be placed in regions to increase the difficulty of moving armies, but also provide increased control and victory points at game's end. The Settlers card will allow a player to pay an opponent for the use of that player's city, while the Builders card will provide a player an additional opportunity to build a city.

In first, every player receive a secret identity (one E.T. card and one Ship card). Players create a line of 4 cards to create the planet where we must land. Players receive 4 cards.On his turn a player must place a card above this planet. In the end of the game, each E.T. or Ship in a good landing position give one point for his owner! But be careful, some cards are "armed" and will eliminate all cards around with the same ship or the same E.T!

Simple war game of maneuver and combat. The flags represent army formations where Infantry and Artillery are assigned in secret to the armies. Terrain, and positioning of attacking and defending units modify the combat results. Dice are rolled to indicate combat results.  The  record pad is where each commander records strength and losses to his units.

The game maybe be played by 2 or 4 players and rules are included to split the commands and you can see in the pictures where each army's home camps are printed along the map edge.

Rules are about 6 pages digest size, a more involved game than the typical 60's kids game like 1863 or the MB Civil war.

Mitaines is a French Canadian game: the French word 'mitaines' means mittens. The game is also known in English as Mitts. Cards are played to a common discard pile, which can be captured by matching its top card or playing a jack. Points are scored for sets of two or more equal ranked cards played to the common discard pile: two equal cards are a mitaine (mitten), three cards are a gant (glove), and four cards are a chausson (sock).

Each turn, a player plays one card to the discard pile. If he plays a Jack or a card matching the top card of the discard pile, he captures the pile, which may be worth points, if it contains the ten of diamonds.

Alternatively, a player may call a mitten, glove or sock. If he does so, he plays the first card of his combination. The next card is played on his next turn, etc. When he completes his combination, he scores points for that combination. When a combination has been called, the discard pile cannot be cleared. However, other players can steal the combination by calling a higher combination (which is either a larger combination or a combination with higher cards). If such a player completes their combination, they score both their own combination and any combinations they have stolen.

Multiple hands are played until a player (or team) reaches 500 points; that player (or team) wins the game.

boardgame: The Front Nine: Conference Facility of Excellence, Promo Card
description:
Single card to be included as an additional special card to increase game length or swapped in following the expansion rules for specials.

Obtainable from the A-Muse-Ment stand at UK Games Expo 28th-31st May 2015.

boardgame: The Great Toilet War (トイレ大戦争)
description: In The Great Toilet War (トイレ大戦争), the player passengers are riding on a train and competing for the limited number of toilets available so that they can do their business before things get messy and they need to return home for a change of clothes.

REBUKE is a cooperative memory game where everyone that plays, will be rebuked! Help one another while fighting off Sin. Sin may not seem like anything severe at first, but watch as the Sins pile up in front of you. Forgiveness, Christian Character, and Recall will aid at discarding your Sins. To win, everyone must be without Sin at the end of the game. Don't hold onto your Sins in secret as the Hypocrites do. Accept the Rebuke and be made new.

WHAT IS A REBUKE AND WHY IS IT SO IMPORTANT?
When most hear this word they apply a negative connotation, however it is quite the opposite. To rebuke means to correct someone in a loving manner. God the Father gives us the 10 Commandments and His word as a rebuke.

Great to play with:
• Ministries
• Bible Studies
• Families
• Friends
• Churches
• Academy Classrooms
• Anti-Bullying School Programs
• Road Tripping
• Game Nights
• Camping

H Of Truce is a short, abstract game of tactical tile movement for two players. The object of the game is to capture the enemy's commander with one of your own soldiers. However your opponent is trying to achieve the same goal. And it seems like he's always one step ahead of you. Or is he?

In H Of Truce both players control six soldier pieces who move across an ever changing playing field and try to reach the enemies immobile commander piece via an "action inflation"-mechanic: Both players alternate turns, always taking one action more than their opponent had beforehand, until the game ends or one of the players chooses to reset the action point track back to one action, if he spots a way for his opponent to win with the number of actions he would get. This leads to short, dynamic games with almost no luck, but a lot of clever moves and surprises.

From the publisher's website:

The Skaven prepare to conquer the world above rising up from their vast Under-Empire. Dwarfs, the Empire of Men, and the ancients of Lustria find themselves overwhelmed by the tide of Vermin. How will it ever end?

Warhammer: Thanquol is the fourth instalment of the epic saga of The End Times. Following the story of the Skaven as they burst forth upon the Warhammer world, no hold is secure, and no continent is safe from their pestilence and technology. The Skaven’s story is described over two hardback books which are presented together in a sturdy slipcase.

Warhammer: Thanquol Book 1 is a 232-page book which details the story of the Skaven during the End Times, chronicling the return of the Verminlords, the rise of Grey Seer Thanquol and the alliance of the Children of the Horned Rat with Archaon, the Everchosen.

Warhammer: Thanquol Book 2 is a 64-page rulebook that contains 6 new scenarios inspired by the End Times story and special rules for jungle fighting in Lustria. It also includes background and rules for the full range of new Skaven miniatures, including five Verminlords: Verminlord Corruptor, Verminlord Warbringer, Verminlord Deceiver, Verminlord Warpseer, and Lord Skreech Verminking. As well as this, there are rules and background for the Stormfiends, and Thanquol on the latest incarnation of Boneripper.

From the publisher´s website:

The Old World lies in ruins, crushed beneath the numberless legions of Archaon the Everchosen and his ratmen allies. Though all seems lost, the embattled survivors cling on to a glimmer of hope that, should their courage hold, there may still be a chance to thwart the machinations of the Chaos Gods and avert the End Times.

Warhammer: Archaon is the fifth installment of the epic saga of The End Times and is described over two hardback books which are presented together in a sturdy slipcase.

Warhammer: Archaon Book 1 is a 256-page narrative book. Bear witness to the might of Archaon Everchosen and his hordes as they enter the battle that will decide the fate of the world.

Warhammer: Archaon Book 2 is an 80-page rules book that contains Armies of the End Times: new army selection rules to represent the desperate battles fought at the time that Archaon moves to crush the Warhammer world. There are also the Lords of Battle campaign rules and Halting the Apocalypse narrative campaign, 12 scenarios inspired by the narrative, and 9 Battlescrolls detailing armies and formations depicted in the story.

From the box:

It's exciting, it's challenging. It's Cubular! Join in the 4-player action where strategies are built and alliances are formed. Be the only player left standing as you out-maneuver and out-wit your opponents. Grow a legion of cubes by jumping your opponents to "flip" their color to your own, but be careful because it's 3 against 1. Cubular is a fast-paced strategy game that will have you fully engaged in every player's move. Developed within the classical style of board games, Cubular adds a fresh, original spin to the game-play. Cubular is also a great game for helping students develop strategic thinking and problem solving skills in the classroom. Included are directions for 4-player and 2-player games.

To become important in society players have to rise in political-social position from laborer to the Secretary General of the ruling political party. The player who reaches this position wins the game.

Each player has 5 pawns and 3 action cards.

The players select an action and reveals it at the same time. The starting player will perform his action and  in clockwise order the next players will.

Actions are:
Wahl (Voting): players role dice, winner moves up and takes a Wahl card.
Meuterei (Position challenge): players role dice, winners take higher position.
Beförderung oder Versetzung (Promotion or Relocation/Transfer): move a pawn up to higher position or place a pawn in another position on the same level.

When all positions in the center circle (Politburo) are filled, a vote for the highest function (Secretary General) can take place. The winner of that vote wins the game.

description:
In this game you compete for points by completing sets of 5 cards in a straight line on a 5 x 5 grid. Block your opponents by covering cards in play or rearranging the grid using special scrambler cards. Your score is multiplied by the variable score multiplier.

Game is very easy to learn and fun to play.

In Magus, each player represent a powerful wizard that want to take contol of a castle that belong to their master (great wizard Ardeth). To do so each mage takes control of a hero and send him to the castle to complete certain tasks that will allow him to prevail over the other acolytes...

Each player has in one hand his hero, and must lead him over the castle to complete quests. And in the other hand he has his spells, and must use it to benefit his hero or punish his opponents.

In Magus, each player plays as a hero and as a "Dungeon Master" at the same time. Is a strategy game with a touch of luck. The objective is to reach 10 points of victory by completing the goals of the castle.

description:
Grexit is a card driven competitive game in which up to 4 creditor countries (USA, Germany, Russia and China) vie for contol over the valuable resources of the debtor country (Greece) through economic pressure and by enforcing policies.

Each Creditor country has it's own special ability that affects it's balance in the game, while the Debtor country draws a "political party" card to determine what kind of government is in power (left, center or right, each with it's own special ability). All the players draw a hidden objective card which will give them their own personal agenda to fullfill. These agendas will most of the time lead to conflict of interest between the players. Objectives can be for example: to control the most of one kind of resource (eg. oil deposits, natural gas), to own public utilities or services of general interest (eg. Power, Water or Telecommunications) or to save the country (preventing the country from either falling apart in revolt or declaring bankruptcy) or ruining the country (to end the game before the 8th turn).

The game begins with each player drawing hidden objective cards
Each turn, an event card is drawn, which will give flavor to the game as well as affect the policies and actions taken during that turn. 
Then, the players controlling the creditor countries, draw cards from the "Memorandum" deck and choose which policies they'd like to have adopted by the debtor country. In order to entice the Debtor, they offer the policies face down with a bribe card face up. Bribes can be either money or relief measures which give victory points and breathing space respectively to the Debtor player.

As a general rule, in order for the creditor player to earn victory points, he needs to control properties and to fullfill their hidden objectives to win the game, while any debt owed to him by the debtor player by the end of the game will be lost. On the other hand, the debtor player earns victory points from the bribes accepted, from the properties that remain public, from the happiness of his people (if any) and from the level of the debt (if he hasn't declared bankruptcy).

boardgame: Flames of War: Bridge At Remagen – The Battle for the Rhine, February-April 1945
description: Bridge at Remagen covers the decisive battles in the Ruhr, Germany’s industrial heartland and centre-stage for the last major fights between US and German forces in the Second World War. American players are presented with US armoured forces, including tank, light tank, armoured rifle, and cavalry companies. German players can field lots of powerful, heavy tanks as well as the fanatical infantry forces of SS-Panzerbrigade Westfalen.

description:
Nordlandsreise is a simple "Roll & Move" game. Starting in Copenhagen, players attempt to be the first to complete a round trip (1-60) through Scandinavia.

Note: likely published end 1920's/early 30's.

The Great War is now just a distant memory in the minds of all but the eldest among us. Battles led to the splintering of the people living around The Shining City while one faction remained: the Hazimin. The Hazimin have done what they can to keep their still-glistening tower standing tall. However, though their monumental home in the center of the city is still impressive, their numbers are few. Even as new generations grow to power, the ruins of Dubai are at the heart of all conflicts among the factions of the wasteland. With the ruins still standing as a beacon of what once was — and a promise of what could be again — the new leaders of each faction (the Hazimin, the Bahriyyin, the Tayyarin, and the New Badu) have decided it is time to reclaim the city and become the leaders of Dubai.

Power over the city will be decided by the people, and only with that power can one faction establish their dominance. As your faction's leader, it is up to you to turn the ruins of the diamond of the desert into a new home for the people — but your control must outreach that of other factions to ensure that you rebuild the ruins in your image and rule the reformed city of Dubai!

Dubai: Rebuild the Ruins combines a unique thematic flavor with worker placement, tile-laying, and resource management elements in a game set in 2299 for 2-4 players. By using roundels for worker placement, players are constantly engaged in the game with little downtime as turn order is always changing. This allows for multiple player interactions as one player's turn often involves reactions from other players. The setting is additionally unique, but draws closely from the buildings of the modern day city of Dubai. In the end, the faction that has rebuilt the most of the city rules Dubai!

Be Legend: Ragnarok is a programing combat system based on legends, myths and other works of fiction and nonfiction. This  set is based on the Norse mythology and has some of the known favorites such as Odin, Loki and of course Thor. Also some other Legends such as Surt the fire giant, Freya the Enchantress and Fenir the Great Wolf. With a total of 8 playable and customizable characters to choose from.

Each player will have a deck basic abilities, which will be identical to the others. Each player, depending on who they picked to be their legend, will add two unique cards to their deck. Out of this deck you will pick only six to use during the game. So chose wisely. All players will use these cards to move around the board, capturing emblems from each player, first to do so wins.

How to move around the board each play will also have a deck of movement cards number 1-6 these are one time use. Also two direction cards that are reusable clockwise and counter clockwise.

During turns everyone at the same time will be playing one ability card, one movement card and a direction. Once all players have locked in their cards played them face down.Players will all reveal just the ability cards some cards are faster, some negate while some do really awesome things.

Once the cards are revealed plays in turn order or priority will move and activate their abilities. You can land on a person's realm and take their emblem. You can also land on them knocking them out and take choice of emblem or use unique ability cards to extend your range of attack or manipulate emblems or decks.

description: Verbal skills and quick wits are used in this exciting spelling game. The spinner determines the size of the words created. Players use their knowledge of vocabulary and quick thinking to make as many words on the game board before the timer runs out.

Platinum Series Baseball, or PSB, is a fast-paced, tabletop dice-and-card baseball strategy game that puts YOU in control of a fantasy style Big League franchise. PSB is an official licensee of the Major League Baseball Players Association.

As the manager of a big league franchise, you are in charge of building a team of all-stars.

Two managers compete in a realistic head-to-head baseball simulation, where the dice determine the outcome of each play. Managers need to use real baseball strategy to gain the advantage and earn the win.  Each game box will provide enough player cards to field 2 full teams. You can build the ultimate team by acquiring more player card packs.

Real player stats drive the outcome of each play, combined with the luck of a dice roll and clever strategies employed by each manager.  Strike out or hit a home run to clear the bases!  As you become a better manager, unlock more gameplay features online to become the ultimate manager.  Access stadiums, weather, and more complex calls that only an expert manager can make!

The stadium is filled and the fans are cheering for you to take the field as the manager of your own big league franchise.  Play ball!

1)  Build your team
2)  Roll to play offense and defense
3)  Use real baseball strategy to win

User summary:
モリヤミクス (Moriya Mikusu) is a Japanese card game based on the very popular fanfic game series Touhou Project (aka Project Shrine Maiden).

In this game, the objective is to simply get rid of all your cards. In your turn, you can play a card with a higher number than that of the previous player.

Furthermore, cards with the same number can be played together with their sum counted as their number, providing a speedy game experience!

If you play strong card(s) carelessly, another player might take over your strength and turn the tables, so play your hand wisely for victory.

Publisher's summary:

ゲームの目的は自分の手札を出し切ること。わかりやすい！

手札を出すのはとても簡単。前の数字より大きい数字なら出せます。

しかも、同じカードならまとめて合計値で出せるから展開がスピーディ！

ところが、迂闊に大きく出すと他の人に成果を乗っ取られて逆転されてしまうかもしれません。

手札の使い方を上手く計画して勝ち抜きを目指しましょう。

Bloodsport has reached its pinnacle. The Ophidian race tyrannically hunts, controls, and exploits the most powerful fighters in the universe. Arenas are packed to watch the spectacle, and worlds tune in to cheer for their teams and champions. Welcome to the arena, coach! Prepare your gladiators for battle.

Ophidian Wars is card-based combat game where you must draft and coach a team of 5 deadly gladiators. You get to choose your own team (from a set of 14), then equip them with a custom arsenal, and train them with maneuvers. The last team standing is the victor.

The game's combat/gameplay is governed by "the Flow" - every action you take in Ophidian Wars is either positive (+) or negative (-).  Positive actions allow to you retain control and continue making further actions, while a negative action transfer control (Flow) to your opponent.   Learning how to best manipulate the Flow while managing your team's field position and resources (Cheer and Adrenaline) is crucial to winning.

If you enjoy minis, all 14 beta set gladiators are available for 3D printing and painting, high detail resin, 54mm. Gladiator stats/abilities are directly on the pedestal, replacing their cards if you so choose.

The Ophidians are watching your every move. Get in the arena, pull up your pants, rally the crowds, and master the flow of combat.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This is a brand new concept for traditional card games. In this game, the cards are colorful particles and the table top is an active collider slamming them into each other for the betterment of science. Experimental patterns called Waveforms are placed into the collider in an attempt to break them down into readable samples. Depending on the strength of the collisions you might get the harmonization of a perfect sample, or the particles might cause an explosion, scattering random particles around the collider and possibly ending the experiment too early.

This game comes with rules for both solitaire and multiplayer games for up to four players. Both versions of the game play very similarly. At the beginning of the game you pick a Waveform, which is a pattern of face up and face down cards played to the table. At the beginning of a player's turn they play one card from their hand by placing it face up onto the table. Once the card is played, the collider engine begins to run.

The collider engine is a set of rules that cause the cards on the table to interact. The highest numbered card located at the bottom of the Waveform will search for the lowest numbered card it can interact with (based on suit). If it finds one, it will pull that lowest numbered card to itself and they will merge into a new number (by adding their numbers together), then one of four different reactions will occur based on that new number. Once that reaction is done, the collider engine begins searching for another possible interaction, and keeps doing so until there are none left, which ends the turn.

Players score points by collecting cards based on some of the possible reactions. In the solitaire game the object is to score well while trying to clear all of the cards from the Waveform. The multiplayer version is all about getting the highest score through multiple rounds of play.

King's Ransom Deluxe, from designer Frank Zazanis II, is a two-player strategy card game pitting royal rivals against each other. Using the basic mechanics of Chess, there's an additional layer of strategy due to each card entering play hidden from the opposing player's view.

On their turn, each player flips a face-down card so it is face up, and then moves any face-up card. He or she then announces check, if applicable. If no check is announced, then a card is placed face down, and a new card is drawn from the deck. If you can set traps and lure your opponent into making mistakes, you might be able to snatch their King and ransom him for control of the kingdom!

This deluxe version contains all the cards in the original King's Ransom game, plus an additional 10 new piece types, 110 new cards (for a total of 160), and instructions for a four-player variation.

boardgame: OUTPOST WAR: A Solitaire Game of Combat in the Hills of Korea (1951-1953).
description:
INTRODUCTION: OUTPOST WAR is a solitaire game of the battles for the Hills and Ridges along the Main Line of Resistance (MLR) during the later stages of the Korean War (1951-1953). The player assumes the role of a commander of an Infantry Company during its grueling and miserable turn manning a Hilltop Outpost before they are relieved. As the Company Commander, you are responsible for securing your Sector of Front by successfully taking enemy positions and defending yours with the least amount of casualties. Although there are many support units available to you, they may often times be limited to you by political, logistical or geographical obstacles. You have the choice to play as a US Marine, US Army, French Army, Foreign Legion or British Commonwealth Infantry Company.

GAME OBJECTIVE 
The objective of OUTPOST WAR is to successfully survive a Tour in the Hilltop Outposts along the MLR in Korea. A Tour consists of a number of Rotations manning an Outpost on a Ridge or Hill. The Player can play a Short Tour (1 Rotation), an Average Tour (2 Rotations) or a Long Tour (3 Rotations). Each Rotation consists of making a Rotation Deck of 20 “Outpost Life” Cards and once all Outpost Life Cards have been drawn and resolved, the Rotation is over and the Player totals his Rotation VP and adds it to his Tour VP. Once all Rotations have been completed, the Player totals his Tour VP and then compares it to the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success.

OUTPOST WAR can be found at the following URL:
http://www.berserkergames.net
SUPER ABDUCTION is a solitaire card game in which the alien player tries to use their UFOs to capture as many cattle as possible.

description: In 達人 -TATSUJIN-, each player has a hand of cards, which come in three colors and are numbered 1-8 in each color. Players take turns playing cards, and they use the color and position of the cards played in order to predict what else the opponent has in hand.

description: In ダイヤキング (Diamond King), players try to mine diamonds by placing black cards (mining facilities) and white cards (the mines themselves) into a grid. Each card is divided into four sections by diagonal lines that cross from one corner to another, and players can cover cards already in play with new cards in order to score.

A game of bluffing and deduction.

description:
Expansion 3 X Invasion of Gamera for Godzilla Rampage board game. Includes NEW and updated Kaiju cards, 2 NEW Scenarios and Special Ability Cards for Kaiju.
Free print and play just like base game.

Superfy is a fun-filled, fast-paced, action-packed card/dice table-top hybrid game of heroic proportions.

Become a hero (or villain), gain unique super powers, lay down some super moves and then roll it out to see who will emerge victorious and win the Event (first to 3 wins the game).

Simple to learn and quick to master, Superfy holds that perfect balance of strategy and luck to keep things interesting, but still give the underdog a fighting chance.

It's simplicity and super-powered theme lends itself well to family gaming - even teaching kids in a relaxed way both counting and assessing probabilities.

description:
Road to Madness is a print & play, solitaire statistical replay game on College Basketball, featuring individual yearbooks with data for all Division I teams and also for the entire NCAA Tournament brackets.

Each team is rated for offense and defense and the game is resolved comparing the opposing team values and rolling a couple of 6-sided dice on a Result table, with modifiers given by a random die modifier, that could be activated or not activated at all, according to real performance during the season or the tournament (a team with a winning record has more chance to activate the random modifier than a team with a losing record, but there is the possibility to the underdog to win a match).

As most of TCS Games sport design, the game privileges easiness and speed of play than deepness and strategy (is a solo game after all, and the player takes both sides, so he doesn't have to make any decision).

Two yearbooks available till now: 1977/78 and 1978/79

In Mingle & Murder, you are attending a dinner party where one guest after another is found dead. It is now a fight against the clock. Will the murderer get away or will the Inspector catch him or her? Will the guests be saved by the doctor or will they take the opportunity to enrich themselves? Whatever happens, this party will indeed be the talk of the high society!

Each player has a unique objective.


     The Murderer wants to murder two guests by playing them murder cards.
     The Inspector wants to arrest the murderer by being played a murder card and prevent items from being stolen by playing the guests catch cards.
     The Doctor wants to save the guests by playing them save cards or be played a murder card.
     Each Guest has a unique list of three rooms that they want to steal valuable items from.
     A murdered Guest may still win by identifying the murderer at the end of the game.


Each player starts with a secret hand of one object card and five action cards. Most action cards are mingle cards of no importance. However, the Murderer has three murder cards, the Inspector has three catch cards and the Doctor has three save cards. The game board consists of 3x3 room cards, on which the players move their meeples.

The players take turns to move between the rooms and leave action markers there (steal from it). They may also leave action markers with another player (steal from him or her). When a player has no action marker left, he or she leaves the game board. When all but one have left, the round ends. Players place and draw new cards where they have placed their action markers, shuffle and resolve them. The game ends when an object is fulfilled. A murdered player is eliminated but may still win by identifying the murderer.

Mingle & Murder has a player elimination mechanism with a unique twist: the eliminated player may avenge his or her death by identifying the winner (the murderer) and claim the victory!

description:
Daring Racers is a print & play, semi-statistical solitaire game on motor racing, with single yearbook dedicated to several different Formula 1 or NASCAR season.
Each Yearbook has data for all the drivers (or drivers that started at least two races or complete one in NASCAR),
Each driver is rated for qualifying ability and racing ability; there are also data for his/her propensity to retire (for mechanical reasons or for an accident).
Each race is resolved through a series of dice rolls where each driver gains or loses positions against a "0" point. Through this system the starting grid is frayed very soon and the race develops in a constant fight, made of passes and change of positions.

World Cup Alpine Ski is a print & play, solitaire, statistical replay game on Alpine Ski, featuring individual yearbook for several seasons of the World Cup.
The game permits to replay each event (downhill, Super G, Giant Slalom or Slalom) from that year, and has data for all skiers (both men and women) that got points during that year.
There are two methods of resolution: a very fast one, where for each race you roll three dice for each skier, add his/her racing value, and got a final classification for the race; and a medium complexity one, where you roll for each skier against the average time given for the race (based on historical results) and you get the final classification through the fastest to the slowest ones.
Until now the following Yearbooks have been released: from 1967 to 1988

From the publisher:
Fearless Gauls take part in a great expedition. They will travel through forests, seas and visit many Roman camps and even Egipt! To proof their accomplishment they must come back with Roman helmets.

In this game players race around the board formed of cards. In a turn a player has to swap a card which lays between him and the first opponent behind him. After swapping she moves to the first card in front of her which shows the same picture as the card just swapped. If a player passes the village she receives a Roman helmet.

The first player to stop on village with 5 Roman helmets or the first to gather 8 helmets is the winner.

This game is a Polish version of Ahoy. It's almost the same game, but one rule is added. Originally the goal of the game was to stop on the starting card with 5 tokens, while here it is also possible to win by gathering 8 of them.

This is a geographical card game based on a popular TV travel show. The aim of the game is to accumulate 10 golden travel cards over a series of rounds.

In each round, players compete to place numbers on each of the 7 regions: Asia, Europe, Far East, Africa, North American and South America. (only five are used in the two player game)

During a turn, a player with play a card, then draw a card (either from the discard pile, or the deck). Play continues until the deck runs out.

Note: There is an optional for playing with the trivia cards included in the game.

デザインタウン: 再利用 (Design Town: Reuse), an expansion for the deck-building game Design Town, includes two different double-sided cards, with 10 copies of each card. The cards are:


     Hardware store/urban redevelopment office: This interferes with the development of your opponent's cities.

description:
Detect. Deduce. Decode. A Battle of Wits over the Code Machine. A word game for the critical mind.

Da Vinci is a genius in coding messages creatively. In this game, you will stretch your imaginative and logical mind to crack the secret behind the Code Machine. Come unlock the genius within! The product is a beautiful fusion of a word game and a logic game. Its interactive game-play makes it a great piece for friends and family pastimes. The difficulty levels are controlled by players, so it is also a nice adult-child educational toy. Something special about this product is that it features a patented word coding method that is unique, but easy to understand. So while enjoying the fun and challenge, players can gain knowledge of vocabulary and coding.

Easy to learn. How to play ... 
Step 1: Draw two game cards to determine the "Word Type" and "Spelling Condition" of the secret word to be formed. Reveal the two cards to all players. 
Step 2: One player uses the chips to form a secret word by the method illustrated on the Code Machine. Cover it with all windows closed. 
Step 3: The other player(s) must crack the secret word with as few windows opened as possible. Ask questions wisely for hints in the process. The player that requires less windows opened is the winner.


In a two-player game, one player is the Encoder, the other the Decoder. The Encoder forms a secret word by the method illustrated on the Code Machine using the information from the "Word Type" and "Spelling Condition" cards, starting from the farthest left position, in correct spelling sequence, and announcing the number of letters in the word. In each round, the Decoder opens one small window at any position and attempts to name the secret word. In each game, the Decoder has a total of three changes to ask the Encoder a yes-or-no question for a hint. The questions can be used all at once or separately. When the secret word is cracked, count the number of windows opened. Switch roles and repeat. The player who required less open windows to decode the secret word is the winner.

In a three-player game, one player is the Encoder and the other two players are the competing Decoders. The Decoders take turns opening windows and attempting to crack the secret word. The Decoder who successfully cracks the secret word first is the winner.

Network Negotiator is a game about the television industry. Players represent high-powered executives who buy and sell TV programs. The game was developed as a promotional item for Turner Broadcasting.

The object of the game is the accumulation of television ratings points. After each player is given one million dollars and assigned a company (CNN, TBS, Headline News, etc.) an assortment of programs will be available for purchase.

The session proceeds for a previously agreed upon number of rounds as players negotiate and gather ratings points. An executive can choose a risky move with ratings based on the roll of the dice or accept "guaranteed" ratings with a safe choice. A special set of Monkey Wrench cards can be used to wreck another player's deal.

At the end of the game a Blockbuster Round is played with three unique programs (Election Night Coverage. etc.) are auctioned and ratings points are totaled. The player with the highest ratings is declared the winner.

The game includes a colorful board, play money, a score pad, 40 Program cards, 16 Monkey Wrench cards, 3 Blockbuster cards, dice, rules, and a storage insert.

Hagiga - a perfect way to learn more about Jewish holidays while playing with your kids in fun and entertaining way!

There are 72 perfect quality colorful cards with various concepts of Jewish Holidays.
The rule of the game is very simple - find 3 cards among 15 spread on the table which all together point to exactly one Jewish Holiday and shout the holiday name.

The winner is the one who collected maximum number of cards.

This is actually very educational game because while playing it you'll find yourself thinking and discussing with your kids various questions about Jewish holidays. For example:

     To which holidays King David is related?
     When Ecclesiastes is read?
     Is Fast related also to Passover and Rosh Hashanah?
     To which holidays is Sefirat HaOmer related?
     Are Shiv'at HaMinim related only to Tu Bishvat?


and more...

Skits-o-Frantic is a party game for people that like to make a fool of themselves.

In this game you will be divided up into teams and act out scenarios that can be politically incorrect, but that’s what makes it fun!

The game is simple. Each round, one person from a team draws three card and acts out the scenario without saying any of those words. The person's teammates then attempt to guess what’s on the three cards.

boardgame: 怪盗ルパン・キャット (Lupin Cat)
description: In 怪盗ルパン・キャット (Lupin Cat), players represent a dog detective team that's trying to track down everyone's favorite food, but hiding amongst them is Lupin Cat, whose only goal is to escape with their favorite food...

Thinktanku is the hilarious party game where players design, create, and brainstorm titles and descriptions for apps, movies, products, restaurants, and websites! The CEO reads the card aloud and asks the other players to create a description or title based on the drawn card.

Players have 1 minute to write their answers. The CEO chooses their favorite response after 1 minute. The winning player is awarded  a point. The first player to reach the set number of winning points wins the game!
In 大盗賊・猫右衛門 (NEGOEMON), players represent cats who are taking on various roles and you're trying to guess what those roles are in order to collect treasure.

Add new strategic options and speed up the start of gameplay with this mini-expansion for Zeppelin Attack! With Meddling Fools you'll have a different opportunity each turn to bend the rules of the game, reveal a hidden ally, and strike at your enemies.

The Meddling Fools deck sits alongside the others, with its top card turned up and offering a different option each turn. Gain extra battle points with the Mad Bomber, shift your resources to deal with Heroic Meddling, or launch additional attacks with Methuselah's Infiniplane.

The skies shall be yours!

Includes 8 cards for use in play, plus two rules cards for tableside reference — which, conveniently, hits the "minimum of 10 cards to place an order" requirement. :)

description:
User summary:

Dreamstarter is a simultaneous action selection investment card game.

Players have a hand of money cards numbered between 0 and 12. Each round, a venture card is drawn with a specific difficulty. Each player invests a hidden amount, which is then turned up, and dice rolled to see if the venture succeeds, with a higher probability if enough people invested. If the venture succeeds, each investor gains a certain amoung of VP. It takes one round for the used investment cards to return to the hand of the players, and even longer if the venture failed. Event cards throws in other unexpected factors in the game.

Most VP at the end wins.

All pirates of the Seven Seas are looking for the Treasure Island, where Barbarosa, the infamous pirate, hid his precious treasure! Are you ready for this incredible voyage? You'll need courage and a bit of luck!

L'Isola del Tesoro is a great adventure game with pirates, ships and treasures. It is a game for the whole family, easy to learn, with a fast gameplay and two bonuses for the bravest pirates.

L'Isola del Tesoro is a basic roll and move game, where you are trying to get from the Start tile to the Treasure Island, progressing through all the Sea tiles placed in a line. On your turn, you roll the six-sided die and move as many spaces as the result of the roll. You flip the tile where you land and apply any effect indicated (move forward one space, move to the tile where stands the last ship, skip your turn...). The first player to reach the Treasure Island wins the game.

The Monster tiles are used to block your progression. When you cross one, you must stop, flip it and stop your turn. On the following turn, you roll the die to try to beat the monster. Whoever beats the monster gets the tile, which doesn't give any advantage.

description:
Rondo  is a game for 2-4 Players, with a rotary disk on the game board.

The Rotary disk is transparent. Except the start and end fields the whole way with all fields are covered with this disk.
The path on the board is separated in 2 concentric circles, with the private starts at the outside and the private destinations in the middle.

The base rule is a simple, roll the die and move one of your four pawns in direction of your private destination.
The pawns have 2 sizes. If your pawn is larger or equal as the pawn before it, you can pass it with a high die number.
If your pawn is smaller than the pawns on your way you have to stay behind it.

Each pawn can throw out each opponents pawn if the pawn reaches exact the position of it. This is independent of their size.  
Some fields are marked. If a pawn hits one of the marked fields, the disk is turned with all pawns on it. The new position is reached, when the players pawn is on the next marked field.
There are only some single links between the circles and only 1 link from the inner circle to your Destination.

Therefore the rotation of all pawns may move them nearer to the destination or they have to run a full round again because they were moved on the wrong side of the link.

The Tabletop Edition has a variant box cover and includes the Felicia Day and Kodiak Colby promotional characters.

Game description from the publisher:

"Crossroads" is a new series from Plaid Hat Games that tests a group of survivors' ability to work together and stay alive while facing crises and challenges from both outside and inside.

Dead of Winter: A Crossroads Game, the first game in this series, puts 2-5 players in a small, weakened colony of survivors in a world where most of humanity is either dead or diseased, flesh-craving monsters. Each player leads a faction of survivors with dozens of different characters in the game.

Dead of Winter is a meta-cooperative psychological survival game. This means players are working together toward one common victory condition — but for each individual player to achieve victory, he must also complete his personal secret objective. This secret objective could relate to a psychological tick that's fairly harmless to most others in the colony, a dangerous obsession that could put the main objective at risk, a desire for sabotage of the main mission, or (worst of all) vengeance against the colony! Certain games could end with all players winning, some winning and some losing, or all players losing. Work toward the group's goal, but don't get walked all over by a loudmouth who's looking out only for his own interests!

Dead of Winter is an experience that can be accomplished only through the medium of tabletop games. It's a story-centric game about surviving through a harsh winter in an apocalyptic world. The survivors are all dealing with their own psychological imperatives, but must still find a way to work together to fight off outside threats, resolve crises, find food and supplies, and keep the colony's morale up.

Dead of Winter has players making frequent, difficult, heavily- thematic, wildly-varying decisions that often have them deciding between what is best for the colony and what is best for themselves.

description:
This game is based on the Disney movie Aladdin.

Each player has his own Aladdin figure in his color. The players try as fast as possible to get to the palace of the Sultan trying to get the most valuable treasures on their way.

The movement of the figures is card driven. Player plays 1 card from 2 each round and gets after the action is done a new card.

There are the following card types

    Aladdin cards: for moving 1 to 5 fields (exact)
    Carpet cards: moving to the next carpet field.
    Dschafar cards: 1 opponent player has to pause.


If the card played has the same color as the last on the trash pile. The player can play an additional card.

There are Special fields (so called wonder caves). If a figure passes it, he gets a treasure Card without looking on the value.
If he hits the field. The player is allowed to check the value and to decide if he wants this or a blind new one.

Each player has 3 different wishes, he is allowed to use in his turn (1 Opponent 6 fields back, all opponents back 1 to 3 fields, himself 4 fields in front) Maximum 1 wish/turn.

In the palace the player can choose 1 of 4 open treasures (value 1-4) if he is the first. Otherwise one of the remaining

Game Ends when all figures are in the palace. The player with the most valuable treasures gets Jasmin as his wife and wins the game. If 2 have equal treasures the first arrived player wins.

Match'n'Turn is a very special lotto!

You must match the images on the tiles with those on your board. It seems simple, but the objects to look for keep changing!

You have to look carefully to gather the most tiles.

This are 2 games in 1 box: Allegro & Zet

For both games: Each player has figures and stones which he moves by dice rolling.
There are possibilities to throw out figures or Stones.
Stones can block the movement of opponent's figures

Allegro:
Start field of the stones is in the middle of the plan. The figures start at a field at the boarder.
On Players turn he moves 1 figur or stone the sum of number of the 2 rolled dice. 
Stones can throw out other stones and other figures. They can cross over figures and stones.
Figures can throw out figures and stones but figures are not allowed to pass by stones. Figures are allowed to pass by other figures.   
The player whose figures are first on his stones start place and additional the Stones on the figures start place has won.

Zet:
All Stones and figures start at the same place. 2 dice are rolled. The Player chooses 2 Stones or 1 Stone and 1 figure and moves each for the number of 1 of the dice.
Stones are placed on other Stones if they reach exact the same place.
Stones are blocking Stones below them. Figures are also blocking stones below. Figures on stones are save (cannot be thrown out).
Figures throw out other figures if they reach the same place. 
Figures and stones are allowed to pass by other figures and stones.
Player whose figures and stones are first on the Destination field wins.

boardgame: TORNADO 1-2-3-Im Sausewind, wir sind gespannt, wer heut gewinnt
description:
Each Player has four figures and tries to get first all of the to the centre in the middle of the circle shaped game board.

The movement is dice driven.
If the 2 dice show a double the transparent disk covering the whole gameboard is rotated to the next mark on the board, before the a figure is moved.
All figures are turned around with this disk move.
Due to the fact that the 7 circular paths have gaps some figures may have after the turn a position outside the path.
These have to start at the start field at the boarder again.
Opponet figures can be thrown out by coming exact on their position.
Movement can be done in both directions.

A competitive deck-trashing game for 2-5 players by Florian Woo.

Each player plays a famous Mind Master. These people have telepathic abilities, which allow them to read the minds of others and exchange and extract memories from there.

Every year, the greatest of these Mind Masters meet in a battle for sanity. During these tournaments, they try to bring the most precious memories of each opponent into the foreground, so that they can access and delete these thoughts forever. Only the ones who keep these hidden until the end will stay sane.

Mind Masters is a competitive card game, where each player has an identical 40 cards deck at the beginning. Inside these decks, there a five "Precious Memory" cards, which each player has to protect. They will play cards forcing the others to discard or even trash cards, to finally remove all of their "Precious Memories". Therefore, the deck gets thinner and thinner over game play. The last player keeping at least one of his "Precious Memories" wins.

This game was created for the March 2015 24h design contest at BGG (theme: confusion).

Box includes 2 Games:
Glückspilz (2-4 players): Looks like Pachisi but with dice instead of pawns.
Master Mix (2 players): Player has to place dice on a chess board.

Each player has 45 stones of his Color.
On a player's turn, he rolls a special die. The die sides show the numbers 1-3, card symbol and star.
Rolling a number allows the player to place this number of Stones.
When rolling a card, the player takes a card and tries to fullfill it (e.g. build a special form with his stones)


     remove 1 Stone.


In most cases placing stones is only allowed if they are neighboard to each other and to previous placed of the same Color.
Game ends when no field is empty or if one Player has placed all his Stones. The Player who placed the most stones wins the game.
Cover description in 3 languages. The box contains only German rules.

boardgame: Dark Tales: Little Red Riding Hood
description:
Second expansion set for Dark Tales, this time based on the Little Red Riding Hood fairy tale.

This set includes:

     A deck of 26 cards:
         24 Playing cards
         2 Setting cards (B)
    
     10 items: Baskets
     33 victory point tokens with different values
     Rulesheet
