family: Series: War of Resistance (Formosa Force Games)

description: Simple roll-and-move-game. The first player to reach space 50 wins the game. On some spaces events occur, that hinder or benefit the player.

15 ○ 9 × is a two-player deduction game, with each player trying to deduce — and identify through card play — the pairs of cards laid down by the opponent.

The deck consists of 15 ○ cards and 9 × cards. Each player receives ten cards from the deck at random, then creates three pairs of cards, leaving the remaining four cards in hand. Each player now tries to correctly identify whether each pair of cards played by the opponent matches (in which case you need to play a ○ on the pair) or whether they don't match (in which case you need to play an ×).

Instead of making a guess, you can reveal one of the cards in one of your pairs, giving up information but delaying the need to guess.

The red bean extended game for Copa– the cups game.

With the red POWER BEANS, you can “spice up” the Copa bean choice, however you like! 
The extra beans can be used in all 4 game versions. They add some surprising effects to each set of game rules. And best of all: players using the beans are always at an advantage. Go ahead – choose your beans!

boardgame: Hoplomachus: Crowd Favor Gladiator Promo Chips
description:
These chips can be used in any of our Hoplomachus series games: The Lost Cities, Rise of Rome, and our new Origins game on Kickstarter.

In TLC and RoR, Crowd Favor Gladiators are randomly selected and await the crowds highest favor before agreeing to fight. The are the best gladiators in the game short of the Champions themselves.

These three chips are only given out at 2 conventions we attend, and only after you have played a full event with one of the game creators explaining the game! For an explanation of each gladiators skills, can be found at the Chip Theory Games website.


    In select packs, we've even swapped Duross with an extremely rare archer (very few in circulation) named: Aneres.

Welcome to the DICE Golf complete game. DICE Golf is more like two games in one and even comes complete with rules and charts for creating all of your own golfers and golf courses. DICE Golf can be played shot by shot using over 200 golfers from the 2008 season. You can also use the Quick Play results from each golfer card to play holes quickly and accurately. The game uses 2 10 sided dice.

A Poker Hybrid Strategy game that will leave your Aces Smoking!

Following a simple turn sequence, players will need to balance playing cards that advance scoring and cards that help or hinder in an ever changing game. Destroy, reclaim, draw, search and even mimic other cards to build the best hand of five while preventing others from doing the same. A "Dead Man's Hand" will have you eliminated if you don't pay close attention. The player that assembles the best five card Poker hand by the time all the cards have been played wins!

Deuce’s wild! Let the game begin!

DICE Basketball is a sports simulation game that recreates individual seasons using detailed formulas and actual statistics from real basketball seasons.
DICE Basketball uses 2 10-sided dice and can be played with 1 or 2 coaches. Each coach chooses a team, and the two decide who will be the home team and who will be the away team.

DICE Football uses 4 10-sided dice and can be played with 1 or 2 players. Each player chooses a team and the two players decide who will be the home team and who will be the away team. DICE Football combines team match ups and individual match ups to give you the authentic feel of real football. In DICE Football you will get to choose how much control over play calling you want or how little. DICE Football offers three unique different gaming experiences. You can jump right in with the basic version of the game, you can use all of the additional advance rules, or you can pick and choose which optional rules you would like to use.

In DICE Quick Play Hockey you can run full seasons in a very short amount of time and get accurate results.  You play entire games with the roll of two 10-sided dice. Each game only takes minutes and allows you to play an entire season in a matter of hours.

In DICE Quick Play Basketball you can run full seasons in a very short amount of time and get accurate results.  You play entire games with the roll of two 10-sided dice. Each game only takes minutes and allows you to play an entire season in a matter of hours.

Little Pig is a strategic, social game of bluffing, trickery, and double-think. The theme is brought to life through highly evocative artwork and amazing components inspired by the classic fairytale. The heart of the game is the Gather phase—you send your pig to collect resources (straw, wood, and brick) through hidden action selection. Players reveal their cards simultaneously: cheers erupt from some as they’ve outwitted their opponents while others watch their little pigs run for their lives. Fairy tale friends, treacherous wolves, and an interactive, dynamic game of deduction await you in the world of Little Pig.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: A variation of the standard Memory where you need to find Triplets instead of pairs. Each triplets represents an animal family.

boardgame: Recon Team: Vietnam – A Solitaire Game of Long Range Reconnaissance Operations in Vietnam (1967-1973).
description:
RECON TEAM: VIETNAM is a solitaire game of Long Range Reconnaissance Operations in Vietnam. The player assumes the role of a commander of a US Marine Force Recon, Long Range Reconnaissance Patrol (LRRP), Special Operations Group (SOG), or Ranger Long Rang Patrol Platoon in Vietnam. As commander, you are responsible for securing your Area of Operations (AOR) by successfully conducting Reconnaissance, Intelligence, Ambush and Raid operations, deep behind enemy lines, in order to locate and destroy the enemy. Although there are many units available to you, they may often times be limited to you by political, logistical or geographical obstacles.

GAME OBJECTIVE 
The objective of RECON TEAM is to successfully complete a number of missions to locate and destroy enemy forces in order to gain Victory Points (VP) for the US Forces. The player can choose to play a short game (4 Missions), an average game (6 Missions), a long game (8 Missions) or a full Tour (12 Missions). Once all Missions have been resolved (successfully or not) the Player totals his VP and compares this with the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success.

boardgame: Tote essen keine Kekse!: Ein mörderisches Rollenspiel für 6-11 Knabberfreunde
description:
The game plays in the cookie factory. It's a mixture of board game and role play.

The director of the factory died and it's not clear if it was a crime or not. The players try to find out, what happened and which scandals happened in the company.

Each player gets a character (manager, purchaser, Marketing assistent, ...), and instructions on how to play their character.

There are 2 parts:

    Moving a pawn around the plan of the factory by rolling a die to find objects.
    Interrogate the other players to get more information.


Both should lead the players as team or against each other to solve the situation.

Simple roll-and-move-game. The first player to reach space 70 wins the game. On certain spaces events occur that hinder or benefit the players.

The game was published in the 1950s.

boardgame: Sylvesterexpress: Ein mörderisches Rollenspiel für 6 bis 11 Bahnreisende
description:
The game plays in a Train - the Sylvester. It's a mixture of board game and role play.

Something strange or even a crime has happened in the train: e.g. someone has been murdered. The players try to find out, what happened and which person(s) have committed the crime.

Each player gets a character (Police officer, passengaers, ...), and instructions on how to play their character.

There are 2 parts:

    Moving a pawn around the 2 plans (Train, area around the Train)by rolling a die to find objects.
    Interrogate the other players to get more information.


Both should lead the players as team or against each other to solve the crime.

boardgame: Kimi To Boku No Maou Toubatsu!!
description:
User summary:
きみとぼくの魔王討伐!! (Kimi To Boku No Maou Toubatsu!!) is a Japanese game produced by the publishing circle Moshimoswitch. Kimi To Boku No Maou Toubatsu!! means something like "Demonlord-punishment by you and me together", so "Together we punish Demonlord" is close.

Unite your power and defeat monsters!
Two magic-fighters are to defend a kingdom against approaching monsters. Monsters are deadly, but they can be overcome by coordinated attacks. Defeat everything that comes to attack the kingdom!

This is a two-player cooperative game where players fight against monsters. Each player plays one action card to damage a monster or to heal themselves. A card itself has little effect, but a bonus effect is added when players play the same card. Players have to try to read each other's intention without saying which card they will be playing.

Players win when they defeat all of 10 monsters. Players lose if both players' HP are reduced to 0 or there are no action cards remaining in draw pile and players' hands.

Publisher's summary:
2人で協力してモンスターを討伐だ！
プレイヤーは王国にせまるモンスター軍団を倒すべく派遣された魔法戦士。
モンスター達は手強いが、2人で協力すればきっと勝てるだろう。
次々と襲いくるモンスターを全てやっつけよう！！

勝敗条件
勝利・・・モンスター10体を全て倒す！！
敗北・・・両プレイヤーのHPが0になる。
　　　　　
山札と手札のアクションカードがすべて無くなる。

The players search for gold, trying to get the most and the best claims (game board fields)
The game for 2 players or 4 playrer in 2 teams.
The game board has 7 x 5 square fields (western scenery back ground without game relevance)
On Players turn he places 1 of his fences on a line between the squares (or at the edge of one at the board border).
Each time all a fence is placed on all 4 edges of a field and the last placed fence has the same color as the fence on the opposit side, the player places one of his "Mini-Boys" (figure name in the game rule) in the field. Now it's his field.
At the end of the game (all fields have 4 fences) the player (or team wins) which has more points.
Some fields give 2 points, middle field 3, all others 1 point.

This is a Poker game, but instead of cards there is a die.

Each player has 5 or 6 figures (dependent of the number of players)
The game board has 2 concentic circles with the same number of fields and additional 4 x 2 places in the player Colors. 1 of them at each corner of the board (owned chips) and 1 in the middle (nearest possible to the same color in the corners (bets).

At the begin of the game each player gets the same amount of chips.
Each player has to place 1 of his figures on a empty field on the outer circle. The players have to do this clockwise till all figures are placed.

The youngest player has started this positioning and also starts the "real" game.

He chooses 1 of his figures, places it in the  neighboring field of the inner circle. This is the start figure. The next 6 figures have to bet clockwise 1 to 10 coins. The minimum bet is always the bet from the previous figure. After all bets are done the owner of the start figure rolls the die. He counts from his figure the number of the die and the owner of this figure gets the all bets and this figure is the start figure of the next round. Players which cannot pay the bet are out of the game. All their figures are taken off. The empty fields are counted for the result. If the die "hits" an empty field the money stays in the pot for the next round. The figure next clockWise is the next start figure.

Game ends when only 1 player is left or after a pregiven time. Player may decide to end the game earlier as planned, then the rules say a Minimum of 6 rounds have to be played.
Game winner is the one who has the most money.

A kind of Lotto game in which players collect cards that correspond to postal codes on their maps based on what city is called out by a caller. The first player to get cards for all the postal codes on his map wins the game.

The legend of the 8-bit battle goes as follows. In a distant kingdom lived a King who ruled over a mighty nation. But alas, the kingdom was torn in two as factions began to form against the King. Now the king must draw upon his closest allies else he will battle against them for the future of the kingdom. They are: The King, The Warrior, The Wizard, The Queen, The Goblin and The Villagers.

This game creates several unique strategies. Do you choose items or characters that you can stack up to ensure victory, but at the risk of letting your opponent have automatic victory points because you cannot engage in every battle? Or will you go with special characters like the wizard who have a high probability of victory on their own? Or will you use special items to gain the advantage by increasing your attributes or peeking at the other players cards before you have to make a decision. The choice is yours, but choose wisely, because every decision you make will lead to victory or your untimely demise.

Setup

The 8 character cards are shuffled and placed between the two players, face down. Each player draws two cards, then decides which of the two to keep and which to put back. Shuffle and repeat until there are only two character cards left.  These two cards are randomly distributed.

Repeat these steps for the item cards except stop once each player has three item cards.  Place the remaining item cards to the side as they will no longer be needed this round.

Game Play

The player who went first is now the attacker. They select one character card and one item card and place them face down.

The second player is now the defender. That player selects a character card face up and an item card face up. If a card allows additional cards to be played, it is played now.

The attacker then reveals their cards. If a card allows additional cards to be played, it is played now.

The battle: If the attacker's Attack value is higher than the defender's Defense value, the attacker wins. Else the defender wins. In the event of a tie, the defender wins.

Winner takes all of the cards and puts them in their discard pile. Once a player is out of characters to play with, the other player places the rest of their cards into their discard pile.

Players go through their discard pile and count up the Health Points (which are victory points). Player with the most points that round wins the round. Winner decides who goes first for the next round. Best of 3 rounds wins the game.

Catching a rat, summoning the fire department, and sneaking into someone’s house may not be your idea of a good time–and in the latter case may even constitute a felony...

unless you are playing Cat Capers! In this small board game you are a cat, your owner is away at work and the world is your oyster. Unfortunately, there are other cats trying to horn in on all the fun feline experiences your neighbourhood has to offer, but with careful allocation of your four paws and crafty use of special cards, you can be the one who emerges from this cat fight with the lion’s share of the points.

The game is played in six rounds. At the beginning of each, colour-coded activity cards worth 2-4 points are displayed at each house on the block. Players secretly allocate their four paws to houses and/or to their basket, which earns them extra cat cards at the end of the round. Certain cards are worth a bonus point to particular cats, which helps guide choices. Then players lift their screens and move their paws to the houses. Cat fights break out, as competing players use cat cards to try to outnumber or outmanoeuvre each other and win the point cards.

At the end of the game, the player with the most points is the winner.

A crowd-funding campaign for Cat Capers on the PledgeMe website met its target and ended on July 30, 2015. The game was released in November 2015.

description:
Roll the die and add or remove petals to your daisy base according to what you roll.

Roll a petal and add it to your disc and roll again.
Roll a + and attach a petal to your disc.
Roll a - and remove a petal from your disc.

First one to complete their daisy wins!

Face the challenge of planning and executing a Ranger patrol on the modern battlefield. Missions include squad reconnaissance or platoon ambush and raid patrols.

The classic solitaire wargame returns! Our first game and one of the biggest selling wargames of all time returns in an all new, revised 3000 Line edition completely updated with new weapons, equipment and tactics. The new maps are UV coated allowing the use of dry erase markers. Four versions of the game now allow patrolling in wooded, mountain, jungle and desert terrain. Face the challenge of planning and executing a Ranger patrol on the modern battlefield. Missions include squad reconnaissance or platoon ambush and raid patrols. Experience the actual decisions faced by today's U.S. Army Ranger. The first of these exciting solitaire games simulates operations in a jungle environment.

Ranger, one of the best selling board wargames of all time, has been completely revised and updated with new weapons, equipment and tactics. This unique game has been a great cross over success with players who do not play traditional wargames.

description:
Players compete in building race cars. The cars are represented by physical models, which are assembled from a chassi, wheels, a seat, a steering wheel, and a variety of beams. Availability of car parts is decided by dice rolls.

Two, three, or four players can join in playing Rääcer Frääser. Each player is to build his/her own racing car. The player who finishes building first will win.

To build, players roll dice. Each number on the dice correspond with a certain car part. 1 is the chassis (you have to start with this), 2 (which is required to come next) is the steering beam, 3 is the seat, 4 is the steering wheel, 5 are the beams, and 6 are the wheels (you need four of these). If a player gets a valid car part, he gets to roll again.

The game is 100% luck but still brings some tactile fun assembling the cars. Of course, when finish, you can play with the built racing cars.

In テトラコンボ (Tetra Combo), each player controls four colored blocks on a 4x4 game board. They alternate taking turns, moving one block on each turn in order to create tetrominos, a tetromino being a pattern created by four adjacent blocks. (Think of the pieces in Tetris, with five tetrominos being the only shapes possible.)

Each time that a player creates a tetromino — whether with their own pieces or with four empty spaces on the game board — that player scores a point for that particular type of tetromino. If you manage to create two more of a tetromino than the opponent, you win!

FRONTIER POST, LIBYAN-EGYPTIAN BORDER, 12 JUNE 1940: The frontier wire was some 12 feet wide and 16 feet high; the Italians had built it along the Libya-Egypt border from the coast near Sollum to the oasis of Jarabub, 150 miles to the south. On 12 June patrols from the 11th Hussars and the KRRC quickly breached the wire. Attacking at dawn and out of the rising sun, the Hussars surprised and confused the Italian defenders, who watched as their machinegun posts were suppressed by British mortar fire and their tankettes destroyed by British armored cars and close-range machinegun fire from Vickers tanks. These raids continued through the rest of June and July of 1940, until the British 7th Armoured Division was recalled for a refit in Egypt. The raids destroyed many Italian vehicles, considerable supplies of ammunition and petrol, and significant water storage. Twenty-five Italian officers (including a general of engineers) and five hundred other ranks were captured. This series of raids also marked the beginning of an overall erosion of Italian morale, as the inadequacy of their tank armor and the scarcity of effective antitank weapons and ammunition became apparent....

ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS: Frontier War is a detailed new ASLComp VARIANT that tells the story of the War in North Africa. It's about time the desert warfare goodness that was spawned by Hal Hock's seminal work TOBRUK back in 1975... was brought to the gaming tables of ASLers. And now it is, in a big way.

The eighth portion of the set-piece is ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS: Frontier War and depicts the British push forward during the blazing hot summer of 1940. Italy had the meatballs to declare war on Great Britain as Mussolini decided to join his fellow dictators and gorge on Europe's spoils. Il Duce failed to take into account the fighting spirit of Tommy Atkins and his commanders. The result was anything but a massive offensive by numerically superior Italian soldiers in the Western Desert. Instead, British probes became attacks. Attacks became offensives. And the entire affair became a major problem for Mussolini's Italy and dreams of more easy conquests on the African Continent. This batch of 8 scenarios takes things from the frontier raid of 12 June 1940 through Graziani's offensive and the "first haul of booty of the desert war" along the coastal road west of Alam el Dab, Egypt some months later.

You receive a set of 8 scenarios, all depicting actions from the exciting offensive, rife with combined-arms actions. And yes, these are aimed at playability. Slap them down, bang them out, do it again. And again. Please do a little hooting and hollering while you're at it... at least when you roll 'snakeyes' (or the other guy rolls 'boxcars').

boardgame: ASL Comp: Afrikakorps – Along the Via Balbia
description:
Near FUKA, EGYPT, 30 June 1942: All Axis supply traffic in North Africa moved eastward along the Via Balbia, the coast road that stretched from El Alamein to Tripoli. Rommel depended on this lifeline for his supplies, well known to the Allied command. For the first time, British fighter-bombers roar overhead looking for prey. As the Gazala positions crumbled, Rommel pushed his men and machines ever eastward along the Via Balbia, in pursuit of what he thought was a beaten enemy. Rommel's best troops raced forward in the van. Italian units followed behind. The Desert Fox knew the enemy was in disarray. Rommel also knew British supply lines and the ability to move units into the front line improved with every retrograde mile they retreated. The Italian XX Corps began moving east along the Via Balbia late on June 30, heading for participation in Rommel's last ditch attempt to reach Alexandria. Their trip would not go without incident as the British suddenly appeared from the desert flank, precipitating a vicious firefight. Their tormentors were units of the British 7th Motorized Brigade, sent forward to buy time for a new line to be formed at El Alamein. The quick-hitting unit handled the Italians roughly before pulling up stakes and moving off. The resulting Axis losses were not serious in terms of manpower. They were in terms of the time bought by tying up the coast road...

ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS: Along the Via Balbia is a detailed new ASLComp VARIANT that tells the story of the War in North Africa. It's about time the desert warfare goodness that was spawned by Hal Hock's seminal work TOBRUK back in 1975... was brought to the gaming tables of ASLers. And now it is, in a big way.

The tenth portion of the set-piece is ASLComp AFRIKAKORPS: Along the Via Balbia is a mixed date set of scenarios meant to depict the back-and-forth of the campaign with actions ranging from Fort Pilastrino to Fuka, with some additional Bir Hacheim and Bedja Pass in between. This set of 8 scenarios brings fort and village overlays into play, providing some interesting positional warfare to your wide-open desert gaming.

You receive a set of 8 scenarios, all depicting actions from the exciting offensive, rife with combined-arms actions. And yes, these are aimed at playability. Slap them down, bang them out, do it again. And again. Please do a little hooting and hollering while you're at it... at least when you roll 'snakeyes' (or the other guy rolls 'boxcars').

boardgame: 梦想之道 (The Way of the Dream)
description:
夢想之道 (The Way of the Dream) is about the journey toward the dream. In the game, each player takes two roles at the same time: the one who tries the best to make their own dreams come true, as well as the key person during the pursuit of other players' dreams. What will happen during the journey depends on other players' choices; some are good, while others might hinder you a bit. Therefore, you can focus on disturbing other players, or concentrating on how to realize your own dream. Or, try to make a win-win situation with wisdom of crowd.

In a player's turn she will draw destiny cards and distribute them among all players as their hand cards. Then each player must give the active player one card from their hands, and the active must use what she has got form her fellow players' "help" to lay the cards onto the game board, paving the way toward her dream. Once a player reached the central goal area, her dream comes true and wins the game.
With アイスクリームコンボ:バブー拡張 (Ice Cream Combo: Baboo Expansion), players now get to take the role of customers who are eating the ice cream cones created in this shop.

Guard your treasures! Kidnap foreign royalty! Bring honor to your family! In the first expansion for Castle Crush!, in addition to destroying enemy castles, looting and kidnapping present new challenges to the players. Additionally, family members of each kingdom's royalty can step out and fight for each kingdom's honor and victory!

With the Castle Crush! Cubes & Cards expansion, players have special scoring depending on the family cards they gain, and they can use new blocks to diversify the world of Castle Crush!
Who will take the throne in the King's Game?

Breakneck Blitz is a game of arena combat, in which players take the role of Blitzers (Cybernetically enhanced skaters), who are trying to outlast their opponent in a brutal skate to the death.  
The game can be played individually or in teams, with each character and team having a unique ability.

Breakneck Blitz is played on a square grid.  Each player draws the movement line for their Blitzer, which may vary in length and shape depending on your Blitzers ability and tactics. Movement requires forward planning in order to outmanoeuvre your opponents and achieve victory.

The Catan Day 2015 Special is an expansion for Catan.  This is an exclusive item only available to the attendees of Catan Day 2015.  It commemorates the first annual Catan Con in Nashville TN.

You use this expansion with the Catan base game, the expansions Catan Seafarers, and Catan Cities & Knights. You can also play with the 5-6 player expansions for all the games and expansions mentioned.

Game Components:
2 Nashville Hexes
1 Lonely Cowboy game piece in 2 parts
Rule Sheet

Setting up the Game

Remove the Desert Hex from the game and replace it with the Broadway Hex. Build your board by randomly distributing terrain hexes, while ensuring that the broadway hex is in the center of the island.

Place the number tokens according to the game rules. Replace the terrain hex that receives the "2" token with the Gaylord Opryland hex and remove the "2" token as well.

Since neither Nashville hex has a number token, they do not produce resources when the dice are rolled.

The Lonely Cowboy

When the lonely cowboy visits you, he will sing for his supper and you will gladly pay him.

He consists of 2 parts.  Slide both parts together so the musical notes are facing each other. The lonely cowboy replaces the robber.  Place him on the Broadway hex during setup.

Advantage of the Nashville Hexes

Whenever you roll a "7", the usual actions are carried out normally. (Discards (if any), move the cowboy, "sing" for a card etc.).  Then before you take the rest of your turn, activate the lonely cowboy.

The lonely cowboy gives one resource from the bank to each player with one or more settlements on a Nashville hex intersection (even if you have 2+ settlements/cities, you only get one card).  You receive the card type matching the terrain hex the Lonely cowboy currently occupies.

Important:  Once the lonely cowboy leaves the broadway hex, it can never be placed on either Nashville hex for the rest of the game.

Cat is a promotional Survivor that was added to Season 3 Kickstarter campaign. A Zombivor version is also included.

THE LOST LAND ADVENTURE is a board game that features dinosaurs and with innovative components that makes it stand out to other games its type, such as:

     Random box system that makes each match different from the other
     Play as the great T. Rex or play as your favorite dinosaur
     You have fun learning or learn having fun with the trivia cards
     Situations, traps and objects that can change the results


LA TIERRA PERDIDA AVENTURA es un juego de mesa con dinosaurios pero que tiene componentes innovadores que lo diferencian de los demás, cómo:

     Sistema de casillas aleatorias que hace que cada partida sea diferente
     Juega como el T. rex o como tu dinosaurio favorito
     Te diviertas aprendiendo o aprendes jugando con las tarjetas trivia
     Situaciones, trampas y objetos que pueden cambiar el resultado de la partida
Dogeza Cat is a microgame. No other details are known at this time.

This game uses a standard deck of playing cards to pit two groups of heroes against one another in an epic battle involving tactics, spells, and espionage.  The game is for 2-players and usually takes 20 to 30 minutes to play.

If you are familiar with fantasy role-playing games then you will immediately recognize many of the game’s concepts.  In this game the three court cards - the King, Queen, and Jack - become the Knight, the Witch, and the Thief.  Each hero type has it’s own unique strengths and abilities.

The setup is easy, the action is fast, and you more than likely already have a deck of cards lying around.  So the next time you and a gaming friend are looking for a little action why not use those cards and see how your valiant knight fares in battle against some wily thieves and crafty witches?

Each player is dealt 4 court cards and these will become their Party of Heroes.  The heroes are placed in a 2x2 pattern in front of each player, called their Marching Order.  In this way the two teams can be imagined to be facing each other in a battle formation.  The position of each hero in the marching order is important as some hero types are more effective on the front lines while others are better off in the rear.

The game is played in rounds similar to RPG games where initiative, movement, special abilities, and attacks are used to hinder and eliminate the members of the opposing party.

Attacks are resolved by comparing cards played from the players hands (their "Inventories") and if it succeeds then an extra combat step is played to determine if the defender survived the attack or not. Throughout the combat decisions must be made and there is a press your luck element to the fighting.

First team to eliminate the other wins!

Invent, Patent and Negotiate Deals to WIN.  Invent and create technology in eight different fields.  Patent your invention or negotiate patent deals to dominate the market place. (from the box)

Each player draws a type of company and random inventions.  Depending on whether the inventions you've drawn match your genre of company or not, you follow flow charts (printed on mats) as to whether to patent or not, whether to license out to another player or develop the product for market.  As you go around the board, you land on spaces that cause you to draw revenue from your invention, pay license fees to the other player, or go to patent court.  Play continues until the first patent expires (or second patent if a longer game is desired).

boardgame: For Glory and Honor: A Game of Soldiering during the Napoleonic Wars.
description:
INTRODUCTION
For Glory and Honor is a game for one to four Players. Each Player creates a Player Soldier during the Napoleonic Wars. The Player Soldiers will attempt to gain Merit Points in order to advance in rank throughout various Campaigns of the Napoleonic Wars. Player Soldiers can be assigned to a Cavalry or Infantry Regiment serving in the British or French Armies. This game is especially suited for fans of Bernard Cornwell's Richard Sharpe Series, Sir Arthur Conan Doyle's Brigadier Gerard Series and Richard Howard's Alain Lausard series.

OBJECTIVE
The Objective of the Game is to have your Soldier be the first to complete his Career during the Napoleonic Wars. Career Progress is tracked by moving your Soldier’s Counter along the Game Board’s Career Track. The Career Track consists of Garrison Duty and Campaign Duty Assignments. These Assignments will result in a variety of situations and encounters including Skirmishes, Patrols, Battles, Encampment, Headquarters, Siege Assaults and Special Missions in which the Player’ Soldier must resolve. The outcome of these Encounters and Situations determine whether the Player’s Soldier advances along the Career Track. While the Soldiers conduct Challenges, they might gain or lose Merit Points, which are used to improve their Attribute Ratings and Rank. The Player Soldier with the Highest Rank after reaching the Ending Space on the Career Track, is the Winner. If this is tied, then the Soldier with the most Merit Points remaining is the Winner. If this is tied then the Soldiers may conduct a Duel to determine the Winner.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Take apprentices' final examinations and attain master craftsman's diplomas to get better trained employees, procure jewelry for your guests, and hire specialists.

The Rococo: Jewelry Box expands the base game Rococo without changing its basic rules. A total of 28 additional employees (10 apprentices, 9 journeymen, and 9 masters) with new bonuses will enter the game in three different ways, increasing the replayabilty tremendously.

On top of it, there are new strategic options such as the Jeweler with the »Jewelry« resource in multiple colors, as well as the appren­tices' final examination and the master crafts­man's diploma. In each game, you'll have to make up your mind again which combi­nation of all those many possibilities to try. And remember to keep an eye on your opponents!

From the introduction:

This game consists in buying properties: islands - islets - realestate, factories, piers and then developing them by building on them. (Note: You must take care to follow the order in which you are authorized to erect buildings). Each investment is provided added value thanks to a rent which becomes higher and higher, as this enables the player to set up a cash flow for himself. But if it is true that all of the players collect rent for their respective properties, they must also pay money out when they pass over the properties of other players.

Moreover, the game has a number of small surprises that the player must evaluate in advance to avoid finding himself at loss. Of course, "Le Jeu de Polynésie" Game, is only a game and the names of the property, islands, assets indicated as well as the card rules have no basis whatsoever in reality. This game is a pure fiction that seeks only to entertain your children and the big kids that we have remained too!

User description:

Players take the part of land owners in French Polynesia, attempting to buy and then develop their islands. Income is gained by other players visiting their properties and money is spent when they visit properties belonging to other players. When times get tough, players may have to mortgage their properties to raise cash for fines, taxes and other misfortunes. The goal of the game is to be the last player remaining with any money or, in a timed game, to be the player with the largest amount of assets.

On his turn, a player rolls two dice and moves that number of spaces around the board. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned property, he has the opportunity to buy it and add it to his portfolio. If a player owns all the spaces within a colour group, he may then build hotels, farms or a fishing fleet on these spaces, generating even more income from opponents who land there. If he lands on a property owned by another player, he must pay that player rent according to the value of the land and any buildings on it. There are other places on the board which cannot be bought, but instead require the player to draw a card and perform the action on the card, pay taxes, collect income, or even go to jail. The game allows a player to purchase insurance or hire an attorney, to have some protection against some forms of misfortune.

Objective: be the first crossing finish line (space 70) .

Simple die rolling game. From the start, players take turns moving round the track, obeying instructions on special (red marked) squares. The first player crossing the finish line wins.

Likely published during 1920s

Objective:

Be the first reaching space 54. Players take turns rolling the dice and moving their pawn. Landing on a marked space has a special effect, which is described in the instructions.

Likely published 1920/30s

Nur kaltes Blut is a variant of Pachisi.

It has one added rule: a pawn on a circular space cannot be beaten; if a second pawn joins him on that space all other meeples behind are blocked

Likely published mid 1920s.

Snap Attack is a fun and interactive wooden board game for 2-4 players. Each player or team receives 7 powerfully magnetic pucks. The object of the game is to not cause the SNAP ATTACK! Players take turns placing their magnetic pucks on the recessed playing surface while trying not to upset the finely balanced magnetic fields and cause the pucks to snap together. When a player makes a move that causes one or more pucks to snap together–SNAP ATTACK–that player loses the game. When this happens, the player who didn’t cause the SNAP ATTACK is awarded a point.

Planet Hysteria is a game of hidden identities for 5-15 people. Depending on a few modular game mechanics, it can take anywhere from 1-3 hours to play.

In the game, you are a group of specialists sent to terraform a new planet, and the survival of the human race depends on you. On the last day of your mission, your shuttle was destroyed. This left you stranded on a planet that is about to be transformed, killing you but terraformimg humanity's new home. At that moment, you realized the planet was already inhabited by a shape-shifting alien race, and it's natives won't give up without a fight.

The game is broken up into two phases, a morning and an evening. In the morning, players take actions to repair equipment and maintain the group's health and sanity. In the evening, players discuss a course of action. Those two phases make up a day, and players only have a few days to root out the aliens that have infiltrated the group.

The game ends when the shuttle is repaired and players vote to leave. If all people on board are human, humans win. But, if humans failed to test enough players, and even one hidden alien is on board... Aliens win.

And to make the game even more panic ridden, each in game location is in a different real world location. That means that every morning, things are happening in secret; and you have to judge for yourself if the other players are lying or telling the truth. Two humans may go to a location, but if one is a hidden alien, two aliens might come back. Without developing tests and merciless interrogation, you will never know.

Only trust what you've seen with your own eyes, because even the most helpful human might be an alien vying for a seat on the shuttle.

description:
You work in a neglected warehouse, where the goods are irregularly on the shelves, which should be ordered.

The goods are coming and coming, and a variety of goods to be moved to the right places.

There are two groups of goods, the "food" and "non-food". In both groups there are various types of goods.
Foods: cereals, vegetables, mineral waters and spices
Non-foods: metals, textiles, plastics and chemicals

After placing a goods, you get points according, how good is the location of the goods.

There's plenty to do, and the working time will soon be over, which lasts until, all goods will be on shelves.

At the end of the game, the storekeeper with the most points is the winner.

This book has 4 board games with some easy games used to explain Ancient Rome to kids. The book also has punchable hard stock counters and even a die.

The four board games are:

All roads lead to Rome. 1-4 players. You must reach Rome following a road full of surprises.

Duodecim Scripta. 2 players. A kind of ancient backgammon played over a Latin inscription.

Gallic War. 2 players. A basic tactical wargame representing an abstract battle between Romans and Gauls.

The game of the Seven Hills of Rome. 2 players. Players alternate rolling a die and placing tokens on the Hills of Rome. The player who takes the Palatine Hill is the winner.

description:
Objective: Be the first racing to position '130'
'Move by turns' game from the 1910's in Germany.

A plastic frog has to be flipped onto a big board showing a large pool with different animals. Depending on the area the frog is landing on you may move forward (acc. number) or backwards (snake, stork...). When you reach space 120 or higher you are safe, but the target must be reached with a matching jump.

The Doctor Who Co-Op Adventure Game is a 1-5 player COOPERATIVE exploration and adventure game set in the crazy universe of the Doctor Who TV series.  This game was hand crafted by Sean Patten as a gift to his family.  Now you can print your own copy and take it for a spin!

The DWCOAG is scenario based game where you take on the role of the Doctor and his assistants.  Encounter tiles reveal items, enemies, minions, traps, and alarms, all which have to be dealt with using character skills (fight, wit, and run).  Event cards cause enemies to regroup or advance, doors to lock,  or even your Tardis to get stolen.  Avoid capture and complete objectives to win the scenario.

boardgame: Pride and Glory: The Peloponnesian War
description:
Pride and Glory: The Peloponnesian War is a strategy game set during a war in Ancient Greece. Players take control of the cities of Athens and Sparta and train soldiers, construct buildings, capture cities, and engage in epic battles for the future of Greece. Players control their empire from a full campaign perspective on the game board, but once a battle begins, players can command their soldiers in tactical, unit-by-unit combat on separate battlefield maps.

Game turns are divided into the four seasons of the year. Each season has four phases in which players: 1) Build up their empire by recruiting units, constructing buildings and conducting diplomacy. 2) Move their units around the Campaign Map and besiege cities. 3) Engage in battle and command their soldiers in tactical, simulation combat. 4) Protect themselves against revolts of the people and the stormy seas of the Aegean.

Every triumph brings your city more glory, and closer to victory, until all Greece kneels before you. Throughout the game players earn things called Glory Points for doing things like capturing cities, winning battles – even just by proving that your enemy is too cowardly to come out and fight. Whichever player is the first to get to 75 Glory Points wins the game.

boardgame: The Heaven Sword and Dragon Saber
description:
武林至尊，寶刀屠龍，號令天下，莫敢不從！倚天不出，誰與爭鋒？

六大派圍攻光明頂，情況危急！玩家扮演倚天屠龍記中各路英雄，誰勝誰敗，就以骰子和技術決勝！

玩家派出弟子，控制門派的跟據地，爭奪寶物，在四輪爭奪完成後，贏得最多寶物價值的，就是贏家！

The Heaven Sword and Dragon Saber

Victory Conditions: 
Players play in classic heroes in the famous novel The Heaven Sword and Dragon Saber, after the completion of the first four wins to win the maximum value of the treasures.

El tesoro del pirata (also known as "Pirate's bridge") is a fun and exciting game of skill, where you have to get the most coins you can. You must extract them carefully, because too much trepidation will make the skull jump out of it's chest, and that'll end the game. Also, not all coins are the same and some are more difficult to extract that others.

It's a fight to reach the summit faster than the other player! The spinner determines the colour of the holes you can use to attach your climbers' limbs each turn. The more risky movements you made, leaving limbs unsecured, the quicker you'll ascend, but also, the easier the other player would make you fall if he lands in the same spot from the other side and kicks you out of the hole. The first player to take his two climbers to the summit, wins the game.

Objective: be the first reaching space 79; learn more about European history/cities - increase patriotism.

Players start in Friedrichshafen (1 & 79). They take turns moving their pawn according to a die roll. Some of the spaces have special rules.

Note:
Likely published early 1910s. Pawns are pewter.

It’s lunch time! The mechanical chef maniacally spins in the middle of the game board and throws them all over the place, and the players manually move their plates so they can get the pizzas delivered to them. But they should be careful! Not all the pizzas are well cooked and if they get one half-cooked, they lose points. In the end, the player with the most points (counting the good pizzas minus the half-cooked ones), wins the game.

boardgame: Ever After More: The Card Game
description:
Ever After More is a mixture of Yu-Gi-Oh, Magic and Munchkin. If you're a fan of any of those games, then you'll like EAM. The way to win is two-fold. Either retain the most Life Points or have the most card points at the end of the game. Oh, this game is also rigged so that anyone can win or one person could win every time. (Believe me, it's happened to me...)

The way to play is really simple. Draw your cards; lay them down; and attack! You choose your opponent, but be careful. Your opponent could have a card to counter your attack. It's all up in the air when you start playing. Anyone could win, at any time too.

Ah-Sitake HA! The ninja battlin', card spinnin', flingin' flangin' action game of well, battling ninjas, spinning cards, and flanging flingings.

Simultaneously choose your battle focus card, then toss it out onto the table and see whether your attack attacked, your counter countered, your block blocked, your throw threw, or your weakness got punched squarely in the junk!  Battle multiple opponents at the same time.  But be careful!  A successful attack on one opponent may leave you exposed in a very sensitive spot!  With every angle there is an equal and opposite re-angle... and yours may just be your undoing!

Battle your friends until they become your enemies!  If anyone is left standing after one round, special attack and block cards come into play to give those that are behind the ability to hopefully SAVE their behind, or more efficiently KICK someone else's!

Are you ready for some fast-paced, ninja animal, card battling?  Oh you think so, do you?  Well, you'll just have to find out.  I say to you, sir (or ma'am), "Ah-Sitake HA!"

You and your Opponent have been rivals all through the Wizard Academy. It is now your final exam and the perfect opportunity to roast that guy once and for all.  The first to deal 20 damage wins.

Like any wizard duel it is a battle of wits to see who will come out on top.  Each player has a character card with special abilities and the same 6 spells, 2 defence, 2 offence and 2 utility.  Players choose a spell from their hand and place it face down in front of them.  Once both players have chosen, they turn them face up simultaneously and resolve the effects in Energy Number order.

However if both spells have the same Energy Number then they are cancelled.  Too many cancelled spells in a round and the characters abilities come into play.  First they punish, then they get desperate and desperate people can do almost anything.

description: Simple roll-and-move-game from the 1920/30s. The first player to reach space 28 (Bremerhaven) wins the game. On certain spaces the players are hindered or favored, on some they are rewarded game coins or lose some.

Landships is a stand alone game for 2-6 players. It includes 6 new Spurs and Sprockets decks and introduces a new set of rules that includes the Fire Away, projectile attack. Players may combine it with the basic Spurs and Sprockets decks or the Revolutionary decks. The game decks also include artwork used with the new Fire Away rules.

Spurs and Sprockets is a perfect information, abstract strategy game. Players each have the same eight tiles/cards that they take turns placing into a grid, in an attempt to link three or four in a row. There is no board in Spurs and Sprockets so the grid grows organically, based on the placement of tiles. The grid also varies in size and dimensions based on the number of players.

This Spurs and Sprockets game comes with 48 tiles/cards and rules for many different ways of playing, ranging from a simple Tic-Tac-Toe-like game where players gain 8 points for four in a row and 3 points for each set of three in a row, to a game where every placement of a tile/card may result in an attack and removal of another.

description:
Heroic Hustle is a fully cooperative team-based experience for 2-4 players. The superheroes (that's you), lead the other heroes of the city into battle to protect a city on the edge of destruction.

A wide range of threats will endanger the city every round, and it's up to the players to nullify them before they can launch an attack. By pairing up your heroic strengths to the weaknesses of your enemies, you'll be able to arrest the attacking forces.

These threats are only the first layer of danger, though. There's always a supervillain in the background working against Cascade City throughout the entire game. Even worse, the more progress you make towards foiling their schemes, the angrier the supervillain gets, and the more dangerous they become.

As if all of that wasn't enough to keep your heroic hands busy, plot twists will change the way the game is played from round to round. One round might see the villains sporting shiny new weapons, while in the next your entire team might be turned into gorillas! As the supervillain's master plan unfolds, these dynamic events will force your team to work together and adapt your strategies.

Thankfully, your elite team has plenty of resources to combat these threats. Some of the heroes in your team work better with each other and can join forces for massive, game-changing unite attacks! You can even summon entire supergroups to show the villains what havoc really means! Not only that, but the denizens of Cascade City are ready to fight for their homes. Whether it's the plucky news team alerting the populous of danger, the tireless repair crews rebuilding the city after vicious attacks, or the dashing Kelly Courier who risks life and limb to help your team get to where they need to be. And if all else fails, each player has their own special superpowers that they can unleash when the situation is dire!

Ortus Tactica expands the gaming experience of Ortus, the fast paced 2-player tactical game. The box contains new units to add to your band of warriors, giving a big twist to the game.

Your tactics will have to adapt to what your opponent brings to the table as well.

This expansion also adds Warlord cards, introducing variable player powers. Try and beat your opponent with each of the 6 different Warlords and in every combination.

Ortus Tactica brings new options, challenges and fun to an already exciting board game.

boardgame: Castle Panic: Tower Promo 2015 Tabletop Day
description:
Replaces one of the six standard tower sections with a piece of the same size but with the Tabletop Day 2015 logo on the front side and on the back, the instructions:

"A Monster destroying this Tower is damaged for 2 points."

This is an increase from the usual damage of 1 point.

Ranka is subtitled "Easy Go" and the gameplay lives up to that description. The game takes place on a hexagonal game board divided into triangles, and players alternate turns placing one triangular piece of their color on the board. If you completely surround an opponent's piece or pieces with pieces of your own (or with a combination of pieces and the edge of the board), you capture the opponent's pieces and remove them from play. Players cannot immediately recreate a board position by playing into a space from which they were just captured.

Ranka also includes five double-sided wall/meal pieces, and if you play the game with them, players take turns placing them on the board before the game begins. If you completely surround a meal or wall piece — or surround a meal or wall in addition to opposing pieces — you capture all of the surrounded pieces. You can also use a wall as if it were a piece of your own color in order to capture an opponent's piece.

When using walls or meals, a player can win by either capturing five opposing pieces or capturing three wall/meal pieces.

The game board in Ranka is double-sided, with one playing area being smaller than the other.

It's that time of year again: the Flower Festival is in full swing, and like every year, there's a price for the most beautiful garden!

Flower Frenzy is a garden building game in which players collect seeds and use them to buy colourful flowers to add to their garden. The more colourful flowers, the higher you will score! And this year, the Jury awards additional points for completing Flower Arrangements!

A set collection game for 2-4 players, from the age of 8. It plays in 15-20 minutes.

To win Flower Frenzy, you need to have the highest score. Points can be scored by:
- buying available FLOWER Cards.
- completing Flower Arrangements depicted on the FLOWER ARRANGEMENT Cards.

These free spirits – the smugglers and spies of the galaxy – are united in their hatred of the Empire’s oppression, and they’re willing to fight to keep their freedom, no matter the odds against them.

The Imperial Entanglements deluxe expansion focuses on the Imperial Navy and the galaxy’s Smugglers and Spies, but every affiliation receives new cards to challenge tyranny or uphold the power of the dark side. Two brand-new fate cards add new tension to your edge battles, while powerful characters and vehicles like Lando Calrissian and TIE Phantoms join the battle for the fate of the galaxy. Choose your side with the newest deluxe expansion for Star Wars: The Card Game!

Contains objective sets 171-184.

"...this must be end!" screamed Skram with his squeaky voice, amplified by the crude loudhailer.

Giù dal Trono is a game of bluff and intrigue, but in a "goblin-style": players play in secret teams, trying to guess the faction of other goblins, each time wants to kill the king with a different weapon, and during each turn a player will choose a room of a dungeon, asking the other players to forge weapons with him, in order to produce enough maces or axes to supply with arms the plotters.
During each turn, player will also play cards to unveil factions, or to hinder or rob the opponents, simulating the not-so-complex discussion system in goblin society.

A player can lead his team to victory killing the king, or try to win alone fulfilling the requirements of his secret objective.

description:
Trivia game with (some tricky) questions related to the Hunsrück, a region situated in Germany, located between the cities Trier, Coblenz, Bingen & Saarbrücken. 
The game consists of cards with one question and answer each. Players take turns asking the other player(s) questions. A player who can answer a question gets the card. The player with most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Similar to Liar's Dice and also known as Poker Menteur, the game is played with a standard poker deck of 52 cards, with jokers as wildcards.

A player is dealt five cards, and names a poker hand that can be formed with it (eg. "pair of kings", "royal flush"). They may lie. The next player chooses to challenge or accept - if they challenge, the hand is revealed and the loser of the challenge take a penalty point. If they accept, they take the hand, discard 0-4 cards and draw replacements, and then make a call as previously.

The game continues until a player reaches a pre-agreed number of penalty points, at which point they are declared the loser and the game ends.

boardgame: Pocahontas: Peace in the Forest Card Game
description:
The year is 1607. The place, Virginia. The Indians & the English settlers have been at odds ever since the villainous John Ratcliffe & his men arrived to search for gold. Chief Powhatan's daughter Pocahontas & the English Captain Jon Smith want you to bring together all their Powhatan & English friends. Gramdmother Willow will help as yo gather Meeko & Flit, Powhatan & Nakoma, & Thomas, Wiggins & Lon. The first player to overcome John Ratcliffe (& his pampered pooch Percy) & collect all the friends will bring peace to the forest - & win the game.
Ages 5 and up.

Each player begins the game with a Pocahontas card and a John Smith card. On your turn, draw a card and if it is one of Pocahontas' friends or John's friends, place the card face up below that character. If you already have one of those friends, place the card in the Friends Discard pile.
Special cards include John Ratcliffe, Percy and Grandmother Willow. 
If Ratcliffe is drawn, you must turn John or Pocahontas face down. When turned face down, one of their friends must be placed in the Friends Discard pile and any friends drawn while they are face down are also discarded.
If Percy is drawn, you play him on an opponent and they must turn over their Pocahontas or John card.
If Grandmother Willow is drawn all face down John Smith and/or Pocahontas get turned face up again.
The first person to get a complete set of the two Friends groups is the winner.

La ovejas y el Dragón ("The sheep and the Dragon") is a game for children.

In a fantasy world, where many sheep escaped the castle, shepherds have the mission to find them and bring them back home. In the adventure they will find objects that help and enemies with which to fight. In turn, the great and almost invincible dragon roams throughout the kingdom seeking to steal sheep and take them to their cave.

The aim of the players is to recover the sheep, bring them back and avoid at all costs the dragon, though, if some brave shepherd manages to defeat the dragon in combat, it will be consecrated the great savior and win the game immediately.
If no defeat the dragon, the pastor with more points (for accumulation of sheep) has wins.

The game mechanics are simple, each turn pastors must perform two actions, move and look. To move roll a die (1d4), after completing its movement they can search the area where it is located and there, stealing a card from the appropriate deck as the area will reveal your search. The areas investigated can not be investigated in the future. In his search you will find objects, events, enemies and sheep. Every sheep has a value in coins (VPs), the pastor must take the sheep back to the castle to win the reward.

As a bonus action, if a pastor result is an 1 in movement (as in the Talisman), after completing their actions must "activate" the dragon.
When the exploration markers are finished, or when the dragon is defeated, the game ends.

description:
This was a project borne out of nostalgia for the photographs from the old Wargames World and Wargames Illustrated as well stories from various expatriate wargamers who moved to Australia and re-told great stories about fighting the Mahdi with Peter Gilder at the Wargames Holiday Centre.

After gathering information, sheets, original source tables and other files, the rules have been put together as close to the Gilder style of game as possible.

The Umpire plays the part of the Mahdists and their entry, troop type, disposition and general attitude is determined by the situation and random dice.  The events cards play the part of creating an additional element of the unknown into every game so that the same scenario can only rarely ever play the same way twice.  Mahdists are on larger style crafted bases for ease of movement and each base represents 100 men (scale of 1:10) even though there could be as few as 5 figures on a base if desired.

The Imperials are all played by the gamers.  "Us" against the Umpire is great fun!

This is also the perfect game for Solo play.

Organisation is based on five companies to a regiment each company consisting of 12 figures.  Once again, you can use 12 or as few figures as you wish as figures are never removed from bases but marked off on an easy to use Roster sheet.

Cavalry work in squadrons and Troops and artillery batteries in sections up.

The game comes with a 44 page gloss laminated booklet and a deck of random event cards. Six blank cards are included so players can tailor events and circumstances to suit your their collections and gaming dispositions.

User Summary:
クライムオン! (Climb ON!) is a Japanese board game produced by the doujin circle Kichikichian.

Climb ON! is a tile placement game with a theme of lead climbing. You assume the role of a climber and create climbing routes by placing wall panels on the board. Several squares are drawn in a wall panel. You roll dice and advance the number of squares, heading the top of the wall.

Every action consumes stamina, so you need to rest during your climb. You can try to make a challenging climb to the top without a rest, but it comes with a risk of falling down. To save you from falling down, a belayer controls a rope tied with you. You win the game if you climb up to the top of the wall first.

Not to be confused with the game Climb On! which is completely separate.

The monkey players in Viva! Coconots want to grab coconuts from the field, with those coconuts ideally lying in a straight line.

To set up the game, place the "Las-nut" card in the center of a 3x3 grid, then shuffle the other coconut cards and lay eight of them in the surrounding spaces of the grid, removing any unused cards from the game. The coconut cards have powers on them that come into effect when that card is active during the game.

The game lasts nine rounds. Each player has a deck of monkey cards numbered 1-10, and to start play, they each shuffle their deck, lay two cards at random face down in the front of them (looking at them whenever they like), then take a hand of three cards. In a round, the players look at the coconut card for this round (as indicated on the Las-nut card), choose a monkey card in hand, then reveal them simultaneously. If two or more players lay down the same card, they're out for the round. After taking into account the coconut's power, whoever played the highest card wins the round and places a token on the card.

The Las-nut card is claimed in the final round, with whoever has the highest total in the two cards set aside claiming it. (These cards can possibly be swapped during play.)

If a player has ever placed three tokens in a line on the grid, that player wins immediately. If this doesn't happen, whoever has placed the most tokens (with the Las-nut counting as two) wins.

The objective of this game is to identify the sounds contained in the audio cassettes as they're played, in order to get rid of your "note tokens" (DO, RE and MI, each representing different difficulty levels). When you think you know what sound is played, press your button in the electronic device and give your answer. If you're right, you can get rid of the correspondent token. If you fail, you have to take another token. The first to get rid of them all, wins the game.
