description:
Outsider is a card game that combines cooperative and hidden traitor mechanics with set collection and hand management, in an immersive science fiction setting.

Players are each assigned or choose a character, each with unique abilities and a different combination of the four skill icons. Each player is also given a random Loyalty card, which is kept secret from the other players. Most players will be Crew and will work together to complete repairs, represented by Goal cards. Crew members win the game when they have completed more goals than there are players, all Crew have at least one completed Goal, and any Crew member correctly declares victory on their turn.

One or two players will be Outsiders. Outsiders win the game if they declare victory on their turn and all Crew members are Doomed. All players lose if the deck runs out or if everyone becomes Doomed from a single source, which adds a cooperative element. An addendum provides alternate rules for groups that find this too harsh or would prefer a more narrative ending.

The setting is gritty sci-fi with art by James Zark lending a cyberpunk flavor to the characters. The basic concept deals with a future where vessels move between universes to save time on space travel, with the occasional side-effect of crew members becoming murderous and paranoid, called "Outsiders".

Outsiders are literally castaways from other dimensions. Having shifted between universes too many times, they suddenly find that they no longer recognized things that should be familiar. Torn from a discarded reality, their only option is to seize control of the ship and reverse the shifts in reality, dooming the rest of the crew in the process.

The game lends itself to a wide variety of strategies, and despite a small learning curve the basics come into focus pretty quickly. Multiple playthroughs reveal increasingly complex possibilities, and each game is distinctly different as new combinations of Loyalties and characters are explored.

Players must master power, speed, and precision to keep up with the frenetic marble shooting play in the B-Daman Break Bomber Battlefield arena, inspired by the B-Daman Season 1 TV series. Players start on opposite sides as they shoot at the rows of blocks in the center of the arena. Blasting away yellow blocks reveal red blocks. The player that blasts three out of five red blocks into the opponents’ territory wins.

The B-DAMAN figures are definitely toys of this generation. They are tied-in with the B-DAMAN Crossfire TV series; there are QR codes on the figures, and an associated app is available, so you can continue the battle online. Yet, even with all these new twists, it is still a classic marble shooter battling game at its core.

Set includes one deluxe target unit with 25 blocks, completely enclosed arena walls, two B-Daman figures, two B-Daman Wide Magazine Accessory pieces, 1 customization tool, 16 B-Da Marbles, 4 Ultra B-Da Marbles, 2 collector cards, a sheet of consumer-applied labels, and instructions. Each of the Wide Magazine accessories takes ten marbles at a time, so players can do less loading and more blasting.

Trivia game with questions related to the German city of Darmstadt. The game consists of cards with one question and answer each. 
Players take turns asking the other player(s) questions. A player who can answer a question gets the card. The player with most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Each card in a deck shows a different geometric pattern. A card is drawn. The player then takes his four thin, transparent, identically-shaped pieces and tries to arrange them - usually with some overlapping necessary - to match the pattern on the card that is drawn. This game can be played solitaire or as a two-player race.

The game comes with three decks of cards --- one easy, one medium, and one hard. Every card in each deck describes an animal. Each player in turn chooses a deck; another player draws a card from that deck and reads the description on the card. The listening player tries to guess the animal that is described. Correct Easy answers score 50 points, Medium 75 points, and Hard 100 points. A player must reach exactly 500 points to win.

Every card in the deck displays six movie titles. Each player in turn draws a card, chooses one of the movies, and then recites either the title of the movie or a quote from it, substituting the word chocolate for one of the words in the title or quote. The first player to guess the title gets a point. Play until an agreed-upon score is reached.

Many, many sunsets ago, it started as a card game. It is a game about an armadillo in a handheld video game. Two twins, who had been playing the game since the womb, decided to create such a game by designing a system they deemed the Intento RiiPlay. It may have had monotone graphics but it had a cool level editor.

However, the prototype was hijacked by their rival creators and codenamed the Mega Raystation. Soon after, the project was apprehended by the government which saw the name as a threat to national security. Nonetheless, Intendo went on, and its founders continued playing RIC O’ Chet, the armored ‘dillo card game.

You too can play this card game! Players create a level from their level cards for their opponent to beat. Each players level starts as 3 cards with controller button icons that must match the icons on their editor cards to score.

As cards are scored from the left, cards shift just as they would in a side scrolling platformer. The creator of the level uses their editor cards to determine which level cards are placed next. The first player to score all 12 of their opponents level cards wins the round and the opponent loses a health. If  a player loses all health, they have lost the game!

Each player places a shark tile of his color on a designated starting space on an 8X8 board. On his turn, a player "moves" his shark by placing another tile of his color on the space next to his previously placed tile to which that earlier tile points. The newly placed tile may point in any direction, except to a space that another shark already points to or to an occupied space. Players eventually will get hemmed in and will be unable to move. Last shark standing or, rather, swimming wins.

Description from the publisher:

As dire omens fill the skies, demons roam the countryside, and plagues ravage civilization, the Harbinger wanders throughout the realm of Talisman, prophesying the imminent end of the world. If you encounter him, he may help you stave off the end of the world and let you peer into the future — or you may be attacked by demon horsemen and stalked by cursed followers.

Time is running out. When the Harbinger entered the realm he brought with him a prophecy of impending doom and a series of seven Omens to watch for: when the seventh appears, the end is upon you. Talisman (fourth edition): The Harbinger Expansion features four unique decks of Omen cards, each making manifest a different downfall. In one Omen deck the constellations align, threatening the extinction of magic. In another, the dead rise from their graves and battle to overthrow the living. In another, the time of judgement arrives bringing with it war, famine, death, plagues, and other forms of destruction.

In the fourth, the world shatters into many pieces and the terrain transforms unpredictably. New Terrain cards enter the game and alter the landscape, supplanting fields with barrens, deserts with floodlands, and cities with bogs. As doom draws nearer, more and more of the realm is transformed until the seventh Omen appears and all of Talisman becomes a deadly devastation.

In game terms, when the Harbinger is in your region, you must draw from the peril-filled Harbinger deck instead of that region's normal Adventure deck whenever you are instructed to draw cards. When you draw an Event, the Harbinger moves into your space, but has no ill effects upon you. However, you move into the space occupied by the Harbinger, you must roll a single die. The result could grant you a second turn, or let you look at the top three cards of any deck you choose. It might force you to populate your space with vicious enemies, treacherous followers, and unpredictable events. Worst of all, your result might cause the current Omen to be discarded, activating the next Omen and hastening the advent of the end times.

TRAP! is a fast-paced, deductive, dice-rolling, card game system for 2-4 players in which you're trying to ensnare the biggest sets of creatures over the course of five rounds.

Each TRAP! game is a standalone item, but they are compatible with one another and parts can be combined to create a personalized TRAP!

Description from the publisher:

Recreate the Battle of St. Mere Eglise with the new scenario expansion Heroes of Normandie: Sainte-Mere Eglise! This expansion features:


    New special abilities: Charge, Urban Combatant, Demolition, Inspiration, Munitions Transport
    New components: Camo, Cricket Lane, Gliders, Hawkins Mines, Knives
    New character traits: Dropped (for paratroopers!), Sprinter, Walking Fire
family: Series: Heroes System Tactical Scale World War 2

description:
Dragon War: Riders of Ashara is a table-top miniatures game of riders and their dragon steeds.  Set in an original world drawing upon dragon mythology from the many legends of our own world, Dragon War: Riders of Ashara focuses on dragons and riders fighting against air, ground and sea adversaries.  Their foes come from the nine kingdoms of man across three separate continents- each with their own motivations and machinations. Ashara is a world of incredibly high mountain ranges that divide the continents, meaning that wars must be fought either from the air or by sea.

Ashara, being the known lands of the kingdoms of mankind is at war.  The year is 1448, being the 120th occurrence of the year of the Falcon in the Zodiac of Shen. Nations battle for conquest, glory, and power. Dragon riders, highly trained warriors and the pride of their nations, sit atop powerful dragons to do battle with one another and defend against threats from the skies, lands and seas of Ashara. Dragons fight with tooth, claws and their mighty breath weapons of fire, ice, lightning, poison, acid, wind or the mysterious life stealing power of the rend. Choose your dragon and the nation you defend as you take to the skies from the back of a young and agile drake or an ancient and powerful wyrm. Or, choose to register as one of the many mercenary wings, small bands of dragon riders fighting for the highest bidder or for your own personal crusade. The time has come for heroes and their mighty steeds to take to the skies. This is the age of the Riders of Ashara.

Publisher's summary:
憑者 (Tsukimono) is a Japanese boardgame from the doujin circle Illgang.

Get 3 YO-RIKI by attaching YO-KAI to the other players, and reach the mysterious mountain first.

集めた妖怪を他のプレイヤーにとり憑かせ、先に妖力３つを手にして妖山にたどりつくことを目指す、危険な遊戯です。

It is the little tiger's birthday and he celebrates a real Tiger Party. All are invited: the fox, the lucky mole, the elegant goose and all the other Tiger Friends. Each brings paintings. The little tiger is highly excited because homemade gifts are the best, right? Splendid images for a terrific card game are a wonderful birthday gift and you can try it: View the pictures carefully and cleverly put cards and win real Tiger!

Game has 32 cards, and tiger points. Everyone gets 6 card and some tiger points. Each card has two or more tiger friends on it. On its turn player must play a card that don't have tiger friends that are on a card that is on a table, or draw a new card. Whoever plays his last card wins the round and gets tiger points from other players depending on how many tiger friends they have in hand.
When someone can't pay a game ends, and whoever has most tiger points is a winner.

A game box forms a board that consist of tree, meadow and a lake.
And there is 14 chips that have pictures of animals, plants and things that child must put on the right spot of the board.
When all chips has been placed on their right spot on the board, the game ends.

Version for older kids, is that every of a two players has 4 spots on tree for himself, and chips are face down. Each player draws one chip and puts it on its place. Whoever puts chips on its 4 spot of a tree wins the game.
Harder version of a game is that all chips that are not placed on a tree are returned back face down next to unused chips.

Trivia game with questions related to the German city of Willich. The game consists of cards with one question and answer each.

Players take turns asking the other player(s) questions. A player who can answer a question gets the card. The player with most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Trivia game with questions related to the German composer Richard Wagner. The game consists of cards with one question and answer each. 
Players take turns asking the other player(s) questions. A player who can answer a question gets the card. The player with most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Trivia game with questions related to German citations. The game consists of cards with one question and answer each. 
Players take turns asking the other player(s) questions. A player who can answer a question gets the card. The player with most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Trivia game with questions related to the German town of Lüneburg. The game consists of cards with one question and answer each.

Players take turns asking the other player(s) questions. A player who can answer a question gets the card. The player with most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Trivia game with questions related to the German town of Göttingen. The game consists of cards with one question and answer each. 
Players take turns asking the other player(s) questions. A player who can answer a question gets the card. The player with most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

boardgame: Dead Men Tell No Tales: Camille E.N. Promo Card
description: Promo card for the game Dead Men Tell No Tales. This pirate can use anyone else's ability once per turn.

The Mercenary Start Card Expansion includes one card/tile that may be used with all Spurs and Sprockets card/tile decks.

The Mercenary is placed in the field first and the start player places his/her card/tile adjacent to the Mercenary. Play continues based on which Spurs and Sprockets rules you have decided to play with. The Mercenary card/tile has lines in all directions and can be added to all players adjacent rows for scoring purposes. Although the Mercenary has a 0 on it and does not give any points to any player. The Mercenary may be destroyed by using Fired Away rules.

boardgame: Ophir: Additional Specialists – Postmaster and Peddler
description:
Originally distributed as Kickstarter extras, these two new specialists will be available (as a set) at conventions and the BGG store.

With the 8 specialists already included, this brings the total available to 10.

Postmaster: May contribute Metal(s) from any adjacent hex to the Temple.

Peddler: May return one less Basic Good of any type during a (-1) Market Trade.

Planning a bank heist is hard. Pulling off a heist without a plan is even harder...

You have an idea for an amazing heist. You know of a bank that is stocked with safe deposit boxes loaded with cash, and you have assembled a team of 13 faithful robbers to do the dirty work. As you settle down to craft the perfect plan for the heist, you find out that several other people are planning to hit the same bank. Naturally, you decide to throw caution to the wind and move forward with the heist, planning it as you go... What’s the worst that could happen?

Underprepared Heist Management is a fast-paced card game for 2-5 players. During the heist, players work with a limited pool of plans that they are dealt, managing their heist as best as they can by ordering their robbers to steal and terminate other robbers. Situations may arise that completely alter the flow of the heist in unexpected (and sometimes deadly) ways. After the heist, everyone tallies up their take, revealing the hidden contents of the safe deposit boxes they managed to grab during the heist. The manager with the highest haul is the winner!

Adventure game for kids set in fairy tale land where each player controls different character.

Modular board is build throughout the game while players explore different places. To move character players have to roll dice and match the color pattern on the leading ways. Meeting friends and rides helps to expend abilities and gives better chances to win fights and pass all kind of tests.

The goal is to find the enchanted princess, break the spell and bring her to the castle.

Palette features a deck of cards, each of which displays one of six colors and some which display the designation, goal card. Players are dealt a hand of cards and take turns playing one card and then replenishing. A goal card is played into one of four slots in a central area; the other cards are played into players' individual tableaux. Special cards also can be played that replace and otherwise manipulate the cards in the tableaux and central area. The winner is the first player to match the colors of the four cards in his tableau with those in the central area.

At the beginning of Shootout players receive an identity: sheriff, deputy, gunfighter, outlaw, etc. Initially, only the sheriff is known to the other players. Before each round, every player chooses a weapon and then all players simultaneously "shoot" other players by pointing at them. Shots can be deflected or dodged; players take wounds and get healed and take more wounds. Identities become easier to figure out. The sheriff and deputies win by killing the outlaws and vice versa.

Key to the Front Door is an educational game aimed primarily at school children to help teach money management. Players take turns rolling dice and moving around a board in Monopoly fashion. The board spaces allow players to purchase "needs" or "wants", to add funds to savings, to splurge on a vacation, to buy or sell at garage sales, or to spend or earn money in a variety of other ways. The winner is either the first player to furnish his apartment with all his "needs" or the last player to go broke.

Talons & Teeth, a 2015 Korea Board Games contest finalist, and Scott Czysz's very first game design, is a wildlife habitat / food chain card game for 1 to 3 players.

Each player is dealt one of three available habitats: Woodland, Grassland, or Wetland, then starts building their habitat with two or more food sources that fit their habitat. Then, add animals to your habitat that eat your foods. Complete your habitat by placing a predator.

A simple pyramid building game.

Players in 桜 (Sakura) want to empty their hand of cards before anyone else. Each player starts with 7-10 cards, and the sakura petal-shaped cards come in four suits with three types of special cards.

On a turn, you can play one card to the table to start a new bloom (with up to three blooms being in play at once), add one or more matching cards to an existing bloom (with no more than five cards in the bloom), play a special card, or (if you can't play anything) discard one card, then draw one. If you place the fifth card in a bloom, remove those cards, then play one standard card to start a new bloom.

The first player to empty their hand of cards wins!

In the advanced game, continue play until two players have emptied their hands, with the first player scoring 2 points and the second player 1 point. Play multiple rounds, and the player who first has 5 points wins!

In テン呼！ (Ten Calls!), players want to empty their hands as quickly as possible, but they may not be able to play cards when they want to.

The deck consists of cards numbered 1-6 (bearing point values of -6 to -1) and a joker valued 1-6 (worth -18 points). Each player starts with ten cards in hand. The starting player chooses a single card from hand and plays it to the table. The next player must play one or more cards that sum to the next number in sequence. (For example, if the first player laid down a 4, the next player must play a 5, a 4 and 1, etc.) If a player can't play, they must draw from the deck and the the next player in clockwise order is obliged to play the required number.

When a player empties their hand, the round ends and everyone scores negative points equal to their cards still in hand. (One restriction: A player cannot lay down jokers as part of their final play.)

Paragon Tactics Arena is a turn-based tactical board game, created by G. Scary T., where 2 to 4 players lead characters in an arena fight to determine who the best tactician is. Between so many enemies and obstacles, your characters cannot move freely. You must command them through action cards you draw during the combat, making the card management even more challenging than the battle itself. Each player must control his/her party strategically to better use each character’s powers and abilities and defeat all enemies. Do you think you have what it takes to lead your characters alive through this deadly skirmish?

Game Aspects:
- Easy Learning
- Tactics and Strategy
- Card Management
- Changeable and Customizable Board
- Replayability
- Easily House Ruled 
- Expandable (more characters on the way!)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
"Sum to 10 sheep, an important number"
Draw the cards to get sheep! When getting more than 10 sheep, the wolf will come and the sheep will escape.  If you get exactly 10 sheep, the escaped sheep will come back.  The game designer made this addition game with his 5 year-old child together, which allow kids to learn adding numbers to 10 while having fun playing.  Bravery and intelligence is the key to winning.

Original description:
「あわせて10匹、大事な数字」カードをめくってひつじをゲット! 10匹を超えたり、狼が出ると羊は逃げていく。逃げた羊はぴったり10匹になると戻ってくる。デザイナーが5才の子供と一緒に作った、たし算ゲーム。足して10になる数を遊びながら楽しく学べる。勇気と智恵が勝利につながるカギだ。

Rumble is a customizable discard game, where the goal of the game is to get rid of all your cards before your opponents and become the RUMBLE MASTER!  There are 90 cards in a RUMBLE deck and contains 10 different RUMBLER cards in 4 colors (red, blue, green, and yellow) and 10 wild RAINBOW CARDS.  By adding expansions called "RUMBLE PACKS" it’s possible to create your own custom games with brand new RUMBLERS to do battle with!

Monster Catcher is a color matching game for young children.

The aim of the game is collect as many monsters as possible by matching the color of monster feet cards to monster head cards. But watch out for the monster catcher! He will capture one of your completed monsters and allow another player to scoop it up.

The game begins with each player selecting 2 monster feet cards (any color). They take turns to pickup a card from the pickup pile.


    Monster Head - if the color matches the player's monster feet cards, then the player keeps the card to make a complete monster. If the color does not match, then the player must put the monster head in the discard pile. 
    Monster Feet - is added to the player's collection
    Monster Catcher - captures one of the player's completed monsters and places it in the middle of the play area
    Empty net - allows the player to rescue all of the captured monsters and add them to their own collection

The Mulberry Bush Game is a roll and move game for young children. Land on a picture of a teddy getting ready for school, mime the action (eg brushing teeth, putting on coat) and you get to collect the card. The first player to collect all 6 cards is the winner!

Cazboin is a card game in which players try to collect as many points a possible. To do so, they draw and play cards that are worth points, including action cards that may affect opponents' points totals. To play a card, players roll a six-sided die to determine which cards they can play, based on a to-play cost.

The game revolves around chance (drawing the best cards), strategy (knowing when to play which action cards), and math (knowing when a certain move is the most effective one to make).

Players draw cards from a single deck and then play cards from their hands to create their own pile of cards, the Stack. At the end of the game, the total value of the stack is the points total for that player. However, by playing action cards players can exchange cards from their own stack with that of an opponent, thereby getting rid of bad cards or stealing good cards. Interaction with stacks is done by Action cards, which have a negative value.

VERBA is the new language learning card game developed by The Pericles Group. The concept is incredibly simple -- one player (acting as a judge) draws a random card containing a fill in the blank sentence in the target language and the other players play a card containing a noun from their hand. The player who has played the sentence card then chooses the best card played and that player scores a point. Play continues with that player as the new judge.

All VERBA players benefit from seeing high frequency words, with many repetitions, in a context that fosters authentic learning and comprehensible input. As with all of The Pericles Group's educational games, the play objectives and the learning objectives are one in the same. Players win by making comprehensible sentences, scaffolded by the cards, the images, and the helpful hints included on the sentence cards. We always want to ensure that the learners are seeing correct forms and those forms are continually reinforced by the mechanics.

This is a core set specially tailored for novice level Spanish learners and works great in or out of the classroom!

This is a climbing card game that uses between four and eight suits, depending on the number of players, and is themed around the French Revolution. The player who gets rid of his hand of cards first receives 5 points, the second 3 points, and so on. Players switch positions at the table after each round based on their performance in the prior round. The first player to 30 points wins. Several special cards that can affect the game play are included.

The game comes with a Match Play deck and a Scratch Play deck, and  each player decides which to use on his turn. Scratch Play proceeds in Scattergories fashion, as players, within a time limit, write phrases that include the word that is read off the card. Score for phrases that no one else has written.
A card in Match Play features three words with a blank either before or after each word. Fill in each blank with a word to create a common phrase. The more players you match, the higher your score.
Play until one player reaches an agreed-upon total.

boardgame: Gist: The Game of Dueling Metaphors
description: This is a twist on Apples to Apples. Deal each player five cards from the OF deck to start the game. Each round, the Decider draws a card from the THE deck. Each other player then plays an OF card and replenishes his hand. Each combination of THE and OF creates a question in the form of, "What is the X of Y?" After everyone has a chance to explain their combination, players predict which combination of THE and OF the Decider will choose, and then the Decider makes his choice. Score for predicting correctly and for playing the chosen OF card. An example might be, "What is THE microwave OF friendship."

Fortify your Warband: Against the Darkness army with ten new battle-ready races from across the five realms! This expansion pack contains 10 new player race boards, player order tokens, and extra cardboard coins.

Player races include: Arboreals, Centaurs, Cyclopes, Dark Elves, Fae, Mantai, Panthera, Phibians, Raccs, and Tengu
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Juxtabo features 126 discs, each with one of seven colors on one side and a different color on the other side. Several discs are chosen randomly to form a grid to start the game, and each player receives seven discs as his hand. Players also receive two challenge cards, each of which shows a pattern consisting of either three or four discs.

The idea is to find the card pattern somewhere in the grid. A player may use his discs to change the colors in the grid. For example, one color in the grid, say, blue, does not match the color he needs, say, orange, to complete the pattern. He can play a disc with a blue side on top of the blue disc in the grid. If the other side of the played disc is orange, then he is done. If not, he can add one or more discs to the same stack in the same fashion until the grid pattern matches the card pattern.

Draw new challenge cards as you complete them. Replenish to seven discs at the end of your turn and play until all discs have been used and no one can make a match.

Can you make a spaceship or reading glasses in 60 seconds? Give it a try with the RiddleCube game. Stretch your imagination as you race your way through this rapid, shape-shifting game of 200 mind-bending picture and word puzzles. Everyone races to beat the clock by folding, twisting, and stretching their cubes into shapes that match the challenge card pulled for the round. Match the challenge card first and collect the card– the first player to collect five challenge cards is the winner!

How to Play-

Each player gets a transformable cube that consists of twelve short, hollow tubes with elastic string running through them and connecting them. It can be twisted and pulled to create various shapes. A card that names or displays a shape is drawn from a deck of 200 designs, and players race to create the solution. The first to create it scores a point and collects the card. The first to complete 5 challenges is the winner.

"An easily digestible game of food and spirits"
A trivia game for self proclaimed "Foodies" with mechanics very similar to Trivial Pursuit.  All questions are focused on food or beverage knowledge.
The categories are 

    ingredients
    spirits
    definition and preparation
    potpourri
    kitchen basics
    history & customs
    food & beverage industry

The world needs power and we have learned to travel to and harness the power of the stars. Star Farmers send ships to get as close to stars as possible to gather the most valuable power. The Star Farmers are also racing to gather this power as quickly as possible. Some pilots get too close to the stars and burn up in the process. Who will be the most successful Star Farmer and win the government contract.

In Star Farmers players are flicking their ships across a flat surface in an attempt to get as close as possible to a set of stars numbered 1 to 5. Players get points for being the closest or second closest at each star. Once any players have scored on each of the 5 stars points are tallied and a winner is declared.

The first edition of Star Farmers is included on the back side of the cards in the first edition of Bocce Piatta.

There are stars that have been determined to be causing problems with the balance of the universe. Pilots have been tasked with traveling to these stars and firing weapons at them in an attempt to destroy them and set the balance right.

Star Slayers is a dexterity game where players are tossing, throwing or sliding 3 Star Slayer ship cards at 5 stars with point values on them from 1 to 5. The ships only fire in certain directions. A line is traced from the weapons to the stars to determine if points are gained.

The first edition of Star Slayers is included on the back side of the cards from the first edition of Bocce Piatta.

Quarters is a dexterity game that is played in the same manner as the popular drinking game. Quarters includes 3 beer cup cards that players attempt to bounce their discs onto in order to give negative points to another player of their choice. When any player gets to 13 points the player with the least points wins the game.

The first edition of quarters is included on the back side of the cards in the first edition of Bocce Piatta.

Get Ready for Some Cosmic Fun Because Alien Run is Out of This World! Think you have a plan?  Think again!  Strategic thinking will be challenged around every bend.

What is Alien Run? Alien Run is a fast-paced strategic board game full of bizarre twist and turns. You can either play independent or with allies. During the race each player can be detoured by an opponent. The play of a Trump Card, Mystery Card, or landing on a Wormhole can send you into a galactic U-Turn. The secret to success is strategy. The player with the most orbs wins the game. It may sound easy, but it's not. Your strategic thinking will be challenged around every bend.

Each Player begins the game without Orbs, Trump Cards or Mystery Cards. That's right, empty handed. Players can collect Orbs from either game spaces, other players, Trump Cards, or Mystery Cards.  Players have the opportunity to purchase Trump Cards from the bank to add to their arsenal, however there are purchasing rules.  The Trump Cards are used for offensive and defensive plays during the game play. The secret to success is strategy, so keep your cards hidden and try to make some allies.  Remember, it's not about who finishes first but who finishes with the most Orbs.  The enticing bonus structure maybe just what a player needs to finish in the lead and be declared the winner. So get the popcorn ready, four players can finish in forty-five minutes. Game night will never be the same.

I almost forgot....

Don't get distracted by the hidden images in the game board. What a player misses can send them orbiting in the wrong direction. Stay focused and Alien Run victory can be had.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Pókháló ("Spider-web") is a simple, enjoyable and funny family, children and party game.

The goal of the game is to escape from the spider's web with our chosen fly hero. The first one who escape succesfully wins the match. But it is not that easy to escape...

There is a double-sided board in the box. Each side is for different game variants and with different complexity. There is a simpler side (which is called "Legyező" and recommended for players from 3-4 years) on which players can play a quick and easy game variant because all events (such as reroll, bonus steps etc.) are drawn on the board. The other side of the board (which is called "Cselszövő" and recommended for players from 7-8 years) is for the more complex game variants. Game variants that are played on the "Cselszövő" side need action cards (the box contains 40 action cards with some different effects). The game rules book contains 2 more game variants (for exemple one for the pro-gamers with challenging complexity) so the constant fun for everyone is guaranteed.

NO! is a quick and unpredictable turn-based casual card game.

At the beginning of the game, shuffle the 100 available cards and equally split them among the players. Everybody draws one card and a random player starts the game.

In your turn, flip the top card of your deck. Every card indicates how the turn passes from one player to the other, so the next player to play is simply the one indicated by the arrow you flipped.

If you have at least one card in your hand, right after somebody else's move you can shout NO! and play one card from your hand on top of the last card played by the active player. This way, that card will be the one to be followed now.

The first player who flips the last card of his deck wins the game.

Straight from minimalist Japan, FLIP MMCG is the ultimate resolution system. Flipping a coin... without the coin. Rock, paper, scissors... without the scissors. Awesome ice breaker at conventions, classrooms, parties, game nights, road trips, and picnics.

FLIP MMCG is a quick and simple competitive game for 2 or more players, by Charles Ward. The goal of the game is to gain as many FLIP cards as possible or be the last player in the game. Ignoring turn order, 2 players face off against each other. One player holds out a card, the other flips it over. The person on the blue side of the card wins, and takes a card from the loser.

The year is 1858 in Carson City, Nevada. You have rounded up a team of courageous cowboys, and your plan is to buy up the best parcels of land in this new town, then build them up with the most prosperous ranches, mines, saloons, etc.

Carson City: Big Box is a collection of the Carson City base game, the Gold & Guns expansion, and the upcoming Horses & Heroes expansion. The game board has been redesigned and updated to support a sixth player and the Horses & Heroes expansion. The game is played in four rounds, and in each one of them, the players choose one of the characters, which give certain advantages. When several players claim the same action, a duel is fought, and the player with the most firepower wins the action. In the end, the most prominent citizen in Carson City — as measured by victory points that can be won both during and after the game — wins.

Carson City: Gold & Guns, the first expansion, contains updated buildings and houses, new buildings, new double-sided characters, the "Indian" character (previously a promotional item at Spiel 2010), and a separate expansion called "The Outlaws".

Carson City: Horses & Heroes, the second expansion, lets players visit the rodeo to claim additional victory points and use horses to unlock special actions. Three new characters are also added to the game.

Don't Be That Guy is a casual party-style card game which highlights the annoying natures of other people. Each card presents a situation that is found by most people to be irritating (Examples: Driving slowly in the left lane, Teaching someone's child to swear, etc).

Game play is as follows: (Normal Rules)
One player reads the card and shows it to the other players. 
Each player votes to see which player is most likely to be guilty of this bad habit. The person with the most votes is "That Guy" and receives the card. The first person who hits 7 cards loses, as the game is called "DON'T be that guy".

Alternate Rules:
For groups where people are not as familiar with each other, before the first card is read, each player chooses a famous person (real or fictional, male or female, etc) and plays as that person for the rest of the game.
Mittlere is an interesting Jass point-trick taking game for three players. What makes this Jass stand out is that players attempt to avoid having the middle score.

The players move their pawns around the board. Movement is determined by a die. Each player controls two character cards (one male and one female) with different relation factors and special abilities. If the relation factors of the two cards complement each other, the player gets money, when he gets on a space "Beziehung". If not, he loses money.

On some spaces the players can buy or sell oil wells or draw event-cards. If a player gets on a space "Zentrum der Macht" he gets the corresponding card, which grants special advantages. The player keeps the card till he loses it or another player takes it. If a player gets on a character-space for a character he doesn't own, he has to swap the character for his own card of the same gender with the owner of that character.

The first player to own $ 3000000 wins the game.

description:
Relative Paradox could best be described as a Deck Building Game, with mechanics including Strategy, Hand Management, and a little bit of Luck. The point is to match up History A with People B and multiplier/bonus for History C with Cosmos and Paradox Cards create "trouble" that alter history all together. All the cards are easily recognizable to their time in history, so you don't need to be a Historian to play. This game features all Public Domain Time Travelers including Professor Helvetius and Doctor Omega.

2 to 8 Players will have to take their Traveler into Time and Space to collect pieces of history and put them back together. First players will be dealt one time traveler to represent them during the game, these are Traveler Cards, The Traveler you are dealt is who you will represent throughout the game. Then each player is Dealt Five Cards from the main deck, the remainder are separated into one pile per the amount of players (i.e. 2 somewhat even piles for two players) and a card is placed face up on all sides (see the image above), these cards are now obtainable.

Each turn you can either create a timeline (matching cards with same symbols) or draw a card from the tabletop that is obtainable. A player can have as many or as few cards as needed in their hand at one time. When you make a pair, you do not draw to replenish. Also, if you have a timeline available to lay--you must use it and lay your pair/three of a kind. Each Timeline Card will have a value that will count towards your final tally, these are called Timeline Points. Each Traveler interacts with a particular timeline differently, either giving them a bonus/multiplier for Timeline Points or immunity from a certain Paradox.

A new game by Masao Suganuma, of Machi Koro's fame, based on the eponymous manga by Kazuhiko Shimamoto, which is basically a parody of the "Super Sentai" series, better known in America for its "Power Rangers" version.

In this game, players are would-be heroes working for the Hero Company, which such fancy names as "Choco-Red", "Soft-Yellow" or "Macaron-Navy". Each turn, which represent a week of game time, they try to resolve three different cases of increasing difficulty (ending with a final battle of supposedly epic proportions), in order to get the best grades from the company. Each hero can only try to resolve one of those, though heroes can queue to resolve the same case. Some cases only rewards the player who actually resolved it (ie, beat the threshold to resolve it with the die/dice), and others rewards every player who participate.

To spice up things, each hero has a hand of skill cards to either boost his capacities or mess up his partners' plan.

While it is important to cooperate to overcome the different cases, in the end, there can be only one winner, so this isn't a cooperative game.

boardgame: Hey Bartender!: The Cocktail Themed Party Game
description:
Hey Bartender! is a cocktail-themed party game for 2-4 players. Players move around the game board collecting ingredients, and develop their world-famous cocktails, with the goal of gathering and matching various ingredients to shake their cocktails. Get ready now for the challenge from your family, friends, and lovers.

How to Play Instruction

1) PREPARATION: Place the game board in the center of the playing area Each player receive 1 chip board, and choose one character to represent him/her while traveling around the board .

2) BARTENDER: Select as Bartender a player who will also maintain the supplies and inventory for the bar. A Bartender who plays in the game must keep his/her chip board separate from the bar board.

3) BARBOARD: The bar boards holds all ingredient chips, cocktail cards, shakers, and cocktail chips. The board supplies ingredient chips to players and exchange cock-tail chips to player upon completing every single cocktail. If the Bar runs of out ingredients, the Bartender is allowed to issue ingredients needed by writing on any ordinary paper.

4) THE PLAY: Starting with the Bartender, each player in turn throws the dice. The player with the highest total starts the play: Place your character on the corner marked “OPEN/START”, throw the dice and move your characters in the direction of the arrow the number of spaces indicated by the dice. After you have completed your play, the character turn passes to the left. Two or more characters may rest on the same space at the same time. According to the space your character reaches, you are entitled to obtain the ingredient designated in the space or follow instruction stated in the space such as Chance, Give, Take, or obliged to rest a turn, jump to any space, or to exchange cocktail card, etc.

5) WHO WIN? The first player to complete all their cocktail cards in hand. If time limit of a game play is set, player with the highest combined total scores of ingredient in hand (please refer to score table) will be the winner. Any completed cocktail chip is awarded 10 bonus points.

Play Option 1: The first player to complete all their cocktail cards in hand wins.
Play Option 2: If time limit of a game play is set (average of 25 minutes), player with the highest combined total scores of ingredient in hand wins.

Players must dodge and utilize Bartender's instructions as it may force you to give or take ingredient chips from other players to make this game unpredictable.

Introduction
Old legends say that a long time ago gods lived in the great boreal forest of Canada. If one got lost in the woods, one might have seen among the trees one of their totems, which were sacred objects they used to channel their energy. The legends also say that the gods loved to play and that, every few years, they would transform the entire forest into their playground and compete for control of these totems.

Borealis is an epic game of conquest in which players represent old gods of the boreal forest, trying to capture each other's totems using units with various abilities. It is played on a modular hex-based board, which is created anew at the start of each game by the players and can be transformed during it. It lasts 9 rounds, and to each round is assigned a season, summer or winter, which affects the units' movements and the arrival of reinforcements.

Goal
Be the player with the most Victory Points at the end of the 9th round. At the end of each round, a Victory Point is awarded to the player who controls the most totems. If there's a tie, no Victory Point is awarded. If, at any point, a player's victory becomes inevitable because his or her point lead is greater than the number of rounds remaining, the game should end then.

User summary

The game consists of 10 thick wooden tiles with the front and back of each tile having one of five patterns (suits). Each tile has different patterns on the front and back, and no two tiles are alike.  Thus, each pattern appears four times.  So, for example, each of the four tiles showing the rose pattern has a different one of the remaining four patterns on the reverse side.

The tiles are randomly placed on the table on their sides on so that each player sees exactly one of the two patterned faces of each tile (the players sit across from each other). The first player rotates one tile 90 degrees so that it is standing up.  Next, the other player topples it either towards or away from herself (she chooses) so that one of the two patterned sides is now face up.  She then puts the tile into a pile on the side of the table so that the revealed patterned side remains face up.  After completing these steps, the players reverse roles.

The goal of the game is to build a complete 2-D tile pyramid, with four tiles in the bottom layer, three tiles in the second layer, two tiles in the third layer and one tile on top. When building on the second or higher layers, a tile can only be placed if it matches the face up pattern of one of the tiles it is being placed on.  The pyramid can be built in any order (e.g., work on the second layer can begin before the bottom layer is completed).

Players may not communicate verbally or otherwise.

On a player's turn, they can either exchange their date spot with one of the open ones, or put another personality on a character, or show their hand card (date spot) and pick two characters who will date on that spot. War of Wonders is a skirmish miniature game set in an alternate, grim World War II fought by superhuman beings.

Details:
War of Wonders Miniature Game is a fast paced 30mm strategic skirmish game based on the deeply immersive setting of Project H.O.P.E. RPG by authors Simone Fini and Riccardo Giuliani, published by Limana Press.
In War of Wonders extraordinary human beings with powers beyond imagination fight on WW II battlefields alongside regular troops and iconic heavy tanks to change History. National heroes, monsters born in laboratories and mysterious beings with their own secret goals: every and each of them will add new tactics, game options and strategies to your games.

Setting:
Nazi Germany declares war on Europe, and its army is unstoppable. Prodigious beings appear on the battlefields to support the Wehrmacht's efforts: flying men shower flames upon the enemy; women wearing iron masks punch through tank armour; bloodthirsty beasts devour entire regiments. The world is about to succumb to the might of the Third Reich. Aaron Spiegelmann, a Jewish scientist who escaped from the Mauthausen concentration camp, is the only hope for the Allied Forces to stop the Nazis. Spiegelmann is the genius who created the Wunderkammer, a new technology that allowed the Germans to create real superheroes with extraordinary powers. With his guidance, the US Army launches Project H.O.P.E. in order to produce their own genetically modified humans to be deployed on the battlefields. As the war rages on and every army creates its own Meta-Humans and war machines, Natural Prodigies, unscrupulous Adventurers and even secretive groups of Warlocks step into battle. From the European battlefields to the Pacific Islands, a conflict like never before is unleashed and spreads with no boundaries: humanity will forever remember it as the first War of Wonders.

Gear Grinders is a board game where players control one of four unique characters maneuvering around an arena to attack the other players. Each character has their own attacks, stats (attack, defense, movement), life, gear slots, gear cost, and steam tank size. Steam is used to power different attacks or shift gears from first to second gear.

The arena has three different steam vents located throughout. A normal steam vent, a gear vent, and a hatch vent. These can be turned on or off with a die roll. When the vents are on, moving over them can increase the amount of steam in your tank but at the cost of some life. Gear cards could appear on the gear vent with a die roll. The hatch vents are used to move a player from the gear vent.

Gear cards are equipped by players to modify the player's stats. Playing cards are used to help launch attacks or defend. Combat is determined by a die roll adding any modifiers and comparing it to the defender's defense with their modifiers.

The game ends when there is only one person left alive.

Gear Grinders is the first game from Roll One Games Limited.

Game time is 30 minutes to 2 hours depending on the number of players and the aggressiveness of the game play.

family: Country: Democratic Republic of the Congo

description:
This expansion adds thirty-two unique kittens to the game in two new groups, allowing for play with up to six players (or teams). It also has color-reminder cards, and flavors.

Flavors add a new dimension to the strategic play of Kittens in a Blender. If you are having trouble rescuing your kittens, you really really can't keep them out of the blender, throw some vanilla extract or strawberries into the mix and make a smoothie! It's a new way to score points toward victory, and now other players have to think long and hard before sending too many of your kittens toward the evil blender.

User summary

Players are all girls fighting a dragon with their swords. On a player's turn, he will roll a die to attack the dragon, and if successful, may add new cards to his sword, making it longer. The player may also play cards for special effects.

The dragon will attack the girls each round. The goal is to remain standing when the Final Round starts (round 7, if not triggered earlier), and defeat the dragon. The player with most points at the end wins.

Each player has a set of cards with votes and scandals on and will play these onto the other members of the group. Each player also has one member they openly support.

Of the set cards each player has, one is Backstage Deal, which alows the player to play cards on a specific sheet, where each played cards will have various effects.

The player with the most votes at the end of the game wins.

These appeal cards must match the requirements on the popularity sheet and come in a specific order, in order to generate a specific amount of sales. The goal of the game is to get as high sales as possible

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Heavy Cardboard
description:
This map was put together to commemorate the 1 year anniversary of Heavy Cardboard podcast, a podcast about heavy boardgames. The city and town names used on this map are boardgames featured or discussed in Heavy Cardboard podcasts.

This map is intended to be fairly open with plenty of space for route building and goods availability. The Heavy Cardboard hex in the center of the map acts as a cube fountain generating new goods every round making it an important location as there is no production action with this map.

A new special action, "Heavy Lifting", adds the ability to deliver over land between Cities. This adds some interesting decisions for players during the game.

ダイス 木 (Daisuki) is a game in which the players take on the role of lumberjacks, chopping down trees in the mountain forest in order to collect wood and build houses. Along the way, they will run into fires and termites, but the lumberjack who can hold onto the most wood and build the best buildings will win!

The game starts with a mountain made of wood disks. Players take turns drawing a disk at random and revealing what they have "harvested" from the forest. As players start controling more wood, they can use those wood chips to buy better equipment, put out fires, build houses, etc. It's a chance and dice game of press-your-luck with a little bit of attack-your-opponents as well!

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Treachery of Rhudaur
description:
From the publisher's website:

"A great host came out of Angmar in 1409, and crossing the river entered Cardolan and surrounded Weathertop. The Dúnedain were defeated and Arveleg was slain. The Tower of Amon Sûl was burned and razed; but the palantír was saved and carried back in retreat to Fornost, Rhudaur was occupied by evil Men subject to Angmar, and the Dúnedain that remained there were slain or fled west."
     –J.R.R. Tolkien, "Eriador, Arnor, and the Heirs of Isildur"

Accompanied by the young Dúnedain Ranger Amarthíul, a handful of Middle-earth's greatest heroes make their way out of the deadly troll-fells and into the crumbling ruins of Rhudaur. Their path leads them past piece after piece of evidence of the region's historic collapse; they cross through tattered cities and make their way around towers of stone and rubble, long forgotten and ravaged by years of disrepair.

At length, even as they can still sense the evils that have pursued them since the dungeons of Mount Gram, the heroes come across the remnants of a keep marked by the same symbol that appears on the pendant that Amarthíul inherited from the captured Ranger, Iárion. They are not safe here, and time is pressing. Still, the heroes realize this ruined keep might hold some clue to the threat currently growing in the North. Despite the dangers, they decide to brave its foreboding stairwells and darkened halls in search of whatever secrets it may yield…
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
The next supplement for the Battlegroup World War II gaming rules covering the invasion of Poland in 1939 and France in 1940.

Designed to be played in 20mm and 15mm, this latest book contains army lists, vehicle data and special rules for the first two years of the war, including France’s command chaos, the Luftwaffe's aerial superiority and the swift German Panzermarsch!

Nine new army lists for the early war fighting. The Polish and German armies for 1939 and German Panzer and Infantry Divisions to fight French Light Mechanized Divisions, Armoured Divisions and Infantry Divisions, the British Expeditionary Force and Belgian army in 1940.

Nine historical scenarios from Poland to Belgium and France. All in a lavish 184 page, hardback book. Heavily illustrated with inspiring miniature photographs, historical photographs and artwork.

boardgame: SAVAGE WAR OF PEACE: A Solitaire Game of the Algerian War (1954-1962).
description:
SAVAGE WAR OF PEACE is a solitaire game of Combat during the French-Algerian War (1954-1962). The player assumes the role of a commander of combined French forces in Algeria. As commander, you are responsible for securing your Area of Operations (AOR) by successfully conducting various military operations in order to locate and destroy the enemy. Although there are many units available to you, they may often times be limited to you by political, logistical or geographical obstacles.

GAME OBJECTIVE
The objective of SAVAGE WAR OF PEACE is to successfully complete a number of missions and destroy enemy forces in order to gain Victory Points (VP) for the French Forces. The player can choose to play a short game (4 Missions), an average game (6 Missions), a long game (8 Missions) or a full Tour (12 Missions). Once all Missions have been resolved (successfully or not) the Player totals his VP and compares this with the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success.

boardgame: Fireforce: Rhodesia – A Solitaire Game of the Rhodesian War (1964-1979)
description:
FIREFORCE: RHODESIA is a solitaire game of the Rhodesian War. The player assumes the role of a Commander of a combined Force of Rhodesian Security Forces, including the Rhodesian Light Infantry (RLI), Rhodesian SAS and the Elite, Selous Scouts. As commander, you are responsible for securing your Area of Operations (AOR) by successfully conducting various military operations in order to locate and destroy the enemy ZANLA and ZIRPA forces. Although there are many units available to you, they may often times be limited to you by political, logistical or geographical obstacles.

GAME OBJECTIVE 
The objective of FIREFORCE: RHODESIA is to successfully complete a number of missions and destroy enemy forces in order to gain Victory Points (VP) for the Security Forces. The player can choose to play a short game (4 Missions), an average game (6 Missions), a long game (8 Missions) or a full Tour (12 Missions). Once all Missions have been resolved (successfully or not) the Player totals his VP and compares this with the Victory Determination Table to determine the level of success.

boardgame: TRADITION OF GLORY: A Game of Naval Adventure during the Age of Nelson
description:
INTRODUCTION
The year is 1795 and France is gathering their Forces for their Armies and Navy in a grand attempt to conquer Europe. It is also a time of revolutions, imperialism, piracy and political intrigue. In this game, you start out as a young Officer serving in the British Royal Navy. As you complete missions, you will gain experience, honor and hopefully promotions. In time, you may command a Ship of your own. This game is for fans of the Nautical Fiction Series of Horatio Hornblower, Richard Bolitho and Jack Aubrey.

OBJECTIVE
In TRADITION OF GLORY each player creates a Player Officer starting out in what will, hopefully be, a long and glorious career in the Royal Navy. During, play, Player Officers (POs) will be assigned to Ships and conducting a variety of Missions as they move along the Career Track Game Board. The Career Track consists of Ports, Sea Travel and Ship Duty Assignments. These Assignments will result in a variety of situations and encounters including Naval Battles, Port Assaults, Land Skirmishes, Shore Bombardment as well as a variety of other Missions in which the Player’s Officer must resolve. The outcome of these Encounters and Situations determine whether the Player’s Officer advances along the Career Track. While the Officers conduct Challenges, they might gain or lose Merit Points, which are used to improve their Attribute Ratings and Rank. The Player Officer with the Highest Rank after reaching the Ending Space on the Career Track, is the Winner. If this is tied, then the Officer with the most Merit Points remaining is the Winner. If this is tied then the Officers may conduct a Duel to determine the Winner.

description:
This simple roll-and-move-game was published as an insert in a "Toffifee" Box and promotes the second "Die drei ???" movie as well as "Toffifee".
The first player to reach the diamonds and answers corectly a question from the question card wins the game. On certain spaces events occur, that hinder or benefit the player. If a player gets on a space with a picture, he has to perform an action as described on the action card.

This game was published on a "Toffifee" Box. The players roll a die. At the result of an even number, the player takes a random christmas Ornament, places it on the christmas tree and gets points awarded, as noted on the ornament. The gameeEnds when all ornaments are placed on the tree. The player with the most points wins the game.

Reclaim Neverland is a strategy game that takes place in Peter Pan's Neverland that is under siege by four pirate captains.

Reclaim Neverland is unlike other deckbuilding games because of the combination of PvP elements and a limitation to the size of each players built deck. Similar to its predecessor, Reclaim Oz, It is a unique tabletop experience that has the play differently than any other games in the same genre. We have added items to the game as well, these will work as multipliers and bonuses for specific characters!

The game takes place in Neverland, home to famous characters such as Peter Pan, Tinker Bell, and Tiger Lily, but at a time when a new band of pirates, including Captain James Hook, are mounting a siege on the inhabitants. All the great Heroes of Neverland have disappeared, and four Pirates from the Mainland have taken over. While J. M. Barrie may have written a much happier world, this is still the same place, with all the characters and places you know and love—but in a time of great terror. The game has several animals, beasts, and mythos from the original book Peter and Wendy including characters from Little White Bird—it is your task to get the World back to the way it once was, with the characters you collect along the way. If you are able to compile the best deck filling each of the categories (HERO, BRAVE, LOST), you win!

    History & legends

In A Bind Expansions contains 3.5 expansions that can be combined with the In a Bind base game in whatever combination(s) you like. The expansions are:


    22 wild cards: Someone will make up part of the card, as in a Mad Lib, when it's played.
    10 speech-based triggered binds: You need to say something whenever anyone draws.
    10 physical triggered binds: You need to perform a movement whenever anyone draws.
     12 communal (physical triggered) binds: These either apply to everyone, or to everyone but the person playing the card, depending on whether you're playing "Fast & Furious" or "Slow & Sadistic".

B-Sieged: Heroes Set 1 introduces three brand-new Heroes to defend the Citadel of Modhelm: Seren, the Trickster; Axel, the Engineer; and Eyra, the Thief!

Seren, the Trickster
As a seasoned artist, Seren, the Trickster boasts a very unique and powerful starting Special Ability: Chicanery! Through Chicanery, Seren is allowed to draw 1 Resource card from the Seasons Resources at the beginning of each of her turns, and she can choose from any of the four types (Armory, Tavern, Market or Sorcery). This ability frees her up from visiting the Citadel's buildings as frequently as other Heroes, and gives the Heroes a way to get at least one Resource card per turn. Note that unlike Kador's Archmastery or Orobox's Intimidation skills, Seren may only draw from the Season Resources, which drains from the Heroes' common resources and makes her options limited if the Season resource decks are depleted.

Axel, the Engineer
As an Engineer, Axel comes with the Royal Artilleryman starting Special Ability. With it, Axel gets a free action per turn to either Rotate or Shoot the Catapult, and it can help the Heroes in crucial moments! Each Catapult shot may kill up to three enemies and it has a Strength of 4, so it can kill Mölens or severely damage the Avatar! The option to rotate the catapult is also important because Axel may use it to aim the catapult for another Hero, allowing him or her to shoot twice with the catapult in their turn if they wish to do so!

Eyra, the Thief
Last, but not least, Eyra the Thief brings two starting Special Abilities that emphasize her roguish nature: Filch and Archer's Discipline. With Filch, Eyra "accidentally comes by" one Market resource card at the beginning of each of her turns. These Tavern Resource cards come directly from the Tavern Resource pool, so she may get a card even if the Tavern is damaged or destroyed. With her trusty one-handed crossbow and Archer's Discipline, Eyra is able to shoot with better precision at enemies in Zone 2, making her a valuable asset to help control Krohns and Gargohs and filching Tavern cards every turn!

Back cover text (translated):

"Swop it" means "to trade", and that's exactly what this game is about -- trading clothes with each other! First, let the clothes-spinner determine who you will challenge, and which item of clothing you two will exchange with each other, then the countdown begins. Let the clothes fly! The person who first succeeds at removing their piece of clothing and putting on the exchanged piece gets to go forward on the gameboard.


The game is played to 23 points (with more points given out in a round if the winner finishes trading clothes before the timer runs out).

boardgame: An Ideal Necklace: A Victorian Gabbing and Gifting Game
description:
Victorian Ladies are on the hunt for fashion and husbands, each seeking to achieve her own personal ambition in An Ideal Necklace, a quick-moving game for one to four players.

Social engineering or by-the-numbers logical deduction are equally valid strategies as is everything in-between. Cards are visible to all, who may talk and influence other players' actions. Alliances may be made, or broken. Each turn, a Lady may: (1) select an event or a necklace from the Store; (2) attend Charm School to gain Charm; (3) gift a necklace to another Lady to earn Favor; (4) teach another Lady Charm in return for a necklace; (5) use Favor to prevent another Lady from acting.

Events are opportunities that may be acquired in lieu of selecting a necklace and they provide special powers, like the ability to recover Discarded necklaces or to Woo a Suitor twice, acquiring him as a husband.

Ladies gift necklaces to others to earn Favor, which is used, along with Charm, to Woo a Suitor, of which there are four. Once a Suitor has been Wooed twice, a Lady acquires him as a husband and if she has also achieved her Ambition, she earns bonus points and ends the game. Ambitions are goals, like acquiring the most necklaces, or gaining the most Favor.

Points are earned for: achieving an Ambition, ending the game, capturing a husband, possessing Charm, and for owning necklaces--some of which are especially valuable! The Advanced variation, also included, adds Suitor Traits, which vary Suitors' point values and which add special abilities.

Throughout, each player gabs and gifts their way to success, wooing Lords and giving necklaces to each other, attempting to attain An Ideal Necklace and An Ideal Husband as each Lady quests for An Ideal Victorian Life.

Game 1: see Little Burro Chase Game

Det är också ett lagarbete där flera kockar måste samarbeta och argumentera för de ingredienser de lägger till. Så vad du än kokar ihop, så måste du också lyckas övertyga medspelarna om att smaka på det…"

The first player to manoeuver four pieces in a square or continuous straight line (in any direction) is the winner.

boardgame: Fateful Days: The Marne Campaign of 1914

This is the third game in the TPS of the Pocket_Battle_Games from LPS, Inc.

Erratum: In scenario #1, "Hatten & Rittershofen" the Mobile Artillery Unit (bottom left of the board) should be an Allied unit, not an Axis one (the Special Rules text is correct).

description:
The world's most powerful criminal organisations have come together in a struggle for control of a rich and corrupt city. Hire Goons, make alliances, and hit the right Bosses to install a new Kingpin and win this game of strategy.
Hiring Goons gives you more cards to use.
Making alliances with Bosses lets you use their Ally Powers (many powerful combinations are possible!).(this is an engine building game)
Putting out a hit on a Boss gives you points (unless it is from the same family as the Kingpin.)
At the end of the game, each player gets 1 point for each Goon they control, 2 points for each Ally they have made, and Bosses they have hit are worth points equal to their hit number (unless they are from the kingpin's family). Most points wins.

boardgame: The Little Big Game of Moocho Sudooko
description: The Big Little Game of Moocho Sudooko turns sudoku puzzles into a board game. The board is a 9x9 square divided into 9 grids of 9. Each player gets 27 tiles (3 sets with the digits 1 to 9) in one of four colours. At the beginning of each turn the player rolls the dice and uses the result to decide which tile to play. A tile can be placed in any spot as long as there are not two identical numbers in the same row, column, or grid square. If the player cannot make a legal move, or the player does not play before the timer runs out, play passes to the next player. Play continues until there are no legal plays that can be made. (Alternatively, players may shift (but not remove) tiles to allow further plays.) The player with the fewest remaining tiles is the winner. In case of a tie, the numbers on the remaining tiles are added and the player with the lowest total wins.
