description:
Basra is a fishing standard deck playing card game similar to Casino. The game plays with 2, 3 or 4 players. 4-player game are usually played in partnerships.

Each player is dealt four cards and four cards are played to the table making the "floor". Jacks and 7's of diamond from the floor are shuffled back into the deck and replaced. Players take turns playing one card to the floor and capturing cards if possible. When each player has played all four cards, the dealer deals out four more to each player, unless the deck is empty in which case the round is scored.

Captures can be made by playing a card of the same rank or a card that sums to two or more cards. Multiple captures are possible, for example a 10 would capture a 3, 7 and 10 from the floor. Queens and Kings do not have numerical value so the only way to capture them is by playing the same rank or a special card.

Players earn a Basra, worth 10 points, by capturing all the cards remaining on the floor . Players keep track of this by keeping the capturing card face up in their point pile. Jacks capture all cards on the floor, but do not count as a Basra. Jacks played on an empty floor capture nothing and remain on the floor. The seven of diamonds plays just like a jack except it earns a Basra if the captured cards add up to 10 or less. After the last card is played, remaining cards on the floor go to the player who made the last capture.

Scoring
The player (or team in partnerships) with the most cards receives 30 points. Ties are pushed to the next round (so 60 points could be earned).
Jacks and aces are worth 1 point. 
The two of clubs is worth 2 points.
The ten of diamonds is worth 3 points.
Basra's are worth 10 points each.

A new hand is dealt unless someone has a total score of 101 in which case the player with the highest score wins. In case of a tie a new hand is played.

This expansion for ARCADE allows players to create larger maps.

Components:


    Two 3x5 terrain tiles
    One 1x5 terrain tile
    9 'ice' squared walls
    20 terrain destruction cards
    VHS box


The terrain tiles are made of 3mm thick laser-cut acrylic (lines engraved). The walls are made of 8mm thick 'ice' acrylic.

Home Raiders is a game played with small scale models. These models represent the combatants that travel from a dimension known as the Inner-space to your very home, in order to battle for the dominance of your space. These fighters are little folk (which means that the scale of the models is actually 1:1) which exist in dimensions parallel to ours, and crash our world in order to thrive in theirs.

The basic game is very easy to grasp, and is a great option for a fast game that takes place in your actual living room coffee table, your office desk, or even around your kitchen sink. It takes no time at all to set up, so you can get your dice rolling in a breeze. If you are an experienced gamer, Home Raiders is a great choice for an improvised gaming session or even a well laid out match amongst a bunch of friends. If you want to play with your younger brothers and sisters, cousins or even your children, you won't find a better option to getting them started with this amazing hobby.

The Road is a post-apocalyptic survival card game set in the zombie-infested Australian outback.

It is a 3-5- player game which explores the social-dynamics of balancing group welfare against self-interest. You need to help each other in order to survive, but at the end of the day you must make ruthless decisions about who lives and dies.

As the name suggests, you are travelling down a road through the outback, attempting to reach the airfield which is your hope of escape. The game proceeds in a series of "days". Each day you choose the next location to explore, fight some zombies and scavenge for useful items. Each night you must decide who you can trust to stand guard while the others sleep.

As the days wear on, hunger and tiredness build, supplies run short, and you face a difficult decision: whether to remain loyal to the group, to run, or to turn on your fellow players (before they turn on you).

The Archmage is dying... a successor must be found from the 4 schools of magic, so a contest is afoot. The Principal of the school with the most Prestige will be the next Archmage.

Each player controls 10 wizards of one of the 4 different schools (on the base game they would be fire, earth, water & air). In the beginning players start with 4 wizards and add more to their ranks as the game progresses.

Each turn players will have to choose different locations on the board that suits their strategies from gathering resources, to recuiting wizards & heroes, going into quests, creating potions and spells they can use later, and fighting in the terrible DARK TOWER.

Since this game is euro styled, all of this elements are somewhat abstract, so in order to go into a quest you place a wizard on one of the adventure cards and if you pay the resources needed and have all the requirements for the quest to be successful you win the card and any reward that is given.

Heroes are an important part of the wizard guild as they provide extra abilities to combat and enhance how the resources are gathered. They can go up levels (up to 4) and with each level they grant more to the school they work for. Up to 2 levels can be earn in the usual tavern brawls... but the other 2 must be gain in quests or challenging the DARK TOWER.

Archimago has several elements shared by traditional Eurostyle games that use worker placement but at the same time potions and spells provide and interaction with the other players that if mastered can be of a great assistance to the School, events change game conditions, Characters can appear and add a new location to go to, etc.

The base game comes also with 1 scenario that is made of a set of cards that are mixed with the adventure cards and that when are dealt change the conditions of the game or offer sudden opportunities for the players that have the resources to risk or the desire to count on fate for better possibilities.

The game is modular so it is highly expandable in several directions that will add to the system new possibilities and the feeling of adventure, starting with new scenarios that will make each game feel different, characters that will appear in the different locations to assist the players, events that will happen when creating potions and more sudden apparitions that will contribute greatly with the atmosphere of the world.

The game ends on 1 of 4 conditions:


    The adventure cards spots cannot be filled.
    2 or 3 lines of potions/spells are depleted.
    Sometimes a Scenario Card will end the game when certain conditions are given.
    When all the Dark Tower Cards are gone.


In the end Archmage is more than an eurogame, an experience that is worth trying. Each adventure card has been carefully designed so the title leads to the story below. Beyond the abstract elements there is a story going on in the game that those players that are fan of the detail will certainly love.

Spanish description:
El Archimago esta muriendo... un sucesor debe ser encontrado de entre las 4 escuelas de magia, asi que un concurso se realizara. El Rector de la escuela con mayor Prestigio sera el siguiente Archimago.

boardgame: Batman: The Joker Air Race Game
description:
Object: Capture the Joker. The first player to fly from Wayne Manor to the Joker's Flying Fortress wins.

Playing the Game: All players start a Wayne Manor. (green start square) The youngest player spins first and then moves his/her playing piece the number shown on the spinner. Play continues with the next player on the left. The first player to reach the Joker's Flying Fortress (red finish square) wins.

Flying: Players move in a straight line (left-right, up-down, diagonally) one direction per turn. Player may pass each other, but they cannot occupy the same space at the end of the turn. If a player lands on an occupied space, he/she must move one space less in another direction. Players CANNOT fly through buildings, they must fly OVER them.

The Spinner: The spinner is divided into six parts. The five numbered sections (2,3,4,5,6) show how many spaces a player can move during his/her turn. The Joker spinner section activates the "Plane Scrambler" forcing ALL PLAYERS to land on the building space directly below. Player resumes when the next player spins.

JOIN THE CIRCLE:
During the mid-nineteenth century, spiritualism became increasingly popular. Mediums would gather a group of believers for a séance as they attempted to contact the spirits of their dearly departed relatives. Now it’s time for you to create your own circle around the table as you attempt to contact these wispy spirits.

OBJECT OF THE GAME: 
Play cards onto the séance circle to “make contact” with Spirits – contact your spirit by matching its incantation icons or by spelling out the word “Séance”. Collect Spirit cards before they close the circle and end the game.

CONTACTING SPIRITS
You get your choice of five actions:
REPLACE a card in the circle with one from your hand
SWAP two adjacent cards in the circle
PLACE a card from your hand over one that matches its icon
PASS a card to your neighbor
DISCARD your hand to draw back up to five cards

HOW TO WIN: Capture 13 points in spirits wins. In the competitive game, capture friendly or unfriendly 7 Spirit cards to win.

The goal is simple. Choose 1 of 4 unique characters, each with his or her own Golem companion, and beat the ever-loving bejesus out of your friends. The last player left with any bejesus being the winner!

Each character’s arsenal will include the following card types to help them achieve victory: Combat, Combo, Reflex, and Fusion cards. Most of the damage you’ll deal will be in the form of Combat and Combo cards.

description:
Each player chooses a Disney character He puts the character’s picture in front of him and pushes out all the pieces; eyes, gloves, pants, shoes, hat and jacket.
Players take turns to flick the Spinner round the Disney Circle. If the pointer comes to rest on a jacket, the player places the jacket on his character. If it comes to rest on a shoe, he puts one shoe on his character and so on. If the character already has the item on which the pointer has come to rest, the player doesn't score that time round. If the pointer comes to rest on a star the player may choose any one item and put it in position. The first player to complete dressing his character wins the game.

All around him, as far as you can look, you see huge sand dunes and arid country with only here and there as if randomly strewn around rocks and scrubby bushes. From the clear blue sky of September and burning sun,shadows here and there is a unbearably hot. But you do not have time to devote to watching these natural phenomena, because You must quickly navigate the terrain and to keep in mind only one thing: to be first in line! As it has many times proved Karel Loprais and his Tatra. Take care not to straying or not fit in the sand and go forward as quickly as possible.

Players throw the dice and move their pieces around the board. Along the way they must cope with fields having different kinds of effects (malfunction, ford ...). Crowned the winner is the player who first reach the finish with everyone figures.

Каркассон: Богатырь на распутье & Избушка (Carcassonne: Hero at the Crossroads & Baba Yaga) is a two-tile semi-unofficial expansion for Carcassonne made by Russian publisher Hobby World and presented to participants of the Russian national Carcassonne league in 2013. One tile represents Bogatyr, a Russian hero from folktales, at a crossroads, while the other tile represents Izbushka, the small hut of Baba Yaga the witch, also from Russian folktales.

Bogatyr's tile has no special rules, but it does feature text on a stone that might be translated as follows:

If you go to the left - you'll find death.
If you go to the right - you'll lose your horse.
If you go straight - you'll live, but will forget who you are.

The Izbushka tile scores somewhat like an anti-cloister as it's worth 1 point for the tile itself and 1 additional point for each empty space around the tile, for a maximum of 8 points.

description:
Join a brave party of adventurers in search of treasures and fame! Explore consecutively three dungeons filled with wandering monsters, traps and treasure, and survive to get the honor and glory.

Unlimited Adventures is a dungeon delve for 1-3 players and 20-30 minutes long, with a great deal of planning, bluffing and memory.

In Unlimited Aventures, each player takes the role of a different adventurer. You'll need to work together to survive until consecutively three dungeons, but only one of you will make it out with the enough treasure and win the game! The dungeons are different each time you play, offering new surprises as you collect treasure, trigger abilities, and fight off horrible wandering monsters!

It is a game where you have to be able to plan your incursion into the Dungeon optimally to avoid ending up dead or vilified by your opponent.

Unlimited Adventures can also be played as a solitaire game.

A funny game that helps children to understand the job of post office men, and the different kinds of letters, parcels etc.

The game is played with 2-6 players. At the start of the game you receive some cards which represent the letters and parcels. The number of the cards depends on the number of players. Then you have to roll the dice and move in the board, in the direction which is the fastest and more convenient for you to deliver the letters. But some times you may land on "best" spots, which might not be the best for you! The winner of the game is whoever delivers all that he's got and get back to the post office first!

description:
Imperial Settlers: Atlanteans, an expansion that requires the base game to play, adds the option for playing Imperial Settlers with up to five players. The expansion includes a new faction board, the Atlanteans faction deck, technology tokens, and expansion cards for the original factions that complement the Atlanteans abilities.

The Atlanteans bring a new resource to the world of Imperial Settlers with technology that can unlock special abilities on faction cards and transform common buildings into powerful allies. The Atlanteans must use their abilities to find unique ways to score because their faction buildings sink at the end of the game and do not score points! While the other factions do not have the knowledge to use the technology of the Atlanteans, some buildings for each faction allow them to use some of that technology to their advantage.

The title refers to a 'level' of an alarm. Level 1 might be helping a cat down a tree but Level 6 might be a massive fire at a chemicals factory.

The board is a track of 60 spaces and, in that regard, it is a 'Goose-Type' game. Throw the die and move along the track and if you land on a space with instructions, you must obey those instructions. It has a few differences to common games of this type. Some spaces 'attract' other players' pieces to come to help you with an Alarm. This in effect means that other people will be helped forward if they are behind or brought back if they are in front. First to get to space 60, wins.

The playing pieces are small metallic fire-engines.

description:
All across America trucks rumble across the interstate carrying products from manufacturers to retailers. As a small start-up company with a truck, an office and a dream you are throwing you hat into the ring to challenge the big boys. The freight has to be moved and there is competition for who can get to loads first and who can make the most profitable routes. But watch out, Smokey is on the prowl. So put the hammer down and let it all hang out. You have to get loaded up & truckin’.

As a player, you represent a small but growing trucking company seeking to pick up and deliver goods in the most efficient manner possible. Amassing money is one way to achieve victory, but there are other ways to score as well. It is important to dominate a region or two with the most deliveries while not ignoring the other regions. In the end, your deliveries will be compared to the other players to determine which company has the best overall presence in the US.

Players gain presence by moving their trucks from city to city across the United States picking up and dropping off deliveries along the way. You can specialize in high profit cross-country runs or you can make numerous short routes. On each turn the vagaries of the business will be determined by a deck of event cards. Each player will then get to buy new hubs and trucks before setting out to scoop up goods before a rival comes along and 'steals' the load.

boardgame: The Little Big Game of Word Maven
description:
The Little Big Game of Word Maven consists of 6 letter dice, a dice cup, paper money and a sand timer. One die is a vowel die, with a e i o u and an 8-ball on the faces. Five dice have a mix of consonants, vowels and wild sides.  Players roll the vowel die to determine who goes first. Each player receives starting money from the bank and a Jackpot Fund is started. 
On their turn each player rolls all 6 dice. The goal is to make the longest word possible with the letters on the face of the dice. The player may roll up to 3 times per turn, but must keep at least one die per roll. The first roll is free; money must be paid to the Jackpot Fund for each extra roll. If the 8-ball face is rolled the player's turn is immediately over. Each turn must be finished before the timer runs out.

Scoring is based on the length of the word the player makes. Longer words pay more money. The Jackpot is won when a player makes a 6 letter word without using any wild sides. The winner is the player who has the most money at the end of a predetermined time, or the player who reaches a predetermined amount of money first. Additionally, each die has an orange letter on one face that spells the word “MAVENS”. Spelling MAVENS instantly wins the game.

A 2-4 player board game that combines elements of Tower Defense and Dice Rolling to see who rules the seven seas. The name of the game is eliminating your opponents and getting the most loot as possible while in the process of taking over the Caribbean. You will sail throughout the board, discovering treasure, obtaining island defenses, and trying to survive a War on Piracy--all while avoiding the dreaded Curse of Neptune.

Each player is dealt a Pirate Captain and a Ship--which will give you a total hit point value for the game. Avoid the cannons, find the treasure, defeat your opponents.

The fortress has fallen. The battle lost. The forces are scattered. You're on the run! You can't tell who is friend or foe. Suddenly you hear a noise! Your enemy is upon you! You have to attack! But with what? You've forgotten his weakness! No matter, attack with all you have! Pray you'll go down with honor and your ancestors will be forgiving. Attack!

Samurai Rundown is a tile based player versus player game with card based battles. Players start on a tile based map and go around collecting items and weapons, and revealing map tiles. When a player thinks they have enough weapons, they go after their enemy to attack them. When they are adjacent to their enemy, they initiate a card battle. The loser of the card battle loses a heart. When a samurai loses 3 hearts they die.

Be the last samurai standing to win.

Samurai Rundown also has a showdown mode. When all the weapons are found and drawn. All samurai drop their hearts to one from being exhausted and head to the nearest temple to duel. The samurai then battle to the death. The last samurai standing here is the winner.

Be the last samurai standing.

You're the manager playing against your division rivals, selecting pitches, organizing batting lineups, and  developing a winning strategy. Each at bat begins with a 3-2 count and one pitch decides the outcome of the play. Full Count Baseball features exciting scenarios, including stolen bases, double plays and pickoffs. The game features four logoed game pieces for all MLB teams. The game board resembles a real ball park with fans in the stands and dugouts. A dry-erase scoreboard and marker are also included. Instructions are simple and easy to learn for players of all ages.

41 (or Forty-One) is a traditional card game commonly played in Syria and Lebanon.  It is a partnership trick-taking game, in the same family as Whist and Boston, with a substantial difference from most such games: players bid and score individually.

Bidding and play is counter-clockwise, and traditionally trumps are always Hearts.  In turn, each player bids a number of tricks they expect to take.  The dealer, who bids last, can potentially cause the hand to be re-dealt by the next player if the total bid score count is less than 11.  Play is as in most trick-taking games: match suit if possible, otherwise trump if desired or  throw off; highest card of the suit led wins, unless trump is played, in which case highest trump wins, and winner leads to the next trick.

A player scores a number of points equal to their bid if they get at least that many tricks on their own (so 3 points for bidding 3 and winning 3 or more tricks), and loses that many points if they fail to get their bid (so -3 points for bidding 3 and winning 2 or fewer tricks).  A bid of 7 or above is for double stakes (14/-14 for 7, 16/-16 for 8) and guarantees that the hand is played.

The winning partnership is the first to have a player reach at least 41 with their partner's score above 0.

boardgame: Ether Ore: An Expansion for Far Space Foundry
description:
The Far Space Federation has discovered an uninhabited system in the Ether region of space. The Federation Engineers have determined that a new foundry will be built near the system's rich asteroid belt. A specific batch of asteroids have been found to have deposits of unusual elements. Can you help the Federation benefit from this serendipitous discovery?

The Ether Ore Expansion adds these new items for even more variability in Far Space Foundry:
(4) Ore Cards
(40) Ore Tokens
(3) Product Cards
(18) Product Tokens
(2) Cloth Bags

A dark age approaches the Realm of Atheria –- the vast ancestral lands of the Amurelden people are under siege by countless hordes of mana-infected beasts. 

While cold steel and piercing iron may help defend the Kingdoms and Empires that stretch across the sub-continent, their only real chance for surviving rests in the hands of wizards.

The passing of the Lorelias comet signalled the coming of a new era –– one marked by a relentless battle for survival.

  The sapphire colossus was last seen more than three centuries ago – it torched the skies, spilling its mana-filled insides across the entire planet. Many believed that the mystical messenger of doom would never return -- 

Some of the more ancient wizards still remembered the chaos of its last passing -- they themselves witnessed the horrors and death that unfolded in its wake -- things they had hoped never to see again.  

Wizard Survival is a card game for 2-4 players. The games mechanics were specifically designed to assure that every game is a new and unique challenge.

One of the games two modes is survival - a free-for-all match that is won by being the last surviving Wizard in the game. Players use magic spells to defend against marauding monsters, and are limited by the scarcity of resources.

The other game mode is cooperative, in which players join forces in order to vanquish the 4 powerful bosses. This mode has four difficulty levels, especially designed to push players to their limits.

Players throw the dice and moves his pawn along the board. They have a several destinations where they try to catch as the most valuable fish. Crowned the winner is the player who is at the end of the season for the most points earned catches.

description:
A fully cooperative survival miniatures board game. Mission-based with a highly modular game board.

Every player takes the roll of a survivor to fight against the reavers and horrifying mutants, causes through the outbreak 5 years ago.

Blood Fall: The Last Days offers a great combat system where positioning is the key to survive. You have to find Items, weapons, crafting upgrades with components to manage your character. You can learn skills to improve your chances to survive.

Beside the combat system one of the great and innovative game features is the enemy AI. Every enemy acts by itself. They have movement paths and smart combat mechanics.

The game design has finished to 90%. The artwork is about 90%. Actual focus is on mission design for great adventures.

Play Testers Voice:
"So complex, but not complicated."
"Highly cooperative. Don't split the group!"
"The best weapon system i've played with...a shotgun feels like a shotgun and a kitchen knife -puh- feels like that. Great."
"Very smart A.I. system. The routing of the enemies is great and acts as a natural timer. If you had the chance to test it. Test it!"

Shadows over Europe is a two- or three-player game simulating the diplomatic, economic and military development leading up to and eventually being unfolded in World War II.

Players assume the roles of Germany, the Soviet Union and the United Kingdom. Players attempt to outperform each nation’s historical record in the world’s most gigantic struggle. The game starts in 1935 and ends by an automatic victory or in the summer of 1945.

Shadows of Europe is played over 11 years, 1935-1945. Each year is represented by three turns; a Between Years turn (diplomacy, resource collection and research), a Winter turn (land operations), and a Summer turn (land and naval operations). Further each turn is divided into several phases.

A fundamental part of the game design is that actions rather than units are hidden from the enemy. This captures the game’s ambition to reflect a strategic rather than tactical level. In WWII the concentration of larger forces was often known to the enemy, whereas their strategic intent was not.

Units are Infantry and Mechanized/Panzer armies, Leaders, Surface raiders, U-boat flotillas, Escort Groups and Convoys. Air forces are abstracted to a combat modifier for each front.

The game facilitates two kind of resources: Minerals used for production and Oil for operations.

Rulebook and Playbook:
The 20-page rulebook is clearly written with many examples and designer notes. Also included is a 14-page playbook with extended examples of play, strategy hints and a card index.

A spin-off of the then-popular game Bild-A-Word, meant for very young players. It includes 36 cards each with a letter, color band, and part of a picture. The object is to match cards by their bands, assemble the picture on the included easel, and see the spelling of the animal pictured.

Manuscripts is a 3-dimensional medieval game. You lead a search party of three characters into a castle to retrieve your kingdom’s stolen manuscript.  Your search party consists of a Warrior who is strong and fast, a Wizard who has three game changing magic cards, and a Princess who is responsible for retrieving her secret Manuscript that is on a face down card selected at the start of the game.

Each board you enter requires summoning a Monster that is controlled by the player on your left.  You will face an Orc, Ogre, Minotaur, Werewolf, Knight of Doom, Dragon, or the Grim Reaper.  Occasionally you will be confronted by a hostile search party.  The monsters cannot follow you on to another board and your magic only works in the room you are in.  You can battle with the strength of your character, use a magic weapon, use a magic spell, or run (honorably of course).

To win your Princess must acquire the correct Manuscript and your search party must have acquired the most jewels of all Princesses who escaped the castle.  The game ends when all Princesses have exited the castle, dead or alive.

Manuscript's Castles are carved from blocks of wood and the Starter Pack 1 includes 2 boards, 7 monsters, 7 jewels, two search parties, and three Manuscripts.

description:
Cute old Dutch game.

Components in my edition:
2 Spoons
4 Balls
Insert that acts as the board
Rules inside lid

The game is intended for children and was designed to help children with their mathematical skills. Game 1 is for the youngest children. Game 2 is for slightly older children. Game 3 is intended for much older children, say, 8 to 10 year olds.

The board is punctuated with holes and each hole has a value next to it. The cute spoons have a long handle and a 'bowl' into which you balance a ball. Hold the spoon over the board and blow the ball off. If the ball is in a hole, do the same with the 2nd ball. If, at any time, a ball does not roll into a hole, the player may blow it again.

Game 1. Add the two numbers together after both balls are in holes. The highest total wins.

Game 2: Start with a base total of 5. Add the value of the hole where the first ball lands. Subtract the value of the hole where the 2nd ball landed. Highest total wins.

Game 3: Use all 4 balls. Again, start with a base total of 5. The first ball's value multiplies the base score. The second ball is added to the running total. The third ball is subtracted from the running total and the 4th ball is divided into the running total after the third ball has been computed. Highest total achieved, wins.

boardgame: Eminent Domain: Base Game Scenarios Promo
description:
A total of eight promo cards included with Eminent Domain: Microcosm for play with Eminent Domain. The cards include three Politics cards and five different Scenario Cards. By name, the Scenarios included with these promos are Bountiful Harvest, Mobilized, Network Admin, Terraformer, and War Path.

These scenarios can be used with the base game of Eminent Domain, no expansions are required. Of course, they can also be used if playing with any/all of the expansions as well.

Horatio D'arven's new play "Jolene and the falcon" breaks women's hearts coast-to-coast. But they are not the only ones with a soft core, because the legendary Holy Order of the Bulky Knights decided to come and see the latest performance, too. And strangely nobody wants to sit next to them and their spiky elbow-guards.

A Knight At The Opera is an abstract game for two to four players, utilizing mechanics like tile placement and area control to simulate the tumultuous processes at the opera house. Players will need to seat male and female audience members next to each other to score points. But perhaps the ladies will fall in love with one of the valiant knights in the end?

boardgame: Father Mole Looks for the Treasure
description:
User summary:

Become the head of a mole household and explore for treasure in a nearby cave.

Each turn, players may either draw from the tile bag and place their spoils in the "search pile", or move the entire contents of the "search pile" to their home for safekeeping.  Just be sure to watch out for ghosts tiles which cause a player to love everything they haven't stored at home.

The first player to get 10 points worth of treasure to their home wins.

The Ginger Wars is a game of battling gingerbread people. Each player has 5 character pieces in which they protect and battle others for their pieces. The first person to complete a brand new gingerbread person wins the game.

It's fast paced where you're constantly attacking and defending with your battle cards. You have to be smart and pick your opponents wisely, manage your revolving deck of cards and keep track of what others have played. You need to get your bluffing game going. Get people to waste their strength and attack them when their momentarily weak.

The Ginger Wars aims to appeal to the casual and more experienced gamers. It doesn't take long to learn the rules, but playing your opponents like a fiddle will take time.

Dinosaur Math consists of 1 double-sided game board, 4 dinosaur counters, 2 number dice and 1 operations die. Each side of the game board is a different game.

Dino Tracks: Each player chooses a dinosaur marker and places it on the same colour start space. The youngest player goes first. Both number dice are rolled and the player adds the numbers together and moves that number of spaces on the board. The first player to move their dinosaur to the home space is the winner.

Dino Stomp: Each player chooses a dinosaur marker and places it on the same colour start space, and rolls the two number dice. The highest number goes first. Players roll all 3 dice and use the numbers and operation shown to determine what number of spaces to move. Some spaces have instructions on them. The first player to reach the finish space is the winner.

Game based on the animated TV series for children Fruittis.
Players roll the dice and move their token through the board. Their goal is to collect as many cards that can be obtained for stepping on the field or by passing the circuit through playing plan. The game ends at the moment when the first player gets the cards to all eight characters, winning player with the most cards.

boardgame: Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game – The Mark of Madness
description:
Description from the publisher:

"There is a whole secret cult of evil men (a man of your mystical erudition will understand me when I link them with Hastur and the Yellow Sign)…"
    –H.P. Lovecraft, The Whisperer in Darkness

The Mark of Madness is the tenth deluxe expansion for Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game, introducing 165 new cards (three copies each of 55 different cards) that explore those depths of insanity that lie near the core of the Lovecraftian mythos. Devoted primarily to the Hastur faction, The Mark of Madness introduces a great number of deranged Artists, Cultists, and Lunatics, as well as the terrible, ancient powers that spur them forward. As Hastur's secret cult expands its powers, you'll also find a maddening array of new characters, support cards, conspiracies, and events, most of which lend new strength to the game's Terror struggle and mechanics for insanity.

Additionally, as Hastur's madness reaches outward to all of the game's eight factions, each gains a powerful new character that enters play insane and reshapes the game when it is restored. It's unknown whether or not these new characters will be able to prevent Hastur's followers from plunging the world into a new age of terror and chaos, but they gain additional support from other characters, events, and support cards.

description:
A children's game that teaches about the development of life on earth. Players spin the spinner and move their dinosaur-shaped pawn that number of spaces, which correspond to different species.  They progress from micro-organisms through plants to invertebrates and then vertebrates, each time doing ocean life and then land life.  There are numerous squares that make you go back 2 if you land on them, and the last 5 spaces must be landed on by exact count, all giving a catch-up mechanism.

The back of the board has family trees for the plant and animal kingdoms, and charts showing forms of life appearing during different epochs of earth history.

The first player to get his 2 pawns from space 1 to space 100 wins the game. Movement is determined by 1 or 2 dies. If a pawn lands on a space with the number 10 or a multiple of 10, its next roll counts double. A pawn that gets on a space with a square number automatically advances by as much spaces as the numbers square root. Spaces with prime numbers can only be left by rolling a prime number.

Costume Party  is a quick card game about bluffing, memory, and looking great. The goal is to score the most points among several rounds of play by wearing certain costumes.

Each round, players have a hand of three cards and can play one of five corny costumes -- Vampire, Mummy, Robot, Ghost, Zombie, or one half of a Horse costume -- and each costume works a little differently. For example, you win 6 points if you and another player can coordinate a Horse costume... but lose 3 points if you go as just a Horse's bottom.

Welcome to the complex world of plants. A place where thriving depends on strategy, tactics and maybe a tiny bit of luck .

Plants compete to be the most successful wild plant. Players will be deciding how to use their resources to best support their plants' success in this game's environment. Conflict of Plants at its core is both an action selection and an area control game, but with a twist. The environment does not always favor the most abundant species.

The game consists of 6 rounds. Each round excluding the first, the player with the fewest points swaps two on-map Non-White Tokens with those of the randomly-chosen icons. Each player, in turn order, sends their cubes to tiles have an icon that matches the icon played to their Player mat. Players then in turn perform one action each three times.

Each player then places one Brown Token onto an empty corner of a tile in the map again matching an icon to what was played on their Player Mat. The map is then examined in order to award points and Brown Tokens. The players then balance each region by removing cubes from each tile until the number of Player Cubes in total is equal to or less than the region’s number.

Finally, the players clean up their Player Mats to prepare for the next round.

In the seventeenth century, the Caribbean, was an area little explored and full of adventure, where the intrepid crews traded and discovered valuable treasures.

Choose your crew, your cargo and weapons, and sailing in the Caribbean, seeking fame and riches.

In 1653: Sailors and Merchants, you will use your ship , which is represented by a deck of cards (which you will change during the game) in order to get the most possible fame points.


En el Siglo XVII, el Caribe, era un área poco explorada y llena de aventuras, donde las tripulaciones intrépidas comerciaban y descubrían valiosos tesoros.

Elige tu tripulación, tu carga y tus armas, y navega el Caribe en busca de fama y riquezas.

En "1653: Marineros y Mercaderes", usarás tu barco, que es representado por un mazo de cartas (que irá cambiando a lo largo de la partida) para conseguir la mayor cantidad de puntos de fama.

Harrie has eaten all your stuff. Try to pick them one by one out of his mouth. He's asleep. Watch out! Don't wake him, he'll grab your hand!

Is the object you grabbed colored? Well done! Did you grab an insect or a black object? Ouch! You have to put the item back into Harrie's mouth and you lose one of your collected items.

When Harrie bites while your hand is in his mouth, you lose 2 of your collected items.

The first player to collect 5 items, wins the game.

There is a special scoring system for older children.

From the publisher:

Play Classic Connect 4 - and MORE! There are 3 NEW Connect 4 games: Backwards Connect 4, Connect 4 Any Color and Connect 4 Teams! Contains 40 Heavy Duty cardboard tiles (20 red and 20 yellow) and illustrated instructions for 6 different games!

boardgame: Spurs and Sprockets: Villager Start Card Expansion
description:
The Villager Start Card Expansion includes one card/tile that may be used with all Spurs and Sprockets card/tile decks.

The Villager is placed in the field first and the start player places his/her card/tile adjacent to the Villager. Play continues based on which Spurs and Sprockets rules you have decided to play with. The Villager card/tile has no lines in any directions and can't be added to any player's adjacent rows for attacking, defending or scoring purposes. It works as a blocking mechanism. The Villager may be destroyed by using Tic Attack Toe or Fired Away rules.

The darkness spread all around the chamber with the quiet howling in the background. There was a spectral sensation in the room, dark and ominous. Don’t get spooked though and sit around the mystic board in a vain hope to catch the spirit... Before it will manage to catch you.
 Third Eye is the truly terrifying hybrid card game of cutthroat decisions, quick wit and brutal interactions. Be the last one to survive  a haunting from beyond and never hesitate to lead the malignant presence towards other, unfortunate souls!

Dominion: Guilds & Cornucopia combines the Dominion: Guilds and Dominion: Cornucopia small box expansions — originally the fifth and the eighth expansions — into a single large box expansion.

The cards from Dominion: Cornucopia — 13 new Kingdom cards, plus 5 unique cards — are focused on variety, with cards that reward you for having a variety of cards in your deck, in your hand, and in play, in addition to cards that help you get that variety.

Dominion: Guilds also consists of 13 new Kingdom cards, with some of these cards allowing you to get more out of them by paying extra when you buy them. In addition, this expansion contains coin tokens that you can save to spend later.

Part of the Dominion series.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Your ancestors were more than mortal: they were travelers of untold lands, legendary figures battling the primeval forces of the world… or even embracing them. So what does that make you?

Norsaga: Into the Myths is an expansion for the puzzle battle card game Norsaga. New types of advanced heroes add unique mechanics and embellishments, while additional basic heroes allow you to increase the player count.

Combine these cards with the hero cards from the base game to maximize the strategic possibilities and player interaction in each game, or tailor the complexity to your liking by picking and choosing which new cards to use.

A spoken party game in which players try to guess the object on the card using closed questions (that can only be answered 'Yes', 'No', or 'Maybe'). If players fail to guess correctly within 20 questions the answerer wins.

Based on the BBC Radio panel game show (which was itself based on a US radio show derived from a 19th century parlour game) the package contains twelve object cards with enough information to answer most questions.

boardgame: A Conta da Copa é Nossa!
description:
A Conta da Copa é Nossa! is a card game who pictures the process of building the infrastructure of Brazil's 2014 World Cup.

Each player represents a contractor which competes with other players to gain more profit and participation in the constructions. To achieve their goals, the players will have at their disposal a humorous deck of illustrated cards with politicians and characters which can help you with many tasks: gain favors, spoil the adversaries, work around difficult situations, among other things.

At the beginning, each player gets 20 cards of characters. Three piles are stocked at the center of the table: constructions, governors, and sponsors. After that, 8 construction cards, 1 governor and 2 sponsors are removed from their piles, and the players can use them, replacing the cards as they are used. There are material tokens that make constructions possible. They are obtained by using the characters and politicians cards.

Each construction sums points to a total, and the highest total wins the game.

boardgame: For the Crown (Second Edition): Expansion #3 – Between Heaven and Earth
description:
The third expansion to For the Crown, Between Heaven and Earth, introduces 12 new kinds of variable cards and 10 new types of units that can be trained using the new cards, in addition to an alternate starting card, the Merchant. You can mix and match cards from the original game with any 2nd Edition expansion.

Note: This expansion kit cannot be used with the first edition of For the Crown.

Shoot Your Friends is a lightning-fast game that pits opponents across from each other with nothing but a toy gun and a deck of cards between them. Be the first to create a lethal combination of cards on the table to become either the shooter or the shootie. To play Shoot Your Friends, you must have fast reflexes, quick thinking, and an itchy trigger finger.

Separate the 6 Bullet Cards from the deck and set them aside. Deal 8 cards to each player. Each player places 4 cards face down, side by side, on the table and his other 4 cards (the player’s hand) face down off to the side. Players may not look at them… yet.

Place the rest of the cards into draw piles on the table within easy reach of every player.

Use Character Cards to create matches and use the toy gun to shoot your friends out of the game.

Use Shield Cards to deflect bullets and stay alive a little while longer.

Use Bullet Cards to determine who wins!

In the near-distant future, after years of war and famine, a global pandemic kills most of the world’s population. Those that survive are transformed by the virus, unlocking a latent genetic code. Now, the world is ruled by the three original species of planet earth: Vampires, Werewolves and super Humans.

BANE is a three-suited competitive card game, where the suits represent powerful races (humans, vampires and werewolves), each hunting another, locked in a conflict for dominance. The Order of Prey (who hunts who) is similar to rock-paper-scissors:

     Humans hunt Werewolves
     Werewolves hunt Vampires
     Vampires hunt Humans


Cards are played to a board in what is called an engagement. Similar to a trick-taking game, once all players put a card into play one winner is determined using the Order of Prey (described above). Cards also have a ranking, called Speed. Unlike other trick-taking games (such as Spades) a player may not play a card whose speed matches that of a card already in play - thus allowing players to 'speed block' other players. There are eight speeds, represented by numbers in the upper-left corner of each card.

Example: After all cards are played during an engagement, Bob sees that his Pack Alpha (Werewolf, Speed 1) is the fastest. He will thus be able to take a trophy from another player, as long as there is a Vampire in play (because Werewolves hunt Vampires).

Taking a trophy means that the player may choose from a pool of face-down cards in front of another player.

The round (Hunt) ends when one player is out of cards or trophies. At that time, the deck is shuffled and a new Hunt (round) begins.

WINNING THE GAME:
Each player tracks their individual score. A Player wins by reaching the MASTERY space on the score track and having more trophies than opponents also in the MASTERY space.

There are other elements to the game that add flavor (Special Power cards, a BANE token).

description: Fluxx Dice is an expansion that can be used with any Fluxx card game. The components are two dice — one showing "Draw 1", "Draw 2", etc. while the other shows "Play 1", "Play 2", "Play All", etc. — and five cards. Instead of following the basic Fluxx rules, each turn players roll the draw and play dice, then follow the new rules set by their roll!

The Encampment rules introduce Spawning Points that may move back and forth from Zone 3 to Zone 2 depending on the Summoning rolls, creating a more dynamic pattern of invasion.

When setting up the game with the Encampment Tiles, players will place Spawning Tokens on the board in the indicated spots - these represent the moving enemy Encampments. When a Square symbol is rolled on the Summoning Roll, the Spawning Point will spawn the enemies listed on the corresponding Enemy card and then the Spawning Token is moved forward to Zone 2. A Triangle result will make the Spawning Point continue where it's at, and a Circle result will move the Spawning Point back to Zone 3.

This mobile nature of the Encampment Tile Set brings a new and fresh experience when defending the Citadel! With enemies appearing on Zones 2 and 3, the pressure builds up on the Heroes! Assault units will be one step closer and the Catapult won't be as strong when the Spawning Points are on Zone 2's, but on the other hand, Heavy Units that Spawn on Zone 2 will have to move to Zone 3 before they start firing upon the Citadel, which will buy the Heroes and the Citadel some time! The symbols to the left of the Encampment Tiles indicate the behaviour of the different enemy types, depending on where they are spawned.

The Encampment Tile Set can be played with any B-Sieged enemy army, whether it be the Abyssal Forces from Sons of the Abyss or the Iron Orc nation from the Darkness & Fury expansion!

Monster Buffet is a real-time, extremely fast paced, quick party game! Every players play a monster with endless hunger whose only purpose in life is to gorge itself full. Mostly on smaller, much less fortunate little critters! But alas, he is not the only one with empty belly and gaping maw. Other hungry monsters want their share too, and gosh, they are greedy!

Each player grabs the token with one hand and puts it in his monster belly. Building and eating stack of critters, from one up to ten. You can take the lower number, same number or higher number, but never skip one in between! At the end of each round the app counts the points. The stack is worth the number of points equal to "meat" icon collected in it. In each round all players draw a random mutagen card. Each card offers them a task to fulfill to get extra points. Dare to get a run at them to tip the victory in your favor!

boardgame: Marignan 1515: La Chevauchée de François 1er en Italie
description:
Marignan 1515: La Chevauchée de François 1er en Italie (Marignan 1515: The Ride of Francis I in Italy) is the new opus of the “Gold & Steel” series started with Les guerres de Bourgogne (Wars of Burgundy).

This is an historical simulation of the 5th War of Italy - July 1515 to February 1517 – featuring Marignano battle: a French victory that shapes the relationships with the Swiss Confederacy for centuries in ending their supremacy on battlefields and that will reach this year its 500th anniversary

One player leads the French armies and their allies (Republics of Venetia and Genoa) and the other one is in command of the Swiss Confederacy and its allies – the Holy League - (Papal States, Duchy of Milano, Holy Roman Empire, Kingdom of Naples). The goal of both of them is to take control of the Duchy of Milano and Venetian territories hold by the Empire, win pitched battles or major sieges, and neutralise the opponent's allies.

One key element of the game is collecting enough resource each turn (the gold) to pay all the mercenaries troops (the steel); German landsknechts and Swiss soldiers. Otherwise they will quit the battlefield… The money can be used to neutralize enemy allies as well.

This is both a strategic & tactical wargame with armies and garrisons evolving on the main strategic map representing Northern and Middle Italy and units fighting on a tactical map featuring a typical XVIth century battleground. Units are men-at-arms, pikemen, crossbowmen, halberdiers, harquebusiers, swords & bucklers, jinetes and stradiots (both light cavalrymen) pioneers, cannons, from 1,500 to 2,000 men at full strength, 750 to 1,000 horsemen or 100 cannons of all calibres. The specifics fighting elements of that period are added with tactical chits (rolling shots, infantry or cavalry charges, harassment, ditch, fence, clash of pikes, feint, hedgehog, cannon battery, etc.), arms combination – early Tercios - and events such as rain, snow, minor ally no fight or betrayal, no wing deployment, no money no mercenaries, Alle Freunde (all friends). Optional rule includes a hidden crossing of the Alps Mountains.

The complete sequence of a turn:
I/ Kingdom of France
1) Reinforcement, replacement
2) Strategic move
3) Attrition phase
4) Combat phase (battles & sieges)
II/ Holy League
Same sequence
III/ Diplomatic phase
Check for victory

From the game:

"The object of the game is to form as many poker hands as possible by placing cards as they are called in the spaces in the bottom of the board (a 5x5 grid). Hands are read horizontally, vertically and diagonally. The maximum number of hands is therefore twelve, but total score will vary according to the skill of player in placing the cards."

Every player has a deck of cards. There is one caller, who shuffles their deck, then draws 25 cards, one at a time, and calls it out. All players then take their copy of that card and everyone, on their own board, places the card somewhere on the 5x5 grid. Once everyone's grid is filled, whoever has the highest total score, calculated based on the poker hands created when placing cards in the grid, is the winner.
publishers: The United States Playing Card Company

description:
Promotional board game for Audentes privat school for their 20th birthday.

Player rolls the die, moves around the board and if the game-piece lands on certain places He/She tries to answer the questions or solves the different tasks. If player answers incorrectly or doesn´t solve the task He/She misses next turn. All questions and tasks are connected with the school somehow.

First player who has made the whole round around the board is the winner.

boardgame: Fantasy Football: Draft, Deals, and Dice
description:
Fantasy Football: Draft, Deals, and Dice (FF3D) is a card drafting and dice rolling game that allows players to simulate the fantasy football experience. FF3D is designed to be played with 2 or 4 players, though expansions can easily allow up to 16 players. FF3D can be played in anywhere from 60-120 minutes, depending on the length of time players spend on their initial drafting process.

The base game of Fantasy Football: Draft, Deals, and Dice allows players to compete with each other over a shortened four week fantasy football season. The Owners have the opportunity to draft their fantasy rosters from a pool of randomized Players at the start of the season. The Team and Player Ratings are randomized at the start of each season, so no two seasons will ever be the same. One year, the San Francisco QB could be a star player with major upside – the next year, he could be a mediocre talent that shrinks in the Primetime. The winner is able to initially assess the random market conditions of the league to maximize their draft selections within their roster restrictions, as well as able to navigate the flow of the season’s Defensive Matchups, Injuries, Hot Streaks better than their opponents.

After establishing agreed upon Roster Requirements for the League, the game begins with (and features) everyone’s favorite part of the fantasy football season – the fantasy draft.

The Game is split into three main segments:
The Fantasy Draft
The Regular Season
The Playoffs

Owners will draft a team of Players and set their starting lineups against the opposing Owner on their Fantasy Schedule. The Owners take turns determining their Weekly Results by rolling a specified number of Offensive and Defensive dice for each Player in their Starting Lineup. The number and types of dice rolled are influenced by the Player's Skill and Upside Levels, as well as any Team Matchup Modifiers, Player Hot Streaks, or Player Injuries that have been assigned to the player during the game play. In-Game Events could also trigger unexpected outcomes that can swing the momentum during a game. After all games have been played, the Owner with the most points in the matchup receives the win. At the end of each week, Players are randomly selected to either go on a Hot Streak or go on the Injured Reserve. Owners may replace Injured players by following a Waiver and Free Agency process against the other Owners to select Players from the pool of unselected Players from the initial draft. The first three weeks make up the Regular Season, with the Top 2 teams competing in Week 4 for the Championship.

Some history. The vervets monkeys arrived from Africa to the Caribbean island of St. Kitts with slaves of the rum industry 300 years ago. The monkeys that escaped, acquired taste (and addiction) for alcohol eating fermented sugarcane.

These funny monkeys, good observers of man, they know, like the locals, that the easiest way to get your daily dose of alcohol is by distracted tourists in the beach bars. They love cocktails, daiquiris, caipirinhas, usually rum-based drinks.

In St. Kitts each player is a waiter who is held to be more cocktails (of each type) serving in a beach snack bar, trying to fill their tables of wealthy and thirsty tourists.

All actions are carried away and combining results of dice. Refer to vervets monkeys, bartenders and cocktails. If you get a result of blue, red, green or yellow refers to a cocktail. They come in four types: Daikiri strawberries (red cocktail), Caipirinha (green cocktail), Banana colada (yellow cocktail) and Blue Ocean (Blue cocktail). Place a record of your color in a table that asks that cocktail to indicate that you've served you, according to rules.

Each full table you've served more cocktails (you have most) awards (knizian) points to the end of the game. If you can add new tables to serve more tourists you will receive tips.

And if that were not enough, we must be aware of groups vervets monkeys in their usual "raids" in search of alcohol miss these tips will make acquired with sweat and tears.

Smic Smac, Collect the food cards to feed your hedgehog so they can sleep. Card game for 2-4 players. Includes 64 playing cards. Comes in a box with magnetic snap closure.

On your turn, turn over the food cards.  If there are more hedgehogs awake than those drawn on the food cards, you may collect it.  You may choose to hibernate your hedgehogs on your turn because when the 4 winter cards in the food cards open, the game ends and anyone with hedgehogs awake will lose!  The player at that time with the most food will win.

Simple roll-and-move game. The first player to reach space 50 wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. On certain spaces events occur, that hinder or benefit the player.

This game was used as a consolation prize for a competition in 1955 but it may have been produced since as early as 1936.
Simple roll-and-move game. The first player to reach space 60 wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. On certain spaces events occur, that hinder or benefit the player.
Simple roll-and-move game. The first player to reach the house wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. On certain spaces events occur, that hinder or benefit the player.

A simple race game in the tradition of Chutes and Ladders. Move by die roll. If you land on a red number (step forward), green number (fall backwards), follow instructions glued into the lid. The first to reach space 130 exactly is the winner.

boardgame: Fields of Fire: Movie Monster Madness
description:
Fields of Fire:  Movie Monster Madness is a short supplement for Fields of Fire: Miniatures Rules for Modern Combat that allows you to battle historical miniatures against giant lizards, zombies, hordes of insects, and more!

What's more, Fields of Fire:  Movie Monster Madness includes fast play rules so that you can get started even if you don't own the core rules set.

There are two kind of cards, red and blue, questions and answers.
Two players, other one take a question card deck, and second one take a answers card deck, which will be shuffled. Player with the question deck ask question from a card and player with the question deck read answer from the answer deck, other one ask and other one answers, until both decks are gone through, than you resuffle decks and can change players. For listeners game is also a fun pass time.

Finnish description:
Kortteja on kahdenlaisia, sinisiä ja punaisia, kysymyksiä ja vastauksia. Pelaajia on kaksi, joista toinen ottaa kysymyspakan ja toinen vastauspakan, jotka sekoitetaan. Nurinpäin käännetystä pakasta ottaa kysyjä kortin kerrallaan, lukee kysymyksen ja toinen pelaaja vastaa, kunnes pakka on käyty läpi. Tällöin sekoitetaan pakat uudelleen ja pelaajat voivat vaihtua. Myös kuuntelijoille peli on hauskaa ajan kulua.

27: Five Elements is a quick and easy to learn card game with various difficulties suitable for little children up to card game fans and even seasoned masters like poker players. Game is challenging and fun, full of action and strategy - every decision counts, any turn can be the last, anyone can win.

Game features: hidden information, bluffing and sniping opponents, changing rules, innovative scoring, beautiful design.

Includes standard 54 card deck with 2 Jokers.

Cards come in five elements - Fire, Water, Earth, Air and Aether. Each element comes in three powers. Players draw one card every turn and play it to that elements stack. Cards of higher power collect all the cards of the lower powers in a stack, Aether cards can be played on any stack. When one player collects 27 or more points any player can end the game; cards left in the players hands are subtracted from their collected cards score.

boardgame: Black Diamond of the Lost Palace
description:
Embark on a mysterious journey of adventure and danger deep into the Cambodian jungle to discover the lost palace of the ancient Khmer empire and its treasures. But beware - the jungle will not give its treasures easy. Can you find the legendary Black Diamond? Or will you open the Potion of Oblivion and forget everything? Oh - just one more thing - you are not afraid of big, wild and dangerous animals, are you?

Players move their explorers on a 7x7 grid based on a die roll. There are 49 treasure tokens facing down (one on each grid tile). When player comes to a closed token they turn it and if it contains their color they can take it if it is a treasure (gives points) or do some action if it is a card (extra points, or actions) or an animal (protection) token. Game is over when one player has 27 or more points and wins.

You move around the board by throwing a die, trying to collect all of the different character cards: Betty, Barney, Pebbles, Bamm-Bamm and Dino. For example, if you end up on a Barney square you take a Barney card. Immediately after you've picked up the card the player to the left of you gets a chance to steal your new card by guessing which one of all Fred's (because this game is about Fred no matter what card you pick up) favorite hobbies is on the back. There are 5 different hobbies represented on each and one of all the character cards (fishing rod, grammophone, grilled meat, golf and TV). So the chance to immediately steal the card is 1 to 5. Once you've collected one of every character card you need to get back home as quickly as possible in order to win.

When you're picking up cards you might get a specialty card. If the back of the card has a picture of Wilma you must move your piece to the beginning. A picture of a happy Fred lets you take one card from an opponent, and an angry Fred makes you give one of your cards to an opponent.

A simple race game in the tradition of the Game of Goose. Move by die roll. If you land on a colored space, obey the instructions glued into the lid.

Cover written in 'Sütterlin' script

Objective: gain most chips (coins) and become the wealthiest player at the end of the game;

Each player has to buy in first; 32 cards are describing the fairy tale of 'Cinderella' by forming a board; cards are distributed among the players who must play cards according rising numbers. Read aloud card before placing it upon the table und follow instructions according an advantage/disadvantage table.

The Heroic Deck is an expansion for the standard Ruddy Vikings Deck filled with all sorts of goodness that brings more crazyness to the game!
The expansion includes Heroic Vikings with unique abilities. The Heroic Viking Cards have built in buffers which make them more likely to get past defences.
This coupled with new defences and new buffers mean that players never have to stop having fun filled bearded gaming sessions.

boardgame: The Battle at Kemble's Cascade: Cetus
description:
The Battle at Kemble's Cascade: Cetus features a huge Lovecraftian eel that serves as an intermediary boss level during the game.

Cetus appears following the asteroid stage and sits at the top of the screen (as a temporary sixth row) while the wormhole stage scrolls normally below it.

At the beginning of each round, Cetus draws from its own "boss" sector card deck, revealing either a wormhole or a burst bomb. To determine which column receives this payload, draw from the regular sector card deck and match its special icon to the corresponding boss segment.

Cetus sticks around long enough to dump two bombs and two wormholes onto the star field, then it takes off. This beast is worth a fair number of points and bellonium, but its head and belly are immune to Gamma, so you need alternative methods to take those out.

Description from the publisher:

Dinner by the campfire! Summer camp is the perfect getaway. It's isolated, quiet, and theres plenty to eat...starting with the counselors and then the campers! The only drawback is something named "Jason" that keeps making your dinner disappear!

Humans!!! 4: Camp Zombie Lake is an expansion for Humans!!! that adds Counselor cards to randomly pick who you encounter and learn what nasty surprises they might have in store.

Fantastic Storytelling is a new card game which enables players to tell hilarious, unique stories and score points. Fantastic Storytelling is a brand new card game for telling unique, hilarious stories.

Amateur and professional linguists alike can craft crazy, imaginative stories with the aim of making people laugh and scoring points - and it's as easy as laying down a few cards. The premise is simple - the longer your story is, the more points you score.

The game combines my love of writing, comedy and games into one little box, which enables players to create ridiculous, silly, funny and crazy stories. There are so many cards that no two stories will ever be alike! 360 professionally designed and printed cards, along with the instructions on how to play the game.

Anybody can play, but due to some of the combinations that could arise whilst playing, it is recommended that players are 18+. You can play with 2-8 players, or even alone to try and improve your Fantastic Storytelling skills!

boardgame: Savage Plains: A Solitaire Game of Indian Fighting on the Southwestern Plains (1865-1890).
description:
BACKGROUND: SAVAGE PLAINS is semi-historical simulation of the American Indian Wars on the Southern Plains from 1865 to 1890. The Game begins after the American Civil War and the re-United States is ready to expand its influence and power. The US feels its Manifest Destiny is to expand and annex the Territories separating the Eastern States and California. In order to accomplish this, they must attempt to bring peace and stability to the region by “Pacifying” the Indian Tribes living there. This can be accomplished either through military victories or through diplomatic Peace Treaties.

OBJECTIVE: Player takes command of the US Army Forces on the Frontier of the Southern Plains. The Player’s Objective is to pacify the Indian Territories in the region in order to make it safe to build US Settlements, as well as, the Transcontinental Railroad. The Player must attempt to build a number of US Settlements with a total Settlement Value (SV) of at least 41 by the end of the Turn in which the last Campaign Card is Played.

FEATURES: SAVAGE PLAINS features many colorful game counters including US Cavalry, US Infantry, US Artillery and Gatling Guns, Sioux, Cheyenne, Comanche and Apache Indian Units. Counters, also, include individual US and Indian Heroes and Scouts.

SAVAGE PLAINS can be found at the following URL:
http://www.berserkergames.net

In this exciting battle strategy card game with 4 methods of play, dueling Kings and Queens gather the greatest warriors from history to battle against their rivals.

Imagine playing the popular "war" card game with actual warriors from history, or playing a "battlefield" strategy game in which you organize an army to capture your opponent's castle using the greatest warriors from different times and places from history, or playing the popular "memory" game with pairs of historical warriors. And imagine playing "capture the flag" outside and giving each player a role to play as a specific warrior from history.

You get these 4 games in 1 from Royal Combat: Warriors of History, the card game.

Led by the Royal Gold Star players and assisted by the Special Silver Star players, each team / player strategizes against their opponent to defend their own castle while attempting to capture their opponent's castle.  In the active role play option and the tabletop battlefield option there is a castle area and a battlefield area on which your green star warriors from history can be placed along with the gold star, silver star and shadow players.  Shadow players are lowest in rank but operate subversively and secretly in the shadows and have special powers that enable them to capture certain members of the higher ranks.

For example, the Royal Gold Star cards (King, Queen and Knight) can take out the Special Silver Cards (General, Admiral and Ace), which can take out the Green Star Warriors from History (Commando, Spartan, Samurai, Mongol, Ming, Viking, Highlander, Zulu, etc...), which can take out the black star Shadow cards (Sniper, Ninja and Spy).  Yet the black star Sniper can take out the Silver Star General and the black star ninja can take out the Gold star King, etc...

Objective: gain most victory points by hitting opposing army

Each player places his army pawns according a spreadsheet (general rearmost). Then they take turns loading their cannons (called 'Brummer') and firing in direction to the enemy by using a puff-ball. Game ends after agreed time (sum up victory points) or if a general is pushed over.

Aching for a two player game? Have a chess set just collecting dust in the house? Afraid to take on the house champion for fear of embarrassment? Then prepare to parry, charm and back stab your way through this strategically clever spin on chess. Beguile is an exciting add on to any standard chess set that will have you looking at strategy in a seedy and underhanded new light. Beguile is a game for 2 players. A standard chess set is required to play.

Players draw a hand of five cards from a shared deck of fifty cards. On their turn they may play a card from their hand to make a pawn move like a knight, raise a piece from the dead via Necromancy, give your opponent's pawns the plague, or perform many other actions to achieve victory.

House Rules is a 3-6 person card game battle royale, where players clash with each other using absurd weapons in order to build their own stash of weapons the largest!

The game is played in 3 rounds. At the beginning of each round players are given a choice of two rules that will go into effect for that round; once players make their choice, the chosen rules are combined and everybody plays by those rules.

All players have the opportunity to create a rule of their own once per game, and need one other players approval in order for their rule to go into effect.

All the cards in House Rules are dry erase, so players also have the opportunity to tailor their weapons and make them even sillier and more absurd than they already are. House rules is fun and frantic from beginning to end for players of all skill levels!

Chromatix is a connection game (in the vein of Hex) for three players. The game is played on a “hexhex” board (a hexagon shaped board made of hexagonal cells). Opposite sides of the board are labeled in the three secondary colors orange, purple, and green. One player owns the orange sides, another owns the purple sides, and the final player owns the green sides. The playing stones are the primary colors of red, blue, and yellow. Each player plays the primary colors that mix to form his/her secondary color (e.g. the player who owns the orange sides plays the red and yellow stones). On his turn, a player must play a single stone in one of his/her two primary colors ("own-stones") to any empty cell. Once placed, stones do not move. Corner cells belong to both of the sides that meet there.

A player can win in two ways: if there is (a) a group of own-stones that connects his sides; or (b) a group of own-stones that connects three non-adjacent sides. If more than one win criterion is achieved simultaneously, then the winner is the first player in move order (beginning with mover) who has thus fulfilled a win criterion. A player who is blocked from both ways of winning is eliminated from the game (but his/her stones remain on the board).

One last rule is the “STOP-NEXT” rule: A player may NOT play in a way that leaves the next player with an immediate win on his/her next turn. (In other words, you must block the next player if possible.)

Chromatix was inspired by the three player connection game Triskelion, designed by Bill Taylor and Joao Neto.

Knight's Journey is a game about Knights going on quest for honor and glory. During their quest they collect gold and information, and bring gold to orphans, church and women they love. 
This is pickup and delivery game that is played on the chess board using chess pieces and tokens. There are two type of tokens gold and information. Players play with knights, and pawns, queens and bishops are the pieces they are trying to collect. information is used to move pieces toward opponent to block him or make him lose points. You can also attack opponent to get his gold. The game is for 2 players, but there's a variant for 3 or 4.
In the files section you can find rules for the game, and tokens you can print.

Description from the publisher:

You are a powerful force for good, and you may be humanity's only hope. You are also a deadly bacteria struggling for dominance in a petri dish against dangerous enemies. Can you prove to the scientists looming above that you are the cure they have been looking for?

In Petri Wars, players collect and expend food to grow larger, move around the dish to gain strategic position, and attack other cells. Each time you add a new cell, it has a special ability, defense, or added value. Choose and use these abilities wisely because you can only use each one once.
.

Description from the Kickstarter:

Petri Wars is a game for 2-4 players, ages 13+. Play time is approximately 30-60 minutes.

An experimental strain of bacteria is ready to be put to the ultimate test.  Facing it are other hostile and deadly creations ready to wage war over the petri dish using all the tricks and adaptations at their disposal for survival.  Can you guide your creation to victory?  Are you ready for an epic battle of microscopic proportions?

In Petri Wars, players collect and expend food to grow larger, migrate throughout the dish to gain strategic position, and attack other cells.  Each time you add a new cell it will have a hidden special ability, such as an added attack strength or a defensive capability. Choose wisely though, as you can only use the ability of each cell once per game.

Players gather DNA by growing their organisms to occupy as much space as possible in the dish, destroying and assimilating enemy cells along the way.  The Bacteria with the most advanced DNA at the end of the game wins!
A simple fast treatment of a small Great Northern War Battle. Find Rules and Vassal Module in Files section this page.

You've waited, you've struggled , finally , you have been nominated for the Grek of the year. You may be one of the 36 nominees , but the odds are still on your side, you are still you !

Som du har väntat, som du har slitit, äntligen har du blivit nominerad till årets grek.Du må vara 1 av 36 nominerade, men oddsen är ändå på din sida, du är ändå du!

I spelet turas spelarna om med att dra ett kort och läsa tillkänna årets grek till att någon spelare har blivit utsedd till årets grek.

Rommel’s Greatest Victory

On 26 May, 1942, Erwin Rommel launched a strong right hook into the desert, flanking the minefields and fortifications painstakingly built by the British to protect Tobruk. Against heavy odds and in a tensely contested battle, Colonel General Erwin Rommel defeated the British and recaptured Tobruk, earning himself a Field Marshal’s baton in the process.

Rommel At Gazala is the 2nd Edition of Jim Werbaneth’s simulation of the daring offensive of Panzerarmee Afrika against the British Eighth Army’s fortified line near Gazala, Libya, in May and June 1941.

Rommel at Gazala is a quick-playing, accessible game. It is sufficiently easy for new players to enter into board wargaming, but with enough possibilities to offer significant replay value to newcomer and veteran player alike. Counter density is low, letting the players concentrate on strategy more than minutiae, and the pace is fast. There are also special rules to evoke the special flavor of the battle; armor superiority, the dreaded “88,” fortifications, and of course the special capabilities of Erwin Rommel himself.

Two dual-side printed Player Aid cards
One 6-sided die

description:
Maneuvers is an abstract naval strategy game published in 1942.

The board is a grid of 11 by 14 interconnected squares representing naval bases. Each player controls 33 tokens representing battleships, submarines, and aircraft.

Players take turns placing ships or planes on the naval bases; when a row of five identical tokens has been created the player wins a victory. Depending on the type of sequence created the victorious player may remove certain combinations of enemy units from the grid. For example, if a submarine pattern has been formed a player may decide to remove two enemy battleships.

The first player to lose seven tokens in any two categories is considered to be incapable of continuing the struggle. The other player is declared the winner.

While the game is designed for two players optional rules permit three or four to enjoy a session.

Players take control of either the Bianco or Nero cat mafia families and lead their side to victory in a series of face-offs.

At the beginning of each face-off, players choose a boss from their initial hand of 5 cards.  That card is then placed, face down on the table in front of them.  From there, players go back and forth playing one or two cards to raise their attack value above their opponents.  When someone finally passes, the owner of the last card played scores a successful attack.  The winner of the face-of then chooses one of their opponents cards and scores points equal to the number of paw prints.

After several face-offs, points are tallied and the player with the highest amount wins the battle.  The first player to win 3 battles sees his family come out on top.

Choose and well organize your deck and choose your players will be as important as the game itself. Each game has 10 to 12 rounds.
