boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Doorways into Darkness
description:
8 plastic doorways and a small card expansion

4 wooden beam Mine Doorways and 4 circular alien stone Other World Doorways.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: The Guardian of Targa XL Enemy Pack
description:
No one knows what has become of the ancient builders of the alien cities on the Targa Plateau! It is clear that they have been gone for sometime though, leaving the robotic Custodians and Guardians to watch over their legacy. In times of incursion, the massive Guardians awake, pulling free from their alcoves and stepping out to defend the city from intruders! Frozen over in a new ice age, the alien city has the chill of death lingering in the air and the halls echo with the sounds of the creatures that have started to take up residence there.

A single new XL Enemy with two Targa Plateau Threat Cards for Damaged Guardians and three Epic Threat Cards including one calling for two Guardians.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Serpentmen of Jargono Deluxe Enemy Pack
description:
Rulers of the murky swamps of Jargono, the savage Serpentmen take objection to your intrusion into their world.

The Shadows of Brimstone: Serpentmen of Jargono expansion adds the Serpentmen Warriors (6) and a Serpentman Shaman (1) as Enemies along with threat cards for both the Swamps of Jargono and the Mines.

As the Flying Frog image used here shows, it is labelled a Deluxe Enemy Set; however, the 2015 GenCon Preview was released as a Deluxe Enemy Pack.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Masters of the Void Deluxe Enemy Pack
description:
The Void is the space between worlds. When you pass through a Gate, you pass through the Void.

This set includes three of each of the Void Hound and Void Sorceror Enemy types and the Threat and Spell cards to go with them.

The Void Hounds slip in and out of reality as they move, passing through other models and savagely surprise attacking different targets each turn. This means that no one is safe from the hounds, even those hiding out in the back or around corners!

The Void Sorcerers cast random spells each round, drawn from a deck of spell cards. You draw a different spell for each Sorcerer each turn. Each card has two different effects listed, depending on whether it’s a normal casting, or was empowered by previous spells.

As the Flying Frog image used here shows, it is labelled a Deluxe Enemy Set; however, the 2015 GenCon Preview was released as a Deluxe Enemy Pack.

Gladiator or Charioteer? You choose!

Just saying the word gladiators conjures up visions of vicious combat between desperate men, who fought to the death for the amusement of the crowd. These games of death continued throughout the Roman Empire for over 900 years. Now, with Heroes of the Colosseum, you can recreate the glory and splendor of these games.

While gladiator matches were the “bread” of the “bread & circuses” that was used to placate the Roman citizens, chariot racing was the “circus”. Included in Heroes of the Colosseum, is a complete second game that lets you race chariots at the Circus Maximus.
As a gladiator or charioteer, you start your journey to Fame & Fortune far from the eye of the crowd. With luck and skill, you increase in abilities and work your way towards Rome. On the way, you will stab, slash, whip and bash all opponents who stand between you and your ultimate goal – freedom!

Inside you’ll find:
•    Rules to personalize your gladiators and charioteers, making each truly unique.   
•    A way to generate Non-Player Opponents, totally controlled by the game mechanics.
•    Two Gladiatorial Arenas, one Racing Oval, beautiful counters, and Roster Sheets to represent the gladiators and charioteers, their equipment, and horses. 
•    Quick to learn, but hard to master, combat and racing rules that accurately reflect the challenges of gladiatorial combat and chariot racing.  
•    An easy to use Campaign System, to track the success of your gladiators and charioteers, as they rise in abilities.
•    Three different Locales to fight in, from the Fringes of the Empire to Rome herself, where only the greatest can gain their freedom.

In Heroes of the Colosseum, you have everything you need to do this and much more. But perhaps the best thing about the game is that it can be played solo against the game mechanics, cooperatively with all players against the game, or competitively against your friends.

Takat is an abstract card game of bluffing and deduction. In Takat you are secretly assigned one of four goal colors at the beginning of a round. This color will be used to determine your
score at the end of the game. On your turn, you will play a card from your hand onto the shared board and then draw a card. The game ends when there are no more cards left to play.

Points are awarded at the end of the game based on the shapes of each players assigned color. Whoever scores the fewest points wins the game.

Children's game. The board represents a swimming pool. Before play starts, place the 25 tiles face-down, one in each square.
Players roll a colour die and may take a face-down tile related to that column or row which matches the colour thrown. If you roll a 'white' then you may choose any colour. 13 of the 25 tiles have a rubber ring on them and twelve are without a ring. When the pool is 'empty', players count up their tiles with rings and the one with them most, wins.

The 2nd game indicated in the rules is for older children. This time two colour dice are rolled together and draw the tile indicated by looking along a row indicated by one dice and also down the column indicated by the 2nd die. In addition, depending on the colours rolled, there are opportunities to give 'empty' tiles to other players or you may lose a tile you have previously taken. When all the tiles are taken, you add up your tiles with rings and subtract the tiles you have without rings. The highest total wins.

Fell is a cooperative board game pitting 2-4 players against the randomly generated Chimeris: gigantic misshapen Beasts that require many men to bring down. The players will hire a group of ruthless mercenaries, equip them carefully and then send them to hunt these gigantic Beasts. Each game will involve teamwork, strategy and risk management to focus on the most important areas of the Beast and bring it down either by taking out the head or by crippling enough limbs that it is crushed by its own weight!

This two-player abstract strategy game plays out an angry disagreement within the forum of a provincial town in Roman Britain. Two groups of citizens have come to blows over missing taxes, and pursue each other across the forum plaza and in its surrounding corridors. All pieces move orthogonally, with the aim being to trap their enemies while avoiding capture themselves. Capture is by blocking. Once trapped, hapless citizens are quickly dispatched (removed from the board). A Magistrate and two forum guards have wavering allegiances, and swap sides each time citizens are murdered. The two guards can also take part in capture. The winner is the player to eliminate all the opposing player's pieces. Leather board and wooden gaming pieces.
family: Series: Play with History (Gothic Green Oak)

description:
In the modern world of edible witchcraft, pizzas have replaced the standard brews as the food of choice. In this tile laying style card game, players race to collect the 20 pizza scoring tokens by completing as many pizzas as possible. But beware the ingredients’ “wicked” special abilities and spell cards that will create havoc if they become activated. When the final scoring token is taken, the game ends and the scores are tallied.

On your turn, you will play either a pizza or spell card from your hand and then draw back up to 3 cards at the end of your turn. The 55 pizza cards have a variety of different layouts with quarter and half slices on them. In order to play a pizza card, it must be laid adjacent to another card already in play and must connect crusts with another card in play if possible. When 4 sections of pizza come together to complete a pizza, 1 or 2 points are scored depending on the number of ingredients that make up that pizza; pizza scoring tokens tokens are immediately claimed. If the pizza is made up of 3 or 4 of the same ingredient, that ingredient's special ability is activated. There are three copies of 4 different spell cards that allow for additional cards to be drawn, pizza cards to be played from the discard pile, add scoring to a non-ability activating pizza, and play a half pizza section of your choice.

There are also 4 bonus scoring cards, each showing 1 ingredient, that have two abilities: 1) worth 3 points if in possession of a player at the end of the game 2) have a half pizza slice on one side that can be played onto the board instead of playing a card from a player's hand. In order to claim these cards, a player must create a pizza that is made up entirely of 1 ingredient. When that happens, that player takes the card and places it in front of them. The card stays there until either the player uses the half pizza or the game ends. However, if another player creates the same type of pizza, they steal it from whoever has it.

The board game NecroVirus is a zombie apocalypse game played by 2-6 players on a 2D or 3D board with miniature figures, vehicles, buildings, chits, cards, and dice.  It pits survivors in a wasted city against zombies, both of which are controlled by all of the players during the course of the game.  Several missions depict rescue, last-man-standing, PvP, and vehicle use making players utilize ever-changing strategies and tactics for a successful outcome.  No two boards are the same nor are the outcomes predictable right to the very end of a mission.

boardgame: U-BOAT COMMANDER: A Solitaire Game of U-Boat Warfare during WWII
description:
BACKGROUND
U-BOAT COMMANDER is a game where the Player assumes the role of a U-Boat Commander during WWII. As the Commander of a U-Boat, you are responsible for destroying Enemy Shipping and reducing the Allies Supplies. In this manner, you may shorten the war.

GAME OBJECTIVE: The objective of U-BOAT COMMANDER is to sink as many tons of Shipping as possible before the War Ends. Campaigns consist of a series of Missions where, as commander of a U-Boat, the Player must attempt to score as many Victory Points (VP) as possible to assist the war efforts. Campaigns can be Short (6 Patrols), Moderate (9 Patrols), or Long (12 Patrols).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Battle of Gods

The Titans have fallen to the hands of new gods. The Olympians struggle against one another for power, and now it is time for all mortals to choose sides. Which gods will you worship, Devotee? Battle one another to see whose gods are mightiest. Command beasts, summon mystical objects, and worship your gods to increase your power and cast spells to eliminate other Devotees.

Quick Review

After the Titans is a themed gateway card drafting game in a 100 card closed set.This game encompasses all the fun of deck building collectible card games, without the time investment found in similar games. Game play is designed to be brisk and competitive. The Core Set is made for 2-4 Devotees, and one game lasts approximately 5 minutes per Devotee.

Meet the Gods

The war Zeus waged against the Titans has ended. The Olympians now rule the Universe, and the Titans are imprisoned in Tartarus.  Following their final victory, the Olympic gods divided the world amongst themselves and are now in a struggle against one another for power.

Apollo
Apollo is the healer. He ensures you and your Beasts can last the battle. He doesn’t just heal, Apollo keeps Beasts dealing maximum damage. He’s deceivingly good.

Ares
Ares seeks the blood of his opponents. He has the most ruthless direct and single target damage spells.

Athena
Athena is wise and patient. She counters spells, steals Beasts, and controls the minds of other Devotees. She waits until an opponent is vulnerable, then sabotages them.

Zeus
Zeus is powerful but slow to rise, making use of Favors to strengthen Beasts and Static cards. He can also fortify structures and works well with Static Cards that will be released in future expansion packs.

The Witchborn: Enter Perdition is the immersive campaign-based miniature skirmish game for 2-6 players (miniatures for 2 players supplied in the core box). Each player creates a war clan of adventurers to explore the dangerous world of Perdition. Portray your warriors with detailed 28mm Reaper® Bones miniatures. Play campaign-style, where your warriors continually develop, find treasures, and level up between games—If they survive the perils of the adventure and the ever bloodthirsty undead - WitchBorn - of course.

• 32 Reaper® Bones miniatures (8 Elves, 8 Dwarves and 16 plains denizens) 
• Adventure guide, gorgeous 46” x 39” battlemap, and app to run the adventure
• 6 war clans books and 6 customizable rosters
• Campaign Rules for your continuing adventures
• Quick Start Rules to get you playing faster
• dice, condition markers, turn chits, combat rulers, and over 90 templates

The Witchborn: Enter Perdition includes 16 Reaper Bones miniatures and rules to build Dwarven or Elven war clans and 16 plains denizens monster miniatures which might appear during the adventures. The box also includes rules to for Guild, Norn, Orc and Paladin war clans (miniatures for these are NOT included in the core box but are available as The Witchborn: War Clans edition). The game can be played with whatever suitable 28-32mm models you may have lying around. Also included is the highly replayable Enter Perdition adventure, with unique battle map and rules and all the tokens and dice you need to play the game.

After its creation your war clan participates in a true “campaign game” with an expanding series of adventures. Each adventure has its own 46” x 39” battlemap depicting an exotic locale. The adventures are highly replayable and more adventures are available as expansions. In the adventure included in the box an airship crashes in the Kasel Plains, jettisoning potentially valuable cargo. Each player leads their war clan in a search for the treasure—but needs caution. The once gentle centaurs of the plains are all WitchBorn now.

During missions warriors’ discoveries, wounds, and horrible fumbles are handled by apps for Android or iOS which tell a unique story every time you play. Smart phone apps take the place of a game master or an overlord, guiding players through a variety of random encounters.

Once a war clan escapes the battlemap with the airship cargo, players adjust their rosters. The war clan roster is the central tool that allows warriors to level up and change after each adventure. Players spend money they earn to choose from hundreds of items to equip their war clans. The roster tracks changes, so players can watch their characters develop from inept beginnings to their rise as dominant heroes.

description:
A centuries long interstellar war leaves survivors of the two factions stranded on a planet, fighting each other as well as internal foes and the two indigenous people of the planet.

Storm of Steel is a sci fi skirmish miniatures game derived from Shieldbash. Each trooper has a simple profile but they fight quite differently from one another. The activation system used in Shieldbash and Shieldwall gives fast turns with unpredictable swings of initiative. Leaders, cavalry, skirmishers, medics, comms, area affect weapons, exotic melee weapons and more are included. The Shieldbash system means figures don't simply trade die rolls until someone runs out of hit points; instead troopers move around the battlefield as they are pushed back or dodge of weapons fire, and infiltrators appear seemingly out of nowhere.

Four included scenarios give a variety of tactical objectives to fight over, and a campaign system allows linking scenarios into Tales, with fighter progression between them.

boardgame: Alien Uprising: Cliff/Corner Crash Map Pack
description:
Both the Cliff Crash and the Corner Crash maps provide alternate crash site scenarios for Alien Uprising.

Cliff Crash
The idea behind Cliff Crash is, the right edge of the board acts like a natural shield for the crew of the Pioneer (the crashed space ship); but this is also a curse, as the number of spaces they will have to move to get to debris will be so much farther away!

Corner Crash
Similar to the Cliff Crash, the Pioneer is naturally shielded on the crash site. However, the far corners of the crash site are really difficult for the crew to reach!

Both maps greatly increase the difficulty of the game and should be considered "advanced" scenarios.

Tropes: Horror Story Edition the ever-changing party game of improvised storytelling and horror movie clichés. Tropes will quickly get the whole room laughing as you and your friends and family use the cards to tell a story using stereotypes from classic horror movies.

Will the Jock make it out of the Cabin in the Woods? What will the Camp Counselor find in the Wood Chipper? And what horrors are in store for the Prom Queen at the Abandoned Motel?

Tropes - Horror Show Edition is a cooperative story telling game. 2-6 players use story cards to create a hilarious horror story. When a player sets down a finale card, one of 12 characters meets their untimely demise. The player then claims that character card as a point, the player with the most character cards wins!"

"Tropes is for ages 13+ and can be played in about an hour. The great thing is that story cards can be played an infinite amount of ways, leading to stories that are always different
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Educational game for the under 10's. Each player is given a board with 9 large squares on it. Each square has a shape in it, diamonds, circles, etc.. Tiles are revealed one by one and the children have to recognise the shape and if it fits on their board. First to find their nine shapes, wins.

There are variants on this theme that can be used depending on the age of the children.

The boards and tiles are incredibly sturdy, better than games made today.

“Not Another One!” is a light print and play dice game where all players use the same dice rolls and same grids.

On a turn, roll the single dice and write the result into your grid. You have to roll the dice 9 times in order to fill the grid arranging the numbers so that, when added together in vertical and horizontal lines, you match the target numbers in the circles.
If you’re successful you get 5 minus points, but if your sum stands above or below the target number then you receive penalty points.

The player who has the least amount of points after three rounds is the winner!

All you need to play is:
x1 dice
Print out of the board for each player
Pen or Pencil

The dinosaurs are the greatest tragedy the world has ever known. For millions of years, the reptilian titans ruled the earth, crushing any creature that dared cross their path. Fast forward to the present. The dinosaurs have suffered a terrible humiliation, all that remains of the noble lizards is a creature called a “chicken,” a ball of meat, with delicious legs and a tiny head. But no longer! The time for de-evolution has come! With your help, the dinosaurs can rise again, piece by piece.

Build your dinosaur out of an amalgamation of parts, gain and use powerful adaptations, and wield a multitude of effects to be the last dino standing!

There's chaos in the café! Countless customers are waiting hungrily for tasty cakes, cookies, sweets and drinks while Ole, chef of the café, is thinking about retiring.
In the shoes of the relentlessly working café staff the players are creating fancy cakes and pies and everything else the customers are ordering. If they are busy enough to fill the whole load of orders before closing hour is there, Ole will be satisfied enough to retire carelessly and one of the players will become the new chef of Café Olé!

Café Olé is a cooperative game in which the 1-6 players:

    Reveal the topmost order-tile from the stack
    Use their dice-cards to gather 3-6 dice
    Roll their dice to gain the right ingredients for the open orders
    Try to gain all open order-tiles before the closing-hour-tile is drawn


That's it and it's a lot of fun. But beware of the "Special Treatment" and "Complaint" tiles!


German description
Im Café Olé ist die Hölle los! Unzählige Gäste warten hungrig auf feine Leckereien und zu allem Überfluss will Ole, Chef des Cafés, auch noch in Rente gehen.
Schlüpft in die Rollen des eifrigen Café-Personals und bereitet leckeren Kuchen, köstliches Eis und kalte und warme Getränke zu. Wenn ihr besonders fleißig seid und die Flut an Bestellungen abarbeitet, kann Ole beruhigt in Rente gehen und einer von euch wird der neue Chef des Café Olé!

Café Olé ist ein kooperatives Spiel, in dem die 1-6 Spieler:

    Jeweils das oberste Plättchen vom Bestellungsstapel aufdecken
    Ihre Würfelkarten nutzen um 3-6 Würfel werfen zu dürfen
    Die Würfel werfen um die richtigen Zutaten für die offen ausliegenden Bestellungsplättchen zu erwürfeln
    Insgesamt versuchen, alle offen ausliegenden Bestellungen abzuarbeiten, bevor das Ladenschlussplättchen aufgedeckt wird


Das ist schon alles und macht eine Menge Spaß. Aber passt auf die "Extrawurst-" und "Beschwerdeplättchen" auf!

A little girl is left alone on the topn of a burning hill and the fire is raging. She is in danger! the rescuers must climb up to the top of the hilltop to save her as fast as possible. But, the mission is not easy. They must watch out for the burning wood and be careful not to lose their way on the hill. Otherwise they will be slowed down, while the time is running out!! The perfect dice game for little ones.

Game-play:
Each player takes turns to roll a multi coloured dice, then move their rescuer to the next space on the board which matches that colour. If a '?' is rolled, that player must draw a card from the deck and follow the instructions; e.g move forward/backwards x spaces, swap places with another player etc... If a player lands on a fire, they miss a turn while they extinguish the flames. The first player to get to the top of the hill, rescues the little girl and is declared the winner.

Deal Corner is a quick game of arms trafficking in which you need bluff and memory in order to control a corner!

Deal Corner uses 50 weapon cards and 60 one million narcodollar bills.

A game is divided into two phases: the traffic phase, when players try to get the best weapons; and the combat phase, when they use these weapons.

The objective of the game is to survive the confrontation while saving up the maximum amount of bills.

All the components needed for a game of DEAL Corner can be found in the Deal: American Dream's box. Also available separately in the Kickstarter.

Blackout is a post-apocalyptic dungeon crawler for 1-4 players. Players take control of one or more characters and venture out into the Northern Wastes in search of glory and renown. Blackout is a mission-based leveling system that offers savage combat and rewarding exploration.

Blackout is designed for solo and co-op play. In addition, Blackout missions can be presented by a gamemaster if the players wish to experience a more narrative adventure. Blackout offers 15-20 hours of play.

The goal of the game is to get with all four pawns into one sector (II, III, V, VI) of the game board. The first player to achieve this wins the game. If a player gets three pawns in an sector, all other players have to vacate this sector. Placement and movement of the pawns is determined by two dies, a standard die (1-6) and a degree-of-latitude-die.

A second game rule is for a travel game, where the players are to get their pawn from their starting point (colored dot on the game board) to three destinations (determined by the draw of 3 "Orts-Karten" and back to their starting points. On the roll of a 6 the Player has to move his pawn and draw a "Zufalls-Karte" and follow the printed instructions on the card.

Pocket Imperium: Prosperity adds yet another layer of strategy to Pocket Imperium's minimalist gameplay. You must now guide your star empire to take advantage of changing new opportunities appearing across the galaxy.

At the beginning of the game, players set the Prosperity cards face-down in a row, which represents the new opportunities and bounty available to those who dare seize them.

Each round of the game, the next round's Prosperity card is revealed, allowing for players to plan to occupy specific systems, raise fleets or take over Tri-Prime, all of which reward the player with additional points.

Each card will also reward players with additional actions, depending on if they played a command card at a certain time in their turn, representing certain actions being more efficient due to market conditions.

Prosperity is meant for those who are looking for more of a "gamer's game" out of Pocket Imperium. It is appropriately sized for a microgame, and offers additional choices at both the tactical (action timing) and strategic (changing goals for bonuses) levels.

description: Diary of a Wimpy Kid - Don't Scramble The Egg Game. Can you take better care of the egg then Greg did in health class? Turn over a category card and read it out loud. Then start the timer on the egg. After you call out an answer to the category, toss the egg to another player. Act fast because you never know when the egg will splattttt! If that happens when you're holding the egg, you collect a card. And if you collect 3 of those, you are out of the game.

boardgame: Conflicts in American History: The Civil War – Module 1
description:
Conflicts in American History: The Civil War - Module 1 is a SPI published soapbox size wargame consisting of 9 copies of Antietam and 9 copies of Cemetery Hill both wargames from their Blue & Gray series.

There is an additional teachers instruction booklet, 2 historical briefings booklets, two test questions booklets and one Bulletin board poster to promote the class activity. The entire collection can be used for teaching in the classroom.

This wargame is extremely rare.

description:
Similar to Blackie's Maze in style, this is a game where you must adventure out and create farms, forts, jacks, and queens to protect your king, who is the center of it all.
 Build farms to supply more men for you to create. Build forts to disrupt your enemy's path. Create jacks for you to combat the enemy with.
 The goal is to discover all the possible cards and get the most points. The two ways to win are 1, discover all cards, or 2, not have enough cards left to battle an enemy.
 Use a basic card deck without jokers to lay out your yet to discover kingdom, and claim rule to your land.

Step into the role of a mafia don in the midst of a brutal mob war. Employing an army of wiseguys and shady tricks, you must eliminate all the other mob bosses. The prize? Absolute control of the criminal underworld.

The game is played utilizing dice rolls and cards. Each player draws action and unit cards. The unit cards are used to take over property cards, where they can then attack other players' unit cards by rolling dice. Each unit has bonuses and detriments that will alter the roll. Each player can also play action cards to aid their attack or impede the other players. Well balanced between chance and skill, players can often come back from the brink of losing to win the game.

http://www.8-bitgangster.com/
https://www.facebook.com/eightbitgangster

Objective: Gain most victory points by bringing home as many of your pawns (geese, ducks, chicken, young chicken) as possible - or as fox: catch many them.

Roll & Move Game, best with (4) 5 players

All players move around the same board like Pachisi; board is divided 10 segments with 6 spaces each. Payers are forced to start with a second pawn as soon as the first has left first segment. After a complete round a turnable fox (in the centre of the board) is spun - the segment he's pointing on is in danger: each involved player has a saving throw escaping into next segment - otherwise the pawn is caught by the fox. Depending on order of running in victory points are granted. Slight modifications with 4 or 3 players.

Cyborgs vs Earth Crew.
 With Earth's depleted resources and overpopulation, another planet must be found to support Human life, and time is running out. One of 11 teams of space explorers, Earth Crew's mission is to travel to Kepler-452b, Earth Twin exoplanet, and confirm potential support for human life.

But first the Crew must survive.

On entering Kepler-452b atmosphere, Earth Crew are attacked by Cyborgs, who invaded the planet already, merging with many of its primitive humanoids. Cyborgs have taken over communications with Earth and they're coming for the Crew.

Who will defeat the other in a battle for survival of their species, and domination of the Earth Twin planet?

Choose a side, Cyborg or Human, then trek your way through Kepler-452b terrain, fighting opponents, encountering strange Humanoids and plants, before taking over the opponent's command centre.

Cyborgs game is a unique sci-fi strategy game, both cooperative and competitive. The goal is to beat your opponent team and dominate the planet.

Six brave fourth-graders join forces to protect the playground from the tyrannical Dark Prince Ipal, but only one of them can be crowed Spell Master. Overcome treacherous obstacles and defeat the evils of recess by spelling!

In Magical Spell, you create words to earn dice, which you use to overcome enemies and obstacles.

On your turn, players will assemble Letter Cards to create words. Each letter grants the caster dice. The more rare the letter, the more powerful the die. To defeat encounters, players must exceed or match a combat value. Defeat the enemy and gain victory points. Fail and take wounds.

Magical Spell includes a deck of Letter Cards which also grant special abilities. Use F to create fire spells, or H to heal. The game is played with a polyhedral set of dice and invokes the feel of a light dungeon crawl set in the imagination of elementary school kids.

Objective: gain penalties - as little as possible - by flipping a rotor through a gate

Build up a gate on a table - flanked by a skittle and a backstop on each side. Use a curved needle to flip the rotor through the gate to opponent's side - avoid to hit a skittle/backstop; otherwise collect a penalty and try again. First player gaining 20 penalties has lost the game.

Note:
This game was invented in Germany before the Second World War. Based on the tools/materials used, one of the possible German publishers before WWII could be Edwin Mieg oPH (Mieg).

In this unpredictable game, each roll determines if you catch a fish, choose a fish from another player, give up a fish or if the fish gets away.

Contains 23 Cards; each with their own point value and facts about the fish.

The most points wins the game.

A quiz game about the music stars and their music. You will be on a musical travel from under the sea to the air - and beyond to the Universe!
First to get out of the "King of Rock'n'Roll" field win and become the the MusiQwiz (Music Wizard)

The backside of the board contains shorter version of the game.

Quiz race the board have five different "districts" from Start to Finish divided by orange paths;
On the orange paths you use the die to move, and some spots have question mark.
When coming to the the coloured districts you drop the die and only move when answering questions depending on the difficulty of the question (if you guess wring you can only move one space).

First to finish is the winner.

Iconic Heroes is the latest release in the Pathfinder Battles series of pre-painted plastic miniatures from WizKids Games and Paizo Publishing.

Iconic Heroes Set 1 includes six all-new miniatures featuring legendary characters from the Pathfinder Roleplaying Game universe:

Valeros, Iconic Fighter
Seelah, Iconic Paladin
Seoni, Iconic Sorcerer
Sajan, Iconic Monk
Lini, Iconic Druid
Droogami, Snow Leopard Animal Companion

Each Iconic Heroes set also includes exclusive Cards (one for each miniature) for use in the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game! These special Cards are only available in this Pathfinder Battles product!

description:
The Autobots and the Decepticons are at war, trying to occupy newterritories and set up bases across planet Earth!

Be the first to capture four territories by leading your Autobots or Decepticons into battle. As Commander, it’s your job to pick the most powerful Robot on each territory.

As an attacker choose a territory you think you can conquer with the Robots in your hand. Choose the Robot (or pair of Robots) that you want to use to win that territory and place them face down on the table. When both sides have played, turn over your Robot cards, count your power ratings. The highest score takes the territory.

Every Robot has two power numbers and Special Skills shown by the colours on his card. If your Robot’s skill colours match all the battle skill colours needed on that Territory, use the large number as your power rating. If they don’t, or you don’t have all the colours, the smaller number is that Robot’s power rating this turn. The highest number wins.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Class Deck – Barbarian
description:
Channel your Fury! Barbarians wield massive weapons and their own rage to create a potent and deadly combatant. The Barbarian Class Deck accessory for the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game allows players to bring these unpredictable warriors to any Base Set, including the demon-infested Wrath of the Righteous campaign!

The 109-card Barbarian Class Deck contains three new characters—including a new version of the iconic barbarian Amiri—and both new and recognizable allies, weapons, armor, and other boons to support your barbarian through an entire Adventure Path. This deck can also be used in the popular Pathfinder Society Adventure Card Guild organized play campaign.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
From the publisher:

Inspired by Seiji Kanai's Love Letter and the Lost Legacy games, Hatsu Koi is a microgame that follows in such gameplay traditions with beautiful anime-style artwork. Rediscover the joy of puppy love in this simple and elegant card game.

This 18-card "draw a card, play a card" microgame focuses on winning and losing. Are you lucky enough to win? Or will taking your chances lead to a broken heart?

User Summary:
Most of the 10 different abilities on the cards provide ways to win the round or cause players to be knocked out of the round. If there is no card to draw, the lowest number on the cards is the winner. Win two rounds, and you can win the game.

A thirst quenching card matching game!

When life deals you lemons, you make lemonade,of course! Collect Lemon, Water and Sugar cards to make lemonades. Make your lemonade even more delicious by adding CHERRY or LIME flavouring. Make it extra awesome by using ULTRA WATER! Serve it on a SUNNY DAY or with POPCORN! Secretly sabotage your arch-nemesis' lemonade with a sprinkling of icky YAK HAIR!

When Life Deals You Lemons is a super fun game for ages 5 and up, and was designed and drawn by Hannah (9), Miles (8) and Erica (5) Barker.

Mongols : La Grande Chevauchée (1194-1294) is a solitaire wargame simulating the Mongol expansion from 1194, when Gengis Khan becomes the chief of principal Mongol clans, to 1294, death of his grandson Kubilaï Khan.

The map displays the historical area of Mongol conquest till the end of XIIIe century, from Europe to the Sea of China and the Indian Ocean, from the Scandinavia to the lands of Egypt and Arabia.

1 turn - 10 years
1cm - 200km
324 counters
1 map (two piece) 56.6cmx40cm

The game lasts 10 turns

boardgame: Love & Laughter: The Game of Smiles
description:
The invitation of color pedagogue Frederike Herrlich makes a playful way a new approach to themselves and others, by the freedom of choice. Regardless of gender, age, culture and religion. Knowledge and personal experiences of individuals are recognized and contribute to original answers to philosophical questions at: not whether, but how we want. Or would you prefer to relax?
The game board shows a path to move which is built out of 3 circled which have field where the pawn can move from one to another circle.
Their are 4 kinds of event fields (drivem by Cards):

     2 types: Inform about something about his person or his life
     Opening a discussion about an issue
     ColourChild: Define parts of yourself with colours, based on the colour psychology 


Each player has a pawn moving on a path of the board, dice role driven.
Game ends when 1 player has finished his colour child. All other Players are allowed to finish also their colour child.

Seas of Iron: Rising Sun is the first expansion for the popular battleship combat game Seas of Iron. Rising Sun is a stand alone game that is also 100% compatible with Seas of Iron. The new game includes the addition of Aircraft Carriers, Fighters and new crew members.

The board shows 100 fields (10x10) which show 50 Muslim counties (each 2 times).
Each player has 3 to 7 country cards on his hand (dependent on number of players).
On Players turn he Plays one of his Cards and places one of his Stone on one of the 2 fields with the same country.
The choosen field has to be empty. If this is not possible he can choose any free field.
The first player (or team) which has first 5 markers in a line (horizontal, vertical or diagonal) wins.
The game is designed for maximum 12 players in maximum 4 teams.

Game of Life with an Islamic theme.

Easy rules: The player who collects the most Good Deeds and builds the largest Family wins the game.
Advanced rules: The player who collects the most Good Deeds (in Gold), the best Career, the best Cars. the best House and can completely fill out any sections of the Personal Player Card wins the game.
All these things are marked with collecting special tiles. For getting the different tiles the player has to hit Special fields for collecting, changing or trading the things.
The movement of the pawns is driven by Spinning a Move Spinner.

The Story behind of this dice driven game is the truck convoy to Romanian orphanages.
The board game shows multiple tracks from Germany to Romainia.
There are many different Action fields.
Some of them you need to hit, e.g. phone, gas station in each country and Orphanage in Romainia. On some fields you can upgrade your convoy. New truck, new trailer, good storage.
Some event fields (Cardstocks for the different contries), negativ event fields e.g. boarder control (user has to stop here, except he got a special card to move on.
The player wins, whos convoy has deliveres goods to 2 orphanages in Romainia and Comes first back to Germany.

boardgame: Auf den Spuren  des Völkerapostels Paulus
description:
The game plays at the time of the live of Paulus.

The board Shows the east Mediterranean Sea, square fields are marked. There are cities (numbered) on the board, which where visited by Paulus.

At the begin of the game 6 city cards are drawn, sorted by number (lowest on top) and placed on the board.

The Paulus pawn is placed on the city of the Card with the lowest number.
3 additional Cards are placed right to the city Card series.
A neutral pawn is placed on a path at the edge of the board. This path Shows fields with the numbers 1 to 3 and fields with P.
On a player's turn he rolls the die, moves the neutral pawn this number and normally gets the number of Cards the field shows.
The player can choose freely if he wants to get person cards or good cards.
The active player can trade Cards with the other Players.
The active player can found a parish in one of the 3 cities shown by the open cards. For this he has to give the 4 person and goods shown on the city card. dependent on the Position of Petrus he may pay less (neighbored area on the map 3, same field 2, but always the love card).
The player gets the city Card, a new one replaces it on the board.
The turn of the player is interrupted if he reaches with his dice roll the P field (exact or not). The pawn is placed on the P. Paulus pawn moves to the next city in the series. Each parish (owner of it) gets one word card. Additional one for some cities (Jerusalem, same area as Paulus).
Player continues with a new die roll.
Game ends when Paulus reaches the last city of the series.
Winner is the player with the most victory points.
Points: 1 VP for each word Card. Bonus for more than half or all of the word cards of a cathegory.

boardgame: Need for Speed Trading Card Game: Canyon Rush
description:
42 cards deck expansion for Need for Speed
The cards are known, it is not a blind booster.

boardgame: Need for Speed Trading Card Game: Beach Blitz
description:

In this terrain tile manipulation game, 2 to 4 Druids match wits to transform the terrain to match their own personal goal. Every Druid has a unique ability, and with the use of movement, standard actions, action cards, and well planned strategy, they set out to transform the land to their liking.

It's not worker placement, it's not tile placement, it's tile manipulation! No dice to decide your fate - just your cunning, wits, and planning. Are you ready to transform the land to the way you want it? After all, you are a Druid; you should be able to do that without any problem. But watch out for the other Druids disrupting your terraforming!

You win the game by completing your personal goal - even on someone else's turn! To complete your goal, all terrain tiles specified on your Goal Card must be face-up on the game board in the layout specified. While you are trying to complete your goal, your opponents are also trying to complete their own - which could disrupt your tactics and planning.

The standard game is played on a field of Terrain Tiles laid out in a 5 x 5 configuration. On your turn, you may Pass (take no moves or actions) and draw a Spell Card. Or you may take one Move Action, one Standard Action, and as many Free Actions as you desire (in ANY order). Then at the end of your turn, draw one Spell Card (yes, that means if you Pass, you get two Spell Cards). Download the Rules to learn how all the Actions work.

The Terrain Tiles are double sided and represent many types of terrain like mountains, water, desert, grassland, and frozen tundra, as well as special areas like a village or a tavern. But there are two very special terrain types: there is the Mystical Wild Tile that is a wild Terrain Tile, and then there is the Void Tile that no player can move through or land on.

Included with the game are four variants: one to make the game easier, one to make the game longer and more challenging, one that increases the size of the game area (to 6 x 6 tiles), and then there is the Black Druid variant, who adds a truly chaotic element to the game.

The Crime: Welcome to the beautiful world of Banff, Alberta: A playground of adventure . . . and intrigue! Charles Vandersnoot, the wealthy owner of posh ski resort, Mystery Mountain, shocked everyone in Banff when he announced he was dying of a rare disease. Even more shocking was when Charles was found murdered – killed by a single gunshot while riding his own chairlift!

Everyone is a suspect in this chilling crime! See if you can help expose the killer.

The Game: Spice up your party by adding a little mystery and a dash of murder to the evening. Just invite eight to ten guests to dress up and play the roles of the scheming suspects and you’re in for an evening of unforgettable fun! No one is who they appear to be . . . can you find the killer?

The Suspects: Bunny Vandersnoot, Cecilia Vandersnoot, Chet Jet-Set, Doctor T Bar, Franz Flash, Gertrude Greenrun, Hank “Hot Dog” Hooper & Rudy Red-Eyes
Extra Players (Non Suspects): Ranger Rick & Roxy Rundle

The Contents:
Party Planner
Eight Character Booklets
Two Character Booklets for bonus players
Ten Character Profi les to include with invitations
Ten Party Invitations and Envelopes
Eight Secret Clues
Compact Disc Co-host: Complete with introduction, evening commentary and solution to the mystery

boardgame: King of New York: Promo Cards
description:
This promotional set of 5 cards is to be used with the base game for King of New York. The set includes:


     Hibernation
     Improvised Weapons

     St. George Theater
     UN Headquarters


Part of the King of Tokyo series.

description:

On the top right corner: Anne Boleyn

description:
Star Trek - Attack Wing: U.S.S. Valiant Expansion Pack is part of the Wave 22 expansion packs from WizKids Games, and this expansion comes with one new ship and a new set of cards for Star Trek: Attack Wing.

The Valiant is a Defiant class ship.

description:
The Kumari is an Adorian battlecruiser. Under the command of Thy'Lek Shran, The Kumari participated in the Battle of Andorra and worked with Captain Jonathan Archer to destroy the Xindi superweapon.
This expansion adds a new ship and a new set of cards to Star Trek: Attack Wing.

This Banebrood Starter Box contains 6 highly detailed metal miniatures for gamers and collectors.

The enclosed miniatures represent a fully playable warband of 160 points, and in addition you get a printed version of the Quick-Start rules.

With this Banebrood warband and the Quick-Start rules you are able to immediately start playing Godslayer® – the fantasy miniatures skirmish game set in a bronze age world of sword and sorcery.

Get your starter box and start discovering the world of Godslayer®!

Contains:

     1x Quick-Start Rules 
     1x Gorelord model (45mm, metal)
     1x Pestbringer model (50mm, metal)
     1x Maldire Mongrels Leader model (40mm, metal)
     1x Maldire Mongrels Trooper 1 model (40mm, metal)
     1x Maldire Mongrels Trooper 2 model (40mm, metal)
     1x Maldire Mongrels Trooper 3 model (40mm, metal)

     2x 40mm round plastic base
     Gorelord profile & item cards

     Tokens


- The plastic bases are not textured.
- Some modeling is required.

"World of Mythology" is an unpredictable card game in which you play for the gods from ancient mythologies.

There is no odd stuff in the game - playing decks and special cards only!

There are 5 mythologies in the World of Mythology game: Greek, Egyptian, Hindu, Norse, and Slavic.
There are 60 playing cards with gods, 19 special cards, and 5 player cards with gods' powers in WOM CLASSIC.

One game round for 4 players lasts about 15 minutes! That's why WOM is a very comfortable way to have an interactive break wherever you are!

Every mythology is presented by 12 gods and goddesses

Greek gods: Zeus (12), Poseidon (11), Hera (10), Hades (9), Ares (8), Athena (7), Hermes (6), Hephaestus (5), Artemis (4), Dionysus (3), Apollo (2), Aphrodite (1).

Egyptian gods: Ra (12), Geb (11), Tefnut (10), Osiris (9), Horus (8), Nephtys (7), Set (6), Anubis (5), Isis (4), Toth (3), Ptah (2), Hathor (1).

Hindu gods: Shiva (12), Vishnu (11), Parvati (10), Ganesha (9), Skanda (8), Kali (7), Indra (6), Surya (5), Lakshmi (4), Varuna (3), Vayu (2), Kama (1).

Norse gods: Odin (12), Thor (11), Frigg (10), Loki (9), Tyr (8), Hel (7), Heimdall (6), Njord (5), Skadi (4), Forseti (3), Bragi (2), Freyja (1).

Slavic gods: Perun (12), Dazhbog (11), Dodola (10), Veles (9), Triglav (8), Morana (7), Stribog (6), Svarog (5), Vesna (4), Hors (3), Jarilo (2), Lada (1).

The playing cards are simple and graphic; all detailed information can be referenced on the big player cards. After a few plays of the game, players will begin to easily recognize all the powers of gods and icons.

So let's start the game:

1. Every player gets a random player card with a mythology (a trump for that game round) and gods' powers description.

2. All players receive a hand of 4 random cards from the shared deck.

3. Dealer shows a random card from the shared deck to assign a player for the first move.

4. WOM is a circle card game, so every player has to play 1 card per game circle: by beating the previous card or dropping his card to the opponent's stake.

5. You can beat an opponent's card with a senior card from the same mythology (Zeus with his 12 points beats any Greek card) or with a god from your mythology (your trump) if his armor is strong enough (trump Hera with her 10 points beats gods from other mythologies with their 1 to 12 points).

6. When you play a card from your mythology (your trump), you use its special power automatically. You should read aloud the power description from your player card to all the opponents.

7. The winner of the hand takes all 4 cards to his stake (his earned points deck). But sometimes the winner can lose some cards from his stake due to gods' special powers.

8. All players take 1 card from the shared deck. The new hand begins with the previous circle winner.

9. The game round ends when the shared deck and playing cards end. It's time to count points from the stake. The winner of the game round becomes a dealer.

10. You can play either by rounds (counting just victories) or by reaching the exact point level (victory by reaching 500 points, for example).

Every character has a special power and an armor. Powers are based on ancient myths (Zeus beats opponents with lightning and Thor can use a thunder hammer, for example).

Armor level depends on god's position in adapted mythological hierarchy. There are gods and goddesses in every mythology whose power is to summon a special card.

There are 3 types of special cards: Wealth, Secret, and War.

Wealth is a card with bonus points: +10 (6 items), +20 (3 items), +30 (1 item). Available for Apollo (arts), Ptah (arts), Isis (fertility), Bragi (poetry), and Jarilo (fertility).

Secret is a card with a secret combination bonus: "Get +5 points for each Greek card in your stake", for example. Available for Athena (wisdom), Toth (knowledge), Frigg (wisdom), and Vesna (nature).

War is a deck of just two cards: Death and Life. It's available for all gods of war: Ares, Horus, Skanda, Tyr, and Triglav. If you play the god of war from your mythology, your opponents have to play a risky mini-game similar to Russian Roulette.

We also plan for great future decks: Chinese gods, Japanese gods, gods of Mesoamerica, gods of Science, The Outsiders, and Good vs. Evil double deck.

Naval Battle in a fantasy world!

Especially for the youngest fairy tales and adventures admirers we present 'Naval Battle'. 2-sided map of fantastic marine world + color models of the ships = Real Sea Chess for your Kids!

Players engage in a struggle for supremacy over the sea. Each player has four ships:
1) the mighty Battleship, 
2) multi-gun Galleon, 
3) fast Frigate and 
4) nimble small Brig.

Ships can move around the playing board and shoot. To win the game, the player must either drown the opponent’s Flagship (Battleship), or capture the enemy base.

Ships miniatures in the Naval battle are equivalent for the Admiral game miniatures.

2 sided board increases variety of the game.

boardgame: THE IDOLM@STER Cinderella Girls: Idol Produce Deck Set "Cinderella Stage"
description:
Based on the anime adaptation of Japanese mobile phone game "THE iDOLM@STER CINDERELLA GIRLS". Player take the role of producer of group of pop idols, your goal is to make them reach stardom.

This is one of deck building game which each player have to have their own deck. There are three set: pink(cute), blue(cool) and orange(passion), which represent character type from original mobile game. You buy a card, play various cards to collect mission and first player to earn 10 victory points win the game.

Let's make these girls successful!!

The merciless battle between Powers of Light and Darkness started in the old times. Powers of darkness retreated, but it was just a temporary respite.

Be prepared for the full battle with the Manticore! Investigate the forest and construct roads to the magical armor and amulets. Monsters – servants of darkness – will hinder you. Time is limited: as soon as magical fetters are off, the monster will start his fire path to the Castle in order to become the Absolute Lord of the Forest. Don’t waste a minute: find the artifacts that are the only chance for a victory.

Two variants of the game: team-play against the Manticore (against the system) where the team either together wins or loose; and individual game face-to-face with the Monster.

The legend returns!

ESSENCE OF THE GAME:
   
Players begin their journey in the Forest world of the City which is soon to undergo an invasion of the awakened Manticore. At first the players should find their way into the abandoned workshops and places of power around the City – where they will be able to acquire weapons and artifacts that enhance certain characteristics of the hero. Pathfinding is carried out with laying on the board of tiles depicting the road. Each of the players paves the way for their own, but can also use the road paved by other players. Some tiles contain a monster - if a player has laid out such tile, then a monster token is placed. Player may try to circumvent a monster, but may also fight him.

After all the tiles are exhausted, the Manticore awakens and begins moving in the direction of the City. If the Manticore reaches the City - all the players loose the game, so the task of the Players is to defeat the Manticore before her arrival in the City.
    
Battle with monsters and with the Manticore is made with a die roll. However, in addition, there are also random battle cards that determine what type of attack (magic, melee, shooting) is used in this round of the fight (see ‘Conducting a fight’). Card is opened blindly, and can bring a pleasant surprise (attack with the most developed hero’s skill) or not pleasant. This encourages players to improve different abilities of their heroes.
AWARDS
Winner of the "Baby Expo Ukraine 2018" (best strategy game)

How do a pig sound? A zebra? A saw? A gardner? In Kockatoo you need to be good at making sounds, so others can guess which card you're holding. You need to sit on your hands, so you can't use hands and feet to mime.

One player takes a card from the stack, and tries to make the sound. The other players have to guess among the 30 cards visible on the table. But be careful! You only have one guess per round. The one who guesses correctly and the sound maker gets one point. if no one guesses correctly then no gets point.

As the play goes on, the options are limited, so the games get easier, so you need to be very fast in the end.

Very simple game based on Disney's Frozen. Main goal is to get to the castle of Elsa, princess who froze the whole kingdom of Arendelle. By throwing dice, you move your meeple, possibly stepping on fields with special effects such as "wait one turn" or "move four fields forward". The player who first reaches frozen castle wins.

From the back of the box:
Join the rescue expedition on behalf of Arendelle Kingdom. Princess Anna and her friends try to overcome many pitfalls and obstacles on the journey to safe [sic] her sister Elsa and whole Kingdom. What will win ice or live?

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Other Worlds – Caverns of Cynder
description:
The Caverns of Cynder is a hostile Alien World, deep underground and overflowing with lakes of molten magma! There is some alien architecture such as pathways and ductwork to funnel the lava in practical and ceremonial ways. In the Caverns, Dark Stone is melted into the magma itself and can be found in liquid form! This has led some industrious explorers, both human and otherwise, to set up mining equipment in an attempt to siphon it out of the rock itself. Of course the Heroes must always beware of the foul creatures lurking the corridors as well as those that will rise up out of the molten rock itself to attack — not to mention the constant threat of eruptions and rising lava pools at your feet!

Shadows of Brimstone: Caverns of Cynder Expansion offers a new Otherworld for players to explore, with a set of 18 new double-sided map tiles. There are new Encounter cards, artifacts, Otherworld threats, and map cards, as well as three of the new lava men Enemy.

Cynder Tiles include a mechanism in which you can take damage if you pass over certain fiery spaces. If you have a high Agility you get to roll two dice, so swift and nimble characters are less likely to burn. Cynder also has mechanisms in which Clothing items make you susceptible to heat exhaustion and burning, so there's some interesting decisions to be made about how many items to keep equipped from turn to turn.

boardgame: Grave Robbers from Outer Space Resurrected Edition
description:
Grave Robbers from Outer Space the Resurrected Edition (GROS:RE) is a reboot of the original B-movie card game, Grave Robbers from Outer Space (GROS).
Back from the dead, with a new layout and art, GROS:RE introduces new gameplay elements to the classic game.
GROS:RE is a strategy card game for 2-6 players. Players are competing to complete the "best" b-movie. Characters and props add to the value of a player's movie, while locations may add or subtract depending on the location in question. FX cards do all manner of different things. Creature cards are used to attck an opponent's movie in an effort to kill off characters.
Cards have a cost to play (anywhere from 0-3 "popcorn"), and some cards generate popcorn. If a player can't afford the cost of a new card by using their cards in play, additional popcorn can be generated by discarding cards from hand. The popcorn mechanic makes players decide between playing a card to do something now, or holding onto it to pay for something later.

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Ancient Myths and Legends
description:
The Myths and Legends Continue

A long time ago, before the Eternal Order of Perseus was formed, all sorts of ancient, terrible magics and evil creatures rampaged across the earth. As the Tomb has traveled our universe, moving across the ley lines of otherworldly energies, it has been the harbinger of mysterious, supernatural, inhuman forces. Now, only the audacious adventurers of the E.O.P. can stop these powers, once lost and long forgotten to the depths of the Tomb, now returned to cry havoc.

This expansion presents six new and exciting scenarios, each with an immersive story and deadly creatures! Will you win the race for the Holy Grail against the German Super Soldier Maelstrom Squad? Or survive the night with a Tomb filled with vampire mobsters? Can you protect an ancient spider queen while she spins her magical web? Find Captain Nemo’s submarine before the Russians steal it or the Hell Golem sinks it! Prevent a deranged doctor from harnessing Medusa’s magic for his evil plans. Stop the ancient nemesis of the E.O.P., the draconians, from returning to Earth!

To aid you in your quest, included are six diverse adventurers, equipped and ready to venture into the Tomb. In addition, there are six lavishly illustrated room tiles to explore. While on these new expeditions, you’ll find new items, artifacts, story cards, companions, and creatures—all in your quest to discover the Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Ancient Myths and Legends!
family: Game: Secrets of the Lost Tomb

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: The Great Apocalypse
description:
The End is Near!

Thousands of years before recorded history, when man was still infantile, the eternal powers protected Earth with the consent of the gods. When those gods saw mankind mistreat the world, though, the deities saw fit to create two horrific punishments: the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse and da Vinci's inventions. These ungodly forces on Earth chime the resounding gong of the end of days, and now only an expedition dispatched by the E.O.P. can prevent the Apocalypse from destroying our world.

This expansion pack includes three great scenarios and the giant Journey to the Tomb tile, each adding great gameplay and immersive stories that will thrill your senses. Will you survive the plagues of Egypt and escape the grasp of Death himself? Can your team undo the prophecy and make their own fate in a race against the Four Horsemen? Will you recover the secret to da Vinci’s greatest invention—and greatest mistake—in order to stop the dead from rising and destroying humankind?

The Journey to the Tomb a dangerous one and the great outdoors can lead to great treasures but also great hazards. By making this journey, the adventurers can reap the rewards of their well-stocked nautical plane, but they must face the beasts of the island’s jungles.

To aid you in your quest, included are two new adventurers, equipped and ready to venture into the Tomb. In addition, you’ll find two lavishly illustrated rooms to explore. While on these new expeditions, you’ll find new items, artifacts, stories, companions, and creatures—all in your quest to discover the Secrets of the Lost Tomb: The Great Apocalypse.
family: Game: Secrets of the Lost Tomb

boardgame: Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Reign of Terror
description:
The Epic Adventure Through Time!

A long time ago, before the Eternal Order of Perseus was formed, all sorts of ancient, terrible magics and evil creatures rampaged across the earth. As the Tomb has traveled our universe, moving across the ley lines of otherworldly energies, it has been the harbinger of mysterious, supernatural, inhuman forces. Now, only the audacious adventurers of the E.O.P. can stop these powers, once lost and long forgotten to the depths of the Tomb, now returned to cry havoc.

Presenting the Epic Adventure campaign booklet: Take all your scenarios from the Secrets of the Lost Tomb universe and play them in an epic campaign with its own scenario, The Epic Conclusion! Will you be able to travel through time and space to stop the Time Terrorist before it’s too late?

This expansion also presents two new, exciting standalone scenarios, each with immersive stories and challenging creatures.

Will you find Leif Ericson’s Helm before the mercenary Viking princess can? Can you defend the Tomb against the horrific and all-powerful Emperor Drakkus and his army of dragon worshippers before he makes his final transformation?

To aid you in your quest, included are new, diverse adventurers, equipped and ready to venture into the Tomb. While on these new expeditions, you’ll find new items, artifacts, story cards, companions, and creatures—all in your quest to discover the Secrets of the Lost Tomb: Reign of Terror!
family: Game: Secrets of the Lost Tomb

description:
Flippin Monsters is a card game that employs both deck-building and memory mechanics. Players are given 10 starter cards and cards will be in the middle to capture.

The starter cards are placed faced down and apart from each other. On the player's turn, they can flip over any 5 of these cards they wish to help them purchase new cards or to earn points.

While some Monsters allow you to purchase other Monster cards, others give you actions like being able to flip over another card or flip over an opponent's card. The trick is remembering where you left that one Monster that allows you to do that one action you really need.

Make Me a God is a card game that revolves around heroes and monsters of Greek Mythology. In this game, demigods, monsters and humans all battle to gain super powers from the ancient temples of the Greek Gods. Each player can control up to 3 characters at a time, which are used to gain power-ups from the temples and to attack other player’s characters. As a character gains more power-ups, they become stronger and harder to kill. When a player’s character successfully gains all 6 powers without being killed, that character becomes a God and that player wins the game.

Set deep in the enchanted forests of the Netherwood, the inhabitants of the mouse kingdom wage a constant war against their adversaries, both from the vermin outside their guarded borders and the secretive conspirators within. This first adventure within the Netherwood explores the mouse rangers of the Northern Waywatch Chapters. Hailing from within the slate walls of the Keystone Fortress, they maintain eternal vigil over their sacred wardens. The legendary hero of the Watch, Chaptermaster Gorix Stormfeather, is preparing plans in the candlelit chambers of his fortress for the defense of his realm. His brave rangers patrol the borders, bracing for a clash that will inevitably come. The fist tightening its grip around their way of life is that of the Shadow King himself, the pale rat Vermithrang, and his legion of rodent warriors.

The Shieldbash system narrows its vision towards the small-scale conflicts of 5-15 fighters per side, involved in Skirmishes depicting a particular encounter between warbands. These encounters may be isolated or part of a Tale, a linked series of encounters where characters and conditions carry over from one fight to the next. Bash can be played on a battlefield as small as 2’x2’. A fighter is usually a single warrior on a 20mm (or 40mm) base, but it can also be a beast or group of lesser warriors. Defined by a few, simple attributes and abilities, each fighter is uniquely suited to his role on the battlefield, without bogging down the commander with unnecessary complication.

During a Round of battle each player will take turns as the active commander. His opponent will draw a Stone for him on his turn, allowing him to assign Surges to his fighters. These Surges allow the fighters to perform actions, which include moving about the battlefield, attacking enemy fighters, or other tasks performed in pursuit of victory. In addition, each commander has access to the resource Mischief. Mischief allows a commander to perform extra movement, modify a die roll, or execute other minor actions that enable him to bend the rules to his favor. However, the active commander’s opponent must agree upon the expenditure of Mischief, creating a dynamic of haggling and compromise.

This game's protagonist is Prince LanLing. His name is Gao ChangGong, an ancient Chinese legendary general of the Northern and Southern Dynasties. According to historical  records, Gao ChangGong not only brave and fierce, and the face is very beautiful, like a woman. He worried that he could not intimidate the enemy on the battlefield every time he regarded terror mask over his face.

AD564, Northern Qi's enemy Northern Zhou sent 100,000 troops besieged LuoYang ,Gao Changgong led only 500 cavalrymen and fought through an army of Northern Zhou, which was attacking the city with 50,000 soldiers. He fought bloody way to the gates the city, surprising the defenders. The soldiers of LuoYang didn't recognize him, so he took off his helmet and mask. The soldiers in the city rejoiced at his arrival and were refilled with courage. They opened the gates and joined the battle outside the city. Soon the army of Northern Zhou was defeated.

In order to celebrate the victory, the soldiers composed a famous song and dance "Prince Lanling in Battle” (蘭陵王入陣曲). This song and the dance are long lost in China itself, however, it was introduced into Japan during the Tang Dynasty and is still being performed in some ceremonies today

boardgame: Pixel Lincoln: The Deckbuilding Game – Expansion 2-4: Advanced Mode
description: These are the cards that will make you sweat. There are 2 new game mode cards that allow the players to choose how difficult they want to play. Plus special meat power cards that take the bosses to the next level of difficulty. Also includes 6 new checkpoint cards and save files. Requires Pixel Lincoln: The Deckbuilding Game to play.

Since 1568, the north and south sides of Pisa (Tramontana und Mezzogiorno) compete yearly on the Il Gioco del Ponte - the battle for the bridge. Which strategy will lead your side to victory? What surprises do your opponents have in store for you? Only the most cunning players will overcome their opponent's assault and be declared the king of the bridge!

The boardgame implementation of this spectacle is a collector's item and artistic masterpiece, in which each of the figures is handmade and hand-painted. You pick a side and engage in a thrilling battle with two, three, four or six players. You'll have to divide your strength wisely, and adjust to the strategy of your opponents, in order to also push the handmade (and in real life seven ton) cart to the other side of the river.

How do you play?

The board game is based heavily on the real-world Il Gioco del Ponte event. There are always two sides competing against each other, and the basic rules of the two-player game also apply to the higher player counts. In the two-player version, each player selects a side (Tramontana or Mezzogiorno) and distributes their strength points across six districts. 
To start a round, each player picks a magistrature. During one round, players choose one attack card each. A rock-paper-scissor-mechanism decides which player is to act, the winner moves the cart and both players loose strenght equal to the chosen attack cards and each chooses a new attack card. A round is over, if one side has no strenght left or if the cart is pushed completely to the opposite side. A game contains of at 6 rounds, a tie-braker decides in case of a tie. Strategy cards might be played as well to change the effects o the attack cards.

The Italian and English version are already available, for the German version there is a crowdfunding campaign running until July 5th 2015.

boardgame: Kaj & Andrea: På Spileventyr med H.C. Andersen
description:
Collection of three games for two children with Kaj & Andrea from Danish children's TV, where they are playing with the fairy tales from Hans Christian Andersen:

Game 1: Find the 10 pictures from your fairy tale; Image Lotto style with two difficulty levels. First one to find their 10 images wins

Game 2: The Fairy Tale picture that dissipated; Show the pictures from the fairy tales - remove one and let the child guess which one is missing.

Game 3: Read, Listen and find the right fairy tale pictures; Read the fairy tales (included in the booklet that also contains rules) and let the kids find the picture that shows what happens right now. The one with most right ones wins.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Battle of Carn Dûm
description:
Description from the publisher:

"Chief among their foes was the dread realm of Angmar and its sorcerer king."
     –J.R.R. Tolkien, The Return of the King

The Battle of Carn Dûm is the fifth and penultimate Adventure Pack in the Angmar Awakened cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game.

Over the course of the Angmar Awakened cycle, the heroes of Middle-earth have become aware of an evil that threatens the North. Now, the time has come to strike. With its sixty new cards, The Battle of Carn Dûm introduces a challenging, combat-intensive scenario that takes your heroes into the heart of Angmar where you'll need to fight your way through hordes of Orcs and other creatures in order to prevent an ancient evil from returning to the world. However, if the walls of Carn Dûm and the raw might of the assembled Orcs and Werewolves aren't enough to slow you, you'll still need to deal with the dread Captain that leads them and the foul and shadowed Sorcery that fills the air.

Nonetheless, your heroes and their courage are not to be underestimated. Along with its scenario, The Battle of Carn Dûm introduces a new Dúnedain hero and twenty-seven player cards (three copies each of nine different cards), including powerful events for each Sphere of influence. These continue to round out the new mechanics explored within the cycle, as Leadership and Tactics players gain new Valour effects, Lore players gain an event that allows their Dúnedain or Rangers to avoid a scenario's deadliest enemies, and Spirit players gain a Noldor-themed card that plays only from the discard pile. With their new allies and these powerful events, your heroes just might stand a chance...

description:
Description from the publisher:

Control your data. Control your destiny.

Data and Destiny is the fourth deluxe expansion for Android: Netrunner The Card Game, focusing on media giant NBN and three groundbreaking Runners.

In the world of Android: Netrunner, knowledge is just about everything, and no one has more or better access to the knowledge that humanity has collected and stored on the network than info-giant NBN. After all, not only does NBN boast the most pervasive data network ever imagined, it literally controls the infrastructure, media, and messages that define modern society. Accordingly, amid the 165 cards from Data and Destiny (three copies each of fifty-five different cards), you'll find a wealth of new media-focused assets, agendas, and resources. You'll be selectively targeted by new advertisements and tagged by new ice as NBN launches the initiatives of three new divisions, each of which rolls out with a new identity card.

Meanwhile, even as Data and Destiny lends new strength to NBN's efforts to shape the world in which we live, it unleashes chaos upon that world in the form of three new Runners. One is a network security officer sent to hack servers with the full blessings of Globalsec. One is a bioroid mysteriously set free from the Third Directive. One is a mysterious and predatory presence on the net, devouring all that it encounters. Each represents a unique take on the Runner's experience and, more than that, each introduces a new Runner mini-faction, so that for the first time since the Core Set, Data and Destiny explores brand new Runner paradigms.

description: Pentris is an abstract tetris like game for 1-2 players. Each player has got the same wood set (12 different tiles, each three times). A Java APP or a card tells you what tile you have to use. The players stack their pieces together and they are trying to get the least negative points.

Up to 5 Wacky Racers try to be the first to reach space 360. They can chose for themselves, which way to follow. Movement is determined by a die. Each player holds 10 cards he can use to hinder the player to his right. Whenever a player plays a card, he draws a new one fom the deck.

boardgame: Start und Ziel: Das große Wicküler-Rallye-Spiel
description: Each player joins the race with 3 cars. Movement is determined by a die. The first player to get all 3 cars to the finish line wins the game. The players are hinderd by 7 traffic signs on the red spaces, which can't be passed by. Only a player who lands direct on a space with a traffic sign can remove the sign and put it on another space to hinder another player. If a Player gets on a green space he has to draw an event-card and follow it's instructions.

The White’s Tale board game makes players “become” a white-tailed deer and thrive by taking advantage of their environment and avoiding perils unique to nature’s four seasons. The game is fun for kids and benefits retailers, too.

“The white-tailed deer’s keen senses, complicated communication system, and commuter-like daily living habits make it essential that hunters understand the deer’s world,” said Emily Beach, the Archery Trade Association’s manager of education and curriculum development. “In the game, players are white-tailed deer, trying to survive and raise fawns. It sounds tough, but The White’s Tale makes learning child’s play.”

The game was created as part of the ATA’s “Explore Bowhunting” program, which teaches kids of all ages the basic skills of hunting with a bow and arrow. Students in “Explore Bowhunting” play The White’s Tale to learn how animals follow patterns when traveling, eating and mating. Students then use that knowledge to their advantage while hunting.

With this classic-style board game, players learn the natural history of white-tailed deer. Players must collect food and water through all four seasons, and breed successfully by collecting five fawns while avoiding predators, hunters, diseases and other unexpected dangers. It’s recommended for two to six players 8 and older.

Although The White’s Tale doesn’t teach players specific hunting tactics, it educates future hunters.

To give new residents wonderful days, every player will work to have the new residents meet with the Nora furniture.

So, become friends with Nora furniture and complete a set of necessary furniture for your shop.
