boardgame: Zombicide: Black Plague Special Guest Box – Carl Critchlow
description:
A set of three Survivors and one necromancer for Zombicide: Black Plague designed by Carl Critchlow.  The three survivors include:
Thrud the Barbarian
Persephone Pursuivant
The Black Currant

And the necromancer is:
To-Me Ku-Pa

Carl Critchlow is a UK based illustrator and comic book artist.

He first created the character of 'Thrud the Barbarian' while still at art college in 1981, Thrud went on to feature in his own comic strip in Games Workshop's 'White Dwarf' magazine for five years during the 1980s and in a series of Eagle Award winning self-published comics in 2002 - 2007 (now available as a collection from Titan publishing).

As well as Thrud he has worked for many companies in the UK and US including DC comics, 2000AD and has illustrated over 200 individual cards for Magic the Gathering.

There are 3 games included:
The Gold Transport Raid: One player has 4 indians which whom to capture the gold transport and get it to the edge of the game board. The other player has 4 cowboys, which try to escort the gold transport to the fort.
Cattle Raid: The indians try to steal as many of the 6 cattle as they can, while the cowboys try to get the cattle from the waterhole to the farm. 
Cowboy-Lotto: Up to 8 Ppayers get a cowboy or an indian to occupy the picture on the game board, which corresponds with the number on their playing piece. Movement is clockwise and determined by a die. The words on the pictures are covered by the letter token. When a player gets on a picture, he may take a letter token (if available) he needs to form the word from his starting picture. The first player to complete his word wins the game.

Description from the publisher:

Your goal in Karma Mini Round is to get rid of all your cards, although in this elimination game everyone wins except for the last player holding cards.

Each turn, you must play a card of equal or higher number to avoid picking up the entire discard pile. Move through your cards faster by playing doubles. The deck includes four types of Karma cards; they can get you out of a jam, but be careful because in this game what comes around goes around...

Five Crowns Mini Round is a five-suited rummy-style card game that's a smaller version of Five Crowns with fewer cards, although the fifth suit of stars and a rotating wild card are still present.

Your goal is to combine all of your cards into sets and runs, be the first to go out, then watch the others scramble as they get one last chance to cut their losses. With each round, the number of cards dealt increases and the wild card changes keep players on their toes.

boardgame: Stadt Land Spielt Limitierter Sonderdruck Tag des Gesellschaftsspiels
description:
A single punch board containing expansions for 4 different games - Castles of Burgundy, Rokoko, Helios, and Limes.

Expansions are:

The Castles of Burgundy: 5th Expansion – Der Lustgarten (Pleasure Garden)
Rokoko Erweiterung - Edle Kleider (Fancy Dresses Promo)
Helios: Riese und Ballonfahrerin Erweiterung 
Limes: Der Leuchtturm (The Lighthouse)

It was released for the Stadt Land Spielt event that took place on September 14, 2014 throughout Germany.

Note that the Rokoko expansion is misprinted and thus unusable (see the relevant thread here)
Funemployed: Seasonal Help, an expansion deck for Funemployed, contains over 100 holiday-themed jobs and qualification cards (e.g., Mall Santa).

From the back cover of the rulebook:

Panzer Squash is a not so serious clay combat game between WWII tanks that you design!

Players craft their vehicles and combat to the death.

Completely compatible with the other Squash series games by Tri Tac Systems.
publishers: Tri Tac Games LLC (Tri Tac Talents)

description:
From the back cover of the rulebook:

This game details super tank combat in the late 21st and early 22nd centuries. This is a stand alone game. It may also be used with the Cassiopeian Empire role playing game as optional rules for tactical combat.

No map is included. You the player make your own map using the outline that is provided. This makes the game different every time you play.

This is a diceless system. Combat is done on a offense vs defense chart. Two vehicle sheets are provided. This gives you two of each type vehicle. Items needed for play. Blank paper, several different colored markers and a ruler. For two or more players.

PrisMix is a color spectrum card game. Players mix and match 12 prismatic colors to create 30 colorful scoring combos with prisms. Players want to strategically create color mixing combos to play multiple cards from their hand at a time to quickly play out all the cards in their hand first to win. So even though a player may have more cards in their hand... they can surprise their opponents by creating color mixing combos with 2, 3, or even 6 cards in a single play. May the best color mixer win! Quick prismatic fun with family and friends is what you will have when playing PrisMix.... let's play!

True Story gets players telling true stories from their lives and teaches storytelling skills in the process.

Gameplay: Each players draws a Theme Card from the deck and tells a true story inspired by that theme. Theme Cards are memory cues to help you think of a story, from your own life, that you feel good about telling, e.g. Unknown Territory: wandering off the map, wild beasts & lands await, words not yet invented. With each round come new theme cards and new stories, plus new story challenges like longer stories, vivid descriptions, suspense, and self-reflection.

Goal: Players earn points telling a series of increasingly complex stories that include specific story elements. At the end of the game, points turn into votes (called Award Points). The player who receives the most Award Points is declared Master Storyteller.

Townsmen is a game for 1 to 4 players where you have to manage a small Town in the Colonial age.

Every turn each player have to play 4 cards and, at the end of their turn they have to replenish their hand with 4 new cards from his deck. Each card provide two different actions to choose from. Some of them give you resources and other give you actions that are used with the personal board to add new cards to your deck or to develop these cards with more powerful cards, etc.....

There are also buildings to build, that give the player new special actions to activate every turn.

Race around the world with Phileas Fogg and his faithful companion Passepartout. Explore the depths of the world's oceans with Captain Nemo. Set forth today on your own extraordinary voyage!

Extraordinary Voyages is played in a series of rounds, over multiple boards using each player's individual deck. A player may draft a card, play up to three cards from their hand, or go adventuring.

Around the board will be various goods you need to acquire while adventuring, but the main goal is to finish any one of the three maps before any of your opponents!

Description from the publisher:

It just doesn't feel like a dungeon worth exploring without hordes of undead! Munchkin Undead adds fifteen new monsters, curses, and items all dealing with the formerly living. Get your pointy sticks and your chainsaws ready because it's time to kill them all over again!

Description from the publisher:

You can't clear thousands of dungeons without making a few frenemies...so that's what we did! As long as your imaginary frenemies are safely stored inside their blister, they help you in combat — but if you release them to get at the cards inside, you can't use their rule anymore. Oh, temptation!

As long as the Munchkin Imaginary Frenemies package remains unopened, you may keep your imaginary frenemies in front of you as a +2 combat bonus. It cannot be lost to Curses, Bad Stuff, or theft, and stays with you even if you die.

At any time, you may open the package, releasing your frenemies, and draw as many Treasures as there are players in your game. Keep one of the Treasures for yourself and give each other player one each. Sign the two Door cards inside, and put them in your hand as well. When you play a card that you have signed, it has double effect. Save this card so you remember this rule in the future!

Outrun your formula rivals on the city freeway!

The excitement of the arcade racer comes to the table top. 2-4 players compete in open wheel racing on the city freeway. Reach top speed, dodge traffic, pass junctions and turbo in a battle to tame the Formula Freeway!

GAMEPLAY
Race by selecting High or Low gear followed by drawing one of two movement cards from the race deck. Decide to play this card or to move another racer in the hope that the second card will be better strategically. Win by driving through all freeways of the race distance and crossing the finishing line first.

FEATURES
Switchable double-sided mats creating an infinite rolling freeway and randomly closed junctions with traffic cones. Selectable gears, turbos and moving traffic vehicles. Choose to race less junctions or tackle the full distance of all five freeway junctions.

INCLUDES
Two double-sided mats, four race cars with turbos, ten traffic cars, two cones, gear & movement cards and rules.

Description from the publisher:

The Ancient Uprising has been put down, and a fragile peace again holds in the galaxy...but suddenly, everything changes. Several new factions rise to disturb the status quo. The Unity nanomachines bend energy and matter to their will. The Shaper ships tear the spacetime fabric apart, while insignificant Octantis factions take a developmental leap after leap forward. It almost seems like the base laws of the universe no longer apply.

Eclipse: Shadow of the Rift introduces several new mechanisms to Eclipse, including Time Distortion, Evolution and Anomalies, as well as several new Rare Technologies, Developments and Discoveries. It includes two new player boards with three new different alien species from which to choose. Due to its modular design, you can use all of these additions or just some of them in any game of Eclipse according to your preferences and play style. The expansion does not require the Rise of the Ancients expansion, but is fully compatible with it.

As the shadows unfold from the rifts of spacetime, can your civilization stand unbroken?

boardgame: Zombicide: Black Plague Special Guest Box – Adrian Smith
description:
A box set of four Zombicide: Black Plague survivors that were designed by Adrian Smith, and come from his highly acclaimed graphic novel The Chronicles of HATE. The survivors include:
Cul'Nar
Jorvak
Sha'Keel
Gurbak

Adrian Smith (born 1969 in England, United Kingdom) is a illustrator/concept designer living in Scotland.
Best known for his work with Games Workshop and numerous similar companies, Adrian has also done concept design work for computer games companies such as THQ , EA, RIOT, Gameloft and others. Other credits include a short comic series in France written by Pat Mills and book illustrations alongside renowned artists from around the world.
Recently Adrian has been self publishing, the first project being his art book “Illuminations”, and a series of graphic novels called ‘Chronicles of HATE’, book 1 came out to great reviews and book 2 is on the way. More recently he has been working with a board game company called Guillotine games. In addition to personal projects, he still finds time for freelance work with various companies.

Blood of Rome is a board game of gothic horror in ancient Rome. Rome will burn, Rome will bleed!

In Blood of Rome, supernatural factions fight a secret war in the streets and catacombs of Rome. Vampires, Werewolves, Gargoyles, Witches, and Slayers form alliances and vendettas in their conflict over the soul of the empire. Each player secretly belongs to one of the five Factions, with the goals of dominating the most locations on the City Board, and slaying the agents of factions with which they have a Vendetta.

Players take turns placing cards from their hands onto the limited slots at the various board locations. Each card belongs to a different faction, and has special abilities which can: add new agents to a location, transform agents from one faction into another, trigger a combat vote, make an accusation against another player, and more. Any player can play a card from any faction on their turn, thereby manipulating their enemies into doing their dirty work for them. A Calendar of Auguries tracks the cycles of the sun and moon, triggering a location's bonus ability when the proper faction card is played.

description: Double Feature: Everyone's a Critic is an expansion for the Double Feature base game in which all you need to know are the movies you've seen!

boardgame: Glory That Was Greece, Volume 1: The Battles of Leuctra and Mantineia
description:
The Glory that was Greece is a series of games depicting several battles from the ancient world. The games are introductory in level and complexity, designed for fun and competitive play, while also depicting the history of the battles. This first volume in the series depicts the battles of Leuctra and Mantineia.

After the Peloponnesian War, Sparta was the uncontested military leader of the Greek poleis. For almost three decades the Spartans were considered nearly invincible in an open field battle due to the strength and tactical organization of their hoplites.

Beginning in 378 BC a coalition of cities from the northern part of Greece, led by Athens and Thebes, began to challenge Spartan power and for a short period of time, under the leadership of two great generals, Pelopidas and Epaminondas, the small city of Thebes achieved a lasting place in ancient history, making the acropolis of their city (called Cadmea and for a long time subject to the control of a Spartan garrison) a sort of paladin of freedom against Lacedaemonian power.

The game’s battles of volume 1 in the series, Leuctra and Mantineia, depict the rise and fall of this short "empire", serving as an eulogy to one of the greatest military leaders of all time, Epaminondas.
family: History: Greek & Persian conflicts of the 4th century BC

description:
Solo expansion for Dolmen.

In solo play you play against three virtual players. Your goal is to achive as many vicorty points as possible, to beat your highscore and to increase the amount by which you beat the best virtual opponent.

Adds 36 new cards to the game.

Expansion for Dolmen that adds 10 tomb tiles and one black stone to the game.

The tomb tiles are placed in the center of each octogonal stone circle. If you are the first player to place a stone in that stone circle you get to take the tomb tile and keep it secret in front of you. When and how the tiles' ability can be used depends on the tile itself.

Expansion for Dolmen that adds ten ritual cards to the game.

Before starting the game shuffle and deal each player two face down cards. Return any spare cards to the box. The ritual cards are goal cards that may give additional victory points in the scoring round at the end of the game.

User summary

In this platform fighting card game that emulates videogames like Super Smash Brothers, players take turns revealing action cards to move and attack. Damage adds CRASH cards to each players deck and they are eliminated after a set number are revealed, or if they're knocked clear of the map.

This game was included in PNP format within the RPG bundle Indie Mixtape Volume 3.

Objective: be the first transporting 5 l sea cow milk (=Seekuhmilch)to hungry shark babies through the stormy sea

Players take turns moving their pawns according to the number (0-4) rolled on a six-sided die, transporting milk. Board with 29 spaces has 4 'traps' which you should avoid - otherwise you'll loose your milk due to stormy weather and must start again.

1001 Odysseys is a game of fantastic space adventures. The first human FTL ship, the Odyssey, sets out to explore a new sector of the galaxy after finding an operational alien stargate. Players take on the roles of the Odyssey's bridge crew, as they find planets, meet strange new aliens, and eventually try to find the way back home!

Each time a group sits down at the table, they play through one chapter of a story: a co-operative adventure will result in a new chapter being unlocked for the next session. The chapters aren't linear though, your path through the story book will be determined by your choices. Every standalone book tells of one of the Odyssey's adventures through alien space, but they do not require the same set of players each time. No two playthroughs of a given story book will be exactly alike!

While playing, the Mission Control board will track your progress through the Chapter. When the crew wants to do something in the game, they combine the letters of an Action, Mission, and Location to form a code such as B-3-D4, and look up that paragraph in the book. After reading the paragraph's story and game instructions, you'll advance your adventure one step, and have new options to choose from. Your journeys are recorded in a booklet called the Passport, which holds items, chapter data, and is effectively the "save file" for the game.

1001 Odysseys comes with 4 full story books, spanning approximately 400 pages. Each chapter can last anywhere from 30-75 minutes, depending on the group and the size of the chapter.

boardgame: Dirty Neighbors: This Game is Not For You
description:
Dirty Neighbors is a party game for mature audiences. The game combines everyone's wit, humor, and personal problems to expose things you never knew about your friends. The more dirt you have on your friends, the more your jaw will hurt from laughing. But, then again... this game is not for you.

How to play?
The first player takes a card.

That card will tell you the dirty neighbor of this round.

Then, everyone, except the dirty neighbor, will fill in the blanks with their most creative, dirty, witty, or brutal 
answers and hand their answers to the dirty neighbor.

The dirty neighbor gets to choose their favorite answer and decides who wins that round’s card.

First player to collect five cards wins.

The underlying concept in this game is that you, as a player, have all means of employing strategies, controlling the movement of your troops and tactical insight in order to win.

The goal of the game is to destroy your opponent’s base, but how you can achieve this is left to the player.

You play the game with cards on a map, each card has their own strengths and movement speeds. Strategic and tactical gameplay are a necessity to win!

In a godforsaken town in the wild west a new saloon has opened. Citizents are scrambling for the admission in hopes of free booze. With wise and smart decisions you need to help them to achieve their goals and at the same time impede on other players as much as you can. Each citizen has unique capabilities supporting their admission but your decisions and tactics can make them succeed in this entertaining card game.

Setup: At the beginning sort all the cards by color. Each players chooses a certain color. The optional characters (negative numbers) can be used or can be excluded from the deck at your will. For the first few playthroughs, until you get familiar with the characters and game mechanics, it’s best to play without the optional characters. Each player shuffles all of their cards, deal out 4 cards for their hands and placing the rest face down in front of them. Place the Entrance card on the table and in clockwise order the players begin to put their cards one-by-one next to the Entrance card.
 Gameplay: (each step is mandatory)

    Place a card
    Execute the action for character of the placed card
    Execute starred characters’ actions in the queue from back to front. In every turn each character executes their action once, hence if a starred card is placed during the turn then its action won’t be executed again.
    Draw a card if you have less than 4 cards in hand.
    If there are at least 5 cards in the queue then the first 2 cards enters the Saloon. At the end of the game (even if Thief is in the queue) when all of the cards are played out the first card of the last queue enters the Saloon and the others go to the Cemetery. Cards are collected facing down.
    If the color of the last card of the queue doesn’t match the color of one of the cards which enters the Saloon then the last card walks sadly to the Cemetery. Optional rule: The player whose character walked to the cemetery is allowed (but not compulsorily) to draw one card from one of their opponent’s hand if this opponent holds at least 4 cards in their hand. The player puts the drawn card facing up in front of the opponent. The opponent must play out this drawn card in their next turn. Card can not be drawn from opponent who already has a drawn card in front of them.


Ending: Game ends when all of the cards are played out. The player who has the most characters in the Saloon wins. If several players end up with a tie then the player whose characters in the saloon have the lowest overall values wins.

Fatesworn is an expansion to Foretold: Rise of a God in which players take on the role of a deity who gathers followers, builds a temple and then smites rival gods. Foretold is a deck-building game with a tower defense element. During the game you may purchase cards or tiles from the Marketplace to grow your followers and your temple. Players may raid a rivals temple and if they get past defenses, roll a mighty smite die to deal damage. You win the game if you build a full set of 4 aligned Fate Relics or your rivals life points drop to zero.

Fatesworn features a number of new and exciting additions to Foretold: Rise of a God including cards that count as having every Fate alignment type and powerful new relics. It adds a new game mechanism called Decree. While a card with Decree is in the Marketplace, its Decree ability is active and affects game play.

Business 2000 is a board game you can play with the whole family or with friends. The game was first released in 1989. The rules of the game are very similar to the well-known series of Monopoly board games . Therefore, a person who had ever played Monopoly, will quickly adapt to the rules of Business 2000.

Business 2000 is an economic strategy that contributes to the development of logic and observation, responsibility for decision making and ability to predict results.

It is a roll-and-move game, where the participants throw the dice and walk around the perimeter of the playing field. They buy or sell various properties, companies and even entire industries. The players can also develop their property.

The players have the right to trade and negotiate with each other at any time. The ultimate goal is to drive opponents into bankruptcy. The main differences from the Monopoly series are special fields and action cards, that have specific rules, namely the "Casino" and "Hippodrome" fields and the "Shares" special action cards, that increase a player's income every turn.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Yargo is turn-based, strategic battle game for two players. Each player controls a ship with one captain, a pirate crew, and a cheeky monkey. Fight from ship to ship and find out who is the true master of the seas. Basic gameplay structure as follows:


    Players start every turn with 4 coins 
    On your turn, spend your coins on actions from the action menu
    Many actions require you to flip a coin to determine outcome
    Once you've spent all your coins, it's your opponents turn 
    The winner is the last crew standing

A horde of zombies has invaded your secret ninja school.  Many fellow ninja students have died all around you.  You ran and found shelter for a brief moment, and have now gathered your Ki Energy and your resolve to escape from your current hiding place.  Before venturing out into a potentially hostile world overrun with undead you are determined to gather some items to build your confidence to face the nightmare.  Grab some things and get out as soon as possible.  Good Luck!

The game is designed to run on Six-Sided Dice and can be played in a solo/survival mode.  Each player will want to have 2 Six-Sided dice to use as counters on their Character sheets, 1 for Ki Energy and the other for Zombie Stalkers that are looking to rip the Ninja Schoolgirl into itty bitty pieces if only they could get their hands on the lithe and sneaky Ninja.

A community of 3-4 Six-Sided Dice can be shared in turn order to the active player.  The typical Action Roll will use 2 Six-Sided Dice.  The extra Di can be purchased with Ki Energy Points that are gained mostly through successfully eliminating a Zombie Stalker.

After that it is up to you to run around the school acquiring items that give you Confidence enough to leave the school.  Whoever has the most Confidence at the end, wins the game!  Who will be the most Confident Ninja?  If anyone actually lives that is…

In Gods of Adventure players take on the roles of classical Gods who together influence the motivations and emotions of a group of eight fantasy adventurers, slaying creatures and overcoming obstacles. However, unlike many Richard Launius games, Gods of Adventure is not a coop.

The game plays either 3 or 4 players.  Every God's (player) deck is identical except for color and another small but important detail: the spoken words on the cards for each hero (divided by color) are all different, reflecting your temperament based on the God you are playing.  Each player will have a unique power associated with your temperament. The four temperaments are associated with Greed, Good Fortune, Aggression, and Deceit.

Find the right animals! What are those with hairs? Which can swim? Listen carefully! To which animal belongs every cry: was it a wild boar or a seal? Become trackers purposes! Which animal has left these footprints? Categorize the animals by various characteristics. Is a frog heavier than a mouse? What maximum speed can reach the elephant?
With tiptoi, young zoo visitors develop their knowledge of domestic and wild animals!

Retrouve les bons animaux ! Quels sont ceux qui ont des poils, lesquels savent nager ? Tendez bien l’oreille ! À quel animal appartient chaque cri : était-ce un sanglier ou un phoque ? Devenez de fins pisteurs ! Quel animal a laissé ces empreintes ? Classez les animaux selon diverses caractéristiques. Une grenouille est-elle plus lourde qu’une souris ? Quelle vitesse maximale peut atteindre l’éléphant ?
Avec tiptoi®, les jeunes visiteurs du zoo développent leurs connaissances sur les animaux domestiques et sauvages !

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary: 
In this game, players advance 1-9 pieces to their opponent's side to compete with all their pieces.

The movement of pieces is determined by the pattern of the tiles making up the board. Prior to playing the game, players randomly shuffle the tiles and create a 9x9 board. This is the most distinguishing feature of the game.

As in Chess, players are able to remove　their opponent's pieces from the board, so the strategy involves moving pieces along the board in coordination with one another. A player is able to gain control over the game by reading the pattern of the board and competing in a location that is advantageous to themselves.

Like all games produced by Logy Games, this game is made with ceramic pieces which results in it weighing nearly 2kg.

Publisher's summary:
1～9点の駒を敵陣地まで到達させてその駒の合計点を競うゲームです。
駒の動きはボードタイルのパターンによって制御されるので、プレー前に9×9マスのタイルを自由にシャッフルしてボードのパターンを作るのが最大の特徴です。
チェスのように敵の駒を取ることができるので、駒を連携させながら動かすのが戦術です。
ボードパターンの読んで自分の有利な場所で戦えれば、ゲームをコントロールできます。

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Wrath of the Righteous – "Sweet Dragon Costume" Promo Card
description:
Armor

Traits: 
Light Armor
Dragon
Mythic

Check to Acquire: 
Constitution / Fortitude 5

Powers: Reveal this card to reduce Fire damage dealt to you to 0, then roll 1d12. On a 1, banish this card.

Bury this card and expend a mythic charge to add 1d20 to your check to defeat a monster.

Banish this card to reduce all damage dealt to you to 0; if proficient with light armors, bury this card instead.

"Rawr! I'm a scary dragon!" -Goblin in kobold costume
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
Βρες το Πες το (Find It, Say It) is a party game similar to Taboo.

You have to describe a word to your teammates without using some words written on your card in a specific time. If successful you gain the card and you start a new one if there is still time. The team with the most cards wins at the end of the game.

There are rules for 2 players as well.

boardgame: Find It, Say It For Children
description:
Βρες το Πες το για παιδιά (Find It, Say It For Children) is a party game similar to Taboo.

You have to describe a word to your teammates without using some words written on your card in a specific time. If successful you gain the card and you start a new one if there is still time. The team with the most cards wins at the end of the game.

There are rules for 2 players as well.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
It's an excellent party game similar to Taboo!

You have to describe a word to your teammates without using some words written on your card in a specific time! If successful you gain the card and you start a new one if there is still time. The team with the most cards wins at the end of the game!

The first player to get his two pawns from space 1 to space 90 wins the game. Movement is determined by a die. On the roll of a 6, the player moves a pawn and gets to place a pit to trap his opponents. But beware, some pawns may fall into their own pit. Pawns that fall into a pit have to start at the beginning again.

The game was published between 1922 and 1938.

Setting: The Year is 2078. The world has changed and will not return to it’s previous state. In 2052 Umbratech, the first major corporation to rise from the ashes of the ecological wars of the mid 40’s grew in power and ambition. With their invention of the world’s first Planetary Core Reactor, located on the undersea Mid-Atlantic Ridge, it was hoped that the world would never again rely on non-renewable sources of energy. However, things did not go as planned. With no knowledge of the deepest crusts scientists blindly drilled to reach the intense heat of the Earth’s Core, relying on theoretical data on the makeup of the Earth’s layers. They were wrong, and a rapid reactor failure ensued, allowing the Earth’s mantle to bleed through the crust. Massive continental shift occurred, along with earthquakes and a total disruption of oceanic ecosystems. A new continent emerged from the ridge, born in a matter of months. Midatlantia, as it was called, was both rich in resources and hotly contested. New ocean currents, melted icecaps, and the worldwide destruction of cities launched a new era on the Earth as world powers fought bitterly for the new and old land alike.

This war was known as the Mid-Atlantic War. By 2057 the war was over, and an alliance of the warring states had been formed to share the land. However, shortly after colonization the Mid-Atlantic Alliance ceded from their mother nations and declared independence, causing worldwide uproar. The troubles of creating a new nation were soon soothed by the vast wealth of Midatlantia, and the world was quickly ready to begin trade despite misgivings about the legitimacy of such a nation.

In 2064, the world’s first, large scale underwater research station was created under the mantle of international cooperation. Recognizing that the ocean held untold secrets which would ultimately benefit mankind, the major nations of the world came together to form the International Maritime Collective. Together, they created the International Oceanic Observatory and Research Facility, often referred to as “The Dome”. This underwater facility was built on the floor of the Mariana trench in the Atlantic Ocean, thousands of feet beneath the surface. It has now been operational for fourteen years. Numerous scientific advances have been achieved through its research and thousands of new species have been cataloged and studied, resulting in cures for many diseases and new technologies that improve the lives of people everywhere. But rumour has it that scientists aboard the station have been conducting unauthorized research as well.

Overview: Fathoms is a cooperative adventure game with miniatures in which a group of players fight against hordes of undersea monsters controlled by a game master. Gameplay is designed to pit players against a changing environment as monsters swarm and water pours through hull breaches. Players advance their characters skills and armament by gaining experience. The game can be played in campaign mode in which a series of missions unfold a storyline, or as one-shot scenarios.

Gameplay: Fathoms begins by one player assuming the role of the Fathom Master, while the others pick from a selection of characters. Both the Fathom Master and the other players earn experience points. Players use it advance their character in skills that benefit either themselves or the whole team and to buy equipment. The Fathom Master uses it to unleash increasing terrors upon the players, such as more monsters or events like hull breaches. Players gain experience when they explore, kill monsters or use computer terminals to perform various tasks. This experience may be used between missions or at equipment lockers. The Fathom Master gains experience by damaging players and may spend it on his turn.

The game board functions as a grid and the line of sight rules are relatively simple. Each player receives two actions which may be divided any way they choose. In any order or repetition a player may move, attack or use an item/door/skill. Turn order is determined by rolling for initiative. Each player has 1 miniature which they maneuver through the rooms on the game board. When new rooms are opened, the Fathom Master places units depending on the number of players he opposes. Some of those units are predetermined while other may be bought with experience to add variability. Each round the Fathom Master follows the mission guide to keep the game evolving.

Modes of Play:
Fathoms offers two modes of play: Campaign mode and Scenarios

There are 7 missions in Campaign mode, all of which are meant to be played sequentially as the story unfolds. Scenarios are one-shot missions that represent possible scenarios that could have occurred over the course of the storyline.

Game End: The game ends when players complete either the Campaign storyline or a one-shot Scenario. The game also ends if all Players are killed during a mission. Players that don't work well with others die, quickly.

Unique Elements: What makes Fathoms different than other adventure games is its use of water as an important game element. As the players progress through the game they encounter pools of water and hull breaches that will flood the map room by room. One simple rule guides the flow of water from room to room, making it simple to work with and compelling in the way it changes the battlefield. In water, weapons may become stronger or weaker, some skills may cease to be effective, and monsters grow stronger and more formidable.

Fathoms also has high-ground, which can give tactical advantage whether a room is dry or flooding with water.

boardgame: Zombicide: Black Plague Special Guest Box – Gipi
description:
A box set of four Zombicide: Black Plague survivors that were designed by the Italian artist Gipi. These four survivors are from Gipi's card game Bruti. The survivors include:

    Donna Carlotta
    The Little Prince
    Clelia La Santa
    Pipino, the Humongous

English:
As a general, each player rules over the regions of influence of his continent. By employing their regions of influence in a carefully considered manner, each general can optimise the fighting power of the army under his command. In battle, each general commands the individual combat units and thereby influences the action. His strategic and tactical conduct determines victory or defeat. The objective is to conquer all regions of influence.

Deutsch:
Als General herrscht jeder Spieler über die Einflussgebiete seines Kontinents. Durch überlegten Einsatz seiner Einflussgebiete optimiert er die Kampfkraft seiner Armee. Im Kampf befehligt er die einzelnen Kampfeinheiten und beeinflusst so das Kampfgeschehen. Sein strategisches und taktisches Vorgehen entscheidet über Sieg oder Niederlage. Ziel ist die Eroberung aller Einflussgebiete.

User summary:

Steam Wars is an abstract territory-occupation game for two players. Your goal is to earn victory points by expanding your territory while making your opponent lose their territory.

You initially control a territory with three hexes and one robot token on the hexagonal grid board. Each turn, you move one of your robots to expand or secure your territory. A robot can move to any hex within your territory or one hex outside, including hexes marked by your opponent, except for a hex adjacent to or occupied by your opponent's robot.

After movement, place one of your occupation markers on the destination hex if there isn't a marker there already. This hex is now part of your territory. If the hex was your opponent's, they lose control of it, and if this causes their territory to be split, they lose control of all hexes not connected with their robot. Discard all their occupation markers on these hexes.

Your opponent's robots cannot move to one of your hexes adjacent to or occupied by your robot, so secure your territory by moving your robot wisely.

Some hexes have a higher value but have a special requirement to control. As you expand your territory, you will be able to deploy more robots and fulfill the requirement to control these hexes.

The game ends when either player uses up their 30 occupation markers. Add up your victory points earned by hexes you control and occupation markers your opponent discarded. The player with higher total wins the game.
English rulebook is now available.

Publisher's summary:
２人用アブストラクト陣取りゲーム
研究所の地下で２体のロボットが戦うゲーム。
勝利条件：どちらかのプレイヤーの歯車タイルが０枚のとき、勝利点の多いプレイヤーが勝利。
勝利点はボード上の歯車タイル＋獲得した相手の歯車タイル。
ヘックスのボード上でロボットを動かし、自分の陣地を示す歯車タイルを置いて陣地を広げていく。
ロボットは陣地の周り１マスに移動でき、相手の陣地も攻められる。
ロボットの周り１マスの同陣営の歯車タイルは相手のロボットに攻められない。

boardgame: War of the Ring: Warriors of Middle-earth
description:
War of the Ring: Warriors of Middle-earth includes Ents, Dead Men of Dunharrow, Great Eagles of the Misty Mountains, Corsairs of Umbar, Wild Hilmen from Dunland, and Giant Spiders, and with this new expansion for War of the Ring (second edition), these warriors and creatures of Middle-earth – previously featured only as special Event Cards – will become an essential part of the game, with unique figures and specific abilities.

Warriors of Middle-earth also presents new mechanisms to enhance the game. Faction Dice and Faction Events, used together with the new figures, open up new and exciting strategies and make the War of the Ring (second edition) even more challenging and fun.
family: Series: War of the Ring (Nexus/Ares Games)

description:
Hunters: Enter the Darkness is a team based, player-vs-player, tactical board game that allows the greatest minds to draft characters, then battle it out in different modes of play like the lane pushing "Assault" Mode or the all out brawl that is the "Arena" Mode.

As games progress, players will earn money to upgrade their characters dashboard with new equipment, skills and feats, all unique to that character, allowing for different builds and tactics with each champion and team!

Only the strongest and smartest champions will prevail, hunter or the hunted? That's up to you as these epic battles unfold!

Hunters: Enter the Darkness features:

     Incredible Miniatures
     Action Point turn system
     Unique development of your character
     Modular Boards
     Custom die for the combat
     Mechanics of popular MOBA video games like DOTA, DOTA 2, League of Legends and Heroes of the Storm

boardgame: DragonFlame: Fury of the Dragon Promo Card
description:
The Fury of the Dragon card was a stretch goal in the DragonFlame Kickstarter campaign.

The card is worth 3 Glory Points and reads:
"When you place the last fire token onto a village, take this card and place it in your hoard."

The card starts out in play, off to the side, or near the villages. When any player fills the last available spot of a village (ensuring it will score at the end of the game), then they take the card into their hoard. If another player fills the last available spot of a village, they will take the Fury of the Dragon from the player who currently controls it. The player with the Fury in their hoard at the end of the game will score the 3 points for controlling it.

Dracovalis is a game about dragons attacking, capturing, and destroying cities. The players send dragons to attack or defend cities in order to control as many cities as possible. Players can also cast spells to strengthen the beasts. Aside from the pure joy of burning your friend's cities down or forcing his dragon to turn tail and run, Dracovalis features simultaneous play, so you'll be guessing where your opponent will go every turn.

During your turn, you will secretly decide what towns you wish to attack, or which of your towns are vulnerable and need defending. Once all players have decided, everyone moves simultaneously and the battles begin. Combat is resolved through a simple multi-round dice-pool mechanic that feels like combat and resolves quickly.

You will have a limited number of spells that you can cast during your turn, and the spells can be cast in multiple ways. Will you burn many spell components to ensure your spell goes off properly, or will you risk spell failure to hold some components in reserve?

At the game end, the player who controls the most territory wins.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Orleans: Neue Ortskarten N°1 ("Place Tiles N°1") is a mini expansion for Orléans that consists of three new place tiles: Bakery, Goldsmith, and Lair.

Pigskin is a game of card drafting and color combos. In each round, players will draft their team and play out four quarters in an attempt to score the most touchdowns. At the end of the fourth quarter, the coach with the higher score wins the championship!

Using quarterbacks, offensive players, and defensive players, you will either score or prevent touchdowns; how these players interact with each other is why it's all about the draft!

Draft five players each round and play four of them for your lineup. The color combo system gives you multiple ways to link cards together in order to pull off special plays and out-think your opponent.

BLUE 42! HUT, HUT, HIKE!

Whistling Captures is a two player abstract strategy game played on a large hexagonal board.  You win by capturing all of your opponents' pieces.  You capture opponent pieces by sliding past them in an adjacent row; hence, the name "Whistling Captures."

The rules to the game can be found in a post to the Rules Forum on this page.

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A children's game system based around marbles with instructions for four different marble based games.

1. Castle Marbles(Schlossmurmeln) - players take turns rolling marbes into the front gates of the castle for points.

2. Tin throwing(Dosenwerfen) - players attempt to throw marbles into the game tin for points.

3. Mini Bowling(Miniboule) - the cork marble is placed in a 1 inch circle and players attempt to roll their marble as close as possible to the cork marble without pushing it out of it's circle.

4. Pyramid rolling(Pyramidenroller) - players build a pyramid with their dice and draw an 8 inch circle around it.  Another player then attempts to roll marbles at the pyramid, all marbles knocked out of the circle to to the rolling player and marbles in the circle go to the player who set up the pyramid.

Hierarchy is a highly interactive negotiation and voting game, built on top of a simple roll-and-move game, with a splash of player selected special player powers on top for spice. A game with 4 players takes 45 to 60 minutes to play.

Each player starts Hierarchy as a pawn, advancing up a simple roll-and-move track. As you advance up the track, you "promote" from pawn to rook, knight, bishop, queen, and (ultimately) king. Each promotion gives you a new special power to help you bend or break the rules of the game. Several of these powers pertain to voting.

Oh, did I forget to mention voting? Each player has a hand of cards. Some have immediate one-shot effects while others are votes. Votes introduce new rules to the game. When you propose a vote, you declare who (one, some, or all players) the new rule will apply to. If the vote passes, it stays in play one round for every vote it passed by (If a vote is 5-to-3 in favor, that vote's rule will stay in play for two rounds.). Votes can affect many different things such as movement, cards in hand or in play, votes, etc.

The first player to reach the last square of the board wins the game. With the player powers, unexpected one-shot card effects, and the possibility of sanction through votes against you from the other players, it's a task easier said than done.

It's like Pente, but on a smaller board with a dash of luck and little press-your-luck for garnish on top! Your goal: Form a straight, contiguous horizontal, vertical, or diagonal line of at least five pieces of your color. For 2 to 4 players. Plays in 30 to 45 minutes.

You play Eight-by-Eight in the spaces of an 8x8 grid. Each column and row is numbered so every space is paired with a unique pair of numbers from 1 to 8 (ex: The spaces 4,2; 4,3; 4,4; and 4,5 are adjacent to each other and form a straight horizontal line.). On your turn you roll an eight-sided die. Now you have a choice: Play a piece in any vacant space in the row or column you rolled (ex: You roll a "6." You may play in any vacant space in the column numbered "6" or the row numbered "6.") or you can place one of your pieces on the number you rolled to "black it out" and roll again. If you roll a number you've blacked out, your turn immediately ends.

The first player to get five or more pieces in a straight line (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal) wins the game. Quick, easy, and with more strategy than you'd expect.

Ice Hockey trivia game.
Players use dice to move along the track. When they land on a ? space they must answer a question. If the correct answer is forthcoming, that player's pawn on the score table is moved up one row. There are 16 rows and if you get to the top first (16 correct answers), you win the game.
On the track there are also ! spaces. Land on one of these you draw an Event Card and resolve it.

The 36 tiles are separately placed face down on the table. The object is to collect a set of 4 characters. A set needs a Kid at first and then followed by related tiles - the In-Training Digimon, the Rookie Digimon and the Champion Digimon. These are collectively known as a Team.

The game play is easy. Reveal a tile. If you can use it, take it. If you can't use it, it is shown to the other players and returned face-down to the table.

If an Evil Digimon is revealed you must duel with another player. A duel is resolved by a die roll each, the highest thrower wins. If the challenger wins, the other player has to turn over one of his tiles. The Challenger gets the Evil Digimon as it is worth points at the end of the game. If the challenged player wins, he gets the Evil Digimon tile and the challenger turns over one of his tiles. A further advantage on winning a duel is that you can turn back a tile in front of you if it has previously been turned face down.

The game continues until there are no more tiles from the middle of the table to be revealed. Points are awarded for:
A complete team (all face-up) scores 5 points.
Any other turned up team members in uncompleted teams score 1 point each.
An Evil Digimon in your possession scores 2 points.
The player with the most points, wins.

User summary:

A 2 player card game that simulates a martial arts duel, or perhaps a typical fighting game.

Each player has a hand of Power, Speed and Technique cards (battle cards) that has a rock-paper-scissors relationship. Each player also has 18 special "moves" cards in a shuffled deck, with a few lined face up. Each turn, a player plays one of his battle cards, and the turn winner (of the rock-paper-scissors) does 1 damage. The battle cards stay turn after turn, building up a line. After winning a turn, a player can use one of his face up moves if the requirements for that move corresponds with what he has in his battle card line, to release combos, dealing more damage. Each move also has a special effect that triggers when released.

For each damage a player gets, he reveals the top card of his moves deck. So, for each damage, the player gets access to more moves.

boardgame: Zhu Zhu Pets: Wild Wacky Hamster Game
description: Welcome to the land of Zhu Zhu Pets and get ready for the Wild and Wacky Hamster Race! Travel along the Zhu Zhu Pets path. But this is anything but a simple race: there are many obstacles you'll encounter along the way. Some will push you ahead, some will send you back, and some can be used to slow down the other players.

boardgame: Der Hobbit: Eine unerwartete Reise: Fürst der Adler – Die Mini-Erweiterung
description:
This card may be shuffled to Carrock Adventure deck.
If drawn the Prince of Eagle help You.
Roll the die:
1-2 = Look at the 2 topmost cards and put back in any order
3-4 = Nothing happens
5 = Move any free dwarf to empty space where the prisoner was hold
6 = 1 or 2 dwarfs or Bilbo may move 6 fields

boardgame: Der Hobbit: Eine unerwartete Reise: Azog – Die Mini-Erweiterung
description:
This card may be shuffled to Trollshaws Adventure deck.
If drawn roll the die:
1-2 = If Gandalf is present in Trollshaws He left 2 fields in chosen direction
3-4 = All game-figures in Trollshaws move 4 fields back
5-6 = Nothing happens

Second game in the series with different themes including movies, music, novels etc.

There is letter in one side of the card and theme (bands, actors, TV-Series etc.) in another side of the card. Turn around top card and be the first to say something that matches the theme from one card starting the letter from another card and You get the card as a point. If nobody can´t say anything or there is a tie the card is kept for the next round.
If the deck runs out the player with the most points is the winner.

Name: Luddit. Mix of "Ludit" (Latin: "He plays") and "Luddite" (-"against the machines"- cause the ultimate purpose of this game -project- is to be impossible to solve by computers; i really hope some of you would want to join forces with me in this holy crusade  ).

Characteristics: the leading objective was (is) to make a game which could be as difficult as possible to solve for the computers (maybe...impossible?). By the moment, contrarily to my interest, the rules are by far not simple, ...im trying to improve that. I tried to achieve complexity by giving to the game a very chaotical and recursive nature. The board is defined in order to try to let an action to have infinite repercussion in the unfolding of the game. Its notable that, strictly speaking, the board doesnt have borders (as may be seen in the rules), so there is no "preferential positions". Its interesting to note that the definition of "loop" does lead to literally infinite possibilities of combination in some cases (its definition is precisely the feature that has leadme to think that this game can be much more than just "dificult" for the computer...). And its interesting to note that wining implies to reduce this possibilities to one, that is, to reduce the number of possibilities, which is the thermodynamic definition of negative entropy. So you have to play with chaos to bring order.

Equipment: 
- 9x9 Torus board with 9x9 square grid (a standard 9x9 GO board can be used, obeying the mechanic of the torus board), 18xwhite stones, 18xblack stones. (i decided this size mostly to facilitate initial playtesting. Of course the game can be played in larger boards)

Rules:

1 - Game is played on the intersections. Adjacency is defined as orthogonal and diagonal (i.e. every intersection point has 8 adjacent points).

2 - There can’t be more than 18 total stones on the board at any time.

3 - We will call "piece" to every single stone or loop. A piece is of the color of a player if every stone that composes it is of the color of that player. A piece is "at range" of another if they shares at least one of their adjacent intersections.

4 - We will call "loop" to every group of pieces (regardless of color) where every piece is at range of at least another two pieces of the group (nothing prevents loops for sharing pieces). Its implied by the rule, but to clarify: If a piece A is composed by all the stones that composes a piece B, and viceversa, then A and B are the same piece, and B cant be considered has "another" piece for A, and viceversa.

5 - We will say that a piece is "conditioned" to a player if its of the player color or if any group of pieces (regardless of color) that are at range of that piece conforms a loop.

6 - A player makes a "move" by either:
a) Taking a single stone of his color from the outside of the board and placing it in an empty place over the board.
b) Removing a piece that is conditioned to him from the board.
c) Removing a piece that is conditioned to him from the board, and then placing it back on an empty space on the board. If the removed piece is a loop, it has to remain a loop when placed back on the board.

7 - At the start of the game the board is empty. Each player is assigned a color. Black plays first. Then both players play alternately. In his turn, a player can do one or two moves, if he has no legal move to perform the game ends, and he loses.

Gnomes, assemble! The factory is in dire need of your skills on the assembly line. Roll dice to amass parts, build components, and be the first gnome to finish their work and go home!

Of course, your co-gnomes won't mind if you borrow some of their parts and use your components to improve your work and mess up theirs, right?

In 9 y medio (9 and a half), each player will beat the opponents in a "gambler game".

The players should get 9 1/2 or near it for beat their opponents. A player will win if all players are eliminated (losing all their coins), or when all rounds are played and who has more coins get the victory.

This game is a "re-version" of Las siete y media

(Summarized from the rules.  Published by Neil A. Kjos Music Co.)

Similar to "Slap Jack" this is an educational card game for 2-8 players to help learn music keys, signatures and chords.

To start the game, the deck of Cat KEY cards are shuffled and set aside and then the Major KEY SIGNATURE cards, the Primary CHORD cards, and the WILD Mouse cards are shuffled together and dealt evenly among players.  A Cat KEY card is turned over to show what key the players are in, and which primary chords and key signatures they are looking for.

Players take turns flipping over the top card from their deck and placing it in the central discard pile until the flipped card shows a Major KEY SIGNATURE or a Primary CHORD card belonging to the key of the Cat KEY card, then players race to "pounce" on it by slapping it with their hand.  The first to slap correctly adds the discard pile to his deck and then play continues.  If someone incorrectly slaps, he has to give a card from his deck to each other player.

The player who can collect the entire deck (or a majority, if playing with a time limit) is the winner!

Using the Napoleonic version of the March into Battle system, this game portrays the Battle of Ligny in a furious, fast moving two turn game. Part of a group of mini-games on the battles of the Waterloo campaign, with  Quatre Bras and La Belle Alliance.

In this system as in reality, units will become ineffective from fatigue and disorder long before they run out of men - unless you, in command, can manage them effectively and maintain their edge. A turn lasts as long as the typical historical time to arrange and execute an assault - every move counts.

Map scale: 1km per hex
Unit scale: divisions, 1000 men per strength point
Time scale: 3.5 hours per turn

Objective: Gain most money

On an outer and inner circuit each player moves forward, based on a  die roll. Ending on spaces with symbols follow instructions printed into the lid. Crossing the money pot in the centre of the board: pay for it. Landing on: get it all.

Objective: Gain most of the cards by finding correct answers

The game contains 32 question cards (on rhyme) and 32 answer cards. The answer cards are distributed among the players. Players take turns drawing a question card. The player with the matching answer card must respond, otherwise each player may give the right answer gaining the card.

Frag mich was! has elements of Bezzerwizzer.

Lembitu: Heavy Cavalry, an expansion for the Lembitu board game, consists of twenty castles that can be used as fortifications, along with six knights; when two blank sides are rolled with the dice of one color, reinforcements arrive for those enemy forces and one knight is placed in front of the chain of enemy units.

Each player gets a Scooby-Doo! eraser mover to use in this exciting race to the finish. The special decoder helps you find the snacks you need to zoom ahead. but watch out for the monsters who will set you back. Special game board spaces can be used with smart phones for augmented reality interaction!

Legends of Rakasa is a sandbox style (open gameplay structure) game  with hunting, crafting, battling, puzzle cracking, and more. The game box is magically packed full of endless adventures.

Legends of Rakasa: Fordra introduces players to the world of Rakasa through Five Introductory Chapters. After players play through those first five chapters, players can go on to create their own unique adventures, or go to the Trivatch.com Forums page to check out game types others are creating.

The game is played on tiles (some holding decks representing the Roads, the Ocean, and the Forest). While seeking to complete different objectives, players hunt across Rakasa by moving their player tokens to the different locations and battle creatures using their player equipment. Players use the parts of defeated creatures to upgrade their equipment. Though upgrading isn't ever the main objective in the Introductory Five Chapters, players are much more likely to succeed in their adventures with stronger equipment.

Along with hunting, crafting, and battling using the cards in Rakasa, players perform different actions using Rakmin, a mini puzzle game included in Legends of Rakasa. Rakmin consist of small tiles and dice. The Rakmin puzzle system is used for actions such as tracking down something or unlocking items.

Five Chapter Story cards guide players through the introductory chapters of Rakasa. The events of these chapters play out over the space of roughly three days in Rakasa. Time passes, hour by hour each turn. Day turns to night and night to day as the events of the chapters unfold. Players may decide to work together cooperatively or work in competition with each other throughout all of the chapters.

The Introductory Five Chapters can be played as individual game instances (each consisting of 30 minutes to an hour of gameplay). The chapters can be played as a set of sequential events, with players progressing their equipment through the chapters. Players can also jump straight into a chapter without playing previous chapters by starting with general gear that matches the difficulty level of the game at that point in the Introductory Five Chapters. After playing through the standard chapters of Legends of Rakasa: Fordra, players will be ready to venture off and create legends of their own!

description: Chunky Fighters: Franck Choillard is the twelfth expansion to Chunky Fighters. The expansion comes with the dice and card for the patriot. This fighter has the power of Camembert (yes, the cheese) to lower all opponent's rolled die rolls by 1 while brandishing his Camembert. He can only Dodge when doing so.

Chunky Fighters: Aimé Rikain is the eleventh expansion to Chunky Fighters. The expansion comes with the dice and card for the cowboy. This fighter has the power of Ambidexterity (he may both attack and cure himself during the same turn) and gets +1 Damage during Standoffs.

Space Brew is a drinking game played with cards.  The game has a retro sci-fi theme and the object is to gather enough pieces of salvage to repair your ship. This is done by battling space monsters using various quantities of alcohol.

Equipment can be won from monsters to help your cause and you can hire mercenaries.  There is also a range of space sicknesses to endure and currency cards to coax away other player's mercenaries.

Mountain Rummy is based on classic draw and discard Rummy style play which melds elements of Rummy 500 and Gin Rummy, along with many fresh play elements to create a game that strives to be more skill based and less luck based than other Rummy based games.  Players will find that it's a poor idea to form a static strategy, since an opponent will undermine any one strategy if performed too often, creating a need for constantly adjusting strategy incorporating math, gambits, psychology and many other elements.  Created by the strategy board game loving couple Keith and Luci Medlock, Mountain Rummy was designed to replace more complex strategy games while mountaineering with only a single pack of cards, a laminated instruction sheet and dry erase marker!

Here are the official rules:

MOUNTAIN RUMMY (2 Players & Standard Deck)

Deadwood: Non-melded cards in hand.

Card Values: A=1 or 14 (Deadwood=14), 2-10=Face Value, Jack=11, Q=12, K=13, & Joker=Wild (Deadwood=15).  Suits rank in alphabetical order, Spades high.

Deal: Jr player shuffles, Sr cuts & Jr deals 1 card to Sr, then self.  High card deals.  Dealer shuffles, non-dealer cuts & 13 cards are dealt from top of deck in alternating fashion to form hands, starting w/ non-dealer.  1 card is dealt face up from top of deck to form discard pile & rest of deck is placed face down to form stock pile.

Set: 3 or 4 same value cards.

Run: ≥3 same suit cards w/ sequential value.

Meld: Set or Run placed face up in your play area.  Joker can’t make run w/ duplicate cards & melded value can’t be changed.

Layoff: Placing card that adds to either player’s existing meld face up in your play area.  If laying off on opponent’s melds, their melds may be reorganized.

Draw: Taking top card of stock pile or any card from discard pile.  If discard pile card is drawn, it may not be discarded the same turn.  If discard pile card other than topmost is drawn, any cards above it must be taken & it must be melded/laid off that turn.  
 Discard: Placing 1 card face up on top of discard pile, ensuring each lower card’s value is visible.

Knock: Discarding face down w/ Deadwood ≤ 10.  Opponent may meld/layoff before scoring.

Base Camp: Discarding face down w/ 0 Deadwood (10 pt bonus).

High Camp: Melding/laying off all cards in hand (20 pt bonus).

Play: Non-dealer plays first.  Turn begins w/ a draw, then melding/laying off (optional) & ends with a discard.  You may end hand by Knocking, Base/High Camp.

Undercut: Non-knocking player’s Deadwood ≤ knocking player’s & value of melds/layoffs ≥ knocking player’s (30 pt bonus).

Summit: Ending hand w/ Base/High Camp w/o melding/laying off on previous turns (40 pt bonus).

Scoring: Standard pts awarded to players for value of cards in their melds/layoffs, less Deadwood.  Player w/ most standard pts wins hand & deals next.  Bonus points transfer to winner.  Add all pts for each player to their previous total. Game won by hand winner w/ final score of ≥1000.

Are you brave or are you chicken? Play Chicken Out, a fast game of risk taking and second guessing.
 Play the turtle and mosey along. Play the frog and take a big leap. But beware the snakes and the vultures!
Chicken Out is a game for 2-6 players and is recommended for players of at least 8 years old

In this game players secretly choose a character to play each turn and then take a die roll and advance on the track.  The die rolls become riskier every turn and the characters that other players choose can change the results of the turn. Players may decide to chicken out and score points, but the player who is furthest along when they chicken out gets the most points. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.
Child's game where you are flipping pieces of bacon into the air & trying to catch them using a pan divided into scoring sections. Published in a children's magazine as a cut-and-assemble game.

"Deliver your passengers first and win!"

A taxi game where you have five passengers needing to go to five different and randomly selected locations. The first to deliver their passengers to their destinations and returns back to the taxi stand at their corresponding color wins!

Battle of France is a board game for two players. There are three levels. In the first level are single engine fighters. In the second level are added twin engine fighters. In the third level participate bombers.

The main characteristic is that the game board is covered with a net of equilateral triangles. The vertices of these triangles are points at which players hold the position and move figurines.

The objective is to shoot down all enemy planes. One figurine can shoot on enemy figurine by moving behind it. The game continues until one player runs out of the plane (first and second level). The main objective in third level is for bombers to pass into bombing zone.

description: "Cat goes around you" is game for younger players. Each has one figurine. Player rolls dice and move figurine in direction of path. When figurine reaches circular part of path, it moves in any direction. The winner is the player who first reach the center, where is the mouse.

Marsolimpijada is a very simple game for 2 to 6 players.
Players move figurines in direction of paths. There are several paths that are intertwined. Shorter paths have more obstacles that slow them down. The winner is the player who first gets to the finish line.
Formula indi is game for younger players. Each has one figurine. 3 laps are driven. Everyone has to stop in the pit stop once. It is important to find the shortest path.

Beaune/Turku is the newest addition to the Town Center line, which started with Alban Viard's brain-burning, elegant city-building sim game.

As Beaune is a walled town surrounded by vineyards, there are no suburbs. Also, the "elevator" black cubes must be built in the center. We're guessing that's because in many medieval towns, no building was allowed to be taller than the church, but who knows, the designer works in mysterious ways!

The Turku map has two seasons, summer and winter, alternating between odd- and even-numbered turns. During the summer, the two halves are considered separate, and during the winter, when the Aura river freezes, the two parts are considered one city - which allows for some clever play, such as building green cubes "next" to green cubes in the summer, when they are not considered adjacent, only for them to be joined in the winter.

Word creation game using cards printed with individual letters placed onto the table in grid format. Players construct words using adjacent consonants and any of three vowels available for that round.

Consonants count positively towards player score, wheres as vowels subtract points. The letter Y is the exception in both of these cases, losing the player points when used as a consonant but gaining the player points when used as a vowel.

The contents in the box allow for three different ways to play:


    Classic Contest


Players construct as many unique words as possible within a given time limit.


    Most Valuable Word


Players choose a single word from those they construct within a given time limit. The player with the highest-scoring word wins.


    Elimination Challenge


Players compete to construct words in a “shout it out” format. The letters used are removed from play until no new words can be formed. The scores on each consonant card are added. The person with the highest total wins.

description: Freeplay '88 is a supplement to Shipwreck with add on rules and campaign information. Based around a NATO military exercise in 1988, it is an in-depth look at what happens when the gloves come off... Features include: A fifty page rulebook with a laminated cover. Nine separate scenarios as well as a full campaign option. Full detailed listing of all ships, aircraft (including payloads) and airfields involved. More than twenty five pictures and illustrations. A two page campaign map of the North Sea. Over seventy fully made out detail sheets of forty nine different ships and five airfields from six different nations. Lists of abbreviations and designations. Extra weapons systems and aircraft.

In Divine Empire, the players are gods. Their target goal is a predetermined level of civilization. Each god has to reach this level as soon as possible.
The gameplay is stuffed with battles, magic and technology. The multiplayer version also adds a serious amount of backstabbing...

You can play the game with a starter deck (76 cards) for each player. More cards were available in booster packs of 12 cards each. Cards had different rarities.

Plans were made to publish English and Dutch versions. Unfortunately the Chinese market back then was not what it is today. We're talking about 1997 and distribution of the game was the ultimate nemesis that led to the demise of this game.

Work as a team to tell a funny and quirky story using the provided picture cards. Remember the numbers that appear in the pictures. Then test your memory and collect markers in the mini games. Once you’ve collected the required number of markers, you have to solve one last challenge: the master card.

Cloud Knows is a shape-recognizing puzzle game!

In each game, one plays the role of Cloud while others play the role of Wind. Cloud has to express the answer to the Winds, but can give hints only through the outline silhouette by gathering the parts of cloud. On the other side, all the Winds can do is take turns providing cloud cards to the Cloud player.

So this game is a big quiz consisting of many little sequential quizzes: Winds first guessing which card Cloud needs to piece together the answer, then trying to figure out what the answer actually is. The Wind who finds out the answer receives the same points as Cloud.

Let's challenge your imaginations!

From the publisher's web site:
The Games: Blood and Spectacle are a set of free wargames rules for gladiator games.

From the game's introduction:
Welcome to The Games: Blood and Spectacle.

In these pages you will find a game that allows you to play out the infamous blood contests between slave warriors of the Roman era. You will be a Lanista, the slave master, owner, and business man who dealt in blood and spectacle for profit. Your management and commands will lead your Gladiator troupe to glory, honor, and immortality; or death and poverty. Will you beg for mercy, or triumph for the crowds?

The game is a completely new breed of Detective Card Game that is based on realistic detective processes, characters and storylines, all set in London during the first half of 2015.

Crime scenes are real London locations and historical facts are blended with fiction. 125 different murder scenarios are waiting to be solved. The game encourages the use of "deductive and abductive reasoning" to assist in reducing the suspect list from 37 to 10 prime suspects, to ultimately identify the only possible killer. The objective of the game has two parts, concerned with discovering the identity of the culprit and having the incriminating evidence to hand!

The game has 3 play formats - OPENplay (2-6), COVERTplay (2-5) or SOLOplay - and has Career, Group and Co-Op variants that provide ample choice in the way the game is played.

The game set comes complete with; 5 Evidence Packs (labelled, ABCDE) a total of 300 cards; "How to be a Deadcert Detective Guide"; a Detective Checklist Pad (with 30 double sided sheets) and 25 Evidence Bags. Each Evidence Pack contains 3 "evidence types" - Victim; Crime Scene and Time of Death - that are used to define murder cases and provide details of the suspects. When the game set is initially opened, the Evidence Packs are each split into 3 Evidence type Packets and kept securely in their individual evidence bags. This will use 15 of 25 bags provided - more about the remaining bags later...

At the start of a game - or "murder episode" as it is referred to - one of each type of Evidence Packet is selected in order to define the murder mystery to be solved. With 5 Evidence Packs this means there are 5x5x5=125 possible episodes that can be played out. With the crime defined, the 18 suspect cards for each evidence type are shuffled together to form the 54 cards in play. 48 of the cards hold information about the suspects - why they had motive, means or the opportunity to carry out the murder. The other 6 are the Snoop cards which play a slightly different role depending on the format being played, but in essence are used to mix up the game play and enable the transfer of suspect evidence between players (or detectives if you wish to fully embrace the experience).

So what about the extra 10 evidence bags? Well 1 is a genuine spare, the other 9 however (and the extra compartment with the game set tray) enable you to safely store up to 3 expansion packs (labelled, FGH) sold separately. These Expansion Packs provide extra victims, crime scenes and times of death to the London mix and interlink with the existing packs. Not only do they increase the number of murder episodes possible, they do so exponentially. If all 3 Expansion Packs are added, 125 episodes expand to a potential 512.

The structure of the game is unique, and is the first of it's kind to provide multiple realistic crime mysteries with a pure card game format - no solution book is required, the murderer is simply the only suspect to have had motive, means and opportunity.

Unlike many card games, it does not lean heavily on the graphics. This, although potentially unpopular with many, is a deliberate move to help focus on the evidence, the textual content of the suspect cards. Font sizing, colouring and new sentences starting on new lines, has all been done to make the reading of the cards as comfortable as possible, to the widest audience as possible. Graphics play an important role in distinguishing the 3 card types, but that is their key role.

The final aspect of the game, that is only set to emerge after playing a few episodes, is the detail and integrity of all the characters that are woven together through the multiple storylines. Any information gathered about a victim or suspect may come in use in future investigations and nothing learnt will be contradicted later by another card. In short, the interwoven stories, events and relationships have as much integrity as the knowledge that you have identified the killer correctly. There are no miscarriages of justice with Deadcert.

Please note: The 15+ age advisory is for reason of content in the detail of the crimes committed, and is not associated with the difficulty of the game play.

description:
In Stolpersteine each player tries to be the first to reach the peak. Before the game begins, a path up the mountain is layed out from stones across the board. Roll the die and move your hiker forward. If you end up on solid ground, he will stick to the stone magnetically. But watch out for stumbling blocks (without a magnet hidden inside) which will make you fall: You'll have to start anew!
